chokri: Abraham nu David, David nuo Jisu Khrista shühye lüsida;
english: The book of the generation of Jesus Christ, the son of David, the son of Abraham.

chokri: Abraham yo Isaac pari, Isaac yo James pari; James yo Judas mo pü sazühzürako pariyi.
english: Abraham became the father of Isaac. Isaac became the father of Jacob. Jacob became the father of Judah and his brothers.

chokri: Süze Juda-e Tamarpü yono Perez mo Zora unyi münuyi; Perez yo Hezron pari mo Hezron yo Aram pariyi;
english: Judah became the father of Perez and Zerah by Tamar. Perez became the father of Hezron. Hezron became the father of Ram.

chokri: Aram yo Aminadab mo Aminadab yo Nasahon mo Nashon yo Salmon pariyi;
english: Ram became the father of Amminadab. Amminadab became the father of Nahshon. Nahshon became the father of Salmon.

chokri: Salmon-e Rakhab pü yono Boaz ngoyi, mo Boaz-e Ruth yono Obed münuyi, mo Obed yo Jese pariyi;
english: Salmon became the father of Boaz by Rahab. Boaz became the father of Obed by Ruth. Obed became the father of Jesse.

chokri: Mo Jesse yo David Küdeo pariyi, David-e thazü Uria cemipü yono Solomon münuyi.
english: Jesse became the father of David the king. David became the father of Solomon by her who had been the wife of Uriah.

chokri: Mo Solomon yo Rehoboam pari; Rehoboam yo Abijah mo Abijah yo Asa;
english: Solomon became the father of Rehoboam. Rehoboam became the father of Abijah. Abijah became the father of Asa.

chokri: Mo Asa yo Jehosafat; mo Jehosafat nu Joram, mo Joram, mo Joram nu Uzia.
english: Asa became the father of Jehoshaphat. Jehoshaphat became the father of Joram. Joram became the father of Uzziah.

chokri: Süsari Uzia yo Jothan pari, Jothan yo Ahaz, mo Ahaz yo Hezekia;
english: Uzziah became the father of Jotham. Jotham became the father of Ahaz. Ahaz became the father of Hezekiah.

chokri: Mo Hezekia yo Manasseh mo Manesseh nu Amon, süsa Amon nu Josia.
english: Hezekiah became the father of Manasseh. Manasseh became the father of Amon. Amon became the father of Josiah.

chokri: Süsa puko tüzüh die Babylon lü vota küto müsühce Josia nu Jekhonia mo sazüdzürako münuyi.
english: Josiah became the father of Jechoniah and his brothers, at the time of the exile to Babylon.

chokri: Mo puko tüzüh Babilon lü kütasa Jekhonia Salethel münuyi; mo Salethel yo Zerubabel pariyi.
english: After the exile to Babylon, Jechoniah became the father of Shealtiel. Shealtiel became the father of Zerubbabel.

chokri: Mo Zerubabel yo Abiud pari; mo Abiud yo Eliakim pariyi; mo Eliakim yo Azor pariyi.
english: Zerubbabel became the father of Abiud. Abiud became the father of Eliakim. Eliakim became the father of Azor.

chokri: Süze Azor yo Zadok yo Akhim mo Akhim yo Eliud pariyi;
english: Azor became the father of Sadoc. Sadoc became the father of Achim. Achim became the father of Eliud.

chokri: Mo Eliud yo Eliazar mo Eliazar yo Mathan mo Matthan yo James;
english: Eliud became the father of Eleazar. Eleazar became the father of Matthan. Matthan became the father of Jacob.

chokri: Mo James nu Joseph, Mary nopu, süpü yono Jisu Khrista li küti-o münuyi.
english: Jacob became the father of Joseph, the husband of Mary, from whom was born Jesus, who is called Christ.

chokri: Hihibi die Abraham yono vo David kütso nuye kürida; mo David yono votüzüh Babilon lü küvo kütso nuye kürida; süze puko tüzüh Babilon lü küvo lüno vori Khrista kütso ri nuye kürida.
english: So all the generations from Abraham to David are fourteen generations; from David to the exile to Babylon fourteen generations; and from the carrying away to Babylon to the Christ, fourteen generations.

chokri: Süze, Jisu Khrista kümünu hibi; Pu zu Mary po Joseph unyi küri küto küpo püh ma kütace; unyi küzüh lhü küta mhuzü Mary Kümüsa Rüpü lüno nulhüpü ba küta seyita.
english: Now the birth of Jesus Christ was like this; for after his mother, Mary, was engaged to Joseph, before they came together, she was found pregnant by the Holy Spirit.

chokri: Süsa pu nupu Joseph kümathomi püh zo tina, pu püh müthi mhüzü thüce thinyi mo die mükhrüh pu kha küva lübata.
english: Joseph, her husband, being a righteous man, and not willing to make her a public example, intended to put her away secretly.

chokri: Eli, pu zü süko müdo kübace, yhie, Nyep-o shoyimi püh pu mholü puce vori die pu yisü, Yhie, No David nu Joseph, no Mary yi püh n cemi thi müprüh tahite. Kükrenamori, pu nu lhüpü kübao sü Kümüsa Rüpü lüno zo kütina.
english: But when he thought about these things, behold, an angel of the Lord appeared to him in a dream, saying, "Joseph, son of David, don't be afraid to take to yourself Mary, your wife, for that which is conceived in her is of the Holy Spirit.

chokri: Pu thüpunumi püh münu süto, mo no Pu za thopüh Jisu zi die cito. Kükrenamori, Pupu thümako küphoma lüno puko küla sü kütona.
english: She shall bring forth a son. You shall call his name Jesus, for it is he who shall save his people from their sins."

chokri: Mha hiko thi küta, kükrenamori, Nyepoo sadzühkesemi talü podie;
english: Now all this has happened, that it might be fulfilled which was spoken by the Lord through the prophet, saying,

chokri: Yhie Rülimi phosühpü püh nulhü pü yito, sidie thüpunumi püh münu yito, mo puko Pu za thopüh Immanuel thito, mo Immanuel co Jihova uzüh bate; zi püh kümütao tsühyi kütona.
english: "Behold, the virgin shall be with child, And shall bring forth a son. They shall call his name Immanuel;" Which is, being interpreted, "God with us."

chokri: Süsa Joseph pu küzüh lüno co süri die Nyiepoo shoyimi puce po küsü bi die pu cemi zühyita.
english: Joseph arose from his sleep, and did as the angel of the Lord commanded him, and took his wife to himself;

chokri: Eli, süpü pu nu nura süo münuyi kümothe pupu cemi süpu züh thünothüpu thimo. Süsano nu süo za thopüh Jisu ciyi.
english: and didn't know her sexually until she had brought forth her firstborn son. He named him Jesus.

chokri: Süze Pu thümakro-o ngo südie ta cejüthu-o khota, mo Pu kho ba küyice Pu müthaküyiko Pu ce khori.
english: Seeing the multitudes, he went up onto the mountain. When he had sat down, his disciples came to him.

chokri: Sidie Pu Pu ta taga sü puko mütha küsü hibi.
english: He opened his mouth and taught them, saying,

chokri: Rüpü celaco kütsakhuko yhevetho, kükrenamoli tüyo küde-e süko sü zo tina.
english: "Blessed are the poor in spirit, For theirs is the Kingdom of Heaven.

chokri: Krayo kütiko yhevetho, kükrenamoli süko ulü luyi zoto tina.
english: Blessed are those who mourn, For they shall be comforted.

chokri: Ulü kümünüko yhevetho, kükrenamori süko cejüo püh uzü thi zoto tina.
english: Blessed are the gentle, For they shall inherit the earth.

chokri: Kümatho dzüh phu küthüri kümüri thiyo kütiko yhevetho, kükrenamoli süko u küchukühi ngozoyi to tina.
english: Blessed are those who hunger and thirst after righteousness, For they shall be filled.

chokri: Phikümüzhe küneko yhevetho, kükrenamoli süko phikümüzhe ngo zoyi to tina.
english: Blessed are the merciful, For they shall obtain mercy.

chokri: Thülü kümüsako yhevetho, kükrenamoli süko Jihova ngo zoto tina.
english: Blessed are the pure in heart, For they shall see God.

chokri: Vetho küthiko yhevetho, kükrenamoli süko yisü Jihova nune lie ci zoto tina.
english: Blessed are the peacemakers, For they shall be called children of God.

chokri: Kümatho dzühvena thilinyi kütako yhevetho, kükrenamoli tüyo küde süko zü zo tina.
english: Blessed are those who have been persecuted for righteousness' sake, For theirs is the Kingdom of Heaven.

chokri: A na mipo neko mushüh, müthimünyi mo kücezü lünyi po püh neko mütsuyo lize neko yhevetho;
english: "Blessed are you when people reproach you, persecute you, and say all kinds of evil against you falsely, for my sake.

chokri: Nenü mo nenü müzüriyite, kükrenamoli tüyo ne zhokhro ve shüh zoto tina, mi süsü bi thazü neko mhuzü sadzüküseko ri mushüh süri zo tina.
english: Rejoice, and be exceedingly glad, for great is your reward in heaven. For that is how they persecuted the prophets who were before you.

chokri: Neko-e cejüo tsa-o zo; eli mütsa zori pu külhü müdi va lize dipüh püh süo va mülhü layi veta? Süo pu küve pühri di ta die mipo kügü püh zürhüh küdü küva zota to.
english: "You are the salt of the earth, but if the salt has lost its flavor, with what will it be salted? It is then good for nothing, but to be cast out and trodden under the feet of men.

chokri: Neko cejü küveo zo, cejüthu yo küba rüna mi pu ngomo tayi lhozo;
english: You are the light of the world. A city located on a hill can't be hidden.

chokri: Mi me müto püh mürhakhro mori metobüh bayo dü mayo, sülize celü kübako mütü va nve bayo.
english: Neither do you light a lamp, and put it under a measuring basket, but on a stand; and it shines to all who are in the house.

chokri: Süsü bi mipo ne mhatho küve ngo die tüyo küba neko Po Jihova za thikümüleyi küto na ne küve po vekrü zoyite.
english: Even so, let your light shine before men; that they may see your good works, and glorify your Father who is in heaven.

chokri: I thüzhoo mo sadzühküseko zü thi müdi va tozu die vori küti neko lüta hite, I süko thi müdi va mori thi tsüh sü to küvori zo.
english: "Don't think that I came to destroy the law or the prophets. I didn't come to destroy, but to fulfill.

chokri: Süze I neko ce potü süto, Tü mo cejü dita kümothe mo mha hiko pühri thitsühyi kümothe, thüzhoo lü tü sü küve mori züco ne pühri mhapühceri di tayilho.
english: For most assuredly, I tell you, until heaven and earth pass away, not even one smallest letter or one tiny pen stroke shall in any way pass away from the law, until all things are accomplished.

chokri: Süna thüma kühopühri zü khaküsüko dolü künyithoo zori varhü die süh mi mütha yo lize tüyo küdelü pu yisü künyithoo thi tato. Eli, thüma kühopühri süko müdzi tso die süh mi müthayo lize tüyo küdelü pu yisü künyetho-o thito.
english: Whoever, therefore, shall break one of these least commandments, and teach others to do so, shall be called least in the Kingdom of Heaven; but whoever shall do and teach them shall be called great in the Kingdom of Heaven.

chokri: Süti-omoli, I neko ce po süto, neko kümatho po lüsiküseko mo Phariseeko kümatho yezüyi zo kümosa, neko mhapüh dori thi die tüyo küde lü lüküyi di zo.
english: For I tell you that unless your righteousness exceeds that of the scribes and Pharisees, there is no way you will enter into the Kingdom of Heaven.

chokri: Thazü popüh kümüta; No mi dokhri sü, mo kühopühri küdokhrimi sheti-olü pu müjo va küto kümüprühlü ba küta neko ri se bate.
english: "You have heard that it was said to the ancient ones, 'You shall not murder;' and 'Whoever shall murder shall be in danger of the judgment.'

chokri: Süri I nekoce potü süto, thüma kühopühri mhacomhala küdilü pu sazüh züra yo pu nümo, mori pulü rüdayo lize pue shetilü pu müjo küva kümüprühlü bate. Mo thüma kühopühri pu sazühzürami yisü ‘raca’ puco mhama pühri di süziyo lize pue mülhiko kühulü pu kümüprühbate; süri thüma kühopühri ‘No kümüloo’ zidie po lize pu Gehenna mezalü pu kümüprüh bate.
english: But I tell you, that everyone who is angry with his brother without a cause shall be in danger of the judgment; and whoever shall say to his brother, 'Raca!' shall be in danger of the council; and whoever shall say, 'You fool!' shall be in danger of the fire of Gehenna.

chokri: Süna no mhapüh kürüsucelü vo küzüce n sazühzürami n yo künyo küba nsa lüyi lize.
english: "If therefore you are offering your gift at the altar, and there remember that your brother has anything against you,

chokri: N mha khapüh kürüsüba-o mhuzülü ma die tala vo n sazühzüra süo züh vetho thiva müra die la vori n rüsu thi mosü.
english: leave your gift there before the altar, and go your way. First be reconciled to your brother, and then come and offer your gift.

chokri: No n yo kenyo küzüo zühdie sholü küta lü küzühve mhayite; molize n yo künyo küzüo n tüpüh sheti küzhoo belü sü, mo küzho süo n tüpüh pu mhu küzhoo belü sü die küzho süo po n tüpüh küdücelü süva ri se lhomo.
english: Agree with your adversary quickly, while you are with him in the way; lest perhaps the prosecutor deliver you to the judge, and the judge deliver you to the officer, and you be cast into prison.

chokri: I nce potü süto, no vo die presa daprane pühri shu tsova kümothe no sülüno pariyi lho ta zoto.
english: Most assuredly I tell you, you shall by no means get out of there, until you have paid the last penny.

chokri: No rügü rüzü sü süzidie popüh küma neko seyi ta zoe;
english: "You have heard that it was said,  'You shall not commit adultery;'

chokri: Süri I neko ce po süto, thüma kühopühri thünomi püh müho die süpü peyo lize pu lüno süpü züh mhaküsü thiva zo.
english: but I tell you that everyone who gazes at a woman to lust after her has committed adultery with her already in his heart.

chokri: Süsübi die mhaceno nmhü nzaco nthi müsü tayo lize n moyono süo khi kügü vate; kükrenamoli, nmo mütü kügü püh mezalü sü küva ceri nmoyo zatho püh müdi küva veku.
english: If your right eye causes you to stumble, pluck it out and throw it away from you. For it is more profitable for you that one of your members should perish, than for your whole body to be cast into Gehenna.

chokri: Mhaceno, nbe nzaco nthi müsü tayo lize, n yono süo da kügü vate; kükrenamoli nmo mütü püh mezalü kügü küva ceri nmoyo zatho püh müdi küva veku.
english: If your right hand causes you to stumble, cut it off, and throw it away from you: for it is profitable for you that one of your members should perish, and not your whole body be thrown into Gehenna.

chokri: Süze thüma kühopühri pu cemi khava to lize pukha küva lüsi püh pu prüte, zi küti po püh müzü.
english: "It was also said, 'Whoever shall put away his wife, let him give her a writing of divorce,'

chokri: Süri, I nekoce po süto, thüma kühopühri kürügükürüzü na zo kümo sa pu cemi khava lize pu püh kürügükürüzülü süva zo; mo kühopühri pu khaküva pü yi lize pu ri kürüzü thi tazo.
english: but I tell you that whoever puts away his wife, except for the cause of sexual immorality, makes her an adulteress; and whoever marries her when she is put away commits adultery.

chokri: Süsari, no kücezü po rushühta mori; n Nyepo-oce n zü khaküsüko thitsühyite; zidie thazü küramiko celü popüh kümüta nekori seyita zo.
english: "Again you have heard that it was said to them of old time, 'You shall not make false vows, but shall perform to the Lord your vows,'

chokri: Eli, I nekoce po süto, mhapühri po rushüh hi; türi po rüshüh hi; süosü Jihova küdeba zo kütina:
english: but I tell you, don't swear at all: neither by heaven, for it is the throne of God;

chokri: Mori, cejüri po rushüh hi; kükrenamoli, süosü Jihova phekhra-o zo tina, mori Jerusalem ripo rushüh hi; süosü Küde küzhoo nade zo tina.
english: nor by the earth, for it is the footstool of his feet; nor by Jerusalem, for it is the city of the great King.

chokri: Mori, no npi ripo rushüh hi; kükrenamoli no thüpitha pri pühri thi mütü, mori thi müga ri yilho tina.
english: Neither shall you swear by your head, for you can't make one hair white or black.

chokri: Süri n zü ‘I’ zilize ‘I’ ziyi mo ‘MO’ zilize ‘MO’ ziyi; modie sü zü ta lize süko küsüo celü pari tazo.
english: But let your 'Yes' be 'Yes' and your 'No' be 'no.' Whatever is more than these is of the evil one.

chokri: Mhü püh khü mhü püh, hu püh khü hu püh, zi püh kümüta neko ri se bata zo.
english: "You have heard that it was said, 'An eye for an eye, and a tooth for a tooth.'

chokri: Eli, I nekoce po süto, thüma küsümi müsüh thihi; süzi kütao mori thüma kühopühri n bro n zaco vü lize, kükreco ri kürüh püh prüte.
english: But I tell you, don't resist him who is evil; but whoever strikes you on your right cheek, turn to him the other also.

chokri: Mo, thüma kühopühri n bola khiyi ta nyi die thüzhoolü künyo sü ta lize n khü ri rhüpüh prü vate.
english: If anyone sues you to take away your coat, let him have your cloak also.

chokri: Süze thüma kühopühri n kümu thi die n züh maldo püh vo to ta lize, pu züh maldo küna ri vovate.
english: Whoever compels you to go one mile, go with him two.

chokri: Mha khapüh nce shoyo kütimi prüte, mu nce lü thüpo mosü küvori-mi po müthu tala ta hite.
english: Give to him who asks you, and don't turn away him who desires to borrow from you.

chokri: No n sühzanumi khrüyi mo n ngomumi phi mütsüyite; süzi püh küma neko seyi tazo;
english: "You have heard that it was said, 'You shall love your neighbor, and hate your enemy.'

chokri: Eli, I nekoce po süto, ne ngomumi khrüyi, mo galiphi püh ne rüphi kütimi prüyi, ne phimu yo kütimi na mha küve thiyi, n phimu die n müthimünyiyo kütimi na shoyite.
english: But I tell you, love your enemies, bless those who curse you, do good to those who hate you, and pray for those who mistreat you and persecute you,

chokri: Sülize, nekoe tüyo küba neko Poo nuneko thiyi küto na; Kükrenamoli Pün ace mütho püh küsümi rimo küvemi ri vasüyo, mo tü mürü püh küzüthomi ri kümomi ri prü süyo tina.
english: that you may be children of your Father who is in heaven. For he makes his sun to rise on the evil and the good, and sends rain on the just and the unjust.

chokri: Süzomoli, neko mi neko khrüyo kütiko libi khrüyo lize; neko dipüh zhokhro ba? Khazhonu kükhrümi ri süziyo zo mome?
english: For if you love those who love you, what reward do you have? Don't even the tax collectors do the same?

chokri: Mo ne ne sazühzürako libi küluyo lize; ne dipüh thi rükri kuyi ta. Khazhanu kükhrümi ri süziyo zo mo me?
english: If you only greet your friends, what more do you do than others? Don't even the tax collectors do the same?

chokri: Süna, tüyo küba neko Po vezhosü küti bi die neko ri vezhosüyite.
english: Therefore you shall be perfect, just as your Father in heaven is perfect.

chokri: Neko mi müjo hi; sülize miri ne müjo talho;
english: "Don't judge, so that you won't be judged.

chokri: Kükrenamoli, ne-e süh mi müjo küsüo miri süo süh ne müjo zoto tina.
english: For with whatever judgment you judge, you will be judged; and with whatever measure you measure, it will be measured to you.

chokri: Sülize, no dipüh bi die ntho n mhülü sühthü kübao lü mozo die n sazüh-o mhülü sühco kübao pe yo la?
english: Why do you see the speck that is in your brother's eye, but don't consider the beam that is in your own eye?

chokri: Mori, no dipüh bi die ntho n mhülü sühthü kübao lü mozo die n sazüh-o yisü, apo n mhülü sühco-o ka vate, sidie poyo la?
english: Or how will you tell your brother, 'Let me remove the speck from your eye;' and behold, the beam is in your own eye?

chokri: No küthüra küti-o, ntho n mhülü sühthü-o khiva mürate, sülize no nsazüh-o mhülü sühco-o ngo müle mülu die khi vato.
english: You hypocrite! First remove the beam out of your own eye, and then you can see clearly to remove the speck out of your brother's eye.

chokri: Mhakümüsa khapüh tüshi prü hite; mori ti püma kürühpü kügü püh thüvo mhuzülü sü hite, süko u phe püh süko küdu mo rürüh pra ukü püh ne rhe ri zoto küti na.
english: "Don't give that which is holy to the dogs, neither throw your pearls before the pigs, lest perhaps they trample them under their feet, and turn and tear you to pieces.

chokri: Shote, süze khapüh ne prüto, phute, süze ne ngoyito; cekha küdü züzo te, süze khrisü ne prüto.
english: "Ask, and it will be given you. Seek, and you will find. Knock, and it will be opened for you.

chokri: Süze thüma kühopühri shoyo kütimi ngoyi yo, mo phuyo kütimi ngoyi yo; cekha küdüyo kütimi ri mi khrisü sümi prüyo.
english: For everyone who asks receives. He who seeks finds. To him who knocks it will be opened.

chokri: Süze, ne dolü sopühno unu-e tsükhrüda sho lize kütsü püh prüyo la?
english: Or who is there among you, who, if his son asks him for bread, will give him a stone?

chokri: Mori, pu khu sho lize tünyhi khi püh prüyo la?
english: Or if he asks for a fish, who will give him a serpent?

chokri: Ne küsümi thori mhaküve püh nenu nene küprü do seyo lize tüyo küba neko Po-e mhaküve dithüdüpüh püh mi Puce shoyo kütiko prü süta?
english: If you then, being evil, know how to give good gifts to your children, how much more will your Father who is in heaven give good things to those who ask him!

chokri: Süna mi neyo mha dipüh thisü lize nenü sheli neri süsü bi die thipüh miyo süte, kükrenamoli, thüzhoo mo sadzühküseko zübo hihi zo tina.
english: Therefore whatever you desire for men to do to you, you shall also do to them; for this is the law and the prophets.

chokri: Neko kharü kümürüho züh lüyi te, kükrenamoli, mi züh kükalü küvo khrü-o müyo mo sho süo müthi, mo thüma kükro sho süo züh lü tayo.
english: "Enter in by the narrow gate; for wide is the gate and broad is the way that leads to destruction, and many are those who enter in by it.

chokri: Süri, mizüh kürhüh lü küvo kharüo mürüh mo pu shori kürühbüh, mo süo ngo küyi thüma tsa.
english: How narrow is the gate, and restricted is the way that leads to life! Few are those who find it.

chokri: Kücezü sadzühküseko yo neko münuyi te, süko tüko khüne süh die neko ce vori yo; eli puko lü lükhrono tüküti saceko zo.
english: "Beware of false prophets, who come to you in sheep's clothing, but inwardly are ravening wolves.

chokri: Neko puko se yono puko se yito; mi tsahubo yono khubüh se rayi yo me? Mori hushihu bo yono shedüse rayi yo me?
english: By their fruits you will know them. Do you gather grapes from thorns, or figs from thistles?

chokri: Sühbo küve kühopühri use küve jiyiyo; eli sühbo küsü khü use küsü ji tayo.
english: Even so, every good tree produces good fruit; but the corrupt tree produces evil fruit.

chokri: Sühbo küve püh-e use küsü ji ta ndi, mori sühbo küsü püh-e use küve ji ta yomo.
english: A good tree can't produce evil fruit, neither can a corrupt tree produce good fruit.

chokri: Sühbo kühopühri use küve jiyi kümo khü ve va die püh melü kügü vayo.
english: Every tree that doesn't grow good fruit is cut down, and thrown into the fire.

chokri: Küthozo, neko use yono puko seyi to.
english: Therefore, by their fruits you will know them.

chokri: Thüma A yisü, Nyepoo, Nyepoo, ziyoti mütü ri tüyo küdebalü lütso tayi lho; eli, tüyo küba Apoo müdoko thiyo küti-o zoto.
english: Not everyone who says to me, 'Lord, Lord,' will enter into the Kingdom of Heaven; but he who does the will of my Father who is in heaven.

chokri: Sünhasü thüma kükro A yisü, Nyepoo, Nyepoo ha nza süh sadzühküse po mo me? Ha N za süh thürüküsü hu mome? Mo ha N zalü no mhatho kuku küzho kükro ri thi sü mome? süzi to.
english: Many will tell me in that day, 'Lord, Lord, didn't we prophesy in your name, in your name cast out demons, and in your name do many mighty works?'

chokri: Eli, I puce po die, I neko se mo ho, neko mha küsü küthiko neko ta a thürütate zita to.
english: Then I will tell them, 'I never knew you. Depart from me, you who work iniquity.'

chokri: Süna thüma kühopüri azü hiko rünyiyi die thi mo züh küyimi püh udo küveo kütsü tsüroyo pu ce thi küyi züh kümüde süto.
english: "Everyone therefore who hears these words of mine, and does them, I will liken him to a wise man, who built his house on a rock.

chokri: Sücesü türü shüh vori die züde süri, mo tükhra khrade vori ce süo hü, süri ce süo rütu prü mota, kükrenamoli, ce süo kütsü tsüro yono pu me shühyi kütana.
english: The rain came down, the floods came, and the winds blew, and beat on that house; and it didn't fall, for it was founded on the rock.

chokri: Eli thüma kühopühri azü hiko rünyiyi yo süri thi mo zühyi yomo lize süo püh thüma udo di die thusünyolü pu ce thi küyi züh kümüde süto.
english: Everyone who hears these words of mine, and doesn't do them will be like a foolish man, who built his house on the sand.

chokri: Sücesü türü sü mo zü de süri, mo tükhra vori die ce süo hü, sücesü ce süo rütu müsü shühta.
english: The rain came down, the floods came, and the winds blew, and beat on that house; and it fell--and great was its fall."

chokri: Süsa Jisu zü hiko pothü küsüsa, thümako Pu kümütha zü na puko malhü thita.
english: It happened, when Jesus had finished saying these things, that the multitudes were astonished at his teaching,

chokri: Kükrenamoli, Pu lüsiküseko bi mo die Pu kuku ne shüh die puko mütha küta na.
english: for he taught them with authority, and not like the scribes.

chokri: Süsa Jisu müthaküyiko mo thüma krooce mha po die:
english: Then Jesus spoke to the multitudes and to his disciples,

chokri: Lüsiküseko mo Phariseekoe Moses balü bayo.
english: saying, "The scribes and the Pharisees sat on Moses' seat.

chokri: Süna pükoe dipüh püh nekoce po sheli süko thi mo zühyite; süri puko bi tahi; Kükrenamoli puko mha poyo eli thi mo zühyi mo tina.
english: All things therefore whatever they tell you to observe, observe and do, but don't do their works; for they say, and don't do.

chokri: Pukoe, thüpüh kümüsiko da püh mi bokayo sü die mipo püyo, eli puko tho zoe ubecine ri püh süko nyi külüri süyo mo.
english: For they bind heavy burdens that are grievous to be borne, and lay them on men's shoulders; but they themselves will not lift a finger to help them.

chokri: Süri puko mhatho küthi mütü ri mipo ngo küto na thiyo küti zo; süzomoli puko kütholüsi züboko tho mülemülu püh u süh thi pu tayo, mo puko khü mhüko thi kümünye pu tayo.
english: But all their works they do to be seen by men. They make their phylacteries broad, enlarge the fringes of their garments,

chokri: Sidie puko tülhüko lüri thürho ba kürako phu die bayo; süze synagogko lüri thüba kürükrü mo thürho bako phu die ba küti püko nüyo.
english: and love the place of honor at feasts, the best seats in the synagogues,

chokri: Mo Ihethothikolü ri mipo puko külü, mo mipo puko yisü, Rabi, Rabi, zi die puko ci küti püko nüyo.
english: the salutations in the marketplaces, and to be called 'Rabbi, Rabbi' by men.

chokri: Eli, neko zoe mipo neko püh Rabi puco kümüthao zi die cihi, kükrenamoli Khrista libi neko kümüthao zo, mo neko mütüko sazühzüra zo.
english: But don't you be called 'Rabbi,' for one is your teacher, the Christ, and all of you are brothers.

chokri: Süze cejü hilü mipühri püh neko po lie cihi. Kükrenamoli tüyo küba neko poe püh libi zo.
english: Call no man on the earth your father, for one is your Father, he who is in heaven.

chokri: Sülibizomoli, mipo neko yisü krütako lie cihite, Kükrenamoli Khrista libi neko krütao zo tina.
english: Neither be called masters, for one is your master, the Christ.

chokri: Süri neko dolü küzho thoo sü neko cetao thitato.
english: But he who is greatest among you will be your servant.

chokri: Kükrenamoli, thüma kühopühri pütho pu lü kürükrüyo kütio thi kürünu vato, eli pütho püthi kürünu yo kütio thi kürükrü vato.
english: Whoever exalts himself will be humbled, and whoever humbles himself will be exalted.

chokri: Süri küthüraküti lüsiküseko mo Phariseeko, nekoe thiyite! Kükrenamoli nekoe mipo tüyo küdelü lüyi küto khayo tina, nekoe neko tho ri lü mu mo kükremi po ri lü nyiyo mo tina.
english: "Woe to you, scribes and Pharisees, hypocrites! For you devour widows' houses, and as a pretense you make long prayers. Therefore you will receive greater condemnation.

chokri: Nekoe Tsee thiyite, Neko küthüraküti lüsiküseko mo Phariseeko! Kükrenamoli, nekoe samepüko ce müzo tiyi tayo, mo küthüra die küsho thi müshoyo, süna nekoe miceri kümüjo rüh ku küti ngoto.
english: "But woe to you, scribes and Pharisees, hypocrites! Because you shut up the Kingdom of Heaven against men; for you don't enter in yourselves, neither do you allow those who are entering in to enter.

chokri: Nekoe tsee thiyite neko lüsiküseko mo Phariseeko, neko küthüra kütiko, kükrenamoli, nekoe mipo Jewmi zho zühyi küto na dzüküzho mo cejü mütü whi tayo; mo mipüh po süzi küyice nekoe pu thipüh neko dü küna mezao nune thi vayo.
english: Woe to you, scribes and Pharisees, hypocrites! For you travel around by sea and land to make one proselyte; and when he becomes one, you make him twice as much of a son of Gehenna as yourselves.

chokri: Nekoe thiyite! Neko mhüce shothako, Nekoe po die; Kühopühri rüsuce po püh rüshu küti zoe mhapühri zo mo, eli kühopühri kürüsucelü sunako po püh rüshü lize pue mi po küpümi tazo küti na, süzi yo,
english: "Woe to you, you blind guides, who say, 'Whoever swears by the temple, it is nothing; but whoever swears by the gold of the temple, he is obligated.'

chokri: Kümülo mo mhüceko. Ka sunako me mo die süko thi kümüsayi küta kürüsuceo zhoku ta?
english: You blind fools! For which is greater, the gold, or the temple that sanctifies the gold?

chokri: Süze kühopühri rüsubao po rüshu lize, süsü mhapüh ri zomo, süri kühopühri süyo küba mhanyoko kha püh rüshu thi küvako po rüshü lize pu jo ta zo, ziyo.
english: 'Whoever swears by the altar, it is nothing; but whoever swears by the gift that is on it, he is obligated.'

chokri: Neko kümülo mo mhüceko. Ka mha khapüh rüsu thi küsüko me süko thi kümüsa küsü rüsubao zhoku ta?
english: You blind fools! For which is greater, the gift, or the altar that sanctifies the gift?

chokri: Thüma kühopühri kürüsuba süh rüshu lize pu süo mo sülü küba mütü süh rüshu vazo.
english: He therefore who swears by the altar, swears by it, and by everything on it.

chokri: Mo thüma kühopühri kürüsuce po die rüshu lize, süo mo sülü küba mütü po die rüshu ta zo.
english: He who swears by the temple, swears by it, and by him who was living in it.

chokri: Tü po rüshu lize pu Jihova küdeba mo süo lü küba mütü sa po die rüshü tazo.
english: He who swears by heaven, swears by the throne of God, and by him who sits on it.

chokri: Neko küthüraküti lüsiküseko mo Phariseeko, nekoe thiyite! Nekoe nyeprü, dunia mo shitsüh za kürizapüh khasüyo eli nekoe thüzholü Jüko ceri mosüku küti kümüzi sheco, mi phikümüzhe, mo kümülü hihibi kütiko kha vayo; hiko ri thi zü tüzo, süri kükreko thimo ta zü mozo.
english: "Woe to you, scribes and Pharisees, hypocrites! For you tithe mint, dill, and cumin, and have left undone the weightier matters of the law: justice, mercy, and faith. But you ought to have done these, and not to have left the other undone.

chokri: Ne mhüce die shotha yo kütiko; Nekoe velhanekoe donya vayo, eli utko müzoyi tayo.
english: You blind guides, who strain out a gnat, and swallow a camel!

chokri: Neko küthüraküti Lüsiküseko mo Phariseeko, nekoe tsee thiyite! Kükrenamoli, nekoe tühe mo mükhu shozüco thi kümüsa vayo, eli ulükue mi mha mükhü küti mo küzüküye küti po chu pu bayo.
english: "Woe to you, scribes and Pharisees, hypocrites! For you clean the outside of the cup and of the platter, but within they are full of extortion and unrighteousness.

chokri: N mhüküce Phariseeko, tühe mo mükhu lüku thi kümüsayi mürate, sülize shozüco ri müsa tato.
english: You blind Pharisee, first clean the inside of the cup and of the platter, that the outside of it may become clean also.

chokri: Neko küthüraküti lüsiküse mo Phariseeko, Nekoe thiyite! Kükrenamoli, nekoe mhakhre mhuzu nyhi kümüga küma bizo, eli ulükue küsami roko mo müsakümo po chu pu küba bizo.
english: "Woe to you, scribes and Pharisees, hypocrites! For you are like whitened tombs, which outwardly appear beautiful, but inwardly are full of dead men's bones, and of all uncleanness.

chokri: Neko shozu thüma küngoce zoe pe kümatho zo, eli neko lüe küthüra küti mo thüzho kümo lünyiko po chu pü bayo.
english: Even so you also outwardly appear righteous to men, but inwardly you are full of hypocrisy and iniquity.

chokri: Neko küthüraküti lüsiküseko mo Phariseeko, nekoe thiyite! Kükrenamoli nekoe sadzühküseko mhakhre thi süyo mo kümathoko mhakhre tho küzüve süyo.
english: "Woe to you, scribes and Pharisees, hypocrites! For you build the tombs of the prophets, and decorate the tombs of the righteous,

chokri: Sidie nekoe sü, mhaceno ue uko po neko tüce Ihü küti püh thi lize ukoe sadzühküsemiko za püh uyo süpü tata mo to mo rü; süziyo.
english: and say, 'If we had lived in the days of our fathers, we wouldn't have been partakers with them in the blood of the prophets.'

chokri: Süzi die nekoe neko tho neko yisü sadzühküsemi küdokhrimi nuneko thi die poyo.
english: Therefore you testify to yourselves that you are children of those who killed the prophets.

chokri: Süze neko ri neko poneko bi die thiyite.
english: Fill up, then, the measure of your fathers.

chokri: Neko tünyhiko, neko nyhibvü shühyeko, neko dibi die Gehenna mezalü ne müjo kütoo thürayi ta?
english: You serpents, you offspring of vipers, how will you escape the judgment of Gehenna?

chokri: Süna Yhie! I sadzühküsemiko, mhaküsemiko mo lüsiküseko kütsüh püh nekoce süto; sidie neko süko humi dokhrimo tüpüh guto, mo neko synagogko lüno humi tüpüh vüto, sidie nade lilü puko müthi münyi to.
english: Therefore, behold, I send to you prophets, wise men, and scribes. Some of them you will kill and crucify; and some of them you will scourge in your synagogues, and persecute from city to city;

chokri: Sidie Abel kümathoo za kümükrüh lüno vo rüsuce mo rüsuba nyi dolüno neko pu dokhri küvao Baraka nuo Zakaria za kütso cejü hilü kümatho mütü za po neko piyo vorito.
english: that on you may come all the righteous blood shed on the earth, from the blood of righteous Abel to the blood of Zachariah son of Barachiah, whom you killed between the sanctuary and the altar.

chokri: I nekoce potü süto, mha süko mütü tsüdo thüma hiko yo vorito, zita.
english: Most assuredly I tell you, all these things will come upon this generation.

chokri: Yhie! Jerusalem, Jerusalem, sadzühküseko dokhri küti mo mi kütsüh püh nce küsüko kütsü püh süko va kütio, vükrüe pu nuneko kükü küngu püh pu si khro süyiyo küti bi I va ditsu dithüdü püh n nuneko ci künguyi nyi süa? Eli, neko tho mute!
english: "Jerusalem, Jerusalem, who kills the prophets, and stones those who are sent to her! How often would I have gathered your children together, even as a hen gathers her chickens under her wings, and you would not!

chokri: Yhie! Neko ce va kümüthu püh n prü va die pe sa mo tato.
english: Behold, your house is left to you desolate.

chokri: Süzomoli, I nekoce po süto, Nekoe Nyepoo za süh vori küzüo yhevetho zi die po kümothe neko a ngo sa lhote; zita.
english: For I tell you, you will not see me from now on, until you say, 'Blessed is he who comes in the name of the Lord!'"

chokri: Süsa Pu dzüde shice no mi mütha la; mu makro künye püh vori Puce künguyi küta la Pü püzuo lülü die ku dzülü bayita, sidie makroo mütü dzüde shice cejü küsolü bata.
english: Again he began to teach by the seaside. A great multitude was gathered to him, so that he entered into a boat in the sea, and sat down. All the multitude were on the land by the sea.

chokri: Süze Pu züvelü no mha kükro püh mütha sü, sidie Pue Pu kümütha lüno sü.
english: He taught them many things in parables, and told them in his teaching,

chokri: Rünyi süte; Yhie tsa küvami püh-e tsava toü prü, rü pu tsava küsüce hu,
english: "Listen! Behold, the farmer went out to sow,

chokri: rütu shoshi prüta mu tüyo rako vori die süko müdoyi mu kükreko rütu kütsü,
english: and it happened, as he sowed, some seed fell by the road, and the birds came and devoured it.

chokri: Tsüro cejü zü si ba kümolü prüta, süze züsi kümola süko tsü mha prari ta,
english: Others fell on the rocky ground, where it had little soil, and immediately it sprang up, because it had no depth of soil.

chokri: Mu nace prari die phrü küsüce; süo ume ba kümo la ceta.
english: When the sun had risen, it was scorched; and because it had no root, it withered away.

chokri: Mu kükre hu rütu sü tsahu dolü prüta sidie tsahuko ma süri süko güyi die süko po use kürüthiyi mo ta.
english: Others fell among the thorns, and the thorns grew up, and choked it, and it yielded no fruit.

chokri: Sülize hu rütu sü cejü zü küve lü prüyi die use ji küthi mamayi, püh se sühmüri, püh se hieshorüh, mu püh se krapüh jiyi zi.
english: Others fell into the good ground, and yielded fruit, growing up and increasing. Some brought forth thirty times, some sixty times, and some one hundred times as much."

chokri: Süze Pu süko yisü thüma kühopühri unyi ne die rünyiyi kütomi po rünyiyi te, zita.
english: He said, "Whoever has ears to hear, let him hear."

chokri: Süsa Pu pu libi kübace Pu hupü kübako kürikünako züh sa die züveo co kütsota.
english: When he was alone, those who were around him with the twelve asked him about the parables.

chokri: Mu Pu süko yisü, Neko po Jihova küde zü kümüsüh seyi no die khasü ve, süri shozuco kübako la zoe züve lüno mha mütü thisü ze;
english: He said to them, "To you is given the mystery of the Kingdom of God, but to those who are outside, all things are done in parables,

chokri: Süna pukoe kürünyie rünyiyi ve eli seyi lho mu pukoe küngoe ngoyi ve eli seyi lho, mu kürünyie rünyiyi ve, süri lükükreyi lho, ukoe puko küdeyi die puko küphomako vayi ta mo kütola.
english: that 'seeing they may see, and not perceive; and hearing they may hear, and not understand; lest perhaps they should turn again, and their sins should be forgiven them.'"

chokri: Sidie Pu süko yisü, neko züve hio semo me? Sülize neko dibi die züve mütü seta?
english: He said to them, "Don't you understand this parable? How will you understand all of the parables?

chokri: Tsa küvao sü züo püh va süyo.
english: The farmer sows the word.

chokri: Mo hikohie shoshice tsao rütulü kütako zo, süri puko rünyi küyice, Satan ta mha vori die tsa puko lü prüh küsüo khipüh tata yo.
english: These are the ones by the road, where the word is sown; and when they have heard, immediately Satan comes, and takes away the word which has been sown in them.

chokri: Süsü bidie tsa rütu kütsü tsürolü prü kütakoe; züo seyi mu uko nüri die züh mhayi yo;
english: These in like manner are those who are sown on the rocky places, who, when they have heard the word, immediately receive it with joy.

chokri: Süri puko ume ba kümo la dopüh libi güyi yo, sidie künuco züoe kümüzhe küti mu kümüthi kümünyi süri küyice; pukoe unümo mhatayo.
english: They have no root in themselves, but are short-lived. When oppression or persecution arises because of the word, immediately they stumble.

chokri: Sülize tsa rütu tsahu dolü prükütako sü hikohi pukoe zü rünyi yi yo.
english: Others are those who are sown among the thorns. These are those who have heard the word,

chokri: Süri cejü hio lünyiko, mu zütho kümo ceneko, mu umünyi kükrekre kütiko ulü lüri küyi ce züo tü müdi vayo, sidie use jiyi mota yo.
english: and the cares of this age, and the deceitfulness of riches, and the lusts of other things entering in choke the word, and it becomes unfruitful.

chokri: Sülize tsa püh cejü küvelü so küsüko sü hikohi; pukoe züo rünyi die yi püh uzü thiyiyo, sidie use jiyi yo, hu se sümüri, hu se heshorüh, mu hu se krapüh.
english: These are those which were sown on the good ground: such as hear the word, and accept it, and bear fruit, some thirty times, some sixty times, and some one hundred times."

chokri: Süzomoli, Pu puko yisü, mi metobüh khapüh mürha khro mori thüzi khro sü ma tayo me? Sidie süo püh ubayo süma yo mo ma?
english: He said to them, "Is the lamp brought to be put under a basket or under a bed? Isn't it put on a lampstand?

chokri: Kükrelamori, mhapüri sühpra mi müsemüngo sü lho li die küva püh küma di, mori, mhapühri süh pra küvelü sü mo to die whüpü küma dizo.
english: For there is nothing hidden, except that it should be made known; neither was anything made secret, but that it should come to light.

chokri: Unyi ne die rünyiyi kütomi po rünyiyite.
english: If any man has ears to hear, let him hear."

chokri: Sidie Pu puko yisü, neko mha kürünyilü münuyi te.
english: He said to them, "Take heed what you hear. With whatever measure you measure, it will be measured to you, and more will be given to you who hear.

chokri: No didüpüh müde püh mi prü sü shili mi südü müde püh n prüto, mu khapüneko rünyiyo kütiko prü sa süto. Mha kübami kha prü sa süto; mu mha ba kümomi pu kübao ri khi yi tato.
english: For whoever has, to him will more be given, and he who doesn't have, from him will be taken away even that which he has."

chokri: Sidie Pu podie, Poo küdee mipüe tsa prüh püh cejü sü die vo züh tayo,
english: He said, "The Kingdom of God is as if a man should cast seed on the earth,

chokri: Mu tüso süri lize co süri zi bayo, eli pu tsakoe dibi die tsü süri müma süri bayo sheli se bayo kümo bizo.
english: and should sleep and rise night and day, and the seed should spring up and grow, he doesn't know how.

chokri: Cejüo tho puse rüthi süyo, thüralü punyi, süsa pu krüh, sidie masü pukrüh chu süriyo tina.
english: For the earth bears fruit: first the blade, then the ear, then the full grain in the ear.

chokri: Sidie puseko ji pariyi ta mütha; süo kürüh tü kütasa mi zeküne khasü tayo.
english: But when the fruit is ripe, immediately he puts forth the sickle, because the harvest has come."

chokri: Mu Pu podie sü; dipüh Poo küde müde süta, mori dipüh züve püh süo züh kümüde ta?
english: He said, "How will we liken the Kingdom of God? Or with what parable will we illustrate it?

chokri: Süo sü gakresi püh bizo, mi süo püh cejü prüh küsüce; süosü tsa dolü künyithoo zo.
english: It's like a grain of mustard seed, which, when it is sown in the earth, though it is less than all the seeds that are on the earth,

chokri: Süo prüh küsüce tsü süri die gako dolü künye püh thi tayo, mu pücoko ma süri die tüyo rakori po vori die pu khrikhro rüsühyi yo.
english: yet when it is sown, grows up, and becomes greater than all the herbs, and puts out great branches, so that the birds of the sky can lodge under its shadow."

chokri: Mu puko rünyiyi ve küba bidie, Pu süsü biti züve kükro süh die züpo püh puko ce sü.
english: With many such parables he spoke the word to them, as they were able to hear it.

chokri: Süze züve sa mo die Pu mhapühri popüh puko ce sümo; Pue mi küdiceno mha mütü potsüh püh müthaküyiko kasü.
english: Without a parable he didn't speak to them; but privately to his own disciples he explained all things.

chokri: Süze thüva vori kütace, Pu süko yisü, Uko ta lishico kuta khe, zisü.
english: On that day, when evening had come, he said to them, "Let's go over to the other side."

chokri: Sidie makroo kütsüh va die, pue ba küba dpüzuo mu Pu züh küba püzu künyikori sa Pu züh ta.
english: Leaving the multitude, they took him with them, even as he was, in the boat. Other small boats were also with him.

chokri: Sücesü tükhra khrade püh vori die dpüzuo hüta, sidie dzü rüphü lüri süo chuyi ta züta.
english: There arose a great wind storm, and the waves beat into the boat, so much that the boat was already filled.

chokri: Süri Pue püzu meco pikhüpüh yono zühzü küta la pukoe Pu küsü die sü, Kümüthao, uko kata to küzühi nlü nyi mozo me? Zita.
english: He himself was in the stern, asleep on the cushion, and they woke him up, and told him, "Teacher, don't you care that we are dying?"

chokri: Süsa pu co süri die tükhra küthi diesü, dzüde yisü, no kümükre tate, zisü. Süsa tükhra lata, sidie rütüzüh ta.
english: He awoke, and rebuked the wind, and said to the sea, "Peace! Be still!" The wind ceased, and there was a great calm.

chokri: Süsa pu süko yihsü, neko dipüh la müprüh zo tala? Dibi die ne kümülü ta mo zoala? Zita.
english: He said to them, "Why are you so afraid? How is it that you have no faith?"

chokri: Süsa puko müprüh shuta die sü huhu yisü; Hiohi dibi ti mapüh ta? Tükhra mu dzüde thori Pu khü züh küta hi? Zita.
english: They were greatly afraid, and said to one another, "Who then is this, that even the wind and the sea obey him?"

chokri: Jisu kechothi keba tüpülü thethi pülü kechothi la ketace, mipü püsameji tade püce poti, Niepoo John püsajiko metha kesübi no sheri kecho kethizho thapü hako meka süte.
english: It happened, that when he finished praying in a certain place, one of his disciples said to him, "Lord, teach us to pray, just as John also taught his disciples."

chokri: Pü ukoce poti, neko kecho kethice hizi thite, hako po tüyokebao, nza kemesao thiyite, Nkede thivorite; Nmedo thiyite cejülü hiri pü tüyo bi süte,
english: He said to them, "When you pray, say, 'Our Father in heaven, May your name be kept holy. May your Kingdom come. May your will be done on Earth, as it is in heaven.

chokri: Thathava (tüsonha) kha pü hatsü süte.
english: Give us day by day our daily bread.

chokri: Hako kephoma va hatsü süte, hari mi hakoyo kekri ketako vapü ketsübi. Hazü medokephilü lühite, siziti mole keshülü hakela yite.
english: Forgive us our sins, For we ourselves also forgive everyone who is indebted to us. Bring us not into temptation, But deliver us from the evil one.'"

chokri: Pü ukopoi, neko humi ukhrethomi ne de mesotülu ta pücevo, de püce poti, Azü-o, tsükrida sü kha atsü süte.
english: He said to them, "Which of you, if you go to a friend at midnight, and tell him, 'Friend, lend me three loaves of bread,

chokri: Kekrelamori azümipü revü ace voritele, mu I mhapüri khapütsüti bamote.
english: for a friend of mine has come to me from a journey, and I have nothing to set before him,'

chokri: Pü celü bade lüti, akebü hite, cekha khave, anuneko sheri azü thezilü züte, Itasü mhapü ntsüsü hote.
english: and he from within will answer and say, 'Don't bother me. The door is now shut, and my children are with me in bed. I can't get up and give it to you'?

chokri: I nekoce posüto, pütho tasü mhakha pütsü mozosheri, süo püzümi tila pütü kemoce zosheri, pü tasü de mha pü sümokesudü khapü tsüto.
english: I tell you, although he will not rise and give it to him because he is his friend, yet because of his persistence, he will get up and give him as many as he needs.

chokri: I nekoce posüto, chote khapü tsüto, phute, ngoyito; cekha kedüsüte, khri pütsüto.
english: "I tell you, keep asking, and it will be given you. Keep seeking, and you will find. Keep knocking, and it will be opened to you.

chokri: Kekrelamori chotimi ngoyiyo, kephumiri ngoyito, cekha ketu kesümi ri cekha khripü pütsüto.
english: For everyone who asks receives. He who seeks finds. To him who knocks it will be opened.

chokri: Mhace miponepü unupüce tsükhri cholize pü ketsü kha pütsü tome? Mori mhace füpü chosü ze tenhipü pütsü tome?
english: "Which of you fathers, if your son asks for bread, will give him a stone? Or if he asks for a fish, he won't give him a snake instead of a fish, will he?

chokri: Mori pü thevüjü cho süze, seba khapü tsütome
english: Or if he asks for an egg, he won't give him a scorpion, will he?

chokri: Mhace nekesumi zori mhakeveko kha nenuneko ketsü do se lize, neko Po tüyo kebao Kemesa Rüpü kha pücecho kebako tsümo tome?
english: If you then, being evil, know how to give good gifts to your children, how much more will your heavenly Father give the Holy Spirit to those who ask him?"

chokri: Pü therükesu jüpoyi keho rüpü husü. Kesu rüpüo taprata, jüpoyi kehoo jüpo yita, süla thümako ungothi ta.
english: He was casting out a demon, and it was mute. It happened, when the demon had gone out, the mute man spoke; and the multitudes marveled.

chokri: Vele humi poti, Beelzebub, therü kesuko kezhoo züde kesu rüpüko huba.
english: But some of them said, "He casts out demons by Beelzebul, the prince of the demons."

chokri: Humi pü medophide tüyo zasi phuta.
english: Others, testing him, sought from him a sign from heaven.

chokri: Vele pü uko lüdo seba, süla ukoce poti, kede kehopüri kezaphro ta lize pü kede ditato; cekro kengomude kezata lize cekrosüo retu tato.
english: But he, knowing their thoughts, said to them, "Every kingdom divided against itself is brought to desolation. A house divided against itself falls.

chokri: Mhace Satan sheri püdolü kengomu ta lize pü kede dipü bide bayita? Kekrelamori neko lüti, I Beelzebub zü rüpü kesuko zü tayo ziba.
english: If Satan also is divided against himself, how will his kingdom stand? For you say that I cast out demons by Beelzebul.

chokri: Mhace I Beelzebub zü rüpü kesuko huba lize, nenu thepumiko sopü züde huya? Süla neko ne ketito (sheco süko).
english: But if I cast out demons by Beelzebul, by whom do your children cast them out? Therefore will they be your judges.

chokri: Vele mhace I Jihova kede voriba keta sejo mote, nekoce vori bate.
english: But if I by the finger of God cast out demons, then the Kingdom of God has come to you.

chokri: Thüma kekumi keredepü pükedeba füba tisa, pümhapüzhüko krü bayo,
english: "When the strong man, fully armed, guards his own dwelling, his goods are safe.

chokri: Vele püce kukutimi püce vori talize, de pümeniva, pü melü keba rineko khi pütata, de püzhüko mpre yita.
english: But when someone stronger attacks him and overcomes him, he takes from him his whole armor in which he trusted, and divides his spoils.

chokri: Sopüno azü tamo sheri pü ametsu ta, sopüno azü mha kengu kesayo moshele pü mha püzhüko kelhu vayo.
english: "He that is not with me is against me. He who doesn't gather with me scatters.

chokri: Therükesu rüpü thüma püyo taketa ce, pü thethi keshükolü voyo, pü kerelü thi phude, ngoyi mo ketace pütasala pükehüthi tapra ketalü lavo tayo.
english: The unclean spirit, when he has gone out of the man, passes through dry places, seeking rest, and finding none, he says, 'I will turn back to my house from which I came out.'

chokri: Pü tavori tice ce süo methu ba mu ketsopü mezü ngoyi.
english: When he returns, he finds it swept and put in order.

chokri: Pü tavo püceri sukuti rüpü thüna züvoriyo. Uko tavori sülü hüta; thümasüo kehü thelaco keraceri suku tayo.
english: Then he goes, and takes seven other spirits more evil than himself, and they enter in and dwell there. The last state of that man becomes worse than the first."

chokri: Pü süko po kethace, theno mipü pü krolü semeto de poti, “thenomi nobu sopü n nune pü sheri thejokepütho, sosopüpü no sheri.”
english: It came to pass, as he said these things, a certain woman out of the multitude lifted up her voice, and said to him, "Blessed is the womb that bore you, and the breasts which nursed you!"

chokri: Püpoti. Vele, Ile, süo ceri sopüno Jihova jüko reni mu khiyi sheri süo thejo kepütho.
english: But he said, "On the contrary, blessed are those who hear the word of God, and keep it."

chokri: Thümako tavo kefükeyice pü ukoce poti, tsünumihi nuhü kesupü, uko zasi phutayo, zasi kha uko tsü hote, Jonah saju kepo yo zo kemosa.
english: When the multitudes were gathering together to him, he began to say, "This is an evil generation. It seeks after a sign. No sign will be given to it but the sign of Jonah, the prophet.

chokri: Jonah yo zasi khapü Ninevimiko tsüsü, sü bide thüma Nuo yo tsü kehü nuko tsü süto.
english: For even as Jonah became a sign to the Ninevites, so will also the Son of Man be to this generation.

chokri: Mekhroco kedepü sürei de tsünumiko yo sheco thito, de uko mejo tato; kekrelamori pü cejü valalü tavori de Solomon mhase ri ne, hihe, Solomon ceri zhoku tipü hice tha.
english: The Queen of the South will rise up in the judgment with the men of this generation, and will condemn them: for she came from the ends of the earth to hear the wisdom of Solomon; and behold, one greater than Solomon is here.

chokri: Nineveh thümako tasü tsünumi zü thayi de uko mejo tato, kekrelamori uko Jonas zhopola thelü redeyi ketala; Hihe, Jonas ceri zhoku tipü hice tha.
english: The men of Nineveh will stand up in the judgment with this generation, and will condemn it: for they repented at the preaching of Jonah, and behold, one greater than Jonah is here.

chokri: Mipüsheri meto khapü keresülü süva yomo, mori kha merhakhro süyo mo; vele kha metobayo süde thüma kevoriko po mhango ketola.
english: "No man, when he has lit a lamp, puts it in a cellar, nor under a basket, but on a stand, that those who enter in may see the light.

chokri: Themo kevesü umhüzo, nmhü ve kebala upho (nmo) metü vekrü bayo; vele nmhü vemo ketasa npho metü züte.
english: The lamp of the body is the eye. Therefore when your eye is good, your whole body is also full of light; but when it is evil, your body also is full of darkness.

chokri: N yo keba kebao la menuyite, zübüta mo ketola.
english: Therefore see whether the light that is in you isn't darkness.

chokri: Mhace nmo metü vekrü ba lize, püceri zü ta moze, meto keve mhava vekrü keba bi.
english: If therefore your whole body is full of light, having no part dark, it will be wholly full of light, as when the lamp with its bright shining gives you light."

chokri: Pü mhapo kebace Pharisee mipü püpo tavo pücelü mhatino püphu vori, pü tavo de mhati ketola bayi.
english: Now as he spoke, a certain Pharisee asked him to dine with him. He went in, and sat at the table.

chokri: Pharisee-o püngo kesüce pübe metü ra kemola püngo thita, mhaketi mhüjü.
english: When the Pharisee saw it, he marveled that he had not first washed himself before dinner.

chokri: Niepoo pücepoti, tsühi ne Phariseemiko chozuco tehe mefü thikemesa phiyo, vele ulüco mhakesu kelü mu kerhoko chu pü tayo.
english: The Lord said to him, "Now you Pharisees cleanse the outside of the cup and of the platter, but your inward part is full of extortion and wickedness.

chokri: Ne kemeloko, chozu thi kesüo thelü laku sheri thisü mo me?
english: You foolish ones, didn't he who made the outside make the inside also?

chokri: Vele mhakha mitsü mi Za süte, no thi kebabi, hihe, nla mhametü mesazo.
english: But give for gifts to the needy those things which are within, and behold, all things will be clean to you.

chokri: Vele ne Phariseemiko kesukerho, nreko zakeri zapü mhakenitho kori khasüyo, gako sheri, si neko yo Jihova kekhrü zalü kejütho mu kekhrü di no; hiko thiti mesüzo mu kekreko sheri thimo keta mesümo.
english: But woe to you Pharisees! For you tithe mint and rue and every herb, but you bypass justice and the love of God. You ought to have done these, and not to have left the other undone.

chokri: Phariseemiko, kesukerho, neko Synagogues kolü theba kerekrü kolü ba, duka kolü thüma kelu tayo.
english: Woe to you Pharisees! For you love the best seats in the synagogues, and the greetings in the marketplaces.

chokri: Phariseemiko, lesikeseko, yo kesukerho, kethera ketako; neko mhakhrelü ngokemo bi, thümako sükoyo ta voyo upi se kemola
english: Woe to you, scribes and Pharisees, hypocrites! For you are like hidden graves, and the men who walk over them don't know it."

chokri: Thezhokesemipü püce poti, kemethao no hako sheri meshude pobate ile,
english: One of the lawyers answered him, "Teacher, in saying this you insult us also."

chokri: Pü pokesü, neko thezhokekhiko yo kesu kerho, mipo püyi keho thezhoko kha mipo püyo, mo neko becüne sheri püjü yomo.
english: He said, "Woe to you lawyers also! For you load men with burdens that are difficult to carry, and you yourselves won't even lift one finger to help carry those burdens.

chokri: Nekoyo kesu kerho, neko saju kepoko mhakhre chüsü de neponeko süko dokhri süyo.
english: Woe to you! For you build the tombs of the prophets, and your fathers killed them.

chokri: Nepo neko thi kesü neko zhopo kozo, uko süko dokhri süzo, neko süko mhakhre chüsü.
english: So you testify and consent to the works of your fathers. For they killed them, and you build their tombs.

chokri: Süla Jihova mhase po kesü, I saju kepo mu Apostleko ketsü süto, süko humi ukopo dokhri to mu methi meni to.
english: Therefore also the wisdom of God said, 'I will send to them prophets and apostles; and some of them they will kill and persecute,

chokri: Thajü saju kepoko za mekrü kesü metü vo thaketso tsüdo nuhüko po shu tato.
english: that the blood of all the prophets, which was shed from the foundation of the world, may be required of this generation;

chokri: Abel za mekrü kesülü vo Zacharia ketso kereshüce dolü mu kereshüba yo; I nekoce potüsüto, süko metü vo tsü nuhüko yo kutato.
english: from the blood of Abel to the blood of Zachariah, who perished between the altar and the sanctuary.' Yes, I tell you, it will be required of this generation.

chokri: Neko thezho kekhikoyo kesukerho, neko mhase cabi khipü tate, neko sheri thiyomo mu mipori süko zümu.
english: Woe to you lawyers! For you took away the key of knowledge. You didn't enter in yourselves, and those who were entering in, you hindered."

chokri: Mhahiko püpo kesüsa, lesikeseko mu Phariseeko püpo mhaposasü ketola pu kethuta.
english: As he said these things to them, the scribes and the Pharisees began to be terribly angry, and to draw many things out of him;

chokri: Püri bata, mu pütalü kepo tüyi ketola side pü mejo ketola.
english: lying in wait for him, and seeking to catch him in something he might say, that they might accuse him.

chokri: Vapü Jisu Pharisee krüta mipü celü mhati tole tavozü Sabbath nha, miko püpe türü püba.
english: It happened, when he went into the house of one of the rulers of the Pharisees on a Sabbath to eat bread, that they were watching him.

chokri: Pü mhüjülü kethi (dropsy) mipü süceba.
english: Behold, a certain man who had dropsy was in front of him.

chokri: Jisu thezho keseko mu Phariseemiko ketso de poti, Sabbath nha mithi kesürho kesü thezho me m a?
english: Jesus, answering, spoke to the lawyers and Pharisees, saying, "Is it lawful to heal on the Sabbath?"

chokri: Uko kemekre bata. Süla Jisu püthi kesürho süde pü ketsü metava.
english: But they were silent. He took him, and healed him, and let him go.

chokri: Jisu ukoce poti, mhace mipü pünunepü mori püthopü retu jüfülü lüta lize, süo sü Sabbath tüsozho nha ta zori neko rekri süo süphra prari ta mo tome?
english: He answered them, "Which of you, if your son or an ox fell into a well, wouldn't immediately pull him out on a Sabbath day?"

chokri: Uko püfü kelasü mota.
english: They couldn't answer him regarding these things.

chokri: Misothi kebako therho bayo keda de tavo ba ketango kesüce Jisu jüvepü posü.
english: He spoke a parable to those who were invited, when he noticed how they chose the best seats, and said to them,

chokri: No nolü hülü kevola mi ncü kesüce, tavo theba kerekrülü bata hite, kekrelamori mi kekre nce meleku timi n niepoo cüpü meta sheri no semo tila.
english: "When you are invited by anyone to a marriage feast, don't sit in the best seat, since perhaps someone more honorable than you might be invited by him,

chokri: N nieposo-o tavori de nce, n thethi (nba) kha putsü vate sizilizo no ta kerenulü kita ketola m u no renümo tato.
english: and he who invited both of you would come and tell you, 'Make room for this person.' Then you would begin, with shame, to take the lowest place.

chokri: Vele ncü kesüce, no tavo theba kerenutho lü ba merayite, n nieposo-o ta nce vori de poti, azü-o, no kerekrülü kute, sizilize nzü keba thüma metüko mhüjülü no therho ngo yito.
english: But when you are invited, go and sit in the lowest place, so that when he who invited you comes, he may tell you, 'Friend, move up higher.' Then you will be honored in the presence of all who sit at the table with you.

chokri: Kekrelamori somi utho uthi kerekrü pü tayo sheri thi renu vato, vele somi uthi kerenu pü tayo sheri thi kerekrü sü ketola.
english: For everyone who exalts himself will be humbled, and whoever humbles himself will be exalted."

chokri: Mi cü kesü niepoo cepoti, no mipo ncelü mhati tole cü kesüce, n khrethomiko, mori nsajü jürako, mori ncekromi, mori mha kenemiko cü hite; uko nposhu vato. Mori n cü sala vato.
english: He also said to the one who had invited him, "When you make a dinner or a supper, don't call your friends, nor your brothers, nor your kinsmen, nor rich neighbors, or perhaps they might also return the favor, and pay you back.

chokri: Vele no mizüvo hi tice, kedimi, pheyhomi kerengami, mhücemi cü süte.
english: But when you make a feast, ask the poor, the maimed, the lame, or the blind;

chokri: No thejo ngoyito, kekrelamori uko npo shu salayiho tila, mu no kemethako rhüla kesüce nshu sala süto.
english: and you will be blessed, because they don't have the resources to repay you. For you will be repaid in the resurrection of the righteous."

chokri: Mha ketilü resoko mipü süo sekesüce, puce poti, “kehopüri Jihova celü mhati ketomisü thejo kepütho.”
english: When one of those who sat at the table with him heard these things, he said to him, "Blessed is he who will feast in the Kingdom of God!"

chokri: Süsa Jisu puce poti, thümapü mi kekro cüpü pü celü mhati nosü,
english: But he said to him, "A certain man made a great supper, and he invited many people.

chokri: Mhati keto tülü pü thekümi ketsüpü cüpü kemetako ce posü, tavorite mha metü kerede pü mezü te.
english: He sent out his servant at supper time to tell those who were invited, 'Come, for everything is ready now.'

chokri: Vele uko metü thiyi sutale sizi tsota, thüma kerao poti, I cejüpü khripü meta, mu I tavo süo pemo sute, akejo vasüte.
english: They all as one began to make excuses. "The first said to him, 'I have bought a field, and I must go and see it. Please have me excused.'

chokri: Kekremipü poti, I metho mha kethipü püngu khripü meta, I süko züvo mhathi to bate, I vori hote, a kejo va süte.
english: "Another said, 'I have bought five yoke of oxen, and I must go try them out. Please have me excused.'

chokri: Kekremipü I ceyo yite tsüdo cehi, I süla vori yi hote.
english: "Another said, 'I have married a wife, and therefore I can't come.'

chokri: Süla choyi kethio ta sala vori süko pü pü niepoo ce posü siri niepoo pünü moshuta, de pü cetao ce poti, rekri tavo chopü kolü chotsu kolü metü rünalü mhakedi miko, lahemiko, mhücemiko, mu pheyhomiko zü vori te.
english: "That servant came, and told his lord these things. Then the master of the house, being angry, said to his servant, 'Go out quickly into the streets and lanes of the city, and bring in the poor, maimed, blind, and lame.'

chokri: Miceta-o, poti, kezhoo n kepoko thitso süte, siri thethi kekro meshopü balu,
english: "The servant said, 'Lord, it is done as you commanded, and there is still room.'

chokri: Süsa niepoo poti, ta cho metülü vode thümako kemuthi zü vori te, sizide acelü chuyi ketola.
english: "The lord said to the servant, 'Go out into the highways and hedges, and compel them to come in, that my house may be filled.

chokri: Kekrelamori I neko ce potü süto, cüpü kemezüko mipüri acelü mhati mo tato
english: For I tell you that none of those men who were invited will taste of my supper.'"

chokri: Thüma kekro püzüta, pü regü sü ukoce poti,
english: Now great multitudes were going with him. He turned and said to them,

chokri: Mhace kehopüri acekevoriko, upo, uzu, ucemi, ununeko, usazüjüra, ulipüko ngo metsü ta kemomi, utho ukerhüro sherei, sümi asaji thishümo.
english: "If anyone comes to me, and doesn't hate his own father, mother, wife, children, brothers, and sisters, yes, and his own life also, he can't be my disciple.

chokri: Pütho pü cross pü kemomi asaji thi shümo.
english: Whoever doesn't bear his own cross, and come after me, can't be my disciple.

chokri: Neko mipü dahu fü keto rhüyi, ba merayi de püma sosü, Thitsoyi tome ho shi?
english: For which of you, desiring to build a tower, doesn't first sit down and count the cost, to see if he has enough to complete it?

chokri: Molize shüdü sü de süo thitsoyi hota, Thüma metü pükrünyü ta.
english: Or perhaps, when he has laid a foundation, and is not able to finish, everyone who sees begins to mock him,

chokri: Miko poti, thüma hio shudüsü thitsoyi mote.
english: saying, 'This man began to build, and wasn't able to finish.'

chokri: Mori kede mipü kedekekrepü zü therithi tole zütatice, bamerayi de pükerüko sosü kemoba me? Thüma nye keri keba, püzü kesü tolizü thüma nye mecü zükesü keto.
english: Or what king, as he goes to encounter another king in war, will not sit down first and consider whether he is able with ten thousand to meet him who comes against him with twenty thousand?

chokri: Mori kro kekreo chokecholü ketace pü thüma ketsüpü kezüküvela jüchoyi khasü.
english: Or else, while the other is yet a great way off, he sends an envoy, and asks for conditions of peace.

chokri: Sübi kehopü seri pü kebako khava meramode a sajimi thiyiho.
english: So therefore whoever of you who doesn't renounce all that he has, he can't be my disciple.

chokri: Metsa veshu, vele metsa zori pükekhrü bamo ta lize dipü khipü thi mekhrüyi veta?
english: Salt is good, but if the salt becomes flat and tasteless, with what do you season it?

chokri: Süo sü cejü la vemo, mori khapü methobü ri thiyi su; thümapo kegükeva thi vayo. Püni kebami po reniyite.
english: It is fit neither for the soil nor for the manure pile. It is thrown out. He who has ears to hear, let him hear."

chokri: Püsajiko cepo. Süziti mesümo, vele nümo kethiko vorato, vele sopü süo pü vori to sheri kesukerho.
english: He said to the disciples, "It is impossible that no occasions of stumbling should come, but woe to him through whom they come!

chokri: Ketsü mhagü ketipü khapü pü voyo khüsü de kegü düpü jü kezholü sü keva püla veku, natsi keni hiko nümo kethiceri.
english: It would be better for him if a millstone were hung around his neck, and he were thrown into the sea, rather than that he should cause one of these little ones to stumble.

chokri: Menu yite, n lano, nsazümi n nüthi ta lize, pü lekhu (rü) vate, mu mhace pülü redela lize pü kejo va süte.
english: Be careful. If your brother sins against you, rebuke him. If he repents, forgive him.

chokri: Mhace pü va thüna n nümo thiva zhopünha, mu tüso pünha va thüna nece poti, I kekrite (alü redete) siti pota lize, pü kejo va süte.
english: If he sins against you seven times in the day, and seven times returns, saying, 'I repent,' you shall forgive him."

chokri: Püsajiko püce poti, Niepoo hako kemelü thi meve sasüte.
english: The apostles said to the Lord, "Increase our faith."

chokri: Niepoo ukoce poti, nkemelü gakre sipü düri ba lize, lekubo hio ce, no nme rela pü jü kezholü lü shuyi tate, sizi sü zori süo nfü zü tato.
english: The Lord said, "If you had faith like a grain of mustard seed, you would tell this sycamore tree, 'Be uprooted, and be planted in the sea,' and it would obey you.

chokri: Vele neko sopü, cetami püce tefühete, mori metho vi tade vori lize püpo, tavo bayite, side mhati yite siti ba?
english: But who is there among you, having a servant plowing or keeping sheep, that will say, when he comes in from the field, 'Come immediately and sit down at the table,'

chokri: Püpo tavo kerede süde npo mhati yite, de achoyi thi süte, I mhati mhakrayi kemothe; süsalü no mhati mhakra to ho, si kemo ba?
english: and will not rather tell him, 'Prepare my supper, clothe yourself properly, and serve me, while I eat and drink. Afterward you shall eat and drink'?

chokri: Pü thekümi mezati bame, pü süko metü thipü pütsü kesü la.
english: Does he thank that servant because he did the things that were commanded? I think not.

chokri: Süla nekopo thino khakesüko metü thitso kevasa, poti meveyi kemo umi cetamiko, upothino kesüko uko thi süzo.
english: Even so you also, when you have done all the things that are commanded you, say, 'We are unworthy servants. We have done our duty.'"

chokri: Pü Jerusalem lü kho kezüce Samaria mu Galilee kolü püthüvo,
english: It happened as he was on his way to Jerusalem, that he was passing along the borders of Samaria and Galilee.

chokri: Pü rena pülü lüri lepra kethimi keri zü kesü, uko rapeco tha,
english: As he entered into a certain village, ten men who were lepers met him, who stood at a distance.

chokri: Uko füphra metode poti, Jisu, kemethao hako phimezhe süte.
english: They lifted up their voices, saying, "Jesus, Master, have mercy on us!"

chokri: Pü süko ngosü, ukoce poti, tavo neko püsü thevoko mengo süte, uko tavo züri uko kethi menüta (mesata)
english: When he saw them, he said to them, "Go and show yourselves to the priests." It happened that as they went, they were cleansed.

chokri: Uko dolü mipü menü ketala regü sala vori de Jihova zapozale posüta.
english: One of them, when he saw that he was healed, turned back, glorifying God with a loud voice.

chokri: Tavo nekitsü püphe ce züta, pü mezale zita, mu pü Samaria mipü.
english: He fell on his face at Jesus' feet, giving him thanks; and he was a Samaritan.

chokri: Jisu püfü kelakesü, mikeri thi menü sü mome? Vele mi thecüko dipüce vota?
english: Jesus answered, "Weren't the ten cleansed? But where are the nine?

chokri: Uko sala vori Jihova zapozale posü mote, thümasovemi (sekemo) hio zo kemosa.
english: Were there none found who returned to give glory to God, except this stranger?"

chokri: Pü püce pode, tasü tate de ta n chodelü vota te nkemelü tho nthi kesürho süteho.
english: Then he said to him, "Get up, and go your way. Your faith has healed you."

chokri: Jihova kede kethi dipü kethice vori to sheri Pharisee miko senyü ketala posüno zita, pü uko fü kela kesü, Jihova kede sü kepelü vori ho.
english: Being asked by the Pharisees when the Kingdom of God would come, he answered them, "The Kingdom of God doesn't come with observation;

chokri: Mori uko poti, Hihe, hice bave, mori luce ba ziho, hihe, Jihova kede sü nlü ba.
english: neither will they say, 'Look, here!' or, 'Look, there!' for behold, the Kingdom of God is within you."

chokri: Pü püsajiko ce poti, neko Thümanu tüsozhoko ngonyüsa to vele ngoyiho.
english: He said to the disciples, "The days will come, when you will desire to see one of the days of the Son of Man, and you will not see it.

chokri: Uko nekocepoti, hice pete, mori luce pete süko sameji tahite mori süce vohite.
english: They will tell you, 'Look, here!' or 'Look, there!' Don't go away, nor follow after them,

chokri: Kekrelamori khüpüco tü reprü süyo tükhro sizide tükhro kekreco vevotayo; sübi thüma Nuo tüsozho nhari sizi zoto.
english: for as the lightning, when it flashes out of the one part under the sky, shines to the other part under the sky; so will the Son of Man be in his day.

chokri: Vele therace (kerace) pü kemezhe merato, mhakekrolü mu melavato, tsü kehü numi dolü.
english: But first, he must suffer many things and be rejected by this generation.

chokri: Noah kehü tüce bi thüma Nuo tülü ri sizi zoto.
english: As it happened in the days of Noah, even so will it be also in the days of the Son of Man.

chokri: Uko mhati, mhakra, mu kesü keri (ceyo) ucemi yi, Noah rulü lüta kemothe jüde vori, uko metü mekava.
english: They ate, they drank, they married, they were given in marriage, until the day that Noah entered into the ark, and the flood came, and destroyed them all.

chokri: Süsübi Lot tüce sheri, uko mhati mhakra, uko mhazhu mu khri, mhate mu cekothi ziba.
english: Likewise, even as it happened in the days of Lot: they ate, they drank, they bought, they sold, they planted, they built;

chokri: Vele Lot tapra keta tüsozho nha, Sodom rüna lü merü süta tüyono. Side uko metü mekava,
english: but in the day that Lot went out from Sodom, it rained fire and sulfur from the sky, and destroyed them all.

chokri: Thüma Nuo tüceri sübi mesemengo sü zoto.
english: It will be the same way in the day that the Son of Man is revealed.

chokri: Sütüce cetuyo kebami püzhü celü ba sheri püpo kiri süo pü hite, sübi yilü ketami po regü sala celü vori hite.
english: In that day, he who will be on the housetop, and his goods in the house, let him not go down to take them away. Let him who is in the field likewise not turn back.

chokri: Lot cemi relela süte.
english: Remember Lot's wife!

chokri: Kehopü sheri upho merhüyi nyütimi medi vato, mu kehopü sheri püpho medi kevami süo merhüyito.
english: Whoever seeks to save his life loses it, but whoever loses his life preserves it.

chokri: I neko ce posüto, mikena thezipülü züto, mipü züyi tato mu kekreo khavato; süzi.
english: I tell you, in that night there will be two people in one bed. The one will be taken, and the other will be left.

chokri: Thenomi kena teha thobato, kezüde, mipü züyi to mu kekreo khava to.
english: There will be two grinding grain together. One will be taken, and the other will be left."

chokri: Mi kena yilü tato, mipü züyi to mu kekreo kha vato.
english: Two men shall be in the field; the one shall be taken, and the other left.

chokri: Uko püfü kela kesü, dipüce sizita? Niepoo pü ukoupoti, dipüce kesamoko ba sheri thirhako vo sülü kengupü bayo.
english: They answering, asked him, "Where, Lord?" He said to them, "Where the body is, there will the vultures also be gathered together."

chokri: Pü ukoce jüvepü posü, pütü metülü kecho thiba moze kesu, mu ho mezata kemo.
english: He also spoke a parable to them that they must always pray, and not give up,

chokri: Pokesü, nade pülü sheco mipü tayo; pü Jihova ri mpe yomo, mori thüma sheri rho yomo.
english: saying, "There was a judge in a certain city who didn't fear God, and didn't respect man.

chokri: Nade sülü samepü mipü ba, theno süpü ta püce vori poti, ametsü ketio yo akhüpo süte,.
english: A widow was in that city, and she often came to him, saying, 'Defend me from my adversary!'

chokri: Dopüla pü muta, vele kenuco pülüti, I Jihova mpe yogmo, mori thüma sheri rho yomo sheri,
english: He wouldn't for a while, but afterward he said to himself, 'Though I neither fear God, nor respect man,

chokri: Sizi zori theno hipü a keyhu ba zote, I pükhü pothi süto, kemosa thenohipü a keba zote.
english: yet because this widow bothers me, I will defend her, or else she will wear me out by her continual coming.'"

chokri: Niepoo poti, jütho kemoo sheco reni süte.
english: The Lord said, "Listen to what the unrighteous judge says.

chokri: Jihova khüpo thisü mota tome pü keda keyi mikoyo pü uko kecho pütühu güpü bata sheri?
english: Won't God avenge his chosen ones, who are crying out to him day and night, and yet he exercises patience with them?

chokri: I nekoce posüto, pü, khüpo thimha sütoi; sizizosheri, thüma Nuo kevorice cejülü kemelü ba ngoyi tome?
english: I tell you that he will avenge them quickly. Nevertheless, when the Son of Man comes, will he find faith on the earth?"

chokri: Jüve hio khapü humi utho ulü kematho de mikekreko lü thathi ketako ce poti;
english: He spoke also this parable to certain people who were convinced of their own righteousness, and who despised all others.

chokri: Mi kena ta kereshücelü kho kechothi tole vo; mipü Pharisee mu kekreo mahomi.
english: "Two men went up into the temple to pray; one was a Pharisee, and the other was a tax collector.

chokri: Pharisee süthayi de kechothi sü, therüo I n meza, I thüma kekremi bi kemola, mizhü kethi kedü tiyo, jüthomo, regü rezhü mori matu kehomi thüma hio ri bi kemola.
english: The Pharisee stood and prayed to himself like this: 'God, I thank you, that I am not like the rest of men, extortioners, unrighteous, adulterers, or even like this tax collector.

chokri: I deoba dopülü zho kena (va kena) mevo yo, I zakeri zapü amhazhü metükolü khasüyo.
english: I fast twice a week. I give tithes of all that I get.'

chokri: Matu kehoo rapeco tha, pü mhü khrüpü türi peyi ho, de pü khoro kekhu poti, Jihova I kephomami a hiri phimizhe süte.
english: But the tax collector, standing far away, wouldn't even lift up his eyes to heaven, but beat his breast, saying, 'God, be merciful to me, a sinner!'

chokri: I nekoce posüto, thüma hio kekreo ceri püthi kematho pü pücelü vote; kehopüri uthi renu pü ketami thi rekrü süto.
english: I tell you, this man went down to his house justified rather than the other; for everyone who exalts himself will be humbled, but he who humbles himself will be exalted."

chokri: Uko nuranuko züpüvo püpo jüsüno püce vori, vele melha kenyiko süko ngosü de süko lekhuta.
english: They were also bringing their babies to him, that he might touch them. But when the disciples saw it, they rebuked them.

chokri: Vele Jisu uko cüpü püce süyi, de poti, natsiko po ace vorite, uko khahite; kekrelamori Jihova kede hibi tiko zhüzo tila.
english: Jesus summoned them, saying, "Allow the little children to come to me, and don't hinder them, for the Kingdom of God belongs to such as these.

chokri: I neko ce posüto, kehopü sheri Jihova kede yiyi ketomi natsi hiko keniko biyi kemosa sülü lüyi hozo tila.
english: Most assuredly, I tell you, whoever doesn't receive the Kingdom of God like a little child, he will in no way enter into it."

chokri: Kezho mipü püce mhaketso, de poti, kemeta keveo kechothe kehü kalü thiyi ketola I dipü thi moze su?
english: A certain ruler asked him, saying, "Good Teacher, what shall I do to inherit eternal life?"

chokri: Jisu pücepoti, no dipü thide a cüpü keveo thiba? Mipüri vemo, mipü lebi sü Jihova zo.
english: Jesus asked him, "Why do you call me good? No one is good, except one--God.

chokri: No khakesü thezhoko seba, mi dokhri su, regü rezhü su, mi rego su, kemeda zhopo thisu, npo nzu rhoyite.
english: You know the commandments: 'Don't commit adultery,' 'Don't murder,' 'Don't steal,' 'Don't give false testimony,' 'Honor your father and your mother.'"

chokri: Pü poti, I anatsilü süko thi mu zü vorite.
english: He said, "I have observed all these things from my youth up."

chokri: Süko sekesüsa Jisu pücepoti, no mhapü tsomo balu; n mhakebako zhupü kedimi za vate, size no tüyo cene kengu yito; mu tavori asa meji yite.
english: When Jesus heard these things, he said to him, "You still lack one thing. Sell all that you have, and distribute it to the poor. You will have treasure in heaven. Come, follow me."

chokri: Pü süko se kesüsa, püthü küshu ta, kekrelamori pü mhane shu timi tila.
english: But when he heard these things, he became very sad, for he was very rich.

chokri: Pülü shüshu keta Jisu ngokesü ce poti, Jihova kedelü lü ketola kenemiko dithedü ri rü zoshi!
english: Jesus, seeing that he became very sad, said, "How hard it is for those who have riches to enter into the Kingdom of God!

chokri: Kekrelamori ut püpo theprü celü lü keta reyi ku, kene mipü po Jihova kedelü lüti ceri.
english: For it is easier for a camel to enter in through a needle's eye, than for a rich man to enter into the Kingdom of God."

chokri: Uko süo keseko poti, süsa sopü kelayi veta?
english: Those who heard it said, "Then who can be saved?"

chokri: Pü poti mhako thüma thiyi kehoko, Jihova thi yi ve.
english: But he said, "The things which are impossible with men are possible with God."

chokri: Süsa Peter poti, Hihe, hako mhametü vava de nsa meji tate.
english: Peter said, "Look, we have left everything, and followed you."

chokri: Pü ukoce poti, I neko ce potü süto, thüma uce khava, ukrüko khava, usazü jüra khava, ucemi khava, uneko khava, Jihova kedelano.
english: He said to them, "Most assuredly I tell you, there is no one who has left house, or wife, or brothers, or parents, or children, for the Kingdom of God's sake,

chokri: Tsükehü tülü mhakekro ngoyi to, mu hisa kehü lü kechothe kerhü ngoyi to.
english: who will not receive many times more in this time, and in the world to come, eternal life."

chokri: Süsa püsaji mi kerikenako züyi de uko ce po kesü, hihe, uko ta Jerusalem lü khoto, mu saju kepoko thüma nuo lapo pü kemetako thitsü süto.
english: He took the twelve aside, and said to them, "Behold, we are going up to Jerusalem, and all the things that are written through the prophets concerning the Son of Man will be completed.

chokri: Pü khapü Gentile (melükemo) mi belü sü vato, de pü krünyü süto, pü meshu mu pü rephri süto.
english: For he will be delivered up to the Gentiles, will be mocked, treated shamefully, and spit on.

chokri: Uko po, methimeni süto, mu pü dokhri vato; mu pü zho sünha rhüla süri to.
english: They will scourge and kill him. On the third day, he will rise again."

chokri: Uko mhasüko püri püco seyimo, mu püco uko mhüjülü kevava tila, mori püpo kesüko püri semo.
english: They understood none of these things. This saying was hidden from them, and they didn't understand the things that were said.

chokri: Pü Jericho meno vori tazü tice, mhücemi pü cholü baamakhrü ti bayo.
