english: It happened, as he came near Jericho, a certain blind man sat by the road, begging.

chokri: Makro tavo kezü sesü, dipüla sheri pü ketso sü.
english: Hearing a multitude going by, he asked what this meant.

chokri: Uko pücepoti Jisu Nazerath mi tavo züle zisü.
english: They told him that Jesus of Nazareth was passing by.

chokri: Pü semeto de poti, David nu Jisu aphimezhe süte.
english: He cried out, "Jesus, you son of David, have mercy on me!"

chokri: Tara kevoko pülekhuta, püpo kemekre tano zita, vele pü se meto sata, David nu Jisu aphimezhü süte.
english: Those who led the way rebuked him, that he should be quiet; but he cried out all the more, "You son of David, have mercy on me!"

chokri: Jisu thayi de püzü püce vono sü, mu pü ta meno pheri tice, pü ketso, de
english: Standing still, Jesus commanded him to be brought to him. When he had come near, he asked him,

chokri: Poti, mhakedipü apo nlathisü ta? Püpoti, Niepoo, apo mhangoyi te.
english: "What do you want me to do?" He said, "Lord, that I may see again."

chokri: Jisu pücepoti, mhango yite, n kemelü n kelasü teho.
english: Jesus said to him, "Receive your sight. Your faith has healed you."

chokri: Sizi sü metha pü, mhango yita, de püsa mejuta, Jihova zapozale pota, mu thüma süo kengo metü Jihova chuta.
english: Immediately he received his sight, and followed him, glorifying God. All the people, when they saw it, praised God.

chokri: Jisu Jericholü tavo zü.
english: He entered and was passing through Jericho.

chokri: Hihe, mipü püza Zachaeus tipü ba. Pü theso keyimi mu mhaneshu.
english: There was a man named Zacchaeus. He was a chief tax collector, and he was rich.

chokri: Pü Jisu sü sopüdipü sheri pesü toli ba, püpeyi mota kekrelamori thüma kebü phi ketala, kekrelamori püda renu ketala.
english: He was trying to see who Jesus was, and couldn't because of the crowd, because he was short.

chokri: Pü mhüjülü lekubo süyo meleku püpe ketola, pü chode sülü vori to kebala.
english: He ran on ahead, and climbed up into a sycamore tree to see him, for he was to pass that way.

chokri: Jisu thethi sülü vori, pü tosü pe, de püngoyi, de püce poti, Zachaeus rekri kirite; kekrelamori tha I n celü ba moze kesula.
english: When Jesus came to the place, he looked up and saw him, and said to him, "Zacchaeus, hurry and come down, for today I must stay at your house."

chokri: Mu pü rekri kiri, pünüshüde püzüyi.
english: He hurried, came down, and received him joyfully.

chokri: Uko süo ngosü de jü kebushuta, de poti, pü kephomami so thi to vote.
english: When they saw it, they all murmured, saying, "He has gone in to lodge with a man who is a sinner."

chokri: Zacchaeus süthayi de poti, hihe, Niepoo azhü phatüpü kha kedimi tsü vato, mhace I kemedalü humi mha khiyi ta lize I zada pü pütsü sala vato.
english: Zacchaeus stood and said to the Lord, "Behold, Lord, half of my goods I give to the poor. If I have wrongfully exacted anything of anyone, I restore four times as much."

chokri: Jisu püce poti, thatüsolü kelakeyi ce hilü vorite, pü, sheri Abraham nu zo tila.
english: Jesus said to him, "Today, salvation has come to this house, because he also is a son of Abraham.

chokri: Kekrelamori thüma Nuo di ketami phu mu kelato vori.
english: For the Son of Man came to seek and to save that which was lost."

chokri: Uko mhasüko sekesü sa pü jüve posasü, kekrelamori uko Jerusalem meno voketala mu uko kelüce Jihova kedekethi vori mha zita totho zi kebala.
english: As they heard these things, he went on and told a parable, because he was near Jerusalem, and they supposed that the Kingdom of God would be revealed immediately.

chokri: Süla püpoti, mipü ta kemeculü vota pü kede khiyi ketola de süsa ta vori.
english: He said therefore, "A certain nobleman went into a far country to receive for himself a kingdom, and to return.

chokri: Pü mi keriko pücetako cüsü, mu presa (pound) keri pü uko tsüsü de uko ce poti, khipü bate I vori kemothe.
english: He called ten servants of his, and gave them ten mina coins, and told them, 'Conduct business until I come.'

chokri: Vele püramiko püngo metsüta, de jüpü kha püsalü, posü, uko thüma hiopo upi kekhaho.
english: But his citizens hated him, and sent an envoy after him, saying, 'We don't want this man to reign over us.'

chokri: Pü kede kethi khiyi side ta sala vori, pü ceta süko po püce vorite side cüsü, presa pü uko tsü kesüko, uko dithedü meveyi ta sheri pü se ketola, hetho Heli kethilü.
english: "It happened when he had come back again, having received the kingdom, that he commanded these servants, to whom he had given the money, to be called to him, that he might know what they had gained by conducting business.

chokri: Vori kerao poti, n presako za keri pra yite;
english: The first came before him, saying, 'Lord, your mina has made ten more minas.'

chokri: Mu püce po kesü, vewe, no ceta keve, no mha kenilü sheri jütho keyi la npo rüna keri pi kekhatato.
english: "He said to him, 'Well done, you good servant! Because you were found faithful with very little, you shall have authority over ten cities.'

chokri: Mi kenao tavori poti, Niepoo npresako za püngu meveyite le,
english: "The second came, saying, 'Your mina, Lord, has made five minas.'

chokri: Pü sübi de po kesü, npo rüna püngu pi kekha tato.
english: "So he said to him, 'And you are to be over five cities.'

chokri: Kekre-o (mi süo) püce voride poti, hi he, n presa I khapü mezü (a fünelü kezhü pü meta).
english: Another came, saying, 'Lord, behold, your mina, which I kept laid away in a handkerchief,

chokri: Kekrelamori I n meprü ketala, no kepatsü shu timipü (nlüsü timi), no khapü meta mori khiyi yo, so (tsava) mori rüyi yo tila,
english: for I feared you, because you are an exacting man. You take up that which you didn't lay down, and reap that which you didn't sow.'

chokri: Püpüce poti, n ta kekha vate, I nshe keti tato (nta kepo bi I nsheco süto). No thekü kesu, no seti I kepatsü timi lezi; I khapü meta mori khiyi yolezi; sosü mori I rüyi yole zite.
english: "He said to him, 'Out of your own mouth will I judge you, you wicked servant! You knew that I am an exacting man, taking up that which I didn't lay down, and reaping that which I didn't sow.

chokri: No dito apresa khapü bank lü (kengu tice) sü meta vama, side apo püpra sheri ngoyi tama?
english: Then why didn't you deposit my money in the bank, and at my coming, I might have earned interest on it?'

chokri: Süsa pü uko ce poti, pukhüce kethako, püyo presa kebao püyi tate, de khapü presa $10 (pound) keri kebao tsüvate.
english: He said to those who stood by, 'Take the mina away from him, and give it to him who has the ten minas.'

chokri: Uko puce poti, Niepoo thüma hio presa keri bale.
english: "They said to him, 'Lord, he has ten minas!'

chokri: Pü uko poti; kehopü sheri mhakeba-a pü tsüsa süto, mu kehopü sheri mhalakemoo kebako sheri püyi tato, pücelü.
english: 'For I tell you that to everyone who has, will more be given; but from him who doesn't have, even that which he has will be taken away from him.

chokri: Vele angokemumiko, apo ukopi kekha nyü kemoko, zü hice vorite, side amhüjülü uko dokhri vate.
english: But bring those enemies of mine who didn't want me to reign over them here, and kill them before me.'"

chokri: Mu pü mha sübi po kevasa, pu uko mhüjülü ta Jerusalem lü khota.
english: Having said these things, he went on ahead, going up to Jerusalem.

chokri: Püta Bethphage mu Bethanymeno vori tice, cejütu cüpü olive siyo, m pü mi kena püsajini ketsüsü.
english: It happened, when he drew near to Bethsphage and Bethany, at the mountain that is called Olivet, he sent two of his disciples,

chokri: Poti, ta rünalü, neni mhüjülü lüte, neni talü tice, gada nepü khüpü mezü ngoyito, mipüri püyoo ku kemopü, phrüyite, püzü hice vorite.
english: saying, "Go your way into the village on the other side, in which, as you enter, you will find a colt tied, whereon no man ever yet sat. Untie it, and bring it.

chokri: Mhace humi neni ketsode dipüla pü phrü bata? Neni püce po süte, kekrelamori Niepoo (püzü) khimoze su bale.
english: If anyone asks you, 'Why are you untying it?' say to him: 'The Lord needs it.'"

chokri: Uni ketsü kesü ni tavo ta, mu uni tavo pü po kesü bide ngoyi.
english: Those who were sent went away, and found things just as he had told them.

chokri: Uni tavo phrü kebace niepoko unice poti, neni dipüla pü (gadanu) phrü bata?
english: As they were untying the colt, the owners of it said to them, "Why are you untying the colt?"

chokri: Mu uni poti, Niepoo pü khimozesu bale.
english: They said, "The Lord needs it."

chokri: Uni gadanu züpü Jisu cevori, mu uko füneko kha gadanuyo khrasü, de Jisu süyo ku bayi.
english: They brought it to Jesus. They threw their cloaks on the colt, and set Jesus on them.

chokri: Mu püta kevo cholü uko füko khrasü.
english: As he went, they spread their cloaks in the way.

chokri: Uko ta olive cejütuyo ki kezüce pü metha keyi makro rechü de Jihova chuta, füphra meto seta, uko pü mhatho keku kene thi kesü kola.
english: As he was now getting near, at the descent of the Mount of Olives, the whole multitude of the disciples began to rejoice and praise God with a loud voice for all the mighty works which they had seen,

chokri: Kedeo Niepoo zalü kevorio po thejo kepü thiyite; tüyo kezü keve, mu zapozale kerekrüthoo.
english: saying, "Blessed is the King who comes in the name of the Lord! Peace in heaven, and glory in the highest!"

chokri: Phariseemiko humi makrodolü püce poti, kemethao, n sajiko lekhu süte.
english: Some of the Pharisees from the multitude said to him, "Teacher, rebuke your disciples!"

chokri: Pü uko fü kela kesü, I neko ce süo posüto, mhace uko kemekretazori, ketsüko rekri se meto prari tato.
english: He answered them, "I tell you that if these were silent, the stones would cry out."

chokri: Püta nadeo meno voritice pü süo pesüde pü krikra ta.
english: When he drew near, he saw the city and wept over it,

chokri: Poti, mhace no n tüso sebalize, ketsatho zori tha tüsohi, nla kezükeve pü voriti, vele süko n mhüce resüte.
english: saying, "If you, even you, had known today the things which belong to your peace! But now, they are hidden from your eyes.

chokri: Kekrelamori pütüpü nce vorito, n ngomuko tekhü chüpü n hu püyito, de nbo yito, mu pükhüko metülü n-hu püyito.
english: For the days will come on you, when your enemies will throw up a barricade against you, surround you, hem you in on every side,

chokri: N khapü cejü mezü vato, mu n nuneko risa, side ketsü püpori kepishu pü bamotato; kekrelamori no nkelakeyi npe kevori semo ketala.
english: and will dash you and your children within you to the ground. They will not leave in you one stone on another, because you didn't know the time of your visitation."

chokri: Püta kereshücelü lüde mhazhu, mhakhri kebako hu metava.
english: He entered into the temple, and began to drive out those who bought and sold in it,

chokri: Ukoce poti, Ace pü kechoce thi keto ketho lesilü tho meta, vele neko süo thipü keregomiko keba cepü thi bate.
english: saying to them, "It is written, 'My house is a house of prayer,' but you have made it a 'den of robbers'!"

chokri: Pü tüsonha kereshücelü mimetha zo bata, vele thevo kezhoko, lesikeseko, thüma kezhomiko pü dokhriva keto rhü ba zota.
english: He was teaching daily in the temple, but the chief priests and the scribes and the leading men among the people sought to destroy him.

chokri: Uko pü, mejo keto phumedita; thümako püfü reni türü ba ketala.
english: They couldn't find what they might do, for all the people hung on to every word that he said.

chokri: Deba zho theranha, keshu rena, uko ta mhakhre lüvo, mharü kethenyüko püvo.
english: But on the first day of the week, at early dawn, they and some others came to the tomb, bringing the spices which they had prepared.

chokri: Uko ketsüo retu zü keta ngo; mhakhreyo.
english: They found the stone rolled away from the tomb.

chokri: Vele uko ta mhakhrelü kelüce themo-o ngo mota.
english: They entered in, and didn't find the Lord Jesus' body.

chokri: Mha süo la uko ve kedi thitha, sizi metha mi kena ufüne mevashuti khide uko khüce thatha.
english: It happened, while they were greatly perplexed about this, behold, two men stood by them in dazzling clothing.

chokri: Thenomiko kerepü uko züpüki cejü merüpü bata; vele uni ukoce poti, neko dipüla kesako dolü rhü ketao phuta? Pü hice ba mote, vele rhü süte.
english: Becoming terrified, they bowed their faces down to the earth. They said to them, "Why do you seek the living among the dead?

chokri: Pü nekoce kepoko rükra süte; pü süce Galilee lü bazolu tice.
english: He isn't here, but is risen. Remember what he told you when he was still in Galilee,

chokri: Thüma Nuo tüpü kephomako belü sü vato, püpü gu kevasa zhosü nha rhüla sütato.
english: saying that the Son of Man must be delivered up into the hands of sinful men, and be crucified, and the third day rise again?"

chokri: Süsa uko püjüko rekrayi.
english: They remembered his words,

chokri: Mhakhrelü uko salavori, uko mi keripü koce, mu kekreko metüce posü.
english: returned from the tomb, and told all these things to the eleven, and to all the rest.

chokri: Hilü, Mary Magdalene, Joana, Mary James zü, mu theno kekreko ri ta, kha apostleko koce posü.
english: Now they were Mary Magdalene, Joanna, and Mary the mother of James. The other women with them told these things to the apostles.

chokri: Vele jü süko ketho kemo pü bita, mu uko melü mota.
english: These words seemed to them to be nonsense, and they didn't believe them.

chokri: Vele Peter tasü de ta mhakhre lü vo, neküki pesü, pü linen fünheko lebi ngo, süla püta celü vorita, mhathi ketako püngota,
english: But Peter got up and ran to the tomb. Stooping and looking in, he saw the strips of linen lying by themselves, and he departed to his home, wondering what had happened.

chokri: Hihe, sünhasü mikena ta Emmaus cholü vozü, Jerusalem zü kemecuti mile thüna mesü.
english: Behold, two of them were going that very day to a village named Emmaus, which was sixty stadia from Jerusalem.

chokri: Uni ta kezhu mhathi ketako, cholü kepovozü.
english: They talked with each other about all of these things which had happened.

chokri: Uni mhakepo mu mhalü keta tülü Jisu ta unice vode uni züta.
english: It happened, while they talked and questioned together, that Jesus himself came near, and went with them.

chokri: Vele uni mhü thipü pü semova.
english: But their eyes were kept from recognizing him.

chokri: Pü unice poti, neni kedipü kepode tavo züla? Uni thayi de uni thüsu bata.
english: He said to them, "What are you talking about as you walk, and are sad?"

chokri: Süsa mipü püza Cleopas püfü kelasü, n lebi sojüsove thide Jerusalem lü ta zotho he, Jerusalem lü mhathi ketako sekemo?
english: One of them, named Cleopas, answered him, "Are you the only stranger in Jerusalem who doesn't know the things which have happened there in these days?"

chokri: Pü uni ketso sa de poti, mha kedipü la? Uni posala kesü, mha sükosü Jisu Nazareth mi keku mhatho Jihova mhüjülü mu thüma metüko mhüjülü sheri.
english: He said to them, "What things?" They said to him, "The things concerning Jesus, the Nazarene, who was a prophet mighty in deed and word before God and all the people;

chokri: Hako thevo kezhoko mu krütako pü khapü mejo vade cross yo gu vale.
english: and how the chief priests and our rulers delivered him up to be condemned to death, and crucified him.

chokri: Vele hako kelüce Isreal rhakeyi mitho zi bale; Ile, hizi kevasa, tha tüsozho süte.
english: But we were hoping that it was he who would redeem Israel. Yes, and besides all this, it is now the third day since these things happened.

chokri: Sülebi mori, hako krolü thenomiko hako kere süte; tsali rena uko ta mhakhrelü vo.
english: Also, certain women of our company amazed us, having arrived early at the tomb;

chokri: Uko pümo ngomo ta, sülü uko poti, tüyo choyimiko zümholü ngole zita; ukoce pü rhüla süte.
english: and when they didn't find his body, they came saying that they had also seen a vision of angels, who said that he was alive.

chokri: Hako kezü kebako humi tavo pe mhakhrelü, de thenoko kepo bi tü pote; vele pü ngomo.
english: Some of us went to the tomb, and found it just like the women had said, but they didn't see him."

chokri: Süsa pü unice poti, neko dithedü nyozoshi, mhase thophi, saju kepoko jü melü kemo.
english: He said to them, "Foolish men, and slow of heart to believe in all that the prophets have spoken!

chokri: Khrista kemezhe keti hiko, thisüzo mome, de pü zapozale lü lüketako?
english: Didn't the Christ have to suffer these things and to enter into his glory?"

chokri: Moses yo shudü vo saju kepo metüko ketho lesilü thopü kemetako po vacho vosü. Sükolü püju thopü kemetako.
english: Beginning from Moses and from all the prophets, he explained to them in all the Scriptures the things concerning himself.

chokri: Uko ta rünalüvoto kezülü meno vota, püta mecu (vazü) vota keto bisü.
english: They drew near to the village, where they were going, and he acted like he would go further.

chokri: Vele uni pü vakha sü de poti, thüva te, mu tüso vote, hice batate; mu pü unizü sülü bata tole lüta.
english: They urged him, saying, "Stay with us, for it is almost evening, and the day is almost over." He went in to stay with them.

chokri: Pü uni zü mhaketi miz yo kebace, pü tsükhri püyi de reliphri sü (kechothisü) mu cephro sü mu khapü uni tsü sü.
english: It happened, that when he had sat down at the table with them, he took the bread and gave thanks. Breaking it, he gave to them.

chokri: Uni mhüko khrisü, uni püseyi ta; mu pü uni mhüjülü ngo mota.
english: Their eyes were opened, and they recognized him, and he vanished out of their sight.

chokri: Uni hunehu cepoti, chodelü püzü ketace pü amice mhapo ketace avu melü lümü ta zomome? Pü ketholesikolü haphra de po kesü kolü?
english: They said one to another, "Weren't our hearts burning within us, while he spoke to us along the way, and while he opened the Scriptures to us?"

chokri: Pütü sülü unita Jerusalem lü la vori, mi keripüko sülü kengupü ba ngoyi, mu thüma kekreko sheri ba.
english: They rose up that very hour, returned to Jerusalem, and found the eleven gathered together, and those who were with them,

chokri: Uni poti, Niepoo rhüla süta kethozo, Simon sheri mengosü.
english: saying, "The Lord is risen indeed, and has appeared to Simon!"

chokri: Uni chodelü mhathi kesüko pü posü mu tsükhri cephro kesülü unipo püse keyi kopü posü.
english: They related the things that happened along the way, and how he was recognized by them in the breaking of the bread.

chokri: Uko side theju süko kepo kebalü Jisu pütho ta zolü uko dolü metsulü thapü ba ngoyita; de ukocepoti, thelü kemenü po neko yo bayite.
english: As they said these things, Jesus himself stood among them, and said to them, "Peace be to you."

chokri: Vele uko keresü de mpe bata, uko kelüce rüpüpü ngoti zotho side lü bata.
english: But they were terrified and filled with fear, and supposed that they had seen a spirit.

chokri: Pü ukoce poti, neko dipüla nekolü kemezheba? Neko melülü kedipü la mhalü bata?
english: He said to them, "Why are you troubled? Why do doubts arise in your hearts?

chokri: Hihe, abezi mu aphezi ko pesüte, I zole, anite (a jüsüte) de seyite; kekrelamori rüpü utrhi upho uroko bamo, neko kengoce I süko ba.
english: See my hands and my feet, that it is truly me. Touch me and see, for a spirit doesn't have flesh and bones, as you see that I have."

chokri: Pü jü süko po kesüsa pü bezini mu pü phezi ni pü uko mekasü.
english: When he had said this, he showed them his hands and his feet.

chokri: Uko rethu ba ketala melü rü bata, uko ngo thide, pü uko ce poti, neko ketico hu pü tame?
english: While they still didn't believe for joy, and wondered, he said to them, "Do you have anything here to eat?"

chokri: Uko fü kechü tipü kha pü tsüsü (mefijüsa).
english: They gave him a piece of a broiled fish and some honeycomb.

chokri: Pü süo püyi de pü tiyi uko mhüjülü.
english: He took them, and ate in front of them.

chokri: Pü uko cepoti, mhahiko I neko ce posü, I neko zü ketace, mha popü kemetako metü thitso sü ketola, Moses thezhokolü, saju kepoko mu Chelekolü ala thopü meta tiko.
english: He said to them, "This is what I told you, while I was still with you, that all things which are written in the law of Moses, the prophets, and the psalms, concerning me must be fulfilled."

chokri: Süsa pü khakhripü ukopo seyi, ukopo ketho lesiko seyi ketola.
english: Then he opened their minds, that they might understand the Scriptures.

chokri: Pü poti, Khrista kemezheto, mu sa keta zhosü nha rhüla sürito.
english: He said to them, "Thus it is written, and thus it was necessary for the Christ to suffer and to rise from the dead the third day,

chokri: Thelü rede keyi mu kejo vakesü Jerusalem lü poshüdü de shuye metülü püza pü posü ketola.
english: and that repentance and remission of sins should be preached in his name to all the nations, beginning at Jerusalem.

chokri: Neko mhahiko zhopo kozo.
english: You are witnesses of these things.

chokri: Hi he, apo jüko khapü neko ketsü I ketsüsüto, vele neko Jerusalem nadelü fü türü bate, mesolü kevori neko thi mekusü kemothe.
english: Behold, I send forth the promise of my Father on you. But wait in the city of Jerusalem until you are clothed with power from on high."

chokri: Pü uko chothazü Bethany ketso kho, mu pübe chopü uko yosü de jopü uko tsü.
english: He led them out as far as Bethany, and he lifted up his hands, and blessed them.

chokri: Pü jopü ukotsü kebace uko zü kekhota mu pü khipü tülü vota.
english: It happened, while he blessed them, that he withdrew from them, and was carried up into heaven.

chokri: Uko pü reshüsü, side uko nüshü mu rethu de ta sala Jerusalem lü vori ta.
english: They worshiped him, and returned to Jerusalem with great joy,

chokri: Tüsonha kereshücelü bazota, Jihova chu mu jopü putsü. Amen.
english: and were continually in the temple, praising and blessing God. Amen.

chokri: Süculü jüo tazo, Jüo Jihova züta, mu Jüo sü Jihova zo.
english: In the beginning was the Word, and the Word was with God, and the Word was God.

chokri: Shüdülü Jihova zü tazo.
english: The same was in the beginning with God.

chokri: Mhametüko pütho thisü, mhathi kesüko püpo thikemo püridi.
english: All things were made through him. Without him was not anything made that has been made.

chokri: Pülü kerhü ba, mu kerhü süo thüma keveo zo.
english: In him was life, and the life was the light of men.

chokri: Keveo kezü vamve tayo, kezüno süo seyi mote.
english: The light shines in the darkness, and the darkness hasn't overcome it.

chokri: Thümapü Jihova pü ketsüsü, püza John.
english: There came a man, sent from God, whose name was John.

chokri: Thüma süo zhopola vori, Keveo zhopo kethila; size thüma metüko püyo melüyi ketola.
english: The same came as a witness, that he might testify about the light, that all might believe through him.

chokri: Püno Keveo mo, vele Keveo zhopo sü ketola ketsü kesüo.
english: He was not the light, but was sent that he might testify about the light.

chokri: Keve kethoo sü thüma metü vamve sü ketola vori.
english: The true light that enlightens everyone was coming into the world.

chokri: Pü cejüo thisü mu cejülü vori, mu cejüo püsemo.
english: He was in the world, and the world was made through him, and the world didn't recognize him.

chokri: Pütho pücejülü vorizori, püzhüko püse mota.
english: He came to his own, and those who were his own didn't receive him.

chokri: Vele püzü keyikhü metü pü süko khipü Jihova nuneko thiyi keve keku kha tsüsü, püza melü keyiko metü.
english: But as many as received him, to them he gave the right to become God's children, to those who believe in his name:

chokri: Menu keyiko uzalü mozori, mori uthiupho medolü mozori, mori thüma medolü rimo vele Jihova medolü.
english: who were born not of blood, nor of the will of the flesh, nor of the will of man, but of God.

chokri: Jüo po uthi meve vori, pü udolü hü, hako püngo, zapozale Poo nu püyo ngokemezhe mu kethopo chupü.
english: The Word became flesh, and lived among us. We saw his glory, such glory as of the one and only Son of the Father, full of grace and truth.

chokri: John pü zhopo thi vori, se de poti, Hiohi püzo I poketao, Pü asa vori vele pü amhüjü tazo.
english: John testified about him. He cried out, saying, "This was he of whom I said, 'He who comes after me has surpassed me, for he was before me.'"

chokri: Püyo kekhrü chupü upo ngokemezhe kengo sari ngoyi.
english: From his fullness we all received grace upon grace.

chokri: Kekrelamori Moses thezho khapü utsü, vele ngokemezhe mu kejütho Jisu Khrista celü vori.
english: For the law was given through Moses. Grace and truth came through Jesus Christ.

chokri: Mipüri Jihova sü ngomo. Süosü Nuo pümelülü kebaozo. Püpü mi mese kesüo.
english: No one has seen God at any time. The one and only Son, who is in the bosom of the Father, he has declared him.

chokri: John zhopo kethi hiohi, thevoko mu Levimiko Jerusalem lü mi ketsüpü pü ketso, “No sopüa?”
english: This is John's testimony, when the Jews sent priests and Levites from Jerusalem to ask him, "Who are you?"

chokri: Pü ukoce potü süzo, pü mela mozo; vele phra sü, I Khrista mole.
english: He confessed, and didn't deny, but he confessed, "I am not the Christ."

chokri: Uko pü ketso ti, Süsa no sopü? No Elijah me? Pü poti I Elija mole. No saju kepo mime? Pü uko fü kela de, I süri mole.
english: They asked him, "What then? Are you Elijah?" He said, "I am not." "Are you the Prophet?" He answered, "No."

chokri: Süsa uko püce poti, No sopü ta? Hako ketsü kesükoce jü püvo posü ketola. No sopüle zide poya?
english: They said therefore to him, "Who are you? Give us an answer to take back to those who sent us. What do you say about yourself?"

chokri: Pü po kesü, I füphrapü rayholü se ketao süle, Niepoo chode thi kemezi yite, side Isaiah popü meta tio.
english: He said, "I am the voice of one crying in the wilderness, 'Make straight the way of the Lord,' as Isaiah the prophet said."

chokri: Uko ketsü kesüko sü Pharisee mikozo.
english: The ones who had been sent were from the Pharisees.

chokri: Uko pü ketso de püce poti, Mhace no Khrista mo lize dipüla jülo khapü mi tsüya, Elijah ri mo mu Isaiah rimo mori saju kepomi rimo lize?
english: They asked him, "Why then do you baptize, if you are not the Christ, nor Elijah, nor the Prophet?"

chokri: John uko fü kela kesü, I jülü jülo khasüyo, vele mipü neko dolü tha neko pü semo.
english: John answered them, "I baptize in water, but among you stands one whom you don't know.

chokri: Pü süozo, pü asalü vori zü, pü amhüjü tazo; pü pheku kepharo ri I phrü shümo.
english: He is the one who comes after me, who is preferred before me, whose sandal strap I'm not worthy to loosen."

chokri: Mha sükosü kethi Jordan khüco Bethany lü John jülo kha sü kebalü thisü.
english: These things were done in Bethany beyond the Jordan, where John was baptizing.

chokri: Süsanha Jisu ta püce vori kezü ngo de John poti, Hihe, Jihova Konuo, Cejüo kephoma khi tata ketoo.
english: The next day, he saw Jesus coming to him, and said, "Behold, the Lamb of God, who takes away the sin of the world!

chokri: Thüma hiohi I pü zhopo thi ketao süzo, Asaco vori kezüo amhüjü ketao, kekrelamori pü a mhüjü tazo tila.
english: This is he of whom I said, 'After me comes a man who is preferred before me, for he was before me.'

chokri: I pü semo, vele püpü Israel miko mesesü, süla I jülo khapü mitsü tayo.
english: I didn't know him, but for this reason I came baptizing in water: that he would be revealed to Israel."

chokri: John zhopo kha kesü, I tülü Kemesa Rüpü kiri kezü mekhropü bi mu taki püyo ba yita.
english: John testified, saying, "I have seen the Spirit descending like a dove out of heaven, and it remained on him.

chokri: I pü semo, vele pü aketsüpü jülo kha mitsü, pü ace poti, No Kemesa Rüpü kiri kezü ngoto, de püyo ba yito, süosü Kemesa Rüpü lü jülo khapü mi tsüto.
english: I didn't recognize him, but he who sent me to baptize in water, he said to me, 'On whomever you will see the Spirit descending, and remaining on him, the same is he who baptizes in the Holy Spirit.'

chokri: I süo ngo, süo zhopo khasüyo, hiohi Jihova Nuo zo.
english: I have seen, and have testified that this is the Son of God."

chokri: Süsanha John pü sajimi kena püzü tha kebace.
english: Again, the next day, John was standing with two of his disciples,

chokri: Jisu chota keba peba, Pesüte, Jihova Konuo hi.
english: and he looked at Jesus as he walked, and said, "Behold, the Lamb of God!"

chokri: Pü sajini mikena pü kepo sesü de Jisu sa meji ta.
english: The two disciples heard him speak, and they followed Jesus.

chokri: Jisu regüsü uni pe süri uni püsa meju kezü ngosü, de Jisu uni ketsoti, neni kedipü phuzü ze? Uni püce poti Rabbi (püco kemethao) No dipüce hü ba?
english: Jesus turned, and saw them following, and said to them, "What are you looking for?" They said to him, "Rabbi" (which is to say, being interpreted, Teacher), "where are you staying?"

chokri: Pü uni cepoti, Tavori pete. Uni tavo pü keba thethi pe de püzü bata sünhasü, süce keba mesü ketala.
english: He said to them, "Come, and see." They came and saw where he was staying, and they stayed with him that day. It was about the tenth hour.

chokri: Uni mipü John kepo fü keseo sü Andrew, Simon Peter sazüo.
english: One of the two who heard John, and followed him, was Andrew, Simon Peter's brother.

chokri: Pü jürao Simon ngoyi de püce poti, hani Messiah sü ngo yite, püco khrista-Greekmi jülü kenhü keyi kedeo.
english: He first found his own brother, Simon, and said to him, "We have found the Messiah!" (which is, being interpreted, Christ).

chokri: Mu pü püjürao Simon züvo Jisu cesü. Jisu püpesü de poti, no Simon Jonah nu ncüpü Cephas püco ketsü thi tato.
english: He brought him to Jesus. Jesus looked at him, and said, "You are Simon the son of Jonah. You shall be called Cephas" (which is by interpretation, Peter).

chokri: Süsanha Jisu ta Galilee lü ki keto lüta. Cholü Philip zü kesüyi de püce poti, Asa meji yite.
english: On the next day, he was determined to go out into Galilee, and he found Philip. Jesus said to him, "Follow me."

chokri: Philip no Bethsaida lü kevorimi, Andrew mu Peter ni kehü nadelü.
english: Now Philip was from Bethsaida, of the city of Andrew and Peter.

chokri: Philip no Nathanael ngoyi, de püce poti, Hako püngo yite Moses thezho lesilü mu saju kepoko thopü kemeta Jisu Nazareth mi, Joseph nu.
english: Philip found Nathanael, and said to him, "We have found him, of whom Moses in the law, and the prophets, wrote: Jesus of Nazareth, the son of Joseph."

chokri: Nathanael püce poti, Nazareth lü mhakeve hu voriyi veme? Philip püce po de Tavori pete.
english: Nathanael said to him, "Can any good thing come out of Nazareth?" Philip said to him, "Come and see."

chokri: Jisu Nathanael püce vori kezü ngosü, de poti, Hihe, Israel mi kejo kedipü.
english: Jesus saw Nathanael coming to him, and said about him, "Behold, an Israelite indeed, in whom is no deceit!"

chokri: Nathanael püce pode, kedi kethice no ase? Jisu püce poti, Philip nkecü mhüjü, no chüdü bo phelü kebace, I nngole.
english: Nathanael said to him, "How do you know me?" Jesus answered him, "Before Philip called you, when you were under the fig tree, I saw you."

chokri: Nathanael püjü kelade poti, no Jihova Nu zole, Rabbi, no Israel mi Kedeo.
english: Nathanael answered him, "Rabbi, you are the Son of God! You are King of Israel!"

chokri: Jisu püce pode, I chüdü bo phelü nngole si kesüla no melü teme? No süceri zhoku ti ngoto.
english: Jesus answered him, "Because I told you, 'I saw you underneath the fig tree,' do you believe? You will see greater things than these!"

chokri: Püce posa kesü, I nce potü süto, no tüyo khri kesü ngoto hisa, tüyo choyi miko Nuo yo kho mu ki ketha ngoto.
english: He said to him, "Most assuredly, I tell you, hereafter you will see heaven opened, and the angels of God ascending and descending on the Son of Man."

chokri: Pharisee mipü püza Nicodemus lesi de Jews miko kekha tayo.
english: Now there was a man of the Pharisees named Nicodemus, a ruler of the Jews.

chokri: Thüma süo tüzülü ta Jisu ce vori de püce poti, Rabbi, hako se keba no Jihova celü kevori kemetha mipü tazo; mipüri no mha ungo thi kesüko thi keyidi, Jihova püzü ta kemosa.
english: The same came to him by night, and said to him, "Rabbi, we know that you are a teacher come from God, for no one can do these signs that you do, unless God is with him."

chokri: Jisu püfü kelakesü, Kethozo, I nce potü süto, thümapü menu kesala kemosa pü Jihova kede ngo yiho.
english: Jesus answered him, "Most assuredly, I tell you, unless one is born anew, he can't see the Kingdom of God."

chokri: Nicodemus püce pola kesü, Mipü pethi ketasa kedipü bide menu kesala yi veta? Pü sala püzu valü lüde va kena menu tome?
english: Nicodemus said to him, "How can a man be born when he is old? Can he enter a second time into his mother's womb, and be born?"

chokri: Jisu püce poti, I nce potü süto, thümapü jü mu Rüpülü menu yi kemosa pü Jihova kede balü lü yiho.
english: Jesus answered, "Most assuredly I tell you, unless one is born of water and spirit, he can't enter into the Kingdom of God!

chokri: Uthi upho kemenu sü uthi upho zo, Rüpülü kemenu sü rüpüzo.
english: That which is born of the flesh is flesh. That which is born of the Spirit is spirit.

chokri: Nngo hite, I nce kepo No menu kesala moze su zi kesüla.
english: Don't marvel that I said to you, 'You must be born anew.'

chokri: Tükhra pütho pümedora khra votayo, no püfü seto, vele dirapü khra vori sheri no seyomo, mu khra dipüce vota sheri seriho, sübi kehopü sheri Rüpülü kemenu sü bizo.
english: The wind blows where it wants to, and you hear its sound, but don't know where it comes from and where it is going. So is everyone who is born of the Spirit."

chokri: Nicodemus püce pode, Dipü bide sizi tata?
english: Nicodemus answered him, "How can these things be?"

chokri: Jisu püce poti, No Israel mi kemetha mipü, sizide mha süko se mola?
english: Jesus answered him, "Are you the teacher of Israel, and don't understand these things?

chokri: I nce potü süto, hako ha kese kengoko poyo, mu kengo süko zhopo thiyo, neko zhopo süko melü yomo.
english: Most assuredly I tell you, we speak that which we know, and testify of that which we have seen, and you don't receive our witness.

chokri: Mhace I cejülü ju nekoce posü zori neko melü mo lize, I tülü ju neko ce pota tisa neko dipübi melü yiveta?
english: If I told you earthly things and you don't believe, how will you believe if I tell you heavenly things?

chokri: Thüma püri tüyo khomo zolu, vele pü tüyo kiri, thüma Nuo tüyo tayo.
english: No one has ascended into heaven, but he who descended out of heaven, the Son of Man, who is in heaven.

chokri: Moses nhatsalü tenhi pü kesübi, thüma Nuo ri püsü zoto.
english: As Moses lifted up the serpent in the wilderness, even so must the Son of Man be lifted up,

chokri: Kehopü sheri pü melü keyiko kata hozo, vele kechothe kerhü ngo yito.
english: that whoever believes in him should not perish, but have eternal life.

chokri: Jihova cejüo khrüthori zode pü Nu pule-o kha süzo, thüma kehopüri pü melü keyimi shütaho, vele kechothe kerhü ngo yito.
english: For God so loved the world, that he gave his one and only Son, that whoever believes in him should not perish, but have eternal life.

chokri: Kekrelamori Jihova pü Nu ketsüpü cejüo mejo va ketola mo, vele cejüo po püyo kelayi ketola.
english: For God didn't send his Son into the world to judge the world, but that the world should be saved through him.

chokri: Pü melü keyimi sümi mejo vaho, vele melü kemomi tsüri mejo vatazo, kekrelamori pü Jihova khakesü Nuo melü mo ketala.
english: He who believes in him is not judged. He who doesn't believe has been judged already, because he has not believed in the name of the one and only Son of God.

chokri: Kemejo süosü hihi, keveo cejülü vorite, thümako kezü unüku keveo ceri, kekrelamori uko mhathoko kesu tila.
english: This is the judgment, that the light has come into the world, and men loved the darkness rather than the light; for their works were evil.

chokri: Kekrelamori mhakesu kethiko keveo retsüyo, mori kevelü vori yomo, umhathoko mengo sümo ketola.
english: For everyone who does evil hates the light, and doesn't come to the light, lest his works would be exposed.

chokri: Vele ketholü hü ketako keveo ce voriyo, pü mhathoko mengosü ketola, uko mhathoko Jihova medolü thi tila.
english: But he who does the truth comes to the light, that his works may be revealed, that they have been done in God."

chokri: Hisalü Jisu mu püsajiko ta Judea cejülü vota, pü sülü bade mipü jülompü sü.
english: After these things, Jesus came with his disciples into the land of Judea. He stayed there with them, and baptized.

chokri: John sheri Aenon lü Salim kemenoce mipü jülompü ba, süce jü kekro ba tila, uko ta vori de jülo püyi.
english: John also was baptizing in Enon near Salim, because there was much water there. They came, and were baptized.

chokri: Kekrelamori John tüpü thejü thi molu süce.
english: For John was not yet thrown into prison.

chokri: John sajiko mu Jews miko dolü mhakebu ta, uthi kemesa keyi zhola.
english: There arose therefore a questioning on the part of John's disciples with some Jews about purification.

chokri: Uko ta John cevode pü ketsoti, Rabbi, pü nzü Jordan khüco ketao no pü zhopo thi kebao, Hihe, thüma metü ta püce vota züle.
english: They came to John, and said to him, "Rabbi, he who was with you beyond the Jordan, to whom you have testified, behold, the same baptizes, and everyone is coming to him."

chokri: John ukofü kelakesü, Thümapü utho mhapüri thiyiho, tüyo khapü pütsüsü kemosa.
english: John answered, "A man can receive nothing, unless it has been given him from heaven.

chokri: Neko akepo füko seba, süko zhopoko nekozo, A kepo, I Khrista mo, vele pü mhüjülü a ketsü kesüo.
english: You yourselves testify that I said, 'I am not the Christ,' but, 'I have been sent before him.'

chokri: Pü zhosapü yi ketio sü zhosao zo, vele pü khrethoko püzüba de püfüko se reni pünü rechü bayo, zhosao fü kesela, hiohi akenü mu thitso süte.
english: He who has the bride is the bridegroom; but the friend of the bridegroom, who stands and hears him, rejoices greatly because of the bridegroom's voice. This, my joy, therefore is made full.

chokri: Pü zho mama ta mozesu, mu I tsü (ni) mama ta moze su.
english: He must increase, but I must decrease.

chokri: Pü mesoyo kiri ketio metüko pi, pü cejülü vori lize cejü zo, cejülü jüko poyo, tüyo kevori yosü metüko pi.
english: He who comes from above is above all. He who is from the Earth belongs to the Earth, and speaks of the Earth. He who comes from heaven is above all.

chokri: Pü se mu ngo tiko posüzo sheri thüma püri melüyi mote.
english: What he has seen and heard, of that he testifies; and no one receives his witness.

chokri: Pü zhopo melü keyimi Jihova sü kethozo ti tha süyo.
english: He who has received his witness has set his seal to this, that God is true.

chokri: Kekrelamori Jihova ketsü kesüo Jihova jüko po süyo, Jihova Kemesa Rüpü mede kemo kha pü tsüsü.
english: For he whom God has sent speaks the words of God; for God gives the Spirit without measure.

chokri: Po no Nuo khrü, mha metü khapü pübelü süte.
english: The Father loves the Son, and has given all things into his hand.

chokri: Pü Nuo melü keyimi kechothe kehü ngo yite, somi Nuo melü mota sheri kehü ngo yi ho, vele Jihova lekhu püyo ba tayo.
english: One who believes in the Son has eternal life, but one who disobeys the Son won't see life, but the wrath of God remains on him."

chokri: Hitüce Jisu sekeyi, Phariseeko “Jisu sajiko jülopü miketsü John ceri kroku bate” siti seba keta seyi.
english: Therefore when the Lord knew that the Pharisees had heard that Jesus was making and baptizing more disciples than John

chokri: Jisu thono jülopü mitsümo vele püsajiko thozo.
english: (although Jesus himself didn't baptize, but his disciples),

chokri: Pü Judea lü tasü Galelee lü kita.
english: he left Judea, and departed into Galilee.

chokri: Pü ta Samaria lü vomo suta.
english: He needed to pass through Samaria.

chokri: Süsa pü ta Samaria nade pülü voyi, süo cüpü Sychar siyo, thethi süo Jacob popü pünu Joseph tsükesü kemenoce ba.
english: So he came to a city of Samaria, called Sychar, near the parcel of ground that Jacob gave to his son, Joseph.

chokri: Jacob jüfü süce ba. Jisu revü ho tata, süla jüfü khüyo ba; süce keba shurü (baro) ta.
english: Jacob's well was there. Jesus therefore, being tired from his journey, sat down by the well. It was about the sixth hour.

chokri: Thenomipü sülü jü püto vori, Jisu püce “jüpü apo krayite” zisü.
english: A woman of Samaria came to draw water. Jesus said to her, "Give me a drink."

chokri: Pü sajiko ta rünalü vo ketico khri tole vota sütüce.
english: For his disciples had gone away into the city to buy food.

chokri: Samaria pü püce pode, dipü bide sita, no Jew mipü, apo jüpü nmekra sü no ti, I Samaria mipüpü zotila? Kekrelamori Jews miko Samaria miko züde lütilüle thi yomo.
english: The Samaritan woman therefore said to him, "How is it that you, being a Jew, ask for a drink from me, a Samaritan woman?" (For Jews have no dealings with Samaritans.)

chokri: Jisu püfü kelade poti, No mhace Jihova khakesü se lize, sopüno nce kepo “jüpü amekra süte” ziba sheri, no püce mhacho metse, size pü kerhü jü khapü ntsü süto.
english: Jesus answered her, "If you knew the gift of God, and who it is who says to you, 'Give me a drink,' you would have asked him, and he would have given you living water."

chokri: Thenopü püce poti, Kezhoo no mhapüri khipü jüthu keto bamo, sizomoli jüfü mesü (relü); dipüce no kerhü jü bata?
english: The woman said to him, "Sir, you have nothing to draw with, and the well is deep. From where then have you that living water?

chokri: No hapotsa Jacob ce zhokume, pü jüfü hio kha hako tsüsü, pü sheri jü hiokra mu pü nuneko sheri mu pü cüriko sheri kra?
english: Are you greater than our father, Jacob, who gave us the well, and drank of it himself, as did his children, and his cattle?"

chokri: Jisu püfü kelade poti, thüma kehopüri jü hio kekrami theri sala tato.
english: Jesus answered her, "Everyone who drinks of this water will thirst again,

chokri: Vele I jü khapü tsü kesüo kra keyimi theri taho, vele khapü ketsü jüo kerhü jüfü pü thide rüla sü kechothe kerhülü vo tato.
english: but whoever drinks of the water that I will give him will never thirst again; but the water that I will give him will become in him a well of water springing up to eternal life."

chokri: Thenopü püce pode, Kezhoo, jü süo khapü atsü süte, side apo theri samo ketola, hice vori jüpü mo ketola.
english: The woman said to him, "Sir, give me this water, so that I don't get thirsty, neither come all the way here to draw."

chokri: Jisu püce pode, Tavo nnopu cüpü hice vorite.
english: Jesus said to her, "Go, call your husband, and come here."

chokri: Thenopü püfü kela kesü, I anopu bamole. Jisu püce poti, No po meve shu süzo, I anopu ba mole sikesüsü.
english: The woman answered, "I have no husband." Jesus said to her, "You said well, 'I have no husband,'

chokri: Kekrelamori no nnopu püngu bave, tsühi nzü kebao ri nnopu kethomo, süla no potü süzo.
english: for you have had five husbands; and he whom you now have is not your husband. This you have said truly."

chokri: Thenopü püce poti, Kezhoo, akelüce no saju kepomi mipü te thohe.
english: The woman said to him, "Sir, I perceive that you are a prophet.

chokri: Hapotsaneko cejütu hiolü reshü vori, neko kepono Jerusalem lü thümako kereshü kethi mesü liyo.
english: Our fathers worshiped in this mountain, and you Jews say that in Jerusalem is the place where people ought to worship."

chokri: Jisu thenopü cekepo, Thenopü, melü yite, pütü süo vori bate, neko cejütuyo ri mo mori Jerusalem lü rimo de Poo reshü tato.
english: Jesus said to her, "Woman, believe me, the hour comes, when neither in this mountain, nor in Jerusalem, will you worship the Father.

chokri: Neko reshü keta semode reshüyo, hako sede reshüyo, kekrelamori kelakeyi sü Jews miko la.
english: You worship that which you don't know. We worship that which we know; for salvation is from the Jews.

chokri: Vele pütü vori bate, tsühi te, reshü ketho kethi miko ketho mu rüpü lü reshü tato; kekrelamori Poo reshü sübi timi phu tayo.
english: But the hour comes, and now is, when the true worshippers will worship the Father in spirit and truth, for the Father seeks such to be his worshippers.

chokri: Jihova sü Rüpü zo, ketho mu rüpülü pü reshü moze su.
english: God is spirit, and those who worship him must worship in spirit and truth."

chokri: Thenopü püce poti, Messiah vori keto I seba, süosü Khrista, pü vori lize mha metüko pü hako ce posü tole siba.
english: The woman said to him, "I know that Messiah comes," (he who is called Christ). "When he has come, he will declare to us all things."

chokri: Jisu püce poti, I nzü kerethi keba I süo zo.
english: Jesus said to her, "I am he, the one who speaks to you."

chokri: Hizi keba tülü pü metha keyiko tavorita, uko vori ngo keyi pü theno mipü zü kerethi keba la uko ngota, siri mipüri no dipü phuba mori no dipü la thenopü zü kerethi ba? zimo.
english: At this, his disciples came. They marveled that he was speaking with a woman; yet no one said, "What are you looking for?" or, "Why do you speak with her?"

chokri: Thenopü süsa pü lekhu khava de tata, pü cholü ta nadelü vota, de thümako ce posü.
english: So the woman left her water pot, and went away into the city, and said to the people,

chokri: Tavorite, de thümapü pete, pü I thi keva aju metüko pü ace po süte, pü Khrista ta motho?
english: "Come, see a man who told me everything that I did. Can this be the Christ?"

chokri: Süsa thümako nadelü prari de ta püce vori.
english: They went out of the city, and were coming to him.

chokri: Sizi keba tülü pü metha keyiko püpo mhati yite, Kemethao, side poba.
english: In the meanwhile, the disciples urged him, saying, "Rabbi, eat."

chokri: Vele pü ukoce poti, I ketico neko se kemo püba.
english: But he said to them, "I have food to eat that you don't know about."

chokri: Süla metha keyiko hunehu ce ketsode humi ketico püvo püpo tino pütsü süme?
english: The disciples therefore said one to another, "Has anyone brought him something to eat?"

chokri: Jisu ukoce poti, A ketico sü apo aketsü kesüo nü thiti, mu süo thitso kesüo zo.
english: Jesus said to them, "My food is to do the will of him who sent me, and to accomplish his work.

chokri: Tekhri dasa rece vori yo siti neko poyo mome? I neko ce kepo, Tosü nmhü khrüpü pete, yiko müpü rüve züte.
english: Don't you say, 'There are yet four months until the harvest?' Behold, I tell you, lift up your eyes, and look at the fields, that they are white for harvest already.

chokri: Yi kerümi ri uzhokhro ngo yiyo, useko püvo kechothe kehülü kengu yito, side tsava miri mu kerü miri kezü unü tayo.
english: He who reaps receives wages, and gathers fruit to eternal life; that both he who sows and he who reaps may rejoice together.

chokri: Poketa jü hio kethozo, mipü so süto, mu kekreo rü yito.
english: For in this the saying is true, 'One sows, and another reaps.'

chokri: I neko ketsüpü rüyi nosü neko sosü kemora, kekremi mhatho kethi lü neko ta mhatho sülü lüzü.
english: I sent you to reap that for which you haven't labored. Others have labored, and you have entered into their labor."

chokri: Samaritan nade sülü thüma kekro theno süpü kepola pü melüyi, pü zhopo thikesü, pü I thikeva metüpü ace posü.
english: From that city many of the Samaritans believed in him because of the word of the woman, who testified, "He told me everything that I did."

chokri: Süla Samaritan miko ta püce vori de püpo uko zü bano chota, süla pü sülü zho kena bata.
english: So when the Samaritans came to him, they begged him to stay with them. He stayed there two days.

chokri: Thüma kekro melüyi kekrelamori püjü la.
english: Many more believed because of his word.

chokri: Uko thenopü ce poti, Tsühi hako ri pü melüte, nkepola zo mori hakotho püfü seyi ketala, Khrista tazo ti seyite, cejü kelakeyi yozo.
english: They said to the woman, "Now we believe, not because of your speaking; for we have heard for ourselves, and know that this is indeed the Christ, the Savior of the world."

chokri: Zho kena salü pü ta Galilee lü kita.
english: After the two days he went out from there and went into Galilee.

chokri: Kekrelamori Jisu püju kepo, saju kepomi pütho pü rünalü thürho bamo.
english: For Jesus himself testified that a prophet has no honor in his own country.

chokri: Pü ta Galilee lü kekice Galileans miko pü züyi, pü Jerusalem lü thenyelü mhathi kesüko ngo tila, kekrelamori uko thenyelü vo tila.
english: So when he came into Galilee, the Galileans received him, having seen all the things that he did in Jerusalem at the feast, for they also went to the feast.

chokri: Süla Jisu ta Galilee rüna Cana lü vori, pü jü khipü hezo thi kesülü. Capernaunlü kezhomipü nu mhathi züde thi kesürho sü.
english: Jesus came therefore again to Cana of Galilee, where he made the water into wine. There was a certain nobleman whose son was sick at Capernaum.

chokri: Pü Jisu Judea lü tasü Galilee lü vo kezü seyi, süla ta püce vori, de püpo ta püce vono chota, pünu thi kesürho sü ketola, kekrelamori pü sata keto tace ba ketala.
english: When he heard that Jesus had come out of Judea into Galilee, he went to him, and begged him that he would come down and heal his son, for he was at the point of death.

chokri: Süla Jisu püce po kesü, neko zasi mu ungo mhathoko ngozo kemosa melü yomo,
english: Jesus therefore said to him, "Unless you see signs and wonders, you will in no way believe."

chokri: Kezhoo püce pode, Kemethao, anu saketa mhüjü ta kiri zote.
english: The nobleman said to him, "Sir, come down before my child dies."

chokri: Jisu püce pode Tavo tate, nnu rhü yito. Thümao Jisu püce kepo fü melüyi de ta vota.
english: Jesus said to him, "Go your way. Your son lives." The man believed the word that Jesus spoke to him, and he went his way.

chokri: Pü ta cholü ki kezüce, pü choyi miko tavori püzü kesü, de püce po kesü, Nnu rhü yite.
english: As he was now going down, his servants met him and reported, saying "Your child lives!"

chokri: Süsa pü ukoce ketsoti, pütü kedipüce pü sürho vorita sheri. Uko püce po kesü, ndo keba thünace (ba püce) kethi dite.
english: So he inquired of them the hour when he began to get better. They said therefore to him, "Yesterday at the seventh hour, the fever left him."

chokri: Süla püpo sekeyi, pütü sü mesüce Jisu püce kepo, Nnu rhü yito, zisü. Püri mu pücekro metü melüyi.
english: So the father knew that it was at that hour in which Jesus said to him, "Your son lives." He believed, as did his whole house.

chokri: Mhatho hio Jisu ungo mhatho thikesü kenao, Jisu Judea lü ta Galilee lü vo kezüce.
english: This is again the second sign that Jesus did, having come out of Judea into Galilee.

chokri: Cukevo nye mhüjü zho shurü kebalü Jisu ta Bethany lü kho, Lazarus celü, püthi merhü kesüo.
english: Then six days before the Passover, Jesus came to Bethany, where Lazarus was, who had been dead, whom he raised from the dead.

chokri: Uko theva-o pümesoyi de püpo mhatiyi, Martha rena püreyisü, vele Lazarus uko dolü mipü püzü miz yo mhati ba.
english: So they made him a supper there. Martha served, but Lazarus was one of those who sat at the table with him.

chokri: Mary tirü püma kerü (spikenard) pound pü lapü Jisu phezi kenhüsü de püpitha pü süva; mharü kethenyü süo celü metü chu bata.
english: Mary, therefore, took a pound of ointment of pure nard, very precious, and anointed the feet of Jesus, and wiped his feet with her hair. The house was filled with the fragrance of the ointment.

chokri: Süsa püsajiko dolü mipü Judas Iscariot, Simon nu hisa pü tüsü ketoo poti,
english: Then Judas Iscariot, Simon's son, one of his disciples, who would betray him, said,

chokri: Tirü kenhü ketio zhupü pence kra kena khriyi mano, de khapü kedimiko tsüsü mano?
english: "Why wasn't this ointment sold for three hundred denarii, and given to the poor?"

chokri: Hio kepo, pü kedimi kekhrü lamo, vele pü keregomi tila, pü presa lekho khiyo, mu presa rego tayo tila.
english: Now he said this, not because he cared for the poor, but because he was a thief, and having the money box, used to steal what was put into it.

chokri: Süla Jisu poti, “Thenopü kebü hite, pü akekhrüne kesü kesüla zo.
english: But Jesus said, "Leave her alone. She has kept this for the day of my burial.

chokri: Kekrelamori neko kedimi züta süto, vele neko azü ta süho.”
english: For you always have the poor with you, but you don't always have me."

chokri: Jews miko kekro tavori, pü süce kebala, Jisu lebi la zo mori, Lazarus sheri pe ketola Jisu pü sa ketalü phü merhü kesüla.
english: A large crowd therefore of the Jews learned that he was there, and they came, not for Jesus' sake only, but that they might see Lazarus also, whom he had raised from the dead.

chokri: Vele thevo kezhoko mharhü Lazarus sheri mesa va keto rhüta.
english: But the chief priests conspired to put Lazarus to death also,

chokri: Kekrelamori püla Jews miko kekro sheri ta ketala, mu Jisu melüta.
english: because on account of him many of the Jews went away and believed in Jesus.

chokri: Süsanha thüma kekro ta thenyelü vori, uko Jisu ta Jerusalem lü lü kezü sekeyice.
english: On the next day a great multitude had come to the feast. When they heard that Jesus was coming to Jerusalem,

chokri: Uko revüniko thüpü mu sü kemejo coko rhopü, de püzü kesüto vori; uko se rekrü de Hosanna, Thejo kepütho Israelmi kedeo, Niepoo zalü vori kezüo.
english: they took the branches of the palm trees, and went out to meet him, and cried out, "Hosanna! Blessed is he who comes in the name of the Lord, the King of Israel!"

chokri: Jisu gadanu püyo bayi, thopü kemeta bide.
english: Jesus, having found a young donkey, sat on it. As it is written,

chokri: Mpe hite, Zion nupü, Hihe nkedeo gadanu (tholi) püyo vori züho.
english: "Don't be afraid, daughter of Zion. Behold, your King comes, sitting on a donkey's colt."

chokri: Mhahiko methakeyiko therace se bamo, vele Jisu zapozale thi kevasa uko sa lü vorita, hiko metü püju thopü meta tila, uko mhatho hiko thipü püyo sü.
english: His disciples didn't understand these things at first, but when Jesus was glorified, then they remembered that these things were written about him, and that they had done these things to him.

chokri: Thüma püzü ketako mha hiko Lazarus mhakhrelü phü merhü kesü ngo mu seba.
english: The multitude therefore that was with him when he called Lazarus out of the tomb, and raised him from the dead, was testifying about it.

chokri: Pü mha hiko ungo mhatho thi kesüla thümako tavo püpe toli vori.
english: For this cause also the multitude went and met him, because they heard that he had done this sign.

chokri: Pharisee miko uko dolü kepoti, neko mhapüri thiyi mo keta lü bame? Hihe, cejü metü püsa meji tazü.
english: The Pharisees therefore said among themselves, "See how you accomplish nothing. Behold, the world has gone after him."

chokri: Thenyelü vori de reshü thi ketoko humi Greekmi.
english: Now there were certain Greeks among those that went up to worship at the feast.

chokri: Thüma süko ta Bethsaidalü Philip cevori, Galilee lü, püce poti hako Jisu penyü tale.
english: These, therefore, came to Philip, who was from Bethsaida of Galilee, and asked him, saying, "Sir, we want to see Jesus."

chokri: Philip tavo Andrew ce posü, süsa Andrew mu Philip ni kezüvo Jisu ce posü.
english: Philip came and told Andrew, and in turn, Andrew came with Philip, and they told Jesus.

chokri: Jisu unice pokesü, pütü (bavüdo) vori bate, thüma Nuo zapozale thita keto sü.
english: Jesus answered them, "The time has come for the Son of Man to be glorified.

chokri: I nekoce potü süto, teha retu cejü tsü de sata kemosa süo pü lebi ba tayo, vele süo mhace sata süze püse kekro zhüyi yo.
english: Most assuredly I tell you, unless a grain of wheat falls into the earth and dies, it remains by itself alone. But if it dies, it bears much fruit.

chokri: Somino pü kerhüro khrüta sheri katato, mu sopüno cejü hilü pü kerhüro khrümo (ngometsü) tato sheri kechothe kehü ngoyito.
english: He who loves his life will lose it. He who hates his life in this world will keep it to eternal life.

chokri: Mhace humi achoyi thiyo lize, püpo asa meji yite; I dipüce ba sheri püpo sülü ba yito: mhace humi achoyi thiyo lize a Po pü rho süto.
english: If anyone serves me, let him follow me. Where I am, there will my servant also be. If anyone serves me, the Father will honor him.

chokri: Tsühi alü kemezhe bate, I dipü pota? Apo pütü hilü akela süte, vele püco hiola I pütü hilü vori.
english: "Now my soul is troubled. What shall I say? 'Father, save me from this time?' But for this cause I came to this time.

chokri: Apo, nzapo zapozale thi yite. Süsa tülü füphrapü kiri, poti, I zapozale thi süzo mu thisa süto.
english: Father, glorify your name!" Then there came a voice out of the sky, saying, "I have both glorified it, and will glorify it again."

chokri: Thümako süce kebako süo sesü, de poti, tüsü süle; kekreko poti, tüyo choyipü püce jü posüle.
english: The multitude therefore, who stood by and heard it, said that it had thundered. Others said, "An angel has spoken to him."

chokri: Jisu ukofü kela kesü, Füphra hio Alamo, vele nekola.
english: Jesus answered, "This voice hasn't come for my sake, but for your sakes.

chokri: Tsühi cejüo neketi bate, hisa cejülü kekha keba kezhoo hu vato.
english: Now is the judgment of this world. Now the prince of this world will be cast out.

chokri: I cejülü tasü ketasa thüma metü tüsüpü aceco vori tato.
english: And I, if I am lifted up from the earth, will draw all people to myself."

chokri: Pü kepo hio pü düpü sü thito sheri süo poba.
english: But he said this, signifying by what kind of death he should die.

chokri: Thümako püfü kela kesü, Hako thezholü thopü kemeta sekeba Messiah kechothe ba sütole ziba; no dipüla thüma Nuo tasü tatole siziba? Thüma Nuo sü sopüta?
english: The multitude answered him, "We have heard out of the law that the Christ remains forever. How do you say, 'The Son of Man must be lifted up?' Who is this Son of Man?"

chokri: Jisu ukoce poti, pütü dopü keve neko zübato. Keve kebace neko kevelü tayite, sizi moze kezüo nekoce vori tato; kekrelamori kezülü ketami pü dipüce vozü sheri seyomo. (Thümako melütho keta).
english: Jesus therefore said to them, "Yet a little while the light is with you. Walk while you have the light, that darkness doesn't overtake you. He who walks in the darkness doesn't know where he is going.

chokri: Keve nekoce kebace neko keve melü yite, sizino keveo nuneko thi yite. Jisu jü hiko po kesüsa pü tavo mi kedira vota.
english: While you have the light, believe in the light, that you may become children of light." Jesus said these things, and he departed and hid himself from them.

chokri: Vele pü mha kekro ungo mhathoko thisü zori uko pü melü muta.
english: But though he had done so many signs before them, yet they didn't believe in him,

chokri: Hihi thajü Isaiah saju kepoo popü kemeta thitsü sü ketola zo, “Niepoo sopü hafü melüyi ta, Niepoo bechopü somi yo sü?”
english: that the word of Isaiah the prophet might be fulfilled, which he spoke, "Lord, who has believed our report? To whom has the arm of the Lord been revealed?"

chokri: Side uko pü melü mota, kekrelamori Isaiah posa kesü;
english: For this cause they couldn't believe, for Isaiah said again,

chokri: Pü uko mhü thi mece ve, uko melü sheri thipü (revo) daduve; side ukopo ngomo ketola, mu uko melüpo mhalüyi mo ketola: uko thelü redeyi de sürhoyi ketolü.
english: "He has blinded their eyes and he hardened their heart, Lest they should see with their eyes, And perceive with their heart, And would turn, And I would heal them."

chokri: Isaiah hiko po kesü püzapozale kethi ngotila mu püju posü.
english: Isaiah said these things when he saw his glory, and spoke of him.

chokri: Sizosheri mikekha keta kezhomi kekro sheri pü melüyi, vele Pharisee miko la phra mese zomo, kekrelamori uko synagogue kolü hupü chozu süva ketola.
english: Nevertheless even of the rulers many believed in him, but because of the Pharisees they didn't confess it, so that they wouldn't be put out of the synagogue,

chokri: Kekrelamori uko Therüopo uko chu keti ceri thümapo uko chuti menyüku tila.
english: for they loved men's praise more than God's praise.

chokri: Jisu semeto de poti, “amelü keyimi, amelü mori aketsü kesüo melü yi.”
english: Jesus cried out and said, "Whoever believes in me, believes not in me, but in him who sent me.

chokri: Akengomi aketsü kesüo ngo sütazo.
english: He who sees me sees him who sent me.

chokri: I cejülü keveo thi vori, somi amelüyi sheri kezülü bati mesü mote.
english: I have come as a light into the world, that whoever believes in me may not remain in the darkness.

chokri: Humi mhace ajüko sede melü mota lize, I pü mejomo, kekrelamori I cejüo mejo tole vorimo; vele cejüo kelayi ketola vori.
english: If anyone listens to my sayings, and doesn't believe, I don't judge him. For I came not to judge the world, but to save the world.

chokri: Pü amela kevao ajüko yimute, mipü pü mejo tato, I jüpo kesü jü süo thela nha pü mejo tato.
english: He who rejects me, and doesn't receive my sayings, has one who judges him. The word that I spoke, the same will judge him in the last day.

chokri: Kekrelamori akepoko atho amedo lamo, vele a Po aketsü kesüo, pü khapü atsü kesü, I po kesü mesüleno.
english: For I spoke not from myself, but the Father who sent me, he gave me a commandment, what I should say, and what I should speak.

chokri: I sekeba pü khakesü jüko kechothe kerhü zo, I kehopü posü zori a Po acepo kesüko la I pü po sü.
english: I know that his commandment is eternal life. The things therefore which I speak, even as the Father has said to me, so I speak."

chokri: Jisu jü hiko po kesüsalü püsajiko züde rütsi Kidron lü tizhepülü lüta mu pü sajiko sheri.
english: When Jesus had spoken these words, he went out with his disciples over the brook Kidron, where was a garden, into which he and his disciples entered.

chokri: Judas, pütü kesüo sheri thethi süo seba, kekrelamori Jisu tsükenu püsajiko zü sülü voyo tila.
english: Now Judas, who betrayed him, also knew the place, for Jesus often resorted there with his disciples.

chokri: Judas sübide thüma kropü, kezho miko, thevo kezhoko, mu Pharisee miko züyi, de metobü, metosü, mu rineko khi vori.
english: Judas then, having taken a detachment of soldiers and officers from the chief priests and the Pharisees, came there with lanterns, torches, and weapons.

chokri: Jisu mha süko metü püyo thisü keto seba tila, ta ukoce vode neko sopü phuzü? zisü.
english: Jesus therefore, knowing all the things that were happening to him, went forth, and said to them, "Who are you looking for?"

chokri: Uko püfü kela kesü, hako Nazarethmi Jisu phu züle. Jisu ukoce poti, I thüma süo le zisü. Judas pütü kesüo ri süce tha uko züde.
english: They answered him, "Jesus of Nazareth." Jesus said to them, "I AM." Judas also, who betrayed him, was standing with them.

chokri: I thüma süo le zisü metha uko tsüsata, de retu cejü tsüta.
english: When therefore he said to them, "I AM," they went backward, and fell to the ground.

chokri: Süsa pü uko ketsode, Neko sopü phu züla? Uko posala kesü, Jisu Nazarethmi phu züle.
english: Again therefore he asked them, "Who are you looking for?" They said, "Jesus of Nazareth."

chokri: Jisu posalasü, I nekoce posü mome, I thüma süo zole, mhace neko aphu ta lize neko atü yite.
english: Jesus answered, "I told you that I AM. If therefore you seek me, let these go their way,"

chokri: Popü kemetako thitso sü ketola, pü po kesü, uko no khapü aketsüko I mipü sheri medi vamo.
english: that the word might be fulfilled which he spoke, "Of those whom you have given me, I have lost none."

chokri: Simon Peter süsa zecho khipü thevo kezhoo cetao ni metava, uzaco ni. Thekü süo za Malchus.
english: Simon Peter therefore, having a sword, drew it, and struck the high priest's servant, and cut off his right ear. The servant's name was Malchus.

chokri: Süsa Jisu Peter cepoti, Zecho khapü pübülü süvate, a Po tehepü aketsü I krazü mome
english: Jesus therefore said to Peter, "Put the sword into its sheath. The cup which the Father has given me, shall I not surely drink it?"

chokri: Süsa kro süo kefüo, Jewsmi kezhoko, Jisu tüpü phayita.
english: So the detachment, the commanding officer, and the officers of the Jews, seized Jesus and bound him,

chokri: Uko pü tüpü Anna, Caiphas ni celü vota, pü tütse sülü thevo kezhoo thi kebala.
english: and led him to Annas first, for he was father-in-law to Caiaphas, who was high priest that year.

chokri: Caiphas no hitülü Jews miko zü kerethi tilü poti, mipüpo thüma kola sava mo keshü posü.
english: Now it was Caiaphas who advised the Jews that it was expedient that one man should perish for the people.

chokri: Simon Peter püsa mejivo, mu methakeyi kekreko ri, thevo kezhoo metha keyio seba pü Jisu zü thevo kezhoo celü vo.
english: Simon Peter followed Jesus, as did another disciple. Now that disciple was known to the high priest, and entered in with Jesus into the court of the high priest;

chokri: Vele Peter chozuco cekhace tha. Pü sajiko thevo kezhoo sekeba la tapra thenopü cekha fü kebao ce Peter zü celü lüri.
english: but Peter was standing at the door outside. So the other disciple, who was known to the high priest, went out and spoke to her who kept the door, and brought in Peter.

chokri: Süsa thenopü cekha fü kethao Peter ce “No sheri püsajimi püzo mome? Pü poti, I no mole.”
english: Then the maid who kept the door said to Peter, "Are you also one of this man's disciples?" He said, "I am not."

chokri: Choyi miko mu kezhoko süce tha, megikrapü methi mevü kebala; tüsülü uko me megu kebala: mu Peter uko dolü tha, pü sheri me megu ba.
english: Now the servants and the officers were standing there, having made a fire of coals, for it was cold. They were warming themselves. Peter was with them, standing and warming himself.

chokri: Thevo kezhoo Jisu ce pü sajiko ketso ba, mu pü kemetha jüko.
english: The high priest therefore asked Jesus about his disciples, and about his teaching.

chokri: Jisu püfü kelakesü, I cejüo ce mhotsu posüzo, I synagogue kolü methasü, kereshüce kolü, sükolü Jews miko vori bazo, mu I kemekhrülü mhapomo.
english: Jesus answered him, "I spoke openly to the world. I always taught in synagogues, and in the temple, where the Jews always meet. I said nothing in secret.

chokri: No dipüla aketsoba, afü keseko ketsote, I ukoce dipü posü sheri; Hihe, uko akepoko seba tila.
english: Why do you ask me? Ask those who have heard me what I said to them. Behold, these know the things which I said."

chokri: Pü jüpo kesü salü kezhoko mipü beziphrepü püvü, no sübi de thevo kezhoo ce jüpo tome?
english: When he had said this, one of the officers standing by slapped Jesus with his hand, saying, "Do you answer the high priest like that?"

chokri: Jisu püfü kelakesü, I mhapo kekri süze no kesü zhopo thisüri ve, vele mhace sizi mo lize no dipüla avüba?
english: Jesus answered him, "If I have spoken evil, testify of the evil; but if well, why do you beat me?"

chokri: Hisalü Anna pütüphapü thevo kezho Caiphas ce süva.
english: Annas sent him bound to Caiaphas, the high priest.

chokri: Simon Peter süce tha de me meguba. Süla uko püce poti, No sheri püsajiko mipü zo mome? Pü süo kela kesü, I mole.
english: Now Simon Peter was standing and warming himself. They said therefore to him, "You aren't also one of his disciples, are you?" He denied it, and said, "I am not."

chokri: Thevo kezho theküo, Peter zechopü püni zathü kevao, pücekromi tila, Peter ce poti, tizhelü no püzü keta I nngo mome?
english: One of the servants of the high priest, being a relative of him whose ear Peter had cut off, said, "Didn't I see you in the garden with him?"

chokri: Süsari Peter püse mole pota, sizisü metha thevü füta.
english: Peter therefore denied it again, and immediately the rooster crowed.

chokri: Süsa Caiphas celü Jisu zü thene keticelü vota, süce keshu renazo tila uko sheco celü lü molu; molize ce süo thimerhova ketola; vele uko cukevo nye mhati to kebala.
english: They led Jesus therefore from Caiaphas into the Praetorium. It was early, and they themselves didn't enter into the Praetorium, that they might not be defiled, but might eat the Passover.

chokri: Pilate-e chozu prari ukoce, de poti, neko kedipü pü thüma hio mejoba?
english: Pilate therefore went out to them, and said, "What accusation do you bring against this man?"

chokri: Uko püfü kela de po kesü, mhace thüma hio thüma kesumi mo tisa hako püzü nce voriho.
english: They answered him, "If this man weren't an evildoer, we wouldn't have delivered him up to you."

chokri: Süsa Pilate-e ukoce poti, neko püzüvo neko zholü püshe ketite. Jews miko püce poti, hako zholü mi dokhri keti bamo.
english: Pilate therefore said to them, "Take him yourselves, and judge him according to your law." Therefore the Jews said to him, "It is not lawful for us to put anyone to death,"

chokri: Jisu popü kemeta thitsoyi ketola, pükepo, pü dipü sü thito sheri po meta tila.
english: that the word of Jesus might be fulfilled, which he spoke, signifying by what kind of death he should die.

chokri: Süsa Pilate-e ta sala sheco balü lü, Jisu cüsü, de püce poti, no Jews miko kedeo me?
english: Pilate therefore entered again into the Praetorium, called Jesus, and said to him, "Are you the King of the Jews?"

chokri: Jisu püfü kela kesü, süsü ntho nkepo zome, mori kekremiko aju nce kepo me?
english: Jesus answered him, "Do you say this by yourself, or did others tell you about me?"

chokri: Pilate-e püfü kela kesü, I Jews mipüme? Nshuye mu thevo kezhoko, nzü ace vori, no dipü thi süta?
english: Pilate answered, "I'm not a Jew, am I? Your own nation and the chief priests delivered you to me. What have you done?"

chokri: Jisu pola kesü, A kede kethino cejü hilü mo; mhace cejü hilü zhü lize asajiko mizü kezheto, atüpü Jews miko belü sümo ketola: vele tsühi, akede hice zhümo.
english: Jesus answered, "My Kingdom is not of this world. If my Kingdom were of this world, then my servants would fight, that I wouldn't be delivered to the Jews. But now my Kingdom is not from here."

chokri: Pilate-e püce poti, süsa No kedemi me? Jisu poti, No apü kedemi le zisüte. Thüla hiola amenu vori, püco hiola I cejülü vori; apo ketho zhopo thisü ketola. Kehopüri ketholü ketami afü reniyo.
english: Pilate therefore said to him, "Are you a king then?" Jesus answered, "You say that I am a king. For this reason I have been born, and for this reason I have come into the world, that I should testify to the truth. Everyone who is of the truth listens to my voice."

chokri: Pilate-e püce poti, Ketho sü dipü? Pü süo po kevasa ta Jews miko ce ceta prata, de ukoce poti, I püyo mha kesu püri ngo yimo.
english: Pilate said to him, "What is truth?" When he had said this, he went out again to the Jews, and said to them, "I find no basis for a charge against him.

chokri: Vele neko thezho püba, neko cukevo nyelü mipü kela süyo, apo Jews miko kedeo kelasü va toma?
english: But you have a custom, that I should release someone to you at the Passover. Therefore do you want me to release to you the King of the Jews?"

chokri: Uko metü serekrü de poti, thüma hio mo vele Barabbas te. Barabbas lize mizhü süketimipü.
english: Then they all shouted again, saying, "Not this man, but Barabbas!" Now Barabbas was a robber.

chokri: Kebu keti la ketasa, Paul kemelü miko cüpü puce süyi, uko mebosü de ta Macedonai lü vo ketola ukozü kekho ta.
english: After the uproar had ceased, Paul sent for the disciples, took leave of them, and departed to go into Macedonia.

chokri: Pü thethi sükolü vo de uko tarho shusü, de ta Greece lü vota.
english: When he had gone through those parts, and had encouraged them with many words, he came into Greece.

chokri: Sülü tükhri sü ba, Jews miko püla bata, pü Syria lü ru thopü voto zü tice, süla pü ta Macedonia lü sala vota keto lüyi.
english: When he had spent three months there, and a plot was made against him by Jews as he was about to set sail for Syria, he determined to return through Macedonia.

chokri: Sülü Sopater, Pyrrhus nu Berea mipü püzü Asia lü vota, mu ta Thesalonia miko Aristarchus mu Secundus ni, Gaius Derbe lü kevori mipü, mu Timothy, Tychicus, Trophimus, hiko Asia lü vori.
english: These accompanied him as far as Asia: Sopater of Beroea; Aristarchus and Secundus of the Thessalonians; Gaius of Derbe; Timothy; and Tychicus and Trophimus of Asia.

chokri: Uko taravo Troas lü hako fü bata.
english: But these had gone ahead, and were waiting for us at Troas.

chokri: Vele hako ru thopü Philippi lü khre khasü kemo nye salü tasü, zho püngu sa tavo uko tsoyi Troas lü, sülü hako zho thüna ba.
english: We sailed away from Philippi after the days of Unleavened Bread, and came to them at Troas in five days, where we stayed seven days.

chokri: Deobado theranha, kemelü miko tavori Niepoo kerekraco tsükhri keti thi ketola, Paul sülü ukoce zhopo thi zoba meso tülu vota, sodo tata ketola.
english: On the first day of the week, when the disciples were gathered together to break bread, Paul talked with them, intending to depart on the next day, and continued his speech until midnight.

chokri: Cebü meso halü keve kekro ba, uko kengupü kebalü.
english: There were many lights in the upper chamber where we were gathered together.

chokri: Khresa mipü kharüce poyo baba, püza Eutychus lezi, püzü relüta, Paul zhopo thi mecho ba ketala, pü zürenyüba retuta, cebü ha sülü bade tsü pü kesükhri va.
english: A certain young man named Eutychus sat in the window, weighed down with deep sleep. As Paul spoke still longer, being weighed down by his sleep, he fell down from the third story, and was taken up dead.

chokri: Paul taki de renutsü pü mebosü, püpoti, nekolü kemezhe hite, kekrelamori pü kerhüro püyo bazo tila.
english: Paul went down, and fell upon him, and embracing him said, "Don't be troubled, for his life is in him."

chokri: Pü tasala khori de tsükhri cethisü, süo ti keyisa pütü kecho jüposü, tüso vori kemothe, side pü tata.
english: When he had gone up, and had broken bread, and eaten, and had talked with them a long while, even until break of day, he departed.

chokri: Uko khresao rhüyi ketao züde prari mu uko metü melü thomelupü uko nüdamo.
english: They brought the boy in alive, and were greatly comforted.

chokri: Hako mejümera ru thopü Assos lü voba, sülü Paul züpü ru lü süyi ketola; kekrelamori pü hakopo sizino pometa tila. Pütho zono püphelü tavori keto lüde sizisü.
english: But we who went ahead to the ship set sail for Assos, intending to take Paul aboard there, for he had so arranged, intending himself to go by land.

chokri: Pü tavo Assos lü hako zü kesüyi, püzüpü ru lü süyi de ta Mitylene lü vo.
english: When he met us at Assos, we took him aboard, and came to Mitylene.

chokri: Hako ru thopü süsanha Chios kemetsu ce vori, süsanha hako tavo Samos lü bayi, side hako Trogyllium lü thothüyi, mu süsanha tavo Miletus tsoyi.
english: Sailing from there, we came the following day opposite Chios. The next day we touched at Samos and stayed at Trogyllium, and the day after we came to Miletus.

chokri: Kekrelamori Paul lüti ru thopü Ephesus cholü vo keto lüba, kekrelamori pü Asia lü pütü mevomo ketola, pü thiyi ve lize tavo Jerusalem lü Pentecost zhonha banyü ketala rekri shuzü.
english: For Paul had determined to sail past Ephesus, that he might not have to spend time in Asia; for he was hastening, if it were possible for him, to be in Jerusalem on the day of Pentecost.

chokri: Pü Miletus lü thüma ketsüpü Ephesus lü kehu mehiko cüsü.
english: From Miletus he sent to Ephesus, and called to himself the elders of the assembly.

chokri: Uko ta püce kevorice pü ukoce poti, I Asia lü nekoce kevorice pütü metülü I dipü bide ba sheri neko metü se mu ngoba
english: When they had come to him, he said to them, "You yourselves know, from the first day that I set foot in Asia, how I was with you all the time,

chokri: Niepoo choyi kethilü I renu metsa de bazo, amhüjü mekri sü, medokephi ayo kevori kolü, Jews miko kemeda la arizo thi keba kolü.
english: serving the Lord with all humility, with many tears, and with trials which happened to me by the plots of the Jews;

chokri: I mhapü lüri akethüta mozo neko la meve keti kolü vele khapü neko mese mengo süzo, methi mhüjülü neko metha sü, ce püpülü sheri.
english: how I didn't shrink from declaring to you anything that was profitable, teaching you publicly and from house to house,

chokri: Zhopo thipü Jews koce ri mu Greek miko ceri süzo, thelü redepü Jihova ketsü mu kemelü khapü Niepoo Jisu Khrista yo kesü.
english: testifying both to Jews and to Greeks repentance toward God, and faith toward our Lord Jesus.

chokri: Tsühi, Hihe, Rüpülü I phapü tate Jerusalem lü vo ketola, mhakedipü ayo vorito sheri semo.
english: Now, behold, I go bound by the Spirit to Jerusalem, not knowing what will happen to me there;

chokri: Kemesa Rüpü tho kese-zo, nade metülü poti ropha mu kemethi lü mezhe ayo ba.
english: except that the Holy Spirit testifies in every city, saying that bonds and afflictions wait for me.

chokri: Vele mha sükola I thipü tsüsa va mozo, mori a kerhüro hihi lüpü ala mhapüri zomo, side I yitholü amesüco anüde thivo tsosü ketola, yitho hio I Niepoo Jisu celü ngoyi, sülü Jihova ngokemezhe jüyikeve po mese sü ketola.
english: But these things don't count; nor do I hold my life dear to myself, so that I may finish my race with joy, and the ministry which I received from the Lord Jesus, to fully testify to the Gospel of the grace of God.

chokri: Tsühisa, Hihe, neko dolü I Jihova kede zhopo thi kevoko azü ngo samo tato.
english: Now, behold, I know that you all, among whom I went about preaching the Kingdom of God, will see my face no more.

chokri: Mha hiola I neko po seta keto tha tüsohi, I mipü zalü sheri kekri mozo mu mesazo.
english: Therefore I testify to you this day that I am clean from the blood of all men,

chokri: Jihova mharhükolü I nekoce mhapü lüri akethü kerü de pomo ta mozo.
english: for I didn't shrink from declaring to you the whole counsel of God.

chokri: Süla neko menu yite, ukroko ce sheri, Kemesa Rüpü neko thipü mikefümi thisü tila, Jihova kehu va thino pütho püzapü neko khri yite.
english: Take heed, therefore, to yourselves, and to all the flock, in which the Holy Spirit has made you overseers, to shepherd the assembly of the Lord and God which he purchased with his own blood.

chokri: Kekrelamori I ta ketasalü sace kemere kethiko neko dolü vori keto I seba.
english: For I know that after my departure, vicious wolves will enter in among you, not sparing the flock.

chokri: Neko dolü sheri thüma hu sajiko shüpü mezikemo lü vota keto süri zoto.
english: Men will arise from among your own selves, speaking perverse things, to draw away the disciples after them.

chokri: Süla fü mu la bate, nesa lüde, I tütse süko tüso tüzü relü mozode nekoce amhüjü kri zode po tazo.
english: Therefore watch, remembering that for a period of three years I didn't cease to admonish everyone night and day with tears.

chokri: Akro miko, I neko khapü Jihova melü sü teho, pü phikemezhe jülü, süo neko phe mesü yive, side nekopo kemesako zü kalü thi yito.
