chokri: Kükrelamori, Puthüma kükro thi küsührho küsü la, u küthi künyi ne kütako pu nyi sü tezü die Pu whü züta.
english: For he had healed many, so that as many as had diseases pressed on him that they might touch him.

chokri: Süze müsakümo rüpüko pu ngo küsüsa rütu prü Pu mhuzülü zü die se zodie sü, No Jihova nuo, zita.
english: The unclean spirits, whenever they saw him, fell down before him, and cried, "You are the Son of God!"

chokri: Mo pu süko po pu popüh mi muse hino die puko khabüh mu küthi va.
english: He sternly warned them that they should not make him known.

chokri: Süsa puta cejüthuyo kho Pu müdomiko ci sü, mu puko ri Puce vori.
english: He went up into the mountain, and called to himself those whom he wanted, and they went to him.

chokri: Sülize pu mi kürikünako küda püh süko po Puzüh ba kütola, mu kütsüh züyi küve zhopo thi sü kütola.
english: He appointed twelve, that they might be with him, and that he might send them out to preach,

chokri: Mu küthi künyiko thi küsührhoyi küto kuku bayi küto, mu küsü rüpüko huyi küto la.
english: and to have authority to heal sicknesses and to cast out demons:

chokri: Süko Simon, puza thopüh Peter thi kütao.
english: Simon, to whom he gave the name Peter;

chokri: Mu Zebedee nu James sazüh-o John; Pu süni zatho püh Boanerges, puco tüküsüh nunyi; thisü.
english: James the son of Zebedee; John, the brother of James, and he surnamed them Boanerges, which means, Sons of Thunder;

chokri: Süze Andrew mu Philip. Mu Bartholomew, mu Matthew, mu Thoma, mu Alfia nu James, mu Thaddaeus, mu Canaanmi Simon;
english: Andrew; Philip; Bartholomew; Matthew; Thomas; James, the son of Alphaeus; Thaddaeus; Simon the Zealot;

chokri: Süsa Pu tüpü mi belü sü küva Iscariot Judas risa.
english: and Judas Iscariot, who also betrayed him. He came into a house.

chokri: Süsa Pu ce pühlü vori küyice thüma kro künye püh la vorita, sidie puko po mhari tiyi süta.
english: The multitude came together again, so that they could not so much as eat bread.

chokri: Süze pu züh kütako sü se sü die ta vo Pu tüyi tozu ta, kükrelamori, pukoe sü pu mutse tha mote zi die lü kütala.
english: When his friends heard it, they went out to seize him: for they said, "He is insane."

chokri: Mu Jerusalem lün kükiri lüsiküsekoe sü, Pu Belzebub züh bate, Pue küsü kükuko züh die küsuko huyo, zita.
english: The scribes who came down from Jerusalem said, "He has Beelzebul," and, "By the prince of the demons he casts out the demons."

chokri: Süze pu süko cipün Pu müno süyi die; küzüve die puko ce po die sü, Dibi die.
english: He summoned them, and said to them in parables, "How can Satan cast out Satan?

chokri: Satan Satan huyi veta?
english: If a kingdom is divided against itself, that kingdom cannot stand.

chokri: Mhaceno küde püh-e putho pu mütsu ta lize, süeprote, mu pu küde thayi kündi tato.
english: If a house is divided against itself, that house cannot stand.

chokri: Mu mhaceno Satan ri pu tho pu mütsu süri Ta lize die küpüphro ta lize pu küde thayi kündi tato.
english: If Satan has risen up against himself, and is divided, he can't stand, but has an end.

chokri: Mipühri kükumi püh celü lü die pu mhako mükayi yomo, pu süo tü püh pha va müra zo kümosa, süzi küva züh pu mhako mükayi küve zo.
english: But no one can enter into the house of the strong man to plunder, unless he first binds the strong man; and then he will plunder his house.

chokri: I neko ce potü süto, thüma nuko küphoma lünyiko mu puko Jihova kümütsu zü küpo thüthako mütü vapüh puko prüto.
english: Most assuredly I tell you, all of the children of men's sins will be forgiven them, including their blasphemies with which they may blaspheme;

chokri: Süri thüma kühopühri Kümüsa Rüpü thayo lize pu külhüthe küjo va küprü ba samo, eli, lhülü pu müjo va küto kümüprüh lü bate.
english: but whoever may blaspheme against the Holy Spirit never has forgiveness, but is guilty of an eternal sin"

chokri: Süosü pukoe; Pu müsakümo rüpü züh ta, zi küta lazo.
english: -- because they said, "He has an unclean spirit."

chokri: Sülize Pu sazühneko mu Pu zu puko vori ceta tha die Pu küyi ba.
english: His mother and his brothers came, and standing outside, they sent to him, calling him.

chokri: Mu thüma kroo ba Pu mütu pü kübakoe pu yisü, Yhie Nzu mu n sazühnekoe ceta thadie n phuzü le zisü.
english: A multitude was sitting around him, and they told him, "Behold, your mother, your brothers, and your sisters are outside looking for you."

chokri: Pu süko zü küla die, sopüh-e azu, mu sopüh-e a sazühzürako la?
english: He answered them, "Who are my mother and my brothers?"

chokri: Süsa Pue Pu mütu pü die ba kübako pe die sü;
english: Looking around at those who sat around him, he said, "Behold, my mother and my brothers!

chokri: Yhie; azu mu a sazühzürako sü; thüma kühopühri Jihova müdoko thiyo kütio sü a sazüh-o, mu alipü, mu azu zo, zita.
english: For whoever does the will of God, the same is my brother, and my sister, and mother."

chokri: Süsa puko tasü dzü kütü shico Gadarenes mi rünalü voyi.
english: They came to the other side of the sea, into the country of the Gadarenes.

chokri: Sidie Pu püzu lüno ta prari mütha küsürüpü po u kübü pü kütami püh mhakhreko lüno ta prari Pu züh küsüh.
english: When he had come out of the boat, immediately there met him out of the tombs a man with an unclean spirit,

chokri: Thüma hio mhakhreko püh pu kübathi thiyo; mu mhapühri püh pu phayi yomo, thügiro zori phayi yomo.
english: who had his dwelling in the tombs. Nobody could bind him any more, not even with chains,

chokri: Kükrelamori, mi tsükünu bekharo mu thügiroko püh pu phayo, süzori pu thügiroko püthü küna thiva yo, mu bekharoko cethü kügü vayo, sidie mipühri pu kükha pü bayi kündi tata.
english: because he had been often bound with fetters and chains, and the chains had been torn apart by him, and the fetters broken in pieces. Nobody had the strength to tame him.

chokri: Sidie tüso tüzüh cejüthuko yo mu mhakhreko lüno se mu kütsüko püh putho pu jo zi ba zota.
english: Always, night and day, in the tombs and in the mountains, he was crying out, and cutting himself with stones.

chokri: Mu pu rapeco no Jisu ngo sü die ta vori die tüprü Pu rüsuta.
english: When he saw Jesus from afar, he ran and bowed down to him,

chokri: Sidie pu se müto zodie, Jisu kürükrüthoo Poo nuo no azüh dipüh thita? I Poo zalü rushüh zo die shoyo, a müthi tahi zote! Zita.
english: and crying out with a loud voice, he said, "What have I to do with you, Jesus, you Son of the Most High God? I adjure you by God, don't torment me."

chokri: Kükrelamori, Pu süo yisü, küsü rüpü no pulüno ta prari tate, zi kütala.
english: For he said to him, "Come out of the man, you unclean spirit!"

chokri: Mu Pu süo Yisü, nza sopüh la? Zidie kütso sü, mu pu küla die Azae Legion, kükrelamori, hako mi kükro ta tila, zita.
english: He asked him, "What is your name?" He said to him, "My name is Legion, for we are many."

chokri: Süze Pu po pu kütsüh pü rüna süo mücu va mo kütola shobü ta.
english: He begged him much that he would not send them away out of the country.

chokri: Sücesü thüvo krode püh-e sü kümünoce mha ngüti ta mu küsürüpüko mütü.
english: Now there was on the mountainside a great herd of pigs feeding.

chokri: Pu ce shodie, hako kütsüh püh thüvoko ce süte, hako po süko lülüta küto la, zita.
english: All the demons begged him, saying, "Send us into the pigs, that we may enter into them."

chokri: Süsa Jisu puko po vota no sü, süsa müsakümo rüpüko ta prari die tasü thüvoko lülüta; süze thüvo kro nyekünako ta kühu dzükütülü lü die mülhü khriva.
english: At once Jesus gave them permission. The unclean spirits came out and entered into the pigs. The herd of about two thousand rushed down the steep bank into the sea, and they were drowned in the sea.

chokri: Sülize, süko kükhükoe ta vo nadekolü mu yi kolü mice rükhrü sü, sidie thümakoe dipühthi ta shi küti pe tozu vori.
english: Those who fed them fled, and told it in the city and in the country. The people came to see what it was that had happened.

chokri: Süze puko Jisu ce vori die thürüküsü puco Legion po kübü züh ta kütao khüne be münemu u mülü tsühyi die ba küta ngoyi die müprüh shuta.
english: They came to Jesus, and saw him who had been possessed by demons sitting, clothed, and in his right mind, even him who had the legion; and they were afraid.

chokri: Süsa thürüküsü po u kübü küvorio yo mu thüvoko yo dipüh thita sheli küti küngokoe mha mütü püh mice posü.
english: Those who saw it declared to them how it happened to him who was possessed by demons, and about the pigs.

chokri: Süsa pukoe Pupo uko rünalüno tata no die uko müzhe mülo ta.
english: They began to beg him to depart from their region.

chokri: Sidie Pu tata tozu die lü dpüzulü ba küyice thürüküsü po u kübü kütaoe Pupo pu züh tata no die shota.
english: As he was entering into the boat, he who had been possessed by demons begged him that he might be with him.

chokri: Süri Jisu mu ta die pu yisü, no ta n celü mu n thümako ce vo die Nyepooe nla dipüh thi sushi küti püh po süte, zita.
english: He didn't allow him, but said to him, "Go to your house, to your friends, and tell them what great things the Lord has done for you, and how he had mercy on you."

chokri: Süsa pu ta sü Dekapoli lü vo die Nyepoo pula dipüh thi sü shi küti po püh mice za sü. Mu mi mütü u malhü thita.
english: He went his way, and began to proclaim in Decapolis how Jesus had done great things for him, and everyone marveled.

chokri: Süze Pu püzu lüno ta lishico ku la küyice, Pu dzükütü poce ba, mu thüma kroo Pu ce vori künguyi.
english: When Jesus had crossed back over in the boat to the other side, a great multitude was gathered to him; and he was by the sea.

chokri: Sücesü, Yhie! Synagoguelü küzhomi püh puza Jaira li küti püh ta vori die pungo küyice tü Pu phelü prü Pu ce sho die;
english: Behold, one of the rulers of the synagogue, Jairus by name, came; and seeing him, he fell at his feet,

chokri: A nupü sata tozü te, pupo ve mu sührholayi küto la no vori die n be yhio püh puyo sü zhie, zita.
english: and begged him much, saying, "My little daughter is at the point of death. Please come and lay your hands on her, that she may be made healthy, and live."

chokri: Mu Pu süo züh vota, süze thüma krode püh Pu whü tata.
english: He went with him, and a great multitude followed him, and they pressed upon him on all sides.

chokri: Sülize thünomi püh tse küriküna thüza krüh die kümüzheri ta küta püh daru küsemi kükrori po pu pe die.
english: A certain woman, who had an issue of blood for twelve years,

chokri: Sükola pu mha küne küdi mvo va zori ve küyio moli süku küta püh.
english: and had suffered many things by many physicians, and had spent all that she had, and was no better, but rather grew worse,

chokri: Jisu vori küzü se sü die makro dolü vori die Jisu saco vo Pu khü nyisü.
english: having heard the things concerning Jesus, came up behind him in the crowd, and touched his clothes.

chokri: Kükrelamori, pue pu lüno, I Pu khü zori nyi sü lize sührhoyi tato, zi die lü kütala.
english: For she said, "If I just touch his clothes, I will be made well."

chokri: Mu nyisü mütha puyo thüza krü kütao so mhata, sidie puyo küthi süo münüyi küta seyita.
english: Immediately the flow of her blood was dried up, and she felt in her body that she was healed of her affliction.

chokri: Süzita mütha Jisu Puyo no kuku pra küta seyi die rügü pra thümako pedie sü, sopühn a khü nyi sü laha? Zita.
english: Immediately Jesus, perceiving in himself that the power had gone out from him, turned around in the crowd, and asked, "Who touched my clothes?"

chokri: Mu müthaküyiko Pu yisü, thüma kroko n whü pü küta ngo zori, No sopühn anyi sü la zi tome? Zita.
english: His disciples said to him, "You see the multitude pressing against you, and you say, 'Who touched me?'"

chokri: Mu Pue Pu nyi küsüpü ngo sü kütola rügü lico hico pe ta.
english: He looked around to see her who had done this thing.

chokri: Sücesü, thünopüo pütho puyo dipüh bi ta sü ti seyi die mu müprüh rüsü zodie ta vori tü Pu mhuzülü prü die pu jüh mütü püh Pu ce posü.
english: But the woman, fearing and trembling, knowing what had been done to her, came and fell down before him, and told him all the truth.

chokri: Süsa Pu süpü yisü, A nupü, n kümülü tho nthi münü süho, nnü die tavo tate, mu n küthi po münü tsüh ta küyio thite; zisü.
english: He said to her, "Daughter, your faith has made you well. Go in peace, and be cured of your disease."

chokri: Pu züpo kübalü thüma hu Synagogue küzhoo celüno vori die sü, n nupü sate; dipühla kümüthao po kümüzhe la züta, zita.
english: While he was still speaking, they came from the synagogue ruler's house saying, "Your daughter is dead. Why bother the Teacher any more?"

chokri: Süri. Jisu süo sesü mütha synagoglü küzhoo yisü, müprüh tahi, no mülütü zoyite, zita.
english: But Jesus, when he heard the message spoken, immediately said to the ruler of the synagogue, "Don't be afraid, only believe."

chokri: Süze Pu Peter, James, mu James sazüh-o John neko zo kümosa mi kükre pühri züh sa muta.
english: He allowed no one to follow him, except Peter, James, and John the brother of James.

chokri: Sidie puko synagoglü küzhoo celü vori küyice mi kra mu thükri thipüh münyomünyi pü küzü ngoyita.
english: He came to the synagogue ruler's house, and he saw an uproar, weeping, and great wailing.

chokri: Mu Pu celü lüri die puko yisü, neko dipüh la kra mu thükri thi hi bata? Unupü sata mo; eli züh küzü zo. zita.
english: When he had entered in, he said to them, "Why do you make an uproar and weep? The child is not dead, but is asleep."

chokri: Süri süko Pu krünyita, Eli Pu mütüko po ceta vota die u nupü ne po mu pu zu, süze Pu züh kütako züh die tasü u nupü küzü thilü vota.
english: They ridiculed him. But he, having put them all out, took the father of the child and her mother and those who were with him, and went in where the child was lying.

chokri: Sidie Pu unupü be ceyi die puce, “Talitha cumi” puco, ngane I nce posüyo ho, ta süri te, zisü.
english: Taking the child by the hand, he said to her, "Talitha cumi;" which means, being interpreted, "Girl, I tell you, get up."

chokri: Süzi sü mütha süpü süri die tayita, kükrelamori süpü tse küriküna kütala.
english: Immediately the girl rose up, and walked, for she was twelve years old. They were amazed with great amazement.

chokri: Sücesü puko u malhü thi shühta, mu Pu mipühri po hihi se hizo no die posü, süsa Pu süko po lüva püh süpü po tiyi no die po sü.
english: He strictly ordered them that no one should know this, and commanded that something should be given to her to eat.

chokri: Süsa sülüno tasü putho Pu rüna vorita, mu Pu sadziko Pu samüdzita.
english: He went out from there. He came into his own country, and his disciples followed him.

chokri: Süze kürülüzho vori küyice, Pu Synagogue lüno mi mütha süta, mu Pu khü küse kükro u malhü thi die sü, hi-ohi dipühce no mha hikohi ngoyi ta la?
english: When the Sabbath had come, he began to teach in the synagogue, and many hearing him were astonished, saying, "Where did this man get these things?" and, "What is the wisdom that is given to this man, that such mighty works come about by his hands?

chokri: Mu mhase dibi zo ti püh pu prü die Pu po Pu be süh die küku mhatho hikohi ri thiyi talaha? Hi-ohi süh rükre-o zo, mu Mary nu James mu Joseph mu Judah mu Simon neko sazüh-o zo mome? Sülize Pu lipü nekori hice u züh ba zo mome? Zita.
english: Isn't this the carpenter, the son of Mary, and brother of James, Joses, Judas, and Simon? Aren't his sisters here with us?" They were offended at him.

chokri: Sülize süko Puyo uko lü kükrita. Süsa Jisu süko yisü, sadzüküsemi püh putho pu rakhülü mu putho pu shühyiemi mu putho pu cekrolü zo kümosa thürho di mozo, zita.
english: Jesus said to them, "A prophet is not without honor, except in his own country, and among his own relatives, and in his own house."

chokri: Süze Pu pube yo püh küthimi hu thi küsührho küsüko zo kümosa pu sülü.
english: He could do no mighty work there, except that he laid his hands on a few sick people, and healed them.

chokri: Küku mhatho thi zo mota, mu süko lü kürüh la Pu malhü thita.
english: He marveled because of their unbelief. He went around the villages teaching.

chokri: Süsa pu rünakolü whi die mi mütha, süze Pu kürikünako cipüh puce süyi die puko mi künana kütsüh sü, mu müsakümo rüpüko mhu küku kha puko prü
english: He called to himself the twelve, and began to send them out two by two; and he gave them authority over the unclean spirits.

chokri: Puko küthü libi sa lakho, mori tsükhrüda, mori u shephalü rakathiko mhapühri püta hi nosü.
english: He charged them that they should take nothing for their journey, except a staff only: no bread, no wallet, no money in their purse,

chokri: Süri puko po pheku püyi, eli bola küna süh hi nosü.
english: but to wear sandals, and not put on two tunics.

chokri: Mu Pu süko yisü, neko kühopühra vo zori tasü cepühlü lü küyice thüthi sülü no talata kümothe ce sülü ba zoyi te.
english: He said to them, "Wherever you enter into a house, stay there until you depart from there.

chokri: Mu kühorari mi neko zühyi muta mori neko zhopo rünyi muta lize mi mhuzü no thaka püh thisü kütola neko sülüno ta prari küzü ce ne pheyo nyophu ri kükhu dü vate, zita. I nekoce potü süto, zünyo küti küto nha rüna süo ceri Sodom mu Gomora rüna nyi rülee yhieyi veku zoto, zita.
english: Whoever will not receive you nor hear you, as you depart from there, shake off the dust that is under your feet for a testimony against them. Assuredly, I tell you, it will be more tolerable for Sodom and Gomorrah in the day of judgment than for that city!"

chokri: Süsa puko ta pra die mi po thülü küdeyi no die povota.
english: They went out and preached that people should repent.

chokri: Süze puko thürüküsü kükro ri huva, mu tier püh su küzüko künhü sü die süko thi münü sü.
english: They cast out many demons, and anointed many with oil who were sick, and healed them.

chokri: Herod küde-o Jisu za müle süri küta se küyi ce; Pu yisü, John mi dzülu kha küsüo la süri ba kütala küku mhatho hikohi Pu yono thi bate zita.
english: King Herod heard this, for his name had become known, and he said, "John the Baptizer has risen from the dead, and therefore these powers are at work in him."

chokri: Mu kükreko-e, Pu Elijah zo zita, mu humi sü pu sadzüküsemipüh mori sadzüküsemi dolü mipüh ta zo zita.
english: But others said, "It is Elijah." Others said, "It is the Prophet, or like one of the prophets."

chokri: Süri Herod süo se küsüse pue sü; hiohi John I pu pi ve küvao zo, pu rhüla sürite; zita.
english: But Herod, when he heard this, said, "This is John, whom I beheaded. He has risen from the dead."

chokri: Süo sü Herod pue yiyi küta pu sazüh-o Philip cemi Herodiapü la mi kütsüh püh John tüyi die phapüh küdü celü mata.
english: For Herod himself had sent out and arrested John, and bound him in prison for the sake of Herodias, his brother Philip's wife, for he had married her.

chokri: Kükrelamori, John-e Herod yisü; No n sazüh-o cemi yiti thüzho mo zi küta la.
english: For John said to Herod, "It is not lawful for you to have your brother's wife."

chokri: Mu Herodia-e zü süo se püh pu nümo die pu dokhri küva lü bata; eli thiyi süta.
english: Herodias set herself against him, and desired to kill him, but she couldn't,

chokri: Kükrelamori, Herod-e John hi kümathomi mu kümüsami püh mu pu mha kükro thi küsüko se die pu nü bayo mu pu müprüh bayo tila.
english: for Herod feared John, knowing that he was a righteous and holy man, and kept him safe. When he heard him, he did many things, and he heard him gladly.

chokri: Mu püh tü püh vori küyi, süosü Herod-e pu kümünu tüso nha pu khro mhaküseko, riwhümi küzhoko mu Galileemi mülhiko la tülhü püh küsüh sü.
english: Then a convenient day came, that Herod on his birthday made a supper for his nobles, the high officers, and the chief men of Galilee.

chokri: Süze Herodias nupü ta lüri die rüzü püh Herod mu pu züh kübako nü thi küsüce küdeoe rülipü yisü no dipüh yinyi sheli ace shote I thipüh nprüto; zita.
english: When the daughter of Herodias herself came in and danced, she pleased Herod and those sitting with him. The king said to the young lady, "Ask me whatever you want, and I will give it to you."

chokri: Sidie pu rüshu zodie sü, no ace mhakühopühri shote, a küdeba kütüpüh ri püh n prü zoto, zita.
english: He swore to her, "Whatever you shall ask of me, I will give you, up to half of my kingdom."

chokri: Sücesü süpü ta pra die puzu yisü, apo dipüh shota? Zisü, mu pue sü John mi dzülu kha küsüo pi shote; zisü.
english: She went out, and said to her mother, "What shall I ask?" She said, "The head of John the Baptizer."

chokri: Süsa pu rükri küde-o ce lüri diesü; npo John mi dzülu kha küsüo pi püh mükhu pühlü sü püh vori a prü no die shoyo; zita.
english: She came in immediately with haste to the king, and asked, "I want you to give me right now the head of John the Baptizer on a platter."

chokri: Mu küde-o pulü su shühta, süri pu zü phrasü küva la mu pu züh kübako la pu rülipü müla mota.
english: The king was exceedingly sorry, but for the sake of his oaths, and of his dinner guests, he didn't wish to refuse her.

chokri: Süsa küdeoe pu rikhumi püh kütsüh mha sü die pupo John pi püh vori nosü.
english: Immediately the king sent out a soldier of his guard, and commanded to bring John's head, and he went and beheaded him in the prison,

chokri: Mu pu tavo die küdücelü no pupi veyi die püh mükhu pühlü süh vori küde-o prü sü, mu pu süo püh rülipü prü sü, mu rülipü süo püh vori puzu prüva.
english: and brought his head on a platter, and gave it to the young lady; and the young lady gave it to her mother.

chokri: Süsa müthaküyiko pu dzüh sesü die ta vo pu rüngüco khi pühvo mhakhre pühlü khrüva.
english: When his disciples heard this, they came and took up his corpse, and laid it in a tomb.

chokri: Süsa müthaküyiko Jisu ce vori die puko dipüh thisü sheli mu dipühpüh po mütha sü sheli mütü po püh pu ce sü.
english: The apostles gathered themselves together to Jesus, and they told him all things, whatever they had done, and whatever they had taught.

chokri: Süze Pu sükoce po die, neko tho ta kresü mi ba kümo thi lü vori die rülü zayite; zita. Kükrelamori thüma taküta mu küvori krori kütala puko mha küti türi di zo küta la.
english: He said to them, "You come apart into a deserted place, and rest awhile." For there were many coming and going, and they had no leisure so much as to eat.

chokri: Sidie puko püzu pühyo no tasü mi ta kümora vota.
english: They went away in the boat to a desert place by themselves.

chokri: Süze puko tata küzü ngo sü die makroo humie Pu seyita, sidie rüna mütülü no uphe ta puko züra vo die vori pu ce künguyi ta.
english: They saw them going, and many recognized him and ran there on foot from all the cities. They arrived before them and came together to him.

chokri: Süze Jisu-e tapra thüma kroo ngo küsüce Pu lü müzhe ta; kükrelamori, süko tüko u kükhü küdi kro bi küta la, sidie pu mha kükro püh süko mütha shühdü ta.
english: Jesus came out, saw a great multitude, and he had compassion on them, because they were like sheep without a shepherd, and he began to teach them many things.

chokri: Süze tüsozhoo vo khrüh kütace Pu sadziko ta puce vori die sü, hicehi thüma lhüyo kümo thipüh zo, mu tsü münuri tazü-e.
english: When it was late in the day, his disciples came to him, and said, "This place is deserted, and it is late in the day.

chokri: Puko kütsüh püh u kümütu yikolü mu rünakolü sü die puko po mhahu khriyite, kükrelamori, puko mhapühri ti küto ba kümo la, zita.
english: Send them away, that they may go into the surrounding country and villages, and buy themselves bread, for they have nothing to eat."

chokri: Pu süko zü küla die sü, nekoe püh puko po tiyite, zita. Mu süko Pu yisü, hako po vo die tsükhrida raka kraküna ma khriyi die püh puko po ti tome? Zita.
english: But he answered them, "You give them something to eat." They asked him, "Shall we go and buy two hundred denarii worth of bread, and give them something to eat?"

chokri: Süsa Pu puko ce kütso die, neko tsükhrüda didü ba, vo die pete, zisü.
english: He said to them, "How many loaves do you have? Go see." When they knew, they said, "Five, and two fish."

chokri: Mu puko-e se küyice, pühngu mu khu küna le! Zisü. süsa Pu thüma mütü po ki nha kümüjo lüno u krokro thidie bayi nosü.
english: He commanded them that everyone should sit down in groups on the green grass.

chokri: Süsa puko ukro thükrakra mu hie pühngungu die bayi.
english: They sat down in ranks, by hundreds and by fifties.

chokri: Sidie Pu tsükhrüda pühngu mu khu künako khi pühyi die tosü tüyo pe die galiphi sü. Mu Pu tsükhrüdako cethi sü die Pu sadziko po khapüh puko mhuzülü sü, sülize, Pu khu künanyi cethi küza püh mütü prüsü.
english: He took the five loaves and the two fish, and looking up to heaven, he blessed and broke the loaves, and he gave to his disciples to set before them, and he divided the two fish among them all.

chokri: Sidie mütüko ti die mügu ve thiyi.
english: They all ate, and were filled.

chokri: Sülize puko-e kürako mu khu ti kümüshoko shi küngu pü becerha küriküna phuyi.
english: They took up twelve baskets full of broken pieces and also of the fish.

chokri: Süze tsükhrüda süko ti küyiko thüma nye pühngu müsüh.
english: Those who ate the loaves were five thousand men.

chokri: Sülize pue thümakroko kütsüh sü kübace Pu sadziko po püzu lüno ta zü lüshico Betsaida lü vo ta nosü.
english: Immediately he made his disciples get into the boat, and to go ahead to the other side, to Bethsaida, while he himself sent the multitude away.

chokri: Mu pue süko züh kükho kü ta sa küsho thi too tasü cejütuo vota.
english: After he had taken leave of them, he went up the mountain to pray.

chokri: Mu thüva tülü dpüzu-o dzükütü mütsulü tata. Mu pu pu lübi cejü küso lü bata.
english: When evening had come, the boat was in the midst of the sea, and he was alone on the land.

chokri: Sülize Pue tükhra kürüh puko mütsu die khra zü kütala dpüzu-o tho rühri zü küta ngo sü, mu Pu tüzüh-o rikhu küvale dao müsühce pukoce vori nyisa die dzü mhuyo no t a vori zü.
english: Seeing them distressed in rowing, for the wind was contrary to them, about the fourth watch of the night he came to them, walking on the sea, and he would have passed by them,

chokri: Süri puko-e Pu dzü mhuyo ta vori küsü ngo sü die Pu yisü thürümi püh ta lizu seta.
english: but they, when they saw him walking on the sea, supposed that it was a ghost, and cried out;

chokri: Kükrelamori, puko mütü Pu ngo die re ta, süla Pu rükri puko rüthi die sü, Nieko nüyite, I no zoho müprüh tahi, zita.
english: for they all saw him, and were troubled. But he immediately spoke with them, and said to them, "Cheer up! It is I! Don't be afraid."

chokri: Sidie pu Tasü dpüzulü pukoce kuri, mu tükhra-o lata; süsa puko unü mu u malhü thita.
english: He got into the boat with them; and the wind ceased, and they were very amazed among themselves, and marveled;

chokri: Kükrelamori, puko-e uko lü rüh ta die tsükhrüdakoyo mho u malhü thi küsüko co seyi mo kütala.
english: for they hadn't understood about the loaves, but their hearts were hardened.

chokri: Süsa puko tasü Ganezaret cejüce dzükütü shi kuyi.
english: When they had crossed over, they came to land at Gennesaret, and moored to the shore.

chokri: Mu puko püzu lüno ta prari küyice thümako pu se mhaita.
english: When they had come out of the boat, immediately the people recognized him,

chokri: Sidie süshi sümhü rünakolü mi küthikünyimiko püh thüziko yo süyi die rha püh Pu kübathilü vori ta.
english: and ran around that whole region, and began to bring those who were sick, on their mats, to where they heard he was.

chokri: Sülize pu rünalüri, nadelüri mori urari küvomütülü mi küthimiko püh vo lhethothikolü süyi die pu po u khüphreri püh süko nyi küsüri shota, mu pu nyi küyi khüo gwi thi nveyita.
english: Wherever he entered, into villages, or into cities, or into the country, they laid the sick in the marketplaces, and begged him that they might touch just the fringe of his garment; and as many as touched him were made well.

chokri: Sülize Phariseeko mu Lüsiküseko humi Jerusalem lüno tavori Puce künguyi.
english: Then the Pharisees, and some of the scribes gathered together to him, having come from Jerusalem.

chokri: Mu pukoe Pu sadziko humi ube mütümo die tsükhrüda ti küba ngo die thata.
english: Now when they saw some of his disciples eating bread with defiled, that is, unwashed, hands, they found fault.

chokri: Kükrelamori, Phariseemiko mu Jewmi koe mülhimiko zho müdzi die ube mütü kümothe mhati mhakra yo mo tila.
english: (For the Pharisees, and all the Jews, don't eat unless they wash their hands and forearms, holding to the tradition of the elders.

chokri: Süla pukoe lhethothi kolü ta die vori lize puko khrü kümüsa va kümothe mhati mhakra yomo. Süzo kümo ukrü zho kükre kükrori ngopüh züh tayo: süko ukhu uhe mu ule, müsüh püh küthi mhabühko mu mez kükhrü biti zhoko züh tayo.
english: They don't eat when they come from the marketplace, unless they bathe themselves, and there are many other things, which they have received to hold to: washings of cups, pitchers, bronze vessels, and couches.)

chokri: Süsa Phariseeko mu lüsiküsekoe Pu kütso die; dipüh la n sadzikoe ube mütümo die mhati die pethimi zhoko varhü tatale? Zita.
english: The Pharisees and the scribes asked him, "Why don't your disciples walk according to the tradition of the elders, but eat their bread with unwashed hands?"

chokri: Pue süko zü küla die sü; Isaiah sadzühküseoe neko küthürakütiko hila; hihibi die tho nve shüh püh müta. Thüma hiko u takhülü a rhoyo, eli uko lün azüh kümücu tayo.
english: He answered them, "Well did Isaiah prophesy of you hypocrites, as it is written, 'This people honors me with their lips, But their heart is far from me.

chokri: Ukoe thüma celün ngo küyi kümütha züko püh mi mütha die uko a rüsu thüdo thi tayo.
english: But in vain do they worship me, Teaching as doctrines the commandments of men.'

chokri: Nekoe pooe khaküsü zhoko khava die thüma celün küyie püh küvori zhoko: sükosü le mükhu tühe khrü küti zhoko mu süsübi küti kükro züh tayo, zita.
english: "For you set aside the commandment of God, and hold tightly to the tradition of men--the washing of pitchers and cups, and you do many other such things."

chokri: Mu Pu puko yisü, Nekoe netho nezhoko züh ba küta la Pooe zü khaküsüko khava vayo.
english: He said to them, "Full well do you reject the commandment of God, that you may keep your tradition.

chokri: Mosae poküti, Npo nzu rhoyite, mu upo uzu rüshu kütio posa tate, ziyo.
english: For Moses said, 'Honor your father and your mother;' and, 'He who speaks evil of father or mother, let him be put to death.'

chokri: Eli nekoe po küti; mhaceno mipühe pupo mori pu zu yisü; Korban te, süosü acelü nve küyi müsühko püh Poo prüve zi küsüce puyo mhapühri thi mota die pu khasü vayo.
english: But you say, 'If a man tells his father or his mother, "Whatever profit you might have received from me is Corban, that is to say, given to God;"'

chokri: Mu süo pupo puzu la mhapühri thisü mo tayo.
english: then you no longer allow him to do anything for his father or his mother,

chokri: Sidie ne zho kha küsüko la Poo züko khi püh puco küdi thiva yo, Mu nekoe süsü bi ti kükre thi tayo.
english: making void the word of God by your tradition, which you have handed down. You do many things like this."

chokri: Sidie Pu makroo ci püh Puce süyi die sü, mütüko a khü rünyiyi die seyite;
english: He called all the multitude to himself, and said to them, "Hear me, all of you, and understand.

chokri: Shozuco no thüma lü lütiko pühri thüma va mürho vayomo, eli mha pu lüno küprariko sükosü thüma va mürho vayo.
english: There is nothing from outside of the man, that going into him can defile him; but the things which proceed out of the man are those that defile the man.

chokri: Thüma kühopühri uni ne die rüniyi kütomi po rüniyite, zita.
english: If anyone has ears to hear, let him hear!"

chokri: Süze Pu thüma kroo lüno tasü ce pühlü lü küyice Pu sadzikoe züve süo co kütso ta.
english: When he had entered into a house away from the multitude, his disciples asked him about the parable.

chokri: Mu Pu süko yisü, nekori mha se kükreyi mozo me? Shozuco no püh thüma lülü tikoe thüma va mürho va yomo.
english: He said to them, "Are you thus without understanding also? Don't you perceive that whatever goes into the man from outside can't defile him,

chokri: Kükrelamori, sükosüe pu mülü lü mo die pu vadelü vo tayo, sidie mha mütü thi kümüsa va die putho pusho müsühlü laprari tayo küti nekoe se mo me?
english: because it doesn't go into his heart, but into his stomach, then into the latrine, thus making all foods clean?"

chokri: Mu Pue podie; thüma lüno küprari kosü mi thi mürho vayo.
english: He said, "That which proceeds out of the man, that defiles the man.

chokri: Kükrelamori, thüma lüno küprari kosü, lüdoküsu, kükhrüküzüh küti, kürüzü.
english: For from within, out of the hearts of men, proceed evil thoughts, adulteries, sexual sins, murders, thefts,

chokri: Küdokhri, kürüyo, mhakümüho, ugwiküne, küphoma külü, müho münyi küti.
english: covetings, wickedness, deceit, lustful desires, an evil eye, blasphemy, pride, and foolishness.

chokri: Mi ngo umhü külo, Poo kütha, kümüko, kübado, mhaküsü hikohi mütü thüma lüno prari die thüma thi müsu vayo.
english: All these evil things come from within, and defile the man."

chokri: Sülize Pu sülüno süri die tasü Tyre mu Sidon taralü voriyi. Sidie Pu cepühlü lüri küyicie mipühri po senyi mota, süri Pu rüsü kündi ta.
english: From there he arose, and went away into the borders of Tyre and Sidon. He entered into a house, and didn't want anyone to know it, but he couldn't escape notice.

chokri: Kükrelamori, Pu küvori sesü die, thünopühmi püh Pu nupüe küsu rüpü po kübü küta pü püh vori die tü Pu phelü prüta.
english: For a woman, whose little daughter had an unclean spirit, having heard of him, came and fell down at his feet.

chokri: Thüno hipüe Syrofonicia yemi Greekmipü püh, pu Jisu po pu nupü yo thürüküsü rüpüo huva no die mülo büh ta.
english: Now the woman was a Greek, a Syrophoenician by race. She begged him that he would cast the demon out of her daughter.

chokri: Süsa Jisu pu yisü; natsimiko po mügu mürate, kükrelamori natsimi lüva khiyi die kügü püh tüshiko mhuzülü sü küti vemo tila, zita.
english: But Jesus said to her, "Let the children be filled first, for it is not appropriate to take the children's bread and throw it to the dogs."

chokri: Süri süpü Puce po la die sü, süri Nyepoo, tüshikori mez khrolüno natsimi lüva kürako pati yo zo le, zita.
english: But she answered him, "Yes, Lord. Yet even the dogs under the table eat the children's crumbs."

chokri: Mu Jisu süpü yisü, zü hio la no tavo tate, küsu rüpüo n nupü lüno ta prari taho, zita.
english: He said to her, "For this saying, go your way. The demon has gone out of your daughter."

chokri: Sidie pu celü vori tice küsu rupüo punupü lüno ta prata die pu püh thüzi lü müzü küta ngoyi.
english: She went away to her house, and found the child having been laid on the bed, with the demon gone out.

chokri: Li cono ta la sü Decapolis püthü sü Galilee dzükütü co voriyi.
english: Again he departed from the borders of Tyre and Sidon, and came to the sea of Galilee, through the midst of the region of Decapolis.

chokri: Sücesü mi uni rüji mu Züpo küyidu küta mi püh züh Pu ce vori die Pupo Pu be püh punyi sü no die shota.
english: They brought to him one who was deaf and had an impediment in his speech. They begged him to lay his hand on him.

chokri: Süla Pu makroo dolüno süo züh prari die, Pu becine püh pu nicelü süyi mu mhütsa sü die pu meyi nyi sü.
english: He took him aside from the multitude, privately, and put his fingers into his ears, and he spat, and touched his tongue.

chokri: Sidie tosü tüyo pe die mühi sü, sidie süo yisü “Ephphatha” puco veyi tate, zisü.
english: Looking up to heaven, he sighed, and said to him, "Ephphatha!" that is, "Be opened!"

chokri: Süzi sü mütha pu ni veyi die mha khü seyi ta, mu pu meyi ri veyi die Züpo utü thiyi ta.
english: Immediately his ears were opened, and the impediment of his tongue was released, and he spoke clearly.

chokri: Mu Pu puko po püh mipühceri pohi no sü, eli Pu uko po püh pohi noku mu ukoe püh po küthi sakuta.
english: He commanded them that they should tell no one, but the more he commanded them, so much the more widely they proclaimed it.

chokri: Sidie pukoe u malhü thizo die sü; pu mha mütü thi nve süve, Pu niji miri po mhakhü seyite, mu züpo kethomiri po züpoyite, zita.
english: They were astonished beyond measure, saying, "He has done all things well. He makes even the deaf hear, and the mute speak!"

chokri: Sü tüso ce kosü thüma kro müzürita, mu ukoe mhapühri ti küto ta mota, süla Pu sadziko cipüh Puce süyi die puko yisü.
english: In those days, when there was a very great multitude, and they had nothing to eat, Jesus called his disciples to himself, and said to them,

chokri: I thüma kroo phimüzhete, kükrelamori puko tha zhotü süh a züh bate, mu puko mhapüri ti küto bamo.
english: "I have compassion on the multitude, because they have stayed with me now three days, and have nothing to eat.

chokri: Mu I puko po tükhü thi die kütsüh püh puko celü sü lize uko sholü phesa tato, kükrelamori puko humie kümüprüh shüh lüno vori tina, zita.
english: If I send them away fasting to their home, they will faint on the way, for some of them have come a long way."

chokri: Süsa Pu sadzikoe pu zü küla die; kühopühn sheri dibi die rayhothi hilühi no tsükhrüda ngo püh vagu thiyi veta? Zita.
english: His disciples answered him, "From where could one satisfy these people with bread here in a deserted place?"

chokri: Süsa Pu süko kütso die; nieko tsükhrüda ditsu pü ta la? Sisü, mu pukoe thüna, sidie küla sü.
english: He asked them, "How many loaves do you have?" They said, "Seven."

chokri: Süsa Pu thüma kroo po ki cejü bayi nosü, süze Pu tsükhrüda thünako pühyi die küsho thisü die cethi püh Pu sadziko po püh zasü nosü, mu pukoe püh zasü.
english: He commanded the multitude to sit down on the ground, and he took the seven loaves. Having given thanks, he broke them, and gave them to his disciples to serve, and they served the multitude.

chokri: Pukoe khu hu püta, mu Pue galiphi sü die puko po süo ri püh zasü
english: They had a few small fish. Having blessed them, he said to serve these also.

chokri: Pukoe süko ti püh u küphru küle thiyi, mu tishoko becerha thüna küngu layi.
english: They ate, and were filled. They took up seven baskets of broken pieces that were left over.

chokri: Mu ti küyiko mütü küsa thüma nyeda müsüh.
english: Those who had eaten were about four thousand. Then he sent them away.

chokri: Süsa Pu süko kütsüh süva, sidie rükri Pu sadziko züh die püzu lü lüyi die Dalmanutha cejübüh co vorita.
english: Immediately he entered into the boat with his disciples, and came into the region of Dalmanutha.

chokri: Mu Phariseekoe tapra die Pu müdephi küzwü Pu celüno tüyo küvori zasi phu die Pu züh kümülita.
english: The Pharisees came out and began to question him, seeking from him a sign from heaven, and testing him.

chokri: Sücesü pue Pu rüpülüno mühi die sü; dipühla tsüdonumiko zasi püh phuyo la? I nekoce potü süto; zasi pühri püh tsüdonumi prü sü lhote; zita.
english: He sighed deeply in his spirit, and said, "Why does this generation seek a sign? Most assuredly I tell you, no sign will be given to this generation."

chokri: Sidie Pu süko khava die püzu pühlü lü die ta kükreco kuta.
english: He left them, and again entering into the boat, departed to the other side.

chokri: Sülize müthaküyikoe tsükhrüda püta rükra mota, püzu püh libi pü küta zota.
english: They forgot to take bread; and they didn't have more than one loaf in the boat with them.

chokri: Mu Jisu puko tarho die sü; Hei! Neko Phariseeko khreo mu Herod khreo no münu yite; zisü.
english: He charged them, saying, "Take heed: beware of the yeast of the Pharisees and the yeast of Herod."

chokri: Süsa puko tho kürüthi die sü; ukoe tsükhrüda bamoe; zita.
english: They reasoned with one another, saying, "It's because we have no bread."

chokri: Hio sesü die Jisu puko yisü; nekoe dipüh la tsükhrüda ba kümo küpo ba la? Nekoe se kükreyi mori ne salüyi mo zo lu me? Nekoe tsüri neko lü rüh ba zo me?
english: Jesus, perceiving it, said to them, "Why do you reason that it's because you have no bread? Don't you perceive yet, neither understand? Is your heart still hardened?

chokri: Neko mhü ba zori nekoe ngo mome? Neko ni ba zori nekoe mha khü seyo mome?
english: Having eyes, don't you see? Having ears, don't you hear? Don't you remember?

chokri: Mu Ie tsükhrüda pühngu püh mi nye pühngu prü küsü mu becerha didüpüh shi küngu layi sü küti rükra mo te me? Zita. Pukoe sü Pu ce; küriküna; zisü.
english: When I broke the five loaves among the five thousand, how many baskets full of broken pieces did you take up?" They told him, "Twelve."

chokri: Sülize thünako püh mi nyeda vathi küsüce ti kümüshoko püh becerha didüpüh phru layia? zita, Pukoe sü; thüna; zisü.
english: "When the seven loaves fed the four thousand, how many baskets full of broken pieces did you take up?" They told him, "Seven."

chokri: Süsa Pu süko yisü; nekoe tsüri seyi molu me? Zita.
english: He asked them, "Don't you understand, yet?"

chokri: Süsa pu Bethsaidalü voriyi, mu mi mhücemi püh rhapüh Puce vori die Pupo nyi sü no die mülo ta.
english: He came to Bethsaida. They brought a blind man to him, and begged him to touch him.

chokri: Mu Pu mhüce süo beceyi die züh rüna shi prata. Sidie pu mhütsapüh pu mhülü sü die Pu be püh puyo südie pue mhapühri ngo ba me moshi küti kütso.
english: He took hold of the blind man by the hand, and brought him out of the village. When he had spit on his eyes, and laid his hands on him, he asked him if he saw anything.

chokri: Pue mha pe sü die poti; I thümako ngopüh sühboko ta küba bite; zisü.
english: He looked up, and said, "I see men; for I see them like trees walking."

chokri: Süsa pue Pu be püh pu mhü nyilasü die pupo müsozu pe sü nosü, sücesü pu vetsühyi ta die mha mütü ngo mütsüce ta.
english: Then again he laid his hands on his eyes. He looked intently, and was restored, and saw everyone clearly.

chokri: Süsa Pu süo kütsüh püh pu celü süva, mu pu yisü, no ta rünalü vohi mori püh rünalü mipüh ceri pohite; ziva.
english: He sent him away to his house, saying, "Don't enter into the village, nor tell anyone in the village."

chokri: Mu Jisu mu müthaküyikoe tasü Kaisaria Philppi lü Prata, süze Pu sholüno Pu sadziko kütsodie; mi a kha sopüh liyoa? zita.
english: Jesus went out, with his disciples, into the villages of Caesarea Philippi. On the way he asked his disciples, "Who do men say that I am?"

chokri: Mu pukoe küla die; John mi dzülu khaküsüo zi, humi Elijah li, mu kükre koe sadzüküsemi, pühli ziyo; zisü.
english: They told him, "John the Baptizer, and others say Elijah, but others: one of the prophets."

chokri: Mu Pue puko yisü; sülize nekoe a yisü sopüh lizu yoa? zisü. Süce Peter sü ‘No Khristao zo;’ zisü.
english: He said to them, "But who do you say that I am?" Peter answered, "You are the Christ."

chokri: Mu Pue puko küthi die puko po püh mipüh ceri pohi nota.
english: He charged them that they should tell no one about him.

chokri: Silize pue Pu sadzikoce thümanuoe mha kükrolü kümüzhe küto, pethiko, sekrüko, mu lüsiküseko po Pu müla die Pudokhri va küto, sidie zho süh nha rhühlasüta küto jühko püh puko mütha sü ta.
english: He began to teach them that the Son of Man must suffer many things, and be rejected by the elders, the chief priests, and the scribes, and be killed, and after three days rise again.

chokri: Mu Pu zü hio po müle mülu zota. Süsa Peter Pu züh kre phe die Pu kütüta.
english: He spoke to them openly. Peter took him, and began to rebuke him.

chokri: Süri pu erürüh vo müthaküyiko ngo küyice Pu Peter küthi die sü; Thürüküsüo Satan no ta asaco vo tayi, kükrelamori, no Poo ceco lü moli thümao ceco lüyo tila; zita.
english: But he, turning around, and seeing his disciples, rebuked Peter, and said, "Get behind me, Satan! For you have in mind not the things of God, but the things of men."

chokri: Sidie Jisue thüma kroo mu Pu sadziko ci püh puce süyi die pukoce; Thüma kühopühri a sa müdzi künyimi putho pu müla va die pu cross püh die asa müdziyite.
english: He called the multitude to himself with his disciples, and said to them, "Whoever wants to come after me, let him deny himself, and take up his cross, and follow me.

chokri: Kükrelamori, thüma kühopühri putho pu kürhühro mükrüyi nyisa yo kütimi pu süo müdi vato, eli thüma kühopühri ala mu züyiküeo la pu kürhühro müka küsümi pu süo külayito.
english: For whoever wants to save his life will lose it; and whoever will lose his life for my sake and the Gospel's will save it.

chokri: Kükrelamori, mipüh-e cejü mütü prapüh pu zü thiyi zori pu kürhühro müka va lize pu dipüh küpra ngoyi ta?
english: For what does it profit a man, to gain the whole world, and forfeit his life?

chokri: Mori thüma püh-e dipüh püh pu kürhühro züh küleyi veta?
english: For what will a man give in exchange for his life?

chokri: Thüma kühopühri kürüzü mu küphoma phru küpü nulhü hio mhuzülü aza mu a züko püh po münga ta lize; thümanuo rio Pu Poo zapo zale lüno tüyoshoyi kümüsakozüh küvori nha pu za po münga tato.
english: For whoever will be ashamed of me and of my words in this adulterous and sinful generation, the Son of Man also will be ashamed of him, when he comes in the glory of his Father with the holy angels."

chokri: Pu uko ce po die, I nekoce potü süto, Hice tha kübako humie Poo küde kuku süh küvori ngo kümothe küsa ngo lhozo, zita.
english: He said to them, "Most assuredly I tell you, there are some standing here who will in no way taste death until they see the Kingdom of God come with power."

chokri: Sülize zho shorüh sa Jisue Peter, jakob mu John uko züh die mükhrüh no kresü cejütu kürükrü püyo khota, sidie Pu süko mhuzü lüno Pu külhü thi küdeyi ta.
english: After six days Jesus took with him Peter, James, and John, and brought them up onto a high mountain privately by themselves, and he was changed into another form in front of them.

chokri: Mu Pu khüneko vekrü mu müga püh makrekro bi die cejülü khümüne pühri thi kümüga püh süsü biyi külhopü thita.
english: His clothing became glistening, exceedingly white, like snow, such as no launderer on earth can whiten them.

chokri: Mu puko Elijah Moses züh die Jisu puko kürüthi küba ngota.
english: Elijah and Moses appeared to them, and they were talking with Jesus.

chokri: Süze Peter Jisu ce po die; Kümüthao uko hicehi ba lize uko la veto le. Mu uko pro süh thiyi khe; püh nla mu püh Moses mu püh Elijah la, zita.
english: Peter answered Jesus, "Rabbi, it is good for us to be here. Let's make three tents: one for you, one for Moses, and one for Elijah."

chokri: Kükrelamori, uko müprühri küta la pu dipüh poto sheli küti lüyi mo küta la.
english: For he didn't know what to say, for they were very afraid.

chokri: Sücesü kümhü püh vori die puko süh püyita. Mu kümhülüno mhakhü püh vori die, hihi anu a khrüo zo, Pu khü rünyiyite, zita.
english: A cloud came, overshadowing them, and a voice came out of the cloud, "This is my beloved Son. Listen to him."

chokri: Mu süzi sü mütha pukoe lico hico pesü eli Jisu mu puko sa mi kükremi pühri ngo sa mota.
english: Suddenly looking around, they saw no one with them any more, except Jesus only.

chokri: Sidie puko cejütu yono ta kiri küzüce pue pukoce; Thümanuo rhülasü ta kümothe pukoe dipüh ngo sheli ti püh mipühceri pohi nota.
english: As they were coming down from the mountain, he charged them that they should tell no one what things they had seen, until after the Son of Man had risen from the dead.

chokri: Sidie pukoe rhülaküsü sü puco dipüh sheli küti puko tho puko dolü kütso küga bata.
english: They kept this saying to themselves, questioning what the "rising from the dead" meant.

chokri: Sidie pukoe Pu kütso die; Elijah po vori müra mosü lie lüsiküsekoe pota mome? zita.
english: They asked him, saying, "Why do the scribes say that Elijah must come first?"

chokri: Süsa Pue puko zü küla die po küti; Elijah vori die mha mütü thi nve lasü to lizi sü kütho; sülize dibi die thümanuo la; Pu po mha kükrolü kümüzhe mu mi po Putha to, zi küti thopüh müta la?
english: He said to them, "Elijah indeed comes first, and restores all things. How is it written about the Son of Man, that he should suffer many things and be despised?

chokri: Süri I nekoce po süto; Elijah ri vori vazo, sidie pu jüh thopüh kümüta bi die pukoe puyo dipüh püh thinyi sheli thive.
english: But I tell you that Elijah has come, and they have also done to him whatever they wanted to, even as it is written about him."

chokri: Mu Pu müthaküyiko ce pheri tice thüma krode püh puko hu püba mu lüsiküsekoe püko züh kümüli küba ngoyi.
english: Coming to the disciples, he saw a great multitude around them, and scribes questioning them.

chokri: Süze thüma kro süko mütü pungo sü mütha puko malhü thi die ta Puce vori Pu külu ta.
english: Immediately all the multitude, when they saw him, were greatly amazed, and running to him greeted him.

chokri: Süsa Pu lüsiküseko kütso die sü; nekoe dipüh süh puko züh kümüli bala?
english: He asked the scribes, "What are you asking them?"

chokri: Sücesü thüma kroo dolüno mipüh Pu khü küla die; Kümüthao I anu züpokütho rüpü püh po u kübü ta küta züh nce vori züle, kühopührari pu kübü vori lize pu nü kütu vava yo, sidie pu mhütsa rügwi rhührhüh die pu hu thüzheyi tayo, süsa pu rükhato die sü mama ta züle!
english: One of the multitude answered, "Teacher, I brought to you my son, who has a mute spirit;

chokri: I nsadziko po süo huva no die sho sü eli pukoe huyi mote, zita.
english: and wherever it seizes him, it throws him down, and he foams at the mouth, and grinds his teeth, and wastes away. I asked your disciples to cast it out, and they weren't able."

chokri: Süsa Pu süko zü küla die Hu! Mülü kümo shuyeko; I zho didüpüh neko züh bata? Pu züh ace vorite, zita.
english: He answered him, "Unbelieving generation, how long shall I be with you? How long shall I bear with you? Bring him to me."

chokri: Süze pukoe züh Puce vori, mu Pungo küsüce rüpüoe pu müthi shühta, sidie pu rütu cejü prü die pu mhütsa mülu mu rükhürüla züta.
english: They brought him to him, and when he saw him, immediately the spirit convulsed him, and he fell on the ground, wallowing and foaming at the mouth.

chokri: Mu Pu süo ce süsübi küta di tüthepüh teshi küti kütso, mu pue pu natsilü no süzi süri ta lizi sü.
english: He asked his father, "How long has it been since this has come to him?" He said, "From childhood.

chokri: Sülize pub rü müsu va kütola tsükünu pu kügü püh melü ri sü mu zülü ri süyo, süla no mhahu thiyive lize ha phimüzhe die ha khrühi süte, zita.
english: Often it has cast him both into the fire and into the water, to destroy him. But if you can do anything, have compassion on us, and help us."

chokri: Süsa Jisu pu yisü; no mülüyi lize mülüküyimi la mha mütü thiyi ve zo, zisü.
english: Jesus said to him, "If you can believe, all things are possible to him who believes."

chokri: Süzi sü mütha nu süo poe kra pu mhüzü krüh zo die; sü Nyepoo iri mülü bazo, akümülü küthathi hi khrühi süyi, zita.
english: Immediately the father of the child cried out with tears, "I believe. Help my unbelief!"

chokri: Süsa thüma kroo ta kühu vori küzü ngo sü die, Jisue müsa kümo rüpüo küthi die sü; züpokütho mu niji rüpü, I nce po süyo, no pu yono ta prari tayi mu puyo lalü sahite, zita.
english: When Jesus saw that a multitude came running together, he rebuked the unclean spirit, saying to him, "You mute and deaf spirit, I command you, come out of him, and never enter him again!"

chokri: Süsa rüpü süo müto mu pu müthi shüh sü die prarita. Sidie pu küsami bi pü züta; thüma kükroe pu yisü sazüte lizita.
english: Having cried out, and convulsed greatly, it came out of him. The boy became like one dead; so much that most of them said, "He is dead."

chokri: Süri Jisu pu be ceyi die pu phu müsü mu pu süthayi ta.
english: But Jesus took him by the hand, and raised him up; and he arose.

chokri: Süze pu ceo lü, lü küyice müthaküyiko mükhrüh Pu kütso die sü; dipüh la hako tsüo huyi mota la? Zita.
english: When he had come into the house, his disciples asked him privately, "Why couldn't we cast it out?"

chokri: Pu puko yisü; küsho mu kümvo lüno zo kümosa mha hipühi prayi yomo, zita.
english: He said to them, "This kind can come out by nothing, except by prayer and fasting."

chokri: Mu puko sülüno tasü Galilee cejü püthü vota, sülize Pu mipühri po süo senyi bamo.
english: They went out from there, and passed through Galilee. He didn't want anyone to know it.

chokri: Sülize Pu Pu sadziko mütha die pukoce po küti; Thümanuo tüpüh mi belü sü die Pu dokhrito; mu Pu dokhri küvasa Pue zho süh nha rhüla sü tato.
english: For he was teaching his disciples, and said to them, "The Son of Man is being handed over to the hands of men, and they will kill him; and when he is killed, on the third day he will rise again."

chokri: Süri pukoe zü süo seyi mota, eli Pu kütso müprüh ta.
english: But they didn't understand the saying, and were afraid to ask him.

chokri: Süsa puko tasü Kapenamlü voriyi, sülize puko cepühlü lüri küyice; pue puko kütsodie sü; nekoe sholüno nekotho neko dolü dipüh po kümüli ta la? Zita.
english: He came to Capernaum, and when he was in the house he asked them, "What were you arguing among yourselves on the way?"

chokri: Eli pukoe zühta kükrelamori, puko huhu yisü sopüh-e küzhotho thito sheli kümüli ta küta la.
english: But they were silent, for they had disputed one with another on the way about who was the greatest.

chokri: Sübi ba die Pu müthaküyiko ci sü die puko yisü; mhaceno thüma kühopüri küra thoo thinyi lize pue mütüko dolü künuthoo thita to, mu mütüko cetao thita to.
english: He sat down, and called the twelve; and he said to them, "If any man wants to be first, he shall be last of all, and servant of all."

chokri: Sidie Pu natsimi püh züh vori puko dolü süyi; mu Pu süo rhapühyi die sü puko ce;
english: He took a little child, and set him in the midst of them. Taking him in his arms, he said to them,

chokri: Thüma kühopühri a zalüno natsimi hihi bi kütiko zühyiyo lize, azühri yiyo. Mu kühopühri a zühyi yo timi a züh küyi zo mori a kütsüh küsüo ri zühyiyo.
english: "Whoever receives one such little child in my name, receives me, and whoever receives me, doesn't receive me, but him who sent me."

chokri: Sücesü John Pu zü küla die sü; Kümüthao, humi n za lüno küsu rüpüko huta die hakoe ngo; eli pu u sa müdzi yomo; mu pue u samüdzi kümola hakoe pu khave; zisü.
english: John said to him, "Teacher, we saw someone who doesn't follow us casting out demons in your name; and we forbade him, because he doesn't follow us."

chokri: Süri Jisu sü; Pu khahite, kükrelamori, mipühri a zalüno kuku mhatho thita die rükri lasü athata yilho tila.
english: But Jesus said, "Don't forbid him, for there is no one who will do a mighty work in my name, and be able quickly to speak evil of me.

chokri: Kükrelamori, u mütsuta kümoko sü uceco zo.
english: For whoever is not against us is on our side.

chokri: Mu thüma kühopühri neko yi Khrista zü lie no a zalün züko hepühri püh neko po krayo lizo, I neko ce potü süto, mhapühceri pu zhokhro müdi vayi lhozo.
english: For whoever will give you a cup of water to drink in my name, because you are Christ's, most assuredly I tell you, he will in no way lose his reward.

chokri: Sülize thüma kühopühri a mülüyo küti künyi hikohi mipühri phe kümüsü thi süyo lize, mhakügütsü püh pu volü khüsü die pu kügü püh dzükütülü sü küva veku.
english: Whoever will cause one of these little ones who believe in me to stumble, it would be better for him if he was thrown into the sea with a millstone hung around his neck.

chokri: Mhaceno nbe nthi müsü lize, süo dathü vate; kükrelamori, lalhe die kürhüthilü lü kütae nbe künanyi ba die tasü mezalü me mhapüceri mhü tayi külho;
english: If your hand causes you to stumble, cut it off. It is better for you to enter into life maimed, rather than having your two hands to go into Gehenna, into the unquenchable fire,

chokri: Mu me sülü ukhokori satayokümo mhü ri tayo kümo lü vo küta ceri veku zo tila.
english: 'where their worm doesn't die, and the fire is not quenched.'

chokri: Mu n phe nthi müsü yo lize; süo da kügü vate, kükrelamori phelhe die kürhülü lü kütae n phe kenanyi ba die n kügü püh Gehenna me mhü tayo kümo mu
english: If your foot causes you to stumble, cut it off. It is better for you to enter into life lame, rather than having your two feet to be cast into Gehenna, into the fire that will never be quenched--

chokri: sülü ukhokori satayo kümo mu mhü “tayo” kümomelü sü küvace veku zotila.
english: 'where their worm doesn't die, and the fire is not quenched.'

chokri: Mhaceno nmhü nthi müsu yo lize; süo kürüla vate, kükrelamori n mhü künanyi ba die n kügü püh mezalü sü küva ceri nmhü püh libi die Poo küdeba lü vo küta veku ti la, n mhü küna khi pü meza lü lü küta ceri.
english: If your eye causes you to stumble, cast it out. It is better for you to enter into the Kingdom of God with one eye, rather than having two eyes to be cast into the Gehenna of fire,

chokri: Sülü sathi sa yomo me ri mhü tayo mo
english: 'where their worm doesn't die, and the fire is not quenched.'

chokri: Kükrelamori me süh die thüma kühopüh yori mütsazü rüto; mu mütsazü süh die rüsu kühopühri rüto.
english: For everyone will be salted with fire, and every sacrifice will be seasoned with salt.

chokri: Mütsae vezo, eli mütsari pu külhü dita lize no dipüh süh die süo thi mülhüyi ta? Mütsa po nelü bayi te, mu huhu züh küzühve bayite.
english: Salt is good, but if the salt has lost its saltiness, with what will you season it? Have salt in yourselves, and be at peace with one another."

chokri: Süsa Jisue sülüno süri die Jordan rü poyo no tasü Judealü vorita, mu thüma kroo la vori die Puce künguyi, sidie Putho Pu zhocethi die süko mütha sü.
english: He arose from there and came into the borders of Judea and beyond the Jordan. Multitudes came together to him again. As he usually did, he was again teaching them.

chokri: Sücesü Phariseeko Pu müno vori Pu küdo die; mipüh pu cemi kha küva thüzho to me moshi küti kütso.
english: Pharisees came to him testing him, and asked him, "Is it lawful for a man to divorce his wife?"

chokri: Mu Pu süko zü küla die sü; Mosae neko po dibi no die zü kha sü la? Zi die kütso ta.
english: He answered, "What did Moses command you?"

chokri: Pukoe po die; Mosae kha küva lüsi püh tho sü die süpü kha va no süzo; zita.
english: They said, "Moses allowed a certificate of divorce to be written, and to divorce her."

chokri: Süze Jisu puko zü küla die sü; neko lü kürüh la pue zü hio kha küsü zo.
english: But Jesus said to them, "For your hardness of heart, he wrote you this commandment.

chokri: Süri Pooe tü cejü thisü prühlüno thüpumi mu thünomi thisü.
english: But from the beginning of the creation, 'God made them male and female.

chokri: Hihi la thüma püh-e pupo puzu vava die pu cemi züh püh thita to.
english: For this cause a man will leave his father and mother, and will join to his wife,

chokri: Mu unyi mi küna thisa mori thipüh thita to, zisü.
english: and the two will become one flesh,' so that they are no longer two, but one flesh.

chokri: Süla Pooe thi küma küsüo thüma po cekho va hite; zisü.
english: What therefore God has joined together, let no man separate."

chokri: Süsari müthaküyikoe celüno süsü biti kütso lata.
english: In the house, his disciples asked him again about the same matter.

chokri: Süla Pu sükoce po die; thüma kühopühri pu cemi khava die thüno kükre yita lize pu süpü züh die kürügü kürüzü thita zo.
english: He said to them, "Whoever divorces his wife, and marries another, commits adultery against her.

chokri: Mu thünomi püh-e pu nupu khava die kükremice ne ta lize pu kürüzü thita zo.
english: If a woman herself divorces her husband, and marries another, she commits adultery."

chokri: Sülize Pupo brü sü no die mi natsimiko züh puce vori, süri müthaküyikoe süko rüta.
english: They were bringing to him little children, that he should touch them, but the disciples rebuked those who were bringing them.

chokri: Mu süsü ngo die Jisu Pu lü rünü mota, sidie sü; natsimiko po ace vorite; mu puko kha hite, kükrelamori Poo küdee süko zü zo tila.
english: But when Jesus saw it, he was moved with indignation, and said to them, "Allow the little children to come to me! Don't forbid them, for the Kingdom of God belongs to such as these.

chokri: I nekoce potü süto, thüma kühopühri natsimi bi zo die Poo küde yiyi mo lize sülü lüyi kündizo.
english: Most assuredly I tell you, whoever will not receive the Kingdom of God like a little child, he will in no way enter into it."

chokri: Sidie pu süko rhapühyi die pu be shopüh süko yosü die galiphi sü.
english: He took them in his arms, and blessed them, laying his hands on them.

chokri: Süsa pu sholü ta vo küzüce mipüh ta puce vori pumhüzülü shetüyi die sü; kümütha keveo, küshothe kürhüh püh azü thiyi küto la I dipüh thi mosüa? zita.
english: As he was going out into the way, one ran to him, knelt before him, and asked him, "Good Teacher, what shall I do that I may inherit eternal life?"

chokri: Eli Jisu pu yisü; No dipüh la a püh küveo lie ciyo la? Mipüh, süo sü Poo zo kümosa keve di.
english: Jesus said to him, "Why do you call me good? No one is good except one-- God.

chokri: Noe, kürügü kürüzü thi sü, mi dokhri su, mi rügo su, kücezü zhopo thi su, cezü die mi swüti su, npo nzu rhoyi, zi die zü kha küsüko se ba tho? Zita
english: You know the commandments: 'Do not murder,' 'Do not commit adultery,' 'Do not steal,' 'Do not give false testimony,' 'Do not defraud,' 'Honor your father and mother.'"

chokri: Pue pu zü küla die; kümüthao, I a natsilüno hikohi mütü züh sürite; zita.
english: He said to him, "Teacher, I have observed all these things from my youth."

chokri: Jisu pupe tha die pu krü die sü; no mhapüh tsomo balu, vo die n mha dipühpüh ba sheli mütü zhüh püh küdimi prü vayi, sülize no tüyo cene neyito sidie vori n cross pühyi die a sa müdziyite; zita.
english: Jesus looking at him loved him, and said to him, "One thing you lack. Go, sell whatever you have, and give to the poor, and you will have treasure in heaven; and come, follow me, taking up the cross."

chokri: Eli pu zü süo la pu thü südie tata, kükrelamori pu mha kükro kübami ti la.
english: But his face fell at that saying, and he went away sorrowful, for he was one who had great possessions.

chokri: Süsa Jisu rürüh lico hico pe die, pu müthaküyiko yisü, Mhakünemikoe Poo küdelü lüyi küto la didüpüh rüh to shehe, zita.
english: Jesus looked around, and said to his disciples, "How difficult it is for those who have riches to enter into the Kingdom of God!"

chokri: Sücesü müthaküyikoe Pu zü süko la u malhü thita. Süla Jisu pukoce po la die; Natsiko, thüma cene mülüyo tiko Poo küdelü lüyi küto rüh zoto le he.
english: The disciples were amazed at his words. But Jesus answered again, "Children, how hard is it for those who trust in riches to enter into the Kingdom of God!

chokri: Kükrelamori, mhakünemi püh-e Poo küdelü lü küyiceri Ut püh pon thüprüh celü müla pra kütae rüyi ku to ti la, zita.
english: It is easier for a camel to go through a needle's eye than for a rich man to enter into the Kingdom of God."

chokri: Sücesü pukoe u malhü thi mizüri zo ta die puko tho puko dolü küpo die sü; Süzilize sopüh-e külayi “veta.” Zita.
english: They were exceedingly astonished, saying to him, "Then who can be saved?"

chokri: Eli, Jisu puko pe die sü; Thüma celacoe thiyi su, eli Poo celacoe süzimo. Kükrelamori, Pooe mha mütü thiyi zo tila, zita.
english: Jesus, looking at them, said, "With men it is impossible, but not with God, for all things are possible with God."

chokri: Süsa Pitore Puce; yhie; Ha mha mütü vava die n sa müdzi tate; zita.
english: Peter began to tell him, "Behold, we have left all, and have followed you."

chokri: Eli Jisu pu zü küla die, I nekoc e potü süto; Mipühri ala mu züyiküveo la pu ce mori pu sazühzüra, mori pu lipüne, pupo mori puzu, mori pu cemi mori pu nuneko mori pu cejü vava die;
english: Jesus said, "Most assuredly I tell you, there is no one who has left house, or brothers, or sisters, or father, or mother, or wife, or children, or land, for my sake, and for the Gospel's sake,

chokri: Tsüdo külhü ce mipo u müthimünyi küti zo kümosa u cekro mu u sazühzürako mu u lipüneko mu puzuneko mu u nuneko mu u cejüko dü thükra ngoyi külho ba mo; sü libizomori vori küto külhü lü küshothe kürhühri ngo yito.
english: but he will receive one hundred times more now in this time, houses, brothers, sisters, mothers, children, and land, with persecutions; and in the age to come eternal life.

chokri: Süri kürami kükro nu tato, mu künumi rata to; zita.
english: But many who are first will be last; and the last first."

chokri: Süsa puko Jerusalemlü kho tozü, mu Jisue puko mhuzü ta, süce pukoe u malhü thita, mu puko sa müdzi kütakoe müprühta. Sücesü Jisue mi kürikünako zühyi die puko ce puyo dipüh thita to bata sheli küti pukoce po die.
english: They were on the way, going up to Jerusalem; and Jesus was going in front of them, and they were amazed; and those who followed were afraid. He again took the twelve, and began to tell them the things that were going to happen to him.

chokri: Yhie! Ukoe ta Jerusalem kho tozüe, sidie mi Thümanuo khapüh pethiküzhoko mu lüsiküseko belü süva to, mu mi Pu müsava kütola Pu müjoto mu Pu khapüh mülükümomi belü sü die;
english: "Behold, we are going up to Jerusalem. The Son of Man will be delivered to the chief priests and the scribes. They will condemn him to death, and will deliver him to the Gentiles.

chokri: Mipo Pu krühnyi mu Pu vü, mhütsa püh Pu yo sü, mu Pu dokhrito, mu Pu zho süh nha rhühla sü tato.
english: They will mock him, spit on him, scourge him, and kill him. On the third day he will rise again."

chokri: Sücesü Zebedee nu nyi James mu John unyi Puce khori die sü; kümüthao, hanyi nce mha kühopüh sho zori npo thi püh hanyi prü küto müri die tale, zisü.
english: James and John, the sons of Zebedee, came near to him, saying, "Teacher, we want you to do for us whatever we will ask."

chokri: Jisu punyi yisü, apo dipüh thi küsü nenyi müdo to la? Zisü.
english: He said to them, "What do you want me to do for you?"

chokri: Unyi Pu yisü, no zapozale thi küyice hanyi mipüh po nzaco mu mipüh po nveco bayi küve kha hanyi prüte; zita.
english: They said to him, "Grant to us that we may sit, one at your right hand, and one at your left hand, in your glory."

chokri: Süri Jisu unyi yisü, nenyie sho kübao se dien sho ba mo; nenyie I kra to küba heo krayi veme? Mu I pü to küba dzüluo nenyi püyi ve me? Zisü.
english: But Jesus said to them, "You don't know what you are asking. Are you able to drink the cup that I drink, and to be baptized with the baptism that I am baptized with?"

chokri: Mu punyi sü, hanyi thiyi ve; zita, Eli Jisu punyi yisü, nenyie I krato küba heo krayi ve mu I püto küba lü nenyio Baptis thiyi ve kütho mutse!
english: They said to him, "We are able." Jesus said to them, "You shall indeed drink the cup that I drink, and you shall be baptized with the baptism that I am baptized with;

chokri: Süri a zaco mu a veco ba kütoe I a müdolü khasü küyidi, süri somi la küsüh müta sheli süko la zo, zita.
english: but to sit at my right hand and at my left hand is not mine to give, but for whom it has been prepared."

chokri: Mu kürikoe sü sesü die James mu johan nyi yo uko nümo ta.
english: When the ten heard it, they began to be indignant towards James and John.

chokri: Süri Jisu puko ci püh Puce süyi die pukoce, mülükümomikoe u kükha yo küti kopo uko mhu nyepo thi tayo sidie puko küzhoko po puko kükha bayo küti neko ri se bato.
english: Jesus summoned them, and said to them, "You know that they who are recognized as rulers over the Gentiles lord it over them, and their great ones exercise authority over them.

chokri: Süri neko dolü zoe süziyi lho; eli neko dolü küzho thi künyio po neko cetao thito.
english: But it shall not be so among you, but whoever wants to become great among you shall be your servant.

chokri: Mu thüma kühopühri kürao thi künyimi po mütüko ceta thito.
english: Whoever of you wants to become first among you, shall be bondservant of all.

chokri: Kükrelamori, Thümanuo zori mipo pu shoyi thi mori pu mi shoyi thi mu mi kükro rhayi küto la pu pho khasü to vori küti la; zita.
english: For the Son of Man also came not to be served, but to serve, and to give his life as a ransom for many."

chokri: Süsa puko tasü Jericholü voriyi sidie Pu mu Pu sadziko mu thüma krode püh zi Jericholü ta prari, mu Timaeus nu püh Bartima mhüceoe shode shice ba die khrüti ba.
english: They came to Jericho. As he went out from Jericho, with his disciples and a great multitude, the son of Timaeus, Bartimaeus, a blind beggar, was sitting by the road.

chokri: Sülize pu süo Nazareth Jisu li küti se küsüce kra sezo die sü, David nu Jisu, no aphimüzhe süte zita.
english: When he heard that it was Jesus the Nazarene, he began to cry out, and say, "Jesus, you son of David, have mercy on me!"

chokri: Mu mi kükro pupo kümükre ba no die pu kükha ta; eli pu se müto ku die sü David nuo a phimüzhe zo süyi; zi bata.
english: Many rebuked him, that he should be quiet, but he cried out much more, "You son of David, have mercy on me!"

chokri: Süsa Jisu thayi die mi po pu cisü nosü, mu puko mhüceo ci sü die pu ce, thüpuyite, süte, pu nci yoho, zisü.
english: Jesus stood still, and said, "Call him." They called the blind man, saying to him, "Cheer up! Get up. He is calling you!"

chokri: Mu pu pu bola khava die, sü die ta Jisu ce vorita.
english: He, casting away his cloak, sprang up, and came to Jesus.

chokri: Süsa Jisu pu rüthi die; Apo n yo dipüh thi küsü n nü ta? Zisü. Mu mhüceoe Puce, kümüthao, apo a mhü mhangoyi zhe! Zita.
english: Jesus asked him, "What do you want me to do for you?" The blind man said to him, "Rhabboni, that I may see again."

chokri: Mu Jisu pu yisü, tavo tate, n kümülü tho n thi nve vaho; zisü, süzisü mütha pu mhü mha ngoyita, sidie pu Jisu samüdzi ta.
english: Jesus said to him, "Go your way. Your faith has made you well." Immediately he received his sight, and followed Jesus in the way.

chokri: Sülize uko tasü Jerusalem münoce Bethphage mu Bethani ce Olive cejütu co vori küyi Pu Pu sadzimi mi küna kütsüh die unyi yisü;
english: When they drew near to Jerusalem, to Bethsphage and Bethany, at the Mount of Olives, he sent two of his disciples,

chokri: Nenyi tasü nenyi rüpeco rünalü hi lüte, mu nenyi lü mütha hizü müpühri puyo ku she kümo Gadane püh ba die ngoyi toho; nenyi süo phrühyi die züh vorite, zisü.
english: and said to them, "Go your way into the village that is opposite you. Immediately as you enter into it, you will find a young donkey tied, on which no one has sat. Untie him, and bring him.

chokri: Mu humi nenyi kütso die, Dipüh la hizi zü la? Zita, Nyepooe hihi süh mosu züle; zivate. Süzi sü mütha pue süo kütsüh mha püh hice sü va toho; zita.
english: If anyone asks you, 'Why are you doing this?' say, 'The Lord needs him;' and immediately he will send him back here."

chokri: Süsa punyi ta vo tice shoprolüno mi cetazu Gadaneo khü püh ma die ngoyi, sidie unyi süo phrühyi.
english: They went away, and found a young donkey tied at the door outside in the open street, and they untied him.

chokri: Sücesü mi shice küthako humie sü unyi yisü, gadaneo phrüh püh dito zü la? Zita.
english: Some of those who stood there asked them, "What are you doing, untying the young donkey?"

chokri: Mu unyie Jisu punyi ce po küsu bi die posü. Süsa mi unyi khamo ta.
english: They said to them just as Jesus had said, and they let them go.

chokri: Sidie unyi gadaneo züh die Jisu ce vori, mu uko khü püh süo yo khrasu die pupo süyo bayi.
english: They brought the young donkey to Jesus, and threw their garments on it, and Jesus sat on it.

chokri: Sücesü thümako kükro u khüko püh sholü lha, kükrekoe sühko yono sühcoko rhopüh shode khra sü.
english: Many spread their garments on the way, and others were cutting down branches from the trees, and spreading them on the road.

chokri: Sidie usa uzü küta mütü se die sü, Hosana! Nyepoo za süh vori küzüo.
english: Those who went in front, and those who followed, cried out, "Hosanna! Blessed is he who comes in the name of the Lord!

chokri: Yhevethoo! Nyepoo za süh vori küzü Upoo David küdebao Yhevetho! Hosana kürükrüthoo.
english: Blessed is the kingdom of our father David that is coming in the name of the Lord! Hosanna in the highest!"

chokri: Sidie Pu Jerusalem lü kürüsucelü lü whi die mha mütü pe sü, mu putü münu küta la Pu mi kürikünako züh die Bethanilü prata.
english: Jesus entered into the temple in Jerusalem. When he had looked around at everything, it being now evening, he went out to Bethany with the twelve.

chokri: Sülize süsanha puko Bethani lüno ta pra küzüce Pu mürita.
english: The next day, when they had come out from Bethany, he was hungry.

chokri: Süce Pu rapeco no shedübo püh unyiko ba die ngosü, mu Pu süyo mhapühri ngoyi totho zi die tavo, mu Puta süce voritice unyi libi sa mhapühri ngoyi mota, kükrelamori sütüce sü shedüse küji tü kümo la.
english: Seeing a fig tree afar off having leaves, he came to see if perhaps he might find anything on it. When he came to it, he found nothing but leaves, for it was not the season for figs.

chokri: Mu Jisu süo yisü, Tsüsayogwino mipühri po n yono nse rati lho; ziva. Mu müthaküyiko zü süo se.
english: Jesus told it, "May no one ever eat fruit from you again!" and his disciples heard it.

chokri: Süze puko ta Jerusalem lü vori. Mu Jisu kürüsücelü lü die mi kürüsucelü mha zhüh mu mha khri kübako püh kügü süta. Süze Pue mi rüka küle küba miz mu thürüvünu zhüh kübako phula va.
english: They came to Jerusalem, and Jesus entered into the temple, and began to throw out those who sold and those who bought in the temple, and overthrew the tables of the money-changers, and the seats of those who sold the doves.

chokri: Mu Pue mipühri po mhabüh püh die kürüsuce püthü muta.
english: He would not allow anyone to carry a container through the temple.

chokri: Sülize Pu süko mütha die, “Ace püh thüma shuyie mütü la küshoce thito;” zidie thopüh müta mo me? Eli nekoe süo yisü kürügomi ce thive; zita.
english: He taught, saying to them, "Isn't it written, 'My house will be called a house of prayer for all the nations?' But you have made it a den of robbers!"

chokri: Mu lüsiküseko mu sekrükoe zü süo se, sidie pukoe Pu müka va küto do phuta. Kükrelamori, pukoe müprüh küti thümakoe Pu kümütha züko la u malhü thi ba küta la.
english: The chief priests and the scribes heard it, and sought how they might destroy him. For they feared him, because all the multitude was astonished at his teaching.

chokri: Süsa thüva vori mu Pu nade süolüno taprata.
english: When evening came, he went out of the city.

chokri: Sülize püko küshwüh-o ta vo küzüce shedüboo u melü kütso sosa zü küta ngoyi.
english: As they passed by in the morning, they saw the fig tree withered away from the roots.

chokri: Süla Peter pusalüyi die sü, kümüthao, liyhie, no rüshu küva shedübo tsü sosa züta; zisü.
english: Peter, remembering, said to him, "Rabbi, look! The fig tree which you cursed has withered away."

chokri: Süla Jisu puko yisü, Neko Poo celü kümülü neyite.
english: Jesus answered them, "Have faith in God.

chokri: Kükrelamori, I nekoce potü süto, Thüma kühopühri cejütu hio yisü, No ta rude sü dzükütülü lü tate, zisü, sülize pu lü külüdo mozo mu pu dipüh po sü sheli süo thiyi zo küto mülüzo lize, süo thiyi tato.
english: For most assuredly I tell you, whoever may tell this mountain, 'Be taken up and cast into the sea,' and doesn't doubt in his heart, but believes that what he says is happening; he shall have whatever he says.

chokri: Nekoe mhakühopühri shoti mütülü ngoyi zo küto mülütü zoyi lize süko thi püh neko prüto.
english: Therefore I tell you, all things whatever you pray and ask for, believe that you receive them, and you shall have them.

chokri: Sülize sho tozu die tha küyice, noe humi mütsu ba küti ba lize süo küjo vava to, sülize tüyo küba n Po ri n kükriko va püh n prüto.
english: Whenever you stand praying, forgive, if you have anything against anyone; so that your Father, who is in heaven, may also forgive you your transgressions.

chokri: Süri neko mi küjo va mi prü mu lize tüyo küba neko po ri ne kükriko va prüyi lho, zita.
english: But if you do not forgive, neither will your Father in heaven forgive your transgressions."

chokri: Süsa puko tala Jerusalem voriyi. Mu puta sü kürüsucelü lü küzüce, sekrüko mu lüsiküseko mu pethiko puce vori.
english: They came again to Jerusalem, and as he was walking in the temple, the chief priests, and the scribes, and the elders came to him,

chokri: Sidie pukoe sü Pu yisü, No dipüh küku süh die mha hikohi thiyo la? Mu sopüh-e mha hikohi thiyi küve küku kha n prü süa? zita.
english: and they began saying to him, "By what authority do you do these things? Or who gave you this authority to do these things?"

chokri: Jisu puko zü küla die, I ri mhapüh neko kütso süto; mu neko a khü küla süte, sülize I ri dipüh küku süh die mha hikohi thiyo sheli küti nekoce po süto;
english: Jesus said to them, "I will ask you one question. Answer me, and I will tell you by what authority I do these things.

chokri: John Dzüluo hi tüyon kiri me thüma celün vori la? Po süte; zisü.
english: The baptism of John -- was it from heaven, or from men? Answer me."

chokri: Süsa puko tho kümüli die sü, Ukoe pu tüyono kiri; zisü to mu Pu uko yisü sülize nekoe dipühla pu mülüyi muta? Zitato.
english: They reasoned with themselves, saying, "If we should say, 'From heaven;' he will say, 'Why then did you not believe him?'

chokri: Eli ukoe sü, Thüma celün vori zo, zisü to mu pukoe thüma kroo müprüh. Kükrelamori, mi mütü John yisü sadzühküsemi püh zo liyo tila.
english: If we should say, 'From men'"--they feared the people, for all held John to really be a prophet.

chokri: Ukoe Jisu fü küla de poti hako poyi sute Jisu pola küsü I ri dipü küku pü mha hiko thiyo sheri poho sita
english: They answered Jesus, "We don't know." Jesus said to them, "Neither do I tell you by what authority I do these things."

chokri: Süsa Pu züve lüno pukoce mhapo shüdü süta. Mipüh nee khubühyi püh teyi die cokha püh süo mütuyi mu khubühzü gü küto thi heyi mu rikhu bari thisü, sidie khapüh yitemiko mülü va die rüna kükrepüh lü vota.
english: He began to speak to them in parables. "A man planted a vineyard, put a hedge around it, dug a pit for the winepress, built a tower, rented it out to a farmer, and went into another country.

chokri: Süsa pu khubühse küra tüce yiteko celüno khubühseko ngoyi totho zidie pu cetami püh kütsüh püh süko cesü.
english: When it was time, he sent a servant to the farmer to get from the farmer his share of the fruit of the vineyard.

chokri: Eli sükoe pu tü püh vü va die bethu pu kütsüh süva.
english: They took him, beat him, and sent him away empty.

chokri: Süsari pu ceta kükre püh kütsüh la püh sükoce la sü, süri puko süo pi vüva die pü brü kümünga shüh sü die pu kütsüh va.
english: Again, he sent another servant to them; and they threw stones at him, wounded him in the head, and sent him away shamefully treated.
