chokri: Thipahemiko sheri sübi, hakopo dipü bita? Pü ukoce poti, mikehikemu thihite, mori mipüyo sheri kemeda pü mejohite; mu netho ne kaza (zhokhro) pü nenüyite.
english: Soldiers also asked him, saying, "What about us? What must we do?" He said to them, "Extort from no one by violence, neither accuse anyone wrongfully. Be content with your wages."

chokri: Thümako kemerice mu metüko kelülü John hi Khrista sü tazo motho, si bata.
english: As the people were in expectation, and all men reasoned in their hearts concerning John, whether perhaps he was the Christ,

chokri: John uko metüce posala kesü, “I jülo pü nekotsüsü, vele mipü aceri kupfü ketipü vorizü; Ipü pheku phari phrüyi kesümi mo; pü Kemesa Rüpülü mu melü neko pü jülo püsüto.
english: John answered them all, "I indeed baptize you with water, but he comes who is mightier than I, the latchet of whose sandals I am not worthy to loosen. He will baptize you in the Holy Spirit and fire,

chokri: Pübelü hahüri ba mu süpü cehükhro tehako hü she süto, side tehako püvo tehacelü kengu yito. Vele sibüko, kha mhütaküho melü süvato.”
english: whose fan is in his hand, and he will thoroughly cleanse his threshing floor, and will gather the wheat into his barn; but he will burn up the chaff with unquenchable fire."

chokri: Mhakekre kekrori pü mi metha mu thümako tarhosü.
english: Then with many other exhortations he preached good news to the people,

chokri: Vele Herod Tetrarch no pü saju-o Philip cemi Herodias yi ketala süsü pütü kemo John no pü lekhusü; mu Herod mhatho kesuthi kesüko sheri sade kerhesü.
english: but Herod the tetrarch, being reproved by him for Herodias, his brother's wife, and for all the evil things which Herod had done,

chokri: Hipi sheri pü thisakesü, John tüpü thejücelü khava.
english: added this also to them all, that he shut up John in prison.

chokri: Hitüce thüma metü jülopü bata, mu Jisu sheri jülopü yide kecho thiba, tükhri (he) süta.
english: Now it happened, when all the people were baptized, Jesus also had been baptized, and was praying. The sky was opened,

chokri: Kemesa Rüpü uthiupho kebabi taki püyo bayita, mu tüyo jüpü vori, süo poti, no anu akhrüo zo, I nngo anü shuzo.
english: and the Holy Spirit descended in a bodily form as a dove on him; and a voice came out of the sky, saying "You are my beloved Son. In you I am well pleased."

chokri: Jisu tütse sümeri ta (Joseph nule ziti) Joseph no Heli nu.
english: Jesus himself, when he began to teach, was about thirty years old, being the son (as was supposed) of Joseph, the son of Heli,

chokri: Süo Matthat nu, Levi nu, Melchi nu, Janna nu, Joseph nu.
english: the son of Matthat, the son of Levi, the son of Melchi, the son of Jannai, the son of Joseph,

chokri: Mattathias nu, Amos nu, Naum nu, Esli nu, Nagge nu.
english: the son of Mattathias, the son of Amos, the son of Nahum, the son of Esli, the son of Naggai,

chokri: Süo Maath nu, Mattathias nu, Semei nu, Joseph nu, Judah nu,
english: the son of Maath, the son of Mattathias, the son of Semein, the son of Joseph, the son of Judah,

chokri: Süo Joanna nu, Rhsa nu, Zorobabel nu, süo salathiel nu, Neri nu,
english: the son of Joanan, the son of Rhesa, the son of Zerubbabel, the son of Shealtiel, the son of Neri,

chokri: Süo Melchi nu, Addi nu, Cosam nu, Elmodam nu, Er nu,
english: the son of Melchi, the son of Addi, the son of Cosam, the son of Elmodam, the son of Er,

chokri: Süo Jose nu, Eliezer nu, Jorim nu, Matthat nu, Levi nu,
english: the son of Josa, the son of Eliezer, the son of Jorim, the son of Matthat, the son of Levi,

chokri: Simon nu, Judah, Joseph nu, Jonan nu, Eliakim nu,
english: the son of Simeon, the son of Judah, the son of Joseph, the son of Jonan, the son of Eliakim,

chokri: Melea nu, Menan nu, Mattatha nu, Nathan nu, David nu.
english: the son of Melea, the son of Menan, the son of Mattatha, the son of Nathan, the son of David,

chokri: Jesse nu, Obed nu, Booz nu, Salmon nu, Noasson nu.
english: the son of Jesse, the son of Obed, the son of Boaz, the son of Salmon, the son of Nahshon,

chokri: Aminadab nu, Aram nu, Esrom nu, Phares nu, Judah nu,
english: the son of Amminadab, the son of Aram, the son of Joram, the son of Hezron, the son of Perez, the son of Judah,

chokri: Jacob nu, Isaac nu, Abraham nu, Thara nu, Nachor nu,
english: the son of Jacob, the son of Isaac, the son of Abraham, the son of Terah, the son of Nahor,

chokri: Saruch nu, Ragau nu, Phalec nu, Heber nu, Sala nu,
english: the son of Serug, the son of Reu, the son of Peleg, the son of Eber, the son of Shelah

chokri: Caiman nu, Arphazad nu, sem nu, Noe nu, Lamech nu,
english: the son of Cainan, the son of Arphaxad, the son of Shem, the son of Noah, the son of Lamech,

chokri: Methuselah nu, Enoch nu Jared nu, Malelul nu, Cain nu,
english: the son of Methuselah, the son of Enoch, the son of Jared, the son of Mahalaleel, the son of Cainan,

chokri: Enos nu, Seth nu, Adam nu, Jihova nu,
english: the son of Enos, the son of Seth, the son of Adam, the son of God.

chokri: Jisu Kemesa Rüpü po püchu pü Jordan lü tala vorita, Kemesa Rüpü lüta rayholü vota.
english: Jesus, full of the Holy Spirit, returned from the Jordan, and was led by the Spirit into the wilderness

chokri: Zhoheda therükesu po pü kedo, tüsozho hidolü pü mhapüri timo; tüsozho süko tsü ketasa püvameri vorita.
english: for forty days, being tempted by the devil. He ate nothing in those days. Afterward, when they were completed, he was hungry.

chokri: Therükesuo pücepoti, no mhace Jihova nu lize ketsü hiopü tsükhri thiyi no posüte.
english: The devil said to him, "If you are the Son of God, command this stone to become bread."

chokri: Jisu püfü kelakesü, thopü kemeta, thüma tsükhri lebi la hüyi yomo; vele Jihova jükolazo.
english: Jesus answered him, saying, "It is written, 'Man shall not live by bread alone, but by every word of God.'"

chokri: Therükesuo püzü cejütu kerekrü püyo vode vapülü cejülü kedeko metü thapü pü mekasü.
english: The devil, leading him up on a high mountain, showed him all the kingdoms of the world in a moment of time.

chokri: Side therükesu-o puce poti, keku metü I khapü ntsü süto, mu sülü zapozale metü sheri; kekrelamori süo pü atsü kevala, süo khapü a medomi tsüsü ketola.
english: The devil said to him, "I will give you all this authority, and their glory, for it has been delivered to me; and I give it to whomever I want.

chokri: Mhace no süla a reshü va ketosa süko metü khapü nzhü thisü tato.
english: If you therefore will worship before me, it will all be yours."

chokri: Jisu püfü kelade po kesü, no Satan ta asaco votate, thopü kemeta, no Niepo N Rüo lebi reshüto, mu pü lebi choyi thito sizipü mezü.
english: Jesus answered him, "Get behind me Satan! For it is written, 'You shall worship the Lord your God, and him only shall you serve.'"

chokri: Püzüpü Jerusalem lü vori de kereshüce pilülü bayi, de pücepoti, no mhace Jihova nu lize retu süte tsüce;
english: He led him to Jerusalem, and set him on the pinnacle of the temple, and said to him, "If you are the Son of God, cast yourself down from here,

chokri: Kekrelamori thopü kemeta, pü tüyo choyimi ketsüpü nbuyi tato de npo krimo ketola.
english: for it is written, 'He will give his angels charge concerning you, to guard you;'

chokri: Nko belü nkekriyi tade npo ketsüyo nphe meshü mo ketola.
english: and, 'On their hands they will bear you up, Lest perhaps you dash your foot against a stone.'"

chokri: Jisu püfü kelakesü, sülü thopü kemeta, “no n Niepo Rüo kedosu.”
english: Jesus answering, said to him, "It has been said, 'You shall not tempt the Lord your God.'"

chokri: Therükesuo medokephi metü thitsü kevasa pücelü dopüla tata.
english: When the devil had completed every temptation, he departed from him until another time.

chokri: Jisu Kemesa Rüpü kekulü ta Galilee lü vori; pü kemele ukhü metülü vota,
english: Jesus returned in the power of the Spirit into Galilee, and news about him spread through all the surrounding area.

chokri: Pü kereshüce kolü mi metha mele ketabi.
english: He taught in their synagogues, being glorified by all.

chokri: Pü Nazareth lüvo, sülü pü thekü vori tila, püzho cebi ta kereshücelü Sabbath nhalü, pü tosü thayi de lesiphri sü.
english: He came to Nazareth, where he had been brought up. He entered, as was his custom, into the synagogue on the Sabbath day, and stood up to read.

chokri: Isaiah lesi khipü tsüsü, pü lesio hasü, hizi thopü metati ngoyi
english: The book of the prophet Isaiah was handed to him. He opened the book, and found the place where it was written,

chokri: Niepoo Rüpü ayota, kekrelamori apo jüyikeve popü kedimikoce süno akenhüyi; pü a ketsüpü umelüpro ketako lü thomelu süno, mijükethiko phrüsüno, umhü keceko mhükhri sü, mipo uvü ketako hesü.
english: "The Spirit of the Lord is on me, Because he has anointed me to preach good news to the poor. He has sent me to heal the brokenhearted, To proclaim release to the captives, Recovering of sight to the blind, To deliver those who are crushed,

chokri: Niepoo kenü tütse po mese kesü.
english: And to proclaim the acceptable year of the Lord."

chokri: Süsa pü lesio khava, khapü kemethao tsü sülava, de bayi kereshücelü kebako metü umhükhrüpü püpe bata, pe türüba.
english: He closed the book, gave it back to the attendant, and sat down. The eyes of all in the synagogue were fastened on him.

chokri: Pü uko shudü jüpo, thahi neko nilü jüthopü kemeta thitso süte.
english: He began to tell them, "Today, this Scripture has been fulfilled in your hearing."

chokri: Uko metü süo zhopo, pütalü jü keve poprari tikola ungo bata, uko kepode, nu hio Joseph nuzo mome?
english: All testified about him, and wondered at the gracious words which proceeded out of his mouth, and they said, "Isn't this Joseph's son?"

chokri: Pü ukoce poti, neko ace hibiti potüzoto, “Darukeseo ntho nthi kesürhoyite, hako kesebi Capernaum lüri thisüte, n rüna lüri thisüte, sizito.”
english: He said to them, "Doubtless you will tell me this parable, 'Physician, heal yourself! Whatever we have heard done at Capernaum, do also here in your hometown.'"

chokri: Püpo kesü, kethozo, I nekoce po süto, saju kepo mipüri utho ura yinyü yomo.
english: He said, "Most assuredly I tell you, no prophet is acceptable in his hometown.

chokri: I nekoce kethopo süto Elijah tüce thenosamepü kekro ba Isreal tütse sü mu tekhri shurü türü mota, kemekre cejü metülü bata.
english: But truly I tell you, there were many widows in Israel in the days of Elijah, when the sky was shut up three years and six months, when a great famine came over all the land.

chokri: Elijah ketsüpü Zarephat Sidon nadelü leti kesüzo, theno samepü celü.
english: Elijah was sent to none of them, except to Zarephath, in the land of Sidon, to a woman who was a widow.

chokri: Isreal lü lepra kethimi kekroba Elisha saju kepoo tüce, Naaman Syria mipü lebi thi kemenü kesüzo.
english: There were many lepers in Israel in the time of Elisha the prophet, yet not one of them was cleansed, except Naaman, the Syrian."

chokri: Kereshücelü kebako mhasüko sekesü ce uko lü redata.
english: They were all filled with wrath in the synagogue, as they heard these things.

chokri: Uko tosü pü hupü nade sa (cosa) süva, uko püpi mesode kelhupü thikha lüsü va keto rhüta (cosalü thikha kebala).
english: They rose up, threw him out of the city, and led him to the brow of the hill that their city was built on, that they might throw him off the cliff.

chokri: Vele pü uko dolü ta pücholü vota.
english: But he, passing through the midst of them, went his way.

chokri: Püta Capernaum lü ki, Galilee lü nadeo Sabbath nha pü kereshücelü mi metha.
english: He came down to Capernaum, a city of Galilee. He was teaching them on the Sabbath day,

chokri: Uko pükepo zhokola ungophita, püjü keku kenela.
english: and they were astonished at his teaching, for his word was with authority.

chokri: Kereshücelü thümapü rüpü mesakemo füphra se meto bayo.
english: In the synagogue there was a man who had a spirit of an unclean demon, and he cried out with a loud voice,

chokri: Poti, hako kebü hite, no hakozü kedipü thita, no Nazareth mi Jisu? No hako meka ketola vorime? I nseba no sopüsheri, Therü kemesao.
english: saying, "Ah! what have we to do with you, Jesus of Nazareth? Have you come to destroy us? I know you who you are: the Holy One of God!"

chokri: Jisu pü lekhusü, de poti, no kemekre tate de püyo tapra tate. Kesu rüpü pü kegüdü pü ukodolü süva; püyo taprata, pü methi mota.
english: Jesus rebuked him, saying, "Be silent, and come out of him!" When the demon had thrown him down in their midst, he came out of him, having done him no harm.

chokri: Uko metü ungothita, de uko kerethi de poti, jühio dipü jüta! Pü keku kene ukopo tatano kesüce kesurüpüko ta pra ketahi.
english: Amazement came on all, and they spoke together, one with another, saying, "What is this word? For with authority and power he commands the unclean spirits, and they come out!"

chokri: Pü kemelü chozucori sevota, pükhü kewhiko rüna metülü.
english: News about him went out into every place of the surrounding region.

chokri: Pü kereshücelü tasüde ta Simon celü vota. Simon cemizu rüce shüshu ba. Uko püpo thi kesürho süno chota.
english: He rose up from the synagogue, and entered into Simon's house. Simon's mother-in-law was afflicted with a great fever, and they begged him for her.

chokri: Pü thenopü pice thayi, de kethio kethisü; süo pükhava tata; rekri thenopü tasü de uko reyi bata.
english: He stood over her, and rebuked the fever; and it left her. Immediately she rose up and served them.

chokri: Hitüce nace lütazü, kethi kekre kekrori zü puce vori; pübe chopü sükoyo süde thikesürho sü.
english: When the sun was setting, all those who had any sick with various diseases brought them to him; and he laid his hands on every one of them, and healed them.

chokri: Kesurüpüko sede taprata, poti “no Khrista zoti seba ketala.”
english: Demons also came out from many, crying out, and saying, "You are the Christ, the Son of God!" Rebuking them, he didn't allow them to speak, because they knew that he was the Christ.

chokri: Tüso vori ketace, püta cejü kesalü vota, thümako püphu, ukota puce vori, ta puce voride püzü bata, püpo tata hino pota.
english: When it was day, he departed and went into an uninhabited place, and the multitudes looked for him, and came to him, and held on to him, so that he wouldn't go away from them.

chokri: Pü ukoce poti, I thethi kekrerari vode Jihova kede popü mice sü mozesu; kekrelamori a ketsü kesü süo tila.
english: But he said to them, "I must preach the good news of the Kingdom of God to the other cities also. For this reason I have been sent."

chokri: Pü Galilee kereshüce kolü vo mi metha.
english: He was preaching in the synagogues of Galilee.

chokri: Thümako pügü tutu tata, Jihova jü kerenila, pü Gennesaret jükezho poyo thayi.
english: Now it happened, while the multitude pressed on him and heard the word of God, that he was standing by the lake of Gennesaret.

chokri: Ru kena jükezho khüce ba, vele fü ketüko bamota, uko füketü jal (fü) ko meni bata.
english: He saw two boats standing by the lake, but the fishermen had gone out of them, and were washing their nets.

chokri: Pü ru pülülü, süo Simon ru, khupü jülü sünode posü, cejü kesozü kemecu kemoce; Pü bayi de thümako metha.
english: He entered into one of the boats, which was Simon's, and asked him to put out a little from the land. He sat down and taught the multitudes from the boat.

chokri: Hice püjüpo mota, mu Simon po püru khupü jü kerelü lüphe no posü, de pü jal (ketüfü) khasüte khutüyi ketola.
english: When he had finished speaking, he said to Simon, "Put out into the deep, and let down your nets for a catch."

chokri: Simon püfü kelade pokesü, kemethao hako tüzüzho metü thehe thizole mhapüri tüyimole; sizosheri I n kepo jüla ajal kelhu süto.
english: Simon answered him, "Master, we worked all night, and took nothing; but at your word I will let down the net."

chokri: Ukosübide thisü, fü kekro boyita, mu uko jal pretazü.
english: When they had done this, they caught a great multitude of fish, and their net was breaking.

chokri: Ukokromi ru kekrelü ketako cüsü de ukopo kekhrühi sünosü. Uko tavori mu füko pü rukena nilü chuyi, süla runi relütanu.
english: They beckoned to their partners in the other boat, that they should come and help them. They came, and filled both boats, so that they began to sink.

chokri: Simon Peter süo ngosüde, Jisu phelü neküki pü reshüta de poti, Acelü tatate, kekrelamori I kephomamipü tila, Oh, Niepoo.
english: But Simon Peter, when he saw it, fell down at Jesus' knees, saying, "Depart from me, for I am a sinful man, Lord."

chokri: Kekrelamori püngothi ketala, mu püzü keta metüko, khu kekro tüyi ketala.
english: For he was amazed, and all who were with him, at the catch of fish which they had caught;

chokri: Zebedee nuni James mu John niri sülüta Simon züta; Jisu Simon cepoti, mpepahite, hisaco nothüma ketümi thitato.
english: and so also were James and John, sons of Zebedee, who were partners with Simon. Jesus said to Simon, "Don't be afraid. From now on you will be catching people alive."

chokri: Uko runi cejükesolü vorita, uko mhametü khavade püsa mejita.
english: When they had brought their boats to land, they left everything, and followed him.

chokri: Pü nadepülü kebace thümapü mpa kethipo püchu pü ba Jisu ngosü pü nekütsü puce umezhemelota, mhace no nmedo lize, no athimeve süyive.
english: It happened, while he was in one of the cities, behold, there was a man full of leprosy. When he saw Jesus, he fell on his face, and begged him, saying, "Lord, if you want to, you can make me clean."

chokri: Pübechopü pü nisü, de poti, I thisüto, no mesa tate, sizisü metha mpa (lepra) kethi dita püyo.
english: He stretched out his hand, and touched him, saying, "I want to. Be made clean." Immediately the leprosy left him.

chokri: Püpo süopü mipüceri pohino sisü; vele no tavo thevoo mhüngo thisüte, mu mesaketa Moses zholü popü kemetako thivate, uko mhüjülü zhopo khakesü.
english: He charged him to tell no one, "But go your way, and show yourself to the priest, and offer for your cleansing according to what Moses commanded, for a testimony to them."

chokri: Vele pü kemele ukhü metülü vo keye süta; makro tavo püfürenita, uko kethathiko thi kesürho sü ketola.
english: But the report concerning him spread much more, and great multitudes came together to hear, and to be healed by him of their infirmities.

chokri: Pü thüma therüta rayhokolü vode kechothi ta.
english: But he withdrew himself into the desert, and prayed.

chokri: Pünhapü pü mimetha bari, Phariseemiko, mu lesi kesethoko süce ba, Galilee rünakolü vori ba, Judea lüri, mu Jerusalem lüri, Niepoo keku ukothikesürho sü ketola ba.
english: It happened on one of those days, that he was teaching; and there were Pharisees and teachers of the law sitting by, who had come out of every village of Galilee, Judea, and Jerusalem. The power of the Lord was with him to heal them.

chokri: Thüma lasaketapü püziyo püpü vori, pü züvo puce süketo chophu, pükhapü pü mhüjülüsü ketola.
english: Behold, men brought a paralyzed man on a cot, and they sought to bring him in to lay before Jesus.

chokri: Uko pü zülüsü keto chophu medita, kekrelamori thümakrori ketala, uko cetuyo kuta, cetu cepha sü püzilü pü püki Jisu mhüjülü süsü.
english: Not finding a way to bring him in because of the multitude, they went up to the housetop, and let him down through the tiles with his cot into the midst before Jesus.

chokri: Pü uko kemelü ngo kesüce “Thümao, n kephomako vasüteho,” ze posü.
english: Seeing their faith, he said to him, "Man, your sins are forgiven you."

chokri: Lesikeseko mu Phariseemiko kepode thüma hio sopüle zi-eno Niepoo kemeshu po bata? Sopüno kephoma vayi veta, Jihova lebisa?
english: The scribes and the Pharisees began to reason, saying, "Who is this that speaks blasphemies? Who can forgive sins, but God alone?"

chokri: Jisu uko lüdo sesü de kelakesü, neko melülü dipü lüba?
english: But Jesus, perceiving their thoughts, answered them, "Why are you reasoning so in your hearts?

chokri: Dipüo poreyi kuta? Nkephomava ntsüvaho siti me? Mori tasüte de chotayi tate, süni dipüo poreyi ku?
english: Which is easier to say, 'Your sins are forgiven you;' or to say, 'Arise and walk?'

chokri: Vele nekopo seketola Thümanuo cejülü keku kephoma va keyi ba (side pü lasao ce poti) I nce posüho, tasüte, nzi püyi de ta ncelü votate.
english: But that you may know that the Son of Man has authority on earth to forgive sins" (he said to the paralyzed man), "I tell you, arise, and take up your cot, and go to your house."

chokri: Rekri thümako mhüjülü pü tasüta, püzü kezüthilü, ta pücelü vota; zapozale Jihova thide.
english: Immediately he rose up before them, and took up that which he was laying on, and departed to his house, glorifying God.

chokri: Thümametü ungo thita, Jihova zapozale pota, meprüti thümako yo vota, tha uko mha ketekhri tipü ngosüte.
english: Amazement took hold on all, and they glorified God. They were filled with fear, saying, "We have seen strange things today."

chokri: Mha hiko thi kevasa pü tavozü ri thümapü Levi letipü khazhonu khrü bade ngoyi, püce poti, “no asameji yite”
english: After these things he went out, and saw a tax collector named Levi sitting at the tax office, and said to him, "Follow me!"

chokri: Pü mhametü khavade püsameji ta.
english: He left everything, and rose up and followed him.

chokri: Levi pücelü thehüpü kezhopü kesüsü, kezhomi kekro mu thüma kekrekori vori uko kezü ba,
english: Levi made a great feast for him in his house. There was a great crowd of tax collectors and others who were reclining with them.

chokri: Vele Phariseemiko mu lesikeseko panyode pü sajiko poti, neko dipüla malimiko mu kephomamiko zü mhati mhakra baya?
english: Their scribes and the Pharisees murmured against his disciples, saying, "Why do you eat and drink with the tax collectors and sinners?"

chokri: Jisu uko fü kelakesü, “kesürhomiko daru kesemiko zü mhapüri kethidi, vele süko kethimiko lazo.
english: Jesus answered them, "Those who are healthy have no need for a physician, but those who are sick do.

chokri: I kemeziko cü ketola vorimo, vele kephomamiko po thelü redeyi ketola zo.”
english: I have not come to call the righteous, but sinners to repentance."

chokri: Uko puce poti, dipla John sajiko tsükenu mevoya, mu kechothiyo; sübi Phariseemiko sheri siziyo; vele neko ti mu kra bazo?
english: They said to him, "Why do John's disciples often fast and pray, likewise also the disciples of the Pharisees, but yours eat and drink?"

chokri: Pü ukoce poti, neko zhosao celü mevo ba tome, zhosao ukozü kebace?
english: He said to them, "Can you make the friends of the bridegroom fast, while the bridegroom is with them?

chokri: Vele tüsozhoko vorito, zhosao züta ketasa, (uko celü bamo ketasa) süsa tüso sünhako uko mevo tato.
english: But the days will come when the bridegroom will be taken away from them. Then they will fast in those days."

chokri: Pü uko ce kejüvede poti, mipüri fükra pü fü kesa mefü yomo, sizilize fü kesaori kroku tato, fü kesao fü kevüo zü kekre mu kemhakebi motayo tila.
english: He also told a parable to them. "No one puts a piece from a new garment on an old garment, or else he will tear the new, and also the piece from the new will not match the old.

chokri: Mipüri hezo kesa khapü vaheri (botol) kevülü süyomo, sizilize hezo kesao vahe kevüo püphro vayo, hezo kelata mu vahe (botol) meka vato.
english: No one puts new wine into old wineskins, or else the new wine will burst the skins, and it will be spilled, and the skins will be destroyed.

chokri: Hezo kesa khapü vahe kesalü süyo, sizitisa kenani mekrü yito.
english: But new wine must be put into fresh wineskins, and both are preserved.

chokri: Hezo kevükramezüyi ketasa mipüri hezo kesa menyü yomo, pü kelüce hezo süketa (kevüo) kevetho (veku) le zi bayo.
english: No man having drunk old wine immediately desires new, for he says, 'The old is better.'"

chokri: Sabbath vakena-o nha püsajiko zü sako yilü vo, püsajiko sako rütita uko belü süyide.
english: Now it happened on the second Sabbath after the first, that he was going through the grain fields. His disciples plucked the heads of grain, and ate, rubbing them in their hands.

chokri: Phariseemipü ukoce poti, neko dipü thide Sabbath zhonha thezhokemo thi ya?
english: But some of the Pharisees said to them, "Why do you do that which is not lawful to do on the Sabbath day?"

chokri: Jisu ukofü kelekesü, neko mha hibiti phrimo zome, David meri ketace, püzü ketako zü dithi.
english: Jesus, answering them, said, "Haven't you read what David did when he was hungry, he, and those who were with him;

chokri: Pü kerüshücelü kelüce: mengo keti tsükrida khipüti mu pü püzü ketako pori ti;
english: how he entered into the house of God, and took and ate the show bread, and gave also to those who were with him, which is not lawful to eat except for the priests alone?"

chokri: Pü ukoce posa kesü, Themanu-o Sabbath pi sheri Niepo zo.
english: He said to them, "The Son of Man is lord of the Sabbath."

chokri: Sabbath kekrepünha pü kereshücelü lü mi methaba, sülü mipü pübo uzaco lasa ketapü ba.
english: It also happened on another Sabbath that he entered into the synagogue and taught. There was a man there, and his right hand was withered.

chokri: Lesikeseko mu Phariseeko pü peba, Sabbath nha pü mithi kesürho sü tome mo sheri; sülü uko pü mejo keto ngoyi ketola.
english: The scribes and the Pharisees watched him, to see whether he would heal on the Sabbath, that they might find an accusation against him.

chokri: Vele pü uko lüdoko seba, süla pübo lasa ketao ce poti, tasü midolü thasüte, siri uko dolü tasü thayi.
english: But he knew their thoughts; and he said to the man who had the withered hand, "Rise up, and stand in the middle." He arose and stood.

chokri: Süsa Jisu ukoce poti, I nekoce mhapü ketsoto, Sabbath nha mhakeve kethi thezho me, mhakesu kethi thezho? Thüma kelasü tome, mi dokhri ti?
english: Then Jesus said to them, "I will ask you something: Is it lawful on the Sabbath to do good, or to do harm? To save a life, or to kill?"

chokri: Pü uko pe metu vosü, side thümao ce poti, nbo meto süte, pübo metosü ri pübo keveco bita.
english: He looked around at them all, and said to the man, "Stretch out your hand." He did, and his hand was restored as sound as the other.

chokri: Uko lükekripü ukotho kerethide Jisu yo kedipü thisüto sheri mebu bata.
english: But they were filled with rage, and talked with one another about what they might do to Jesus.

chokri: Pü ta cejütuyo vode kechothi bata, tüzüzho metü sülü bata.
english: It happened in these days, that he went out to the mountain to pray, and he continued all night in prayer to God.

chokri: Tüso vori cu pü sajiko cüsü, mikerikena kedapü püsaji thiyi, mu süko thopü apostels zisü.
english: When it was day, he called his disciples, and from them he chose twelve, whom he also named apostles:

chokri: Simon, püzathopü Peter thi kesüo, Andrew pü sazüo, James mu John ni, Philip mu Bartholomew.
english: Simon, whom he also named Peter; Andrew, his brother; James; John; Philip; Bartholomew;

chokri: Mathew mu Thomas, James Alphaeus nu, Simon cüpü Zealots siyo.
english: Matthew; Thomas; James, the son of Alphaeus; Simon, who was called the Zealot;

chokri: Judas, James sazüo, Judas Iscariot pütüpü zhu kevao.
english: Judas the son of James; and Judas Iscariot, who also became a traitor.

chokri: Pü cejü kethezholü kiri mu püsajiko risa, thüma kekro Judea metülü mu Jerusalem lü sheri, jükezhokhükolü Tyre, Sidon lüko voride püfü reni mu ukethiko thikesürho ketola vori.
english: He came down with them, and stood on a level place, with a crowd of his disciples, and a great number of the people from all Judea and Jerusalem, and the sea coast of Tyre and Sidon, who came to hear him and to be healed of their diseases;

chokri: Therükesu rüpüko po ukebü ketako, süko thikesürho sü.
english: as well as those who were troubled by unclean spirits, and they were being healed.

chokri: Makro tavo pü jüsünyüta, kekrelamori mhakeve püyo prade süko metü thi kesürho sü ketola.
english: All the multitude sought to touch him, for power came out from him and healed them all.

chokri: Pümhü khrüpü methakeyiko pede poti, “neko mhakediko thejo kepütho, kekrelamori Jihova (tüyo) kede neko zhüzo tila.
english: He lifted up his eyes to his disciples, and said, "Blessed are you who are poor, For yours is the Kingdom of God.

chokri: Neko tsühi merikebako tsühi krakebako thejo kepütho, kekrelamori neko nüyi (nyüta) ketola.
english: Blessed are you who hunger now, For you will be filled. Blessed are you who weep now, For you will laugh.

chokri: Mi neko ngometsüyo tiko thejo kepütho, side mi krolü neko hu kevako, neko meshü, nza cüpü thüma kesuthomithi, kekrelamori thümanuo la.
english: Blessed are you when men shall hate you, and when they shall exclude and mock you, and throw out your name as evil, for the Son of Man's sake.

chokri: Neko sünha neko rechuyite, de prürehü süte, hihe, nekozhokhro tüyo vephizo ketola; uko potsaneko sheri sübiti thipü saju kepokoyo süzotila.
english: Rejoice in that day, and leap for joy, for behold, your reward is great in heaven, for their fathers did the same thing to the prophets.

chokri: Vele neko mhakeneko kesukerho, neko lü kelu ngoyi ketala
english: "But woe to you who are rich! For you have received your consolation.

chokri: Neko chu kebako kesukerho, neko merita ketola, neko tsühi nyü kebako kesu kerho, kekrelamori nekokra mu thekrithita ketola.
english: Woe to you, you who are full now! For you will be hungry. Woe to you who laugh now! For you will mourn and weep.

chokri: Neko mi metü nechu (pomeve) kebako kesukerho, kekrelamori upotsaneko sheri kecejü saju kepoko yo sübiti thizo tila.
english: Woe, when men speak well of you! For their fathers did the same thing to the false prophets.

chokri: Vele I neko reni kebakoce kepo, nengomumiko khrüyite, neko metsü ketakoyo mhakeve thisüte.
english: "But I tell you who hear: love your enemies, do good to those who hate you,

chokri: Neko reshu ketimiko yo jo (reliphrü) pü tsüte; neko krünyü (ne meshu) ketimila kechothite.
english: bless those who curse you, and pray for those who mistreat you.

chokri: Nejo püco vü (kekhu) sülize, kekreco kerüpü tsüsa süte; n bola pükhiyi talize nbola (meso-o) ri pü putsü sa süte.
english: To him who strikes you on the cheek, offer also the other; and from him who takes away your cloak, don't withhold your coat also.

chokri: N ce mhacho keba metü khapü tsü süte, mu nzhü püyi ketakoce cho sala hite.
english: Give to everyone who asks you, and don't ask him who takes away your goods to give them back again.

chokri: Miponekoyo dipü thisüze nenüyo sheri neri miyo sübi thisüte.
english: "As you would like people to do to you, do exactly so to them.

chokri: Mi nekekhrüko lebi khrüyo lize, dipü kemeza ngoyita? Kephomako sheri siziti thiyo tila.
english: If you love those who love you, what credit is that to you? For even sinners love those who love them.

chokri: No mhakeve thipü mi mhakeve thipü n ketsüko lebi tsüyo lize, dipü kemezangoyi ta? Kephomamiko sheri sübi keti thiyo tila.
english: If you do good to those who do good to you, what credit is that to you? For even sinners do the same.

chokri: No mhangoyi ketokoce mhangoyi keto kemerila mhapü mi thepo ti lebi lize, dipü kemeza ngoyita, kekrelamori kephomiko sheri mhapü mithepo süde mhangoyi keto meri yozo.
english: If you lend to those from whom you hope to receive, what credit is that to you? Even sinners lend to sinners, to receive back as much.

chokri: Vele n ngomumiko khrüyite, mhakeve thi pü sükoyo süte, mhapü süko tsüte, mhapüri sükoyo meri hite; n zhokhro zhoshuzoto, mu neko kerekrüthoo nune thiyito, kekrelamori pü thüma kesuko yo sheri sizi yo.
english: But love your enemies, and do good, and lend, expecting nothing back; and your reward will be great, and you will be children of the Most High; for he is kind toward the unthankful and evil.

chokri: Neko süla mingomezhe zote, neko pori mingo mezheyo tila.
english: Therefore be merciful, Even as your Father is also merciful.

chokri: Mi mejo hite, size mi neko mejo taho; milümesu hite silize mi nelü mesuho; mikejo va mitsüte, silize nekejo va süto.
english: Don't judge, And you won't be judged. Don't condemn, And you won't be condemned. Set free, And you will be set free.

chokri: Mhakha mi tsüte, size mhakha netsüto; medetsüsüto, nü ketü süto, kena (kenü) süto, tsole (chule) ketakori lapü n khoroyo süpü ntsüto. Kekrelamori no dipü bide thisü sheri sübi tüti thipü ntsü süto.”
english: "Give, and it will be given to you: good measure, pressed down, shaken together, and running over, will be given to you. For with the same measure you measure it will be measured back to you."

chokri: Pü jüve pü ukoce poti, mhücemipü mhücemi chothayi veme? Uni kenani retu tekhülü lüta motome?
english: He spoke a parable to them. "Can the blind guide the blind? Won't they both fall into a pit?

chokri: Misajimi pü niepoo ce zhokumo, vele vetsütso ketimi pü kemethami bizo.
english: A disciple is not above his teacher, but everyone when he is fully trained will be like his teacher.

chokri: No dipüla kerho nsazüo mhülü kebao peba, n mhülü kerho kebao pe mera mo deno,
english: Why do you see the speck of chaff that is in your brother's eye, but don't consider the beam that is in your own eye?

chokri: Mori dipübide no n sazüo ce poyiveta, a sazüo apo kerho n mhülü kebao pü vate siziyi veta, nmhülü kerho kebao khiva meramono? No kethera ketao, kerho n mhülü khiva merate, size no kerho ngometseyito mu nsazüo mhülü kerho kebao khivato.
english: Or how can you tell your brother, 'Brother, let me remove the speck of chaff that is in your eye,' when you yourself don't see the beam that is in your own eye? You hypocrite! First remove the beam from your own eye, and then you can see clearly to remove the speck of chaff that is in your brother's eye.

chokri: Kekrelamori sükeve püse kesuzhüta yomo; mori sükesu püse kevezhü yomo.
english: For there is no good tree that brings forth rotten fruit; nor again a rotten tree that brings forth good fruit.

chokri: Kekrelamori husho süyo khubü sezhü yomo.
english: For each tree is known by its own fruit. For people don't gather figs from thorns, nor do they gather grapes from a bramble bush.

chokri: Thümakevemi pü melü lü mhakeve kengu pü metatila mhakeve khiprari yo; mu thüma kesumi mhakesu pumelülü kebala mhakesu khiprariyo. Pü melülü mhakekro kengupü metatila süko utalü püprari yo.
english: The good man out of the good treasure of his heart brings out that which is good, and the evil man out of the evil treasure of his heart brings out that which is evil, for out of the abundance of the heart, his mouth speaks.

chokri: Neko dipüla akhipü, “Niepoo, Niepoo” siziba; akepoko thisü mode?
english: "Why do you call me, 'Lord, Lord,' and don't do the things which I say?

chokri: Kehopü sheri ace kevorimi, akepoko reniyi mu süko thimu züyi lize, I thapü neko meka süto pü kedipü bitosheri.
english: Everyone who comes to me, and hears my words, and does them, I will show you who he is like.

chokri: Thüma süo pücekhü chürelütsü tsüroyo umeshu yide pücethiyi, jüde süri süova, siri ce süo renamota, süo tsüroyo düyi ketala.
english: He is like a man building a house, who dug and went deep, and laid a foundation on the rock. When a flood arose, the stream broke against that house, and could not shake it, because it was founded on the rock.

chokri: Vele reniyo, thiyomo lize thümasüo puce thipü nyolulü shukesü cepübizo, jüde vori ce süo va, metha ce süo retuta, ce süo kashuta.
english: But he who hears, and doesn't do, is like a man who built a house on the earth without a foundation, against which the stream broke, and immediately it fell, and the ruin of that house was great."

chokri: Pü mhahiko potso kevasa, thüma krode koce, püta Capernaun lülüta.
english: After he had finished speaking in the hearing of the people, he entered into Capernaum.

chokri: Krapikezho thekümipü, pü khrüphi yori mhathi tade satanu züta.
english: A certain centurion's servant, who was dear to him, was sick and at the point of death.

chokri: Pü Jisu vori kezü sesüde Jews mehimi ketsüpü Jisu cesü, püpo tavo pü theküo thikesürho süno chota.
english: When he heard about Jesus, he sent to him elders of the Jews, asking him to come and save his servant.

chokri: Uko ta Jisu ce voride püpo tavo büno rüdata, poti pü vosheri kezüzo ketithüma püle sizita.
english: When they came to Jesus, they begged him earnestly, saying, "He is worthy for you to do this for him,

chokri: Pü hashuye kekhrümi mu pü synagogue sheri thisü.
english: for he loves our nation, and he built our synagogue for us."

chokri: Süla Jisu ukozü tavota, pü tavo ce süozü kemecu bakemolü tsoyi, krapi kezhoo püzümiko ketsüpü püce süde “no kemezhehite, I npo ace khrikhro lü kesümi mole, Ino”,
english: Jesus went with them. When he was now not far from the house, the centurion sent friends to him, saying to him, "Lord, don't trouble yourself, for I am not worthy for you to come under my roof.

chokri: Mori Ita nce vorei kesümi mole, vele no jüpü posü lize, athekü-o sürhoyito.
english: Therefore I didn't even think myself worthy to come to you; but say the word, and my servant will be healed.

chokri: Kekrelamori I sheri kekukhrotayo, akhrosheri thipahemiko ba, I püpo tavote, sizi süze pü tavotayi; mu kekrepüce, tavorite zisüsüze, pü tavoriyo; mu athekü mice n mhahio thisüte sizi süze pü thisüyo.
english: For I also am a man placed under authority, having under myself soldiers. I tell this one, 'Go!' and he goes; and to another, 'Come!' and he comes; and to my servant, 'Do this,' and he does it."

chokri: Pü süko po kesüce Jisu püngo thita, de regüpra pükhüko pesüde, püsameji ketakoce poti, I nekoce posüto, I thüma ukemelü kene hibiti ngomo, Isreal lü zosheri bamo.
english: When Jesus heard these things, he marveled at him, and turned and said to the multitude who followed him, "I tell you, I have not found such great faith, no, not in Israel."

chokri: Uko ketsü kesüko ta sala ce sülü vota, tavo theküo mhathi kebao sürho bade ngoyi ta.
english: Those who were sent, returning to the house, found that the servant who had been sick was well.

chokri: Tüso süsanha, püta nade Nain lüvo, pü sajiko mu thüma kekrosheri.
english: It happened soon afterwards, that he went to a city called Nain. Many of his disciples, along with a great multitude, went with him.

chokri: Pü nade kharü meno vori kezüce, hihe! Kesa mopü püde vorizü ngoyi, thenosamepü mipü nu pule; thümakekro püsamejita.
english: Now when he drew near to the gate of the city, behold, one who was dead was carried out, the only son of his mother, and she was a widow. Many people of the city were with her.

chokri: Niepoo süo ngosü de püphimezheta, de püce poti, “krahite”
english: When the Lord saw her, he had compassion on her, and said to her, "Don't cry."

chokri: Pütho phe mopüra püsü, mu kesao pü ketako thakemekre bata, pü poti, “kresa-o, I ncepoyo, tasürite.”
english: He came near and touched the coffin, and the bearers stood still. He said, "Young man, I tell you, arise!"

chokri: Kesao tasüri bayi, de mhapo shüdü ta, pü thimerhüpü püzu tsüsü.
english: He who was dead sat up, and began to speak. And he gave him to his mother.

chokri: Kemeprü thema metüyo bata; Jihova chu zapozale thita. Uko dolü, mu Jihova pü thümako meho süte.
english: Fear took hold of all, and they glorified God, saying, "A great prophet has arisen among us!" and, "God has visited his people!"

chokri: Theju hio se Judea cejü metülü vota, mu pükhü kemetuko metülü ri vota.
english: This report went out concerning him in the whole of Judea, and in all the surrounding region.

chokri: Mha hiko metü John sajiko pü John ceposü.
english: The disciples of John told him about all these things.

chokri: John pü sajimi kena ketsüpü Jisu cesüde pü ketso, no vori ketoo me? Mano hakopo kekrepü fü bata?
english: John, calling to himself two of his disciples, sent them to Jesus, saying, "Are you the one who is coming, or should we look for another?"

chokri: Ketsü kesüni püce voride uni poti, John Baptist no hani ketsüsüle, no vorito ketao me? Mode hakopü kekre pü fü bata?
english: When the men had come to him, they said, "John the Baptizer has sent us to you, saying, 'Are you he who comes, or should we look for another?'"

chokri: Hitücehi pü kethimi kekrothi kesürho sü, thathi ketako, therükesu ukebü ketako, mu mhücemi kekro mhüthipü mhangosü.
english: In that hour he cured many of diseases and plagues and evil spirits; and to many who were blind he gave sight.

chokri: Jisu süla unicepoti, neni tavote, de John ceposüte, mhücemi mhangoyi bate, pheyhomi chotayi bate, lepra kethiko thi menüsüte, nizhümi mhafü seyibate, kesasmiko thimerhüyi bate, jüyikeve popü kediko cesü bate.
english: Jesus answered them, "Go and tell John the things which you have seen and heard: that the blind receive their sight, the lame walk, the lepers are cleansed, the deaf hear, the dead are raised up, and the poor have good news preached to them.

chokri: A yo kejothikemomi thejo kepütho.
english: Blessed is he who is not offended by me."

chokri: John sajiko ta ketasa Jisu John zhopo thipü thümakoce kesü, “dipüla neko ta nhatsalü vo dipü peya? Tekhrapo hü melü keba pe toli züme?
english: When John's messengers had departed, he began to tell the multitudes about John, "What did you go out into the wilderness to see? A reed shaken by the wind?

chokri: Vele neko tapra dipü peya? Thümapü füne kemenü khiketa püme? Hihe, keretso pü keta thümako kedemi celü bayoho.
english: But what did you go out to see? A man clothed in soft clothing? Behold, those who are gorgeously dressed, and live delicately, are in kings' courts.

chokri: Vele neko tavo kedipü pe ketola vo? Saju kepomi me? Ile, I nekoce kepo, saju kepomi ceri kezükeye.
english: But what did you go out to see? A prophet? Yes, I tell you, and much more than a prophet.

chokri: Thüma hio thezho lesilü thopü kemetao Hihe, I n mhüjülü saju kepomi, a choyikethi, ketsüsüto, pü n choko thi kerede süto.
english: This is he of whom it is written, 'Behold, I send my messenger before your face, Who will prepare your way before you.'

chokri: I nekoce kepo, thenomi po menu kesüko dolü John ce saju kepo kezho basamo, vele Jihova kedebalü kenithoo sheri püce zhoku.”
english: "For I tell you, among those who are born of women there is not a greater prophet than John the Baptizer, yet he who is least in the Kingdom of God is greater than he."

chokri: Thümako metü, püfü sesü, malimiko sheri, Jihova po meveta, uko John Baptist zalü jülopüyi.
english: When all the people and the tax collectors heard this, they declared God to be just, having been baptized with John's baptism.

chokri: Vele Phariseemiko mu thezhokeseko Jihova medolü vori muta, John Baptist zalü ri jülo püyi mota.
english: But the Pharisees and the lawyers rejected the counsel of God, not being baptized by him themselves.

chokri: Niepoo poti. Tsüdo nuko pü kedipü zü kemede süta? Dipü bile zide poyi veta?
english: But the Lord said, "To what then will I liken the people of this generation? What are they like?

chokri: Süko natsimiko dukalü bade, hunehu cü de poti, hako leli khusü neko mese, nekonasü mote; hako thekri thisü vele neko krasü mote.
english: They are like children who sit in the marketplace, and call one to another, saying, 'We piped to you, and you didn't dance. We mourned, and you didn't weep.'

chokri: John Baptist voriti tsükhri timo, mori hezori kramo; neko püpü therü kesu püzüta sizite.
english: For John the Baptizer came neither eating bread nor drinking wine, and you say, 'He has a demon.'

chokri: Thüma nuo tavori mhati mhakra; Hi he, mhati kenyüo mu hezo krakenyü-o, malimi, kephomamiko, khriethoo sizite.
english: The Son of Man has come eating and drinking, and you say, 'Behold, a gluttonous man, and a drunkard; a friend of tax collectors and sinners!'

chokri: Vele, mhaselü kemezi mesesüte, pü nune metükola.
english: Wisdom is justified by all her children."

chokri: Phariseemipü Jisu po pücelü mhatino pücüsü, pü tavo de bayi sülü mhati ketola.
english: One of the Pharisees invited him to eat with him. He entered into the Pharisee's house, and sat at the table.

chokri: Thenomipü, kephomapü, nade sülü, Jisu Pharisee celü keba sesü. Tirü Alabastar büpü, mharükethenyüpü püvori.
english: Behold, a woman in the city who was a sinner, when she knew that he was reclining in the Pharisee's house, she brought an alabaster jar of ointment.

chokri: Püphece usaco kratha, pümhüjü popü püphe vade püpitha khi shüsü, de püphe mebosü, süsa tirü süo pü kenhüsü.
english: Standing behind at his feet weeping, she began to wet his feet with her tears, and she wiped them with the hair of her head, kissed his feet, and anointed them with the ointment.

chokri: Pharisee pücü kesüo süo ngosü de pülülü ti, mhace thüma hio saju kepomi lize theno hipü sopü mu dibiti nopü sheli seti mesü, püjü kethao; pü kephoma püpü,
english: Now when the Pharisee who had invited him saw it, he said to himself, "This man, if he were a prophet, would have perceived who and what kind of woman this is who touches him, that she is a sinner."

chokri: Jisu süo kelakesülü poti, Simon, I n ce mhapü po süto, pü poti, kemethao, posüte.
english: Jesus answered him, "Simon, I have something to tell you." He said, "Teacher, say on."

chokri: Mhapü mithepo ketimipü, püpo kepümi mikena ba, mipü presa krapengu mu kekreo hepüngu de püpo püba.
english: "A certain lender had two debtors. The one owed five hundred denarii, and the other fifty.

chokri: Unikenani mhapüri pü püpo shuyi hota, pü süla unipo metü vasüva, uni sopü pü khrü (unü) kutata aceposüte.
english: When they couldn't pay, he forgave them both. Which of them therefore will love him most?"

chokri: Simon püfü kelakesü, akelüce püpo pü mekrokeba-o totho (vamekro kesüo). Pü ce posala kesü, no sheco thimeve shusüzo.
english: Simon answered, "He, I suppose, to whom he forgave the most." He said to him, "You have judged correctly."

chokri: Pü regüsü thenopü ceco peyi de poti, Simon, theno hipü no ngoba ile? I ncelü kelürice no jüpü a phekhrümo, vele thenopü pümhüjü pü aphekhrüsü, mu püpithapü kezhü mesosü.
english: Turning to the woman, he said to Simon, "Do you see this woman? I entered into your house, and you gave me no water for my feet, but she has wet my feet with her tears, and wiped them with the hair of her head.

chokri: No amebo sümo, vele I lüri mu metha (culü) aphe mebo zoba, relümo zo,
english: You gave me no kiss, but she, since the time I came in, has not ceased to kiss my feet.

chokri: No tirü pü apitha kenhümo, vele theno hipü tirü pü aphe kenhüsü.
english: You didn't anoint my head with oil, but she has anointed my feet with ointment.

chokri: Süla I nce posüto, pü kephoma krophi zori vapütsüsüte, kekrelamori pü kekhrü neshu tila, vele kedopü kekhrü kebako kedopü lebi va kesüzo.
english: Therefore I tell you, her sins, which are many, are forgiven, for she loved much. But to whom little is forgiven, the same loves little."

chokri: Pü thenopü ce pokesü, n kephomako vapü ntsü süte ho.
english: He said to her, "Your sins are forgiven."

chokri: Püzü mhati kebako utho ulü lüti, thüma hio mikephoma va keti hio sopüle zi bata?
english: Those who sat at the table with him began to say to themselves, "Who is this who even forgives sins?"

chokri: Pü thenopü ce po kesü, n kemelü la nkela süteho, nnüde tavo tate, site.
english: He said to the woman, "Your faith has saved you. Go in peace."

chokri: Hisalü, Jisu revü khi nadelü mu natsü kolüvo. Zhopothi kenü thejupo, Jihova kede popü mi metha, mu metha keyiko mikerikena püzüta.
english: It happened soon afterwards, that he went about through cities and villages, preaching and bringing the good news of the Kingdom of God. With him were the twelve,

chokri: Thenomikori hu uko züta, therü-kesu rüpü mu kethilü ne thimenü kesüko; Mary Magdalene, therükesu thüna püyo tapra ketapü sheri ta.
english: and certain women who had been healed of evil spirits and infirmities: Mary who was called Magdalene, from whom seven demons had gone out;

chokri: Joanna, Chuza cemi, Herod kedeo celü mhanyoko kepe kezhoo cemi rita; Susanna mu kekremi kekro sheri ta, theno hiko mha khapü uko tsakhu khrühi ketako.
english: and Joanna, the wife of Chuzas, Herod's steward; Susanna; and many others; who ministered to them from their possessions.

chokri: Thüma kekro vori kenguyita, rüna kolü ta Jisu cevori, pü jüve khi ukoce posü
english: When a great multitude came together, and people from every city were coming to him, he spoke by a parable.

chokri: Yitemipü teso sosü toli tsü (tsava). Pü tehatsa pü prüsü, humi ta chodelü tsüta, süko mipo kedüva mu merapo tiyita.
english: "The farmer went out to sow his seed. As he sowed, some fell along the road, and it was trampled under foot, and the birds of the sky devoured it.

chokri: Humi tsüroyo tsüta, mu süko cüsüri tice renyo kedila nacepo süko thsü mesa va.
english: Other seed fell on the rock, and as soon as it grew, it withered away, because it had no moisture.

chokri: Humi tatsahu tsalü tsüta, süko cüsüri tice tsahupo pügükhriva.
english: Other fell amid the thorns, and the thorns grew with it, and choked it.

chokri: Humi ta renyo kevelü kiyi de use zhüyi, sekrapü kezü pü hiko po kesüsa, sesü, de poti, uni kenemi po reni yite.
english: Other fell into the good ground, and grew, and brought forth fruit one hundred times." As he said these things, he called out, "He who has ears to hear, let him hear!"

chokri: Pü sajiko pü ketso de poti, jüve hio püco dipüta?
english: Then his disciples asked him, "What does this parable mean?"

chokri: Pü uko ce poti, “nekola Jihova kede ketekhri tiko seyive, ti khasü te; vele kekremiko la jüvelü poyo. Kepe no uko peto vele ngoyi ho, reni sheri uko seyi ho (lüyi ho)”
english: He said, "To you it is given to know the mysteries of the Kingdom of God, but to the rest in parables; that 'seeing they may not see, and hearing they may not understand.'

chokri: Jüve püco hibi, telha tsa sü Jihova jü.
english: Now the parable is this: The seed is the word of God.

chokri: Chokhü tsüketako, jüo reniyi; therü kesu vorita uko melülü jüo khipü tata, kemosa uko melüyi mu kelariyito.
english: Those along the road are those who hear, then the devil comes, and takes away the word from their heart, that they may not believe and be saved.

chokri: Tsürolü ki ketako, jüo reniyi, uko nüyi; süko ume bamo, dopü melüyi vele medo kephi kevorice retuta.
english: Those on the rock are they who, when they hear, receive the word with joy; but these have no root, who believe for a while, then fall away in time of temptation.

chokri: Tsahu dolü ki ketako, jüo reniyi de cüsüri vele cejülü zhoko, cesücene mu ukemenyü kopo uko royita de usezhüyi mo ketako.
english: That which fell among the thorns, these are those who have heard, and as they go on their way they are choked with cares, riches, and pleasures of life, and bring no fruit to maturity.

chokri: Vele cejü kevelü kekiko, kejütho keve melü, jüo reniyi thi mu züyi; usezhüyi mu sügüsüheyi.
english: That in the good ground, these are such as in an honest and good heart, having heard the word, hold it tightly, and bring forth fruit with patience.

chokri: Mipüri metokhapü merhakhro süyomo mori kha thezikhro süho; vele khapü ubalü miva meve süyo, sülü kelüko mhango ketola.
english: "No one, when he has lit a lamp, covers it with a container, or puts it under a bed; but puts it on a stand, that those who enter in may see the light.

chokri: Kekrelamori, khapü resü va yomo, mipo ngomo ketodo thide; mhakehopü püri khapü resü revayomo, püchozuco mipo ngomo toleno.
english: For nothing is hidden, that will not be revealed; nor anything secret, that will not be known and come to light.

chokri: Süla no renimenuyite, kekrelamori khapü kebami tsüsüto, mhaba kemoo püyo kebakori pütatato,
english: Be careful therefore how you hear. For whoever has, to him will be given; and whoever doesn't have, from him will be taken away even that which he thinks he has."

chokri: Süsa püzu mu püsazüko ta püce vori, vele uko talü püzü kesüyi hota, kekrelamori thümako kefüphi ketala,
english: His mother and brothers came to him, and they could not come near him for the crowd.

chokri: Humi püce poti, nzu mu nsazüko nphu voride chozu thale, uko nzü kesünyü bale,
english: It was told him by some saying, "Your mother and your brothers stand outside, desiring to see you."

chokri: Pü uko fü kelasü, de poti, azu mu a sazüko sü Jihova jü reniyi mu thisü keyi kozo.
english: But he answered them, "My mother and my brothers are these who hear the word of God, and do it."

chokri: Pünhapü püsajiko zü rulü tavozü, pü poti, uko ta jü kezho khü kekreco kukhe, side uko tavozü.
english: Now it happened on one of those days, that he entered into a boat, himself and his disciples, and he said to them, "Let's go over to the other side of the lake." So they launched out.

chokri: Uko rulü tavozüri pü zürenyü ta, tükhrakhradepü vorita ruyo, mu uko ru jüpo chuyita; uko vekedi thita.
english: But as they sailed, he fell asleep. A wind storm came down on the lake, and they were taking on dangerous amounts of water.

chokri: Uko tavo pükesü de poti kemethao, kemethao, uko kata tozütele. Süsa pü cosüri, de tükhra-o kethisü, mu jürephü sheri; siri lazü zita, kemekre züta.
english: They came to him, and awoke him, saying, "Master, master, we are dying!" He awoke, and rebuked the wind and the raging of the water, and they ceased, and it was calm.

chokri: Pü ukoce poti, neko kemelü dipüce ba? Uko mpe ketala uko ngo phita; de ukodolü kepoti thümahio zhorele dipü bita? Pü jü mu tükhra sheri kethi vati he, uko sheri püfü zü ketahi.
english: He said to them, "Where is your faith?" Being afraid they marveled, saying one to another, "Who is this, then, that he commands even the winds and the water, and they obey him?"

chokri: Uko tavo Gadarenes rüna tsoyi, Galilee peco;
english: They arrived at the country of the Gadarenes, which is opposite Galilee.

chokri: Uko cejü sülü tavo kezüc ethümapü therü kesu rüpüko ukebü ketamipü pütü kecho sizi tata, füne khimo, ce pülüri hüyomo; vele mhakhre kolü hütayo.
english: When Jesus stepped ashore, a certain man out of the city who had demons for a long time met him. He wore no clothes, and didn't live in a house, but in the tombs.

chokri: Pü Jisu ngosü, de seta, de retuki pü reshüta, füphra metode poti, I n zü dipü thita no Jisu, no kerekhrütho Jihova nu? I ncechoyo, no amethi hite.
english: When he saw Jesus, he cried out, and fell down before him, and with a loud voice said, "What do I have to do with you, Jesus, you Son of the Most High God? I beg you, don't torment me!"

chokri: Kekrelamori pü thümao celü tapra tano mesakemo rüpü ce posü ketala, tsükenu thegiro khapü püpha pübayo, mu tsahuko, po püzü nhatsalü vo tayo.
english: For Jesus was commanding the unclean spirit to come out of the man. For the unclean spirit had often seized the man. He was kept under guard, and bound with chains and fetters. Breaking the bands apart, he was driven by the demon into the desert.

chokri: Jisu pü ketsoti, “nza sopü?” Pü pokesü aza “legion” kekrelamori therü kekro pülü lü ketala.
english: Jesus asked him, "What is your name?" He said, "Legion," for many demons had entered into him.

chokri: Uko püce choti, püpo uko ketsü pü kerelü lü süva hino chota.
english: They begged him that he would not command them to go into the abyss.

chokri: Süce thevo kekro cejütuyo nha patita, uko püce chode ukopo thevo kolü lütano chota. Pü süla uko ketsü süva.
english: Now there was there a herd of many pigs feeding on the mountain, and they begged him that he would allow them to enter into those. He allowed them.

chokri: Süsa therükesüko thümayo ta thevokrode kolü lüta; side thevoko ta rechü thikhalü tsüta, de jülü uko mehü khri phrüva.
english: The demons came out from the man, and entered into the pigs, and the herd rushed down the steep bank into the lake, and were drowned.

chokri: Süko füpü nhapati ketako dipü bi sheri tavo rünakolü, nadekolü posü.
english: When those who fed them saw what had happened, they fled, and told it in the city and in the country.

chokri: Süla thümako tapra dipü thita sheri pe, ta Jisu cevori mu Thümao ngo, püyo therükesuko tapra ketao, Jisu phece babata, ufüne khi, mu ukelü tsüba keta, side uko mpeta.
english: People went out to see what had happened. They came to Jesus, and found the man from whom the demons had gone out, sitting at Jesus' feet, clothed and in his right mind; and they were afraid.

chokri: Pü kengoko ukoce po küsü dipübide therükesüpo ukebü ketao sürho ta sheri posü.
english: Those who saw it told them how he who had been possessed by demons was healed.

chokri: Süsa makro Gadarenes rüna kemetuko metü püpo tatano chota, kekrelamori uko mpeshu ketala; süla püta rulü lüde sala püra vota.,
english: All the people of the surrounding country of the Gadarenes asked him to depart from them, for they were very much afraid. He entered into the boat, and returned.

chokri: Therükesu püyotade praketa-o püzü batanyü de püce chosü, vele Jisu pü ketsü metava, püce poti,
english: But the man from whom the demons had gone out begged him that he might go with him, but Jesus sent him away, saying,

chokri: Ta ntho ncelü votate, Jihova mhakezho n yo thikesü pü mi mhüngo thisüte, pütho pücholü vota, nadelü metü Jisu püyo mhakezho thi kesü povota.
english: "Return to your house, and declare what great things God has done for you." He went his way, proclaiming throughout the whole city what great things Jesus had done for him.

chokri: Jisu sala voritice miko unüshude püzüyi, kekrelamori uko pü meri kebala.
english: It happened, when Jesus returned, that the multitude welcomed him, for they were all waiting for him.

chokri: Hi he, mipü Jairus zitipü synagogue lü kezhomi ta vori nekütsü Jisu phece pü reshüta, side püpo tapücelü vorino chota.
english: Behold, there came a man named Jairus, and he was a ruler of the synagogue. He fell down at Jesus' feet, and begged him to come into his house,

chokri: Kekrelamori pünupü mipü lebi kebao tsekerikena bari mhathi bata de sata tolizü vele pü tavozü ticethümako pügü tutu tata.
english: for he had an only daughter, about twelve years of age, and she was dying. But as he went, the multitudes pressed against him.

chokri: Thenopüpü tütse kerikena thüzakrü tata, pü mhango keyiko metü khipü daru kesemi la ketseva, sizizori püthi kesürhoyi mo.
english: A woman who had a flow of blood for twelve years, who had spent all her living on physicians, and could not be healed by any,

chokri: Süpü tavori de pü bolaphre nisü, sizi sümetha thüza krümota.
english: came behind him, and touched the fringe of his cloak, and immediately the flow of her blood stopped.

chokri: Jisu poti “sopü a jüsü?” Miko metü pü nimole zi ketasa, Peter püce poti mu püzü ketakosheri, kemethao, thümako n gü tutu ta, (nfüta) no, “sopü anisü” zitola?
english: Jesus said, "Who touched me?" When all denied it, Peter and those with him said, "Master, the multitudes press and jostle you, and you say, 'Who touched me?'"

chokri: Jisu poti “humi ani süle, kekrelamori kekupü ayo taprate.”
english: But Jesus said, "Someone did touch me, for I perceived that power has gone out of me."

chokri: Thenopü resüyi (kevayi) mota, tatsü pü mhüjülü pü reshüta, dipüla pü tavo pünisüsheri popü thüma metüko mese süta, pü dipü bide sürho mhai tasheri posü.
english: When the woman saw that she was not hidden, she came trembling, and falling down before him declared to him in the presence of all the people the reason why she had touched him, and how she was healed immediately.

chokri: Pü thenopüce poti, “Anupü nnütate, n kemelü nthi meve süteho; nlümenü de tavotate.”
english: He said to her, "Daughter, cheer up. Your faith has made you well. Go in peace."

chokri: Pü sükopo kebalü, mipü synagogue kezhoo celü tavori, de püce poti, nnupü sateho kemethao kebühitate.
english: While he still spoke, one from the ruler of the synagogue's house came, saying to him, "Your daughter is dead. Don't trouble the Teacher."

chokri: Vele Jisu süo se kesüce, püfü kelade poti, mpe hite, melü keyi lebizo, sizide thenopü veyito.
english: But Jesus hearing it, answered him, "Don't be afraid. Only believe, and she will be healed."

chokri: Pü celü vori, mipüpori celü lü mota, Peter, James mu John ko lebisa, mu püpo püzuni, (thenopü nepo mu uzuni).
english: When he came to the house, he didn't allow anyone to enter in, except Peter, John, James, the father of the child, and her mother.

chokri: Miko kra mu thekri thiba, vele pü ukoce poti, “krahite, thenopü satamo, vale zü kebazo.”
english: All were weeping and mourning her, but he said, "Don't weep. She isn't dead, but sleeping."

chokri: Uko pükrünyü bata, pü sa keta se kebala.
english: They were ridiculing him, knowing that she was dead.

chokri: Pü ukopo cetavota, pübepü thenopü be ceyi (thezüyi) de poti, relipü, tasürite.
english: But he put them all outside, and taking her by the hand, he called, saying, "Child, arise!"

chokri: Pü rüpü sala vorita, rekri thenopü tasürita, süsa pü ketico khapütsüno posü.
english: Her spirit returned, and she rose up immediately. He commanded that something be given to her to eat.

chokri: Püpo püzu neko ungophita, vele pü ukopo mhathi kesüko pü mipü ceri pohino, ketüva.
english: Her parents were amazed, but he charged them to tell no one what had been done.

chokri: Süsa püsaji mi kerikenako cü kengusü, mu keku khapü uko tsüsü therükesu hukeyi mu kethi thi menü keyi.
english: He called the twelve together, and gave them power and authority over all demons, and to cure diseases.

chokri: Uko ketsüpü Jihova kede zhopo thi mu kethikeni thi kesürho sünode.
english: He sent them forth to preach the Kingdom of God, and to heal the sick.

chokri: Pü ukoce poti, neko revüla mhapüri pü hite, nthü, lesi, tsükhri, mori presa sheri, mori bola sheri kena khita hite.
english: He said to them, "Take nothing for your journey--neither staffs, nor wallet, nor bread, nor money; neither have two coats apiece.

chokri: Nekota celü kehopülü lüsheri sülüba zode tasalatate.
english: Into whatever house you enter, stay there, and depart from there.

chokri: Neko züyi kemuko kehopüsheri neko nade sülü taprata ketoce, npheyo nyophasheri kekhuvate, rüna süo metsü kesü zasi.
english: As many as don't receive you, when you depart from that city, shake off even the dust from your feet for a testimony against them."

chokri: Uko taprata side jüyikeve zhopothi rüna metülü, mu kethi thi kesürho sü.
english: They departed, and went throughout the villages, preaching the Gospel, and healing everywhere.

chokri: Herod tetrarch (governor) püsüko kethi seba. Pülü kehokero (meyhu) thita, kekrelamori humi pücepoti John saketa-o rhüla sütele sizi süta.
english: Now Herod the tetrarch heard of all that was done by him; and he was very perplexed, because it was said by some that John had risen from the dead,

chokri: Humi poti, Elijah lavorite, kekremiko poti, saju kepomi hijü voketako (saketako) rhüla vorite,
english: and by some that Elijah had appeared, and by others that one of the old prophets had risen again.

chokri: Herod lüti, I john pi veve, püsopüta, I püju seshu bate, süla püngosünyü tata.
english: Herod said, "John I beheaded, but who is this, about whom I hear such things?" He sought to see him.

chokri: Apostleko voritice uko thi keyiko pü püce posü, pü ukozü mikedira cejükesa pülü Bethsaida nadeko yizulü vota.
english: The apostles, when they had returned, told him what things they had done. He took them, and withdrew apart to a deserted place of a city called Bethsaida.

chokri: Thümako süse kesü püsa mejivorita, mu pü Jihova kede jupü ukoce posü; mu uko thikesürhosü moze kesuko yhi kesürhosü.
english: But the multitudes, perceiving it, followed him. He welcomed them, and spoke to them of the Kingdom of God, and he cured those who needed healing.

chokri: Tüsozho vota kezüce, methakeyiko püce vori (mikerikenako) de püce poti, makroko ketsü metavate ukopo rüna ukhüce tavo keba kezüthi mu u ketico ngoyi ketola; uko kesa cejü hico ba ketala.
english: The day began to wear away; and the twelve came, and said to him, "Send the multitude away, that they may go into the surrounding villages and farms, and lodge, and get food, for we are here in a deserted place."

chokri: Pü uko ce poti, neko mhapü ukopo tiyite, uko poti, tsükhrida püngu mu fü kena kezü bamole, uko tavo thüma metüla mhakhriyi kemosa.
english: But he said to them, "You give them something to eat." They said, "We have no more than five loaves and two fish, unless we should go and buy food for all these people."

chokri: Kekrelamori uko thüma nyepüngu ta ketala, püsajikoce poti, ukopo hiepüngu kezü kropü thi bayi te,
english: For they were about five thousand men. He said to his disciples, "Make them sit down in groups of about fifty each."

chokri: Uko sübi thiyi, mu ukopo bayi,
english: They did so, and made them all sit down.

chokri: Süsa pü fü kena mu tsükhrida püngu khiyi, tüyo peyi, de pü jopü süko yosü süko cephro süde khapü methakeyiko po kezapü makro tsüsüno khasü.
english: He took the five loaves and the two fish, and looking up to the sky, he blessed them, and broke them, and gave them to the disciples to set before the multitude.

chokri: Uko metü tiyi, de meguyi, süsalü tishoko kengupü merha kerikena chuyi.
english: They ate, and were all filled. They gathered up twelve baskets of broken pieces that were left over.

chokri: Pü lebi kechothi bari, methakeyiko püce ba; pü uko ketsode, “thümako apü sopülizi baya?”
english: It happened, as he was praying alone, that the disciples were with him, and he asked them, "Who do the multitudes say that I am?"

chokri: Uko püce poti, John Baptist lezi bate, humi poti, Elijah lezi bate; kekre miko poti, saju kepomi hijü vo ketako mepüle zi bale (rhüla voritale).
english: They answered, "'John the Baptizer,' but others say, 'Elijah,' and others, that one of the old prophets is risen again."

chokri: Pü uko ketsode “vele neko apü sopüle ziba”? Peter püjü kelakesü, Jihova, Khrista zole.
english: He said to them, "But who do you say that I am?" Peter answered, "The Christ of God."

chokri: Pü rekrisü ukoce poti, mhasüo pü mipü ceri pohite.
english: But he warned them, and commanded them to tell this to no one,

chokri: Thümanuo mhakekrolü kemezhe zoto, mehimi, thevo kezhoko, mu lesikeseko pü melavato, mu pü dokhri vato, side zhosülü rhüla sütato.
english: saying, "The Son of Man must suffer many things, and be rejected by the elders, chief priests, and scribes, and be killed, and the third day be raised up."

chokri: pü uko metüce poti, mhace thüma humi asamejito lize, pütho pü mela va moze su, pü Cross (n phe medü) pü asameji mozesu.
english: He said to all, "If anyone desires to come after me, let him deny himself, take up his cross, and follow me.

chokri: Kekrelamori pü kerhüro kelayi nyüsa tisa pü medi vato, vele pü kehü medi va lize, alano, süo ngolayito.
english: For whoever desires to save his life will lose it, but whoever will lose his life for my sake, the same will save it.

chokri: Mhace mipü cejü metü prapü püzhü thiyi zori pü medi vaze, mori pühu vaze, kedipü kemeve ba.?
english: For what does it profit a man if he gains the whole world, and loses or forfeits his own self?

chokri: Kehopüri ala mengata lize mori ajükola sheri, thümanuo zapozale thide Püpo mu tüyochoyiko zü kiri tinha püri (sheri) püzapo menga tato.
english: For whoever will be ashamed of me and of my words, of him will the Son of Man be ashamed, when he comes in his glory, and the glory of the Father, and of the holy angels.

chokri: I nekoce jüthopü posüto, hice ketha humi Jihova kede ngokemothe sata keho kropü tha.
english: But I tell you the truth: There are some of those who stand here, who will in no way taste of death, until they see the Kingdom of God."

chokri: Pü hikopokevasa, Peter, John, mu James ko zü cejütuyokho, kechothi ketola.
english: It happened about eight days after these sayings, that he took with him Peter, John, and James, and went up onto the mountain to pray.

chokri: Pü kechothi kebace pühü thimekre prarita, pü füneko megapü vava türüprü prari ta.
english: As he was praying, the appearance of his face was altered, and his clothing became white and dazzling.

chokri: Hihe, thüma kena püzü kerethi bata, süni Moses mu Elijah ni.
english: Behold, two men were talking with him, who were Moses and Elijah,

chokri: Zapozale thide mengosü, kepo keti püco lize püpo dipüsü Jerusalem lü thimo shüta sheri kepo.
english: who appeared in glory, and spoke of his departure, which he was about to accomplish at Jerusalem.

chokri: Vele Peter mu püzü ketani zünyü bata, uko co kesürice pü zapozale kethi ngo mu püzü kebani sheri ngo.
english: Now Peter and those who were with him were heavy with sleep, but when they were fully awake, they saw his glory, and the two men who stood with him.

chokri: Uni püzü kekho ketace Peter püce poti, kemethao, hako hice vori tsühi verizo; hakopo kereshüce 3 (sü) thisü toma, pü nla, pü Moses la mu pü Elijah la; dipüla pü süpo ba sheri pü semo.
english: It happened, as they were parting from him, that Peter said to Jesus, "Master, it is good for us to be here. Let's make three tents: one for you, and one for Moses, and one for Elijah," not knowing what he said.

chokri: Pü sizipo kebalü kemhüpü kiri de tavo uko zhu yita, mu uko mpe bata kemhülü lü ketala.
english: While he said these things, a cloud came and overshadowed them, and they were afraid as they entered into the cloud.

chokri: Kemhülü thefüpü voride poti, hiohi anu akhrüo zo, püfü reniyite.
english: A voice came out of the cloud, saying, "This is my beloved Son. Listen to him!"

chokri: Mhafü-o ta ketasa, Jisu lebi bade ngoyi, uko mhasüko pü mipü ceri pomode khapü meta, uko kengoko.
english: When the voice came, Jesus was found alone. They were silent, and told no one in those days any of the things which they had seen.

chokri: Süsanha uko cejütuyo kiri tice thüma kekro tavo püzü kesü.
english: It happened on the next day, when they had come down from the mountain, that a great multitude met him.

chokri: Thüma krolü mipü semüto de püce poti, Hihe, kemethao, nce choyo, anu pesütehe, kekrelamori anu pü lebi zole.
english: Behold, a man from the crowd called out, saying, "Teacher, I beg you to look at my son, for he is my only child.

chokri: Kesu rüpü pütüyi de pü methi mu püpo mhütsa melu tatale, püvü kesüsa pü khava tatayo.
english: Behold, a spirit takes him, he suddenly cries out, and it convulses him so that he foams, and it hardly departs from him, bruising him severely.

chokri: I nsajiko pühu vano posü, vele uko thiyi hote.
english: I begged your disciples to cast it out, and they couldn't."

chokri: Jisu püfü kelasü de poti, neko kemelü kediko mu kebü keta shuyeko, apo dithe pü neko zü bata, mu neko khrühi zo bata? Nnu zü hice vorite.
english: Jesus answered, "Faithless and perverse generation, how long shall I be with you and bear with you? Bring your son here."

chokri: Pütavori züri, therüo pü kedüsü püpo retuta, side pü cerhasü, Jisu mesakemo rüpü kethisü, mu natsio thi kesürhosü; de khapü püpo tsüsü.
english: While he was still coming, the demon threw him down and convulsed him violently. But Jesus rebuked the unclean spirit, and healed the boy, and gave him back to his father.

chokri: Uko metü Jihova keku ngopü ungo shuta, vele Jisu uko ngothi kebace sajiko cepoti.
english: They were all astonished at the majesty of God. But while all were marveling at all the things which Jesus did, he said to his disciples,

chokri: Hikopo neko nilü lüte, kekrelamori thüma Nuo tüpü mibelü sü vato,
english: "Let these words sink into your ears, for the Son of Man will be delivered up into the hands of men."

chokri: Vele uko ke po süo seyimo, ukola kevapü meta ketala, süla uko lüyirimo; mu uko kepo süo ketsori mpeta,
english: But they didn't understand this saying. It was concealed from them, that they should not perceive it, and they were afraid to ask him about this saying.

chokri: Ukolü sopü kezhotho thito sheri mhalü bata,
english: There arose an argument among them about which of them was the greatest.

chokri: Jisu uko lüdo seyi, de natsimipü khapü ukodolü methasü de.
english: Jesus, perceiving the reasoning of their hearts, took a little child, and set him by his side,

chokri: Uko cepoti, sopü azalü thümane hio züyi sheri azü yizo, mu azükeyimi a ketsü kesüo ri züyi yo; kekrelamori nekodolü kenithoo sü kezhothoo thito.
english: and said to them, "Whoever receives this little child in my name receives me. Whoever receives me receives him who sent me. For whoever is least among you all, this one will be great."

chokri: John püfü kelasü, poti kemethao, mipü nzakhi therükesu hu tade hako ngole, hako pü khasüte, kekrelamori pü uko zü tamo tila.
english: John answered, "Master, we saw someone casting out demons in your name, and we forbade him, because he doesn't follow with us."

chokri: Jisu püce poti, pü khahite, kekrelamori uko metsu kemomi sü ukokromi zo.
english: Jesus said to him, "Don't forbid him, for he who is not against us is for us."

chokri: Pütü tsovori ketabi pü Jerusalem lükho ketosü lüsü ta, püzü pü süpe zo vota.
english: It came to pass, when the days were near that he should be taken up, he intently set his face to go to Jerusalem,

chokri: Jü choyimiko ketsü pü pü mhüjü sü mu uko tavo rüna keni pülü lüyi. Samaritan rüna, püla thethi kesü ketola.
english: and sent messengers before his face. They went, and entered into a village of the Samaritans, so as to prepare for him.

chokri: Uko püzüyi muta, kekrelamori pü Jerusalem lü kho ketola.
english: They didn't receive him, because he was traveling with his face set towards Jerusalem.

chokri: Pü sajiko James mu John ni poti, Niepoo, nmedo lize hako me tüyo chosüde kiri uko thüva toma, Elijah thi kesü bide?
english: When his disciples, James and John, saw this, they said, "Lord, do you want us to command fire to come down from the sky, and destroy them, just as Elijah did?"

chokri: Vele pü rügüsü ni kethita, neni dipü rüpü zhorüle sheri semo.
english: But he turned and rebuked them, "You don't know of what kind of spirit you are.

chokri: Kekrelamori thüma nuo mi kerhüro meka ketola vorimo, vele uko kelasü ketola, mu uko ta rena kekre pülü vota.
english: For the Son of Man didn't come to destroy men's lives, but to save them." They went to another village.

chokri: Uko chodelü tavo kezüce, mipü püce poti, Niepoo, no dipüce voto sheri I n saji thitatole.
english: As they went on the way, a certain man said to him, "I want to follow you wherever you go, Lord."

chokri: Jisu püce poti, mesüriko ucene, tüyorako ukro ba, vele thüma Nuo püpi khakesü thiri di.
english: Jesus said to him, "The foxes have holes, and the birds of the sky have nests, but the Son of Man has no place to lay his head."

chokri: Pü mikekre püce poti, asameji yite, vele süo poti, Niepoo apo tavo apo khrüva merate.
english: He said to another, "Follow me!" But he said, "Lord, allow me first to go and bury my father."

chokri: Jisu püce po kesü, kesami po pü kesako khrü vate, vele no tavo Jihova kede zhopo thisüte.
english: But Jesus said to him, "Leave the dead to bury their own dead, but you go and announce the Kingdom of God."

chokri: Kekremipü poti, Niepoo I nsa meji tole, vele apo tavo ukoce vebate sizi mera vate, acelü kebakoce.
english: Another also said, "I want to follow you, Lord, but first allow me to bid farewell to those who are at my house."

chokri: Jisu püce poti, mipüri metho dico tüsü püba regüsala püsaco kepemi Jihova kede po shümo.
english: But Jesus said to him, "No one, having put his hand to the plow, and looking back, is fit for the Kingdom of God."

chokri: Mhahiko salü Niepoo thüma kekre hethüna ketsüsü, mikena na de pü mhüjülü ketsü pü nade metülü sü, pü voketo kolü.
english: Now after these things, the Lord also appointed seventy others, and sent them two by two ahead of him into every city and place, where he was about to come.

chokri: Pü ukocepoti, yirü keto bashüte, vele rüketomi tsarizo, süla yi Niepoo ce chosüte, püpo yi kerümi ketsüpü yirülü sü ketola.
english: Then he said to them, "The harvest is indeed plentiful, but the laborers are few. Pray therefore to the Lord of the harvest, that he may send out laborers into his harvest.

chokri: Tavote, I neko ketsü süzüho, teko ketsü pü sace dolü kesübi.
english: Go your ways. Behold, I send you out as lambs among wolves.

chokri: Presabü, mori pheku, mori lesi sheri püta hite; cholalü mipüri reshütahi.
english: Carry no purse, nor wallet, nor sandals. Greet no one on the way.

chokri: Celü kehopülü lü sheri, theralü, thelü kemenü (kezüküve) po cehilü bayite side posüte.
english: Into whatever house you enter, first say, 'Peace be to this house.'

chokri: Mhace cesülü kezükeve ba lize thelü kemenü süo bayi toho, molize thelü kemenü süo n yo la vori tato.
english: If a son of peace is there, your peace will rest on him; but if not, it will return to you.

chokri: Ce sülü keti kekra khapü n ketsüko timu krayite, kekrelamori mha kethimi püzhokhro ngokeyi mesü, cepülü ba zote, kekrelü vo hite.
english: Remain in that same house, eating and drinking the things they give, for the laborer is worthy of his wages. Don't go from house to house.

chokri: Neko nade kehopülü lüzori mi mhakhapü neketsüko timu krayite.
english: Into whatever city you enter, and they receive you, eat the things that are set before you.

chokri: Kethimi thi kesürho süte, sülü, side ukoce Jihova kede vori ba keta posüte.
english: Heal the sick who are therein, and tell them, 'The Kingdom of God has come near to you.'

chokri: Vele neko nadelü kelüce, mi neko züyi muta lize, rüna sülü tapra tate, ne pra kezüce hiziti posüte.
english: But into whatever city you enter, and they don't receive you, go out into the streets of it and say,

chokri: Nyopha ne nadelü hako phe kopü ketako sheri kekhupü ne rüna metsu süzüho, Jihova kede nece vori.
english: 'Even the dust from your city that clings to us, we wipe off against you. Nevertheless know this, that the Kingdom of God has come near to you.'

chokri: Vele I nekoce posüto, sünha rüna süo ju Sodom rüleno hüyi reyiku tato.
english: I tell you, it will be more tolerable in that day for Sodom than for that city.

chokri: Hesheherü chorazin, hesheherü Bethsaida, mhatho ungo keti thipü Tyre mu Sidon lüko sü lize nece thi kesüko biti, hi mhüjü uko thelü rede batato, de uko gakhrofü mu megünyo lü bata te vele.
english: "Woe to you, Chorazin! Woe to you, Bethsaida! For if the mighty works had been done in Tyre and Sidon which were done in you, they would have repented long ago, sitting in sackcloth and ashes.

chokri: Vele jünyo keti ketonha Tyre mu Sidon ni rele neko relece reyiku tato.
english: But it will be more tolerable for Tyre and Sidon in the judgment than for you.

chokri: Capernaum no nthi kerekrüpü kho tüyotso keba thi siri n kelhupü mezalü sü vato.
english: You, Capernaum, who are exalted to heaven, will be brought down to Hades.

chokri: Somi nekofü reni sheri afü reniyi, somi neko thalice ta sheri a thalice tazo, süla a thalice ketami sü aketsü kesüo thalice tazo.
english: Whoever listens to you listens to me, and whoever rejects you rejects me. Whoever rejects me rejects him who sent me."

chokri: Mi hethünako rethu (rechü) de ta sala vori, de poti, Niepoo therükesuko sheri nzalü hako khrote (füzüthite)
