english: The seventy returned with joy, saying, "Lord, even the demons are subject to us in your name!"

chokri: Pü ukoce poti, Hihe, Satan tüyo metha kekrithi cejü kiri keta I ngo ho.
english: He said to them, "I saw Satan having fallen like lightning from heaven.

chokri: Hihe, I keku pü nekotsüve, nhibü, sebako kedü vaketo, neko ngomuko pi keku mhapüri neko methiva keho.
english: Behold, I give you authority to tread on serpents and scorpions, and over all the power of the enemy. Nothing will in any way hurt you.

chokri: Mhasükola neko nüta hite, rüpüko kha neko khro sükevala; vele neko za thopü tüyo meta ketala neko rechü yite.
english: Nevertheless, don't rejoice in this, that the spirits are subject to you, but rejoice that your names are written in heaven."

chokri: Pütü sülü Jisu ri rechüto, pü rüpülü, de poti, “I n meza, Apo, tücejü Niepoo, mha hiko kesemi mu kemerhüko mhüjülü kevapü meta de halapü natsi theba thejüko meka sü, Apo, sizi ti n nü ketala.”
english: In that same hour Jesus rejoiced in the Holy Spirit, and said, "I thank you, O Father, Lord of heaven and earth, that you have hidden these things from the wise and understanding, and revealed them to little children. Yes, Father, for so it was well-pleasing in your sight."

chokri: Apo mha metü khapü atsü süte, mipüri Nuo sü semo, vele Pootho seba; mori mipüri poo semo, vele Nuo thozo, mu Nuo tho Poo sü thapü mika süyo.
english: Turning to the disciples, he said, "All things have been delivered to me by my Father. No one knows who the Son is, except the Father, and who the Father is, except the Son, and he to whomever the Son desires to reveal him."

chokri: Pü regü metha keyiko ce vode uko ce poti, nemhüpü mha kengoko rüku thoko (thejo kepütho).
english: Turning to the disciples, he said privately, "Blessed are the eyes which see the things that you see,

chokri: Kekrelamori saju kepoko mu kedemi kekrosheri mha hiko ngosü nyüshu vorizo, vele ngo mote; neko keseko sesü nyüshuzo vele uko semote.
english: for I tell you that many prophets and kings desired to see the things which you see, and didn't see them, and to hear the things which you hear, and didn't hear them."

chokri: Thezhokesemipü süthayi de pü kedo ketola pü ketsoti, kemethao, kechothe kehü kalü thiyi ketola I kedipü thimoze suta?
english: Behold, a certain lawyer stood up and tested him, saying, "Teacher, what shall I do to inherit eternal life?"

chokri: Pücepo kesü, thezho lesilü dipü thometa? No dipüle zide phriba?
english: He said to him, "What is written in the law? How do you read it?"

chokri: Püfü kelakesü, no n Niepoo n Rüo khrü mu rhoyite nmelü metü, nkeku metü, nkhrirarü, metü, n kelü metü; mu nsüzanumi sheri khrüpü ntho n kekhrü biyite.
english: He answered, "You shall love the Lord your God with all your heart, with all your soul, with all your strength, and with all your mind; and your neighbor as yourself."

chokri: Püce pokesü, no potü süzo, hibi thiyi te, mu size no rhüyito.
english: He said to him, "You have answered correctly. Do this, and you will live."

chokri: Vele pütho mezileti thinyü de Jisu cepoti, sopü asüzanumita?
english: But he, desiring to justify himself, asked Jesus, "Who is my neighbor?"

chokri: Jisu püfü kela kesü, thümapü Jerusalem lü ta Jericho cholü kizü, ri ta süketimi dolü lüta, püfüneko khiva, pü rezasü, de pü khava tata, pü vüpü phesa mezüva.
english: Jesus answered, "A certain man was going down from Jerusalem to Jericho, and he fell among robbers, who both stripped him and beat him, and departed, leaving him half dead.

chokri: Pütüsüce thevomipü cho sülü tavo, püngo zo vele pü chorhe tata.
english: By chance a certain priest was going down that way. When he saw him, he passed by on the other side.

chokri: Levimipü ta sülüvo, püngosü thethi sülü cho kekrepülü ta vota,
english: In the same way a Levite also, when he came to the place, and saw him, passed by on the other side.

chokri: Vele Samaritanmipü revükhi pü kezülü vori, püngosü de püphi mezheta.
english: But a certain Samaritan, as he traveled, came where he was. When he saw him, he was moved with compassion,

chokri: Ta püce phe de pü rezakuko therü mu phasü, tirü mu hezo lapü süko khrüva, pü khapü pü khonu (gada) yo süyi, de pü züvo prolü süde pü theküsü.
english: came to him, and bound up his wounds, pouring on oil and wine. He set him on his own animal, and brought him to an inn, and took care of him.

chokri: Süsanha pütata, pü presa (raka) kena pü (pü theküo tsüsü) cieniepo-o tsüsü, de püce poti, pü thekü menu bate, I tala kevori ce no mhakekre ketse kevako I shupü ntsü vato.
english: On the next day, when he departed, he took out two denarii, and gave them to the host, and said to him, 'Take care of him. Whatever you spend beyond that, I will repay you when I return.'

chokri: Thüma misüko sopü mizhü süti ketako belü kemezhe ketao süzanuta?
english: Now which of these three do you think seemed to be a neighbor to him who fell among the robbers?"

chokri: Pü pokesü, püphi mezhe kesüo zo, süsa Jisu püce poti, nori tavo sübiti thisüte.
english: He said, "He who showed mercy on him." Then Jesus said to him, "Go and do likewise."

chokri: Uko tavo kezüce, püta rüna pülü lüta, thenomipü püza Martha siti püba, püzüvo pücelü süyi.
english: It happened as they went on their way, he entered into a certain village, and a certain woman named Martha received him into her house.

chokri: Pü sazüpü za Mary, pü Jisu phe ceba de jüko reniba.
english: She had a sister called Mary, who also sat at Jesus' feet, and heard his word.

chokri: Vele Martha choyi kethi renade merezoba, ta püce vori, de poti, Niepoo, asazüpü alebipo mhathoko la mere zoba tino n lüni mozome? Püpo tavori akhrühi süno posüte.
english: But Martha was distracted with much serving, and she came up to him, and said, "Lord, don't you care that my sister left me to serve alone? Ask her therefore to help me."

chokri: Jisu püfü kelasü de püce poti, Martha, Martha, no mha kothi mere zoba, mhakekrolü.
english: Jesus answered her, "Martha, Martha, you are anxious and troubled about many things,

chokri: Vele khimokesu, mhacopü lebizo, Mary no mhakeve süo kedayiteho, mha süo pücelü khipü tata hote.
english: but one thing is needed. Mary has chosen the good part, which will not be taken away from her."

chokri: Thüma phriphayi keho tavo kengu keyice uko hunehu kedü vota, pü sajiko cepoti, Neko Pharisee kethera ketako khre yo menu yite.
english: Meanwhile, when a multitude of many thousands had gathered together, so much so that they trampled on each other, he began to tell his disciples first of all, "Beware of the yeast of the Pharisees, which is hypocrisy.

chokri: Hupü kemetako mhapüri kehala sümo taho; mori resü ketako semo keta bamo.
english: But there is nothing covered up, that will not be revealed, nor hidden, that will not be known.

chokri: Süla no tüzülü pokesüko cetuyo po mese metosüto.
english: Therefore whatever you have said in the darkness will be heard in the light. What you have spoken in the ear in the inner chambers will be proclaimed on the housetops.

chokri: Azüneko I nekoce posüto, uthiupho dokhri ketio mpe hite, uko süpi mhathisayi keto basamote.
english: "I tell you, my friends, don't be afraid of those who kill the body, and after that have no more that they can do.

chokri: Vele I mejü neko sopü mpe moze susheli posüto, mpe moze kesuo sü dokhri kevasa ne kegüdüpü mezalü sü va ketoo pü keku keneo, I posüto pü mpe yite.
english: But I will warn you whom you should fear. Fear him, who after he has killed, has power to cast into Gehenna. Yes, I tell you, fear him.

chokri: Cükhra püngu pü presa kena khriyi yo süko püri Jihova mhüjülü rükramo keta bamo (semo keta bamo)
english: "Aren't five sparrows sold for two assaria coins? Not one of them is forgotten by God.

chokri: Vele npithapri metü phriyi te, neko mpe hite, kekrelamori neko cükhra kekroce sheri uma neku.
english: But the very hairs of your head are all numbered. Therefore don't be afraid. You are of more value than many sparrows.

chokri: I neko ce posasüto, kehopürei thüma mhüjülü aza phrakesüo, Niepoo sheri tüyo Jihova choyiko mhüjülü süko phrasüto.
english: "I tell you, everyone who confesses me before men, him will the Son of Man also confess before the angels of God;

chokri: Vele thüma mhüjülü a phramu ketami I sheri tüyo Jihova choyiko mhüjülü phramo tato,
english: but he who denies me in the presence of men will be denied in the presence of the angels of God.

chokri: Kehopü sheri Nuo kemetsu kepomi, kejovasü to vele Kemesa Rüpü kethami kejo vasü ho.
english: Everyone who speaks a word against the Son of Man will be forgiven, but those who blaspheme against the Holy Spirit will not be forgiven.

chokri: Uko neko zü synagogue kolü, shecomiko, mu kekumiko cevoto; neko kedipü poto sheri mhalü kedodu tahite.
english: When they bring you before the synagogues, the rulers, and the authorities, don't be anxious how or what you will answer, or what you will say;

chokri: Kekrelamori Kemesa Rüpü bavüdo sülü mhapo kezüko nceposüto.
english: for the Holy Spirit will teach you in that same hour what you must say."

chokri: Thüma krolü mipü püce poti, kemethao ajürao ceposüte, püpo kalü mhako azü kezayi ketola.
english: One of the multitude said to him, "Teacher, tell my brother to divide the inheritance with me."

chokri: Pü pücepoti, “thümao, sopü a thipü mha keza ketimi thisü tola mori shecomi thisü ta, neko pi?”
english: But he said to him, "Man, who made me a judge or an arbitrator over you?"

chokri: Pü ukoce po kesü, menuyite, mhakeholü uce riyite, thümapü kehülü mhakekro kengu keyilü bamotila.
english: He said to them, "Beware! Keep yourselves from covetousness, for a man's life doesn't consist of the abundance of the things which he possesses."

chokri: Pü jüvepü ukoce po kesü, kenemipü cejülü mhakekro ngoyi.
english: He spoke a parable to them, saying, "The ground of a certain rich man brought forth abundantly.

chokri: Pütho pülü lüti, I kedipü thita, kekrelamori amhako kengu yi keto thethi basa mote.
english: He reasoned within himself, saying, 'What will I do, because I don't have room to store my crops?'

chokri: Pülüti, I hizi thito, ahaceko cele (rhü) vato, de thi mezhoyito; mu sülü ahako mu azhüko kha kenguyi tato.
english: He said, 'This is what I will do. I will pull down my barns, and build bigger ones, and there I will store all my grain and my goods.

chokri: I athi apho cepoti, relüyite, no mhakekro tütse kekrola kengu metave; krokrü tate, ti mu krade nnü bata te.
english: I will tell my soul, "Soul, you have many goods laid up for many years. Take your ease, eat, drink, be merry."'

chokri: Vele Jihova püce poti, no kemeloo, tsüzi n yo nrüpü zü tata to; sizitalize mha süko sopü zhü thitata, no ntho nla mha kengupü kemetako?
english: "But God said to him, 'You foolish one, tonight your soul is required of you. The things which you have prepared--whose will they be?'

chokri: Süla sübi pütho püla mhakengu yo, vele Jihova la pü mhane kemoo sizi tato.
english: So is he who lays up treasure for himself, and is not rich toward God."

chokri: Pü sajiko cepoti, süla I neko ce posüto, nekehü la mhapüri medo thahite, no dipü tita, mori n mola dipü khi be meneta ziti.
english: He said to his disciples, "Therefore I tell you, don't be anxious for your life, what you will eat, nor yet for your body, what you will wear.

chokri: Kerhü (kehü) ketico ce veku, mu uthiumo sheri fünece veku.
english: Life is more than food, and the body is more than clothing.

chokri: Tüyo rako rele lüsüte, uko tsava mori rüri yomo; uko tühace mori kengu ketice ri bamo, Jihova süko va thori thiyo, (ntheküyo) neko sükoce veku mome, türakoce?
english: Consider the ravens: they don't sow, they don't reap, they have no warehouse or barn, and God feeds them. How much more valuable are you than birds!

chokri: Neko mhalü medo kebala ne botho thepü ri thi pü rekrükuyi tome?
english: Which of you by being anxious can add a cubit to his height?

chokri: Mhace neko mha kenithoo zori thiyi ho lize, dipüla ne dipüthide mhakekreko lü medo ba?
english: If then you aren't able to do even the least things, why are you anxious about the rest?

chokri: Rübapü rele lüsüte, dipü bide uko hü mu mabaya, uko mhathi yomo, uko terhi thi (tachozü) yomo, siri I neko ce posüto, Solomon tho zori pü retso therho kethi thoce sheri keretso pü süko bimo.
english: Consider the lilies, how they grow. They don't toil, neither do they spin; yet I tell you, even Solomon in all his glory was not arrayed like one of these.

chokri: Mhace Jihova nha tha hüba sedo khapü melü süva keto retso thisüyo süze dithedüpü neko retso thisü mota; neko kemelü kediko.
english: But if this is how God clothes the grass in the field, which today exists, and tomorrow is cast into the oven, how much more will he clothe you, O you of little faith?

chokri: Neko dipü ti mu krato sheri süko lü hite, süko la nelü kemezhe de kehokero thi hite.
english: Don't seek what you will eat or what you will drink; neither be anxious.

chokri: Melü kemomi süko phu tayo, mu neko poneko süko khimoze kesu seba,
english: For the nations of the world seek after all of these things, but your Father knows that you need these things.

chokri: Vele Jihova kede kethi phu mera yite, mhasüko metü kha neko tsü süto.
english: But seek God's Kingdom, and all these things will be added to you.

chokri: Mpe pa hite, kro ketsako, neko po Pü kede kha neko tsü keto pü medo ketala.
english: Don't be afraid, little flock, for it is your Father's good pleasure to give you the Kingdom.

chokri: Neko, mhakebako zhu yite, kha ketsakhumi vaphi süte; neko khapü ne lekholü süyi, sühü po tiva keho cene, tüyo dita keho mhangu, kerügomi ri mori mhothsü tiri khipü tata keho.
english: Sell that which you have, and give gifts to the needy. Make for yourselves purses which don't grow old, a treasure in the heavens that doesn't fail, where no thief approaches, neither moth destroys.

chokri: Kekrelamori n mha nzhü kebara n melü sheri sülü bayo.
english: For where your treasure is, there will your heart be also.

chokri: N chephayi mo n metopo tovekrüyite.
english: "Let your loins be girded and your lamps burning.

chokri: Thüma uniepomi nolü hülü la vori ketofü kebabi, pü, sala ta kevori ce tavo cekha kedü sülize uko rekri cekhakhri süyo tibi.
english: Be like men watching for their lord, when he returns from the marriage feast; that, when he comes and knocks, they may immediately open to him.

chokri: Choyimi füba uniepomi kevorice ngokeyi miko thejo kepütho; I neko ce posüto, püvori de püchephayi to, mu ukopo bayito, mhati ketola, side pü renapü uko tsüto.
english: Blessed are those servants, whom the lord will find watching when he comes. Most assuredly I tell you, that he will dress himself, and make them recline, and will come and serve them.

chokri: Mhace pü rifü kenalü vori lizo mori süolü vori zori tavo uko fü keba ngoyi tisa süko thejo kepütho choyiko.
english: They will be blessed if he comes in the second or third watch, and finds them so.

chokri: Hihi seyi te, mhace thüma kevemi kerügomi vori keto seba lize pü fü türü bato, mu püce mipo thi mesu vaho.
english: But know this, that if the master of the house had known in what hour the thief was coming, he would have watched, and not allowed his house to be broken into.

chokri: Neko süla kerede pü bate, thüma Nuo dipü tülü vorito sheri sekemola. (Nelüba kemolü vori ketola).
english: Therefore be ready also, for the Son of Man is coming in an hour that you don't expect him."

chokri: Peter pücepoti, Niepoo jüve hiopo hakoce mori metü koce posü home?
english: Peter said to him, "Lord, are you telling this parable to us, or to everybody?"

chokri: Mhakepe mipü Niepoo poti, jütho mu mhase ti cetami pü thipü mi kekhati thisü, püce pe ketola, ketico pü pütü tülü ukopo mhatiyi; sopü siziyi veta?
english: The Lord said, "Who then is the faithful and wise steward, whom his lord will set over his household, to give them their portion of food at the right times?

chokri: Pü niepoo la vori tice sizi ba ngoyi lize veto.
english: Blessed is that servant whom his lord will find doing so when he comes.

chokri: I nekoce posüto, pü khapü pümha püzhü metüpi kezhoo (kepeo) thi tato.
english: Truly I tell you, that he will set him over all that he has.

chokri: Vele cheta süo pülü lüti a niepoo la vori mhai hote, mu pü choyi miko thepu thenoko vü bata mu keti kekra thi mezü bata.
english: But if that servant says in his heart, 'My lord delays his coming,' and begins to beat the menservants and the maidservants, and to eat and drink, and to be drunken,

chokri: Ceta süo niepoo lüba kemo pülü vori ta, tüsozho, bavüdo, pü füba kemolü pü dothü phapha vato, mu pü khapü melü kemoko thethi lü sü vato.
english: then the lord of that servant will come in a day when he isn't expecting him, and in an hour that he doesn't know, and will cut him in two, and place his portion with the unfaithful.

chokri: Ceta süo pü niepoo medo semo, pü kerede pü bamo, mori pü thino kesüko ri thimo, süla va kekro sürü pü püvü süta.
english: That servant, who knew his lord's will, and didn't prepare, nor do what he wanted, will be beaten with many stripes,

chokri: Vele mhapüri sebamode mhathi ba ketao süpü vüzori kedopü kevü zoto; kekrelamori mhakekro mha melü kesüo püyo mhakekro choto, mhakekro khikeyi miyo mhakekro püpo khasüto.
english: but he who didn't know, and did things worthy of stripes, will be beaten with few stripes. To whoever much is given, of him will much be required; and to whom much was entrusted, of him more will be asked.

chokri: I me kha cejülü süketola vori, I kedipü thita, mhace süosü to bata lize.
english: "I came to throw fire on the earth. I wish it were already kindled.

chokri: Vele I jülo kepü püba, mhatho süo thitsüyi kemothe I thazoto.
english: But I have a baptism to be baptized with, and how distressed I am until it is accomplished!

chokri: Neko I kezükeve khasü tole lübame, cejülü? Mo, sizi mori kelükreti khasüto.
english: Do you think that I have come to give peace in the earth? I tell you, no, but rather division.

chokri: Kekrelamori mipüngu cepülü kebako misü mikena metsu tato, mu mikena misü metsu tato.
english: For from now on, there will be five in one house divided, three against two, and two against three.

chokri: u Po pünu metsuto, pünu püpo metsu tato, unupü püna metsu tato, püzu pü nupü metsu tato; thenopü pünu cemi metsu, pünu cemi püzu metsu tato,
english: They will be divided, father against son, and son against father; mother against daughter, and daughter against her mother; mother-in-law against her daughter-in-law, and daughter-in-law against her mother-in-law."

chokri: Pü ukoce posa kesü, neko kemhü nakroco prari kezü ngo lize türü ta keto neko poyito, sübiu türü vori tayo.
english: He said to the multitudes also, "When you see a cloud rising from the west, immediately you say, 'A shower is coming,' and so it happens.

chokri: Mekhroco tükhra voriti neko ngo lize, neko poti, tü thi tato (lütato) sizi bayo, mu sübide tülü tayo.
english: When a south wind blows, you say, 'There will be a scorching heat,' and it happens.

chokri: Ne kethera ketako, neko tü mu cejü züyo kevekesu seyi ve; vele neko tsütülü kevekesu se kekre yi hola?
english: You hypocrites! You know how to interpret the appearance of the earth and the sky, but how is it that you don't interpret this time?

chokri: Ile, neko thozori kemezi seyi hola?
english: Why don't you judge for yourselves what is right?

chokri: No n ngomumi züde shecomi (teshe keti) cevo, neko tsüdo thi kebabi, neko khapü pübelü süva lize, pü neko khapü sheco mi tsü vato, mu shecomi ne tüpü thejü celü süva keto lü mu seyite.
english: For when you are going with your adversary before the magistrate, try diligently on the way to be released from him, lest perhaps he drag you to the judge, and the judge deliver you to the officer, and the officer throw you into prison.

chokri: I nekoce posüto, no sülü tayi ho tato presa kenitho (thela) shutsüsü kemothe.
english: I tell you, you will by no means get out of there, until you have paid the very last penny."

chokri: Galileemiko thüzapü kereshülü kenekerhü keva Pilate-e no thi kesü humi püvo püce posü.
english: Now there were some present at the same time who told him about the Galileans, whose blood Pilate had mixed with their sacrifices.

chokri: Jisu uko fü kelakesü, Galilee misüko thüma kekreko ceri phomaku tilame?
english: Jesus answered them, "Do you think that these Galileans were worse sinners than all the other Galileans, because they suffered such things?

chokri: I neko ce posüto, sizimo, vele neko thelü redeyi kemosa neko sheri süsü bide ka tato.
english: I tell you, no, but, unless you repent, you will all perish in the same way.

chokri: Ukomi keritütha Siloam dahu yo retutsü uko vakhri va, süko Jerusalem lü kehü miko metü ceri phomaku teme?
english: Or those eighteen, on whom the tower in Siloam fell, and killed them; do you think that they were worse offenders than all the men who dwell in Jerusalem?

chokri: Mozo, vele neko sheri nelü redeyi kemosas süko bide kata zoto.
english: I tell you, no, but, unless you repent, you will all perish in the same way."

chokri: Pü jüve hio ri posü, mipü chüdü sü khapü püzhelü shusü; mu pü tavori de püse phu, ngoyi mota.
english: He spoke this parable. "A certain man had a fig tree planted in his vineyard, and he came seeking fruit on it, and found none.

chokri: Pü tavori de tizhe kefü-o ce poti, Hihe, I tütse sü chüdüse phu vori, ngoyi mote; ve vate, dipüla pü khapü cejü thethi khapü bata?
english: He said to the vine dresser, 'Behold, these three years I have come looking for fruit on this fig tree, and found none. Cut it down. Why does it waste the soil?'

chokri: Püfü kelakesü, Niepoo tetha tütselü sheri khapü mezüte, I püphece nyorho khapü vasüto mu mesa süto.
english: He answered, 'Lord, leave it alone this year also, until I dig around it, and fertilize it.

chokri: Mu mhace zhüyi lize vezo, mota tisa mhace no süo veto lize vevate.
english: If it bears fruit, fine; but if not, after that, you can cut it down.'"

chokri: Pü Sabbath nha synagogue lü kemetha thiba.
english: He was teaching in one of the synagogues on the Sabbath day.

chokri: Süce, hihe, thenomipü püche cu ketapü tetse keritütha tha medüyi ho tata.
english: Behold, there was a woman who had a spirit of infirmity eighteen years, and she was bent over, and could in no way straighten herself up.

chokri: Jisu püngo süde pücüpü püce süyi, de püce poti, thenopü nthathilü nphrü süteho.
english: When Jesus saw her, he called her, and said to her, "Woman, you are freed from your infirmity."

chokri: Pübe chopü püyo sü, mu metha thenopü tha medü yita, de Jihova zapozale posüta.
english: He laid his hands on her, and immediately she stood up straight, and glorified God.

chokri: Synagogue lü kezhoo pülü mejode poti, kekrelamori Jisu Sabbath nha püthi kesürho kesüla; zho shurü mhathothiyi keve tüsozho ba, süko dolü mithi kesürho sürive, thüma süko dolü mhathi ti mesü; vele Sabbath nha thi ti mesümo.
english: The ruler of the synagogue, being indignant because Jesus had healed on the Sabbath, said to the multitude, "There are six days in which men ought to work. Therefore come on those days and be healed, and not on the Sabbath day!"

chokri: Niepoo püfü kela kesü, no kethera ketao, neko mipü pütho pünyü züvo jü mekra sü yo mome sabbathnha zori?
english: Therefore the Lord answered him, "You hypocrites! Doesn't each one of you free his ox or his donkey from the stall on the Sabbath, and lead him away to water?

chokri: Thenopü hio Abraham shuye mipü Satan püphapü tate tetse keritütha, püpha phrü süzü mome, Sabbath nha?
english: Ought not this woman, being a daughter of Abraham, whom Satan had bound eighteen long years, be freed from this bondage on the Sabbath day?"

chokri: Pü hiko po kesü sa, pü kemetsuko mengata, thümako metü rechü ta pü mhakezho zapozale thi kesüla.
english: As he said these things, all his adversaries were disappointed, and all the multitude rejoiced for all the glorious things that were done by him.

chokri: Pü poti, Jihova kede dipü zü kemede süta?
english: He said, "What is the Kingdom of God like? To what shall I compare it?

chokri: Süosü Gakre si pü bi, mipü süo khapü tizhelü sosü; cüsüri de ma mezho sü sübo pü thita, tüyo rako sherü vo relüyi yo.
english: It is like a grain of mustard seed, which a man took, and put in his own garden. It grew, and became a large tree, and the birds of the sky lodged in its branches."

chokri: Pü posa kesü, ‘I kedipü pü Jihova kede mede süta?’
english: Again he said, "To what shall I compare the Kingdom of God?

chokri: Süosü, thenomipü tehe sü kechu pü metsü pü merhalü meta, metü cüsüri kemothe.
english: It is like yeast, which a woman took and hid in three measures of flour, until it was all leavened."

chokri: Jerusalem chodelü kevo nadeko mu natsü kolü pü revü kevo chodelü pü mi metha.
english: He went on his way through cities and villages, teaching, and traveling on to Jerusalem.

chokri: Mipü püce poti, Niepoo kelakeyi thüma tsazo tome? Püfü kela kesü,
english: One said to him, "Lord, are they few who are saved?" He said to them,

chokri: Kharü kemerülü talü ketola thehe thite, I nce posüto, thüma kekro sülü lü keto thehe thizoto vele lüyi ho tato.
english: "Strive to enter in by the narrow door, for many, I tell you, will seek to enter in, and will not be able.

chokri: Ce niepo mipü tasü de cekhakhava, mu neko tavo chozuco thayi de cekha kedü, Niepoo, Niepoo, cekha khri süte; mpü neko fü, kela kesü neko dipü ce vori sheri I neko semo.
english: When once the master of the house has risen up, and has shut the door, and you begin to stand outside, and to knock at the door, saying, 'Lord, Lord, open to us!' then he will answer and tell you, 'I don't know you or where you come from.'

chokri: Neko süsa püce poti, hako n mhüjülü mhati mhakra bazo, mu no hako chodekolü hako metha tayole.
english: Then you will begin to say, 'We ate and drink in your presence, and you taught in our streets.'

chokri: Vele pü poti, I neko ce posüto, neko dipü cevori sheri I neko semo, Acelü tata te neko mhakesu kethiko.
english: He will say, 'I tell you, I don't know where you come from. Depart from me, all you workers of iniquity.'

chokri: Sülü uko kra mu uhu thezhepü ba tato, neko sülü abraham, Isaac, Jacob mu saju kepomiko ngoto, Jihova kede thilü, mu neko hu kevalü.
english: There will be weeping and gnashing of teeth, when you see Abraham, Isaac, Jacob, and all the prophets, in the Kingdom of God, and yourselves being thrown outside.

chokri: Nathoco, nakroco, mesoco mu mekhroco ta Jihova kedelü vorito.
english: They will come from the east, west, north, and south, and will sit down in the Kingdom of God.

chokri: Hihe, nu ketako tara vori tato mu kerami thelaco vo ta to.
english: Behold, there are some who are last who will be first, and there are some who are first who will be last."

chokri: Sünhasü Pharisee mipü ta vori, püce poti, no ta chozu votate, tatate, kekrelamori Herod n dokhri vato bate.
english: On that same day, some Pharisees came, saying to him, "Get out of here, and go away, for Herod wants to kill you."

chokri: Pü uko ce poti, neko tavo sace tsüo ce posüte, Hi he, I Therü kesuko hu sü, I sodo mithi kesürhosü mu zhosü nha I vetsü tato.
english: He said to them, "Go and tell that fox, 'Behold, I cast out demons and perform cures today and tomorrow, and the third day I complete my mission.

chokri: Sizo sheri (kehothi sheri) I tha, sodo, mu sanha, süsanhako chode taba zoto; kekrelamori Jerusalem lü saju kepoko kata motila.
english: Nevertheless I must go on my way today and tomorrow and the next day, for it can't be that a prophet perish outside of Jerusalem.'

chokri: O, Jerusalem, Jerusalem, kedokhrio saju kepoko, ketsüpü nce kesüko süko no ketsüpü süko vakhrisü, I n nuneko cüpü kenguyi nyüzo vele, vükrü pü pünuko kengu pü püsikhro vükeyi kethibi; vele no mute.
english: "Jerusalem, Jerusalem, that kills the prophets, and stones those who are sent to her! How often I wanted to gather your children together, like a hen gathers her own brood under her wings, and you refused!

chokri: Hihe, nce po methu tato, I neko ce potü süto, nekopo angoyi hote, neko “Niepoo zalü kevorio thejo kepütho” sizi vori kemothe.
english: Behold, your house is left to you desolate. I tell you, you will not see me, until you say, 'Blessed is he who comes in the name of the Lord!'"

chokri: Süsa mali miko mu kephomamiko püfü renitole vori.
english: Now all the tax collectors and sinners were coming close to him to hear him.

chokri: Pharisee miko mu thezhokeseko panyode poti, Thüma hio kephomako zü mhatiyole.
english: The Pharisees and the scribes murmured, saying, "This man welcomes sinners, and eats with them."

chokri: Pü ukoce jüve hio pü posü.
english: He told them this parable.

chokri: Neko mipü teko krapü keba dolü pü mediva lize hethecü thecü khapü nhalü mezüde tavo pü diketa-o phu kemo sopüba?
english: "Which of you men, if you had one hundred sheep, and lost one of them, wouldn't leave the ninety-nine in the wilderness, and go after the one that was lost, until he found it?

chokri: Pü süo ngokeyice pü pübokayo süyi rechü tavorito.
english: When he has found it, he carries it on his shoulders, rejoicing.

chokri: Pücelü voride pükhretho mu püzanu miko cü kenguyi de ukocepoti, azü nenüyite, kekrelamori a teko di ketao ngoyi ketala.
english: When he comes home, he calls together his friends and his neighbors, saying to them, 'Rejoice with me, for I have found my sheep which was lost!'

chokri: Sübi tüyo sheri kephomamipü thelü rede keyila unüyo, mi hethecü thecü kemathoko thelü rede mori vetiko.
english: I tell you that even so there will be more joy in heaven over one sinner who repents, than over ninety-nine righteous people who need no repentance.

chokri: Mori thenopümipü presa keri bade pü mediva lize pü me metosü de celü chokemesasü presa süo phukemo so püba, ngoi kemothe?
english: Or what woman, if she had ten drachma coins, if she lost one drachma coin, wouldn't light a lamp, sweep the house, and seek diligently until she found it?

chokri: Thenopü süo ngoi ketasa püzümiko mu püzanuko cükenguyi de poti, azü nenüyite kekrelamori I presa medi kevao ngoyi ketala.
english: When she has found it, she calls together her friends and neighbors, saying, 'Rejoice with me, for I have found the drachma which I had lost.'

chokri: Sübi I neko ceposüto, tüyo Jihova choyimiko kephoma mipü thelü rede keyila u nüyo.
english: Even so, I tell you, there is joy in the presence of the angels of God over one sinner repenting."

chokri: Püpoti, thümapü pünu thepumi kena ne,
english: He said, "A certain man had two sons.

chokri: Natsio püpo cepoti, apo, amha azhü mesüko kha atsü vate; mu mhakezapü unitsü va.
english: The younger of them said to his father, 'Father, give me my share of your property.' He divided his livelihood between them.

chokri: Tüsozho hu sa natsio pü mhako khiyi de ta chokemecu rünalü vota; sülü pümhako pü kesu kethilü ketse bata.
english: Not many days after, the younger son gathered all of this together and traveled into a far country. There he wasted his property with riotous living.

chokri: Pü süko ketsetsi kevasa, rena sülü thekre vorita, pü mhamenyü ta.
english: When he had spent all of it, there arose a severe famine in that country, and he began to be in need.

chokri: Pü tavo rüna sülü thümakocevo, mu mipü püpo thevo vi bata, yikolü.
english: He went and joined himself to one of the citizens of that country, and he sent him into his fields to feed pigs.

chokri: Pü merita mu thevo co sheri pü püvalü sünyü bata, mipü ri mhakha pütsü mota.
english: He wanted to fill his belly with the husks that the pigs ate, but no one gave him any.

chokri: Pü mhalü vorita, de pütho pü rethiti, apo choyi kethimi mizhokhripü ketako mi ditsupü ketikekra ukechu thitasü mu sükezü sheri siri I meripü satato züte.
english: But when he came to himself he said, 'How many hired servants of my father's have bread enough to spare, and I'm dying with hunger!

chokri: I tasü de apo ce votato, mu püce posüto, apo I tüyo kehümiko mu n mhüjülüri phomate
english: I will get up and go to my father, and will tell him, "Father, I have sinned against heaven, and in your sight.

chokri: I n nune le pori shümote, apo no mi zhokhri pü keta thekümi pü thi tato.
english: I am no more worthy to be called your son. Make me as one of your hired servants."'

chokri: Pü tasü de ta püpo cevota, vele pü rapeco ketalü püpo püngosü de tavo püvo cho püyi de pü mebosü.
english: "He arose, and came to his father. But while he was still far off, his father saw him, and was moved with compassion, and ran, and fell on his neck, and kissed him.

chokri: Nuo püpo cepoti, Apo I tüyo kehümi mu nmhüjülüri phomate, mu I nnune thiyi shü mote.
english: The son said to him, 'Father, I have sinned against heaven, and in your sight. I am no longer worthy to be called your son.'

chokri: Vele püpo thekükoce poti, pü füne kevetho khipü vorite (Kedefüne) kha püpo bemene yite, bekha ri kha pübelüsüte, pheküri khapüpo püyite.
english: "But the father said to his servants, 'Bring out the best robe, and put it on him. Put a ring on his hand, and shoes on his feet.

chokri: Metho upho kevetho püvo dokhriyite, side uko mhati mhakra unü tolekhe.
english: Bring the fattened calf, kill it, and let us eat, and celebrate;

chokri: Kekrelamori anu hio satari rhüyite, medi vari ngo sala yi ketala. Uko süla unü bata.
english: for this, my son, was dead, and is alive again. He was lost, and is found.' They began to celebrate.

chokri: Hitüce pünu pethio yilü ta, de celü meno vorizüri tsale kethifü mu nasü keba sesü.
english: "Now his elder son was in the field. As he came near to the house, he heard music and dancing.

chokri: Pü thekü mipü cüsü de dipü bibata sheri ketso.
english: He called one of the servants to him, and asked what was going on.

chokri: Pücepokesü, nsazüo tavoritele, mu npo thodo (or Tholi) upho keve dokhripü (Ukopoti) Kekrelamori pü sürho tade la vori keta la.
english: He said to him, 'Your brother has come, and your father has killed the fattened calf, because he has received him back safe and healthy.'

chokri: Pünümota (pülü redata) mu pü ta celü lü muta; süla püpo ta prade püshu (pünüpo) de pürethi (püpo celü lüte side).
english: But he was angry, and would not go in. Therefore his father came out, and begged him.

chokri: Püpo ce pü poti, hihe, I tütse kekro n choyi thita, mori ncho vajo (n nümothi) vamo, no po kesüko mhapü ceri zümo ta mozo; siziri no tekone püri kha atsümo, apo azümiko zü anü ketola.
english: But he answered his father, 'Behold, these many years I have served you, and I never disobeyed a commandment of yours, but you never gave me a goat, that I might celebrate with my friends.

chokri: Vele n nu hio n mha n zhüko khipü vo kethamiko (thethenuko) zü di ketseva, no metho upho keve dokhripü pütsüsü.
english: But when this, your son, came, who has devoured your living with prostitutes, you killed the fattened calf for him.'

chokri: Pü po pücepoti, Anu no kechotheazü tazo, amha azhü kebako metü nzhüzo.
english: "He said to him, 'Son, you are always with me, and all that is mine is yours.

chokri: Neko unüti mesü, rechü ti mesü, nsazüo ditari ngoyite, satari rhüyi ketala.
english: But it was appropriate to celebrate and be glad, for this, your brother, was dead, and is alive again. He was lost, and is found.'"

chokri: Pü püsajiko ce posa kesü, mhakene mipü ba, püce kefümipü riba, pü mhako mediva kezü pü pü mejosü ta.
english: He also said to his disciples, "There was a certain rich man who had a manager. An accusation was made to him that this man was wasting his possessions.

chokri: Pü cüpü püce süyide poti, I nju seba ketahi no dipü bi bata? No mhape kebako phri kengu süte, kekrelamori no hisa ce kepe thisamo ta ketola.
english: He called him, and said to him, 'What is this that I hear about you? Give an accounting of your management, for you can no longer be manager.'

chokri: Süsa cekefü-o pütho pü rethi de poti, I dipü bize veta? A niepo0o apo cekefü thimo tato bate. I cejü mekü yiho (te thomi thiyi ho) mice khrüti keto sü menga.
english: "The manager said within himself, 'What will I do, seeing that my lord is taking away the management position from me? I don't have strength to dig. I am ashamed to beg.

chokri: I dipü thi to sheri lüyi te (rhüyito) apo cekefü thimo ketasa, miko azü uko celüko vo tato.
english: I know what I will do, so that when I am removed from management, they may receive me into their houses.'

chokri: Süla pü niepoo popü kebako cüpü püce süyi, de kerao ce poti, no dipü aniepoo popüba?
english: Calling each one of his lord's debtors to him, he said to the first, 'How much do you owe to my lord?'

chokri: Püce po kesü, tirü bü krapü süsa püpo kesü, no bayite de hepüngu tho mhai te.
english: He said, 'A hundred batos of oil.' He said to him, 'Take your bill, and sit down quickly and write fifty.'

chokri: Süsari kekreo ce poti, no dipü püba? Püfü kela kesü, ata rha krapü, püce po kesü, n popü rha hetütha thoyite.
english: Then said he to another, 'How much do you owe?' He said, 'A hundred cors of wheat.' He said to him, 'Take your bill, and write eighty.'

chokri: Mu niepoo poti, jütho kemo choyio ce kekrelamori pü sübi thi kesü, mhasephi, cejü hilü kehümi kevelü kehü miko ceri mhaseku, utho ushu nuye dolü.
english: "His lord commended the dishonest manager because he had done wisely, for the children of this world are, in their own generation, wiser than the children of the light.

chokri: Neko ce posüto, mezi kemo cene khipü nekhretho thiyi süze no thiyi su ketace uko nzü vo kechothe kehü thethilü sü yito.
english: I tell you, make for yourselves friends by means of unrighteous mammon, so that when you fail, they may receive you into the eternal tents.

chokri: Sopü mhakeni lüri jüthoyi sheri süo mha kekro lü sheri jüthoyi to.
english: He who is faithful in a very little is faithful also in much. He who is dishonest in a very little is also dishonest in much.

chokri: Mhaceno ketho kemo cene lü sheri jütho yi ho lize sopü ketho ceneko pü n melü süta?
english: If therefore you have not been faithful in the unrighteous mammon, who will commit to your trust the true riches?

chokri: Mhace no mizhüyo jütho molize sopü nzhü khapü ntsüta?
english: If you have not been faithful in that which is another's, who will give you that which is your own?

chokri: Cetamipü niepo kena choyithi yi ho; kekrelamori pü mipü ngo metsü mu kekreo khrü tato, Jihova mu cene ni thekümi thiyi su.
english: No servant can serve two masters, for either he will hate the one, and love the other; or else he will hold to one, and despise the other. You aren't able to serve God and mammon."

chokri: Pharisee miko mha meho ketako süko metü sekesüce uko pü melüta, (ngomu ta)
english: The Pharisees, who were lovers of money, also heard all these things, and they scoffed at him.

chokri: Pü süko cepoti, neko thüma mhüjülü netho nethi kematho pü tayo;, vele Jihova ne lüdo seba, kekrelamori thüma mhüjülü ulü meve (lükerekrü) kebami Jihova mhüjülü ngo keretsü zo.
english: He said to them, "You are those who justify yourselves in the sight of men, but God knows your hearts. For that which is exalted among men is an abomination in the sight of God.

chokri: Thezho mu saju kepoko vo John ketso Jihova kede zhopo thisü mu thüma kehopüri süko phutazo.
english: The law and the prophets were until John. From that time the Gospel of the Kingdom of God is preached, and everyone is forcing his way into it.

chokri: Tü mu cejü di keta reyiku, thezho ketho pü di keta ceri.
english: But it is easier for heaven and earth to pass away, than for one tiny stroke of a pen in the law to fall.

chokri: Thüma kehopüri pücemi huva de kekrepü yi lizo süo mha kesu (kekhrü kezü) tazo; mu kehopü sheri theno ta ketao yi ketimi mha kesu (kekhrü kezü) tazo.
english: Everyone who divorces his wife, and marries another, commits adultery. He who marries one who is divorced from a husband commits adultery.

chokri: Kene mipü fü mere zütati bemene mu linen khitayo, side tüsonha ngotho tayo.
english: "Now there was a certain rich man, and he was clothed in purple and fine linen, living in luxury every day.

chokri: Lazarus khrüketimi pü rita; pü cekhrilü babayo, kethiku püyo kekro ba.
english: A certain beggar, named Lazarus, was laid at his gate, full of sores,

chokri: Keneo ketico, retu, pü miz lü, ki ketako sheri ti nyüsa bayo; sülebi mori tehi vori de pü kethi kuko meyo bayo.
english: and desiring to be fed with the crumbs that fell from the rich man's table. Yes, even the dogs came and licked his sores.

chokri: Sizi tari pünha khrü ketio sata, tüyo choyiko pü kekripüvo Abraham khoroyo mezü va; mu keneo sheri sata de pü khrüva.
english: It happened that the beggar died, and that he was carried away by the angels to Abraham's bosom. The rich man also died, and was buried.

chokri: Pü mezalü pümhü khrüsü, pürü methi ketala, Abraham laco (repeco) ba de ngosü khrü ketio tavo pü khoroyo bata.
english: In Hades, he lifted up his eyes, being in torment, and saw Abraham far off, and Lazarus at his bosom.

chokri: Pü semeto de poti, Apo, Abraham aphimüzhe süte, Lazarus ketsüpü ace süte, püpo pübecüne khipü jülü süyi de püvo mepopü a meyi vasü te, kekrelamori I meli hilü athü ba ketala.
english: He cried and said, 'Father Abraham, have mercy on me, and send Lazarus, that he may dip the tip of his finger in water, and cool my tongue! For I am in anguish in this flame.'

chokri: Vele Abraham poti, anu, nsa lü (rekra) süte, no n kerhüce mhakeveko ngoyi, sübi Lazarus sheri pü kerhüce mhakesu ngoi, vele tsühi pülü lu bate, mu no kemezhe bate.
english: "But Abraham said, 'Son, remember that you, in your lifetime, received your good things, and Lazarus, in like manner, bad things. But now here he is comforted and you are in anguish.

chokri: Sülebi mori hani mu no uko dolü thekhü kesüpü bate; süla hice mipüri nceco pheri yi hote, mipüri mipüce pheyi sute.
english: Besides all this, between us and you there is a great gulf fixed, that those who want to pass from here to you are not able, and that none may cross over from there to us.'

chokri: Süsa püpoti. Apo no pü ketsüpü apocelü süte.
english: "He said, 'I ask you therefore, father, that you would send him to my father's house;

chokri: Kekrelamori asazü miko mi pünguba, püpo tavo ukoce posü ketola, molize uko sheri ta thethi hilü vori kemezhe taze sumo.
english: for I have five brothers, that he may testify to them, so they won't also come into this place of torment.'

chokri: Abraham pücepoti, uko Moses mu saju kepoko sheri ba, ukopo süko fü reni yite.
english: "But Abraham said to him, 'They have Moses and the prophets. Let them listen to them.'

chokri: Püpoti, mole apo, Abraham, vele mipü kesalü rhü salavo posü tisa uko thelü redeyito.
english: "He said, 'No, father Abraham, but if one goes to them from the dead, they will repent.'

chokri: Pü pücepoti, mhace uko Moses mu saju kepoko fü reni molize, mipü rhü salavo uko cepo zori reni yiho.
english: "He said to him, 'If they don't listen to Moses and the prophets, neither will they be persuaded if one rises from the dead.'"

chokri: Pü kereshücelü mi metha keba pünha, jüyikeve zhopo thi kebace, thevoko, thezhokeseko mu mehiko ta püce vori.
english: It happened on one of those days, as he was teaching the people in the temple and preaching the Gospel, that the priests and scribes came to him with the elders.

chokri: Püce poti, hako ce posüte, dipü keku khipü no mha hiko thiba sheri?
english: They asked him, "Tell us: by what authority do you do these things? Or who is giving you this authority?"

chokri: Pü uko fü kelasü de poti, I sheri mhapü neko ketso süto mu afü kela sü mozesu.
english: He answered them, "I also will ask you one question. Tell me:

chokri: John mipü jülo kepü tülü kirime mori thüma celü vori la?
english: the baptism of John, was it from heaven, or from men?"

chokri: Mu ukodolü mha kepo bata, uko lüti, mhace uko tülü kiri; sizilize, pü poketo, neko dipüla melü mota? Zitato.
english: They reasoned with themselves, saying, "If we say, 'From heaven,' he will say, 'Why didn't you believe him?'

chokri: Vele mhace uko, thümacelü vori, süsa thüma metü ketsüpü uko zhetato, kekrelamori uko melüti John hi saju kesemi pü ziba tila.
english: But if we say, 'From men,' all the people will stone us, for they are persuaded that John was a prophet."

chokri: Uko püfü kelakesü, dipü bi sheri semo, sita.
english: They answered that they didn't know where it was from.

chokri: Jisu ukoce poti, I sheri kedipü keku pü mha hiko thiyo sheri posü hote.
english: Jesus said to them, "Neither will I tell you by what authority I do these things."

chokri: Süsa pü jüve posüta, mipü khubü yi lüpü meta, süo khapü kefümiko melüsüva, mu uko püse khipüvo pütsü nosü, mu ta rüna kemeculü vo pütü kecho bata.
english: He began to tell the people this parable. "A man planted a vineyard, and rented it out to some farmers, and went into another country for a long time.

chokri: Pütüce pü cetamipü ketsüpü kefüthümako cesü, ukopo püseko püvo putsü sü ketola; vele uko pü vüva de mechü pühu metava.
english: At the proper season, he sent a servant to the farmers to collect his share of the fruit of the vineyard. But the farmers beat him, and sent him away empty.

chokri: Pü ceta kekremipü ketsü salasü, uko pü vüva, mu püthi kezhoshu sü, de methu pü ketsüva.
english: He sent yet another servant, and they also beat him, and treated him shamefully, and sent him away empty.

chokri: Süsasheri vasüo pü ketsü sasü, mu uko pü reza sü de kelhupü chozu süva.
english: He sent yet a third, and they also wounded him, and threw him out.

chokri: Sü tizhe niepoo poti, I dipü thize veta? I anu akhrüo ketsü süto; uko püngo tisa pü resesa sütori se homo.
english: The lord of the vineyard said, 'What shall I do? I will send my beloved son. It may be that seeing him, they will respect him.'

chokri: Vele tizhe kefüko püngosü, de uko dolü mhakepoti, hiohi pü kalü-o tazo, tavorite de uko pü dokhri vakhe sizide uko pü kalü thita khe.
english: "But when the farmers saw him, they reasoned among themselves, saying, 'This is the heir. Come, let's kill him, that the inheritance may be ours.'

chokri: Süla uko pü kegüdü chozu süva, mu pü dokhriva; süla tizhe niepüo-o süko yo dipü thi tata?
english: They threw him out of the vineyard, and killed him. What therefore will the lord of the vineyard do to them?

chokri: Pü tavori de tizhe kefüko meka vato, mu tizhe khapü kekremi tsüvato, uko jü süo sesü de, therüo po süo khavate.
english: He will come and destroy these farmers, and will give the vineyard to others." When they heard it, they said, "May it never be!"

chokri: Pü uko ce thapü meka kesü, thopü kemeta dipü poba, ketsü khakeva-o, süo püvo pü külü tsüthisüte zi mome?
english: But he looked at them, and said, "Then what is this that is written, 'The stone which the builders rejected, The same was made the chief cornerstone?'

chokri: Kehopüri ta ketsüyo tsü timi kesüphro vato, vele ketsü süo tatsü pü kesü tisa pü kesü phrephapha pü megünyo thitato.
english: "Everyone who falls on that stone will be broken to pieces, But it will crush whomever it falls on to dust."

chokri: Thevo kezhoko, mu thezho keseko sütülü uko be khapü püyo sütole süta, vele uko thümako mpeta; kekrelamori uko kelüce pü jüve hio popü uko metsu sütho sizi ketala.
english: The chief priests and the scribes sought to lay hands on Him that very hour, but they feared the people--for they knew He had spoken this parable against them.

chokri: Uko püla bata, mu salimi ketsü sü, uko kemezimi khravü, uko pü jüpokolü pütüyi ketola, side uko pütüpü kekumi belü sorkari kesümi governor tsüsü ketola.
english: They watched him, and sent out spies, who pretended to be righteous, that they might trap him in something he said, so as to deliver him up to the power and authority of the governor.

chokri: Uko pücepoti, kemethao, hako se keba no kemezilü mi methaba, mhacepüri thüma rhota yomo, vele Jihova cho jütho po süyo.
english: They asked him, "Teacher, we know that you say and teach what is right, and aren't partial to anyone, but truly teach the way of God.

chokri: Hako po khazhonu pü Caesar ketsü thezho mema?
english: Is it lawful for us to pay taxes to Caesar, or not?"

chokri: Vele pü uko do kerü seyi de poti, neko dito a kedo ba?
english: But he perceived their craftiness, and said to them, "Why do you test me?

chokri: Presapü khapü ameka süte; sopü marhe mu thopü kemeta tsüyo ba? Uko püce poti, Caesar.
english: Show me a denarius. Whose image and inscription are on it?" They answered, "Caesar's."

chokri: Pü ukoce poti, Caesar zhüko kha Caesar tsüvate, mu Jihova zhüko kha Jihova tsü vate.
english: He said to them, "Then give to Caesar the things that are Caesar's, and to God the things that are God's."

chokri: Uko thümako mhüjülü pü tüyi hota; mu uko pü kelakesü füla uko ngo phita; side uko kemekre thita.
english: They weren't able to trap him in his words before the people. They marveled at his answer, and were silent.

chokri: Süsa Sadducee mipü ta püce vori, uko rhülakesü bamo le ziyo; mu pü ketsoti.
english: Some of the Sadducees came to him, those who deny that there is a resurrection.

chokri: Moses thopü hako ketsü, mhace thümapü sazüjüra dolü sata, pücemi kebapü, pü nune bamo de sata; süsa pü sazüo süo cemi yiyi, thüma mehüpü pü jürao shuye thi ketola.
english: They asked him, "Teacher, Moses wrote to us that if a man's brother dies having a wife, and he is childless, his brother should take the wife, and raise up children for his brother.

chokri: Süko sazüjüra mi thüna ba, mu kerao theno yi, vele pünu di de sata.
english: There were therefore seven brothers. The first took a wife, and died childless.

chokri: Mi kena-ori süpü yi, vele nudi thi sata.
english: The second took her as wife, and he died childless.

chokri: Mi süo sheri süpü yi, sübi de mi thünako metü süpü yi, vele nu di thi satsota.
english: The third took her, and likewise the seven all left no children, and died.

chokri: Thelatho thenopü sheri sata.
english: Afterward the woman also died.

chokri: Süla rhüla kesüce süpü sopü cemi thita? Uko mi thünako metü püyi tila.
english: Therefore in the resurrection whose wife of them will she be? For the seven had her as a wife."

chokri: Jisu püfü kela sü de poti, cejü hilü nuko thenoyi yo, mu nhi siyo.
english: Jesus said to them, "The children of this age marry, and are given in marriage.

chokri: Vele sopü cejü sülü voyi zü sheri rhüla kesü thethilü, kesasalü, theno keyi mori nhi (ceyo) ti bamo.
english: But those who are considered worthy to attain to that age and the resurrection from the dead, neither marry, nor are given in marriage.

chokri: Mori uko sasa hote, uko tüyochoyimiko zü ketüte, mu uko Jihova nuneko thi tato, uko rhüla kesü nuneko thi ketala,
english: For they can't die any more, for they are like the angels, and are children of God, being children of the resurrection.

chokri: Tsüdo, kesami phu merhü kesü, nhatsalü (sünyholü) Moses mengo kesü pü süo cüpü Niepoo, Abraham Rüo, Isaac Rüo, mu Jacob Rüo.
english: But that the dead are raised, even Moses showed at the bush, when he called the Lord 'The God of Abraham, the God of Isaac, and the God of Jacob.'

chokri: Kekrelamori pü kesami Rüo moli, vele rhü kebako Rüo; kekrelamori metüko püla rhütayo.
english: Now he is not the God of the dead, but of the living, for all are alive to him."

chokri: Lesikesemi pü püfü khosü de poti, kemethao, no po meveshu süzo.
english: Some of the scribes answered, "Teacher, you speak well."

chokri: Süsaco uko pü ketso meprüta.
english: They didn't dare to ask him any more questions.

chokri: Pü uko cepoti, kedipü bide Khrista David nu le sizita?
english: He said to them, "Why do they say that the Christ is David's son?

chokri: David sheri chelelü (Ps) lü poti, Niepoo a Niepoo ce poti, no azaco bayite.
english: David himself says in the book of Psalms, 'The Lord said to my Lord, "Sit at my right hand,

chokri: I n ngomuko pü nphezho thiva kemothe
english: Until I make your enemies the footstool of your feet."'

chokri: David zori pücü pü a Niepoo ziba lize, dipü bide pü nu tata?
english: "David therefore calls him Lord, so how is he his son?"

chokri: Süsa püfü reni keba thümako pü metha keyi ce poti.
english: In the hearing of all the people, he said to his disciples,

chokri: Lesikeseko yo neceri yite, uko füne rechu süpü mi mhüjülü keta uko nüyo mu methi thethi kolü mipo uko kelü ti reyo mu kereshücelü theba kerekrü kolü ba nyü, mu thenye kolü thethi kemelekolü tanyü phi.
english: "Beware of the scribes, who like to walk in long robes, and love greetings in the marketplaces, the best seats in the synagogues, and the best places at feasts;

chokri: Uko theno samepüko kedoti tayo, mu mipo ngo ketola kechothi mechoshuyo; süko kemejo ngo mekro kuto.
english: who devour widows' houses, and for a pretense make long prayers: these will receive greater condemnation."

chokri: Pü, mhapesü, kenemiko mhakhapü presa kengu kebalü khasü keba ngosü.
english: He looked up, and saw the rich people who were putting their gifts into the treasury.

chokri: Mha kedi samepü sheri presa kena khakesü ringo, sülü
english: He saw a certain poor widow casting in two small brass coins.

chokri: Püpo kesü, kethopü I nekoce posüto, mhakedi samepü hio kekreko metü ceri kroku khasü te.
english: He said, "Truly I tell you, this poor widow put in more than all of them,

chokri: Kekrelamori hiko mha kekro kebalü pü khasüba, Jihova mhangulü; vele theno hipü pü tsakhulü keba metü püvo khasüte.
english: for all these put in gifts for God from their abundance, but she, out of her poverty, put in all that she had to live on."

chokri: Humi kereshüce ju kepo ba tice, ketsüko dithedü ri vezho mu mhakha kesüko sheri kepoba.
english: As some were talking about the temple and how it was decorated with beautiful stones and gifts, he said,

chokri: Pü po kesü, neko ngo keba hiko metü, pütü pü vorito, ketsü hiko püri kepishu kemo, kelhu keva.
english: "As for these things which you see, the days will come, in which there will not be left here one stone on another that will not be thrown down."

chokri: Uko pü ketso, kemethao dipü kethice mha süko thi vorita? Zasi dipü khasüta, mha süko thi vori ta ketoce?
english: They asked him, "Teacher, so when will these things be? What is the sign that these things are about to happen?"

chokri: Pü ukoce poti, neko menuyite, neko kedo va mo ketola, thüma kekro azalü vorito, uko poti, I Khrista zole (süle); pütü süo meno bate, süla neko süko sameji ta hite.
english: He said, "Watch out that you don't get led astray, for many will come in my name, saying, 'I AM,' and, 'The time is at hand.' Therefore don't follow them.

chokri: Vele theri kethiko mu kebü keti seto, neko kere va hite; süko vori mera zo ketola, vele pütü thela thi holu.
english: When you hear of wars and disturbances, don't be terrified, for these things must happen first, but the end won't come immediately."

chokri: Süsa pü uko ce po kesü, shuye shuye kemetsu to, kedepü kede kekrepü zü kengomuto;
english: Then he said to them, "Nation will rise against nation, and kingdom against kingdom.

chokri: Cece thethi kekretilü sü mezhoshu tato, tüsa therü mu kemekre; kempe kengo zasi kezho thipü tüyo süto.
english: There will be great earthquakes, famines, and plagues in various places. There will be terrors and great signs from heaven.

chokri: Vele süko kethi mhüjü uko be khapü neko yo sü to; neko methito, neko zü kereshücekolü voto, thejücekolü, kedeko mu kezhomiko mhüjülü voto; azalan.
english: But before all these things, they will lay their hands on you and will persecute you, delivering you up to synagogues and prisons, bringing you before kings and governors for my name's sake.

chokri: Neko po zhopopü thisü ketola.
english: It will turn out as a testimony for you.

chokri: Süla n melü kerede yite, neko kedipü püpo to sheri lü medo hite.
english: Settle it therefore in your hearts not to meditate beforehand how to answer,

chokri: Kekrelamori I mhase mu kepota kha netsü süto, ne ngomuko ne meliva keho mori keprü yi keho.
english: for I will give you a mouth and wisdom which all your adversaries will not be able to withstand or to contradict.

chokri: Ne sazüjürako, nekrüko, neshuyeko mu nekhretho miko neko tüsüto; mu neko humi dokhri ri va zoto.
english: You will be handed over even by parents, brothers, relatives, and friends. Some of you they will cause to be put to death.

chokri: Mi metü neko ngo metsü tato, azalano.
english: You will be hated by all men for my name's sake.

chokri: Vele neko pitha pripüri nepiyo shütaho.
english: And not a hair of your head will perish.

chokri: Neko sügü sühe keyilü nkhri n rarü khi bayi to, (nthinpho merhü yito).
english: By your endurance you will win your lives.

chokri: Thipahe tavo Jerusalem whipü keyi neko ngoto, süce süo kemeka meno keba seyi te.
english: "But when you see Jerusalem surrounded by armies, then know that its desolation is at hand.

chokri: Süce Judah lü kebako ta cejütuyo vota te, mu sülü keba kopo tapra tate; mu yilü ketako po sülü vohi tate.
english: Then let those who are in Judea flee to the mountains. Let those who are in the midst of her depart. Let those who are in the country not enter therein.

chokri: Kekrelamori tüsozho süko khüpo kethila mhasüko thopü kemetako thitso sü ketola.
english: For these are days of vengeance, that all things which are written may be fulfilled.

chokri: Vele natsimi kebako, no kebako, sütüce kemezhephi tato thethi sülü kehüko hesheherü lekhu sülü thümako yo vo ketala.
english: Woe to those who are pregnant and to those who nurse infants in those days! For there will be great distress in the land, and wrath to this people.

chokri: Mu uko zecholü sa tato, mu uko tüzü shuye metülü thejü thivo tato, mu Gentilemiko Jerusalem kedüda vato, Jewmi kemoko pütü tsota kemothe.
english: They will fall by the edge of the sword, and will be led captive into all the nations. Jerusalem will be trampled down by the Gentiles, until the times of the Gentiles are fulfilled.

chokri: Naceyo zasiko thisüto, khriyori, mu thenükoyori, mu cejülü zimo tato shuye metülü, krokrü kemo, jükezho mu pü rephüko rü meho tato.
english: There will be signs in the sun, moon, and stars; and on the earth anxiety of nations, in perplexity for the roaring of the sea and the waves;

chokri: Thüma melüko di tato mpe ketala, mha süko cejülü vori ketala, kekrelamori tülü kekuko kena süto.
english: men fainting for fear, and for expectation of the things which are coming on the world: for the powers of the heavens will be shaken.

chokri: Thüma Nu kekupü tüyo zapozale thi vori kezü ngoto.
english: Then they will see the Son of Man coming in a cloud with power and great glory.

chokri: Mhasüko thi vori kezüce neko pi pükhrü sü pete, kekrelamori neko rha keyio meno ba ketala.
english: But when these things begin to happen, look up, and lift up your heads, because your redemption is near."

chokri: Pü jüve khipü ukocepo, chüdübo mu sübo metüko pede.
english: He told them a parable. "See the fig tree, and all the trees.

chokri: Püse zhü kerethi prari ticoneko seyite, tülü menote.
english: When they are already budding, you see it and know by your own selves that the summer is already near.

chokri: Süla mha hiko thi vori ketasa neko seyite Jihova kede kethi meno bate.
english: Even so you also, when you see these things happening, know that the Kingdom of God is near.

chokri: I nekoce potüsüto, tsüdo kehüko di taho mha süko thitso kemothe.
english: Most assuredly I tell you, this generation will not pass away until all things are accomplished.

chokri: Tü mu cejü di tato vele ajüko di ta hozo.
english: Heaven and earth will pass away, but my words will by no means pass away.

chokri: Fü mula bate, kehü zhorele po ne melü thi mesu vamo ketola, mezünyonyü, meyhu mezhe tiko po ne tüpü ba keredeyi mo bata mo ketola: zoma bü pübi cejülü kehü metüko yo vori zo ketola.
english: "So be careful, or your hearts will be loaded down with carousing, drunkenness, and cares of this life, and that day will come on you suddenly.

chokri: Rhorhü pü bate pütü metülü kechothi metü mode.
english: For it will come like a snare on all those who dwell on the surface of all the earth.

chokri: Mhakesu vori ketoko therüyi ketola Thüma Nuo mhüjülü thayi ketola.
english: Therefore be watchful all the time, asking that you may be counted worthy to escape all these things that will happen, and to stand before the Son of Man."

chokri: Tüsonha pü kereshücelü mimethaba, mu tüzülü ta olive cejütuyo vo süzi kechothi batayo.
english: Every day Jesus was teaching in the temple, and every night he would go out and spend the night on the mountain that is called Olivet.

chokri: Thüma metüko tavo kereshücelü püfü reni bayo.
english: All the people came early in the morning to him in the temple to hear him.

chokri: Hi tüce cukevonye khrekhasü kemo tsükhri nye meno bata.
english: Now the feast of unleavened bread, which is called the Passover, drew near.

chokri: Thevokezhoko mu thezho keseko Jisu tüpü dokhri va keto chode phu bata, kekrelamori uko thümako mpe ketala.
english: The chief priests and the scribes sought how they might put him to death, for they feared the people.

chokri: Süsa Satan Iscariot Judas yo lü ta.
english: Satan entered into Judas, who was surnamed Iscariot, who was numbered with the twelve.

chokri: Pü tavo kereshücelü thevokezhoko, mu mhakeseko zü kerethi de kedipü bize pütüpü uko tsüsü to sheri ziti.
english: He went away, and talked with the chief priests and captains about how he might deliver him to them.

chokri: Uko nü shuta de presa khapü pütsü keto kepoi.
english: They were glad, and agreed to give him money.

chokri: Sülü pü sheri pütü kevengo keyi sa pü tüpü uko tsüsü keto, thüma makro kedice, pü medota.
english: He consented, and sought an opportunity to deliver him to them in the absence of the multitude.

chokri: Susa khre khasü kemo tsükhri nye, cukevolü teko pü reshü kethi, tü vorita.
english: The day of unleavened bread came, on which the Passover must be sacrificed.

chokri: Süla Jisu Peter mu John ni ketsüsü, de poti, tavo uko cukevo nye nyeketo kerede süte side uko kezü mhatito.
english: He sent Peter and John, saying, "Go and prepare the Passover for us, that we may eat."

chokri: Uni pü ketsoti, no thethi dipüce hanipo tavo kerede sünyü ba ukola?
english: They said to him, "Where do you want us to prepare?"

chokri: Reni süte, “pü poti, neni nadelü talü tice mipü lekhulü jüpü keta ngoyi to. Neni püzü kesü keyice püta dipülü lüta sheri ce sülü neni püsa meji lüte.
english: He said to them, "Behold, when you have entered into the city, a man carrying a pitcher of water will meet you. Follow him into the house which he enters.

chokri: Ce niepoo ceposüte, kemethao ketsoti, resoce dipü ceba apo methakeyiko zü mhati ketola?
english: Tell the master of the house, 'The Teacher says to you, "Where is the guest room, where I may eat the Passover with my disciples?"'

chokri: Cebü mesolü thethi kemethipü thapü neni ka süto, ketsopü kemetapü, sülü ukola mhakerede yite.”
english: He will show you a large, furnished upper room. Make preparations there."

chokri: Süla uni tavo pü kepobi mhathipü kemeta ngoyi; mu uni cukevonye kesüyi.
english: They went, found things as he had told them, and they prepared the Passover.

chokri: Pütülü pü miz ce bayi mu pu sajiko.
english: When the hour had come, he sat down with the twelve apostles.

chokri: Pü ukoce poti, I kemezheti mhüjü neko zü cukevo nye mhaco tinyü shu tatele.
english: He said to them, "I have earnestly desired to eat this Passover with you before I suffer,

chokri: Kekrelamori I neko cekepo, Jihova kedela thitso sü kemothe I mhati sahote.
english: for I tell you, I will no longer by any means eat of it until it is fulfilled in the Kingdom of God."

chokri: Süsa pü tehe-o püyi de kemeza (kethezü) posü, de poti, hihi püyi te neko dolü kezayite.
english: He received a cup, and when he had given thanks, he said, "Take this, and share it among yourselves,

chokri: Kekrelamori Therhü-o kedethi vori kemothe I khubüzo krasa hote.
english: for I tell you, I will not drink at all again from the fruit of the vine, until the Kingdom of God comes."

chokri: Süsa pü tsükhri pü khiyi de kemeza posü, süsa cephro sü de khapü uko tsüsü. Hibiti thite arekra ta ketola. Athi apho kha neko tsü kesü zoho.
english: He took bread, and when he had given thanks, he broke it, and gave to them, saying, "This is my body which is given for you. Do this in memory of me."

chokri: Tsükhri ti keyi salü tehe sheri püyi de poti tehe la kesü hio neko la aza kha kesü (mekrü) jükho kesa.
english: Likewise, he took the cup after supper, saying, "This cup is the new covenant in my blood, which is poured out for you.

chokri: Vele atü kesüo azü bazo, mu pübe miz yo ba.
english: But behold, the hand of him who betrays me is with me on the table.

chokri: Thüma Nuo ta tato, popü kemeta bi, vele sopü pütüsüto sheri thüma süo hesheherü.
english: The Son of Man indeed goes, as it has been determined, but woe to that man through whom he is betrayed!"

chokri: Süsa uko hunehu ketsode, sopü thüma süo süta.
english: They began to question among themselves, which of them it was who would do this thing.

chokri: Uko dolü sopü kezhotho thito sheri kebuta.
english: There arose also a contention among them, which of them was considered to be greatest.

chokri: Vele pü ukoce poti, Gentile (Jewmi kemoko) kedeko uthümako pi kekhayo, mu süko cüpü keprakemeve miko ziyo.
english: He said to them, "The kings of the Gentiles lord it over them, and those who have authority over them are called 'benefactors.'

chokri: Vele neko siziho, sizimori neko dolü kezhothoo po natsitho bito, krüta-o po mi thekümi thito.
english: But not so with you. But one who is the greater among you, let him become as the younger, and one who is governing, as one who serves.

chokri: Sopü zhoku sheri püpo mi thekümi mori mi reyimi thito, I neko kemethao miz lü neko reyi ba mome?
english: For who is greater, one who sits at the table, or one who serves? Isn't it he who sits at the table? But I am in the midst of you as one who serves.

chokri: Neko azü mhakerülü tha.
english: But you are those who have continued with me in my trials.

chokri: Apo mhapü Amelü kesübi, I sheri mhapü neko melü süzü; kede kethi.
english: I confer on you a kingdom, even as my Father conferred on me,

chokri: Süla neko a miz yo azüde mhati mhakra ketola, a kede kethilü, mu nekopo kedeba keri kena kolü bato side Isreal shuye kerikena kolü uko jünyo (sheco) ketito.
english: that you may eat and drink at my table in my Kingdom. You will sit on thrones, judging the twelve tribes of Israel."

chokri: Simon, Simon, renisüte, Satan neko rezo keto cho bate, tehakerezothito.
english: The Lord said, "Simon, Simon, behold, Satan asked to have you, that he might sift you as wheat,

chokri: Vele I nla chosüte n kemelü thathi ta mo ketola, no sala vori tice tavo nsazüko thi kethepu süte.
english: but I prayed for you, that your faith wouldn't fail. You, when once you have turned again, establish your brothers."

chokri: Pü pücepoti, Niepoo I nzü thejü kethilü mu kesalü sheri vo keto kerede pü tazo.
english: He said to him, "Lord, I am ready to go with you both to prison and to death!"

chokri: Jisu poti, Peter I nceposüto, no vasü asemole ziti amela tato, tha thevü kekhu mhüjü.
english: He said, "I tell you, Peter, the rooster will by no means crow today until you deny that you know me three times."

chokri: Pü uko ce poti, presabü, lekho mu pheku samo de I neko ketsü kesüce neko tsakhu teme? Uko püce poti “mhapüri tsakhu mole,”
english: He said to them, "When I sent you out without purse, and wallet, and shoes, did you lack anything?" They said, "Nothing."

chokri: Pü ukoce poti, vele tsüzo, presabü kebami khiyite, sübi lekho kebami ri; mu zecho kedimi ubola zhupü zecho khriyite.
english: Then he said to them, "But now, whoever has a purse, let him take it, and likewise a wallet. Whoever has none, let him sell his cloak, and buy a sword.

chokri: Kekrelamori I nekoce kepobi, ketho lesilü thopü kemetako thitsosü ketola, Ayono, “püpü thezho kevarhüko saphri süte, mu sübi ala thopü kemetako thitsü sü tazo.”
english: For I tell you that this which is written must still be fulfilled in me: 'He was counted with the lawless.' For that which concerns me has an end."

chokri: Uko poti, pete, hice zecho kena ba. Pü uko fü kela kesü, tsüzi zori vete.
english: They said, "Lord, behold, here are two swords." He said to them, "That is enough."

chokri: Pü tapra pü zhoce kethibi olive cejütuyo vota, mu püsajiko püsa mejivo.
english: He came out, and went, as his custom was, to the Mount of Olives. His disciples also followed him.

chokri: Pü tavo thethi yo tso keyice ukoce poti neko kechothiyite, medo kephilü lüta mo ketola.
english: When he was at the place, he said to them, "Pray that you don't enter into temptation."

chokri: Süsa pü tsüzaphe tsüdü thepülü, chütuyi de kechothi bata.
english: He was withdrawn from them about a stone's throw, and he knelt down and prayed,

chokri: Apo, mhace n medo tolize tehe hio acelü khipü tatate, sizori amedomo vele n medo thi yite,
english: saying, "Father, if you are willing, remove this cup from me. Nevertheless, not my will, but yours, be done."

chokri: Süsa tüyo choyi mipü tavo püthi kethepusü.
english: An angel from heaven appeared to him, strengthening him.

chokri: Pü lükemezhe tilü kechothi thehe thiku sasü; mu thüza rekhrü ti kezho retu cejü kita.
english: Being in agony he prayed more earnestly. His sweat became like great drops of blood falling down on the ground.

chokri: Pü kecholü tasüri tice ta methakeyiko ce pheri mu uko zü renyü ba keta ngoyi, thelü su ba ketala.
english: When he rose up from his prayer, he came to the disciples, and found them sleeping because of grief,

chokri: Pü ukoce poti, neko dito zü bata ta süte de kechothi yite, medo kephilü lüta mo ketola.
english: and said to them, "Why do you sleep? Rise and pray that you may not enter into temptation."

chokri: Pü jüpo kebalü thüma krodepü ta vori, mipü Judas, kerikenako dolü, ketapü thümakro chothazü vori, pütavo Jisu mebosü, Thüma Nuo tüsü ketola.
english: While he was still speaking, behold, a multitude, and he who was called Judas, one of the twelve, was leading them. He came near to Jesus to kiss him.

chokri: Vele Jisu püce poti, Judas no thüma Nuo tüsü ketola pü mebosü tozüme?
english: But Jesus said to him, "Judas, do you betray the Son of Man with a kiss?"

chokri: Püzü kebako mhathi sü keto vori kezü ngo de poti, “niepoo hako zecho pü dosü tome?”
english: When those who were around him saw what was about to happen, they said to him, "Lord, shall we strike with the sword?"

chokri: Uko süsa mipü zecho pü thevo kezhoo cetao uzaco ni zathü va.
english: A certain one of them struck the servant of the high priest, and cut off his right ear.

chokri: Vele Jisu poti, mha hiziti thisa hite, pü ni jüsü mu thi menüsü.
english: But Jesus answered, "Let me at least do this"--and he touched his ear, and healed him.

chokri: Süsa Jisu thevo kezhoko, kereshücelü kefüko, mu mehiko ta püce voritiko ce poti, neko zechoko, sürüko, bekhako pü acevori züme, apü thüma kesumi bi?
english: Jesus said to the chief priests, captains of the temple, and elders, who had come against him, "Have you come out as against a robber, with swords and clubs?

chokri: I tüsonha kereshücelü neko züba, neko süce nebe khapü ayo sümo, vele tsühi neko tüte, kezülü ketako keku.
english: When I was with you in the temple daily, you didn't stretch out your hands against me. But this is your hour, and the power of darkness."

chokri: Süsa uko pütüyi de zü thevo kezhoo celü vota; vele Peter kemecu zalü püsa mejita.
english: They seized him, and led him away, and brought him into the high priest's house. But Peter followed from a distance.

chokri: Uko cehükhro metsulü me thisü, de ba metupüba, Peter ukodolü ba.
english: When they had kindled a fire in the middle of the courtyard, and had sat down together, Peter sat among them.

chokri: Süsa relimipü püpe türü sü, mekevelü, depoti, thüma hiori püzüta.
english: A certain servant girl saw him as he sat in the light, and looking intently at him, said, "This man also was with him."

chokri: Vele pü mole zita, de poti, thenopü Ipüse mole.
english: He denied Jesus, saying, "Woman, I don't know him."

chokri: Süsazace mipü püngo sü de poti, “thümao nori mipü zole, püzü ketako. Vele Peter poti, ‘thümao, I mole.’”
english: After a little while someone else saw him, and said, "You also are one of them!" But Peter answered, "Man, I am not!"

chokri: Süsa bavüdopü salü mipü po sala de thüma hiohi kethozo püzü ketami pü, kekrelamori pü Galilee lü kehümi tsüla.
english: After about one hour passed, another confidently affirmed, saying, "Truly this man also was with him, for he is a Galilean!"

chokri: Vele Peter poti, “thümao, no kedipü poba sheri I semo,” sizi po kebalü thevü füta.
english: But Peter said, "Man, I don't know what you are talking about!" Immediately, while he was still speaking, a rooster crowed.

chokri: Niepoo regüsü Peter pesü; süsa Peter Niepoo jü püsa lüyi, püce kepo, tha thevü kekhu mhüjü no asemole ti vasü po tato.
english: The Lord turned, and looked at Peter. Then Peter remembered the Lord's word, how he said to him, "Before the rooster crows you will deny me three times."

chokri: Mu pü ta chozu prade kra shuta.
english: He went out, and wept bitterly.

chokri: Hitülü Jisu tüpü kebako pü krünyü m u pü vüta.
english: The men who held Jesus mocked him and beat him.

chokri: Uko pü mhü juvade mhapü pü ketso, saju po süte, sopü nvü ba sheri.
english: Having blindfolded him, they struck him on the face and asked him, "Prophesy! Who is the one who struck you?"

chokri: Uko mha kekrolü pü meshu (kenga) ta.
english: They spoke many other things against him, insulting him.

chokri: Tüso vori ketace mehi miko, thevo kezhoko, mu thezhokeseko tavo kehulü kenguyi, mu uko püzü kehulü vo.
english: As soon as it was day, the assembly of the elders of the people was gathered together, both chief priests and scribes, and they led him away into their council, saying,

chokri: No Messiah lize hakoce posüte; pü kela kesü, mhace I neko ce posü zori neko melüyi ho.
english: "If you are the Christ, tell us." But he said to them, "If I tell you, you won't believe,

chokri: I neko ce mhaketso zori neko acepo süho.
english: and if I ask, you will in no way answer me or let me go.

chokri: Vele tsüsahi thüma Nuo Jihova keku zaco batato.
english: From now on, the Son of Man will be seated at the right hand of the power of God."

chokri: Uko metü pü ketso, “no Jihova Nu zome?” Pü po kesü “neko kepo I le.”
english: They all said, "Are you then the Son of God?" He said to them, "You say it, because I AM."

chokri: Süsa uko poti, uko zhopo dipü phusa bata? Uko utho seyite pü mülü kepo.
english: They said, "Why do we need any more witness? For we ourselves have heard from his own mouth!"

chokri: Kehulü thüma makro püzü Pilate-e ce vori.
english: The whole company of them rose up and brought him before Pilate.

chokri: Uko pü mejota, uko poti, thüma hio u rünalü mhakebü tate, mha khapü Caesar khrü thi hino, mu pütho püpü Khrista kedeo zole; si tate.
english: They began to accuse him, saying, "We found this man perverting the nation, forbidding paying taxes to Caesar, and saying that he himself is Christ, a king."

chokri: Mu Pilate-e pü ketsoti, No Jews miko kedeo me? Mu pü pokesü, no süo po süzo.
english: Pilate asked him, "Are you the King of the Jews?" He answered him, "So you say."

chokri: Süsa Pilate-e poti, thevo kezhoko mu thümako ce, I thüma hio yo kejo ngoyimole,
english: Pilate said to the chief priests and the multitudes, "I find no basis for a charge against this man."

chokri: Vele uko khrü kuta de poti, pü thümako hüpü mhakebü, Jew miko kehü metülü Galilee lüvo hice ketso.
english: But they insisted, saying, "He stirs up the people, teaching throughout all Judea, beginning from Galilee even to this place."

chokri: Pilate-e se keyice, pü Galilee lü kehümi me de ketso.
english: But when Pilate heard Galilee mentioned, he asked if the man was a Galilean.

chokri: Mu pü Herod khro keba se keyi la pü ketsüpü Herod ce sü. Sütüce Herod Jerusalem lü kezhothi kebala.
english: When he found out that he was in Herod's jurisdiction, he sent him to Herod, who was also in Jerusalem during those days.

chokri: Herod Jisu ngo kesüce pünü shuta, kekrelamori püju se bayo, mu pü mha ungo zasi thisü totho meri kebala.
english: Now when Herod saw Jesus, he was exceedingly glad, for he had wanted to see him for a long time, because he had heard many things about him. He hoped to see some miracle done by him.

chokri: Pütü kecho pü ketsosü, vele Jisu mhapüri po mota.
english: He questioned him with many words, but he gave no answers.

chokri: Thevokezhoko mu lesikeseko süce tha, uko pü mejo shu bazo.
english: The chief priests and the scribes stood, vehemently accusing him.

chokri: Herod sheri pü paheko zü pü krünyü mu pü zhoshu sü, fü kemere khapü bemenesü side pü ketsü sala Pilate-e ce süva.
english: Herod with his soldiers humiliated him and mocked him. Dressing him in luxurious clothing, they sent him back to Pilate.

chokri: Sünha Herod mu Pilate-e ni kekhretho (kezüveyi) sümhüjü zowe uni kengomu tayo,
english: Herod and Pilate became friends with each other that very day, for before that they were enemies with each other.

chokri: Pilate-e, thevoko, krütako, mu thümako cüsü.
english: Pilate called together the chief priests and the rulers and the people,

chokri: De ukoce poti, neko thüma hio zü acevori pü mhapükerhü tale side, mu I pü ketso neko mhüjülü mu pü kejo mhapüri ngoyimo neko pü mejo kebako.
english: and said to them, "You brought this man to me as one that perverts the people, and see, I have examined him before you, and found no basis for a charge against this man concerning those things of which you accuse him.

chokri: Herod sheri mhapüri ngoyi mo de pü ketsüpü acesü; sübi pü sata keshü mhatho püri di.
english: Neither has Herod, for I sent you to him, and see, nothing worthy of death has been done by him.

chokri: Süla I püvüsü de pü khasü vato,
english: I will therefore chastise him and release him."

chokri: kekrelamori thenyelü mipü khasü keva mesü.
english: Now he had to release one prisoner to them at the feast.

chokri: Uko metü se meto de poti, thüma hio po ta tate, Barabbas khapü hako tsü vate.
english: But they all cried out together, saying, "Away with this man! Release to us Barabbas!" --

chokri: Thüma hio kedekecholü mhakebü de mi dokhri keva mipü, pü tüpü thajü thi meta;
english: one who was thrown into prison for a certain revolt in the city, and for murder.

chokri: Pilate-e pü (Jisu) khasüva nyüde thümako jüpo sala.
english: Then Pilate spoke to them again, wanting to release Jesus,

chokri: Vele uko se meto ba zota; “Pü khapü cross yo gu vate”
english: but they shouted, saying, "Crucify! Crucify him!"

chokri: Vasü lüri pü poti, pü kedipü kesu thiva, I püyo mhakesu püri ngoyi mo, püpo sata keshü, I pü vüva de pü khasü vato.
english: He said to them the third time, "Why? What evil has this man done? I have found no capital crime in him. I will therefore chastise him and release him."

chokri: Vele uko semeto shude püpü gu vate mu uko füphra khiyita.
