english: But they were urgent with loud voices, asking that he might be crucified. Their voices and the voices of the chief priests prevailed.

chokri: Süla Pilate-e püjü khakesü uko kecho thipü tsü süno pova.
english: Pilate decreed that what they asked for should be done.

chokri: Pü uko cho keba thümao khapü uko tsüsü, mi kedokhri mu mhakebü ketao, thejücelü tüpü kemetao, mu Jisu khapü uko menyü kebabi pü uko belü süva.
english: He released him who had been thrown into prison for insurrection and murder, for whom they asked, but he delivered Jesus up to their will.

chokri: Uko pütüpü tavo kezüce Cyren mipü Simon pürünalü tavori züde pütüyi, de püpo cross pü Jisu sameji vo.
english: When they led him away, they grabbed one Simon of Cyrene, coming from the country, and laid on him the cross, to carry it after Jesus.

chokri: Thüma kekro pü samejita, süko dolü thenoko ukhoro kekhu mu pükri krata.
english: A great multitude of the people followed him, including women who also mourned and lamented him.

chokri: Jisu regüpra ukoce poti; Jerusalem püko alakra hite, vele netho nela mu nenuneko la krayite.
english: But Jesus, turning to them, said, "Daughters of Jerusalem, don't weep for me, but weep for yourselves and for your children.

chokri: Kekrelamori tüsozhoko vorizoto, sünha uko poketo, nudipüko, mu nune kemo nobuko, mu nokemo noko thejo kepütho.
english: For behold, the days are coming in which they will say, 'Blessed are the barren, the wombs that never bore, and the breasts that never nursed.'

chokri: Süsa uko cejütuko ce pode, retu hayo tsü hava süte, side hahuyi tate.
english: Then they will begin to tell the mountains, 'Fall on us!' and tell the hills, 'Cover us.'

chokri: Kekrelamori sü rhü kebao yo thori uko mha hizi ti thisü lize, sü, kesao yo dipü bitatoshe?
english: For if they do these things in the green tree, what will be done in the dry?"

chokri: Keregomi kena tüzü vo püzü kesa dokhri va tole tavo.
english: There were also others, two criminals, led with him to be put to death.

chokri: Uko tavo thethi pü “mipifüro” tsoyi, Jisu pü sülü guva;
english: When they came to the place that is called The Skull, they crucified him there with the criminals, one on the right and the other on the left.

chokri: Süsa Jisu poti, Apo, uko kejo vapü uko tsüte, kekrelamori uko thi kethao sede thiba motila. Side uko pü füneko pü hevehesu (chethikhüle) thide kezayi.
english: Jesus said, "Father, forgive them, for they don't know what they are doing." Dividing his garments among them, they cast lots.

chokri: Thümako süce tha de peba, vele krütako pü zhoshu sü de poti, pü, mi kelayiyo, pütho pü kelayite mhace pü Messiah süze. Jihova pü keda keyi mipü lize.
english: The people stood watching. The rulers with them also scoffed at him, saying, "He saved others. Let him save himself, if this is the Christ of God, his chosen one!"

chokri: Thipaheko sheri pükrünyüta, meleku de kephrojü kha pü mekrasü.
english: The soldiers also mocked him, coming to him and offering him vinegar,

chokri: Uko poti, mhace no Jew miko kedeo lize ntho nkeleyite!
english: and saying, "If you are the King of the Jews, save yourself!"

chokri: Püpice tho kesü, “Hiohi, Jews miko kedeo”
english: An inscription was also written over him in letters of Greek, Latin, and Hebrew: "THIS IS THE KING OF THE JEWS."

chokri: Keregomi pü püzü gupü kemetao pü kenga, de poti, “No Messiah mo me? Ntho n kelayite, mu hani sheri kelayite.”
english: One of the criminals who was hanged insulted him, saying, "If you are the Christ, save yourself and us!"

chokri: Vele kekreo pü lekhu de poti, No Therü-o mpe mome? No sheri pü bide n mejo keva mi thi bate?
english: But the other answered, and rebuking him said, "Don't you even fear God, seeing you are under the same condemnation?

chokri: Avu mejo keva pütüzo, kekrelamori avu mhathola zhokhro ngo züzo, vele thüma hio kekriti thimo.
english: And we indeed justly, for we receive the due reward for our deeds, but this man has done nothing wrong."

chokri: Süsa pü poti, Jisu ava rekra süte, no n kedelü kelüce.
english: He said to Jesus, "Lord, remember me when you come into your Kingdom."

chokri: Püfü kelakesü, I nce kethopo süto, tha no azü kereyi balü (tülü) lü tato.
english: Jesus said to him, "Assuredly I tell you, today you will be with me in Paradise."

chokri: Sütüce bavüdo shurüta, kezüpü thethi metülü vota; keba thecü ta kemothe, thevace.
english: It was now about the sixth hour, and darkness came over the whole land until the ninth hour.

chokri: Nace vesü mota, kereshücelü füha cekhro kena thita.
english: The sun was darkened, and the veil of the temple was torn in two.

chokri: Süsa Jisu semeto sü, poti Apo arüpü khapü nbelü süva züho; süo po kesüsa pü süshü ha thüla thita.
english: Jesus, crying with a loud voice, said, "Father, into your hands I commit my spirit!" Having said this, he breathed his last.

chokri: Thüpahemi krapi kezhoo süko ngosü, Jihova chuta, de poti, thümahio mesa (kejodi) kethozo.
english: When the centurion saw what was done, he glorified God, saying, "Certainly this was a righteous man."

chokri: Makro süce kengupü kebako mhathi ketako ngosü, uko khoro kehu de ta ucelü vota.
english: All the multitudes that came together to see this, when they saw the things that were done, returned home beating their breasts.

chokri: Vele püzü kemekro ketako, thenoko risa de püsa meji vori Galilee lü, kemeculü thade mha hiko peba.
english: All his acquaintances, and the women who followed with him from Galilee, stood at a distance, watching these things.

chokri: Hi he, thüma kevepü, jütho mezi, püza Joseph kehulü piphri mipü (sanhendrin).
english: Behold, a man named Joseph, who was a member of the council, a good and righteous man

chokri: Uko medo zü kerhü kemo. Pü Jews mi rüna Arimathea lü vori, pü Jihova kede fütayo.
english: (he had not consented to their counsel and deed), from Arimathaea, a city of the Jews, who was also waiting for the Kingdom of God:

chokri: Thüma hio ta Pilate-e cevo Jisu mo cho.
english: this man went to Pilate, and asked for Jesus' body.

chokri: Side pü süo khipü kiri, süo khapü linen fülü kezhüyi, ketsüro mekü kemeta mhakhre, mipüri kha sülü sü kemo pülü mezüsü.
english: He took it down, and wrapped it in a linen cloth, and laid him in a tomb that was cut in stone, where no one had ever been laid.

chokri: Sünha Sabbath la kerede ti tüsozhota.
english: It was the day of the Preparation, and the Sabbath was drawing near.

chokri: Galilee lü kevori theno miko püsa mejita, pümopü mhakhrelü mezü keva ngo.
english: The women, who had come with him out of Galilee, followed after, and saw the tomb, and how his body was laid.

chokri: Süsa uko tavo mharü kethenyüko mu tirü kesüyi, sala ucevo ketasalü.
english: They returned, and prepared spices and ointments. On the Sabbath they rested according to the commandment.

chokri: Süsa zhosü nha Galilee lü Cana rüna lü nolü pü ba, Jisu nezu sülü ba.
english: The third day, there was a marriage in Cana of Galilee. Jesus' mother was there.

chokri: Jisu mu pü sajiko ri cüpü nolü lü sü.
english: Jesus also was invited, with his disciples, to the marriage.

chokri: Uko hezo di ketace Jisu nezu püce poti, uko hezo ba mo tele.
english: When the wine ran out, Jesus' mother said to him, "They have no wine."

chokri: Jisu püce pode, Thenopü I nzü dipü thita? Atü vori molu.
english: Jesus said to her, "Woman, what does that have to do with you and me? My hour has not yet come."

chokri: Püzu choyi kethikoce poti, Pü dipü posü sheri neko süsü thi süte.
english: His mother said to the servants, "Whatever he says to you, do it."

chokri: Süce ketsü leki shurü ba, Jews miko uthi kemesa keti thezholü, pülü lekhu kena sü lüyi keve (gallon mecü mori sümeri).
english: Now there were six water pots of stone set there after the Jews' manner of purifying, containing two or three metretes apiece.

chokri: Jisu ukoce poti, jüpü lekiko mele chu süte. Uko lekiko jüpü mele chusü.
english: Jesus said to them, "Fill the water pots with water." They filled them up to the brim.

chokri: Pü ukoce poti, chüpü yite, tsühi, side püvo thehü kesü keba kezhoo tsü süte, uko püvo pü tsüsü.
english: He said to them, "Now draw some out, and take it to the ruler of the feast." So they took it.

chokri: Thehü pe keba kezhoo süo kra metha sü, jüo thipü hezo thi kevao, dipüce pü vori sheri pü semo; vele hezo chü keyi thümao seba, thehü kesü keba kezhoo zhosa cüsü.
english: When the ruler of the feast tasted the water now become wine, and didn't know where it came from (but the servants who had drawn the water knew), the ruler of the feast called the bridegroom,

chokri: Püce po kesü, thüma kehopü sheri therace hezo keve kha süyo; süsa uko hezo kra uso thi mezü ketasa, süsalü kesuko khasü tayo; vele no tsü ketso hezo keveko khasü bazo.
english: and said to him, "Everyone serves the good wine first, and when the guests have drunk freely, then that which is worse. You have kept the good wine until now!"

chokri: Ungo mhathoko kethi thera Galilee lü Cana rüna lü thisü, zapozale kethi pü mi mengosü, side pü sajiko pü melüyi.
english: This beginning of his signs Jesus did in Cana of Galilee, and revealed his glory; and his disciples believed in him.

chokri: Hisalü pü ta Capernaun lü kita, püzu, püsazüko mu püsajiko kezüde pütü hu sülü bata.
english: After this, he went down to Capernaum, he, and his mother, his brothers, and his disciples; and they stayed there a few days.

chokri: Jews mi cukevo nye meno ba ketala Jisu ta Jerusalem lü kho.
english: The Passover of the Jews was at hand, and Jesus went up to Jerusalem.

chokri: Kereshücelü metho, tenyü teko, mu therüvü zhu keba ngoyi, mu presa kele kebako sülü bade presa keleba.
english: He found in the temple those who sold oxen, sheep, and doves, and the changers of money sitting.

chokri: Pü kero khipü sürü thiyi de pü Kereshücelü metho, teko tenyü, mu therüvüko huva; süsa presa kele keba presako pü lava; side mizko phü melava.
english: He made a whip of cords, and threw all out of the temple, both the sheep and the oxen; and he poured out the changers' money, and overthrew their tables.

chokri: Pü therüvü zhu kebako cepoti, mha hiko hice khipü tatate, Apo ce pü hetho Heli thiva hite.
english: To those who sold the doves, he said, "Take these things out of here! Don't make my Father's house a marketplace!"

chokri: Püsajiko usalü vorita thopü kemetako, “Nce rele kelü lünipo amezoyita.”
english: His disciples remembered that it was written, "Zeal for your house will eat me up."

chokri: Sülü Jews miko püce poti, No zasi kedipü khapü hako mengo süta, no mha hiko thi kebahi?
english: The Jews therefore answered him, "What sign do you show us, seeing that you do these things?"

chokri: Kereshüce hio meka kevasa I zhosülü phu mesü vato.
english: Jesus answered them, "Destroy this temple, and in three days I will raise it up."

chokri: Jews miko pode, tütse heda shurü kereshüce hiohi thi, no zhosü lü phu mesüva tola?
english: The Jews therefore said, "Forty-six years was this temple in building, and will you raise it up in three days?"

chokri: Vele pü kepono Kereshüce sü püthi püpho poba.
english: But he spoke of the temple of his body.

chokri: Pü rhüla sü ketasa püsajiko usa lüyi pü uko ce po kesüko, uko ketho lesi melüyi, Jisu jü po kesüko.
english: When therefore he was raised from the dead, his disciples remembered that he said this, and they believed the Scripture, and the word which Jesus had said.

chokri: Pü Jerusalem lü kebace cukevo nye lü thüma kekro püza melüyi, pü ungo mhatho thi kesülü.
english: Now when he was in Jerusalem at the Passover, during the feast, many believed in his name, observing his signs which he did.

chokri: Vele Jisu pütho pü khapü uko tsü sümo, kekrelamori pü thümako metü se kebala.
english: But Jesus didn't trust himself to them, because he knew everyone,

chokri: Thüma kehopüri kezhopo phizo mo sheri vezo, kekrelamori püse keba thüma lulu kedipü bashi ti seba tila.
english: and because he didn't need for anyone to testify concerning man; for he himself knew what was in man.

chokri: Hisalü Jews miko thenyepü ba, Jisu ta sülü vo.
english: After these things, there was a feast of the Jews, and Jesus went up to Jerusalem.

chokri: Jerusalem lü Teko kharü ce rüzapü ba, Hebrew jülü süo cüpü Beth-zatha liyo, süo kü püngu ba.
english: Now in Jerusalem by the sheep gate, there is a pool, which is called in Hebrew, "Bethesda," having five porches.

chokri: Hilü thüma sürhomo ketami kekro bayo, mhücemi, pheyhomi, lahemi, uko jü rephü kesüri meri bayo.
english: In these lay a great multitude of those who were sick, blind, lame, or paralyzed, waiting for the moving of the water;

chokri: Kekrelamori tüyo choyimi pütü huce kiri de jü kebu süyo tila, jü melü kesürice kehopüri ta sülü lü kerami kethi kehopü thiba zo sheri sürho tayo tila.
english: for an angel of the Lord went down at certain times into the pool, and stirred up the water. Whoever stepped in first after the stirring of the water was made whole of whatever disease he had.

chokri: Thümapü sülü sürho mode tütse sümeri tütha zü tata.
english: A certain man was there, who had been sick for thirty-eight years.

chokri: Jisu sülü vori de pü tütse kekro sülü züzü keta ngoyi, de püce po kesü, no sürho ta nyü bame?
english: When Jesus saw him lying there, and knew that he had been sick for a long time, he asked him, "Do you want to be made well?"

chokri: Sürho kemo thümao püce poti, Kezhoo, mipüri jü rephü kesürice apü jülü kesü ba mole, vele I talü ketoce kekremi amhüjü lü tata yole.
english: The sick man answered him, "Sir, I have no one to put me into the pool when the water is stirred up, but while I'm coming, another steps down before me."

chokri: Jisu püce pode, Tasüte de nzi khipü tatate.
english: Jesus said to him, "Arise, take up your mat, and walk."

chokri: Sizisü metha thümao sürho yita, püzi khipü tata; tüsozho sünha Sabbath.
english: Immediately, the man was made well, and took up his mat and walked. Now it was the Sabbath on that day.

chokri: Jews miko thüma thi kesürho kesüo ce poti, Tha Sabbath, no nzi khipü tatihi uzhomo.
english: So the Jews said to him who was cured, "It is the Sabbath. It is not lawful for you to carry the mat."

chokri: Pü uko fü kelade po kesü, Thümao athi kesürho kesüo ace poti Nzi khipü tatate zisü.
english: He answered them, "He who made me well, the same said to me, 'Take up your mat, and walk.'"

chokri: Süsa uko pü ketsode, Thüma süo sopüta, npo nzi khipü tata no kesüosü?
english: Then they asked him, "Who is the man who said to you, 'Take up your mat, and walk'?"

chokri: Sopü püthi kesürho sü sheri pü seyi mota, kekrelamori Jisu thüma krolü ta vo ketala.
english: But he who was healed didn't know who it was, for Jesus had withdrawn, a crowd being in the place.

chokri: Kenuco Jisu tavo kereshücelü püngoyi, de püce po kesü, no sürho taile, kephoma thi sahite, sizi kemo sasü hice suku ti vori ta mo ketola.
english: Afterward Jesus found him in the temple, and said to him, "Behold, you are made well. Sin no more, so that nothing worse happens to you."

chokri: Thümao tata, de pü Jisu le ti pü mice posü, püthi kesürho kesüo.
english: The man went away, and told the Jews that it was Jesus who had made him well.

chokri: Süla Jews miko Jisu meshu ta, de pü dokhri va keto rhüta, kekrelamori pü Sabbath zhonha mhathoko thi kesüla.
english: For this cause the Jews persecuted Jesus, and sought to kill him, because he did these things on the Sabbath.

chokri: Vele Jisu po kesü, Apo tsüri mhatho thizo kebabi, I sheri mhatho thi bayo.
english: But Jesus answered them, "My Father is still working, so I am working, too."

chokri: Süla Jews miko pü dokhri va keto rhüku sata, pü Sabbath zho varhü ti lebimo vele Jihova pü pü Po le zi ketala, pü khipü Jihova zü ketü le zi keta.
english: For this cause therefore the Jews sought all the more to kill him, because he not only broke the Sabbath, but also called God his own Father, making himself equal with God.

chokri: Jisu uko fü kela de po kesü, I nekoce potü süto, Nuo pütho zono mhathi keyi di, vele Poo mhatho ngoba ketila pü thi kebabi Nuo sheri sübi thiba.
english: Jesus therefore answered them, "Most assuredly, I tell you, the Son can do nothing of himself, but what he sees the Father doing. For whatever things he does, these the Son also does likewise.

chokri: Kekrelamori Poo Nuo kekhrüla mhatho metüpü pümengo süyo, mu pü mhatho hice zhoku ti sheri pü mengo süto mu neko ngo thi tato.
english: For the Father has affection for the Son, and shows him all things that he himself does. He will show him greater works than these, that you may marvel.

chokri: Kekrelamori Poo kesami phü merhülasü, mu süko thi merhü süyo, sübide Nuo sheri pü medomi phü merhü süto.
english: For as the Father raises the dead and gives them life, even so the Son also gives life to whom he desires.

chokri: Kekrelamori Poo mipüri mejo yomo, vele sheco metü khapü Nuo melü süte.
english: For the Father judges no one, but he has given all judgment to the Son,

chokri: Thüma metü Nuo rhoti mesü, uko Poo rhoyo tibi. Sopüno Nuo rhomo sheri pü ketsü kesü Poo ri rho yomo.
english: that all may honor the Son, even as they honor the Father. He who doesn't honor the Son doesn't honor the Father who sent him.

chokri: I nekoce potü süto, sopüno ajü reniyi de aketsü kesüo melüyi sheri kerhü ngo yito, kemejolü vota moli pü kesao cupü kerhülü vo tayo.
english: "Most assuredly I tell you, he who hears my word, and believes him who sent me, has eternal life, and doesn't come into judgment, but has passed out of death into life.

chokri: I nekoce potü süto, pütü süo vori tazü, tsühi tazo, kesamiko Jihova Nu fü se tato, püfü keseko rhü yito.
english: Most assuredly, I tell you, the hour comes, and now is, when the dead will hear the Son of God's voice; and those who hear will live.

chokri: Kekrelamori Poo kerhü püzüta, süla pü kerhü khapü Nuo tsüsü,
english: For as the Father has life in himself, even so he gave to the Son also to have life in himself.

chokri: Sheco keku khapü pü tsüsü, kekrelamori pü thüma Nuo tila.
english: He also gave him authority to execute judgment, because he is a son of man.

chokri: Mhahikola nengo hite, kesamiko mhakhrelü püfü se keto tüpü vorizü.
english: Don't marvel at this, for the hour comes, in which all that are in the tombs will hear his voice,

chokri: Uko tasürito, mhakeve thi ketami rhülasü kerhülü votato, mu mhakesu kethi miko rhülasü mejo kevalü vo tato.
english: and will come out; those who have done good, to the resurrection of life; and those who have done evil, to the resurrection of judgment.

chokri: I atho ala mhapüri thi keyidi, I sekeyi la sheco thiyo, mu asheco kethozo (mezizo), kekrelamori I atho amedo phu yomo, vele a ketsü kesü Poo medo phuyo.
english: I can of myself do nothing. As I hear, I judge, and my judgment is righteous; because I don't seek my own will, but the will of my Father who sent me.

chokri: Mhace atho azhopo thiyo lize azhopo kethomo.
english: "If I testify about myself, my witness is not valid.

chokri: Mipüba azhopo kethi, pü zhopo ketho zole ti I seba.
english: It is another who testifies about me. I know that the testimony which he testifies about me is true.

chokri: Neko John ketsüsü, mu pü zhopo kethozo.
english: You have sent to John, and he has testified to the truth.

chokri: Vele akehüju thüma celü vorimo, vele I hiko po kesü nekopo kelakeyi ngoyi ketola.
english: But the testimony which I receive is not from man. However, I say these things that you may be saved.

chokri: Püsü to keba kevepü, mu neko dopüla pü kevelü neko nüta nyü süzo.
english: He was the burning and shining lamp, and you were willing to rejoice for a while in his light.

chokri: Vele I John ceri zhoku tipü zhopo ba, kekrelamori Poo mhatho khapü amelü kesüko I thitso sü mozesu, mhatho süko azhopo thiyo, Poo aketsü süzole ti.
english: But the testimony which I have is greater than that of John, for the works which the Father gave me to accomplish, the very works that I do, testify about me, that the Father has sent me.

chokri: Poo sheri pü aketsü kesü zhopo thiyo. Neko mhapüceri püfü semo, mori pühü sheri ngo mozo.
english: The Father himself, who sent me, has testified about me. You have neither heard his voice at any time, nor seen his form.

chokri: Püjüko nekoyo bamo, kekrelamori neko pü ketsü kesüo melü yi kemola.
english: You don't have his word living in you; because you don't believe him whom he sent.

chokri: Lesilü thopü kemetako phri süte, kekrelamori sükolü kechothe kerhü bale melüba, sükolü aju thopü kemeta ngo yito.
english: "You search the Scriptures, because you think that in them you have eternal life; and these are they which testify about me.

chokri: Kerhü ngoyi ketola neko ace vori mute.
english: Yet you will not come to me, that you may have life.

chokri: I thüma celü therho ngoyimo.
english: I don't receive glory from men.

chokri: Vele I neko seba, nekoyo Jihova kekhrü bamo.
english: But I know you, that you don't have God's love in yourselves.

chokri: I a Po zalü vori, neko azüyi mote; mhace kekremipü pütho püzalü vori lize neko pü zü yito.
english: I have come in my Father's name, and you don't receive me. If another comes in his own name, you will receive him.

chokri: Neko kedipü bide melü yita, neko hunehu celü therho phuyo, Jihova celü lebi therho phu yomo?
english: How can you believe, who receive glory from one another, and you don't seek the glory that comes from the only God?

chokri: Poo mhüjülü I neko mejo keto lühite, mipü neko mejo keto püba, süo Moses neko melü ketao.
english: "Don't think that I will accuse you to the Father. There is one who accuses you, even Moses, on whom you have set your hope.

chokri: Neko pü melü ketosa asheri melü tevele, kekrelamori pü aju thopü meta.
english: For if you believed Moses, you would believe me; for he wrote about me.

chokri: Vele neko pütho kesüko melüyomo lize dipü bide neko ajüko melüyi veta?
english: But if you don't believe his writings, how will you believe my words?"

chokri: Hisalü Jisu ta Galilee jü kezholü kita, Tibarias jükezho laco.
english: After these things, Jesus went away to the other side of the sea of Galilee, which is also called the Sea of Tiberias.

chokri: Makro püsa meji vorita, kekrelamori uko mha ungo kethiko ngo kesüla, kethimiko thi kesürho kesüko lari.
english: A great multitude followed him, because they saw his signs which he did on those who were sick.

chokri: Jisu ta cejütuyo khota, sülü pü sajiko zü babata.
english: Jesus went up into the mountain, and he sat there with his disciples.

chokri: Jews miko thenyepü, takepranye meno bata.
english: Now the Passover, the feast of the Jews, was at hand.

chokri: Süce Jisu pümhü khrüpü pe süri, thüma kekro ta püce vori tazü, pü Philip ce pode, dirapü tsükhri khri püvo hikopo ti yita?
english: Jesus therefore lifting up his eyes, and seeing that a great multitude was coming to him, said to Philip, "Where are we to buy bread, that these may eat?"

chokri: Pü medephi kesü zo, kekrelamori pü sheri seba pü dipü thi sü to sheri.
english: This he said to test him, for he himself knew what he would do.

chokri: Philip püfü kelasü, penny (presa) krakena (tekhri shurü zhokra) zori ukola ti kemegu ngo yiho, mipü tho kedopüpü lebi zo sheri.
english: Philip answered him, "Two hundred denarii worth of bread is not sufficient for them, that everyone of them may receive a little."

chokri: Pü saji mipü za Andrew, Simon Peter sazüo, püce poti,
english: One of his disciples, Andrew, Simon Peter's brother, said to him,

chokri: Khresa mipü hice chütsükhri püngu mu fü kenine kena pü tale, vele süko makro hi thedüla dipü bita?
english: "There is a boy here who has five barley loaves and two fish, but what are these among so many?"

chokri: Jisu poti, Thümako po ba yite. Nha süce ba shu. Süla thümako bayi, mi nye püngu.
english: Jesus said, "Have the people sit down." Now there was much grass in that place. So the men sat down, in number about five thousand.

chokri: Jisu tsükhri püyi, pükemeza fü posü, side khapü thüma ba kebako zasü, fü sheri moze kesudü khapü zasü.
english: Jesus took the loaves; and having given thanks, he distributed to the disciples, and the disciples to those who were sitting down; likewise also of the fish as much as they desired.

chokri: Uko ti megu keyisa, pü sajiko cepoti, timesho keyiko pfü kengu yite, usü püri khava hite.
english: When they were filled, he said to his disciples, "Gather up the broken pieces which are left over, that nothing be lost."

chokri: Süla uko pfü kenguyi, uko tisho chütsükhri kengupü merha kerikenalü chuyi,
english: So they gathered them up, and filled twelve baskets with broken pieces from the five barley loaves, which were left over by those who had eaten.

chokri: Süsa thümako kengo, Jisu ungo mhatho thi kesü la poti, Kethozo thüma hiohi saju kese mipü cejülü vori tole po ketao tazo.
english: When therefore the people saw the sign which Jesus did, they said, "This is truly the prophet who comes into the world."

chokri: Uko kelüce pükemu thide pü tüpü püpo kede thita keto rhüta, pü lebi tasü cejütuyo vota.
english: Jesus therefore, perceiving that they were about to come and take him by force, to make him king, withdrew again to the mountain by himself.

chokri: Theva vorita, pü sajiko ta jü kezholü kita.
english: When evening came, his disciples went down to the sea,

chokri: Uko ta rulü lüta, side ta Capernaun kevaco vo tazü. Tüzü vorita, Jisu ukoce vori mo bata.
english: and they entered into the boat, and were going over the sea to Capernaum. It was now dark, and Jesus had not come to them.

chokri: Jü rephü süta, tükhra kerüla.
english: The sea was tossed by a great wind blowing.

chokri: Süla uko thehethide ru thopü miledo sü/da pü tata, uko Jisu jüpi tavori kezü ngoyi, ru meno pheri kezüce uko meprüta.
english: When therefore they had rowed about twenty-five or thirty stadia, they saw Jesus walking on the sea, and drawing near to the boat; and they were afraid.

chokri: Vele pü ukoce pode, I zole meprü hite.
english: But he said to them, "I AM. Don't be afraid."

chokri: Süsa uko püzüpü rulü kesü umedode züyi, siziyi metha uko ta cejü kesolü voyita.
english: They were willing therefore to receive him into the boat. Immediately the boat was at the land where they were going.

chokri: Süsanha thüma jükhü kebako ru kekre di keta, methakeyiko kevori ruo zo kemosa, Jisu ru sülü methakeyiko züde lü mota, vele methakeyiko lebi tata.
english: On the next day, the multitude that stood on the other side of the sea saw that there was no other boat there, except the one in which his disciples had embarked, and that Jesus hadn't entered with his disciples into the boat, but his disciples had gone away alone.

chokri: Sizi kebace ru kekrehu Tiberias laco vori, uko tsükhri keti thethi kemenoce, Niepoo kethezü kha kesü sa.
english: However boats from Tiberias came near to the place where they ate the bread after the Lord had given thanks.

chokri: Thümako Jisu sülü keba ngomo ketala, mori methakeyiko sheri sülü phu medi ketala, uko ru thopü Capernaun lü Jisu phu vorita.
english: When the multitude therefore saw that Jesus wasn't there, nor his disciples, they themselves got into the boats, and came to Capernaum, seeking Jesus.

chokri: Uko tavori jükhü kekre laco pü ngoyi, uko püce poti, Rabbi (kemethao) dipü tüce no hilü vota?
english: When they found him on the other side of the sea, they asked him, "Rabbi, when did you come here?"

chokri: Jisu ukoce poti, I neko ce potü süto, Neko aphu ketahi mha ungo thi kesüko kengola mo, vele kekrelamori neko mhati megu keyi lanozo.
english: Jesus answered them, "Most assuredly I tell you, you seek me, not because you saw signs, but because you ate of the loaves, and were filled.

chokri: Ketikekra suta ketokola mhathi hite, vele ketico kechothe kehülü tayo tikola mhathi yite; süo thüma Nuo khapü neko tsü süto; kekrelamori Therü Poo püyo mohor vü süte.
english: Don't work for the food which perishes, but for the food which remains to eternal life, which the Son of Man will give to you. For God the Father has sealed him."

chokri: Süsa uko püce poti, Jihova mhathoko hako thiyi ketola kedipü thiti mesü?
english: They said therefore to him, "What must we do, that we may work the works of God?"

chokri: Jisu uko jü kelakesü, Mhatho hiohi Jihova mhatho zo, neko pü ketsü kesüo melü keyi.
english: Jesus answered them, "This is the work of God, that you believe in him whom he has sent."

chokri: Süla uko püce poti, süsa no zasi kedipü thisü hako meka süta, hako po ngo de melüyi ketola. No dipü mhatho thi süta?
english: They said therefore to him, "What then do you do for a sign, that we may see, and believe you? What work do you do?

chokri: Hako potsa neko rayho thilü manna keti thopü meta, Pü tüyo tsükhri rüpü ukopo ti.
english: Our fathers ate the manna in the wilderness. As it is written, 'He gave them bread out of heaven to eat.'"

chokri: Jisu ukoce poti, I nekoce potü süto, Moses tüyo tsükhri kha sümo, vele a Po tüyo tsükhri khapü uko tsü ketho.
english: Jesus therefore said to them, "Most assuredly, I tell you, it wasn't Moses who gave you the bread out of heaven, but my Father gives you the true bread out of heaven.

chokri: Kekrelamori Jihova tsükhri tüyo kiri, kehü khapü cejüo tsüsü.
english: For the bread of God is that which comes down out of heaven, and gives life to the world."

chokri: Süsa uko poti, Niepoo tsükhri süo kechothe khapü hako tsü süte.
english: They said therefore to him, "Lord, always give us this bread."

chokri: Jisu ukoce poti, I kehü tsükhri süo zo, ace kevorimi mhapüceri meri taho; mu ace kevorimi mhapü ceri theri ri taho.
english: Jesus said to them, "I am the bread of life. He who comes to me will not be hungry, and he who believes in me will never be thirsty.

chokri: Vele I nekoce kepo, neko ango süte, vele amelü yomo.
english: But I told you that you have seen me, and yet you don't believe.

chokri: Apo khapü atsü kesü metü ace vorito, ace kevorimi I mhapü ceri hu vaho.
english: All those who the Father gives me will come to me. Him who comes to me I will in no way throw out.

chokri: Kekrelamori I tüyo kiri zo, atho amedo kethila kiri mo, vele aketsü kesüo nü thi ketola vori.
english: For I have come down from heaven, not to do my own will, but the will of him who sent me.

chokri: Hiohi a Po aketsü kesüo medozo, pü khapü aketsüko mipüri medi vakemo, vele thüla tünha süko phu mesüyi ketola.
english: This is the will of my Father who sent me, that of all he has given to me I should lose nothing, but should raise him up at the last day.

chokri: Hiohi aketsü kesüo medozo, kehopüri Nuo kengoko pü melü keyi, kechothe kerhü ngoyi ketola, thüla tü nha pü kelayi ketola.
english: This is the will of the one who sent me, that everyone who sees the Son, and believes in him, should have eternal life; and I will raise him up at the last day."

chokri: Jews miko uko dolü kemeli shuta, kekrelamori pü kepo, I tüyo kiri ti tsükhri da yozo si kesüla.
english: The Jews therefore murmured concerning him, because he said, "I am the bread which came down out of heaven."

chokri: Uko poti, hiohi Jisu zo mome, Joseph nu, püpo püzu uko se baze? Dipüla pü tüyo kirile poba?
english: They said, "Isn't this Jesus, the son of Joseph, whose father and mother we know? How then does he say, 'I have come down out of heaven?'"

chokri: Jisu ukoce poti, neko dolü panyo (kebu) hite,
english: Therefore Jesus answered them, "Don't murmur among yourselves.

chokri: Mipüri ace vori yiho a Po tho pü tüsüpü vori zo kemosa, mu thela nha pü phu mesü yito.
english: No one can come to me unless the Father who sent me draws him, and I will raise him up in the last day.

chokri: Saju kepoko hiziti thopü meta, uko metü Therüoju pü metha süto. Thüma kehopüri sekeyimi Poo se yite, mu ace vorito.
english: It is written in the prophets, 'They will all be taught by God.' Therefore everyone who hears from the Father, and has learned, comes to me.

chokri: Mipüri Poo sü ngomo, pücelü kevorio lebi tho zo kemosa, pü Poo sü ngo.
english: Not that anyone has seen the Father, except he who is from God. He has seen the Father.

chokri: I nekoce potü süto, amelü keyimi kerhü ngoyi bate.
english: Most assuredly, I tell you, he who believes in me has eternal life.

chokri: I kerhü tsükhrida süozo.
english: I am the bread of life.

chokri: Nekrü netsüpumi rayho thilü manna ti ketho, side sarite.
english: Your fathers ate the manna in the wilderness, and they died.

chokri: Hiohi tüyo kevori tsükhri da yozo, thüma püti keyimi sata yiho.
english: This is the bread which comes down out of heaven, that anyone may eat of it and not die.

chokri: I kerhü tsükhri da yozo tüyo kevorio, mhace thüma kehopüri püti keyimi pü kechothe rhü tato; tsükhri da süo athi apho hiozo, cejüopo kerhü ngoyi ketola I ta vori.
english: I am the living bread which came down out of heaven. If anyone eats of this bread, he will live forever. Yes, the bread which I will give for the life of the world is my flesh."

chokri: Jews miko uko dolü kemelita, de poti, thüma hio kedipü bide püthi püsü ukopo tiyi vetole po bata?
english: The Jews therefore contended with one another, saying, "How can this man give us his flesh to eat?"

chokri: Süsa Jisu ukoce poti, I potü süto, neko thüma Nuo thi ti mu püzakra kemosa nekoyo kerhü di.
english: Jesus therefore said to them, "Most assuredly I tell you, unless you eat the flesh of the Son of Man and drink his blood, you don't have life in yourselves.

chokri: Kehopüri athi ti mu azakra keyimi kechothe kerhü nete, mu thüla nha I püphu mesü yito.
english: He who eats my flesh and drinks my blood has eternal life, and I will raise him up at the last day.

chokri: Athi sü themu kethozo, mu azari kekra zhüzo.
english: For my flesh is food indeed, and my blood is drink indeed.

chokri: Somi athi ti mu aza kra sheri sümi alü ba yito, mu I pülü hüto.
english: He who eats my flesh and drinks my blood lives in me, and I in him.

chokri: Rhü keta Po a ketsüsü, I a Polü hüyo, süla ati ketimi alü hüto.
english: As the living Father sent me, and I live because of the Father; so he who feeds on me, he will also live because of me.

chokri: Tsükhri da hio tülü kiri, nepotsa neko manna ti de sa keta bimo, somi tsükhri hio tiyi sheri kechothe rhü tato.
english: This is the bread which came down out of heaven-- not as our fathers ate the manna, and died. He who eats this bread will live forever."

chokri: Pü hiko Capernaun lü synagogue lü popü mi metha kesüko.
english: These things he said in the synagogue, as he taught in Capernaum.

chokri: Pü sajiko süko se kesüce thüma kekro poti, mha hio jü kerü shupü te, sopü süko seyi veta?
english: Therefore many of his disciples, when they heard this, said, "This is a hard saying! Who can listen to it?"

chokri: Jisu pü sajiko du kebu ba keta pülü sesüta, pü ukoce poti, mha hikola neko nümo teme?
english: But Jesus knowing in himself that his disciples murmured at this, said to them, "Does this cause you to stumble?

chokri: Mhace neko Nuo ta tüyo kho keta ngo de pü thajü keba thilü kho taze di bi ta?
english: Then what if you would see the Son of Man ascending to where he was before?

chokri: Rüpü tho kerhü khasüyo, uthi upho zono mhapüri thi yiho, I nekoce jüpo kesüko rüpüzo mu kerhü zo.
english: It is the spirit who gives life. The flesh profits nothing. The words that I speak to you are spirit, and are life.

chokri: Vele neko dolü humi melü kemoba. Kekrelamori theralü Jisu somi melümo sheri seba, mu sopüno pü tüsü to sheri seba tila.
english: But there are some of you who don't believe." For Jesus knew from the beginning who they were who didn't believe, and who it was who would betray him.

chokri: Pü poti, I süla neko cepoba, mipüri ace vori yiho, a Po tho khapü atsü sü zo kemosa.
english: He said, "For this cause have I said to you that no one can come to me, unless it is given to him by my Father."

chokri: Süyovilü pü sajiko kekro tata, püzü tasa mota.
english: At this, many of his disciples went back, and walked no more with him.

chokri: Jisu pü saji mi keri kena koce poti, Neko sheri tata nyü bame?
english: Jesus said therefore to the twelve, "You don't also want to go away, do you?"

chokri: Süsa Simon Peter püce poti, Niepoo hako sopüce vokha? No kechothe kerhü jüko ba.
english: Simon Peter answered him, "Lord, to whom would we go? You have the words of eternal life.

chokri: Hako melü tazo, no Khrista kethozo, Jihova rhü keta Nuo zo.
english: We have come to believe and know that you are the Christ, the Son of the living God."

chokri: Jisu ukoce poti, I neko mi keri kena kedayi mome, vele neko mipü therü keshü.
english: Jesus answered them, "Didn't I choose you, the twelve, and one of you is a devil?"

chokri: Pü Iscariot Judas, Simon nu poba. Kekrelamori pü hisa pü tüpü mice sü ketola, püri keri kena kodolü mipü.
english: Now he spoke of Judas, the son of Simon Iscariot, for it was he who would betray him, being one of the twelve.

chokri: Hizi kevasa Jisu ta Galilee lü tavota, Judea lü tasa mota, kekrelamori Jews miko pü dokhri va keto rhü ba ketala.
english: After these things, Jesus was walking in Galilee, for he wouldn't walk in Judea, because the Jews sought to kill him.

chokri: Jews miko kereshüce nye vori meno bata.
english: Now the feast of the Jews, the Feast of Booths, was at hand.

chokri: Pü sazü jürako püce poti, hice takhi Judea lü kho tate, n sajiko pori nmhathoko ngo ketola.
english: His brothers therefore said to him, "Depart from here, and go into Judea, that your disciples also may see your works which you do.

chokri: Kekrelamori mipüri kemekhrülü mhathi yomo, mipo nse nyüsa ba lize. Mhace no mhathi kebako pü cejülü mesesü nyü ba tisa.
english: For no one does anything in secret, and himself seeks to be known openly. If you do these things, reveal yourself to the world."

chokri: Kekrelamori pü sazü jürako ri pü melü kemola.
english: For even his brothers didn't believe in him.

chokri: Jisu ukoce pode, Atü tso vori molu vele neko tü tsosü bayo.
english: Jesus therefore said to them, "My time has not yet come, but your time is always ready.

chokri: Cejüo neko ngometsü taho, vele ango metsüte, kekrelamori I zhopo thipü süo metsu kesüla, süo mhathoko kesu zo tila.
english: The world can't hate you, but it hates me, because I testify about it, that its works are evil.

chokri: Neko ta thenyelü khote, I thenye sülü khohote, kekrelamori atü tsotü vori molu tila.
english: You go up to the feast. I am not yet going up to this feast, because my time is not yet fulfilled."

chokri: Pü jü hiko po kevasa Galilee lü basata.
english: Having said these things to them, he stayed in Galilee.

chokri: Vele püsazü jürako takho ketasa püri thenyelü khota, melemelu zode khomo vele kemekhrü pübi.
english: But when his brothers had gone up to the feast, then he also went up, not publicly, but as it were in secret.

chokri: Süce Jews miko thenyelü püphu de, pü dipüce vota?
english: The Jews therefore sought him at the feast, and said, "Where is he?"

chokri: Thümako pü ketso de mebü bata, kekrelamori humi poti, pü thüma kevele; kekreko poti, Mole, pü thüma shüketse ta tele.
english: There was much murmuring among the multitudes concerning him. Some said, "He is a good man." Others said, "Not so, but he leads the multitude astray."

chokri: Kehothi sheri mipüri Jews miko mpe tila chozu jüpo zomo.
english: Yet no one spoke openly of him for fear of the Jews.

chokri: Thünye metsu hice pü ta kereshücelü vo de mi metha bata.
english: But when it was now the midst of the feast, Jesus went up into the temple and taught.

chokri: Jews miko ungo shuta de poti, thüma hio dipübi mhase phita, pü lesiphri mo zo sheri?
english: The Jews therefore marveled, saying, "How does this man know letters, having never been educated?"

chokri: Jisu ukoce poti, akemetha jüko azhümo vele aketsü kesüo.
english: Jesus therefore answered them, "My teaching is not mine, but his who sent me.

chokri: Thüma kehopüri Jihova medo thiyo süze akepo jüko se yito, Jihova celü vori me, mode atho amedo jüpo ba sheri.
english: If anyone desires to do his will, he will know about the teaching, whether it is from God, or if I am speaking from myself.

chokri: Somi pütho püju poba sheri pü thechu therho phu bayo, vele pü ketsü kesüo la thechu therho phuyo lize süo zhopo kethozo, matho mezi kemo püri püyo tamo.
english: He who speaks from himself seeks his own glory, but he who seeks the glory of him who sent him, the same is true, and no unrighteousness is in him.

chokri: Moses thezho khapü neko tsüsü mome, siri neko mipüri thezho züta mote? Neko dipüla adokhri keta lüba?
english: Didn't Moses give you the law, and yet none of you keeps the law? Why do you seek to kill me?"

chokri: Thümako püfü kela kesü, mipüri ndokhri keto rhü bamozo, therü kesu n yo keba la zotho?
english: The multitude answered, "You have a demon! Who seeks to kill you?"

chokri: Jisu ukoce pode, I mhathopü thisü mu neko ngophite.
english: Jesus answered them, "I did one work, and you all marvel because of it.

chokri: Moses yikerü khapü neko tsü, süo Moses mori neko potsa neko khasü; neko Sabbath tüsozho nha yirüyo.
english: Moses has given you circumcision (not that it is of Moses, but of the fathers), and on the Sabbath you circumcise a boy.

chokri: Mhace mipü Sabbath nha Moses zho varhü mo ketola yirü lizo I Sabbath nha mipü thi kesürho tso kesüla dito amejo ayo neko nü moya?
english: If a boy receives circumcision on the Sabbath, that the law of Moses may not be broken, are you angry with me, because I made a man every bit whole on the Sabbath?

chokri: Umhüpü kengola sheco thi hite, vele sheco kemezilü thite.
english: Don't judge according to appearance, but judge righteous judgment."

chokri: Jerusalem lü kevori thüma humi poti, uko pü dokhri vanyü phu keta thüma hio zo mome?
english: Therefore some of them of Jerusalem said, "Isn't this he whom they seek to kill?

chokri: Hice pü mimhüngoce thepu shude zhopo thi sefü bate, vele mipüri mhapo mote. Kezhomiko hiohi Messiah zole ti seba teme?
english: Behold, he speaks openly, and they say nothing to him. Can it be that the rulers indeed know that this is truly the Christ?

chokri: Kehothisheri uko thüma hio dipüce vori sheri seba; vele Khrista vori ketosü mipüri dipüce vori to sheri seho.
english: However we know where this man comes from, but when the Christ comes, no one will know where he comes from."

chokri: Jisu kereshücelü jüpo meto shude poti, neko I dipüce vori sheri seba mu ase riba, I amedolü vorimo, vele aketsü kesüo lazo ketho, neko süo semo.
english: Jesus therefore cried out in the temple, teaching and saying, "You both know me, and know where I am from. I have not come of myself, but he who sent me is true, whom you don't know.

chokri: I püseba kekrelamori I pücelü vori zo tila, pü aketsü süzo.
english: I know him, because I am from him, and he sent me."

chokri: Sizi kebace uko pü tüyi keto lüta, vele mipüri ube cho püyo sümo, kekrelamori pütü tso molu tila.
english: They sought therefore to take him; but no one laid a hand on him, because his hour had not yet come.

chokri: Thüma kekro pü melüyi, de poti, Messiah kevorice zasi ungo mhathoko kethi thüma hioceri thi mekroku sü tome?
english: But of the multitude, many believed in him. They said, "When the Christ comes, he won't do more signs than those which this man has done, will he?"

chokri: Pharisees miko thümako kepo keba sesü, mu Pharisees miko ri mu thevo kezhoko ri kesa de kezhoko ketsüpü pütü tole sü.
english: The Pharisees heard the multitude murmuring these things concerning him, and the chief priests and the Pharisees sent officers to arrest him.

chokri: Sütüce Jisu poti, pütü kedopü I neko züba tolu, mu süsa I ta aketsü kesüo ce votato.
english: Then Jesus said, "I will be with you a little while longer, then I go to him who sent me.

chokri: Neko aphuto vele ango yiho, I dipüce bata sheri neko sülü vori yiho.
english: You will seek me, and won't find me; and where I am, you can't come."

chokri: Jews miko kepode, pü dipüce vota tole bata, uko püngo mota ketosü? Pü ta Jews kemoko (Gentile) koce vota tole bame, side süko metha ta tome?
english: The Jews therefore said among themselves, "Where will this man go that we won't find him? Will he go to the Dispersion among the Greeks, and teach the Greeks?

chokri: Pü keposü dipüco po bata, neko aphuto vele ango yiho, akebara neko vori yiho?
english: What is this word that he said, 'You will seek me, and won't find me; and where I am, you can't come?'"

chokri: Thenye thelatho nha, Jisu thade po kebace, thüma kehopüri theri ketako ace vori de krayite.
english: Now on the last and greatest day of the feast, Jesus stood and cried out, "If anyone is thirsty, let him come to me and drink!

chokri: Amelü keyimi ketholesi popü kemeta püvalü kerhü jüri rela prarito.
english: He who believes in me, as the Scripture has said, from within him will flow rivers of living water."

chokri: Vele pü kepo hiohi Kemesa Rüpü poba, pü kemelüko ngoyi keto, Kemesa Rüpü hitüce khasü molu, kekrelamori Jisu zapozale thiyi molu tila.
english: But he said this about the Spirit, which those believing in him were to receive. For the Holy Spirit was not yet given, because Jesus wasn't yet glorified.

chokri: Jü hiko se keyice thüma kekro pode, Hihi saju kepomi ta zo kethole.
english: Many of the multitude therefore, when they heard these words, said, "This is truly the prophet."

chokri: Kekreko poti, Hiohi Khrista tazo. Vele humi poti, Khrista Galilee lü voriyi veme?
english: Others said, "This is the Christ." But some said, "What, does the Christ come out of Galilee?

chokri: Ketholesilü kepo Khrista David shuyelü vori tole siba. Mu Bethlehem rünalü, David kevori lü.
english: Hasn't the Scripture said that the Christ comes of the seed of David, and from Bethlehem, the village where David was?"

chokri: Süla thümako dolü kejükre salata pü lano.
english: So there arose a division in the multitude because of him.

chokri: Humi pü tükeyi lüsü vele mipüri ube chopü püyo sümo.
english: Some of them would have arrested him, but no one laid hands on him.

chokri: Kezho miko ta Phariseeko mu thevo miko ce vo, de ukoce poti, neko ditono pütüzü pü vorima?
english: The officers therefore came to the chief priests and Pharisees, and they said to them, "Why didn't you bring him?"

chokri: Kezho miko po kesü, Mipüri pübide jükepo dile.
english: The officers answered, "No man ever spoke like this man!"

chokri: Pharisees miko po kesü, nekori püpo neko kedo valume?
english: The Pharisees therefore answered them, "You aren't also led astray, are you?

chokri: Mikekha yo tiko mu Pharisee miko mipüri pü melü ta motho?
english: Have any of the rulers believed in him, or of the Pharisees?

chokri: Vele thüma hiko thezho se kemoko reshu ve.
english: But this multitude that doesn't know the law is accursed."

chokri: Nicodemus ukoce poti, (theva Jisu ce kevoo), püri uko mipü.
english: Nicodemus (he who came to him by night, being one of them) said to them,

chokri: Uko thezho mipü reni mozo de mejo keva thezho ba motho, pü dipü thita sheri reni ketso ti mhüjü.
english: "Does our law judge a man, unless it first hears from him personally and knows what he does?"

chokri: Uko sü püce posala de, nori Galilee mipü zome? Phu (pe) te ketho lesilü, saju kepomi mipüri Galilee lü vori mozole.
english: They answered him, "Are you also from Galilee? Search, and see that no prophet has arisen out of Galilee."

chokri: Uko metü ta utho ucelü vota.
english: Everyone went to his own house,

chokri: Jisu ta Olive cejütu yo vo ta.
english: but Jesus went to the Mount of Olives.

chokri: Keshu rena pü ta kereshücelü vori, thüma kro ta püce vori; pü bayi de uko metha.
english: Now very early in the morning, he came again into the temple, and all the people came to him. He sat down, and taught them.

chokri: Thezho keseko mu Pharisee miko theno mipü thepu khrü bade pütüpü Jisu cevori, uko süpü khapü metsulü süsü.
english: The scribes and the Pharisees brought a woman taken in adultery. Having set her in the midst,

chokri: Kemethao, theno hipü mha kesu thizo kebalü pü tü yite.
english: they told him, "Teacher, we found this woman in adultery, in the very act.

chokri: Moses thezho khapü hako ketsü ketsüpü sübi ketako vakhri vate zisü; vele no kedipü pota?
english: Now in our law, Moses commanded us to stone such. What then do you say about her?"

chokri: Uko jü hio kepo pü kejo tüyi ketola zo, side pü mejo ketola. Vele Jisu neküki pü becüne khipü cejü (renyoyo) tho de pü uko fü se kemo bi bata.
english: They said this testing him, that they might have something to accuse him of. But Jesus stooped down, and wrote on the ground with his finger.

chokri: Süla uko pü ketso sasa bata, sizi kebace pü tha medü süri de ukoce poti, Neko dolü kephoma kediopo ketsü pü püva mera süte.
english: But when they continued asking him, he looked up and said to them, "He who is without sin among you, let him throw the first stone at her."

chokri: Süsari pü nekü salaki renyoyo lesikho tho bata.
english: Again he stooped down, and with his finger wrote on the ground.

chokri: Uko süo sekesüce utho ulü mejo ketala, mipüpü redüta, pethithoo celü shudü de natsitho ketso taprüta; Jisu lebi keba zota thenopü uko dolü metsulü thazori.
english: They, when they heard it, being convicted by their conscience, went out one by one, beginning from the oldest, even to the last. Jesus was left alone with the woman where she was, in the middle.

chokri: Jisu tha medü süri tice mipüri dita, Jisu mu thenopü ni lebita; Jisu püce poti, thenopü nmejo ketako dipüce vota? Mipüri nmejo moteme?
english: Jesus, standing up, saw her and said, "Woman, where are your accusers? Did no one condemn you?"

chokri: Thenopü poti, Niepoo mipüri mote. Jisu püce pode, I sheri nmejo ho, tavo tate, mu kephoma thi sahite.
english: She said, "No one, Lord." Jesus said, "Neither do I condemn you. Go your way. From now on, sin no more."

chokri: Süsa Jisu ukoce poti, I cejü keveo zo, kehopüri asa kemejimi mhapü ceri kezülü taho, vele kehü kevelü tato.
english: Again, therefore, Jesus spoke to them, saying, "I am the light of the world. He who follows me will not walk in the darkness, but will have the light of life."

chokri: Pharisee miko püce poti, Ntho nzhopo thiyo, mu nzhopo kethomo.
english: The Pharisees therefore said to him, "You testify about yourself. Your testimony is not valid."

chokri: Jisu uko fü kela kesü, Atho azhopo thizo sheri azhopo kethozo; kekrelamori I dirapü vori sheri mu dipüce vota to sheri seba tele; vele neko I dipüce vori mu dirapü vota to ba sheri poyiho.
english: Jesus answered them, "Even if I testify about myself, my testimony is true, for I know where I came from, and where I am going; but you don't know where I came from, or where I am going.

chokri: Neko uthi upho relelü sheco thiyo, I mipüri mejo yomo.
english: You judge according to the flesh. I judge no one.

chokri: I mhace mi sheco thizo sheri asheco sü kethozo, I alebi tamo, vele a Po aketsü kesüo hani ta.
english: Even if I do judge, my judgment is true, for I am not alone, but I am with the Father who sent me.

chokri: Neko thezholü sheri thopü meta, mikena zhopo sü kethozo leno.
english: It's also written in your law that the testimony of two people is valid.

chokri: Atho azhopo kethi mu a Po aketsü kesüo azhopo thi tazo.
english: I am one who testifies about myself, and the Father who sent me testifies about me."

chokri: Süsa uko püce poti, n Po dipüce ba? Jisu pola kesü, neko asemo mu a Po sheri semo; neko ase süze a Po sheri sete vele.
english: They said therefore to him, "Where is your Father?" Jesus answered, "You know neither me, nor my Father. If you knew me, you would know my Father also."

chokri: Jü hiko kepo Jisu uko fü kela kesü, pü kereshücelü mi metha kebace; mipüri ube chopü püyo sümo, kekrelamori pütü tso molu tila.
english: Jesus spoke these words in the treasury, as he taught in the temple. Yet no one arrested him, because his hour had not yet come.

chokri: Süsa Jisu ukoce posala kesü, neko aphuto vele ango yiho, mu neko kephomalü sa tato; I dipüce vota to sheri neko akevolü voriyi kehola.
english: Jesus said therefore again to them, "I am going away, and you will seek me, and you will die in your sins. Where I go, you can't come."

chokri: Süsa Jews miko poti, Pütho pü dokhri va to bame? Kekrelamori pü poti, I dipüce vota to sheri neko tavori yiho.
english: The Jews therefore said, "Will he kill himself, that he says, 'Where I am going, you can't come?'"

chokri: Pü ukoce poti, neko mekhroco kevorimi, I mesoyo kevorimi, neko cejü hiolü thüma, I cejü hiolü thümamo.
english: He said to them, "You are from beneath. I am from above. You are of this world. I am not of this world.

chokri: Süla I nekoce kepo neko nekephomalü sa tato; kekrelamori mhace I nekoce kepo I zo, sizisü zori neko melümo ketala; nekephomalü sa tato.
english: I said therefore to you that you will die in your sins; for unless you believe that I am he, you will die in your sins."

chokri: Süsa uko püce poti, No sopü lezibata? Mu Jisu ukoce poti, therathoce I neko ce kepoo zo.
english: They said therefore to him, "Who are you?" Jesus said to them, "Just what I have been saying to you from the beginning.

chokri: I mhakekro nekola po keto mu mejo keto ba, vele I ketsü kesüo sü kethozo, I mha süko pü cejüo ce po süte, I pücelü se keyiko.
english: I have many things to speak and to judge concerning you. However he who sent me is true; and the things which I heard from him, these I say to the world."

chokri: Uko Poo ju se yimote pü ukoce posü zori.
english: They didn't understand that he spoke to them about the Father.

chokri: Jisu ukoce poti, neko thüma Nuo phumesü kesüce seyito, I süo zoleti, atho ala I mhapüri thita mozo, vele Poo a ketsü kesüo ametha kesüko lebi, I süko pü poyo.
english: Jesus therefore said to them, "When you have lifted up the Son of Man, then you will know that I am he, and I do nothing of myself, but as my Father taught me, I say these things.

chokri: Pü aketsü kesüo azü tazo, a Po akhava mozo, kekrelamori I pünü pümedo lebi thi süta zo tila.
english: He who sent me is with me. The Father hasn't left me alone, for I always do the things that are pleasing to him."

chokri: Pü jü hiko kepola thüma kekro pü melüyi.
english: As he spoke these things, many believed in him.

chokri: Süsa Jisu Jews miko pümelü ketakoce poti, Mhace neko ajü melüyi ta lizo neko asaji miko tazo.
english: Jesus therefore said to those Jews who had believed him, "If you remain in my word, then you are truly my disciples.

chokri: Mu neko kethoo seyito, mu ketho-osü neko phrü vato.
english: You will know the truth, and the truth will make you free."

chokri: Uko püfü kelade, hako Abraham shuyemi mu mhapü nhari mijü thimozo, no dipüla neko phrü vato siti poba?
english: They answered him, "We are Abraham's seed, and have never been in bondage to anyone. How do you say, 'You will be made free?'"

chokri: Jisu ukoce poti, I nekoce Ketho potü süto, Kehopüri kephoma thi ketami kephoma jü thi tazo.
english: Jesus answered them, "Most assuredly I tell you, everyone who commits sin is the bondservant of sin.

chokri: Mijümi kechothe micelü basü yomo, vele Nuo kechothe basü tayo.
english: A bondservant doesn't live in the house forever. A son remains forever.

chokri: Mhace Nuo neko phrüsü ta lize, neko phrü süva kethozo.
english: If therefore the Son makes you free, you will be free indeed.

chokri: I seba, neko Abraham shuye zo, vele neko adokhri tole rhü bayo, kekrelamori ajüko nekolü bayi mo ketala.
english: I know that you are Abraham's seed, yet you seek to kill me, because my word finds no place in you.

chokri: Apo I ngo kesüko pü nekoce hala süyo, neko a Po celü se keyiko thiti mesü.
english: I say the things which I have seen with my Father; and you also do the things which you have seen with your father."

chokri: Uko püfü kelade püce pokesü, Abraham hako Potsa. Jisu ukoce pokesü, Mhace neko Abraham nuneko lize Abraham mhathoko thiti mesü.
english: They answered him, "Our father is Abraham." Jesus said to them, "If you were Abraham's children, you would do the works of Abraham.

chokri: Vele neko adokhri vanyü bate, thümapü nekoce ketho I a Po celü sekeyiko püposü; Abraham sizimo.
english: But now you seek to kill me, a man who has told you the truth, which I heard from God. Abraham didn't do this.

chokri: Neko neponeko mhatho thiyo. Uko püce po sala kesü, Hako kephoma mhatho kesulü menu yimo; ha Po püba mu süo Jihova zo.
english: You do the works of your father." They said to him, "We were not born of sexual immorality. We have one Father, God."

chokri: Jisu ukoce poti, Mhace neko Jihova neko Po lize, neko akhrüto; kekrelamori I Jihova celü tavo rizo; atho amedolü tavo rimo, vele pü aketsü süzo.
english: Therefore Jesus said to them, "If God were your Father, you would love me, for I came out and have come from God. For I haven't come of myself, but he sent me.

chokri: Ditono neko afü seyi mota? Neko sheri akepo seyi mozo.
english: Why don't you understand my speech? Because you can't hear my word.

chokri: Neko po sü therü kesuo zo, nekopo kemengu (kecülu) ko thi tayo. Pü shüculü mi kedokhrimi, ketho-olü bayi yomo, kekrelamori ketho püyo kedila. Pü kemeda po süyo mu pütho püyo kevela poyo, pü kemedami, mu pü süo po-ozo.
english: You are of your Father, the devil, and you want to do the desires of your father. He was a murderer from the beginning, and doesn't stand in the truth, because there is no truth in him. When he speaks a lie, he speaks on his own; for he is a liar, and the father of it.

chokri: Kekrelamori I ketho kepola neko melü mote.
english: But because I tell the truth, you don't believe me.

chokri: Neko sopü apü kephomami le ziti poyi ve? Mhace I ketho po lize neko dipüla amelü mota?
english: Which of you convicts me of sin? If I tell the truth, why do you not believe me?

chokri: Somi Jihova füse sheri Jihova jüzüyo, neko süko se yomo kekrelamori neko Jihova Ma motila.
english: He who is of God hears the words of God. For this cause you don't hear, because you are not of God."

chokri: Süsa Jews miko püce poti, Hako pomeve shu süzo, no Samaritan mipü therü kesu n yo ba?
english: Then the Jews answered him, "Don't we say well that you are a Samaritan, and have a demon?"

chokri: Jisu po kesü, therü kesu ayo bamo, vele I apo rhoyo, neko arho yomo.
english: Jesus answered, "I don't have a demon, but I honor my Father, and you dishonor me.

chokri: I atho atherho phu yomo, mipü süo phuyo mu mi mejoyo.
english: But I don't seek my own glory. There is one who seeks and judges.

chokri: I nekoce potü süto, mhace humi ajüko khipü bayi lize pü mhapü ceri sa taho.
english: Most assuredly, I tell you, if a person keeps my word, he will never see death."

chokri: Jews miko püce poti, hako se bate, therü kesu nzüta. Abraham sate, mu saju kepoko ri sate; mu no poti, mhace neko akepoko züyi lize thüma süo mhapü ceri sa taho.
english: Then the Jews said to him, "Now we know that you have a demon. Abraham died, and the prophets; and you say, 'If a man keeps my word, he will never taste of death.'

chokri: No hako potsa Abraham ce zho kume? Pü sate, saju kepoko sheri sate, no sopüle po bata?
english: Are you greater than our father, Abraham, who died? The prophets died. Who do you make yourself out to be?"

chokri: Jisu ukoce polakesü, Mhace I atho azhopo thita lize süo kethomo, a Po azhopo thiyo de arhoyo, neko pükhapü neko Rüo le kebao.
english: Jesus answered, "If I glorify myself, my glory is nothing. It is my Father who glorifies me, of whom you say that he is our God.

chokri: Sizosheri neko pü semo I püseba, mhace akepo I püsemo zita lize neko biti I kemedami thi tato, vele I pü seba mu pükepoko thitazo.
english: You have not known him, but I know him. If I said, 'I don't know him,' I would be like you, a liar. But I know him, and keep his word.

chokri: Neko potsa Abraham atüsozhoko ngode pü rechü tazo, pü süo ngotila I anü we.
english: Your father Abraham rejoiced to see my day. He saw it, and was glad."

chokri: Süsa Jews miko püce poti, No tütse hepüngu ri molu zori no Abraham ngola?
english: The Jews therefore said to him, "You are not yet fifty years old, and have you seen Abraham?"

chokri: Jisu ukoce poti, I nekoce potü süto, Abraham mhüjü I tazo.
english: Jesus said to them, "Most assuredly, I tell you, before Abraham came into existence, I AM."

chokri: Süsa uko ketsü khipü püva toli süta, vele Jisu resüta, mu kereshücelü pü taprata; uko dolü tapra de pü tata.
english: Therefore they took up stones to throw at him, but Jesus was hidden, and went out of the temple, having gone through the midst of them, and so passed by.

chokri: Jisu tapra kezüce mipü umhü ce de menu keyi mipü zü kesüyi.
english: As he passed by, he saw a man blind from birth.

chokri: Pü sajiko püce ketso de poti, Kemethao, thüma hio lame mode upo uzu kephoma la thüma hio mhüce de menu yita?
english: His disciples asked him, "Rabbi, who sinned, this man or his parents, that he was born blind?"

chokri: Jisu ukoce poti, thüma hio kephoma lari mo, mori ukrüne la rimo, vele püyo Jihova mhatho thipü mi mese mengo sü ketolazo.
english: Jesus answered, "Neither did this man sin, nor his parents; but, that the works of God might be revealed in him.

chokri: A ketsü kesüo mhathoko I thisü moze su, tüso kebace; tüzü vori talize mipüri mhatho thimo tato.
english: I must work the works of him who sent me, while it is day. The night is coming, when no one can work.

chokri: I cejülü keba the, I cejü keveo zo.
english: While I am in the world, I am the light of the world."

chokri: Pü jü süko posü de mhütsapü cejü sü renyo pü nyoba thiyi, renyo pü mhüceo mhü kenhüsü.
english: When he had said this, he spat on the ground, made mud with the saliva, anointed the blind man's eyes with the mud,

chokri: Püce po kesü, tavo de Siloam zalü jüpü khrü vate, (Siloam, püco ketsü kesü). Pü ta vota, de jüpü khrüsü siri mhango vori ta.
english: and said to him, "Go, wash in the pool of Siloam" (which means "Sent"). So he went away, washed, and came back seeing.

chokri: Pü zanu miko mu sümhüjü pü kengo miko pümhü kece ngo; side uko kepoti, thüma hio umhü ce de mhakhrü ti kebao zomome?
english: The neighbors therefore, and those who saw that he was blind before, said, "Isn't this he who sat and begged?"

chokri: Humi poti, püzole, kekre miko poti, pübizo tipüle, sizi bata. Vele pü po kesü, I püzole.
english: Others were saying, "It is he." Still others were saying, "He looks like him." He said, "I am he."

chokri: Süla uko püce poti, dipü bide nmhü mhango yita?
english: They therefore were asking him, "How were your eyes opened?"

chokri: Pü ukofü kela kesü, Thümapü Jisu lezi tipü, nyoba khapü amhü kenhüsü, side ace poti, tavo Siloam zalü tavo khrü vate zisü. Side I tavo khrüva de mhango yi tele.
english: He answered, "A man called Jesus made mud, anointed my eyes, and said to me, 'Go to the pool of Siloam, and wash.' So I went away and washed, and I received sight."

chokri: Süsa uko püce poti, pü dipüce vota? Mu pü po kesü, I se mole.
english: Then they asked him, "Where is he?" He said, "I don't know."

chokri: Uko umhü keceo tüzü Pharisee miko cevo.
english: They brought him who had been blind to the Pharisees.

chokri: Sünhasü Sabbath zho, Jisu renyopü nyoba thiyi de püpo mhango keyi nhasü.
english: It was a Sabbath when Jesus made the mud and opened his eyes.

chokri: Süsa Pharisee miko pü ketso de dipübi pü mhango ta sheri. Pü uko ce poti, Pü nyoba khipü amhü kenhüsü, I khrüva side mhango praritele.
english: Again therefore the Pharisees also asked him how he received his sight. He said to them, "He put mud on my eyes, I washed, and I see."

chokri: Süla humi poti, Thüma hio Therümi mhatho mo, kekrelamori pü Sabbath tüsozho rhomo. Humi poti, kephomi dipübi de mhatho ungo thiyi veta? Thüma dolü kelakre de kebü ta.
english: Some therefore of the Pharisees said, "This man is not from God, because he doesn't keep the Sabbath." Others said, "How can a man who is a sinner do such signs?" There was division among them.

chokri: Uko mhüceo ketso sala de poti, no dipüle pü lüba, nmhü thipü mhango kesüo? Pü po kesü, pü saju kepo mipü zo.
english: Therefore they asked the blind man again, "What do you say about him, because he opened your eyes?" He said, "He is a prophet."

chokri: Vele Jews miko pü kepo melü muta, pümhü ce de mhangoyi, süla uko püpo püzuni cüsü.
english: The Jews therefore did not believe concerning him, that he had been blind, and had received his sight, until they called the parents of him who had received his sight,

chokri: Uko uni ketso de hiohi neni nu zome, neko poti pümhüce zode menuyi le kebao, dipü bide pü tsühi mhango pra rita?
english: and asked them, "Is this your son, who you say was born blind? How then does he now see?"

chokri: Püpo püzuni ukofü kela de po kesü, Hani se keba hiohi hani nuzo, umhüce de menuyi.
english: His parents answered them, "We know that this is our son, and that he was born blind;

chokri: Vele dipü colü pü mhangoyi teshi, hani semole, mori sopü pümhü khri sü sheri, hani süri semo; püsü pethite, pü ketsote: pütho püju posüto.
english: but how he now sees, we don't know; or who opened his eyes, we don't know. He is of age. Ask him. He will speak for himself."

chokri: Jü hiko pükrüni posü, kekrelamori uni Jews miko mpe ketala; kekrelamori Jews miko metü sopüno püpü Khrista leno poto sheri synagogue lü pühu va keto uko medo keta seba ketala.
english: His parents said these things because they feared the Jews; for the Jews had already agreed that if any man would confess him as Christ, he would be put out of the synagogue.

chokri: Süla pükrüni poti, pü pethite, pü ketsote sita.
english: Therefore his parents said, "He is of age. Ask him."

chokri: Süla uko umhü cede mhango ketao cüsala sü, de püce poti, Jihova mezale püchu süte, hako püse keba thüma hio kephoma mizo.
english: So they called the man who was blind a second time, and said to him, "Give glory to God. We know that this man is a sinner."

chokri: Pü ukofü kela kesü, Pü kephomami me mosheri I semo, mhapü I seba süosü I thajü amhüceri tsühi I mhangote.
english: He therefore answered, "I don't know if he is a sinner. One thing I do know: that though I was blind, now I see."

chokri: Uko pü ketso sala de poti, pü n yo kedipü thisüla? Dipü bide nmhüthipü mhango süta?
english: They said to him again, "What did he do to you? How did he open your eyes?"

chokri: Pü ukofü kela kesü, I neko ce po süve, neko reni mute; dithito süo ketso sala ba? Nekori pü sajimi thita nyü bame?
english: He answered them, "I told you already, and you didn't listen. Why do you want to hear it again? You don't also want to become his disciples, do you?"

chokri: Süsa uko pü kerhe de poti, No pü sajimite, vele hako Moses sajimiko.
english: They insulted him and said, "You are his disciple, but we are disciples of Moses.

chokri: Hako se keba Jihova Moses ce jüpo, thüma hiola zono hako semo pü dipüce vori sheri.
english: We know that God has spoken to Moses. But as for this man, we don't know where he comes from."

chokri: Thümao ukofü kelade ukoce po kesü, pü hicehi mha ungo thi kesü neko pü dipüce vori sheri semo zori, pü amhü thipü mhango süte.
english: The man answered them, "How amazing! You don't know where he comes from, yet he opened my eyes.

chokri: Tsühi uko se keyi Jihova kephomami mipüri fü reni mo zolu, vele mhace humi Jihova reshü mu pü medo thiyo lize pü sümi fü reniyo.
english: We know that God doesn't listen to sinners, but if anyone is a worshipper of God, and does his will, he listens to him.

chokri: Cejü thiyi culü vo tha ketso mipüri umhü cede menu keyimi thi meve kesü semo zolu.
english: Since the world began it has never been heard of that anyone opened the eyes of someone born blind.

chokri: Mhace thüma hio Jihova celü vorimo lize pü mhapüri thi yiho.
english: If this man were not from God, he could do nothing."

chokri: Uko püfü kela kesü, no kephomalü menuyi side hako metha tola? Uko pü hupü chozu süva.
english: They answered him, "You were altogether born in sins, and do you teach us?" They threw him out.

chokri: Jisu pühu chozu sü keva seyi, tavo püngoyi de püce poti, “No Jihova Nu melü teme?”
english: Jesus heard that they had thrown him out, and finding him, he said, "Do you believe in the Son of God?"

chokri: Püfü kelakesü, “Niepoo püsopü sheri I pü melüyi ketola?”
english: He answered, "Who is he, Lord, that I may believe in him?"

chokri: Jisu püce po kesü, “No püngo bate, mu püzü kerethi kethao zo.”
english: Jesus said to him, "You have both seen him, and it is he who speaks with you."

chokri: Püpo kesü, “Niepoo I melüte.” Mu tatsü pü reshüta.
english: He said, "Lord, I believe!" and he worshiped him.

chokri: Jisu poti, I cejülü sheco la vori, mhakengo miko po ngomo ta ketola, mhango kemo mikopo mhangoyi ketola.
english: Jesus said, "I came into this world for judgment, that those who don't see may see; and that those who see may become blind."

chokri: Pharisee miko jü süko se kesüce püce poti, hakori mhücemi thite me?
english: Those of the Pharisees who were with him heard these things, and said to him, "Are we also blind?"

chokri: Jisu ukoce pokesü, neko mhüce lize kephoma baho, vele neko tsühi poti, “hako mhango ba,” süla ne kephoma bate.
english: Jesus said to them, "If you were blind, you would have no sin; but now you say, 'We see.' Therefore your sin remains.

chokri: I nekoce potü süto, cekhalü talü teko krolü lü kemomi, vele sazho mele kelümi, süo keregomi mu mizhü süketimi zo.
english: "Most assuredly, I tell you, one who doesn't enter by the door into the sheep fold, but climbs up some other way, the same is a thief and a robber.

chokri: Vele cekhalü kelü süo teko kefü-o zo.
english: But one who enters in by the door is the shepherd of the sheep.

chokri: Cekha kefüo cekha khripü pütsü süyo, teko kro püfü seyo; pü teko koza cü süyo; mu teko chotha zü ta voyo.
english: The gatekeeper opens the gate for him, and the sheep listen to his voice. He calls his own sheep by name, and leads them out.

chokri: Pü koko züpra rize pü uko mhüjülü tara voyo, mu teko kro püsa meji tavoyo, kekrelamori uko püfü seyo tila.
english: Whenever he brings out his own sheep, he goes before them, and the sheep follow him, for they know his voice.

chokri: Uko sekemomi sa meji yomo, vele pücelü tatayo, uko sekemo miko fü semo ketala.
english: They will by no means follow a stranger, but will flee from him; for they don't know the voice of strangers."

chokri: Jüve hio Jisu ukoce posü, vele mhasüko pü kedipüla poba sheri uko semo.
english: Jesus spoke this parable to them, but they didn't understand what he was telling them.

chokri: Süla Jisu püsaji koce poti, I nekoce potü süto, I teko kro kharü yozo.
english: Jesus therefore said to them again, "Most assuredly, I tell you, I am the sheep's door.

chokri: Amhüjü kevoriko metü keregomi mu süketi miko zo, vele teko kro uko fü reni mote.
english: All who came before me are thieves and robbers, but the sheep didn't listen to them.

chokri: I kharüo zo, kehopüri ayo kelümi, pü kelayi to; pü tapra talüde püco ngo yito.
english: I am the door. If anyone enters in by me, he will be saved, and will go in and go out, and will find pasture.

chokri: Keregomi rego toli voriyo, mi dokhri tolino, mi meka sü tolino, vele I mipo kehü ngoyi ketola, mu süo ngo shuyi ketola.
english: The thief only comes to steal, kill, and destroy. I came that they may have life, and may have it abundantly.

chokri: I teko kefü keveo zo, teko kefü keveo püpho khapü teko kroko la kha süyo.
english: I am the good shepherd. The good shepherd lays down his life for the sheep.

chokri: Vele püzho khrizü ketami, teko kroko püzhü kemola sace vori kezü ngo süde pü tata yo, sace vori de teko kro tüva mu hupho vayo.
english: He who is a hired hand, and not a shepherd, who doesn't own the sheep, sees the wolf coming, leaves the sheep, and flees. The wolf snatches the sheep, and scatters them.

chokri: Püzho khrizü ketao mipo püzho khripü ketala, teko kro khrü phi menu yomo.
english: The hired hand flees because he is a hired hand, and doesn't care for the sheep.

chokri: I teko kefü keveo zo, akokro seba mu azhüko aseba.
english: I am the good shepherd. I know my own, and I'm known by my own;

chokri: Apo aseba, sübi I sheri a Po seba, apho khapü teko kro tsü süto.
english: even as the Father knows me, and I know the Father. I lay down my life for the sheep.

chokri: Ako kekre kropü ba, süko teko kro hilü bamo, süko sheri züpü kro hilü vori moze su, süko afü seyito; side hiko zü kropü thi tato, teko kefü ri püzo tato.
english: I have other sheep, which are not of this fold. I must bring them also, and they will hear my voice. They will become one flock with one shepherd.

chokri: Süla a Po aphi pünüyo, kekrelamori I apho khapü sü ketola, side I khila yito.
english: Therefore the Father loves me, because I lay down my life, that I may take it again.

chokri: Mipüri süo acelü pü tata mo, atho kha kesü zo. Süo khasü keto akekuba, mu püsalayi keto sheri a keku ba. Hio thino khakesü jü a Po celü I ngoyi.
english: No one takes it away from me, but I lay it down by myself. I have power to lay it down, and I have power to take it again. I received this commandment from my Father."

chokri: Jews miko dolü kelakreta jü hiko kepola.
english: Therefore a division arose again among the Jews because of these words.

chokri: Thüma kekro uko dolü poti, pü therü kesu zütale, pü nyote; neko dito püfü reni ba?
english: Many of them said, "He has a demon, and is insane! Why do you listen to him?"

chokri: Kekreko poti, Jü hiko kepo therü kesu zü ketami jü mole, therükesu mimhü thipü mhangoyi veme?
english: Others said, "These are not the sayings of one possessed by a demon. It isn't possible for a demon to open the eyes of the blind, is it?"

chokri: Tüsü tüce, Jerusalem lü kereshüce thi kemesa ti thenye tülü.
english: It was the Feast of the Dedication at Jerusalem.

chokri: Jisu Solomon cehükhro kereshüce lü taba.
english: It was winter, and Jesus was walking in the temple, in Solomon's porch.

chokri: Süsa Jews miko tavo pühu metuyi, de püce poti, dithepü no hakopo lüyi su bazota? Mhace no Khrista le zize hakoce po kemetse vate.
english: The Jews therefore came around him and said to him, "How long will you hold us in suspense? If you are the Christ, tell us plainly."

chokri: Jisu ukoce poti, I nekoce posüzo vele neko melü mute, mhatho kethiko I a Po zalü thi tazo; süko azhopo zo.
english: Jesus answered them, "I told you, and you don't believe. The works that I do in my Father's name, these testify about me.

chokri: Vele neko melü mute, kekrelamori neko ateko kro mo tila, sizi I neko ceposüte.
english: But you don't believe, because you are not of my sheep, as I told you.

chokri: Akoko afü seyo, I süko seba mu uko asameji yo.
english: My sheep hear my voice, and I know them, and they follow me.

chokri: I kechothe kerhüpü uko tsü süyo, uko mhapü ceri su taho, mori mipüri abelü süpü tataho.
english: I give eternal life to them. They will never perish, and no one will snatch them out of my hand.

chokri: Apo süko pü aketsüo metüko ceri zhoku, mu mipüri a Po belü süko süpü tata ho.
english: My Father, who has given them to me, is greater than all. No one is able to snatch them out of my Father's hand.

chokri: I mu a Po hani püzo.
english: I and the Father are one."

chokri: Süsa Jews miko ketsüpü püva to züta.
english: Therefore Jews took up stones again to stone him.

chokri: Jisu ukoce poti, I apo celü mhakeve kekro thipü neko mekasü; mhatho sükola neko ketsüpü ava tome?
english: Jesus answered them, "I have shown you many good works from my Father. For which of those works do you stone me?"

chokri: Jews miko püfü kelasü de poti, mhatho keve kola hako ketsüpü nva tomo; vele no Niepoo zhoshu tila; no thüma zori ntho npü Jihova thi ketala.
english: The Jews answered him, "We don't stone you for a good work, but for blasphemy: because you, being a man, make yourself God."

chokri: Jisu ukoce poti, nethezholü thopü kemeta, akepo “neko therü mizo.”
english: Jesus answered them, "Isn't it written in your law, 'I said, you are gods?'

chokri: Mhace pü süko cüpü therümi le zi sütaze, Jihova celü vori keta ketholesi jü khipü mole zi va ho.
english: If he called them gods, to whom the word of God came (and the Scripture can't be broken),

chokri: Apo ketsü kesüo, püthi kemesa sü de ketsüpü cejülü sü, size neko püpü Jihova zhoshu bale siziyi veme: I neko ce kepo I Jihova Nu zo zi kesü.
english: Do you say of him whom the Father sanctified and sent into the world, 'You blaspheme,' because I said, 'I am the Son of God?'

chokri: Mhace I a Po mhathoko thiyo mo tisa amelü hite.
english: If I don't do the works of my Father, don't believe me.

chokri: Vele mhace I thiyo tisa neko amelü mota sheri mhathola melü yite, zize neko seyito mu melü yi tato; a Po alüta mu I pülü tazo.
english: But if I do them, though you don't believe me, believe the works; that you may know and believe that the Father is in me, and I in the Father."

chokri: Süla uko pütüyi tatole süta, vele pü uko belü therü tata.
english: They sought again to seize him, and he went out of their hand.

chokri: Süsa pü ta John therace mipü jülopü kesü thethilü Jordan rükhüco cuvota de sülü bata
english: He went away again beyond the Jordan into the place where John was baptizing at first, and there he stayed.

chokri: Thüma kekro ta püce vo de poti John ungo mhatho thi mo, vele John thüma hiola po pü kemeta metüko kethozo.
english: Many came to him. They said, "John indeed did no sign, but everything that John said about this man is true."

chokri: Thüma kekro sülü pü melüyi.
english: Many believed in him there.

chokri: Thümapü püza Lazarus lezino mhathi bata, Bethany rünalü Mary mu püsazüpü Martha ni ra.
english: Now a certain man was sick, Lazarus from Bethany, of the village of Mary and her sister, Martha.

chokri: Mary tirü khapü Jisu kenhü kesüpü, pü pitha ri pü Jisu phezini shüsü, püprü-o Lazarus mhathi bata.
english: It was that Mary who had anointed the Lord with ointment, and wiped his feet with her hair, whose brother, Lazarus, was sick.

chokri: Süla pülipüni mi ketsüpü Jisu cesü, de poti, Hihe, no pükhrü ketao mhathi bate.
english: The sisters therefore sent to him, saying, "Lord, behold, he for whom you have great affection is sick."

chokri: Jisu süo sekeyice poti, kethi hio sata ketola mo, vele Niepoo zapo zale kethila, Jihova Nu zapozale thiyi ketola.
english: But when Jesus heard it, he said, "This sickness is not to death, but for the glory of God, that God's Son may be glorified by it."

chokri: Hitüce Jisu Martha, püjürapü mu Lazarus hiko khrü phi tayo.
english: Now Jesus loved Martha, and her sister, and Lazarus.

chokri: Pü süse keyisa zho kena lüthü bata, pü keba thethilü.
english: When therefore he heard that he was sick, he stayed two days in the place where he was.

chokri: Süsa pü sajiko cepoti, uko ta Judea lü sala kho tokhe.
english: Then after this he said to the disciples, "Let's go into Judea again."

chokri: Pü sajiko püce poti, Jews miko ketsüpü nva toli ba tila, Kemethao, no sülü sala kho tola?
english: The disciples told him, "Rabbi, the Jews were just trying to stone you, and are you going there again?"

chokri: Jisu ukoce poti, tüso kebace bavüdo kerikena ba mome? Thüma kehopüri tüso kebace chota lize püphe mesü vaho, kekrelamori pü cejülü keveo ngo kebala.
english: Jesus answered, "Aren't there twelve hours of daylight? If a man walks in the day, he doesn't stumble, because he sees the light of this world.
