chokri: Vele kehopü sheri tüzülü ta lize, püphe meshü yito, kekrelamori keve püyo ta kemola.
english: But if a man walks in the night, he stumbles, because the light isn't in him."

chokri: Pü mha süko posü, süsalü pü ukoce poti, “Uko züo Lazarus zü bate, vele I tavo de pü kesümeco süto.”
english: He said these things, and after that, he said to them, "Our friend, Lazarus, has fallen asleep, but I am going so that I may awake him out of sleep."

chokri: Süsa püsajiko püce poti, “Pü züba lize uzho ba zotho.”
english: The disciples therefore said, "Lord, if he has fallen asleep, he will recover."

chokri: Sizo sheri Jisu püsü poba, vele uko kelüce pü zürelü keba lübata.
english: Now Jesus had spoken of his death, but they thought that he spoke of taking rest in sleep.

chokri: Süsa Jisu ukoce po metsü metsesü, “Lazarus sa tele.”
english: So Jesus said to them plainly then, "Lazarus is dead.

chokri: “I nekola anü tipü ba, I süce ba kemola; nekopo melü yive ketola: kehothi sheri uko ta püce vo khe.”
english: I am glad for your sakes that I was not there, so that you may believe. Nevertheless, let's go to him."

chokri: Süsa Thomas, cüpü Didymus (nube) lezi ketao püzükoce poti, “ukori tavo püzü sata khe.”
english: Thomas therefore, who is called Didymus, said to his fellow disciples, "Let's go also, that we may die with him."

chokri: Süsa Jisu tavori, pü khapü makhrelü khrü keva tüsozho da keta ngoyi.
english: So when Jesus came, he found that he had been in the tomb four days already.

chokri: Bethany hi Jerusalem zü kemecumo, furlong keripüngu lebi zo.
english: Now Bethany was near Jerusalem, about fifteen stadia away.

chokri: Jews miko kekro ta Mary mu Martha nice vori ba, uko tavo uni lü thomelu ketola, uni prüo lano.
english: Many of the Jews had joined the women around Martha and Mary, to console them concerning their brother.

chokri: Süsa Martha Jisu vori kezü sesü metha tavo püzü kesü, vele Mary püthilü bazo.
english: Then when Martha heard that Jesus was coming, she went and met him, but Mary stayed in the house.

chokri: Süsa Martha Jisu cepode, mhace no hice ba lize, aprü-o sata ho vele.
english: Therefore Martha said to Jesus, "Lord, if you would have been here, my brother wouldn't have died.

chokri: Vele I sekeba, tsühi sheri no Jihova ce mhakehopü sheri chosü lize, Jihova khapü ntsü sü zoto.
english: Even now I know that, whatever you ask of God, God will give you."

chokri: Jisu püce poti, “Nprüo rhüla sürito.”
english: Jesus said to her, "Your brother will rise again."

chokri: Martha püce posala sü, I sekeba pü thela nha rhü sala sürito.
english: Martha said to him, "I know that he will rise again in the resurrection at the last day."

chokri: Jisu püce po, I rhüla kesüo zo, mu kerhüro yozo; amelü ketami sata zori pü rhüla yito.
english: Jesus said to her, "I am the resurrection and the life. He who believes in me, though he die, yet will he live.

chokri: Kehopü sheri rhü kebalü amelü keyimi sata ho. No sü melü teme?
english: Whoever lives and believes in me will never die. Do you believe this?"

chokri: Thenopü püce poti, Ile, I melüte Niepoo, no Khrista zole, Jihova Nu, cejülü vori tole ketao zo.
english: She said to him, "Yes, Lord. I have come to believe that you are the Christ, God's Son, he who comes into the world."

chokri: Thenopü pokevasa ta pücholü vota, de vo pü sazüpü Mary cüsü, püce mekhrü de poti, Kemethao hice voribale mu ncü bale zisü.
english: When she had said this, she went away, and called Mary, her sister, secretly, saying, "The Teacher is here, and is calling you."

chokri: Thenopü süsesü metha rekri ta püce vo.
english: When she heard this, she arose quickly, and went to him.

chokri: Hice Jisu rünalü vori molu, vele Martha püzü kesüti thethilü balu.
english: Now Jesus had not yet come into the village, but was in the place where Martha met him.

chokri: Jews miko thenopü zü pücelü kebako pülü thomelu ba, thenopü rekri tosü keta ngode püsa mejivo, uko kelüce thenopü tavo thekri thi toli zütho ziba.
english: Then the Jews who were with her in the house, and were consoling her, when they saw Mary, that she rose up quickly and went out, followed her, saying, "She is going to the tomb to weep there."

chokri: Süsa Mary vo Jisu keba ngoyi de tatsü püphelü pü reshüta, de poti, Niepoo, mhace no hice ba lize aprüo sata ho vele.
english: Therefore when Mary came to where Jesus was, and saw him, she fell down at his feet, saying to him, "Lord, if you would have been here, my brother wouldn't have died."

chokri: Jisu thenopü kekra ngosü mu Jews mikori kra keta ngosü, pü rüpü melo bata, pülü kemezhe bata.
english: When Jesus therefore saw her weeping, and the Jews weeping who came with her, he groaned in the spirit, and was troubled,

chokri: Pü poti, neko pü khapü dipüce mezü? Uko püce poti, Niepoo, tavori pete.
english: and said, "Where have you laid him?" They told him, "Lord, come and see."

chokri: Jisu krata.
english: Jesus wept.

chokri: Süsa Jews miko poti, Hihe, pü didüpüri pükhrü sheri!
english: The Jews therefore said, "See how much affection he had for him!"

chokri: Humi poti, pü mimhü thimeve kesüo thüma hio pori samo tati thiyi ve mome?
english: Some of them said, "Couldn't this man, who opened the eyes of him who was blind, have also kept this man from dying?"

chokri: Jisu pülü panyo de ta mhakhre cephe. Mhakhre süo kece cepü mu ketsü kezhopü khipü gü metha.
english: Jesus therefore, again groaning in himself, came to the tomb. Now it was a cave, and a stone lay against it.

chokri: Jisu poti, Neko ketsüo püvate. Martha kesao lipü poti, pü sa mena tatole, Niepoo, kekrelamori pü sa keta zho date.
english: Jesus said, "Take away the stone." Martha, the sister of him who was dead, said to him, "Lord, by this time there is a stench, for he has been dead four days."

chokri: Jisu püce poti, I nce posü mome, mhace no melüyi lize no Jihova zapozale ngoto.
english: Jesus said to her, "Didn't I tell you that if you believed, you would see God's glory?"

chokri: Süsa uko ketsüo püva, kesao khapü kemezülü. Jisu pümhü pü tüpede poti, Apo, no afü reni kebala nmeza.
english: So they took away the stone from the place where the dead man was lying. Jesus lifted up his eyes, and said, "Father, I thank you that you listened to me.

chokri: No afü reni sü tayo keti I seba, vele thümako azü kethako la I hihi posü, no aketsü süzo keti ukopo seyi ketola.
english: I know that you always listen to me, but because of the multitude that stands around I said this, that they may believe that you sent me."

chokri: Pü süko po kesüsa se de füphra meto shusü, “Lazarus, tasüri te.”
english: When he had said this, he cried with a loud voice, "Lazarus, come out!"

chokri: Sa ketao tasü rita, püphe pübe kesa füne khipü phapü süri; püzü sheri füpü kezhü meta. Jisu ukoce poti, “Püphrü süte, side püpo tavo tate.”
english: He who was dead came out, bound hand and foot with wrappings, and his face was wrapped around with a cloth. Jesus said to them, "Free him, and let him go."

chokri: Süsa Jews miko kekro Mary vori kebako, mha süko Jisu thikesü ngo de pü melüyi.
english: Therefore many of the Jews, who came to Mary and saw what Jesus did, believed in him.

chokri: Vele humi ta Pharisee koce vota, mu Jisu thikesüko pü ukoce posü.
english: But some of them went away to the Pharisees, and told them the things which Jesus had done.

chokri: Süsa thevo kezhoko mu Pharisee miko kenguyi de kerethita, de uko poti, Uko kedipü bita? Thüma hio mha ungo keti kekro thi süte.
english: The chief priests therefore and the Pharisees gathered a council, and said, "What are we doing? For this man does many signs.

chokri: Mhace uko pü khapü hizi zo ba ketosa, thüma metü pü melü tato; mu Roman miko tavori de uko thethi mu urüna sheri püyi tate.
english: If we leave him alone like this, everyone will believe in him, and the Romans will come and take away both our place and our nation."

chokri: Mipü püza Caiphas lezitio, tütse sülü thevo kezhoo thiba; püpoti, Neko mhapüri semo.
english: But a certain one of them, Caiaphas, being high priest that year, said to them, "You know nothing at all,

chokri: Mori ukola moze kesu lüri yomo, mipüpo thümakola sa keta mesü, size shuye metü katamo ketola.
english: nor do you consider that it is advantageous for us that one man should die for the people, and that the whole nation not perish."

chokri: Jü hio kepo pütho zomo, vele tütse hilü pü thevo kezhoo thiba tila, pü saju kepo Jisu po shuyela sa keta mesü ziba.
english: Now he didn't say this of himself, but being high priest that year, he prophesied that Jesus would die for the nation,

chokri: Shuye süo lebi lamo, vele Jihova nuneko hupre kevako züvo kenguyi ketola risa.
english: and not for the nation only, but that he might also gather together into one the children of God who are scattered abroad.

chokri: Süla sünha yowino pü dokhri va keto rhü tata.
english: So from that day forward they took counsel that they might put him to death.

chokri: Jisu süla melemelu Jewsmi dolü ta mota, vele rezü rüna mi kedira vota, rünapü Ephraim lü vota. Mu sülü püsajiko zü bata.
english: Jesus therefore walked no more openly among the Jews, but departed from there into the country near the wilderness, to a city called Ephraim. He stayed there with his disciples.

chokri: Jewsmi cukevo nye meno bata, thüma kekro uthi kemesa ketola ta Jerusalem lü voba.
english: Now the Passover of the Jews was at hand. Many went up from the country to Jerusalem before the Passover, to purify themselves.

chokri: Süla uko Jisu phu bata, uko dolü kepoti, kereshücelü kebace, “Pü thenyelü vori mota lize neko dipü biti lüba”?
english: Then they sought for Jesus and spoke one with another, as they stood in the temple, "What do you think--that he isn't coming to the feast at all?"

chokri: Thevo kezhoko mu Pharisee miko jü khakesü, mhace humi pü dipüce ba sheri se lize po kemese süte, pütüyi ketola.
english: Now the chief priests and the Pharisees had commanded that if anyone knew where he was, he should report it, that they might seize him.

chokri: Cukevo nye mhüjüce Jisu cejülü tata keto pütü tsoba keta seba tila cejülü pümako khrü zovo thela tsozo.
english: Now before the feast of the Passover, Jesus knowing that his time had come that he would depart from this world to the Father, having loved his own who were in the world, he loved them to the end.

chokri: Uko theva mhaco ti keyisalü therükesu ta Iscariot Judas, Simon nu lü lüta, pü tüsü ketola.
english: After supper, the devil having already put into the heart of Judas Iscariot, Simon's son, to betray him,

chokri: Jisu pü Po mhametü khapü pümelü sü keta seba, pü Jihova celü vori zo, mu Jihova celü vota keto sheri.
english: Jesus, knowing that the Father had given all things into his hands, and that he came forth from God, and was going to God,

chokri: Pü mhati keyisa tasü de püfüneko khipü kerüva, (towel) fünepü khiyi, mu püche phayi.
english: arose from supper, and laid aside his outer garments. He took a towel, and wrapped a towel around his waist.

chokri: Jü rüpü büfü pülü süyi, de püsajiko phe khrü shüdü, fünheo pü uko phe shü meso sü pü püche phapü kebao.
english: Then he poured water into the basin, and began to wash the disciples' feet, and to wipe them with the towel that was wrapped around him.

chokri: Side pü ta Simon Peter ce pheri, ri Peter püce poti, Niepoo no aphe khrü tola?
english: Then he came to Simon Peter. He said to him, "Lord, do you wash my feet?"

chokri: Jisu püce pokesü, I dipü thiba sheri neko tsühi seyiho, vele no hisa seyito.
english: Jesus answered him, "You don't know what I am doing now, but you will understand later."

chokri: Peter po kesü, npo mhapü ceri aphezi khrü hole. Jisu püce posala kesü, mhace I nphe khrümo lize no ala mote (azü ta mote).
english: Peter said to him, "You will never wash my feet!" Jesus answered him, "If I don't wash you, you have no part with me."

chokri: Simon Peter poti, Niepoo aphe lebi mori, abe mu api sheri khrü vate.
english: Simon Peter said to him, "Lord, not my feet only, but also my hands and my head!"

chokri: Jisu püce po kesü, Jürelo pü ketami sizi mori ve püphe lebisa, püpho metüko mesa bazo; mu no mesaba vele neko metüko mo.
english: Jesus said to him, "Someone who has bathed only needs to have his feet washed, but is completely clean. You are clean, but not all of you."

chokri: Kekrelamori sopü pütüsü to sheri se kebala, sülü püpoti, neko metü mesa tsomo.
english: For he knew him who would betray him, therefore he said, "You are not all clean."

chokri: Süla pü uko phe khrü kevasa, pü füneko khisalayi de bayi, pü ukoce poti, neko seyi tetho I nekoyo dipü thisü sheri.
english: So when he had washed their feet, put his outer garment back on, and sat down again, he said to them, "Do you know what I have done to you?

chokri: Neko acüpü Kemethao mu Niepoo siziyo; süsü potü süzo I süozo tila.
english: You call me, 'Teacher' and 'Lord.' You say so correctly, for so I am.

chokri: Mhace I neko kemethao mu Niepoo zori neko phe khrüyo lize, neko sheri hunehu phe khrü zü zo.
english: If I then, the Lord and the Teacher, have washed your feet, you also ought to wash one another's feet.

chokri: I mhathopü thapü neko meka kesüla neko sheri I thi kesü bide thiyi züzo.
english: For I have given you an example, that you also should do as I have done to you.

chokri: I nekoce potü süto mithekümi pü niepoo ce zhoku tamo, mori ketsü kesüo mi pü ketsü kesüo ce zhoku mo.
english: Most assuredly I tell you, a servant is not greater than his lord, neither one who is sent greater than he who sent him.

chokri: Mhace neko süo seyi lize nenü yite, nekola keve zoho.
english: If you know these things, blessed are you if you do them.

chokri: I neko metü pobamo, I pü kedakeyio poba tizo; vele ketholesi po tsoyi ketola, pü azü tsükhri ketio püphekapü amesü: siti kepo.
english: I don't speak concerning all of you. I know whom I have chosen. But that the Scripture may be fulfilled, 'He who eats bread with me has lifted up his heel against me.'

chokri: Tsühi nekoce posüte, süko thi vori keta mhüjü, nekopo I süo le ziti melüyi ketola.
english: From now on, I tell you before it happens, that when it happens, you may believe that I AM.

chokri: I nekoce potü süto, kehopüri I ketsü kesüo zü keyimi, azüyizo; mu azü keyimi aketsü kesüo ri züyizo.
english: Most assuredly I tell you, he who receives whomever I send, receives me; and he who receives me, receives him who sent me."

chokri: Jisu mhahiko po kesüsa pürüpülü kemezhe vorita, mu posa kesü, I nekoce potü süto, neko dolü mipü atü süto.
english: When Jesus had said this, he was troubled in spirit, and testified, "Most assuredly I tell you that one of you will betray me."

chokri: Süsa methakeyiko hunehu pe de sopü poba sheri ulü tsakhu bata.
english: The disciples looked at one another, perplexed about whom he spoke.

chokri: Püsajiko mipü süce pükhoro mengu püba, Jisu khrü kebao.
english: One of his disciples, whom Jesus loved, was at the table, leaning against Jesus' breast.

chokri: Simon Peter püketso de sopü po ba sheri pübe chopü jüpoba.
english: Simon Peter therefore beckoned to him, and said to him, "Tell us who it is of whom he speaks."

chokri: Jisu khoro mengupü kebao püce poti, Niepoo, süo sopüta?
english: He, leaning back, as he was, on Jesus' breast, asked him, "Lord, who is it?"

chokri: Jisu püfü kela kesü, I tsükhri pü jülü sü kesüsa süo khapü tsü kesüo toho. Süosalü pü tsükhri pü jülü süsü (metsüsü) de khapü Iscariot Judas tsüsü, Simon nu.
english: Jesus therefore answered, "It is he to whom I will give this piece of bread when I have dipped it." So when he had dipped the piece of bread, he gave it to Judas, the son of Simon Iscariot.

chokri: Pü tsükhri metsü kesüo ti keyisa Satan pü melülü lüta. Süsa Jisu püce poti, “No thiketha-o thimha süte.”
english: After the piece of bread, then Satan entered into him. Then Jesus said to him, "What you do, do quickly."

chokri: Hicehi miz khüce kebako mipüri pü dipü cola sizi püce poba sheri se bamo.
english: Now no man at the table knew why he said this to him.

chokri: Kekrelamori humi lüti, Judas no presabü khita tila Jisu püpo thenyela mhakhriyi nosütho siziba, side kedimikola mha khapü tsüsü keto latho ziziba.
english: For some thought, because Judas had the money box, that Jesus said to him, "Buy what things we need for the feast," or that he should give something to the poor.

chokri: Pü tsükhripü jüsü kesüo ti keyisa tarekri ceta vota, süce tüzü ta.
english: Therefore, having received that morsel, he went out immediately. It was night.

chokri: Pü taketa salü Jisu ukoce poti, Tsüsa thema Nuo zapo zale thitato, mu Jihova püyo zapozale thi tato.
english: When he had gone out, Jesus said, "Now the Son of Man has been glorified, and God has been glorified in him.

chokri: Mhace Jihova püyo zapozale thiyi ta lize, Jihova sheri zapozale thiyi tato, side zapozale sü thiyi to.
english: If God has been glorified in him, God will also glorify him in himself, and he will glorify him immediately.

chokri: Natsiko, pütü dopü I neko zü bati zo tato. Neko aphuto, I Jews mikoce po kesübi, I dopüce vota to sheri neko sülü vori yiho, süla I nekoce tsühi poba.
english: Little children, I will be with you a little while longer. You will seek me, and as I said to the Jews, 'Where I am going, you can't come,' so now I tell you.

chokri: I jükesapü khapü neko tsüsü tolizü, Neko hunehu khrüyite; I neko khrü vori tibi, neko sheri hunehu khrü yi züzo.
english: A new commandment I give to you, that you love one another, just like I have loved you; that you also love one another.

chokri: Sizi keyilü thümako neko asajimi leti seyito, mhace neko hunehu khrüyi lize.
english: By this everyone will know that you are my disciples, if you have love for one another."

chokri: Simon Peter püketso de poti, Niepoo no dipüce vota tola? Jisu püfü kela kesü, I dipüce voto sheri neko asalü vori yiho, tsücehi. Vele hisaco asa meji vorito.
english: Simon Peter said to him, "Lord, where are you going?" Jesus answered, "Where I am going, you can't follow now, but you will follow afterwards."

chokri: Peter püce posala kesü, Niepoo dipüla I nsa meji yihole zita? I apho sheri nla khasü zoto.
english: Peter said to him, "Lord, why can't I follow you now? I will lay down my life for you."

chokri: Jisu püfü kela kesü, no ala npho khasü tola? I nce potü süto, thevü kekhu mhüjü no vasü aphramo tato.
english: Jesus answered him, "Will you lay down your life for me? Most assuredly I tell you, the rooster won't crow until you have denied me three times.

chokri: Nlü kemezhe tasu, neko Jihova melü bayi, mu amelüri bayite.
english: "Don't let your heart be troubled. Believe in God. Believe also in me.

chokri: Apo celü thüma ba baphi, mhace sizimo süze I nekoce posüva tevele, I tavo theba kesü pü neko füba tato.
english: In my Father's house are many mansions. If it weren't so, I would have told you. I am going to prepare a place for you.

chokri: Mhace I tavo nekola theba kesüyi ta lize, I sala tavori to; side neko zü akeba thethilü vori tato: akeba thethilü neko sheri bayi ketola.
english: If I go and prepare a place for you, I will come again, and will receive you to myself; that where I am, you may be there also.

chokri: I vota keto neko seba mu choo sheri neko se bate.
english: Where I go, you know, and you know the way."

chokri: Thomas püce poti, Niepoo hako nekevo choo semole, dipübi hako cho süo seyita?
english: Thomas says to him, "Lord, we don't know where you are going. How can we know the way?"

chokri: Jisu poti, I cho-ozo, kethoo mu kerhüo zo; mipüri Poo celü vo keyi bamo vele ayo lebi.
english: Jesus said to him, "I am the way, the truth, and the life. No one comes to the Father, except through me.

chokri: Mhace neko ase ba tisa neko a Po sheri se batato, tha yowi neko Poo se bate mu püngori süte.
english: If you had known me, you would have known my Father also. From now on, you know him, and have seen him."

chokri: Philip püce poti, Niepoo Poo sü pü hako mhüngo thi süte size hakola tso tato.
english: Philip said to him, "Lord, show us the Father, and that will be enough for us."

chokri: Jisu püce poti, Philip neko azü pütü kecho vorite, siziri, neko ase mo zola? A ngo kevami Poo sheri ngo vazo, süla dipüla neko Poo khipü hako mhüngo thi süte zibata?
english: Jesus said to him, "Have I been with you such a long time, and do you not know me, Philip? He who has seen me has seen the Father. How do you say, 'Show us the Father?'

chokri: Neko I Poo lüba mu Poo alü ba siti neko semo zo me? I jüpo kesüko atho ala jüpo sü mozo, vele Poo alü ba de mhathoko thi tazo.
english: Don't you believe that I am in the Father, and the Father in me? The words that I tell you, I speak not from myself; but the Father who lives in me does his works.

chokri: I a Poo celü ba ti melüyite, mu Poo alü bati sheri, mo sheri mhathoko la sheri amelü yite.
english: Believe me that I am in the Father, and the Father in me; or else believe me for the very works' sake.

chokri: I nekoce potü süto, amelü keyimi amhatho kethiko pü sheri thiyi to, mu mhatho süko ceri zhokuti thi yito; kekrelamori I a Po celü vota tokezüla.
english: Most assuredly I tell you, he who believes in me, the works that I do, he will do also; and greater works than these will he do; because I am going to my Father.

chokri: Neko azalü mhakehopü cho zori I süko thipü tsü süto, sizi keyilü Nu-ola Poo zale thiyito.
english: Whatever you will ask in my name, that will I do, that the Father may be glorified in the Son.

chokri: Mhace neko azalü mhacho lize, I süko thipü neko tsü süto.
english: If you will ask anything in my name, I will do it.

chokri: Mhace neko akhrü yo lize, I jü khapü neko ketsüko thi mu züyite.
english: If you love me, keep my commandments.

chokri: I Poo ce mhacho süto, mu pü thelü thokemelu khapü neko tsü süto, side pü neko zü kechothe ba tato.
english: I will pray to the Father, and he will give you another Counselor, that he may be with you forever,--

chokri: Ketho Rüpü, cejüo süo ngoyi keho, kekrelamori püse püngo kemola; vele neko püse bate, pü neko zü kebala, mu neko zü tasü tato.
english: the Spirit of truth, whom the world can't receive; for it doesn't see him, neither knows him. You know him, for he lives with you, and will be in you.

chokri: I neko khapü thelü thokemelu kedi thi vaho, I nekoce vorito.
english: I will not leave you orphans. I will come to you.

chokri: Vele pütü dopü cejüo ango motato, vele neko angoto; kekrelamori I rhüta zo tila, neko sheri rhüta zoto.
english: Yet a little while, and the world will see me no more; but you will see me. Because I live, you will live also.

chokri: Sünha neko I a Po zü keta seyito, neko alü ba mu I neko lü kebala.
english: In that day you will know that I am in my Father, and you in me, and I in you.

chokri: Sopüno akepoko kha püta mu züyi sheri pü akhrüta; pü akhrü ketao sü a Po sheri khrü tazo: I sheri pü khrüto, mu I akhapü pü mese mengo süto.
english: One who has my commandments, and keeps them, that person is one who loves me. One who loves me will be loved by my Father, and I will love him, and will reveal myself to him."

chokri: Judas (Iscariot kemoo), poti, Niepoo no dipü bide pü hako mese mengo süto vele cejüo mese mengo süho-ena?
english: Judas (not Iscariot) said to him, "Lord, what has happened that you are about to reveal yourself to us, and not to the world?"

chokri: Jisu püfü kelakesü, mhace thümao akhrü süze ajüko khipü bato, size a Po pü khrüto süsa a Po hani püce vorito, mu pülü hani ba tato.
english: Jesus answered him, "If a man loves me, he will keep my word. My Father will love him, and we will come to him, and make our home with him.

chokri: Pü akhrü kemoo ajüko pü baho, neko ajü kepo keseko atho ajü mozo, vele a Po aketsü kesüo jüko zo.
english: He who doesn't love me doesn't keep my words. The word which you hear isn't mine, but the Father's who sent me.

chokri: I jü hiko kepo neko se bate, tsühi I neko zü kebalü.
english: I have said these things to you, while still living with you.

chokri: Vele thelü thokemelu süosü Kemesa Rüpü, a Po azalü ketsüpü neko ce süto, pü mha metü pü neko metha süto; mu nekopo mha metü neko salü la vorito, I neko ce po kesü metüko.
english: But the Counselor, the Holy Spirit, whom the Father will send in my name, he will teach you all things, and will remind you of all that I said to you.

chokri: Thelü thsi zi tipü khapü neko tsü süto, alü thelü kemenüpü neko tsü süto; cejüo khapü mi ketsü biho, I khapü neko ketsüno. Neko lü keho kerü thi tasu, mori mpe ri tahi.
english: Peace I leave with you. My peace I give to you; not as the world gives, give I to you. Don't let your heart be troubled, neither let it be fearful.

chokri: Neko tsühi I nekoce kepoko se bate, I ta tato, nekoce sala vorito. Mhace neko a khrüyo lize, neko rechü yite, kekrelamori I a Po celü vota to kezüla; kekrelamori a Po ace zhoku.
english: You heard how I told you, 'I go away, and I come to you.' If you loved me, you would have rejoiced, because I said 'I am going to my Father;' for the Father is greater than I.

chokri: Mha süko thi vori keta mhüjü I nekoce posüve, mha süko thi vori taze neko süko seyi mu melüyi ketola.
english: Now I have told you before it happens so that, when it happens, you may believe.

chokri: Hisalü I neko zü mhakepo phizo hote, cejülü kezho miko ta vori tazü mu ala mhapüri bamo te.
english: I will no more speak much with you, for the prince of the world comes, and he has nothing in me.

chokri: Vele cejüo po I a Po khrü keta seyi ketola, apo thi ketopü a Po khapü atsüsü, sübide I thi süte, Khe, uko tasü takhe, de ta vo tato.
english: But that the world may know that I love the Father, and as the Father commanded me, even so I do. Arise, let us go from here.

chokri: I khubübo botho-o zo, mu a PO-o kefü-o zo.
english: "I am the true vine, and my Father is the farmer.

chokri: Kehopüri ayo use zhüyi kemomi rhodü vato, uco kehopüri use zhü keyimi pü ketso süyo side püpo use zhü shuyi ketola.
english: Every branch in me that doesn't bear fruit, he takes away. Every branch that bears fruit, he prunes, that it may bear more fruit.

chokri: Neko tsüce mesa bate I nekoce jüpo kesü jükola.
english: You are already pruned clean because of the word which I have spoken to you.

chokri: Ayo bayi mu apo nekoyo bayi. Süco pütho zono püse zhüyi yomo püboyo ba kemosa sübi, neko ayo bakemosa nese zhü yiho.
english: Remain in me, and I in you. As the branch can't bear fruit by itself, unless it remains in the vine, so neither can you, unless you remain in me.

chokri: I khubübo botho-o zo, neko pücoko, Pü ayo kebami mu apo püyo kebami püse zhüphi yito; kekrelamori neko asamode mhapüri thiyi hozo tila.
english: I am the vine. You are the branches. He who remains in me, and I in him, the same bears much fruit, for apart from me you can do nothing.

chokri: Mhace thümapü ayo bamo lize, süco dathü kevao revi tayo tibi tato; side mipo püpü methü vayo, püto medi vato.
english: If a man doesn't remain in me, he is thrown out as a branch, and is withered; and they gather them, throw them into the fire, and they are burned.

chokri: Mhace neko ayo bayi mu ajüko neko lü bayi lize, neko mha nenüko choyite; süko thipü neko tsü süto.
english: If you remain in me, and my words remain in you, you will ask whatever you desire, and it will be done for you.

chokri: Hibi tilü a Popo zale thiyi to, neko se zhüshuyito, sizi de neko asajimi thi yito.
english: "In this is my Father glorified, that you bear much fruit; and so you will be my disciples.

chokri: Apo akhrü keta bi I sheri neko khrütayo, neko akekhrülü tayite.
english: Even as the Father has loved me, I also have loved you. Remain in my love.

chokri: Mhace neko I nekoce khakesü jüko thiyi lize, neko akekhrülü bayito; I sheri a Po jü khapü aketsüko zü tayo tibi, mu pü kekhrülü bayo.
english: If you keep my commandments, you will remain in my love; even as I have kept my Father's commandments, and remain in his love.

chokri: I hiko popü nekoce kesü akenüpo nekoyo bayi ketola, side neko kenü thitsosü ketola.
english: I have spoken these things to you, that my joy may remain in you, and that your joy may be made full.

chokri: Nekopo thino kesü thezho I khapü neko ketsü süo neko hunehu khrüyite, I neko khrü kevori bi.
english: "This is my commandment, that you love one another, even as I have loved you.

chokri: Kekhrü hioce kezho püridi, thümapü ukehü khapü püzüko tsükesü.
english: Greater love has no one than this, that someone lay down his life for his friends.

chokri: Neko akhrethoko, mhace neko akepoko thi mu züyi lize.
english: You are my friends, if you do whatever I command you.

chokri: Thayovi I neko cüpü thekümi lezi hote, kekrelamori mithekümi niepomi kedipü thiyo sheri seyomo; vele I neko cüpü khrethoko sizito: kekrelamori I a Po celü sekeyiko khapü neko mese mengo süketala.
english: No longer do I call you servants, for the servant doesn't know what his lord does. But I have called you friends, for everything that I heard from my Father, I have made known to you.

chokri: Neko akedayimo, vele I neko kedayi; neko thi mekrepü nekopo nese zhüyi ketola, side neko sepo bayi ketola: neko azalü Poo ce mhakehopü cho sheri pü thipü neko tsü süto.
english: You didn't choose me, but I chose you, and appointed you, that you should go and bear fruit, and that your fruit should remain; that whatever you will ask of the Father in my name, he may give it to you.

chokri: Mhahiko I nekopo thino kesüko, neko hunehu khrü yite.
english: "I command these things to you, that you may love one another.

chokri: Mhace cejüo neko meshu ta lize, himhüjü ameshu mera süte mu neko sheri meshu zoto.
english: If the world hates you, you know that it has hated me before it hated you.

chokri: Mhace neko cejüo thüma lize, cejüo püthümako khrüte vele neko cejüo kemola, I cejülü neko keda yite; süla cejüo neko metsü tato.
english: If you were of the world, the world would love its own. But because you are not of the world, since I chose you out of the world, therefore the world hates you.

chokri: I nekoce kepoko rükra yite, mithekümi niepomi ce zhoku tamo. Mhace uko amethi lize neko sheri methi zoto tila; mhace uko akepoko seyi lize neko kepoko sheri seyi zoto.
english: Remember the word that I said to you: 'A servant is not greater than his lord.' If they persecuted me, they will also persecute you. If they kept my word, they will keep yours also.

chokri: Vele uko azalano mha hiko thipü nekoyo sü zoto, kekrelamori uko aketsü kesüo se kemola.
english: But all these things will they do to you for my name's sake, because they don't know him who sent me.

chokri: Mhace I ukoce posü molize uko kejo baho, vele tsüsa uko kejokeva basa mote.
english: If I had not come and spoken to them, they would not have had sin; but now they have no excuse for their sin.

chokri: Pü ango metsü ketao a Po sheri ngo metsü tazo.
english: He who hates me, hates my Father also.

chokri: Mhace mipüri thiyo kemoko I thisü molize, uko kephoma bamo; vele uko tsücehi ngo süte mu uko a Po hani kenani ngo metsüte.
english: If I hadn't done among them the works which no one else did, they wouldn't have had sin. But now have they seen and also hated both me and my Father.

chokri: Vele thajü ketho lesi thezholü thopü kemetako tsüyi ketola uko ango metsüte, kemechülü, mhapüri mozo sheri.
english: But this happened so that the word may be fulfilled which was written in their law, 'They hated me without a cause.'

chokri: Vele thelü thokemelu vorito, I a Po celü chopü neko tsü süto; ketho Rüpü, a Po celü vorito, pü azhopo thisüto.
english: "When the Counselor has come, whom I will send to you from the Father, the Spirit of truth, who proceeds from the Father, he will testify about me.

chokri: Mu neko sheri azhopo thito, kekrelamori neko theralü azü tazo tila.
english: You will also testify, because you have been with me from the beginning.

chokri: I hiko popü nekoce kesü nekopo ayo jo tamo ketola.
english: "These things have I spoken to you, so that you wouldn't be caused to stumble.

chokri: Uko synagogue cekolü neko hu vato, Ile, humi neko dokhri tisü Jihova choyi thi bale lüto.
english: They will put you out of the synagogues. Yes, the time comes that whoever kills you will think that he offers service to God.

chokri: Mu uko mha siziti nekoyo thi süto, kekrelamori uko a Po ri mu ari semo tila.
english: They will do these things because they have not known the Father, nor me.

chokri: Vele I mejümera nekoce posüve, siziti vori keto, süce neko nesa lüyi to, sizi keto ukoju pü I nekoce po kesüko. I Theralü neko zü ketace po süte.
english: But I have told you these things, so that when the time comes, you may remember that I told you about them. I didn't tell you these things from the beginning, because I was with you.

chokri: Vele I ta a Po aketsü kesüo ce votato, vele neko mipüri aketsomo, “no dipüce votata”?
english: But now I am going to him who sent me, and none of you asks me, 'Where are you going?'

chokri: Vele I mha hiko pü nekoce pokesüla, neko lü su bate.
english: But because I have told you these things, sorrow has filled your heart.

chokri: Sizizo sheri I nekoce ketho posüto, I ta keta nekola kevezo, kekrelamori I tata molize, thelü tho kemelu voriho; vele I mhace tata süze I pü ketsüpü nekoce süto.
english: Nevertheless I tell you the truth: It is to your advantage that I go away, for if I don't go away, the Counselor won't come to you. But if I go, I will send him to you.

chokri: Pü vori tisa cejü kephoma mejo süto, kemezi mu sheco po süto.
english: When he has come, he will convict the world about sin, about righteousness, and about judgment;

chokri: Kephoma kekrelamori a melümo ketala.
english: about sin, because they don't believe in me;

chokri: Kemezi, kekrelamori I ta a Po celü votato kezüla, neko ango samo tato.
english: about righteousness, because I am going to my Father, and you won't see me any more;

chokri: Sheco, kemejo, kekrelamori cejülü kedeko mejopü mezüte.
english: about judgment, because the prince of this world has been judged.

chokri: I mhakekro nekoce posü ketoba, vele neko süko tsühi seyi ho.
english: "I have yet many things to tell you, but you can't bear them now.

chokri: Kehothi sheri ketho Rüpü kevorice pü neko cho thapü kethokolü süto; pütho püju po taho, vele pü keseko pü po süto, mu vori ketoko pü nekoce po süto.
english: However when he, the Spirit of truth, has come, he will guide you into all truth, for he will not speak from himself; but whatever he hears, he will speak. He will declare to you things that are coming.

chokri: Pü apo pü zapozale thi süto, kekrelamori pü acelü ngo keyila, süko khapü neko mese mengo süto.
english: He will glorify me, for he will take from what is mine, and will declare it to you.

chokri: Apo celü mhakeba metüko azhüzo, süla I nekoce kepo, pü azhü khipü neko mengo süto.
english: All things whatever the Father has are mine; therefore I said that he takes of mine, and will declare it to you.

chokri: Dopü neko angomo tato, süsa pütü dopü neko angoto, kekrelamori I a Po celü vota tozü tila.
english: A little while, and you will not see me. Again a little while, and you will see me."

chokri: Süsa pü sajiko hunehu ketso de dopü neko angomo tato, süsa pütü dopü neko angoto, I a Po celü vota to zü tila, süsü dipü po bata?
english: Some of his disciples therefore said to one another, "What is this that he says to us, 'A little while, and you won't see me, and again a little while, and you will see me;' and, 'Because I go to the Father?'"

chokri: Süsa uko poti, Pü kepo pütü dopü sizi tisü dipü po bata?
english: They said therefore, "What is this that he says, 'A little while?' We don't know what he is saying."

chokri: Hice, Jisu uko kelü se keyi uko pü ketsonyü ba keta, neko “pütü dopü” süo ketso nyü bame, neko dopü angomo ta ketosü, mu dopü ango sala to, siti?
english: Therefore Jesus perceived that they wanted to ask him, and he said to them, "Do you inquire among yourselves concerning this, that I said, 'A little while, and you won't see me, and again a little while, and you will see me?'

chokri: I nekoce potü süto, neko krato mu neko lü shü tato, vele cejüo unü phito, neko thelü shüshu tato, vele neko lü keshü süo kenuco neko nüshu de rechü tato.
english: Most assuredly I tell you, that you will weep and lament, but the world will rejoice. You will be sorrowful, but your sorrow will be turned into joy.

chokri: Thenomi nukemenulü ulü kemezhe bayo, pütü vori ketala; vele pünu menu kevasa pü mezheko rekra motayo, kekrelamori thepu mipü menupü cejülü sü ketala.
english: A woman, when she gives birth, has sorrow, because her time has come. But when she has delivered the child, she doesn't remember the anguish any more, for the joy that a human being is born into the world.

chokri: Neko süla tsücehi nekolü su bate, vele I neko zü kengo sala to, neko melü nelü nenü phi tato, mu kenü süo mipüri necelü süpü tata ho.
english: Therefore you now have sorrow, but I will see you again, and your heart will rejoice, and no one will take your joy away from you.

chokri: Sünha neko mhapüri aketso sa hote. I nekoce potü süto, neko mha kehopüri azalü cho süze a Po süo khapü neko tsü zoto.
english: "In that day you will ask me no questions. Most assuredly I tell you, whatever you may ask of the Father in my name, he will give it to you.

chokri: Tsüketso neko azalü mhapüri chomo zolu, chote, khapü neko tsü süto, side nepo nenüko thitsüyi ketola.
english: Until now, you have asked nothing in my name. Ask, and you will receive, that your joy may be made full.

chokri: Mha hiko I jüvelü nekoce po süte, vele pütüpü vorito süce I jüvelü nekoce pomo tato, vele a Po khapü mhotsu neko mengo süto.
english: I have spoken these things to you in figures of speech. But the time is coming when I will no more speak to you in figures of speech, but will tell you plainly about the Father.

chokri: Sünha neko azalü mhachoto, I a Poce nekola mhacho süto siziti I nekoce posü mome.
english: In that day you will ask in my name; and I don't say to you, that I will pray to the Father for you,

chokri: Kekrelamori a Po akhrü yo, neko akhrü tila mu I a Po celü vorizo ti melü ketala.
english: for the Father himself loves you, because you have loved me, and have believed that I came forth from God.

chokri: I a Po celü vorizo, I cejülü vori; mu I cejüo khava tatato, side ta a Po ce votato.
english: I came out from the Father, and have come into the world. Again, I leave the world, and go to the Father."

chokri: Pü sajiko pü ketso, Hihe, no tsühi jüpo mesa mesho süte, jüvelü po mori.
english: His disciples said to him, "Behold, now you speak plainly, and speak no figures of speech.

chokri: Tsühi hako nkepoko se mesa mesho bate, mipüri nketso mori vete, No Jihova celü vori zo ti hako melü bate.
english: Now we know that you know all things, and don't need for anyone to question you. By this we believe that you came forth from God."

chokri: Jisu uko ketsode, Neko melü teme?
english: Jesus answered them, "Do you now believe?

chokri: Hihe, pütüpü vori zü, mu tsühi vori tso bate, neko huphrüpü pre tato, utho ucelü votato, side alebi batato. Sizi sheri alebi bamo, kekrelamori a Po azü kebala.
english: Behold, the time is coming, yes, and has now come, that you will be scattered, everyone to his own place, and you will leave me alone. Yet I am not alone, because the Father is with me.

chokri: Mhahiko I nekoce kepo nekopo ayo nelü menü ba ketola. Cejülü neko kemezhe zoto, vele neko nezü vekrü yite; I cejüo pi kuyi ketala.
english: I have told you these things, that in me you may have peace. In the world you have oppression; but cheer up! I have overcome the world."

chokri: Jisu jü hiko po kevasa, pümhüpü tü pede poti, Apo pütü sü vori tso bate, n Nupü zapozale thi süte, n Nu sheri npü zapozale thisü ketola.
english: Jesus said these things, and lifting up his eyes to heaven, he said, "Father, the time has come. Glorify your Son, that your Son may also glorify you;

chokri: No keku pü pütsüsü uthiupho metükopi, kechothe kerhüpü no khatsü kesü metüko yo.
english: even as you gave him authority over all flesh, he will give eternal life to all whom you have given him.

chokri: Hihi kechothe kehü, No Therü kethoo zo mu Jisu Khrista no ketsü kesüo zole ti seyi keto.
english: This is eternal life, that they should know you, the only true God, and him whom you sent, Jesus Christ.

chokri: I cejülü npü zapozale thi süho, I mhathoko thitso süte, no apo thino kha kesüko.
english: I glorified you on the earth. I have accomplished the work which you have given me to do.

chokri: Tsühi, O a Po, cejü thi keyi mhüjü no athipü zapo zale thi kesübi, apü zapozale thi süte. I nza phrapü cejülü no thüma khapü aketsüko mhüjülü süte, mu uko njü züyite.
english: Now, Father, glorify me with your own self with the glory which I had with you before the world existed.

chokri: Cejülü thümako no khipü aketsüko ce I nza thipü mengo süte, süko nzhüzo no khapü aketsüko, mu uko njü zü yiteho.
english: I revealed your name to the people whom you have given me out of the world. They were yours, and you have given them to me. They have kept your word.

chokri: Tsühi uko seyi keta no khapü aketsüko nzhü zoleti seyite.
english: Now they have known that all things whatever you have given me are from you,

chokri: Kekrelamori njüko no khapü atsü kesüko pü uko tsü süte, uko süko khiyite, I ncelü vorizoleti se bate, mu no aketsü kesü uko melü yite.
english: for the words which you have given me I have given to them, and they received them, and knew for sure that I came forth from you, and they have believed that you sent me.

chokri: I ukola kecho thi süte, cejüola kecho thimo, vele no khapü atsü kesükola, süko nzhüzo.
english: I pray for them. I don't pray for the world, but for those whom you have given me, for they are yours.

chokri: Azhü metüko nzhüzo. Mu nzhü metüko azhüzo, I sükolü zapozale thi süte.
english: All things that are mine are yours, and yours are mine, and I am glorified in them.

chokri: Tsühi I cejüolü zhü mote, vele hiko cejüolü balu, I nce vori tato. Kemesa Poo, no uko yoyite, nzalü, no khapü aketsüko, ukopo pü thiyite, avu kezü pü kethibi.
english: I am no more in the world, but these are in the world, and I am coming to you. Holy Father, keep them through your name which you have given me, that they may be one, even as we are.

chokri: I cejülü uko zü kebace, nzalü I uko yopüba; mipü sheri medi vamo, vele kekri keta nuo lebi: jüopo tsüyi ketola.
english: While I was with them in the world, I kept them in your name. Those whom you have given me I have kept. None of them is lost, except the son of destruction, that the Scripture might be fulfilled.

chokri: Tsüsa I ta nce vori tato, mhahiko I cejüo ce posüve, ukopo ayo uko kenü tsüyi ketola.
english: But now I come to you, and I say these things in the world, that they may have my joy made full in themselves.

chokri: I njüko khapü tsü süte, cejüo uko phi metsüte, kekrelamori uko cejüo mo tila, I sheri cejüo mo ketibi.
english: I have given them your word. The world hated them, because they are not of the world, even as I am not of the world.

chokri: Npo cejülü uko züyi tate sizi I chomo, vele mhakesulü npo uko yoyi te ziti.
english: I pray not that you would take them from the world, but that you would keep them from the evil one.

chokri: Uko cejüo mote, I sheri cejüo kemobi.
english: They are not of the world even as I am not of the world.

chokri: Uko thi kemesa yite, nketho-o yono, njü kethozo.
english: Sanctify them in your truth. Your word is truth.

chokri: No aketsüpü cejülü kesü bi, I sheri uko ketsüpü cejülü süzüho.
english: As you sent me into the world, even so I have sent them into the world.

chokri: Ukola I athi kemesa tayo, siziti ukopo sheri nketholü uthi kemesa ta ketola.
english: For their sakes I sanctify myself, that they themselves also may be sanctified in truth.

chokri: Akecho hiko lebi la mori, vele uko jükolü melü vori ketoko la sheri.
english: Not for these only do I pray, but for those also who believe in me through their word,

chokri: Sizide uko metü kezü püthi yi ve ketola, no a Po alüba mu I nlüba, sübi ukopo sheri uvuyo püthiyi ketola; side cejüopo no aketsü süzo ti seyi ketola.
english: that they may all be one; even as you, Father, are in me, and I in you, that they also may be one in us; that the world may believe that you sent me.

chokri: No zapozale khapü atsü kesüko, I khapü uko tsü süte, sizide ukopo avu pü kethibi uko pori pü thiyi ketola.
english: The glory which you have given me, I have given to them; that they may be one, even as we are one;

chokri: I ukolüba, no alüba, side ukopo kezü püthi meveshuyi ketola, side cejüopo no aketsü kesü seyi ketola, mu no uko khrü tazo, no akekhrü bi.
english: I in them, and you in me, that they may be perfected into one; that the world may know that you sent me, and loved them, even as you loved me.

chokri: Apo, no khapü aketsükopo akebalü bayi ketola, side ukopo azapozale ngo, no pü aketsü, kekrelamori cejü thikeyi mhüjü no akhrü keta la.
english: Father, I desire that they also whom you have given me be with me where I am, that they may see my glory, which you have given me, for you loved me before the foundation of the world.

chokri: O Kemezi Po, cejüo nsemo, vele I nseba, mu hiko no aketsü kesü se bate.
english: Righteous Father, the world hasn't known you, but I knew you; and these knew that you sent me.

chokri: I nza pü hala mese süte, mu posü zoto, kekhrü no kha aketsü pori ukoyo bayi ketola, I sheri ukolü bayi ketola.
english: I made known to them your name, and will make it known; that the love with which you loved me may be in them, and I in them."

chokri: Pilate-e Jisu tüpü methi sü.
english: So Pilate then took Jesus, and flogged him.

chokri: Thipaheko tsahu pira thipü püpiyo sü, mu mere keti füpü pü pü menesü.
english: The soldiers twisted thorns into a crown, and put it on his head, and dressed him in a purple garment.

chokri: Pükrünyü de poti, He! Jews miko kedeo, sisü de uko bezi phrepü püvü.
english: They kept saying, "Hail, King of the Jews!" and they kept slapping him.

chokri: Pilate-e ta sala ukoce pra, de ukoce poti, Hihe, I püzü nekoce prari züho, nekopo seyi keto I püyo kejo püri ngoyi mozo.
english: Then Pilate went out again, and said to them, "Behold, I bring him out to you, that you may know that I find no basis for a charge against him."

chokri: Süsa Jisu tsahu pirapü, fü kemere be. Pilate-e ukoce poti, Hihe, thüma sü.
english: Jesus therefore came out, wearing the crown of thorns and the purple garment. Pilate said to them, "Behold, the man!"

chokri: Thevo kezhoko mu kezhomiko pü ngo kesüce, semeto de poti, Püpü gu vate, pügu vate. Pilate-e ukoce poti, Neko püzü püvo gu vate; kekrelamori I püyo kejo ngoyi mozo.
english: When therefore the chief priests and the officers saw him, they shouted, saying, "Crucify! Crucify!" Pilate said to them, "Take him yourselves, and crucify him, for I find no basis for a charge against him."

chokri: Jews miko püce poti, hako thezho püba; mu pü thezholü sata ti mesüzo, kekrelamori pütho püpopü Jihova Nu zole zi ketala.
english: The Jews answered him, "We have a law, and by our law he ought to die, because he made himself the Son of God."

chokri: Pilate-e süo se kesüsa mpe sukuta.
english: When therefore Pilate heard this saying, he was more afraid.

chokri: Pü ta sala lü sheco balü bayi de Jisu ketsoti, No dipüce vori la? Vele Jisu jüpo mota.
english: He entered into the Praetorium again, and said to Jesus, "Where are you from?" But Jesus gave him no answer.

chokri: Süsa Pilate-e püce poti, No ace mhaposü home? I npü guvari ve mu nkela sürive ti I keku keba no semome?
english: Pilate therefore said to him, "Aren't you speaking to me? Don't you know that I have power to release you, and have power to crucify you?"

chokri: Jisu püfü kela kesü, no mhapü ceri ametsuyi hozo, mesoyo khapü ntsü kemosa; süla atüpü ntsü kesüo kephoma kro kute.
english: Jesus answered, "You would have no power at all against me, unless it were given to you from above. Therefore he who delivered me to you has greater sin."

chokri: Hisalü Pilate-e pükelasü keto rhüta, vele Jews miko se kechü de poti, No mhace thüma hio khasüva ketosa no Caesar khrethomi mote; kehopüri utho ukhipü kedemi le sitimi Caesar metsu yo.
english: At this, Pilate was seeking to release him, but the Jews cried out, saying, "If you release this man, you aren't Caesar's friend! Everyone who makes himself a king speaks against Caesar!"

chokri: Pilate-e süko se keyice Jisu zü prari, pü sheco bayo bayi, cehükhro (nyho kemesa kesülü) vele Hebrewmi jülü Gabbatha ziyo.
english: When Pilate therefore heard these words, he brought Jesus out, and sat down on the judgment seat at a place called "The Pavement," but in Hebrew, "Gabbatha."

chokri: Sünhasü deoba zho püngu nha cukevo nyela kerede keto tü, keba shurü (12:00) pü po kesü, Hihe neko Kedeo.
english: Now it was the Preparation Day of the Passover, at about the sixth hour. He said to the Jews, "Behold, your King!"

chokri: Vele uko se rekrü de poti, püpo ta tate, ta tate, pügu vate. Pilate-e ukoce poti, I neko Kedeopü guva tome? Thevo kezhoko poti, hako kede bamo, vele Caesar lebizo.
english: They cried out, "Away with him! Away with him! Crucify him!" Pilate said to them, "Shall I crucify your King?" The chief priests answered, "We have no king but Caesar!"

chokri: Süla pü pükhapü uko tsüva, guvano de. Uko Jisu zü tata.
english: So then he delivered him to them to be crucified. So they took Jesus and led him away.

chokri: Pütho pü cross (nphemedü sü) püde ta mipifürolü vota, Hebrew miko jülü Golgotha.
english: He went out, bearing his cross, to the place called "The Place of a Skull," which is called in Hebrew, "Golgotha,"

chokri: Sülü uko püpü guva, mi kekre kena sheri pü püsagu, püchü luco hico, mu Jisu metsulü.
english: where they crucified him, and with him two others, on either side one, and Jesus in the middle.

chokri: Pilate-e thüzapü thopü cross yo sü, tho kesüosü hibi, “Jisu Nazarethmi Jews miko Kedeo.”
english: Pilate wrote a title also, and put it on the cross. There was written, "JESUS OF NAZARETH, THE KING OF THE JEWS."

chokri: Jews miko thüma kekro sheri hio phrisü, kekrelamori Jisu kegu thethi nade zü kemeno tila; mu süo thopü Hebrew lü, Greek lü mu Latin lü sü.
english: Therefore many of the Jews read this title, for the place where Jesus was crucified was near the city; and it was written in Hebrew, in Latin, and in Greek.

chokri: Süsa Jews thevo kezhoko Pilate-e cepoti, Jews miko Kedeo ziti tho hite; vele pü po kesü, I Jews miko Kedeo.
english: The chief priests of the Jews therefore said to Pilate, "Don't write, 'The King of the Jews,' but, 'he said, I am King of the Jews.'"

chokri: Pilate-e posala kesü, I dipü thova sheri tho süvazo.
english: Pilate answered, "What I have written, I have written."

chokri: Süsa thipaheko Jisu gu keva salü, püfüneko püyi, de keza zada thiyi; bola mesoyo (coat) utherü bamo, uvolü ki uphe ketso.
english: Then the soldiers, when they had crucified Jesus, took his garments and made four parts, to every soldier a part; and also the coat. Now the coat was without seam, woven from the top throughout.

chokri: Süla uko udolü kepo de uko cekhro va hi khe, vele khüle heve hesu pe khe; sopüza thito sheri, thopü kemeta ketho lesi jüko tsüyi ketola: “uko afüneko khipü kezayi mu a bola pü khüle (hevehesu) thide khiyi.” Hiko thipaheko thisü.
english: Then they said to one another, "Let's not tear it, but cast lots for it to decide whose it will be," that the Scripture might be fulfilled, which says, "They parted my garments among them. For my cloak they cast lots." Therefore the soldiers did these things.

chokri: Cross (nphemedüsü) phece kethako püzu, püsazüpü, Mary Cleophas cemi, mu Mary Magdalene.
english: But there were standing by the cross of Jesus his mother, and his mother's sister, Mary the wife of Clopas, and Mary Magdalene.

chokri: Jisu püzu mu püsajio, pükekhrüo süce ketha ngosü, püzuce poti, tsüce! Thenopü n Nu sü.
english: Therefore when Jesus saw his mother, and the disciple whom he loved standing there, he said to his mother, "Woman, behold your son!"

chokri: Süsa püsajio cepoti, Nzu peyite, süyovilü püzu züvo pücelü thekütata.
english: Then he said to the disciple, "Behold, your mother!" From that hour, the disciple took her to his own home.

chokri: Hisalü, Jisu mhametüko thitso sü ketala, thopü kemeta jü-opo tsüyi ketola, I therite, side posü.
english: After this, Jesus, seeing that all things were now finished, that the Scripture might be fulfilled, said, "I am thirsty."

chokri: Hice kephrojü mhabüpülü chuba, fünhe pülü süo süyi (metsüyi) kekhujü khapü sülüsü, de püvo pütalü süsü.
english: Now a vessel full of vinegar was set there; so they put a sponge full of the vinegar on hyssop, and held it at his mouth.

chokri: Jisu süo krayi de poti, Tsüte, mu nekütsüta de pürüpü khasüva. (Jisu tsü chepha kesü, kengupü).
english: When Jesus therefore had received the vinegar, he said, "It is finished." He bowed his head, and gave up his spirit.

chokri: Jews miko cukevonye la mhakesü keyi tüta, themoko po bati mesümo, Sabbath nha, kekrelamori Sabbath nhasü tüso kezho tila. Uko tavo Pilate-e ce choti, uko pheko dochu va keto choyi, side uko pü tata ketola.
english: Therefore the Jews, because it was the Preparation Day, so that the bodies wouldn't remain on the cross on the Sabbath (for that Sabbath was a special one), asked of Pilate that their legs might be broken, and that they might be taken away.

chokri: Side thipaheko taphe kerao phe dochu sü, mu kekreo püzü gupü kemezü-o phe niri dochusü.
english: Therefore the soldiers came, and broke the legs of the first, and of the other who was crucified with him;

chokri: Vele uko phe Jisu ngo tice, pü sazü keta ngoyi, püpheni dochu mota.
english: but when they came to Jesus, and saw that he was already dead, they didn't break his legs.

chokri: Vele thipahe mipü püngu khipü Jisu tsü züsü, süce thüza mu jü kesa krü kirita.
english: However one of the soldiers pierced his side with a spear, and immediately blood and water came out.

chokri: Thüma hiko kengo-o ngo kethozo, mu pükepoko kethozo; pü sekeba pükepoko kethozo, sübi nekopo melüyi ketola.
english: He who has seen has testified, and his testimony is true. He knows that he tells the truth, that you may believe.

chokri: Mhahiko thi, ketho lesi po tsoyi ketola, Püro püri dochu mozo siziti popü kemeta.
english: For these things happened, that the Scripture might be fulfilled, "A bone of him will not be broken."

chokri: Ketholesi kekrepülü popü kemeta, uko pütsü zükesüo peto (phuto).
english: Again another Scripture says, "They will look on him whom they pierced."

chokri: Hisalü, Joseph Arimathea püsaji mipü, vele mekhrü tayo kekrelamori Jews miko kempela, pü tavo Pilate-e ce Jisu mo cho, mu Pilate-e khapü pütsüsü. Pü süla tavo Jisu mo khiyi.
english: After these things, Joseph of Arimathaea, being a disciple of Jesus, but secretly for fear of the Jews, asked of Pilate that he might take away Jesus' body. Pilate gave him permission. He came therefore and took away his body.

chokri: Nicodemus sheri tavori, pü tüzülü Jisu ce kevoo, mharü kethenyü aloe mu myrrh kere keti Kg. hepüngu khi vori.
english: Nicodemus, who at first came to Jesus by night, also came bringing a mixture of myrrh and aloes, about a hundred pounds.

chokri: Side uko Jisu mo püyi, mharükenyüko kesa fünhe khipü pü kezhüsü, Jews miko kesa kekhrü zho bide.
english: So they took Jesus' body, and bound it in linen cloths with the spices, as the custom of the Jews is to bury.

chokri: Hice Jisu kegu thilü tizhe püba, tizhe sülü mhakhre püba. Sülü kesamo püri pü khrü molu.
english: Now in the place where he was crucified there was a garden. In the garden a new tomb in which no man had ever yet been laid.

chokri: Sülü uko Jisu mo khapü khrüva, sünha cukevonye la keredeti tü ketala; mu mhakhre uko meno kebalari sa.
english: Then because of the Jews' Preparation Day (for the tomb was near at hand) they laid Jesus there.

chokri: Deobado sülü tüsozho thera nha, Mary Magdalene keshu rena tüzü kebalü ta mhakhre lüvo, ketsü phüketu keva ngoyi mhakhreyo.
english: Now on the first day of the week, Mary Magdalene went early, while it was still dark, to the tomb, and saw the stone taken away from the tomb.

chokri: Süsa thenopü ta Simon Peter mu püsajio Jisu kekhrüo nice vori, de unice poti, Uko Niepoo mhakhrelü khipü tate, mu hako semote, püpü dipüce vota sheri.
english: Therefore she ran and came to Simon Peter, and to the other disciple whom Jesus loved, and said to them, "They have taken away the Lord out of the tomb, and we don't know where they have laid him!"

chokri: Peter mu püsajio ni süla ta mhakhrelü vo.
english: Therefore Peter and the other disciple went out, and they went toward the tomb.

chokri: Uni kenani ta kemetha vo, mu püsajio Peter mhüjü tavota, de ta mhakhrelü ra vota.
english: They both ran together. The other disciple outran Peter, and came to the tomb first.

chokri: Pü talü mhakhre lü pesüri, linen füko sülü ngo, siri pü ta ulü lümolu.
english: Stooping and looking in, he saw the linen cloths lying, yet he didn't enter in.

chokri: Süsa Simon Peter püsalü vori, de ta mhakhrelü lü, linen fünheko keba ngo.
english: Then Simon Peter came, following him, and entered into the tomb. He saw the linen cloths lying,

chokri: Mu fükeni pü püpi kepha riba, vele linen füko zü bamo, pütho pülebi kezhi püba.
english: and the cloth that had been on his head, not lying with the linen cloths, but rolled up in a place by itself.

chokri: Süsa püsajio sheri ta mhakhrelü lüri, pü ta mhakhrece vori ra vele, mu pü talü ngoyi de melüta.
english: So then the other disciple who came first to the tomb also entered in, and he saw and believed.

chokri: Sütüce uko ketholesilü thopü kemetako melü molu tila, pü sa kevasa rhüla süta keto.
english: For as yet they didn't know the Scripture, that he must rise from the dead.

chokri: Süsa pü sajini ta sala utho ucelü vota.
english: So the disciples went away again to their own homes.

chokri: Vele Mary süce tha de kraba, pü krakezhü mhakhrelü nekü kipe.
english: But Mary was standing outside at the tomb weeping. So, as she wept, she stooped and looked into the tomb,

chokri: Tüyo choyimi kena bade ngo, mipü püphece mu kekreo püpico, Jisu pü khrü kesü thilü.
english: and she saw two angels in white sitting, one at the head, and one at the feet, where the body of Jesus had lain.

chokri: Uni püce poti, thenopü no dipüla kratha? Thenopü poti, Hihe, mi a Niepoo khipü tatele, püvo dipüce süva sheri I semo tele.
english: They told her, "Woman, why are you weeping?" She said to them, "Because they have taken away my Lord, and I don't know where they have laid him."

chokri: Thenopü süko po kevasalü regü püsaco pesü, ri Jisu süce tha de ngoyi, vele Jisu tho ziti semo.
english: When she had said this, she turned around and saw Jesus standing, and didn't know that it was Jesus.

chokri: Jisu püce poti, Thenopü, no dito kraba? No sopü phu züta? Thenopü püpü tizhe kefüo le ziti lüba, süla püce poti, Kezhoo, no pü püvo dipüce süva sheri ace po süte, I pü püyi ta tomo.
english: Jesus said to her, "Woman, why are you weeping? Who are you looking for?" She, supposing him to be the gardener, said to him, "Sir, if you have carried him away, tell me where you have laid him, and I will take him away."

chokri: Jisu pücüsü, “Mary.” Thenopü regü püpesü, de püce poti, (Rabboni) Kezhoo, (Kemethao).
english: Jesus said to her, "Mary." She turned and said to him, "Rhabbouni!" which is to say, "Teacher!"

chokri: Jisu püce poti, “Ani hite, I tüyo a Po ce kho molu, vele ta a sazüjürako ce vode ukoce po süte, I ta a Po, neko Po, mu a Rüo mu neko Rüo ce khota züho.”
english: Jesus said to her, "Don't touch me, for I haven't yet ascended to my Father; but go to my brothers, and tell them, 'I am ascending to my Father and your Father, to my God and your God.'"

chokri: Mary Magdalene tavo ukoce posü, pü Niepoo ngo ti mu püce po kesü kori.
english: Mary Magdalene came and told the disciples that she had seen the Lord, and that he had said these things to her.

chokri: Sünha theva (deoba zho thera nha) metha keyiko tavo kengu püba, Jews miko mpe tila. Jisu talüri de uko dolü thayi, de ukoce poti, thelü kemenü po neko yo ba keyi thite.
english: When therefore it was evening, on that day, the first day of the week, and when the doors were locked where the disciples were assembled, for fear of the Jews, Jesus came and stood in the midst, and said to them, "Peace be to you."

chokri: Pü süko po kesüsa, pübe mu pütsü (rezakuko) pü uko mekasü. Süsa methakeyiko unü vorita, uko Niepoo ngo kesüla.
english: When he had said this, he showed them his hands and his side. The disciples therefore were glad when they saw the Lord.

chokri: Jisu ukoce posa kesü, Thelü kemenü po nekoyo ba yite; a Po aketsü kesübi I sheri neko ketsü süzüho.
english: Jesus therefore said to them again, "Peace be to you. As the Father has sent me, even so I send you."

chokri: Pü süposü de püsüshü hapü uko yosü, Neko Kemesa Rüpü khiyi te.
english: When he had said this, he breathed on them, and said to them, "Receive the Holy Spirit!

chokri: Neko somi kephoma vasü sheri sümi kephoma va süte, mu sopü kephoma vamo ta sheri süko kephoma vayi motato.
english: Whoever's sins you forgive, they are forgiven them. Whoever's sins you retain, they have been retained."

chokri: Vele Thomas cüpü Didymus li ketao metha keyiko mikerikena dolü mipü, pü süce bamo, Jisu kevorice.
english: But Thomas, one of the twelve, called Didymus, wasn't with them when Jesus came.

chokri: Methakeyiko püce poti, hako Niepoo ngole. Vele pü ukoce poti, amhü khipü pübelü gojal kevükuko ngo mu abepü süko jüsü, süsa abe pü pütsülü rengukulü kerhüsü kemothe I melüho.
english: The other disciples therefore said to him, "We have seen the Lord!" But he said to them, "Unless I see in his hands the print of the nails, and put my hand into his side, I will not believe."

chokri: Tüsozho tütha sa methakeyiko celü kezüba, mu Thomas sheri sülü uko züba, Sülü Jisu ta lüri, cekhako kha meta, vele pü talü uko dolü thayi, de poti, Thelü kemenü po nekoyo ba keyi thite.
english: After eight days again his disciples were inside, and Thomas was with them. Jesus came, the doors being locked, and stood in the midst, and said, "Peace be to you."

chokri: Pü Thomas cepoti, nbecüne khipü abelü thekuko pete, side nbe khipü atsü kerhü süte (rezakulü); side nkemelü kedi thita hite, vele melü yi zote.
english: Then he said to Thomas, "Reach here your finger, and see my hands. Reach here your hand, and put it into my side. Don't be unbelieving, but believing."

chokri: Thomas püce poti, “A Niepoo mu a Rüo.”
english: Thomas answered him, "My Lord and my God!"

chokri: Jisu püce poti, Thomas no kengola melüte, vele ngomo zori melü keyiko thejo kepütho.
english: Jesus said to him, "Because you have seen me, you have believed. Blessed are those who have not seen, and have believed."

chokri: Zasi kekro sheri Jisu pü methakeyiko mhüjülü thisü, süko thopü lesilü sümo.
english: Therefore Jesus did many other signs in the presence of his disciples, which are not written in this book;

chokri: Vele hiko tho kesü, Jisu hi Khrista zo ti nekopo melüyi ketola, Jihova Nu zo; melü keyila neko kerhü ngoyito püzalü.
english: but these are written, that you may believe that Jesus is the Christ, the Son of God, and that believing you may have life in his name.

chokri: Hisalü Jisu püpü methakeyiko mengosü Tiberias jükezho khülü, hibide.
english: After these things, Jesus revealed himself again to the disciples at the sea of Tiberias. He revealed himself this way.

chokri: Uko kezü ketako, Simon Peter, Thomas cüpü Didymus kethio, Nathanael Cana mi Galilee lü, Zebedee nuni, püsajimi kekre kena sa.
english: Simon Peter, Thomas called Didymus, Nathanael of Cana in Galilee, and the sons of Zebedee, and two others of his disciples were together.

chokri: Simon Peter ukoce poti, hano tatsü füthi tatoho. Ukori püce poti, hako sheri nzü tsü tole. Side uko kezü tsüta, uko rekri rulü lüta. Vele süzi mhapüri tüyimo.
english: Simon Peter said to them, "I'm going fishing." They told him, "We are also coming with you." They immediately went out, and entered into the boat. That night, they caught nothing.

chokri: Vele keshu rena Jisu jüpo cethayi, vele uko süo Jisu tho ziti semo.
english: But when day had already come, Jesus stood on the beach, yet the disciples didn't know that it was Jesus.

chokri: Süsa Jisu ukoce poti, natsi miko neko ketico hupü tame? Uko püfü kho kesü, Mo.
english: Jesus therefore said to them, "Children, have you anything to eat?" They answered him, "No."

chokri: Pü ukoce poti, neko jal (füketüfü) kehüpü ru thezaco süte, neko ngoyi toho. Süla uko kehüpü ru thezaco sü, siri uko khu kekro boyi ketala jal tü süsa yi hota.
english: He said to them, "Cast the net on the right side of the boat, and you will find some." They cast it therefore, and now they weren't able to draw it in for the multitude of fish.

chokri: Süla Jisu kekhrü sajio Peter ce poti, Niepoo tele. Simon Peter Niepoo tele siti se kesüce, pü füketü füne khipü püzheyi, (kekrelamori pü mechü tatila), de ta jülü lüta.
english: That disciple therefore whom Jesus loved said to Peter, "It's the Lord!" So when Simon Peter heard that it was the Lord, he wrapped his coat around him (for he was naked), and threw himself into the sea.

chokri: Pü saji kekreko ru keni püyo vorita, kekrelamori süce cejü keso zü kemecuba kemola, (botho kra kenapü püzo), jal lü füko süsapü vori.
english: But the other disciples came in the little boat (for they were not far from the land, but about two hundred cubits away), dragging the net full of fish.

chokri: Uko taphe cejü tsoyi mu metha, uko megikrapü methi meto keba ngoyi, mu füko süsa tsükhrida sheri bade
english: So when they got out on the land, they saw a fire of coals there, and fish laid on it, and bread.

chokri: Jisu ukoce poti, Fü püvorite, neko tsüce tü keyi.
english: Jesus said to them, "Bring some of the fish which you have just caught."

chokri: Simon Peter tavo füko süsa pü cejü pheri, khu kezho kekro ta, krapü hepüngu sü; fü kekro ta sheri jal pa (kro) tamo.
english: Simon Peter went up, and drew the net to land, full of great fish, one hundred fifty-three; and even though there were so many, the net wasn't torn.

chokri: Jisu ukoce poti, “Tavori de mhati yite.” Uko mipüri “no sopü”? zimota. Pü Niepoo zoti seba ketala.
english: Jesus said to them, "Come and eat breakfast." None of the disciples dared inquire of him, "Who are you?" knowing that it was the Lord.

chokri: Jisu tavori de tsükhri mu khu ri pü uko tsüsü.
english: Then Jesus came and took the bread, gave it to them, and the fish likewise.

chokri: Hiohi vasüo Jisu püpü methakeyiko mengo kesü, pü sa keva salü rhüla sü ketasa.
english: This is now the third time that Jesus was revealed to his disciples, after he had risen from the dead.

chokri: Uko mhati ketasa, Jisu Simon Peter cepoti, Simon Jonas nu, no hikoceri (füko) akhrüku yome? Pü poti, Ile, Niepoo, I nkhrü yoti no seba. No akuko vathi yite.
english: So when they had eaten their breakfast, Jesus said to Simon Peter, "Simon, son of Jonah, do you love me more than these?" He said to him, "Yes, Lord; you know that I have affection for you." He said to him, "Feed my lambs."

chokri: Va kenao ri püce poti, Simon, Jonas nu, no akhrü yome? Ile, no seba, I nkhrü yoti. Püce posa kesü, No akunuko thekü yite.
english: He said to him again a second time, "Simon, son of Jonah, do you love me?" He said to him, "Yes, Lord; you know that I have affection for you." He said to him, "Tend my sheep."

chokri: Pü vasüo ri poti, Simon, Jonas nu, No akhrü yome? Peter pülü suta. Püce vasüo ri No akhrü yome? Sizi ketala. Püce po sala kesü, Niepoo no mha metü seba, I nkhrü yoti sheri seba. Jisu püce poti, “A kunuko thekü yite.”
english: He said to him the third time, "Simon, son of Jonah, do you have affection for me?" Peter was grieved because he asked him the third time, "Do you have affection for me?" He said to him, "Lord, you know everything. You know that I have affection for you." Jesus said to him, "Feed my sheep.

chokri: I nce potü süto, no khresa kebace ntho nche phayi to, mu dipüce vonyü ba sheri ta no voto; vele no pethi (tsa) ketasa no nbe chopü mikekre po nche pha süto, de nzü vonyü kemora vo tato.
english: Most assuredly I tell you, when you were young, you dressed yourself, and walked where you wanted to. But when you are old, you will stretch out your hands, and another will dress you, and carry you where you don't want to go."

chokri: Kepo hio püco tisa, dipü sü (sa) thide Therüoza thimelesü to sheri poba. Pü hiko po kevasa püce po kesü, “Asa meji yite.”
english: Now he said this, signifying by what kind of death he would glorify God. When he had said this, he said to him, "Follow me."

chokri: Süsa Peter regü prari Jisu khrü ketao püsa meji kezü ngoyi, pü Jisu khoroyo mengu püba theva mhaco tülü, Jisu ce pü poti, Niepoo, sopü ntü süla?
english: Then Peter, turning around, saw a disciple following. This was the disciple whom Jesus sincerely loved, the one who had also leaned on Jesus' breast at the supper and asked, "Lord, who is going to betray You?"

chokri: Peter püfü sekesü, Pü Jisu ce Niepoo thüma hio dipü bi tata?
english: Peter seeing him, said to Jesus, "Lord, what about this man?"

chokri: Jisu püce poti, Mhace amedo lize I vori kemoce püpo tsizi bate, nla süsü kedipü ta? No asa meji yite.
english: Jesus said to him, "If I desire that he stay until I come, what is that to you? You follow me."

chokri: Süla methakeyiko dolü kepoti, saji süo sataho le zi tayo, siri Jisu püce sizi sümo, Pü sataho; vele amedo lize püpo fü bate I vori kemoce, nlano süsü kedipü ta?
english: This saying therefore went out among the brothers, that this disciple wouldn't die. Yet Jesus didn't say to him that he wouldn't die, but, "If I desire that he stay until I come, what is that to you?"

chokri: Pü saji hio süko pü po sü, mu hiko tho risü; mu hako se keba pü zhopo ketho zo.
english: This is the disciple who testifies about these things, and wrote these things. We know that his witness is true.

chokri: Jisu thi kesü kekre kekro riba, süko püpü tho ketosa, I a kelüce cejü lesiko thotsü keyimo, tho kezüko thoto lize. Amen.
english: There are also many other things which Jesus did, which if they would all be written, I suppose that even the world itself wouldn't have room for the books that would be written.

chokri: Hei, Theophilus I vakerao lesi tho kesü Jisu therace mhatho kethi mu mi metha keti ju thosü.
english: The first book I wrote, Theophilus, concerned all that Jesus began both to do and to teach,

chokri: Püzü tüyo khota kemothe, süsa pü Kemesa Rüpü lü jü khapü kedakeyi Pü methakeyiko tsüsü.
english: until the day in which he was received up, after he had given commandment through the Holy Spirit to the apostles whom he had chosen.

chokri: Pü kemezhe kevasa pümerhüpü mipo püngosü püco kekre ketilü, tüsozho hedapü mimhüngo thi, Jihova kede kethi juko pü po.
english: To these he also showed himself alive after he suffered, by many proofs, appearing to them over a period of forty days, and speaking about God's Kingdom.

chokri: Mu süko züvo kengusü de ukoce poti Jerusalem lü tata hite sisü, vele u Po püjü kha kesüo fübate, Hihe, neko acelü seyite.
english: Being assembled together with them, he charged them, "Don't depart from Jerusalem, but wait for the promise of the Father, which you heard from me.

chokri: Kekrelamori John zono jülü nekopü jülopü sü vele neko lano hisa zho choba kemolü Kemesa Rüpü lü jülopü tato.
english: For John indeed baptized in water, but you will be baptized in the Holy Spirit not many days from now."

chokri: Uko vo kengu keyice uko pü ketsoti “Niepoo no Israelmi kede khasü tatome”?
english: Therefore, when they had come together, they asked him, "Lord, are you now restoring the kingdom to Israel?"

chokri: Pü ukoce poti, neko lazono pütü mu kedi kethice to sheri seyiho, Pootho pü kekulü süko khiba.
english: He said to them, "It isn't for you to know times or seasons which the Father has set within His own authority.

chokri: Vele neko keku ngoyito, Kemesa Rüpü neko yo vori keyisa; neko azhopo thi Jerusalem, Judea, Samaria mu cejü vala ketso voto.
english: But you will receive power when the Holy Spirit has come upon you. You will be witnesses to me in Jerusalem, in all Judea and Samaria, and to the uttermost parts of the earth."

chokri: Pü mha hiko po kevasa uko püpe kebalü püzü tüyo khota, kemhüpü voride uko mhüngolü püzü tata.
english: When he had said these things, as they were looking, he was taken up, and a cloud received him out of their sight.

chokri: Püzü kho ketala uko tüyope mhüdemo bari, Hihe, mikena fü kemega süde uko khüce thade ngoyi.
english: While they were looking steadfastly into the sky as he went, behold, two men stood by them in white clothing,

chokri: Uni ukoce poti, Ne Galilee thümako, dipüla neko tüyo pezo bata? Jisu hio neko dolü zü tüyo kho ketabi hihi bide pü lavorito.
english: who also said, "You men of Galilee, why do you stand looking into the sky? This Jesus, who was received up from you into the sky will come back in the same way as you saw him going into the sky."

chokri: Süsa uko Olivet cejütuyo ta sala Jerusalem lü vorita, Sabbath nha revu chotalü.
english: Then they returned to Jerusalem from the mountain called Olivet, which is near Jerusalem, a Sabbath day's journey away.

chokri: Uko tavo kengu keyice celü kerekrüo (mesoyo) bayi, sülü Peter, James, John, Andrew, Philip, Thomas, Bartholomew, Matthew, James Alphaeus, Simon Zealot, mu Judas, James hiko metü ba.
english: When they had come in, they went up into the upper room, where they were staying; that is Peter, John, James, Andrew, Philip, Thomas, Bartholomew, Matthew, James the son of Alphaeus, Simon the Zealot, and Judas the son of James.

chokri: Uko metü lüpü thide kechothi mu umezhe melo, thenomiko riba mu Mary Jisu zu risa mu Jisu sazüko ri saba.
english: All these with one accord continued steadfastly in prayer and supplication, along with the women, and Mary the mother of Jesus, and with his brothers.

chokri: Hitüce Peter kemelümiko dolü sütha bayo, metükesa thüma krapü mecü kezüba.
english: In these days, Peter stood up in the midst of the disciples (and the number of names was about one hundred twenty), and said,

chokri: Akhreko ketholesi jüko thitso ketola, thajü David talü Kemesa Rüpü popü kemetako Judas (Iscariot) michotha süde mipo Jisu tüzü taketa.
english: "Brothers, it was necessary that this Scripture should be fulfilled, which the Holy Spirit spoke before by the mouth of David concerning Judas, who was guide to those who took Jesus.

chokri: Kekrelamori püpü uko saphriyi de yitho lü ukozü keza.
english: For he was numbered with us, and received his portion in this ministry.

chokri: Tsühi yipü thüma hio mesakemolü khripü metate, püpi meso de retute, pü kesü phrepha va, pübü püsü püra metü kesüla ve.
english: Now this man obtained a field with the reward for his wickedness, and falling headlong, his body burst open, and all his intestines gushed out.

chokri: Theju süo Jerusalem lü kehüko metü se bate, yi süo cüpü Aceldama püco “Thüza yi.”
english: It became known to everyone who lived in Jerusalem that in their language that field was called 'Akeldama,' that is, 'The field of blood.'

chokri: Kekrelamori Süo ju ketholesi Cheleko thopü meta, “Pü kehüthi po mechü tate, mipü pori sülü hü hitate, mi kekre püpo püsülü yitho thi tate.”
english: For it is written in the book of Psalms, 'Let his habitation be made desolate, Let no one dwell therein,' and, 'Let another take his office.'

chokri: Süla mipü Jisu uko dolü tapra talü ketace uko zü tatipü.
english: Of the men therefore who have accompanied us all the time that the Lord Jesus went in and out among us,

chokri: John mipü jülompü ketilü shudü de tavo uko dolü püzü khoketa nha ketso, mipü züpü uko züde rhüla kesü zhopo thita moze su.
english: beginning from the baptism of John, to the day that he was received up from us, of these one must become a witness with us of his resurrection."

chokri: Mu uko mikena kethayi Joseph cüpü Barnabas leziyo, mori cüpü Justus le sizi riyo, mu Matthias ni.
english: They put forward two, Joseph called Barsabbas, who was surnamed Justus, and Matthias.

chokri: Uko kecho thisü, “Niepoo no thüma lüdo metü se, hini dolü sopüpo n medolü kedasü to sheri thatü süte.”
english: They prayed, and said, "You, Lord, who know the hearts of all men, show which one of these two you have chosen

chokri: Püpo apostle yitholü mhathi to sheri, Judas retu vajo keta sülü, süo thethilü.
english: to take part in this ministry and apostleship from which Judas fell away, that he might go to his own place."

chokri: Uko heve hesu (chethikhele) thisü, sülü Matthias khiyi ta, mu uko pü phripü apostleko mi keripü sa phri yita.
english: They drew lots for them, and the lot fell on Matthias, and he was numbered with the eleven apostles.

chokri: Pentacost kevori nhasü, uko metü thethi pülü kezü lüpü thi ba.
english: Now when the day of Pentecost had come, they were all with one accord in one place.

chokri: Tüyo mhafüpü tükhra khrade fübi, tavo celü uko keba thilü chu yita.
english: Suddenly there came from the sky a sound like the rushing of a mighty wind, and it filled all the house where they were sitting.

chokri: Meli thüma meyi bi ngota, tavo uko mipüpü yo bata.
english: Tongues like fire appeared and were distributed to them, and one sat on each of them.

chokri: Uko metü Kemesa Rüpü uchu yita, de jü kekrekre ti pota, Rüpü jükepo kha uko ketsü bi.
english: They were all filled with the Holy Spirit, and began to speak with other languages, as the Spirit gave them the ability to speak.

chokri: Jerusalem lü, Jews kemathomiko, tükhro shuye metülü,
english: Now there were dwelling in Jerusalem Jews, devout men, from every nation under the sky.

chokri: Füphra hio kesece uko ngota, makro mipüpü jü kekrekre tilü po mu seta.
english: When this sound was heard, the multitude came together, and were bewildered, because everyone heard them speaking in his own language.

chokri: Uko ungo mu umhata, de hunehu cepoti, Hihe, uko metü Galilaean mizo mome?
english: They were all amazed and marveled, saying to one another, "Behold, aren't all these who speak Galileans?

chokri: Dipü bide uko jü umeyi kekrekre tilü uko rethi bata?
english: How do we hear, everyone in our own native language?

chokri: Parthians, Medes, Elamites, mu Mesopotamia lü kehüko jüri, Judea, Cappadocia, Pontus, Asia lü.
english: Parthians, Medes, Elamites, and people from Mesopotamia, Judea, Cappadocia, Pontus, Asia,

chokri: Phrygia, Pamphylia, Egypt, Libya lü hu, Cyrene, Rome lü sekemoko, Jews mu Judakemoko Judah nani zü ketako,
english: Phrygia, Pamphylia, Egypt, the parts of Libya around Cyrene, visitors from Rome, both Jews and proselytes,

chokri: Cretes, Arabians, uko seti utho ujülü urethita Jihova mhatho ungoti meyi thi süte.
english: Cretans and Arabians: we hear them speaking in our languages the mighty works of God!"

chokri: Uko metü ungota side seyisu ketala uko poti hihi kedipü cota?
english: They were all amazed, and were perplexed, saying one to another, "What does this mean?"

chokri: Kekremiko uko krünyüde poti thüma hiko hezo kesa kra mezü bale.
english: Others, mocking, said, "They are filled with new wine."

chokri: Vele Peter uko mikeripüko zü thayi de se meto ukoce poti Ne Judea thümako mu Jerusalem lü kehüko neko hihi setüyite, de ajü reni süte.
