chokri: Thüma metümo vele Jihova kedakeyi hako, püzhopo thi ketoko, püzü mhati mhakra; pü rhüla sü ketasa.
english: not to all the people, but to witnesses who were chosen before by God, to us, who ate and drank with him after he rose from the dead.

chokri: Hakopo thümako ce zhopo thino pü hako ketsüsü, Jihova pü kesüpü kerhüko mu kesako sheco thi keto püzo.
english: He charged us to preach to the people and to testify that this is he who is appointed by God as the Judge of the living and the dead.

chokri: Saju kepoko metü zhopo süo thitazo, somi püza melüyi sheri sümi kejo keva ngo yito.
english: All the prophets testify about him, that through his name everyone who believes in him will receive remission of sins."

chokri: Peter jü süko pokebalü Kemesa Rüpü jüreni kebako metü yokuta.
english: While Peter was still speaking these words, the Holy Spirit fell on all those who heard the word.

chokri: Yi kerümi Peter zü kevori kemelüko ungo thita, kekrelamori Kemesa Rüpü khapü Gentiles (Judah kemomiko) yo ri sü ketala.
english: They of the circumcision who believed were amazed, as many as came with Peter, because the gift of the Holy Spirit was also poured out on the Gentiles.

chokri: Kekrelamori uko jü sekemo meyilü po ketala, side Jihova po kerekrü süta.
english: For they heard them speaking in other languages and magnifying God. Then Peter answered,

chokri: Peter süsa uko ketsode poti, ukopo jülo kepü jü khayi veme humi, uko bide Kemesa Rüpü ngoyi ketako?
english: "Can any man forbid the water, that these who have received the Holy Spirit as well as we should not be baptized?"

chokri: Süla pü ukopo Jisu Khrista zalü jülo püyi keveposü, side uko püpo tüsozhohu uko zübatano chota.
english: He commanded them to be baptized in the name of Jesus Christ. Then they asked him to stay some days.

chokri: Apostleko mu kemelümi Judea lü kebako Gentiles kori Jihova jü thimzüyi keta sesü.
english: Now the apostles and the brothers who were in Judea heard that the Gentiles had also received the word of God.

chokri: Peter ta Jerusalem lü khori, süce yikerüko püzü kemelita.
english: When Peter had come up to Jerusalem, those who were of the circumcision contended with him,

chokri: Uko poti, no yirükemoko ce vo ukozü mhati.
english: saying, "You went in to uncircumcised men, and ate with them!"

chokri: Vele Peter ukoce theralü dipü thita sheri pozovo tsosü.
english: But Peter began, and explained to them in order, saying,

chokri: I tavo Joppa nadelü kecho thiba, siri zümholü I mhapü ngo; mhabüpü kiri, tüyo mhabü kezhopü küda thi kiri, süo ta ace kirita.
english: "I was in the city of Joppa praying, and in a trance I saw a vision: a certain container descending, like it was a great sheet let down from heaven by four corners. It came as far as me.

chokri: I süo amhüpü pe türü kebace cejülü uphe kepü pheda khonu, gu ketako ri mu türako ri ngo.
english: When I had looked intently at it, I considered, and saw the four-footed animals of the earth, wild animals, creeping things, and birds of the sky.

chokri: Thefüpü ace kepo se, Peter tasüte, de khonuko dokhri pü tite.
english: I also heard a voice saying to me, 'Rise, Peter, kill and eat!'

chokri: Vele I püfü kelasü, Hole, Niepoo, mhakeho mesakemo püri atalü süyo mo zole.
english: But I said, 'Not so, Lord, for nothing unholy or unclean has ever entered into my mouth.'

chokri: Mhafü süo tüyo kiri ace posalasü, Mhakedipü Jihova thi kemesa sü sheri no süo po merho hite.
english: But a voice answered me the second time out of heaven, 'What God has cleansed, don't you call unclean.'

chokri: Mha süo vasü thipü amengosü, süsa süko pü tüyo lakhota.
english: This was done three times, and all were drawn up again into heaven.

chokri: Hihe, siziba metha, Caesarea lü misü ketsüpü akeba celü aphu vorita.
english: Behold, immediately three men stood before the house where I was, having been sent from Caesarea to me.

chokri: Rüpü apo süko zü tavono sita, mhapüri kelüdu hino zi. Kemelümi hiko mi shurü azüde tavo, hako ta thümao celü vo.
english: The Spirit told me to go with them, without discriminating. These six brothers also accompanied me, and we entered into the man's house.

chokri: Pü hakoce tüyo choyimipü ngo, pücelü, tha de püce poti, thüma ketsüpü Joppalü süte, Simon püza thopü Peter thi ketao cü süte sizi püce po sülezi.
english: He told us how he had seen the angel standing in his house, and saying to him, 'Send to Joppa, and get Simon, whose surname is Peter,

chokri: Pü nce jüko po süto, no mu ncekroko kelayi ketola.
english: who will speak to you words by which you will be saved, you and all your house.'

chokri: I jüpo shudü kebalü Kemesa Rüpü ukoyo kuta, therace ukoyo vo ti bide.
english: As I began to speak, the Holy Spirit fell on them, even as on us at the beginning.

chokri: Süsa I Niepoo jü dipü posü sheri rekra vorita, ‘John jüpü jülo khasü, vele neko Rüpülü jülo khasüto.’
english: I remembered the word of the Lord, how he said, 'John indeed baptized in water, but you will be baptized in the Holy Spirit.'

chokri: Mhace Jihova ukoyo sheri mhaphrü kemhati uko Jisu Khrista melü keyice bide ngoyi ta lize, I sü sopüle zide, Jihova mhathoko khava yiveta?
english: If then God gave to them the same gift as us, when we believed in the Lord Jesus Christ, who was I, that I could withstand God?"

chokri: Süko se keyice uko kemekreta, side Jihova chuta, side poti, Süsa Jihova thelü rede keyi mu kerhü ngo keyi khapü Gentiles kori tsü süte-ese.
english: When they heard these things, they held their peace, and glorified God, saying, "Then God has also granted to the Gentiles repentance to life!"

chokri: Hitüce kehu kemethila uko phopü preta Stephen yolü, uko ta Phoenicia, Cypres, mu Antioch ketso vota, zhopo thi Jews mi dolü sasü kekremi ce sümolu.
english: They therefore who were scattered abroad by the oppression that arose about Stephen traveled as far as Phoenicia, Cyprus, and Antioch, speaking the word to no one except to Jews only.

chokri: Süko dolü humi Cypresmi, mu Cerenemi ta, uko ta Antioch lü vori, side Greek (Hellenists) koce Niepoo Jisu zhopo thisü.
english: But there were some of them, men of Cyprus and Cyrene, who, when they had come to Antioch, spoke to the Greeks, preaching the Lord Jesus.

chokri: Niepoo be uko züta, thüma kekro melüyi de Niepoo ce vorita.
english: The hand of the Lord was with them, and a great number believed and turned to the Lord.

chokri: Theju hiko sevo Jerusalem lü kehuko nilü tsota, uko Barnabas ketsüsü, de tazo Antioch ketso vo.
english: The report concerning them came to the ears of the assembly which was in Jerusalem. They sent out Barnabas to go as far as Antioch,

chokri: Pü tavo Niepoo ngokemezhe ngode pünü phita, side uko tarho thi kethepu sü, ukopo umelü pü Niepoo yo tha kemetü yino posü.
english: who, when he had come, and had seen the grace of God, was glad. He exhorted them all, that with purpose of heart they should remain near to the Lord.

chokri: Kekrelamori pü thüma kevepü, kemelü kemetü, mu Kemesa Rüpü püchupü ta tila, thüma kekro sapü Niepoo cesü.
english: For he was a good man, and full of the Holy Spirit and of faith, and many people were added to the Lord.

chokri: Süsa Barnabas ta Tarsus lü Saul phu vota.
english: Barnabas went out to Tarsus to look for Saul.

chokri: Tavo püngoyi de zü Antioch lü vorita. Tütsepü metü kehuko zü bata sülü, kehuko metha shusü. Sajiko cüpü Christian miko zikeyi Antioch lü cü theratho thiyi.
english: When he had found him, he brought him to Antioch. It happened, that for a whole year they were gathered together with the assembly, and taught many people. The disciples were first called Christians in Antioch.

chokri: Pütü hitüce sajukepoko Jerusalem lü ta Antioch lü vori.
english: Now in these days, prophets came down from Jerusalem to Antioch.

chokri: Uko dolü mipü püza Agabus, Rüpü lü zasi tha kesü cejü metülü kemekre vori ta keto posü; Claudius Caesar tüce vorita.
english: One of them named Agabus stood up, and indicated by the Spirit that there should be a great famine all over the world, which also happened in the days of Claudius.

chokri: Süsa kemelü sajiko mipüpü thiyi kevedü kekhrühi ketsüpü usazüjürako Judea lü hükebako tsüsü keto rhüyi.
english: As any of the disciples had plenty, each determined to send relief to the brothers who lived in Judea;

chokri: Uko süo thipü ketsüsü, Saul mu Barnabas ni belü ketsüsü.
english: which they also did, sending it to the elders by the hands of Barnabas and Saul.

chokri: Pütü süce Herod kedeo pübe kemethi chopü thüma humi kehulü ketako yo süta.
english: Now about that time, Herod the king stretched out his hands to oppress some of the assembly.

chokri: Zechopü James John jürao dokhriva.
english: He killed James, the brother of John, with the sword.

chokri: Jews miko süola uko nü keta pü ngo, side pü Peter ri tüyita. Hitüce khre khasü kemo tsükhri nye thiba.
english: When he saw that it pleased the Jews, he proceeded to seize Peter also. This was during the days of unleavened bread.

chokri: Peter tüpü thejü celü khapü mezüva, cebü hadalü thipahemi po fü bata, pükelü cukevo nye nha püpüvo thümako mhüjülü sü keto lüba.
english: When he had arrested him, he put him in prison, and delivered him to four squads of four soldiers each to guard him, intending to bring him out to the people after the Passover.

chokri: Sülano Peter khapü thejü celü kha mezü, vele kehuko tümozode pula Jihova ce kecho thi zoba.
english: Peter therefore was kept in the prison, but constant prayer was made by the assembly to God for him.

chokri: Herod püzü prari ketonha, süzi tüzülü Peter thipahemi kena dolü zü kebalü, thegi ropha kenapü pha mezü, thejü kefüko cekha kolü fütha.
english: The same night when Herod was about to bring him out, Peter was sleeping between two soldiers, bound with two chains. Guards in front of the door kept the prison.

chokri: Rekri tüyo choyi mipü ta püce lüri de thejüce va vekrüta, side Peter kekhusü, pükhüco, de püce poti, Tamha süte! Pü bekhani pübeyo rezüta.
english: And behold, an angel of the Lord stood by him, and a light shone in the cell. He struck Peter on the side, and woke him up, saying, "Stand up quickly!" His chains fell off from his hands.

chokri: Tüyo choyio püce pode, nchepha yite, mu npheku pü yite. Pü sübi thiyi. Nfü be yite de asa meji yite.
english: The angel said to him, "Get dressed and put on your sandals." He did so. He said to him, "Put on your cloak, and follow me."

chokri: Pü tapra de püsa meji vota, tüyo choyio dipü thisü sheri pü mhapüri lü vorimozo, pü kelüce zümhopü makeba bizo.
english: And he went out and followed him. He didn't know that what was being done by the angel was real, but thought he saw a vision.

chokri: Uni ta cepra kenalü cuprata, side ta nadelü thegi kharü lü vota; cekha süo pütho khripü uni tsüsüta: sizi ketasa tüyo choyio pü khava.
english: When they were past the first and the second guard, they came to the iron gate that leads into the city, which opened to them by itself. They went out, and went down one street, and immediately the angel departed from him.

chokri: Süsa Peter püsa lüvorita, tsüsahi asa setüyite, Niepoo pü choyimi ketsü atsü süte, Herod belü akela süte, Jews miko kemerilü ri akela süte.
english: When Peter had come to himself, he said, "Now I truly know that the Lord has sent out his angel and delivered me out of the hand of Herod, and from everything the Jewish people were expecting."

chokri: Pü chota kezhu mha süko lü kebalü ta John zu Mary celü vorita, John püza thopü Mark thi ketao, thüma kekro sülü kecho thiba.
english: Thinking about that, he came to the house of Mary, the mother of John whose surname was Mark, where many were gathered together and were praying.

chokri: Peter tavo cekha ketusü. Reli mipü püza Rhoda tavo püfü renisü.
english: When Peter knocked at the door of the gate, a maid named Rhoda came to answer.

chokri: Theno süpü Peter fü sesü, reli süpü pükenüpü cekha khrisü mota, vele ta rekri ukocevo, Peter cekha ce tha keba posü.
english: When she recognized Peter's voice, she didn't open the gate for joy, but ran in, and reported that Peter was standing in front of the gate.

chokri: Uko püpü no nyo bate sita. Vele relipü poti sizo kethozole zita, uko puce pode, pükhri zole sita.
english: They said to her, "You are crazy!" But she insisted that it was so. They said, "It is his angel."

chokri: Vele Peter cekha kedü zo bata, uko tavo cekha khri kesüce püngoyi de uko ngo phita.
english: But Peter continued knocking. When they had opened, they saw him, and were amazed.

chokri: Vele pübe chopü ukoce pode füphra hino posü, Niepoo kedipü bide püü thejü celü prasü shi tiko posü. Pü ukoce poti, “mha hiko pü James ce mu usazüjürako ceri posüte.” Pü redekhi thethi kekrelü vota.
english: But he, beckoning to them with his hand to be silent, declared to them how the Lord had brought him out of the prison. He said, "Tell these things to James, and to the brothers." Then he departed, and went to another place.

chokri: Tüso prari ta metha thipaheko dolü mha kebu shuta, Peter yo dipü thita sheri.
english: Now as soon as it was day, there was no small stir among the soldiers about what had become of Peter.

chokri: Herod püphu medi kevasa püfü kebako pü dokhri vano posü. Pü Judea lü tasü Caesarea lü vo bata.
english: When Herod had sought for him, and didn't find him, he examined the guards, and commanded that they should be put to death. He went down from Judea to Caesarea, and stayed there.

chokri: Tyre mu Sidon koyo Herod pü nümo shuta, uko füpü thide ta püce vori; kedeo cene kekhio Blastus zü khretho thiyi, side püzü vethori thiyita; kekrelamori uko kedeo rünalü cene khipü uko rüna khrühi yo tila.
english: Now Herod was very angry with the people of Tyre and Sidon. They came with one accord to him, and, having made Blastus, the king's chamberlain, their friend, they asked for peace, because their country depended on the king's country for food.

chokri: Tüso khimekre keti pünha Herod kede füne khipü keretsoyi, de kedebayo bayi, thüma koce pü jüposü.
english: On an appointed day, Herod dressed himself in royal clothing, sat on the throne, and gave a speech to them.

chokri: Thümako semeto de püchu keti, “süsü therümi füphra zole, thüma fü mole” sisü.
english: The people shouted, "The voice of a god, and not of a man!"

chokri: Rekri Niepoo tüyo choyimi püvüva, kekrelamori pü zapozale pü Jihova tsü kemola; sathi po pütiva de sata.
english: Immediately an angel of the Lord struck him, because he didn't give God the glory, and he was eaten by worms and died.

chokri: Vele Niepoo jü ma keye mu kroku ta.
english: But the word of God grew and multiplied.

chokri: Barnabas mu Saul nit a sala Jerusalem lü vorita, uni zhopo thi ketoko tso kevasa, uni John püza thopü Mark thi ketao zü tavori.
english: Barnabas and Saul returned to Jerusalem, when they had fulfilled their service, also taking with them John whose surname was Mark.

chokri: Tsühi, Antioch kehulü sajukepo mu kemetha sheri ba, hiko biti: Barnabas, Simeon pücüpü Niger siyo, Lucius Cyrenemi, Manaen Herod kede balü kebamipü, mu Saul.
english: Now in the assembly that was at Antioch there were some prophets and teachers: Barnabas, Simeon who was called Niger, Lucius of Cyrene, Manaen the foster-brother of Herod the tetrarch, and Saul.

chokri: Uko Niepoo zhopo thi mu mevo kebace, Kemesa Rüpü poti, Saul mu Barnabas ni kho kekre vate alano, mhatholü achoyi thi ketola I uni cüyi.
english: As they served the Lord and fasted, the Holy Spirit said, "Separate Barnabas and Saul for me, for the work to which I have called them."

chokri: Uko kecho thi mu mevo bade ube chopü uniyo sü, side uni ketsü süva.
english: Then, when they had fasted and prayed and laid their hands on them, they sent them away.

chokri: Kemesa Rüpülü uni ketsü kesüsa uni ta Seleucia lüvo de rutho khi Cyprus lü vota.
english: So, being sent out by the Holy Spirit, they went down to Seleucia. From there they sailed to Cyprus.

chokri: Uni ta Salamis lü vori tice, uni Jews kehuce Synagogues kolü Jihova jü zhopo thivo, uni yitholü John (Mark) ri züta.
english: When they were at Salamis, they proclaimed the word of God in the synagogues of the Jews. They had also John as their attendant.

chokri: Uni tavo cejü rethi tsokemo Paphos lü vori, mipüza Bar-jesus Jewmi zü kesüyi, kemedasaju kepo thenyümi.
english: When they had gone through the island to Paphos, they found a certain sorcerer, a false prophet, a Jew, whose name was Bar Jesus,

chokri: Pü Sergius Paulus khro kezho mipü, mhakese mipü, pü Barnabas mu Saul ni cüsü de unice Jihova jü reni nyüde.
english: who was with the proconsul, Sergius Paulus, a man of understanding. This man summoned Barnabas and Saul, and sought to hear the word of God.

chokri: Kemedu keti mipü Elymas (pü zatho cebi) vori uni zü thatha, kekrelamori kezhoo (pro-consul) po unifü reni mo mu kemelü lü vori mo ketola.
english: But Elymas the sorcerer (for so is his name by interpretation) withstood them, seeking to turn aside the proconsul from the faith.

chokri: Süsa Saul (püza thopü Paul thi ketao) Kemesa Rüpü pü chupü pümhüpü püpe türüsü.
english: But Saul, who is also called Paul, filled with the Holy Spirit, fastened his eyes on him,

chokri: Kedove medamedu keta, no therü kesuo nu, no kemezi metsu mu ngomu ketao, no Niepoo cho kemezi mu keveko thi mesu ketalü ukethüyi ho zome?
english: and said, "Full of all deceit and all cunning, you son of the devil, you enemy of all righteousness, will you not cease to pervert the right ways of the Lord?

chokri: Hihe, tsühi Niepoo be n yo kutato, no nmhü cetato, pütühu no nace ngomo tato. Sizisü metha kemhü mu kezü püyo vota; humipo pübece püchotha de püzü votano phuta.
english: Now, behold, the hand of the Lord is on you, and you will be blind, not seeing the sun for a season!" Immediately there fell on him a mist and darkness. He went around seeking someone to lead him by the hand.

chokri: Süsa kezhoo sü süo ngode püngo thita mu Niepoo zhoko kengola melü ri yita.
english: Then the proconsul, when he saw what was done, believed, being astonished at the teaching of the Lord.

chokri: Paul mu püzüko Paphos lü reyipra ketasa ta Perga lü Pamphylia lü vota. Vele John (Mark) ta sala Jerusalem lü vota.
english: Now Paul and his company set sail from Paphos, and came to Perga in Pamphylia. John departed from them and returned to Jerusalem.

chokri: Vele uni Perga lü ta Antioch lü Pisidia lü vorita. Uni deoba nha ta kereshücelü lü de bayi.
english: But they, passing on from Perga, came to Antioch of Pisidia. They went into the synagogue on the Sabbath day, and sat down.

chokri: Thezho lesiko mu saju kepoko phri kevasa kereshücelü kezhoko uni ketsode poti, Ne usazüjüramiko mhace ketarho ti jühu thümako ce posü nyü ba tisa po süri veho.
english: After the reading of the law and the prophets, the rulers of the synagogue sent to them, saying, "Brothers, if you have any word of exhortation for the people, speak."

chokri: Süsa Paul süthayi, pübepü ukoce chosü de poti, Ne Israel thümako, ne Jihova reshü ketako, neko afü reni süte.
english: Paul stood up, and beckoning with his hand said, "Men of Israel, and you who fear God, listen.

chokri: Jihova thüma hiko potsa neko kedayi, uko Egypt lü sekemo thüma thi ketace pü uko phu rekrüsü, pübo kerekrü khipü uko zü prasü.
english: The God of this people chose our fathers, and exalted the people when they stayed as aliens in the land of Egypt, and with an uplifted arm, he led them out of it.

chokri: Tütse heda mesüpü uko nhatsalü kemezhe.
english: For a period of about forty years he put up with them in the wilderness.

chokri: Pü rüna thüna meka va Canaan lü, pü cejü süko kezapü uko tsüsü, heshe heve kethilü.
english: When he had destroyed seven nations in the land of Canaan, he gave them their land for an inheritance, for about four hundred fifty years.

chokri: Süsalü tütse krada mu hepüngupü copemi khapü ukotsü, tavo Samuel saju kepoo ketso.
english: After these things he gave them judges until Samuel the prophet.

chokri: Süsalü uko kedepü menyüta, Jihova Saul, Kishmi pü uko tsüsü, Benjamin shuye, tütse hedapü püpo kede thi.
english: Afterward they asked for a king, and God gave to them Saul the son of Kish, a man of the tribe of Benjamin, for forty years.

chokri: Pü kha kevasalü David po uko kede thi; zhopo kha kesü I David ngo yite, Jesse nu amelü bi ketipü, pü akelüko thikhrü yito.
english: When he had removed him, he raised up David to be their king, to whom he also testified, 'I have found David the son of Jesse, a man after my heart, who will do all my will.'

chokri: Hio salü Jihova Jisu kha Israel mi Kelakeyio thisü, pü hijü popü kemeta bi.
english: From this man's seed, God has brought salvation to Israel according to his promise,

chokri: Pü kevori mhüjü John thelü redekeyi jülo pü Israel thümako tsü.
english: before his coming, when John had first preached the baptism of repentance to Israel.

chokri: John püthoko tso ketasa, neko apü sopüle lüba? I püo mo. Vele Hihe, mipü asaco vorizüho, I pü pheku sheri phrüyi sümo le.
english: As John was fulfilling his course, he said, 'What do you suppose that I am? I am not he. But behold, one comes after me the sandals of whose feet I am not worthy to untie.'

chokri: Thümako, sazüjürako, mu Abraham nuneko, neko dolü somi Niepoo khri mpe tayo sheri, neko ce kelakeyi jü hio khasü züho.
english: Brothers, children of the stock of Abraham, and those among you who fear God, the word of this salvation is sent out to you.

chokri: Kekrelamori Jerusalem lü kehüko mu kezhoko püse kemola, mori saju kepoko jü deoba metsü phri kebako sekemola, thajü popü kemeta thitsü sü ketola pü mejova.
english: For those who dwell in Jerusalem, and their rulers, because they didn't know him, nor the voices of the prophets which are read every Sabbath, fulfilled them by condemning him.

chokri: Uko pü mesa keshü ngoyi mo zo sheri püpü Pilate-e mhüjülü dokhri vano sita.
english: Though they found no cause for death, they still asked Pilate to have him killed.

chokri: Uko thajü popü kemeta jüko thitso kevasa, süyo pü pükiri, püvo mhakhre pülü khrüva.
english: When they had fulfilled all things that were written about him, they took him down from the tree, and laid him in a tomb.

chokri: Vele Jihova kesalü püphü merhü süte.
english: But God raised him from the dead,

chokri: Tüsozho kekro pü minhüngo thisü, Galilee lü püzü Jerusalem lü kevoriko, pü kengo zhopo kethiko zo.
english: and he was seen for many days by those who came up with him from Galilee to Jerusalem, who are his witnesses to the people.

chokri: Hani neko ce kenü theju keve pü po süyo, thajü upotsa neko ce popü kemetako.
english: We bring you good news of the promise made to the fathers,

chokri: Jihova u uko nuneko ce posüze, popü kemeta jüko thi kesü Jisu phü merhü süte; Cheleko kenalü thopü metati bi, “No anu, tha yowilü I nmenu sü teho.”
english: that God has fulfilled the same to us, their children, in that he raised up Jesus. As it is also written in the second psalm, 'You are my Son. Today I have become your father.'

chokri: Kesalü püphü merhü sü ketasü, sasa hote, hioju thopü metati “I David kekhrüpü nkhrü süto.”
english: "Concerning that he raised him up from the dead, now no more to return to corruption, he has spoken thus: 'I will give you the holy and sure blessings of David.'

chokri: Chele lesida kekrelü poti, “No n Kemesao po kesa ngoho.”
english: Therefore he says also in another psalm, 'You will not allow your Holy One to see decay.'

chokri: Kekrelamori David Jihova medolü pütülü kehüko choyi thi, zü züte, püponeko züde züte, kecekere thite.
english: For David, after he had in his own generation served the counsel of God, fell asleep, and was laid with his fathers, and saw decay.

chokri: Vele Jihova püphü merhü kesüo kece kere ngomo.
english: But he whom God raised up saw no decay.

chokri: Süla asazüjürako, mu thümako neko seyite, thüma hio yono kejo vakeyi khapü neko tsüsüte.
english: Be it known to you therefore, brothers, that through this man is proclaimed to you remission of sins,

chokri: Pü melü keyiko metü mha metülü thi kemezi yito, Moses zholü uthi kemezi yiho ta ketalü.
english: and by him everyone who believes is justified from all things, from which you could not be justified by the law of Moses.

chokri: Süla menu yite, saju kepoko dipü popü meta sheri neko yo vori mo ketola.
english: Beware therefore, lest that come on you which is spoken in the prophets:

chokri: Pete, ne mhalü kerejo ketoko, nengo thiba ka tate, kekrelamori netüce I mhatho thisü, mhatho süo neko püri melü mote, humi nekoce posü zo sheri.
english: 'Behold, you scoffers, and wonder, and perish; For I work a work in your days, A work which you will in no way believe, if one declares it to you.'"

chokri: Paul mu Barnabas ni tata kezüce thümako unipo theju süo deoba nha posasü no zita.
english: So when the Jews went out of the synagogue, the Gentiles begged that these words might be preached to them the next Sabbath.

chokri: Kehu lata kezüce Jews miko mu Judahmi kemoko nani zü ketako Paul mu Barnabas ni sameju ta; side uni ukopo Jihova ngokemezhelü tha metüyi no uko tarho sü.
english: Now when the synagogue broke up, many of the Jews and of the devout proselytes followed Paul and Barnabas; who, speaking to them, urged them to continue in the grace of God.

chokri: Süsanha nadelü thüma metü tavori kenguyi de Jihova jü renita.
english: The next Sabbath almost the whole city was gathered together to hear the word of God.

chokri: Vele Jews miko makro tavori ketala uko rota, jü süko Paul pokesüko metsuta, ketherüti po mu zho shüta.
english: But when the Jews saw the multitudes, they were filled with jealousy, and contradicted the things which were spoken by Paul, and blasphemed.

chokri: Siri Paul mu Barnabas ni thepu shude poti, Jihova jü popü neko ce sü mera ti mesüzo, vele neko se mu ngo zori melümo ta lize neko tho lüyi tate, kechothe kehü ngoyi shümo ta tisa, hani ta Gentiles (Jews kemoko) ce vo tato.
english: Paul and Barnabas spoke out boldly, and said, "It was necessary that God's word should be spoken to you first. Since indeed you thrust it from you, and judge yourselves unworthy of eternal life, behold, we turn to the Gentiles.

chokri: Kekrelamori Niepoo hani cepoti, nekopo Gentileko mhüjülü kevepü thi tato, nepo cejü valakolü kelakeyi thi ketola.
english: For so has the Lord commanded us, saying, 'I have set you as a light for the Gentiles, That you should bring salvation to the uttermost parts of the earth.'"

chokri: Gentileko hio sekeyi ce uko nüta, uko Niepoo jü thi mele melu süta, kechothe kehü kedapü kemeta kekro kemelümi thiyi.
english: As the Gentiles heard this, they were glad, and glorified the word of God. As many as were appointed to eternal life believed.

chokri: Niepoo jü keyepü sükhü sümhü metüko süva.
english: The Lord's word was spread abroad throughout all the region.

chokri: Vele Jewko kemelü keve mu ukhakesü thenopüko lü tho mesu süta, nadelü thüma kemele miko Paul mu Barnabas ni methi to süta, mu uni hupü jükezho khü kolü süva.
english: But the Jews stirred up the devout and prominent women and the chief men of the city, and stirred up a persecution against Paul and Barnabas, and threw them out of their borders.

chokri: Vele uni pheziyo renyoko kekhupü süko metsu sü, mu uni ta Iconium lü vorita.
english: But they shook off the dust of their feet against them, and came to Iconium.

chokri: Sajiko kenüpo uchupü mu Kemesa Rüpü lü hüta.
english: The disciples were filled with joy with the Holy Spirit.

chokri: Sübide Paul mu Barnabasni ta Iconium lü kevoce uni ta Jews kereshücelü lü, de jü posü side Jews kori mu Greek kori kekro melüyi.
english: It happened in Iconium that they entered together into the synagogue of the Jews, and so spoke that a great multitude both of Jews and of Greeks believed.

chokri: Vele Jews melükemoko Gentiles kemelüko lü thimesu püsü usazüjürako metsusüta.
english: But the disbelieving Jews stirred up and embittered the souls of the Gentiles against the brothers.

chokri: Uni pütü kecho sülü bata thepu shude Niepoo jüpo mu ngokemezhe zhopo thi sü, ubepü zasiko mu ungo mhathoko thisü.
english: Therefore they stayed there a long time, speaking boldly in the Lord, who testified to the word of his grace, granting signs and wonders to be done by their hands.

chokri: Vele nadelü thümako kezata: humi Jews kro züta mu humi kemelü krolü vorita.
english: But the multitude of the city was divided. Part sided with the Jews, and part with the apostles.

chokri: Jews miko, Gentiles mu rüna kekha yo tikori kesa de uniyo mhatho kesu thi keto rhüta, uni meshu mu ketsü khipü va keto thita.
english: When some of both the Gentiles and the Jews, with their rulers, made a violent attempt to mistreat and stone them,

chokri: Apostleko süko seyi ketala ta nadekolü vota, Lystra, Derbe, mu Lycaonia kolü mu sükhükolü vota.
english: they became aware of it, and fled to the cities of Lycaonia, Lystra, Derbe, and the surrounding region.

chokri: Uni sükolü Jüyikeve zhopo thi zo vota.
english: There they preached the Gospel.

chokri: Lystra lü mipü püzu valü pheyho zode menu keyi mipü, vapüri chode tayo kemopü.
english: At Lystra a certain man sat, impotent in his feet, a cripple from his mother's womb, who never had walked.

chokri: Paul jüpo keba reni püpe zoba ta, Paul püpe türü kesüce pükemelü sürhoyi keto baketi ngoyi.
english: He was listening to Paul speaking, who, fastening eyes on him, and seeing that he had faith to be made whole,

chokri: Pü füphra meto de, “npheyo tosütha süte.” Siri pü rehü süthayi de chota yita.
english: said with a loud voice, "Stand upright on your feet!" He leaped up and walked.

chokri: Thümako Paul mhatho thi kesü ngo, uko Lycaonia mifü phra meto de poti, “therümiko thüma hü thipü udolü vori bate” sita.
english: When the multitude saw what Paul had done, they lifted up their voice, saying in the language of Lycaonia, "The gods have come down to us in the likeness of men!"

chokri: Paul pü Mercurius rüo thi mu Barnabas pü Jupiter rüo thita. Kekrelamori Paul no jükepo-o thi tila.
english: They called Barnabas "Jupiter," and Paul "Mercury," because he was the chief speaker.

chokri: Süsa Jupiter kereshü thevoo uko mhüjü nadelü bayori thodoko mu menyepüko khipü kharülü vorita, side thümako zü kereshü thipü uni reshü to süta.
english: The priest of Jupiter, whose temple was in front of their city, brought oxen and garlands to the gates, and would have made a sacrifice along with the multitudes.

chokri: Barnabas mu Paul apostleni süse kesüce uni füneko cekhrota, de tasü thümako dolü se vota.
english: But when the apostles, Barnabas and Paul, heard of it, they tore their clothes, and sprang into the multitude, crying out,

chokri: Uni poti, ne kezhomiko dito hibiti thita? Hani sheri neko bi thümazo uthi upho kebamizo, jüyikeve pü neko cekepo nekopo kemethu hikolü redeyi, de rhüketa Jihova tü mu cejü, jükezho, mu sükolü ketako metü thi kesüo ce vori tate.
english: "Men, why are you doing these things? We also are men of like passions with you, and bring you good news, that you should turn from these vain things to the living God, who made the sky and the earth and the sea, and all that is in them;

chokri: Voketa tükolü shuyeko khapü utho umedo chokolü ta vori zo.
english: who in the generations gone by allowed all the nations to walk in their own ways.

chokri: Sizizori zhopo kedithi pütho pü khavamo zo, mhakeve kethilü, tüyo tümerüpü uketsü, pütülü raseko mehü kesü, umelü thipü unü ketiko mu keticoko sheri kha uketsüko.
english: Yet he didn't leave himself without witness, in that he did good and gave you rains from the sky and fruitful seasons, filling our hearts with food and gladness."

chokri: Jü hiko posü zo sheri uko kethüyi mozota, de kereshü thipü uni reshü tole zota.
english: Even saying these things, they hardly stopped the multitudes from making a sacrifice to them.

chokri: Jews miko Antioch mu Iconium lü kevoriko thümako hüpü ukopo ketsüpü Paul vata, ketsüpü püzhe kevasa uko pü süsapü chozu vota, uko kelüce Paul satele lüba.
english: But some Jews from Antioch and Iconium came there, and having persuaded the multitudes, they stoned Paul, and dragged him out of the city, supposing that he was dead.

chokri: Vele kemelü miko tavo pü hutuyi, süce pü tasüri de ta nadelü lüta; mu süsanha pü Barnabas züde ta Derbe lü vota.
english: But as the disciples stood around him, he rose up, and entered into the city. On the next day he went out with Barnabas to Derbe.

chokri: Uni nade sülü zhopo thi kesüsa thüma kekro methasü, uni tala Lystra, Iconium mu Antioch lü vota.
english: When they had preached the Gospel to that city, and had made many disciples, they returned to Lystra, Iconium, and Antioch,

chokri: Süsa uni tavo sajiko thi kemetü mu thi kethepu sü, de ukopo kemelülü tha metü ba zono sü, kemezhe keti kekrolü ta Jihova kede kethilü vo ketola.
english: confirming the souls of the disciples, exhorting them to continue in the faith, and that through many afflictions we must enter into the Kingdom of God.

chokri: Uni ta kevolü kehu pethi mehi kedasü, kechothi mu mevode, Niepoo zalü khapü uko melüsü.
english: When they had appointed elders for them in every assembly, and had prayed with fasting, they commended them to the Lord, on whom they had believed.

chokri: Uni ta Pisidia metülü püthüvo de ta Pamphylia lü vori.
english: They passed through Pisidia, and came to Pamphylia.

chokri: Uni Perga lü jü zhopo thi kesüsa ta Attalia lü kita.
english: When they had spoken the word in Perga, they went down to Attalia.

chokri: Sülü uni ru thopü ta Antioch lü kho salata, Niepoo kekhrülü mhathoko thikhrü keyi kola.
english: From there they sailed to Antioch, from where they had been committed to the grace of God for the work which they had fulfilled.

chokri: Uni ta vori keyice kehuko vori kenguyi, sükoce Jihova uni züde mhatho kethiko popü mekasü, dipü bide kemelü cekha khripü Gentileko tsü sü sheri.
english: When they had arrived, and had gathered the assembly together, they reported all the things that God had done with them, and that he had opened a door of faith to the Gentiles.

chokri: Sülü uni pütü kecho sajiko zü bata.
english: They stayed there with the disciples for a long time.

chokri: Judea lü sazüjürako thümahu voride kemethathi poti, neko Moses zho züde yirü kemosa kelakeyi ngoyiho.
english: Some men came down from Judea and taught the brothers, "Unless you are circumcised after the custom of Moses, you can't be saved."

chokri: Paul mu Barnabas ni süko zü kekre phita mu uko zü kemelita, sülü kepo keyi Paul, Barnabas mu sazüjürako humi ta Jerusalem lü kho de sülü apostleko, mu mehiko zü jü hiola kepono sisü.
english: Therefore when Paul and Barnabas had no small discord and discussion with them, they appointed Paul and Barnabas, and some others of them, to go up to Jerusalem to the apostles and elders about this question.

chokri: Süla kehuko uko ketsü kesüce uko ta Phoenicia mu Samaria kolü püthü vo, Gentileko thelü redeba keta popü ukoce sü, süla kemelü miko metü unü shuta.
english: They, being sent on their way by the assembly, passed through both Phoenicia and Samaria, declaring the conversion of the Gentiles. They caused great joy to all the brothers.

chokri: Uko ta Jerusalem lü vori tice, kehuko uko mesoyi, apostleko mu mehiko ri, uko Jihova mhathoko metü popü uko kasü.
english: When they had come to Jerusalem, they were received by the assembly and the apostles and the elders, and they reported all things that God had done with them.

chokri: Vele sülü Pharisees kropü kemelülü vori keta ba, Yikerü sü thimo suzo zita, ukopo Moses thezho kha kesüko zümoze su.
english: But some of the sect of the Pharisees who believed rose up, saying, "It is necessary to circumcise them, and to charge them to keep the law of Moses."

chokri: Apostleko, mehiko vori kezüde mha hio keti moze suta.
english: The apostles and the elders were gathered together to see about this matter.

chokri: Sülü ketü shuta, sizi kebace Peter süthayi de ukoce poti, “Thümako mu sazüjürako, pütühu vo ketace nekori seba, Jihova uko dolü keda kesü, Gentiles kopo atalü jüyikeve seyi mu melüyi no posü.
english: When there had been much discussion, Peter rose up and said to them, "Brothers, you know that a good while ago God made choice among you, that by my mouth the Gentiles should hear the word of the Gospel, and believe.

chokri: Jihova mimelüko keseo süo zhopo zo, Kemesa Rüpü khapü uko tsüsü ukoyo thi kesü bide.
english: God, who knows the heart, testified about them, giving them the Holy Spirit, just like he did to us.

chokri: Uko mu uko zide kelakre keti bamo, kemelü khipü uko melü thi kemesa sü.
english: He made no distinction between us and them, cleansing their hearts by faith.

chokri: Tsühi, dipüla neko Niepoo medephi bata, voce khapü sajiko volü dusü to züme, upotsaneko mu uko sheri thi mu züyi ho ketako?
english: Now therefore why do you tempt God, that you should put a yoke on the neck of the disciples which neither our fathers nor we were able to bear?

chokri: Vele Niepoo Jisu Khrista ngokemezhe yono uko kela yito mu uko sheri.”
english: But we believe that we are saved through the grace of the Lord Jesus, just as they are."

chokri: Süsa thüma metüko kemekre zü zi bata, mu Barnabas mu Paul nipo jüpo, Jihova ungo mhathoko mu zasiko uniyo thipü Gentileko dolü kesüko posü.
english: All the multitude kept silence, and they listened to Barnabas and Paul reporting what signs and wonders God had done among the Gentiles through them.

chokri: Uko metü kemekre züzi batari, James tasü jü poti, “asazüjürako, afü reni süte,”
english: After they were silent, James answered, "Brothers, listen to me.

chokri: Simeon (Peter) Jihova therace dipü bide Gentileko pesü sheri posüte, thüma süko khiphrapü püzalü süyi ketola.
english: Simeon has reported how God first visited the Gentiles, to take out of them a people for his name.

chokri: Hihi saju kepoko jüko zü kekhüba, hibi thopü meta:
english: This agrees with the words of the prophets. As it is written,

chokri: Hisalü I tavorito, tavo David kereshüce thi sala süto, retuzü ketao, nrhü ketao ketso sala yito, side pü metha süto.
english: 'After these things I will return. I will again build the tent of David, which has fallen. I will again build its ruins. I will set it up,

chokri: Thüma mehapü ketakopo Niepoo phu vorito, mu Gentileko metü, aza cükeyiko metü, Niepoo sizi posü, pütho hiko metü thisü.
english: That the rest of men may seek after the Lord; All the Gentiles who are called by my name, Says the Lord, who does all these things.

chokri: Jihova pümhathoko metü cejü thikeyi theralü se bazo.
english: All his works are known to God from eternity.'

chokri: Süla akelüce uko Gentiles mi Jihova ce vori kezüko kebü hize veto.
english: "Therefore my judgment is that we don't trouble those from among the Gentiles who turn to God,

chokri: Vele uko lesi thopü uko tsüsü ketoko, ukopo marhe kereshüko, kekhrükezü, keropü khonu gukhri, mu thüza hikolü ukethü yi moze su.
english: but that we write to them that they abstain from the pollution of idols, from sexual immorality, from what is strangled, and from blood.

chokri: Kekrelamori thajü zori Moses ju phripü nade metülü sütazo, deoba nha synagogue kolü.
english: For Moses from generations of old has in every city those who preach him, being read in the synagogues every Sabbath."

chokri: Süsa apostleko, mehiko, mu kehu metü umedo unüta, side uko dolü thüma kedapü Paul mu Barnabas ni zü Antioch lü vo ketola Judas, püza thopü Barsabas thi ketao, mu Silas uko sazüjürako dolü krüta kemeleko.
english: Then it seemed good to the apostles and the elders, with the whole assembly, to choose men out of their company, and send them to Antioch with Paul and Barnabas: Judas called Barsabbas, and Silas, chief men among the brothers.

chokri: Uko lesi hibi thosü, “Apostleko, mehiko, kemelükroko, kelü kesü pü sazüjüra thi keta Gentileko Antioch, Syria, mu Cilicia lü kebako ce süzüho.
english: They wrote these things by their hand: "The apostles, the elders, and the brothers, to the brothers who are of the Gentiles in Antioch, Syria, and Cilicia: greetings.

chokri: Sekeyi thümahu hako celü ta nekoce kevoko jükopü neko kebü sü keta sesüte, ukopo jü süko posüte zimo; nekopo yirüte, mu thezhoko züte sizi sümo: mhapo kesüko la neko lü relümo mu neko kebü bate.
english: Because we have heard that some who went out from us have troubled you with words, unsettling your souls, saying, 'You must be circumcised and keep the law,' to whom we gave no commandment;

chokri: Kepokeyi hako metü lüpü thide thümahu hako dolü kedapü kekhrü Paul mu Barnabas ni sa ketsüpü nekoce sü züho.
english: it seemed good to us, having come to one accord, to choose out men and send them to you with our beloved Barnabas and Paul,

chokri: Thüma hiko Niepoo la kemeche lü upho kha kesüko, Niepo Jisu Khrista zala.
english: men who have risked their lives for the name of our Lord Jesus Christ.

chokri: Hako süla Judas mu Silas ni ketsü süzüho, unitalü jükopü nekoce posüto.
english: We have sent therefore Judas and Silas, who themselves will also tell you the same things by word of mouth.

chokri: Kekrelamori hako kelü mu Kemesa Rüpü medo kevelü mha kemele hiko kezüpü neko pü thimeshi sü moze veketo kepoyi.
english: For it seemed good to the Holy Spirit, and to us, to lay no greater burden on you than these necessary things:

chokri: Sükosü, nekopo themu khapü marhe reshü kevako tiho, thüza mu khonu keropü rokhri ketiko tiho, mu regürezhü ho. Mhace neko mha hiko züyi süze verizo. Vebayite.”
english: that you abstain from things sacrificed to idols, from blood, from things strangled, and from sexual immorality, from which if you keep yourselves, it will be well with you. Farewell."

chokri: Süla uko ketsü süva, mu uko ta Antioch lü vota. Uko vori keyice thüma kekro vo kenguyi, uko lesio khapü uko tsüsü.
english: So, when they were sent off, they came to Antioch. Having gathered the multitude together, they delivered the letter.

chokri: Uko lesi süo phrisü mu uko rechüta side uko lüluyi.
english: When they had read it, they rejoiced over the encouragement.

chokri: Judas mu Silas ni sheri saju kepomi zo süla uni jüko popü kemelü miko tarhosü mu thelü tho melu shusü.
english: Judas and Silas, also being prophets themselves, encouraged the brothers with many words, and strengthened them.

chokri: Pütühu uko sülü bata, side kemelü miko uko ketsü salapü apostleko koce süva.
english: After they had spent some time there, they were sent back with greetings from the brothers to the apostles.

chokri: Vele sizosheri Silas sülü basa nyü de bata. (Version hulü hio bamo)
english: Notwithstanding it pleased Silas to abide there still.

chokri: Paul mu Barnabas ni Antioch lü basata thüma kekro sa, zhopothi mu mimetha Niepoo jüpü.
english: But Paul and Barnabas stayed in Antioch, teaching and preaching the word of the Lord, with many others also.

chokri: Tüsozho husa Paul Barnabas cepo, Khe, ami sala nadekolü vode thajü avu Niepoo jüpü zhopo thi kemetakolü ukromiko dipübiba sheri pesükhe.
english: After some days Paul said to Barnabas, "Let's return now and visit our brothers in every city in which we proclaimed the word of the Lord, to see how they are doing."

chokri: Barnabas no John, püza thopü Mark thi ketao züta tole sizita.
english: Barnabas planned to take John, who was called Mark, with them also.

chokri: Vele Paul no püzü tati vemole sita, pü Pamphylia lü uni khava de mhatho kolü uni zü tamo ketala.
english: But Paul didn't think that it was a good idea to take with them someone who had withdrawn from them in Pamphylia, and didn't go with them to do the work.

chokri: Uni sülü ketü shuta, mu uni hunehu zü tamuta kekhota; süla Barnabas no Mark züde ta Cyprus lü rutho khi vota.
english: Then the contention grew so sharp that they separated from each other. Barnabas took Mark with him, and sailed away to Cyprus,

chokri: Paul Sila kedayi de tata, kemelükro Niepoo ngokemezhe lü uni ketsüsüva.
english: but Paul chose Silas, and went out, being commended by the brothers to the grace of God.

chokri: Pü ta Syria mu Cilicia kolü kehuko pe vota.
english: He went through Syria and Cilicia, strengthening the assemblies.

chokri: Pü ta Derbe mu Lystra lü vori, süce, Hihe, sajimipü püza Timothy püzu Jews kemelümi mu püpo Greek mi.
english: He came to Derbe and Lystra: and behold, a certain disciple was there, named Timothy, the son of a Jewess who believed; but his father was a Greek.

chokri: Lystra mu Iconium lü keba kemelüko püpo mveshu kebapü.
english: The brothers who were at Lystra and Iconium gave a good testimony about him.

chokri: Paul püzü tanyüta, püzüvo püyirüyi, kekrelamori Jews miko süce kebakola, kekrelamori uko kese püpo Greek mi tila.
english: Paul wanted to have him go out with him, and he took and circumcised him because of the Jews who were in those parts; for they all knew that his father was a Greek.

chokri: Uni ta nadekolü kevoce Jerusalem lü apostleko mu mehiko kehu kepo keyifü züyi node pü ukoce po vosü.
english: As they went on their way through the cities, they delivered the decrees to them to keep which had been ordained by the apostles and elders who were at Jerusalem.

chokri: Süla kehuko thi kemetü, mu kemelü thüma kro mama prarita.
english: So the assemblies were strengthened in the faith, and increased in number daily.

chokri: Uni ta Phrygia mu Galatia khükolü vo kezüce Kemesa Rüpü unipo Asia lü jü zhopo thihino khata.
english: When they had gone through the region of Phrygia and Galatia, they were forbidden by the Holy Spirit to speak the word in Asia.

chokri: Uni ta Mysia lü vori de ta Bithynia lü ru thovo keto lüba, vele Kemesa Rüpü unipo vo muta.
english: When they had come opposite Mysia, they tried to go into Bithynia, but the Spirit didn't allow them.

chokri: Uni Mysia vathü ta Troas lü kita.
english: Passing by Mysia, they came down to Troas.

chokri: Tüzülü zümhopü khapü Paul mengosü, thümapü thade püce choti, ta Macedonia lü kiri de hako khrühi süthaze.
english: A vision appeared to Paul in the night. There was a man of Macedonia standing, begging him, and saying, "Come over into Macedonia and help us."

chokri: Zümho süo ngo kesüsa hani rekri ta Macedonia lü ki keto rhüyi, Niepoo hani cüpü ukoce jüyikeve posüketo melü tüte.
english: When he had seen the vision, immediately we sought to go out to Macedonia, concluding that the Lord had called us to preach the Gospel to them.

chokri: Hani Troas lü rutho mezi Samothrace lü vo, süsanha Neapolis lü,
english: Setting sail therefore from Troas, we made a straight course to Samothrace, and the day following to Neapolis;

chokri: Sülü ta Philippi lü vo, rüna süo Macedonia bülü kemelepü, Roman mipo kekhapü kebapü. Rüna sülü hani pütühu relü bata.
english: and from there to Philippi, which is a city of Macedonia, the foremost of the district, a Roman colony. We were staying some days in this city.

chokri: Deoba nha hani nade sülü kerükhü tavozü, kecho kethi la süce bayi, thenomiko süce kebakoce jüposü.
english: On the Sabbath day we went forth outside of the city by a riverside, where we supposed there was a place of prayer, and we sat down, and spoke to the women who had come together.

chokri: Thenomipü püza Lydia, kelo shüha loropü zhu bayo, Thryatira nadelü; pü Jihova kereshümipü, pü hani fü sesü Niepoo pülü khrisü, Paul jüpo kesüko reni menuyi.
english: A certain woman named Lydia, a seller of purple, of the city of Thyatira, one who worshiped God, heard us; whose heart the Lord opened to listen to the things which were spoken by Paul.

chokri: Theno süpü mu pücekroko jülo püyi, püpoti, mhace neni halüpü Niepoo kemelümi kethole zize tavori acelü bate. Thenopü sizide hani redata.
english: When she and her household were baptized, she begged us, saying, "If you have judged me to be faithful to the Lord, come into my house, and stay." So she persuaded us.

chokri: Tüso pünha hani kecho kethilü ta vozü, relimi miceta püpü, therüzü kemedu tayo tipü zü kesüyi, urükeve kepo thenyümi kethila presa kekro krapü pü niepoko tsüsüyo.
english: It happened, as we were going to prayer, that a certain girl having a spirit of divination met us, who brought her masters much gain by fortune telling.

chokri: Süpü Paul hani sa mejizo tata, mu sefü poti, thüma hini Therü kerekrü thoo choyimi ho! Uni kelakeyi cho thapü uko meka sü toho.
english: Following Paul and us, she cried out, "These men are servants of the Most High God, who proclaim to us the way of salvation!"

chokri: Thenopü tüsozho kekro sizita. Süsü Paul keyhu phita, süla Paul relesü rüpü kesuo cepode Jisu Khrista zalü thenopüyo tapra tate. Pütü bavüdo sülü taprata.
english: She was doing this for many days. But Paul, becoming greatly annoyed, turned and said to the spirit, "I charge you in the name of Jesus Christ to come out of her!" It came out that very hour.

chokri: Thenopü cepoko süo ngo side uko kepra kemeveko thiyi su keta la Paul mu Silas ni tüpü kezhoko kehu keba thethilü vota.
english: But when her masters saw that the hope of their gain was gone, they seized Paul and Silas, and dragged them into the marketplace before the rulers.

chokri: Uni tüpü shecoko mhüjülü vota, thüma hini Jews mi, unade hio kebü shute.
english: When they had brought them to the magistrates, they said, "These men, being Jews, are agitating our city,

chokri: Thezho kropü uko züyi keho thezho püpozüte, mori u Roman mi thajü züyo kemo.
english: and set forth customs which it is not lawful for us to accept or to observe, being Romans."

chokri: Makro tasü uni metsuta, shecoko ufüne cekhro de poti, thegi sürüpü uni vüsüte.
english: The multitude rose up together against them, and the magistrates tore their clothes off of them, and commanded them to be beaten with rods.

chokri: Uni vü meshüshu kevasa, uni tüpü thejü celü süva de thejüce kefüopo pe menu bayino posü.
english: When they had laid many stripes on them, they threw them into prison, charging the jailer to keep them safely,

chokri: Jü kerü puce kepo sükola, uni khapü thejüce kerelütholü süva, side uni phekhüpü thegiro yo sü meta.
english: who, having received such a charge, threw them into the inner prison, and secured their feet in the stocks.

chokri: Meso tülu Paul mu Silas ni kecho thisü, tsale thipü Jihova chusü, süko metü thejü kethiko metü se.
english: But about midnight Paul and Silas were praying and singing hymns to God, and the prisoners were listening to them.

chokri: Sizisü mu metha cece süshu vorita, thejüce mejuko metü kena shuta; mu thejüce cekhako khri yita, thejü miko phekhako rezüta.
english: Suddenly there was a great earthquake, so that the foundations of the prison were shaken; and immediately all the doors were opened, and everyone's bonds were loosened.

chokri: Thejüce kefüo kezülü kesü mecova, thejüce cekhako khripü keba ngosü, pü zechopü pütho pü dokhri va tozüta, pü kelüce thejüko metü tata lizu ketala.
english: The jailer, being roused out of sleep and seeing the prison doors open, drew his sword and was about to kill himself, supposing that the prisoners had escaped.

chokri: Vele Paul semeto de poti, ntho nmethi va hite, hako metü hice bazo.
english: But Paul cried with a loud voice, saying, "Don't harm yourself, for we are all here!"

chokri: Süsa pü mekeve chosü, de kegüpü vasü, mpe reshü zode Paul mu Silas ni mhüjülü chütü tsüta.
english: He called for lights and sprang in, and, fell down trembling before Paul and Silas,

chokri: Uni züpra chozu uni ketsoti, Kezhoni! Apo kelakeyi ngoyi ketola dipü thita?
english: and brought them out and said, "Sirs, what must I do to be saved?"

chokri: Uni püce poti, Niepoo Jisu Khrista melüyite, size no mu ncekro sheri kela yito.
english: They said, "Believe in the Lord Jesus Christ, and you will be saved, you and your household."

chokri: Uni püce Niepoo jüko püposü, mu pücelü kebako metü.
english: They spoke the word of the Lord to him, and to all who were in his house.

chokri: Pü bavüdo sülü tüzülü uni züvo kevükuko khrü kemesa va; side rekri uko jülo püyi, pü mu pücekro metü.
english: He took them the same hour of the night, and washed their stripes, and was immediately baptized, he and all his household.

chokri: Uni zü pücelü vode ketico khapü uni tsüsü, rechüta, pücekro metü zü Niepoo melü yita.
english: He brought them up into his house, and set food before them, and rejoiced greatly, with all his household, having believed in God.

chokri: Tüso vori ketasa, thejüce kefü kezhoo poti, thüma hiko khasü vate.
english: But when it was day, the magistrates sent the sergeants, saying, "Let those men go."

chokri: Thejü kefüo Paul ce süopü posü, shecomi nenipo tata ri ve sizi bale, süla neni tatate, neni nüde.
english: The jailer reported these words to Paul, saying, "The magistrates have sent to let you go; now therefore come out, and go in peace."

chokri: Vele Paul ukoce poti, uko hani vü shusü hani mejo keva thimo zo sheri, hani Roman mizo sheri hani khapü thejücelü sü; tsühino kemekhrülü hani khala süva tola? Sizi hozo! Ukopo tavori de hani züpra süte.
english: But Paul said to them, "They have beaten us publicly, without a trial, men who are Romans, and have cast us into prison! Do they now release us secretly? No, most assuredly, but let them come themselves and bring us out!"

chokri: Jü süko thejü kefü kezhoo khivo shecokoce posü, süsa uko sekeyi uni Roman milezi keti meprü shuta.
english: The sergeants reported these words to the magistrates, and they were afraid when they heard that they were Romans,

chokri: Uko tavori de unipo pratano sita, ta nade saco votano zita.
english: and they came and begged them. When they had brought them out, they asked them to depart from the city.

chokri: Uni thejücelü taprari de ta Lydiapü celü vota; uni tavo ukroko ngokeyice uko thi kethepu mu lütho melu shusü de tata.
english: They went out of the prison, and entered into Lydia's house. When they had seen the brothers, they encouraged them, and departed.

chokri: Paul mu Silas ni ta Amphipolis mu Apolonica vathü Thessalonica lü vori, sülü Jews mi Synagogue ba.
english: Now when they had passed through Amphipolis and Apollonia, they came to Thessalonica, where there was a synagogue of the Jews.

chokri: Paul püzhoce bi ta sülü thüma koce vo, deoba dosü sülü uko zü ketho lesi phri kemeliba.
english: Paul, as was his custom, went in to them, and for three Sabbath days reasoned with them from the Scriptures,

chokri: Pücoko posü mu thapü uko meka de Messiah (Khrista) kemezhe süto side kesalü pü rhüla süri keto posü. Hiohi, Jisu (Messiah) zo mu I süo zhopo thipü neko ce süyo.
english: explaining and demonstrating that the Christ had to suffer and rise again from the dead, and saying, "This Jesus, whom I proclaim to you, is the Christ."

chokri: Uko humi süo melüyi, Paul mu Silas ni zü kemekrota, metsü keta Greekmi kekro, mu theno kemelemiko kekro sübi Paul mu Silas ni züta.
english: Some of them were persuaded, and joined Paul and Silas, of the devout Greeks a great multitude, and not a few of the chief women.

chokri: Vele Jews miko hu melü mota, süko uro kenela kehoshü ketako mu mhakebü nyüsa ketako kenguyi, de uko nadeo kebu shusü, side tavo Jason ce rüta, Paul mu Silas ni tüpü methi krolü vo keto kephula.
english: But the unpersuaded Jews took along some wicked men from the marketplace, and gathering a crowd, set the city in an uproar. Assaulting the house of Jason, they sought to bring them out to the people.

chokri: Uko Jason mu kemelü kroko hu kesa tüpü nade kekha yotiko ce vota, semeto de poti, Thüma hiko cejü phu kekhümela ketako hiceri vori bate.
english: When they didn't find them, they dragged Jason and certain brothers before the rulers of the city, crying, "These who have turned the world upside down have come here also,

chokri: Süko Jason mesoyi, Caesar zhoko zü ketherü ketako, kede kekrepü balezi mu süo Jisu.
english: whom Jason has received. These all act contrary to the decrees of Caesar, saying that there is another king, Jesus!"

chokri: Uko nadelü thümako mu kekha kebako kebü bate, uko jü süko se keyila.
english: The multitude and the rulers of the city were troubled when they heard these things.

chokri: Uko Jason mu püzüko sülü (bail) pü keyice uko kha süva.
english: When they had taken security from Jason and the rest, they let them go.

chokri: Süzi kemelümiko Paul mu Silas ni ketsüpü Beroea lü süva, uni sülü vokeyice ta Jews mi synagogue lülü.
english: The brothers immediately sent Paul and Silas away by night to Beroea. When they arrived, they went into the Jewish synagogue.

chokri: Jews mi hilü kebako menuku mu jü reniku, Thessalonica lü kebako ceri; uko unü sa-i de jüko semhamhayi, tüsonha ketholesi phri mu phu, ketho me mo sheri.
english: Now these were more noble than those in Thessalonica, in that they received the word with all readiness of the mind, examining the Scriptures daily to see whether these things were so.

chokri: Süla uko thüma kekro melüyi, Greek mi theno therhoko, mu thepu miko sheri.
english: Many of them therefore believed; also of the prominent Greek women, and not a few men.

chokri: Vele Jews miko Thessalonica lü kebako Paul Jihova jü zhopo Beroea lü kethi sesü de ta sülü ri vorita, side sülüri mhapü kerhüsü.
english: But when the Jews of Thessalonica had knowledge that the word of God was proclaimed by Paul at Beroea also, they came there likewise, agitating the multitudes.

chokri: Rekri kemelü miko Paul ketsüpü jükezho khü süva, vele Silas mu Timothy ni sülü bata.
english: Then the brothers immediately sent out Paul to go as far as to the sea, and Silas and Timothy still stayed there.

chokri: Püchotha ketao püzü zovo Athens ketso sü, Silas mu Timothy nipo mezeyi kevedü tavo püzü kekhrühi tano de po ketala uni ri ta vota.
english: But those who escorted Paul brought him as far as Athens. Receiving a commandment to Silas and Timothy that they should come to him very quickly, they departed.

chokri: Paul Athens lü uni fükebace nade sülü marhe kereshü lebi tho chupü keba ngopü pü kere phita.
english: Now while Paul waited for them at Athens, his spirit was provoked within him as he saw the city full of idols.

chokri: Süla synagogue lü Jews kozü kemeli shusü, nanilü kemetsüko ri sa, mu kehuba (hetho Heli kethiba) kolü thümako zü tüsonha mha kepo.
english: So he reasoned in the synagogue with the Jews and the devout persons, and in the marketplace every day with those who met him.

chokri: Süsa Epicureans (umelü nüko kethi) mhakelü miko mu Stoicks (ukethü kerü) mha kelü miko zü ketü. Uko dolü humi poti, natsimi hio dipü poba? Kekreko poti, pü therü ketekhri ketihu ju pozüle; kekrelamori pü Jisu rhüla kesü zhopo kethila.
english: Some of the Epicurean and Stoic philosophers also were conversing with him. Some said, "What does this babbler want to say?" Others said, "He seems to be advocating foreign deities," because he preached Jesus and the resurrection.

chokri: Uko püzü tavo thethipü Areopagus lü püketso de hakoce posü tome, no dosapü po ketao?
english: They took hold of him, and brought him to the Areopagus, saying, "May we know what this new teaching is, which is spoken by you?

chokri: Kekrelamori no mha ketekhri tipü khipü hako nilü süte, püco süko dipü sheri hako senyüba.
english: For you bring certain strange things to our ears. We want to know therefore what these things mean."

chokri: Kekrelamori Athenians miko mu sozüsove miko sheri utü khapü mhakekrelü sümo de mhapo mori theju kesa reni ti thiyo.
english: Now all the Athenians and the strangers living there spent their time in nothing else, but either to tell or to hear some new thing.

chokri: Süsa Paul Mars Hill metsulü süthayi, de poti, Ne Athens thümako akelüce mhametülü neko therü mpe tayo timi.
english: Paul stood in the middle of the Areopagus, and said, "You men of Athens, I perceive that you are very religious in all things.

chokri: Kekrelamori I ta kevoce neko melü keta kereshübayo hizi thopü kemeta ngo, “Therü sekemoo.” Neko pü semo de reshüyo, süo I neko ce po süzüho.
english: For as I passed along, and observed the objects of your worship, I found also an altar with this inscription: 'TO AN UNKNOWN GOD.' What therefore you worship in ignorance, this I announce to you.

chokri: Jihova cejü thisü, mu sülü ketako metü sheri, Pü türi mu cejüri Niepoo zo, pü ube khi kethi kereshü cekolü bayomo.
english: The God who made the world and all things in it, he, being Lord of heaven and earth, doesn't dwell in temples made with hands,

chokri: Mori ube khapü pü reshü ketiko ri thi yomo, pü uce mhapüyi keto bamo. Mhametü pütho khasü, kerhü sheri, mu usüshü keha sheri.
english: neither is he served by men's hands, as though he needed anything, seeing he himself gives to all life and breath, and all things.

chokri: Mipüyolü shuye metü mehüpü cejü metü chusü, side mipüla püzho chesü mu pü kehü thethi rako rhesü.
english: He made from one blood every nation of men to dwell on all the surface of the earth, having determined appointed seasons, and the boundaries of their dwellings,

chokri: Sizi süze uko püphuyi totho mu püngoyi totho, pü uko zü kemecu ta mozo tila.
english: that they should seek the Lord, if perhaps they might reach out for him and find him, though he is not far from each one of us.

chokri: Kekrelamori upüla hüyo, melü tayo, uthiupho rhüketahi; neko lizolü sheri se keyi “uko sheri pü nuneko zo.”
english: 'For in him we live, and move, and have our being.' As some of your own poets have said, 'For we are also his offspring.'

chokri: Süsübi uko pünuneko zo tila, uko Jihova sü suna, mori rakathi, mori ketsü, thüma tho rhe kesü bitimo.
english: Being then the offspring of God, we ought not to think that the Divine Nature is like gold, or silver, or stone, engraved by art and design of man.

chokri: Thüma mhase kemo pütü süko Jihova seri sekemo bi vorite, vele tsüsahi thüma metüpo thelü rede yite sizi po süte.
english: The times of ignorance therefore God overlooked. But now he commands that all people everywhere should repent,

chokri: Kekrelamori tüso zhopünha cejü kehüko metü une ketito, kemezilü, thümapü po thino ketsü kesüopo; thümakoce po kemetü kesüo sü sata de rhüla sü ketao zo.
english: because he has appointed a day in which he will judge the world in righteousness by the man whom he has ordained; of which he has given assurance to all men, in that he has raised him from the dead."

chokri: Thüma sata de rhüla sü keta ju se keyice humi krü nyüta, vele kekreko poti, theju hio hako hisa reni sato.
english: Now when they heard of the resurrection of the dead, some mocked; but others said, "We want to hear you again concerning this."

chokri: Süla Paul uko dolü ta sala ta.
english: Thus Paul went out from among them.

chokri: Vele humi Paul samejita, side melüyi; süko dolü Dionysius, Areopagus lü kebamipü, theno mipü püza Damaris, mu kekre miko sheri süko zü kesayi.
english: But certain men joined with him, and believed, among whom also was Dionysius the Areopagite, and a woman named Damaris, and others with them.

chokri: Hisalü Paul Athens lü ta Corinth lü vota.
english: After these things Paul departed from Athens, and came to Corinth.

chokri: Sülü mipüza Aquila tipü Pontus lü menuyi, sümhüjüce pücemi Priscilla zü Italy lü voriba. Kekrelamori Claudius Jews metüpo Rome lü tata no po ketala, Paul süla tavo püni zü kesü.
english: He found a certain Jew named Aquila, a man of Pontus by race, who had recently come from Italy, with his wife Priscilla, because Claudius had commanded all the Jews to depart from Rome. He came to them,

chokri: Pü hetho kerhü tila pü tavo uni celü bata, kezüde mhatho thibata; uko mhatho lizo jüfü ce kethimi.
english: and because he practiced the same trade, he lived with them and worked, for by trade they were tent makers.

chokri: Pü sabath tüsonha Synagogues kolü Jews mu Greek miko zü kemeli de uko rethibayo.
english: He reasoned in the synagogue every Sabbath, and persuaded Jews and Greeks.

chokri: Silas mu Timothy ni Macedonia lü tavori, Paul Jews koce Jisu sü Messiah zoketi posüba.
english: But when Silas and Timothy came down from Macedonia, Paul was compelled by the Spirit, testifying to the Jews that Jesus was the Christ.

chokri: Uko pümetsu ketala mu püzhoshu ketala püfüyo keta kerhoko kekhusü de ukoce poti, Neko zapo neko piyo, I zono mesazo, hisaco I Gentiles koce votato.
english: When they opposed him and blasphemed, he shook out his clothing and said to them, "Your blood be on your own heads! I am clean. From now on, I will go to the Gentiles!"

chokri: Pü sülü (synagogue) tata, mipüza Titius Justus lizitio celü vo bata, pü Jihova kereshü mipü tila, puce synagogue zü keduba.
english: He departed there, and went into the house of a certain man named Justus, one who worshiped God, whose house was next door to the synagogue.

chokri: Crispus synagogue lü kezhoo, pü cekro züde Niepoo melüyi, Corinth mi thüma kekro melüyi mu jülo püyi.
english: Crispus, the ruler of the synagogue, believed in the Lord with all his house. Many of the Corinthians, when they heard, believed and were baptized.

chokri: Niepoo tüzülü zümholü Paul cepode, Mha mpe hite, vele jüpo süte mu kemekre ta hite.
english: The Lord said to Paul in the night by a vision, "Don't be afraid, but speak and don't be silent;

chokri: Kekrelamori I nzü bazoho, mipüri nmethi meni vaho, nade hilü athüma kekro ba tila.
english: for I am with you, and no one will attack you to harm you, for I have many people in this city."

chokri: Pü sülü tütsepü mu pütsü bata, uko dolü Jihova jüko pü uko metha sü.
english: He lived there a year and six months, teaching the word of God among them.

chokri: Gallio Achaia lü kezho (proconsul) thiba, Jews miko Paul metsu sürita side pütüzü sheco kezhoo cevo.
english: But when Gallio was proconsul of Achaia, the Jews with one accord rose up against Paul and brought him before the judgment seat,

chokri: Thüma hio mishupü Jews mi zho kemolü Jihova reshüno sizi tate.
english: saying, "This man persuades men to worship God contrary to the law."

chokri: Hice Paul püta chopü posü to kezüce, Gallio Jews koce poti, mhace kekri keti thezho kevarhü mhatho kesü hu lize, I neko Jewko züde mha ketito.
english: But when Paul was about to open his mouth, Gallio said to the Jews, "If indeed it were a matter of wrong or of wicked crime, you Jews, it would be reasonable that I should bear with you;

chokri: Vele jükola mori theza kola, ne thezho rele kola zono nekotho peyite; kekrelamori I sübi ti kola sheco thiho.
english: but if they are questions about words and names and your own law, look to it yourselves. For I don't want to be a judge of these matters."

chokri: Side pü sheco balü uko huva.
english: He drove them from the judgment seat.

chokri: Süsa Greek miko Sosthenes, synagogue lü kezhoo tüpü shecoba mhüjülü vüva. Gallio mhapüri süo la pülüni mota.
english: Then all the Greeks laid hold on Sosthenes, the ruler of the synagogue, and beat him before the judgment seat. Gallio didn't care about any of these things.

chokri: Paul pütühu sülü ba side süsalü pükroko khava, ru thopü Syria lü vota, Priscilla mu Aquila ni sade; Cenchrea lü püpi thava kekrelamori pü reshupü thi meta tila.
english: Paul, having stayed after this many more days, took his leave of the brothers, and sailed from there for Syria, together with Priscilla and Aquila. He shaved his head in Cenchreae, for he had a vow.

chokri: Pü ta Ephesus lü vori, side sülü uni khamezüva, vele pü ta synagogue lülü, de Jews kozü mhakepovo.
english: He came to Ephesus, and he left them there; but he himself entered into the synagogue, and reasoned with the Jews.

chokri: Uko püpo pütühu uko zü mha kepo süno choba, vele pü basa mota.
english: When they asked him to stay with them a longer time, he declined;

chokri: Vele pü uko zü kekhota sürho bate side, ukoce poti, thenye hio I Jerusalem lü ba moze su; vele I sala tavorito nekoce, mhace Jihova medo lize. Süsa pü Ephesus lü ru thopü tata.
english: but taking his leave of them, and saying, "I must by all means keep this coming feast in Jerusalem, but I will return again to you if God wills," he set sail from Ephesus.

chokri: Pü tavo Caesarea tsoyi de ta Jerusalem lü kho tavo kehucelü kebako kelüsü, side ta Antioch lü kita.
english: When he had landed at Caesarea, he went up and greeted the assembly, and went down to Antioch.

chokri: Pütühu sülü ba, süsa ta Galatia mu Phrygia rünako lü vo, side kemelü miko thi kethepu shusü.
english: Having spent some time there, he departed, and went through the region of Galatia, and Phrygia, in order, establishing all the disciples.

chokri: Jew mipü püza Apollo, Alexandria lü pümenuyi, jüpo kese shupü, ketho lesi se meve shu, ta Ephesus lü vori.
english: Now a certain Jew named Apollos, an Alexandrian by race, an eloquent man, came to Ephesus. He was mighty in the Scriptures.

chokri: Thüma hio Niepoo zhorele kolü metha pü ketamipü, pülüni kene shupü, pü kepo keti kolü metsü mu Niepoo zhüko metha menu shuyo, pü John jülo kepüo zhoko kese lebi.
english: This man had been instructed in the way of the Lord; and being fervent in spirit, he spoke and taught accurately the things concerning Jesus, although he knew only the baptism of John.

chokri: Pü thepu shude Synagogue lü jüpo sü, Aquila mu Priscilla ni püfü renisü, uni püzü unice voriyi, side Jihova choko povacho pü pücesü mesa meshosü.
english: He began to speak boldly in the synagogue. But when Priscilla and Aquila heard him, they took him aside, and explained to him the way of God more accurately.

chokri: Pü ta Achaia cholü vota ketoce, kemelü kroko lesi thopü sajiko tsüsü, püzüyi node; Pü ta kevorice tavo uko khrühi shusü, ngokemezhelü melü keyiko.
english: When he had determined to pass over into Achaia, the brothers encouraged him, and wrote to the disciples to receive him. When he had come, he greatly helped those who had believed through grace;

chokri: Kekrelamori pü methi mhüjülü Jewko hüpü ketho lesiko popü uko mekasü, Jisu sü Khristao (Messiah) zo.
english: for he powerfully refuted the Jews, publicly showing by the Scriptures that Jesus was the Christ.

chokri: Apollos Corinth lü kebace Paul ta jükezho khükolü vo, side tavo Ephesus lü kemelümi hu bade ngoyi.
english: It happened that, while Apollos was at Corinth, Paul, having passed through the upper country, came to Ephesus, and found certain disciples.

chokri: Pü ukoce poti, neko melü keyice Kemesa Rüpü ngoyi me? Uko puce poti, hako Kemesa Rüpü tayo ti seri sü mole.
english: He said to them, "Did you receive the Holy Spirit when you believed?" They said to him, "No, we haven't even heard that there is a Holy Spirit."

chokri: Pü posala kesü, neko sopü zalü jülo püyi la? Uko püce pode, hako John Jülo kepü zalüle.
english: He said, "Into what then were you baptized?" They said, "Into John's baptism."

chokri: Süla Paul ukoce pode, John jülo kepü sü, thelü rede keyi jülo khasü, pü poti, mipü asa vori kezüo melü yite sidepo, süosü Jisu Khrista le.
english: Paul said, "John indeed baptized with the baptism of repentance, saying to the people that they should believe in the one who would come after him, that is, in Jesus."

chokri: Uko süo sekeyice Niepoo Jisu zalü jülo püyi.
english: When they heard this, they were baptized in the name of the Lord Jesus.

chokri: Paul pübe chopü ukoyo kesüce, Kemesa Rüpü ukoyo vorita; mu uko saju kopo mu fü kesalü mhapo zita.
english: When Paul had laid his hands on them, the Holy Spirit came on them, and they spoke with other languages and prophesied.

chokri: Thepumi sülü kebako mi keri kena.
english: They were about twelve men in all.

chokri: Pü tekhri sü sülü ba synagogue lü thepu shude jüpo, Jihova kede kethi popü mihü.
english: He entered into the synagogue, and spoke boldly for a period of three months, reasoning and persuading about the things concerning the Kingdom of God.

chokri: Thelü kekhri humi püzü kemelita mu methi kehulü choo po meshü ketala pü sülü kemelü miko zü tata, pü tüsonha Tyrannus hall lü mizü kemeli ba zota.
english: But when some were hardened and disobedient, speaking evil of the Way before the multitude, he departed from them, and separated the disciples, reasoning daily in the school of Tyrannus.

chokri: Tütse kena sülü bata, süla Asia lü kebako metü Niepoo Jisu jü seyi, Jewko sheri mu Greek miko sheri.
english: This continued for two years, so that all those who lived in Asia heard the word of the Lord Jesus, both Jews and Greeks.

chokri: Paul beyolü Jihova mha ungo kekro kerekri thisü.
english: God worked special miracles by the hands of Paul,

chokri: Süla püyo keta hanki (füne) zori mori füne püvo püni kesü zori, khipüvo kethi miko ni kesüsa kethiko menüta mu therü kesüko tata yo.
english: so that even handkerchiefs or aprons were carried away from his body to the sick, and the evil spirits went out.

chokri: Jews mi thüma kesuhu therü kesu huta, kesu rüpü miyo ketako ce poti, Niepoo Jisu zalü huyo, Paul zhopo thi ketao hako poyo.
english: But some of the itinerant Jews, exorcists, took on themselves to name over those who had the evil spirits the name of the Lord Jesus, saying, "We adjure you by Jesus whom Paul preaches."

chokri: Sceva siti mipü pünu thepumi thüna ba, Jewmi, thevo kezhoo, uko sübi thita.
english: There were seven sons of one Sceva, a Jewish chief priest, who did this.

chokri: Kesu rüpü ukofü kela kesü, Jisu I seba, Paul sheri I seba, vele no sopü?
english: The evil spirit answered, "Jesus I know, and Paul I know, but who are you?"

chokri: Mipüyo kesu rüpü kebao, prüsü ukoyo vota, (uko cheta), ukoce kuku, mu uko meniva, süla uko rezayi mu mechü tata (ufüne nrhüta)
english: The man in whom the evil spirit was leaped on them, and overpowered them, and prevailed against them, so that they fled out of that house naked and wounded.

chokri: Theju süo Jews kori mu Greek miko sheri se kesüce, uko ngo thi mu mpeta; Ephesus lü kehüko metü se vota mu Niepoo Jisu zapo kemele shu süta.
english: This became known to all, both Jews and Greeks, who lived at Ephesus. Fear fell on them all, and the name of the Lord Jesus was magnified.

chokri: Melü keyiko tavori de uko phrasü, hijü thi ketako püvo uko mekasü.
english: Many also of those who had believed came, confessing, and declaring their deeds.

chokri: Kemedu ti lesiko pü cadu thi ketako khipü vorita, süko metü pü melü thüva thümako mhüjülü; mhama süko phri kesüce püma rakathi nye hepüngu (50, 000) mesüta.
english: Many of those who practiced magical arts brought their books together and burned them in the sight of all. They counted the price of them, and found it to be fifty thousand pieces of silver.

chokri: Hibide Jihova jü kushü mu ma mekro süta.
english: So the word of the Lord was growing and becoming mighty.

chokri: Mha hiko thi kevasa, Paul Kemesa Rüpülü lükeyi, Macedonia mu Achaia cutha sü Jerusalem lü vo keto rhüta. I sülü vo kevasa Rome lüri vo moze su.
english: Now after these things had ended, Paul determined in the spirit, when he had passed through Macedonia and Achaia, to go to Jerusalem, saying, "After I have been there, I must also see Rome."

chokri: Süla püzü keta mi kena ketsüpü Macedonia lüsü, Timothy mu Erastus ni, vele pü Asia lü pütühu thothü bata.
english: Having sent into Macedonia two of those who ministered to him, Timothy and Erastus, he himself stayed in Asia for a while.

chokri: Sümesü cesü süce mhakebü kebu damo, Choo la.
english: About that time there arose no small stir concerning the Way.

chokri: Kekrelamori mipü püza Demetrius, raka rekremipü, Diana rüo thipü kereshücelü kesüla meve damo tari.
english: For a certain man named Demetrius, a silversmith, who made silver shrines of Artemis, brought no little business to the craftsmen,

chokri: Pü rekremiko cüpü puce süyi de poti, Kezhomiko, neko seba uko cene ngo keyi mhatho hiola.
english: whom he gathered together, with the workmen of like occupation, and said, "Sirs, you know that by this business we have our wealth.

chokri: Süpi sheri neko metü kese kengo, Ephesus lü lebimo, vele Asia metülü, Paul hio thüma kekro thi rede keva, hikopü therü mole zite, uko bepü kethiko.
english: You see and hear, that not at Ephesus alone, but almost throughout all Asia, this Paul has persuaded and turned away many people, saying that they are no gods, that are made with hands.

chokri: Süla uko mhathoko kemethu thita keto kemechelü bate; sülebimo Diana kereshüce sheri mipo thalice va keto lü bate, pü züveko jü mesu vato, Asia lü sheri mu cejülü sheri reshü ketao.
english: Not only is there danger that this our trade come into disrepute, but also that the temple of the great goddess Artemis will be counted as nothing, and her majesty destroyed, whom all Asia and the world worships."

chokri: Uko mha süko se keyice uko lü reda shuta, de se metota, Ephesian mikola Diana sü zho shuwe.
english: When they heard this they were filled with anger, and cried out, saying, "Great is Artemis of the Ephesians!"

chokri: Nade metülü mhakebü phita, mu tavo Gaius mu Aristarchus ni Macedoniami Paul revülü zü vori kebani tüyi, uko metü kezü lüpü thi ta kehu thethilü lüta.
english: The whole city was filled with confusion, and they rushed with one accord into the theater, having seized Gaius and Aristarchus, men of Macedonia, Paul's companions in travel.

chokri: Paul ta sülü voto kesüce kemelü miko püpo lü muta.
english: When Paul wanted to enter in to the people, the disciples didn't allow him.

chokri: Asia lü püzümi kemeleko thüma ketsüpü püce süde püpo kehuthi lü lühino sü.
english: Certain also of the Asiarchs, being his friends, sent to him and begged him not to venture into the theater.

chokri: Humi mhapüpo, kekre miko kekre po side kehu sülü mha kebu shuta, mu metü khrüri kedipüla uko sülü vo kenguyita sheri püco seyi mota.
english: Some therefore cried one thing, and some another, for the assembly was in confusion. Most of them didn't know why they had come together.

chokri: Uko Alexander zitipü makro dolü züprari, Jews miko pü khapü mi mekasü; Alexander pübe chopü mice mhapo tolesü, thümako mhüjülü pümezi potole thayi.
english: They brought Alexander out of the multitude, the Jews putting him forward. Alexander beckoned with his hand, and would have made a defense to the people.

chokri: Vele uko püseti, pü Jew mipü, süla bavüdo kena mesü uko seta de Diana sü Ephesus mikola Kezhoo.
english: But when they perceived that he was a Jew, all with one voice for a time of about two hours cried out, "Great is Artemis of the Ephesians!"

chokri: Rüna kezholü kethoo miko kekha de poti, Ne Ephesus miko, Ephesus miko nadelü kehüko Diana therüpü kereshümi ziti sopü semo bata, marhe süo Jupiter thenyü yo retu kiri?
english: When the town clerk had quieted the multitude, he said, "You men of Ephesus, what man is there who doesn't know that the city of the Ephesians is temple-keeper of the great goddess Artemis, and of the image which fell down from Zeus?

chokri: Süo se kebala mipüri süo metsu keti kepo bamo, neko süla kemekre tate, kekri mhalü kemo mhatho thi hite.
english: Seeing then that these things can't be denied, you ought to be quiet, and to do nothing rash.

chokri: Kekrelamori neko thüma hiko zü vori, uko kehulü mizhü süketimi mo, mori neko rüpü sheri tha zhoshü bamo.
english: For you have brought these men here, who are neither robbers of temples nor blasphemers of your goddess.

chokri: Mhace Demetrius mu pürekreko mhahu panyo ti bali mha de kenyo to lize, thezho hepü meta zo, süla uko po hunehu yo panyo khipü vo shecoko ce umezi poyite.
english: If therefore Demetrius and the craftsmen who are with him have a matter against anyone, the courts are open, and there are proconsuls. Let them press charges against one another.
