chokri: Vele neko mhace mhakekre ketso ba tisa thezho lüno kehulü kepo yito.
english: But if you seek anything about other matters, it will be settled in the regular assembly.

chokri: Kekrelamori uko tha mhakebü keti la uko mejota keto kemeprü lü bate, püco kedi pülü makro kebu keta hio uko mezi poyi motato.
english: For indeed we are in danger of being accused concerning this day's riot, there being no cause. Concerning it, we wouldn't be able to give an account of this commotion."

chokri: Pü hibi po kevasa kehu khava.
english: When he had thus spoken, he dismissed the assembly.

chokri: Pü uko zü kekho ketasa ru thopü mezizo vo Coos (Cos) tsoyi, süsanha ta Rhodes lüvo, sülü ta Patara lü vo.
english: When it happened that we had parted from them and had set sail, we came with a straight course to Cos, and the next day to Rhodes, and from there to Patara.

chokri: Hako rupü Phoenicia lü voto kezü ngoyi de sülü lüyi side ru tho vota.
english: Having found a ship crossing over to Phoenicia, we went aboard, and set sail.

chokri: Hako Cyprus ngoyi, ru süo khava hako veco, de sü ru thopü Syria lü vo de tavo Tyre lü bayi, sülü ru püko khasüva ketola.
english: When we had come in sight of Cyprus, leaving it on the left hand, we sailed to Syria, and landed at Tyre, for there the ship was to unload her cargo.

chokri: Sülü kemelü miko bade ngoyi mu hako sülü zho thüna bata; sülü mipü Rüpü lü saju poti, Paul po Jerusalem lü kho hinota.
english: Having found disciples, we stayed there seven days. These said to Paul through the Spirit, that he should not go up to Jerusalem.

chokri: Tüsozho süko tso ketasa hako ta hako cholü vota, uko metü uneko mu ucemineko züvo hako cholü hako zü vosü, hako nade cuta kemothe; side hako kezü jüpolü chütüyi de kecho thiyi.
english: When it happened that we had accomplished the days, we departed and went on our journey. They all, with wives and children, brought us on our way until we were out of the city. Kneeling down on the beach, we prayed.

chokri: Hako hunehu zü kekho ketasa hako ru lü lüta, mu uko ta uko celü vota.
english: After saying goodbye to each other, we went on board the ship, and they returned home again.

chokri: Hako Tyre lü mhathoko thitso kevasa ta Ptolemais lü vori, de sülü kemelü miko kelu sü de uko zü zhopü bayi.
english: When we had finished the voyage from Tyre, we arrived at Ptolemais. We greeted the brothers, and stayed with them one day.

chokri: Hako Paul züketako süsanha tavo Caesarea tsoyi, ta Philip Evangelist celü vo, pü mi thünako dolü mipü, pü bata.
english: On the next day, we, who were Paul's companions, departed, and came to Caesarea. We entered into the house of Philip the evangelist, who was one of the seven, and stayed with him.

chokri: Pünu reli phosa mi da ba, mu uko metü saju kepomi.
english: Now this man had four virgin daughters who prophesied.

chokri: Hako sülü zho kekro bata, süce Judea lü saju kepo mipü püza Agabus.
english: As we stayed there some days, a certain prophet named Agabus came down from Judea.

chokri: Pü ta hako ce vori, pü Paul chepha püyi de khipü püphe pübeko phayi, de poti, Kemesa Rüpü hizi poyo, Jerusalem lü keba Jewko chepha hio sopü zhü sheri püphapü Gentileko tsüva toshe.
english: Coming to us, and taking Paul's belt, he bound his own feet and hands, and said, "Thus says the Holy Spirit: 'So will the Jews at Jerusalem bind the man who owns this belt, and will deliver him into the hands of the Gentiles.'"

chokri: Mha süko se keyice, hako sheri mu thethi sülü kebako sheri püpo Jerusalem lü kho hitano posü.
english: When we heard these things, both we and they of that place begged him not to go up to Jerusalem.

chokri: Süsa Paul hako ce poti, neko krapü alü thi mesu kesü püco dipü? Kekrelamori atüpü pha keva zo mori I Niepo Jisu zala sa ri süto tazo.
english: Then Paul answered, "What are you doing, weeping and breaking my heart? For I am ready not only to be bound, but also to die at Jerusalem for the name of the Lord Jesus."

chokri: Hako pü valayi ho ketace poti, Niepoo medo thi yite.
english: When he would not be persuaded, we ceased, saying, "The Lord's will be done."

chokri: Tüsozho süko salü hako mhanyoko khipü tasü, de tavo Jerusalem tsoyi.
english: After these days we took up our baggage and went up to Jerusalem.

chokri: Kemelü miko humi Caesarea lü hako züvo mu Cyprus mipü püza Mnason celü bayi, therace melü keyi mipü.
english: Some of the disciples from Caesarea also went with us, bringing one Mnason of Cyprus, an early disciple, with whom we would stay.

chokri: Hako tavo Jerusalem tso keyice, kemelü miko unü shude hako züyi.
english: When we had come to Jerusalem, the brothers received us gladly.

chokri: Süsanha Paul hako züvo James zü kesü, mehi miko metü sülü ba.
english: The day following, Paul went in with us to James; and all the elders were present.

chokri: Pü uko kelu kesüsa, pü yitholü Jihova püzü Gentiles miko dolü mhathi kesüko pü ukoce posü.
english: When he had greeted them, he reported one by one the things which God had worked among the Gentiles through his ministry.

chokri: Uko süko sesüde Niepoo zapozale pota, uko puce poti, asazüo, no se mu ngoba, thüma nye kekro melü keyiko metüri thezholü urha thi ketakozo.
english: They, when they heard it, glorified God. They said to him, "You see, brother, how many thousands there are among the Jews of those who have believed, and they are all zealous for the law.

chokri: Uko nju poti, no Jews miko Gentiles miko dolü hü ketakoce potale siti, Moses zhoko kha vate, neko nuneko yirü hite, mori thezho süko züri hite.
english: They have been informed about you, that you teach all the Jews who are among the Gentiles to forsake Moses, telling them not to circumcise their children neither to walk after the customs.

chokri: Dipü bita? Makropo vori kenguyi moze sutho, kekrelamori no vori keba uko se süte.
english: What then? The assembly must certainly meet, for they will hear that you have come.

chokri: Süla hako nce kepoko thi süte, hako dolü mida reshu ti pü bate.
english: Therefore do what we tell you. We have four men who have taken a vow.

chokri: No talü uko zü nthi kemesa yite, uko pe menu yite, uko pi tha vato, ukopo se keto miko n yo mhapo kebako kethomo ziti seyi ketola, vele no kemezilü tazo mu thezhoko zütazo keti seyi ketola.
english: Take them, and purify yourself with them, and pay their expenses for them, that they may shave their heads. Then all will know that there is no truth in the things that they have been informed about you, but that you yourself also walk keeping the law.

chokri: Gentiles kemelü miko la zo lize, uko lesi thopü uko tsü süve, ukopo thajü reshü ketako hitano, mu mhapü therü reshü ketako, keropü chokhri tiko mu thüzako ti hino, kerügüko thi hino posüte.
english: But concerning the Gentiles who believe, we have written our decision that they should observe no such thing, except that they should keep themselves from food offered to idols, from blood, from strangled things, and from sexual immorality."

chokri: Süsa Paul thümako züyi de süko zü püthi kemesayi, mu süsanha uko kereshücelü lü, püthi kemesa tsü keyi zasi, uko mipüpü la kereshü khasü ketotü.
english: Then Paul took the men, and the next day, purified himself and went with them into the temple, declaring the fulfillment of the days of purification, until the offering was offered for every one of them.

chokri: Tüso zho thüna tso ketasa Jews miko Asia lü ketako kereshücelü püngo sü, thümako pükerhüsü, side uko püvüsü.
english: When the seven days were almost completed, the Jews from Asia, when they saw him in the temple, stirred up all the multitude and laid hands on him,

chokri: Se metode poti, Israel miko, tavorite thüma hiohi, thüma metü methapü thümako zü kemetsu ketao, thezho varhü, mu thethi hio sheri metsu, sülebimo ri pü Greek miko züpü kereshücelü lüde thi merho bate.
english: crying out, "Men of Israel, help! This is the man who teaches all men everywhere against the people, and the law, and this place. Moreover, he also brought Greeks into the temple, and has defiled this holy place!"

chokri: Kekrelamori hi mhüjü uko Trophimus Ephesian mipü püzü keta ngosü, uko kelüce Paul thüma süo zü kereshücelü lü keta la.
english: For they had seen Trophimus, the Ephesian, with him in the city, and they supposed that Paul had brought him into the temple.

chokri: Nade metü melüta, thümako kezüde ta vorita, Paul tüyi, pü tüpü kereshü cekhü kegüdüva, side cekhako khava.
english: All the city was moved, and the people ran together. They seized Paul and dragged him out of the temple. Immediately the doors were shut.

chokri: Uko pü dokhri va tozüta, theju süo sevo thipahe krapi kezhoo tsota, Jerusalem nade metülü kebu ketala.
english: As they were trying to kill him, news came up to the commanding officer of the regiment that all Jerusalem was in an uproar.

chokri: Rekri thipaheko mu kezhoko züde ta ukoce vo, uko dolü krapi kezhoo mu thipaheko ngo kesüce, uko Paul vüsa mota.
english: Immediately he took soldiers and centurions, and ran down to them. They, when they saw the chief captain and the soldiers, stopped beating Paul.

chokri: Süsa krapi kezhoo taphe pü menoyi, de püpüyi, thegi ro kenapü pü phayi no posü, pü sopü sheri ketso, mu pü dipü thiva sheri.
english: Then the commanding officer came near, arrested him, commanded him to be bound with two chains, and inquired who he was and what he had done.

chokri: Thüma krolü humi mhapü po mu kekremiko kekrepüpo, pü makrola mhapüri se kemetsüyi mota, pü püde kezhomi ce pükeba thilü vota no posü.
english: Some shouted one thing, and some another, among the crowd. When he couldn't find out the truth because of the noise, he commanded him to be brought into the barracks.

chokri: Pü ta cekerekrülü kho keyice, thipaheko pü kekripü khosü thümako mhakebu shu ketala.
english: When he came to the stairs, it happened that he was carried by the soldiers because of the violence of the crowd;

chokri: Kekrelamori makro püsamejide se ketako poti, “püpo tatate” (mori pü dokhri vate)
english: for the multitude of the people followed after, crying out, "Away with him!"

chokri: Paul zü kezhomi celü vokeyice, pü krapi kezhoo ce poti, apo nce mhapo yi veme? Püjü kela kesü, No Greek mi jü poyi veme?
english: As Paul was about to be brought into the barracks, he asked the commanding officer, "May I speak to you?" He said, "Do you know Greek?

chokri: No Egypt mipütsü mome? Himhüjüce mhakebu kesüo, pü mhakebu kesüsa kedokhrimi mi nye da ketsüpü nhatsalü sü kevao mome?
english: Aren't you then the Egyptian, who before these days stirred up to sedition and led out into the wilderness the four thousand men of the Assassins?"

chokri: Vele Paul poti I Jew mipü, I Tarsus lü kevorimi, Cilicia lü nadepü, nade süo melekemo pümo, I nce choyo apo thümako ce jü posüte.
english: But Paul said, "I am a Jew, from Tarsus in Cilicia, a citizen of no insignificant city. I beg you, allow me to speak to the people."

chokri: Püpo jüpoyi keve thisü, metha Paul pheniejulü thayi de thümakopo kemekrede püfü reniyi no pübe chopü uko kekhasü. Süsa thümako kemekre züzi bata, pü Hebrew mi meyilü jü posü.
english: When he had given him permission, Paul, standing on the stairs, beckoned with his hand to the people. When there was a great silence, he spoke to them in the Hebrew language, saying,

chokri: Pü po kesü, thümako, asazüjürako, uponeko, amezi tsühi nekoce apo po kesü reni süte.
english: "Brothers and fathers, listen to the defense which I now make to you."

chokri: Uko pü Hebrew jülü po ketala uko kemekre zü zikuta.
english: When they heard that he spoke to them in the Hebrew language, they were even more quiet. He said,

chokri: Pü poti, I Jew thüma püzo, Tarsus lü amenuyi, Cilicia lü nadepü, sizori I Gamaliel phece ametha püyi, uponeko zho rele vetsütso ti pü amethasü, Jihova ceco arha thi ketamipü neko tsüdo thi kebako bi.
english: "I am indeed a Jew, born in Tarsus of Cilicia, but brought up in this city at the feet of Gamaliel, instructed according to the strict manner of the law of our fathers, being zealous for God, even as you all are this day.

chokri: I mimethi meni de dokhri mu phapü thejü cekolü sü thepu mi sheri mu thenomi sheri.
english: I persecuted this Way to the death, binding and delivering into prisons both men and women.

chokri: Thevo kezhoo sheri azhopo süo seba, mu mehiko metü sheri; I uko celü lesi kethoko püyi, side ta Damascus lü vo, uko tüphapü Jerusalem lü vori tolezino, uko mejo ketola.
english: As also the high priest and all the council of the elders testify, from whom also I received letters to the brothers, and traveled to Damascus to bring them also who were there to Jerusalem in bonds to be punished.

chokri: I revü vo kezüce kehetülu Damascus meno vorita, metha kevepü tüyo ava vekrü akhü metülü.
english: It happened that, as I made my journey, and came close to Damascus, about noon, suddenly there shone from the sky a great light around me.

chokri: I retu cejü kita, mhafüpü ace poti, Saul, Saul, dipüla no (amethimeni) ameshu ta?
english: I fell to the ground, and heard a voice saying to me, 'Saul, Saul, why are you persecuting me?'

chokri: I püfü kelakesü, Niepoo no sopü? Pü ace poti, I Jisu Nazareth mi, no pü methi ketao
english: I answered, 'Who are you, Lord?' He said to me, 'I am Jesus of Nazareth, whom you persecute.'

chokri: Azü ketako keve süo ngo mu mpe ta, vele uko füphra ace kepoko semo.
english: "Those who were with me indeed saw the light and were afraid, but they didn't understand the voice of him who spoke to me.

chokri: I poti, Niepoo apo kedipü thita? Niepoo ace poti, Tasüte, ta Damascus lü kite, sülü npo thi ketoko metüpü nce posüto.
english: I said, 'What shall I do, Lord?' The Lord said to me, 'Arise, and go into Damascus. There you will be told about all things which are appointed for you to do.'

chokri: I mhapüri ngo mota zapozale keve süola, azü revü ketakopo abe cepü Damascus lü ki.
english: When I couldn't see for the glory of that light, being led by the hand of those who were with me, I came into Damascus.

chokri: Ananias thezholü hümetsü ketamipü, Jews mi sülü kehüko püpo meve yo tipü.
english: One Ananias, a devout man according to the law, well reported of by all the Jews who lived in Damascus,

chokri: Ace vori, thayi de acepoti, asazüo Saul, mhangoyi tate. Sütülü I mhangoyi, püpesü.
english: came to me, and standing by me said to me, 'Brother Saul, receive your sight!' In that very hour I looked up at him.

chokri: Pü poti, upotsa neko Rüo I kedapü pümedo seyi, mu npo Kejutho Kemesa-o ngo mu püfü se süte.
english: He said, 'The God of our fathers has appointed you to know his will, and to see the Righteous One, and to hear a voice from his mouth.

chokri: Kekrelamori npo thüma metüko ce nkese kengo pü zhopo thisü ketola.
english: For you will be a witness for him to all men of what you have seen and heard.

chokri: No dipüla thothü bata? Tasü de jülo püyi tate, side nkephomako khrü kemesa vate, Niepoo za cüpü.
english: Now why do you wait? Arise, be baptized, and wash away your sins, calling on the name of the Lord.'

chokri: I sala ta Jerusalem lü vode kereshücelü kecho thi kebace, I züda rengu ta.
english: "It happened that, when I had returned to Jerusalem, and while I prayed in the temple, I fell into a trance,

chokri: I Jisu ngosü, pü ace poti, rekri tasüte de tamhazi Jerusalem cupra tate kekrelamori uko no azhopo kethi reni yiho tila.
english: and saw him saying to me, 'Hurry and get out of Jerusalem quickly, because they will not receive testimony concerning me from you.'

chokri: Niepoo I Synagogues kolü nkemelü miko vü mu tüpü thejü kethi uko metü seba.
english: I said, 'Lord, they themselves know that I imprisoned and beat in every synagogue those who believed in you.

chokri: Stephen dokhri püza mekrü kebace I ri sülü tha, püpo satari ve keti amedo ketala, pü kedokhriko füne khapü ace mezü.
english: When the blood of Stephen, your witness, was shed, I also was standing by, and consenting to his death, and guarding the cloaks of those who killed him.'

chokri: Pü acepoti, Rede tapra tate, kekrelamori I nketsüpü thethi kemecura Gentiles koce sü ketola.
english: "He said to me, 'Depart, for I will send you out far from here to the Gentiles.'"

chokri: Jü hiko po keba ketso uko kemekre de püfü reniyi, süsa uko se meto de poti, thüma hio po cejülü tatate, püpo hü keba mesü mote.
english: They listened to him until he said that; then they lifted up their voice, and said, "Rid the earth of this fellow, for he isn't fit to live!"

chokri: Uko se mu ufüko kelhu, süsa nyopha kelhupü tükhralü sü zita.
english: As they cried out, and threw off their cloaks, and threw dust into the air,

chokri: Krapi kezhoo püzü kezhomi celü votano posü, püvü de pü ketso süno posü, sübi kedipüla uko pü metsu bateshi keti seyi ketola.
english: the commanding officer commanded him to be brought into the barracks, ordering him to be examined by scourging, that he might know for what crime they shouted against him like that.

chokri: Uko thiyi pü kero kethiko khi püpha kebace, Paul krapi kezhoo tha kebaoce poti, Roman thüma mejo kemopü vüde ketso ti thezho me?
english: When they had tied him up with thongs, Paul asked the centurion who stood by, "Is it lawful for you to scourge a man who is a Roman, and not found guilty?"

chokri: Krapi kezhoo süse kesüce tavo ukopi kezho-oce posü, “Menu yite, no thi kebako, thüma hio Roman mipü tila.”
english: When the centurion heard it, he went to the commanding officer and told him, "Watch what you are about to do, for this man is a Roman!"

chokri: Uko pi kezhoo tavori de püce poti, Ace posüte, no Roman mi me? Pü posala sü, Ile.
english: The commanding officer came and asked him, "Tell me, are you a Roman?" He said, "Yes."

chokri: Kezhoo puce pode, I presa kekropü ukekha kedi hio khriyi. Paul puce po kesü, Vele I kekha kedilü a menuyi.
english: The commanding officer answered, "I bought my citizenship for a great price." Paul said, "But I was born a Roman."

chokri: Rekri pükha süva de tata, pü ketso tokebalü, mu kezhoo ri mpeta kekrelamori pü tüpü pha kevala, pü Roman le ziti se keyi sa.
english: Immediately those who were about to examine him departed from him, and the commanding officer also was afraid when he realized that he was a Roman, because he had bound him.

chokri: Süsanha dipüla Jews miko Paul mejo ta sheri seyi ketola pü phrüsü side thevo kezhoo mu Council lü kebako metüpo vorino posü, Paul züvori uko mhüjülü sü.
english: But on the next day, desiring to know the truth about why he was accused by the Jews, he freed him from the bonds, and commanded the chief priests and all the council to come together, and brought Paul down and set him before them.

chokri: Paul Council thümako pe türü sü de poti, thümako mu sazüjürako, I tha ketso kematho mu kemezilü hü tazo Jihova mhüjülü.
english: Paul, looking steadfastly at the council, said, "Brothers, I have lived before God in all good conscience until this day."

chokri: Anania, thevo kezho-opükhüce kebakoce po, püta vüsüno posü.
english: The high priest, Ananias, commanded those who stood by him to strike him on the mouth.

chokri: Süsa Paul poti, Jihova po nvüsüte, no sazho kemegao; kekrelamori no asheco thiketola bala, thezho kemolü avüsüno no pobale?
english: Then Paul said to him, "God will strike you, you whitewashed wall! Do you sit to judge me according to the law, and command me to be struck contrary to the law?"

chokri: Pükhüce kethako poti, no Therü thevo kezhoo meli tome?
english: Those who stood by said, "Do you malign God's high priest?"

chokri: Süsa Paul pode, asazüjürako, pü thevo kezhoo leti I sebamo; kekrelamori thopü meta ti nethüma kekhayo timiyo jü kesu pohi.
english: Paul said, "I didn't know, brothers, that he was high priest. For it is written, 'You shall not speak evil of a ruler of your people.'"

chokri: Vele Paul Saducees kori püco ba mu Pharisees kori kekreco bati lüyi, süla pü füphra meto de kehulü poti, Thümako mu sazüjürako, I Pharisee mipü, mu apo sheri Pharisee mipü; kesami rhüla kesü kemeri keba hiola tha ashe kepoba.
english: But when Paul perceived that the one part were Sadducees and the other Pharisees, he cried out in the council, "Men and brothers, I am a Pharisee, a son of Pharisees. Concerning the hope and resurrection of the dead I am being judged!"

chokri: Pü süpo kesüce Sadduceeko mu Phariseeko dolü kelakre sürita, side makro keza kena thita.
english: When he had said this, an argument arose between the Pharisees and Sadducees, and the assembly was divided.

chokri: Kekrelamori Sadduceeko kelü kesami rhüla kesü bamo, mori tüyo choyimi sheri, mori rüpü zo sheri, vele Phariseeko süko melüyo.
english: For the Sadducees say that there is no resurrection, nor angel, nor spirit; but the Pharisees confess all of these.

chokri: Uko dolü kebu metota, Pharisees lesikeseko kropü süthade poti, Hako thüma hioyo mha kesu ngoyimo, vele rüpü mori tüyo choyimi püce mhapo lize uko Therümi zü kezhe bahi khe.
english: A great clamor arose, and some of the scribes of the Pharisees part stood up, and contended, saying, "We find no evil in this man. But if a spirit or angel has spoken to him, let's not fight against God!"

chokri: Uko dolü kelakre shu ketala, thipahe kezhoo mpeta, Paul cethüphapha va ri sehomo, süla thipaheko ketsüsü de kemuthi uko dolü Paul züyi tano posü, de püzü kezho micelü khotano posü.
english: When a great argument arose, the commanding officer, fearing that Paul would be torn in pieces by them, commanded the soldiers to go down and take him by force from among them, and bring him into the barracks.

chokri: Süzi tüzülü Niepoo püce thade poti, Nnü yite, Paul, kekrelamori no Jerusalem lü azhopo thisü, sübi Rome lü sheri azhopo thisü mozesu.
english: The following night, the Lord stood by him, and said, "Cheer up, Paul, for as you have testified about me at Jerusalem, so you must testify also at Rome."

chokri: Tüso kebace, Jews miko humi kezü kropü thiyi de rephri reshu keti thipü bata, Paul dokhri va kemothe uko mhati mhakra hole zide.
english: When it was day, some of the Jews banded together, and bound themselves under a curse, saying that they would neither eat nor drink until they had killed Paul.

chokri: Uko thüma heda kezü mekhrü mhatho hihi thipü bata.
english: There were more than forty people who had made this conspiracy.

chokri: Uko ta thevo kezhoo ce vo poti, hako reshu thipü bate Paul dokhri va kemothe hako mhapüri tiho.
english: They came to the chief priests and the elders, and said, "We have bound ourselves under a great curse, to taste nothing until we have killed Paul.

chokri: Tsühi, neko Council lü thipahe krapi kezhoo ce mhahu ketso menu sasü moze kesula leno sodo püzü kirite, pü cholü ta kiri tice hako pü dokhri va tomo.
english: Now therefore, you with the council inform the commanding officer that he should bring him down to you tomorrow, as though you were going to judge his case more exactly. We are ready to kill him before he comes near."

chokri: Paul lipü thepu mipü süko püfüla ba keta sesü, pü ta kezhomi celü khode süopü posü.
english: But Paul's sister's son heard of their lying in wait, and he came and entered into the barracks and told Paul.

chokri: Paul süo sekeyice, thejü fükebao cüpü püce süyi de poti, natsimi hio züpüvo thipahe krapi kezhoo ce süte, kekrelamori pü püce mhahu posü keto ba tila.
english: Paul summoned one of the centurions, and said, "Bring this young man to the commanding officer, for he has something to tell him."

chokri: Süla püzü tavo krapi kezhoo cesü, Paul acüsü de apo natsi hio züpüvo nce mhahupo süno bale.
english: So he took him, and brought him to the commanding officer, and said, "Paul, the prisoner, summoned me and asked me to bring this young man to you, who has something to tell you."

chokri: Süsa kezhoo pübe ceyi, de züpü thüma kedira vota, püce poti, mhakedipü ace posü keto bala?
english: The commanding officer took him by the hand, and going aside, asked him privately, "What is it that you have to tell me?"

chokri: Püpoti, sodo Jews miko npo Paul züde kehulü vono ziti uko medo bale, uko püce mha ketso kemesa va ketola.
english: He said, "The Jews have agreed to ask you to bring Paul down to the council tomorrow, as though intending to inquire somewhat more accurately concerning him.

chokri: Vele no uko medo zü hite, kekrelamori uko mi heda kelepü rizo thiba, uko reshu tipü bate, uko pü dokhri va kemothe mhati mhakra hole zino, uko dojüpü fü bate, uko nmedo ketso ketolü bate.
english: Therefore don't yield to them, for more than forty men lie in wait for him, who have bound themselves under a curse neither to eat nor to drink until they have killed him. Now they are ready, looking for the promise from you."

chokri: Süsa kezhoo püpo tata, no azü kesü keti mha hiko pü mipü ceri po hite, sizi posü.
english: So the commanding officer let the young man go, charging him, "Tell no one that you have revealed these things to me."

chokri: Kefümi mikena cüsü de poti, thipahemi kra kena mu gura pahe mi hethüna, mu rengu kepümi kra kena, tüzülükeba thecüce (sü) ta Caesarea lü khoto la dojü yite.
english: He called to himself two of the centurions, and said, "Prepare two hundred soldiers to go as far as Caesarea, with seventy horsemen, and two hundred men armed with spears, at the third hour of the night."

chokri: Khonuko khapü Paul po ta ketola kesü yiye, side püpo tavo Felix Angh zü kesü ketola.
english: He asked them to provide animals, that they might set Paul on one, and bring him safely to Felix the governor.

chokri: Pü lesipü hibide thosü.
english: He wrote a letter like this:

chokri: Claudius Lysias lesi thopü Felix Angh kevethoo kelu kesü.
english: "Claudius Lysias to the most excellent governor Felix: Greetings.

chokri: Thüma hio Jews miko celü khiyi, uko pü dokhri vato kezüce, I thipaheko züvo pü kelayi, pü Roman miti se keyila.
english: "This man was seized by the Jews, and was about to be killed by them, when I came with the soldiers and rescued him, having learned that he was a Roman.

chokri: I püco dipüla sizi bata sheri seyi ketola püzü kehulü vo.
english: Desiring to know the cause why they accused him, I brought him down to their council.

chokri: I sekeyi püco oko zhoko la, vele mhapü lüri pü mejo keva mori sata keshü ngomo.
english: I found him to be accused about questions of their law, but to have nothing laid to his charge worthy of death or of bonds.

chokri: Jews miko thümao riba keta seyi, rekri I pü ketsüpü nce süzüho, pümejo kebako pori ta nce vori de kedipü la bashi ti nmhüjülü posü ketola. Vebate.
english: When I was told that the Jews lay in wait for the man, I sent him to you immediately, charging his accusers also to bring their accusations against him before you. Farewell."

chokri: Thipahe ketsü kesüko tüzülü Paul züde tavo Antipatris tsoyi.
english: So the soldiers, carrying out their orders, took Paul and brought him by night to Antipatris.

chokri: Süsanha gura paheko po püzü vota, vele kekreko sala uko thilü (thipahe kebathi) vota.
english: But on the next day they left the horsemen to go with him, and returned to the barracks.

chokri: Uko ta Caesarea lü kevorice lesi thopü ketao khapü Angh tsüsü, mu Paul sheri khapü pü mhüjülü sü.
english: When they came to Caesarea and delivered the letter to the governor, they also presented Paul to him.

chokri: Angh lesio phrisü, mu pü State dipülü vori sheri ketso. Pü sekeyi Cilicia mile zi.
english: When the governor had read it, he asked what province he was from. When he understood that he was from Cilicia, he said,

chokri: N mejo kebako vori keyi sa I nfü reni süto. Side pü khapü Herod jünyo keti keto celü meba.
english: "I will hear you fully when your accusers also arrive." He commanded that he be kept in Herod's palace.

chokri: Tüsozho püngu sa Anania takiri, mehi miko ri sa, mu jüpo kese mipü Tertullus uko Paul kemejo jüko khi posü.
english: After five days, the high priest, Ananias, came down with certain elders and an orator, one Tertullus. They informed the governor against Paul.

chokri: Pücü kesüce Tertullus pü kemejo po shüdüta, pü poti, Keveo, nla hako pütü kecho kezükevelü vori, nkebala mhatho keve thipü rüna kolü sü.
english: When he was called, Tertullus began to accuse him, saying, "Seeing that by you we enjoy much peace, and that excellent measures are coming to this nation,

chokri: Hako mhatho süko hakonü de züvori, thethi metülü, nmeza shu zode.
english: we accept it in all ways and in all places, most excellent Felix, with all thankfulness.

chokri: Vele ntü kha mecho mo ketola, hako phi mezhe zode holini ta mode hako jü kedopü reni sü tome siti choyo.
english: But, that I don't delay you, I entreat you to bear with us and hear a few words.

chokri: Kekrelamori thüma hio mha kebu keta tüsa therü khopü, Jews miko dophro kekre kesü, cejü metülü Jews miko kebalü, kebü keti krüta Nazarene mi kro ketsüpü.
english: For we have found this man to be a plague, an instigator of insurrections among all the Jews throughout the world, and a ringleader of the sect of the Nazarenes.

chokri: Pü kereshüce sheri thi merho, mu hako pü tüpü hako zholü keti nyüba.
english: He even tried to profane the temple, and we arrested him.

chokri: Vele Lysia lü thipahe krapi kezho ta hako ce vori, hako kemu thi hako belü pü tüzü tate
english: But the chief captain Lysias came upon us, and with great violence took him away out of our hands,

chokri: Pükemejoko po nce vori tano po, no ntho sheri mha hiko seyito, dipüla hako pü mejo ta sheri.
english: By examining him yourself you may ascertain all these things of which we accuse him."

chokri: Jews miko kebako metü süsü kethozo sita.
english: The Jews also joined in the attack, affirming that these things were so.

chokri: Angh pübepü püpo jüpo süno thasü, Paul süsa po kesü, I sekeba no pütü kecho shuye hiola sheco thita te, I anü shude mhapopü kela süto.
english: When the governor had beckoned to him to speak, Paul answered, "Because I know that you have been a judge of this nation for many years, I cheerfully make my defense,

chokri: No sebata metse, I zho kerikena vo ketace, Jerusalem lü kho kereshü thi ketola.
english: seeing that you can recognize that it is not more than twelve days since I went up to worship at Jerusalem.

chokri: Kereshücelü I mipüri züde kemeliri ba uko ngomo, mori mipüri hüpü mha kebüri bamo, synagogue lü sheri mori nadelü sheri.
english: In the temple they didn't find me disputing with anyone or stirring up a crowd, either in the synagogues, or in the city.

chokri: Tsühi amejo keba uko mhapüri seli ngomo.
english: Nor can they prove to you the things of which they now accuse me.

chokri: Vele uko po keba I nani khakeva pü amejo, I apotsaneko reshü keta Jihova reshütazo, mhametü thopü kemetako melü mu thezho saju kepoko thopü kemetako.
english: But this I confess to you, that after the Way, which they call a sect, so I serve the God of our fathers, believing all things which are according to the law, and which are written in the prophets;

chokri: Jihova celü akemeri ta, uko sheri süko khamo, kesami rhülakesü ba, kemezimi sheri mu matho kemoko sheri.
english: having hope toward God, which these also themselves look for, that there will be a resurrection of the dead, both of the just and unjust.

chokri: A kehülü Jihova sheri mu thüma sheri nümo thimo keto la mhalü tayo.
english: Herein I also practice always having a conscience void of offense toward God and men.

chokri: Tsühi süze, tütse kekro sa mhakhapü ashuye tsüsü ketola mhakengupü I tavo rizü.
english: Now after some years, I came to bring gifts for the needy to my nation, and offerings;

chokri: Jews miko humi Asia lü tavori kereshücelü athi kemesa keba ngoyi, makro sheri bamo mori mhakebu keti sheri azü bamo.
english: amid which certain Jews from Asia found me purified in the temple, not with a mob, nor with turmoil.

chokri: Uko nmhüjülü hice vori ti mesü, uko mhace mhahu amejo keti ba tisa.
english: They ought to have been here before you, and to make accusation, if they had anything against me.

chokri: Mori hiko kedipü I thikekri ba ngo sheri I kehu mhüjülü kebace.
english: Or else let these men themselves say what injustice they found in me when I stood before the council,

chokri: Kesami rhüla kesü kepola zokemosa I tha nmhüjülü tha de ane kepo kebahi.
english: unless it is for this one thing that I cried standing among them, 'Concerning the resurrection of the dead I am being judged before you today!'"

chokri: Felix süko se kemesa kesüsa pü poti, pü uko khapü thela thiva, de poti, Lysia krapi kezhoo takiri süze I pücelü nju se kemesa yito.
english: But Felix, having more exact knowledge concerning the Way, deferred them, saying, "When Lysias, the commanding officer, comes down, I will decide your case."

chokri: Pü kefümipü cepoti, püpo pümedo thiyi keve pü pütsü süte, vele püzümiko püce kevoriko kha hite.
english: He ordered the centurion that Paul should be kept in custody, and should have some privileges, and not to forbid any of his friends to serve him or to visit him.

chokri: Pütü husa Felix pücemi Drusilla zü vori, theno süpü Jewmi, pü Paul cüsü, side Khrista kemelü reniyi.
english: But after some days, Felix came with Drusilla, his wife, who was a Jewess, and sent for Paul, and heard him concerning the faith in Christ Jesus.

chokri: Pü kematho, ukethükerü keti, mu hisa jünyo keti keto püco po kesüce, Felix mpeta de püce poti, Ta tate, Tsühila, I pütü uzhoti ngoyi süze ncü süto.
english: As he reasoned about righteousness, self-control, and the judgment to come, Felix was terrified, and answered, "Go your way for this time, and when it is convenient for me, I will summon you."

chokri: Pü kemerice Paul presa khapü pütsüsü keto lüba, size pü khasü ketola; süla tsükenu pü miketsüpü pü rethi bayo.
english: Meanwhile, he also hoped that money would be given to him by Paul, that he might release him. Therefore also he sent for him more often, and talked with him.

chokri: Tütse kena vota, Felix vale Porcius Festus tavo rita, Jews miko nü thi ketola Felix Paul khapü thejü thi mezüta.
english: But when two years were fulfilled, Felix was succeeded by Porcius Festus, and desiring to gain favor with the Jews, Felix left Paul in bonds.

chokri: Tsüzo Festus ta State lü vorita, zhosü salü pü Caesarea lü ta Jerusalem lü vori.
english: Festus therefore, having come into the province, after three days went up to Jerusalem from Caesarea.

chokri: Süsa thevo kezhoo mu Jews dolü kemeleko tavo püce Paul metsu keti posü, mu tüshe chosü.
english: Then the high priest and the principal men of the Jews informed him against Paul, and they begged him,

chokri: Püpo uko nüthisü de Paul metsu ketola, püzü Jerusalem lü kino zisü, size uko cholü pü fü ba pü dokhri va ketola.
english: asking a favor against him, that he would summon him to Jerusalem; plotting to kill him on the way.

chokri: Vele Festus ukoce poti, Paul khapü Caesarea lü metamo zesu, pü rekri ta sülü vo ketola.
english: However Festus answered that Paul should be kept in custody at Caesarea, and that he himself was about to depart shortly.

chokri: Süla neko dolü humi azü takite pü mejo keto mha kesuhu püyo mhace ba süze.
english: "Let them therefore," said he, "that are in power among you go down with me, and if there is anything wrong in the man, let them accuse him."

chokri: Pü tüsozho keri pü uko dolü relüba, süsa pü ta Caesarea lüki, süsanha tavo pü jünyo keti balü bayi de Paul züvorino posü.
english: When he had stayed among them more than ten days, he went down to Caesarea, and on the next day he sat on the judgment seat, and commanded Paul to be brought.

chokri: Pü tavori tice Jews miko Jerusalem lü kiri tiko tavo pü metuyi, side Paul yo mha kesu kekropü pümejosü, süko kethozo keti bamozo sheri.
english: When he had come, the Jews who had come down from Jerusalem stood around him, bringing against him many and grievous charges which they could not prove,

chokri: Pütho pümezi kepolü, Jews mithezho püri varhümo, mori kereshüce sheri thi merhomo, mori Caesar sheri metsu mo, mhapü lüri I kevarhü thimo.
english: while he said in his defense, "Neither against the law of the Jews, nor against the temple, nor against Caesar, have I sinned at all."

chokri: Vele Festus Jews miko nü thinyüta, de Paul ketso, no Jerusalem lü kho tome? Mha hiko amhüjülü keti ketola?
english: But Festus, desiring to gain favor with the Jews, answered Paul and said, "Are you willing to go up to Jerusalem, and be judged by me there concerning these things?"

chokri: Süsa Paul poti, I Caesar sheco bayo tha, sülü ashe keti kesü mesü, I Jews mila mhakesu püri thimo ti no setüba.
english: But Paul said, "I am standing before Caesar's judgment seat, where I ought to be tried. I have done no wrong to the Jews, as you also know very well.

chokri: Kekrelamori I mhace kejo hu thiva sakeshü thita süze I sata sheri mumo, vele mhace uko amejo keba ngoyi moze, thüma püri akhapü uko tsü vaho, I Caesar ce choyo.
english: For if I have done wrong, and have committed anything worthy of death, I don't refuse to die; but if none of those things is true that they accuse me of, no one can give me up to them. I appeal to Caesar!"

chokri: Süsa Festus Council zü kevasesü de poti, no Caesar cecho kebabi, no Caesar ce kho tato.
english: Then Festus, when he had conferred with the council, answered, "You have appealed to Caesar. To Caesar you shall go."

chokri: Tüsozho husa Agrippa kedeo mu Bernice ta Caesarea lü kiri Festus kelü ketola.
english: Now when some days had passed, Agrippa the King and Bernice arrived at Caesarea, and greeted Festus.

chokri: Tüsozho kekro sülü bade Festus Paul ju popü kedeo ce sü, Felix thümapü phapü khametale.
english: As he stayed there many days, Festus laid Paul's case before the king, saying, "There is a certain man left a prisoner by Felix;

chokri: I Jerusalem lü kebace Jews thevoko mu mehiko ace poti, uko pü mejo va nyü bale.
english: about whom, when I was at Jerusalem, the chief priests and the elders of the Jews informed me, asking for a sentence against him.

chokri: I ukoce po kesü, pü mesa va keto mhüjü püpo pümezi pomo de mesa keva sü Roman zhomo, pükemejoko zü mhotsu kesü de mhatho kesu dipü thiva sheri popü mejo sü kemosa.
english: To whom I answered that it is not the custom of the Romans to give up any man to destruction, before the accused has met the accusers face to face, and has had opportunity to make his defense concerning the matter laid against him.

chokri: Süla I hice kevori sanha thothü mozode sheco balü bayi, de Paul zü vori no posü.
english: When therefore they had come together here, I didn't delay, but on the next day sat on the judgment seat, and commanded the man to be brought.

chokri: Pü kemejoko tavo thayi, akelüce uko mhapüri mejo keshü khi vorimo.
english: Concerning whom, when the accusers stood up, they brought no charge of such things as I supposed;

chokri: Vele uko sizitari sehomo ziti utho uko dolü mipü Jisu zile tipü, pü sata vele Paul poti rhütazo side pota.
english: but had certain questions against him about their own religion, and about one Jesus, who was dead, whom Paul affirmed to be alive.

chokri: I mha süpü setü mo tila I pü ketso de püzü Jerusalem lü kho püne keti lize.
english: Being perplexed how to inquire concerning these things, I asked whether he was willing to go to Jerusalem and there be judged concerning these matters.

chokri: Vele Paul Augustus po reni tole chota, süla I pü khapü Caesar ce sü ketola kha metate.
english: But when Paul had appealed to be kept for the decision of the emperor, I commanded him to be kept until I could send him to Caesar."

chokri: Agrippa Festus cepoti, I atho thüma süo reni sü nyüle; Püpoti, sodo no süo reni rive.
english: Agrippa said to Festus, "I also would like to hear the man myself." "Tomorrow," he said, "you shall hear him."

chokri: Süsanhasü, Agrippa mu Bernice ni keretso shupü lüri, sheco balü, krapi kezhoko, nadelü kemele miko, Festus kepolü Paul zü vori.
english: So on the next day, when Agrippa and Bernice had come with great pomp, and they had entered into the place of hearing with the commanding officers and principal men of the city, at the command of Festus, Paul was brought in.

chokri: Festus poti, Kedeo Agrippa mu thüma hice hako zü kebako, neko thüma hio ngoba, Jews makro ace (Caesarea) mu Jerusalem lü sheri, hice sheri, thüma hiopo hübasati mesü mole.
english: Festus said, "King Agrippa, and all men who are here present with us, you see this man, about whom all the multitude of the Jews petitioned me, both at Jerusalem and here, crying that he ought not to live any longer.

chokri: Vele I ngo keyi pü sakeshü mhatho püri thimo, mu pü sheri Augustus cecho ketala I pü khapü püce süvaze.
english: But when I found that he had committed nothing worthy of death, and as he himself appealed to the emperor I determined to send him.

chokri: I mhapüri thotüpü aniepoo ce kesü bamo. Süla I püzü vorizü, baho sheri nmhüjülü, O Agrippa kedeo, uko pü ketso kevasa I mhahu thosü rive ketola.
english: Of whom I have no certain thing to write to my lord. Therefore I have brought him forth before you, and especially before you, King Agrippa, that, after examination, I may have something to write.

chokri: Kekrelamori thejü mipü pükemejo thasü mozo keto sü püco bamo.
english: For it seems to me unreasonable, in sending a prisoner, not to also specify the charges against him."

chokri: Süsa Agrippa Paul cepoti, No ntho nmezi poyi keve khri süho. Paul pübe chosü, de püfü kelasü.
english: Agrippa said to Paul, "You may speak for yourself." Then Paul stretched out his hand, and made his defense.

chokri: I nmhüjülü kethahi arüve, Agrippa kedeo, kekrelamori I Jews miko amejo kebako nmhüjülü atho popü nce süyi ve ketala.
english: "I think myself happy, King Agrippa, that I am to make my defense before you this day concerning all the things that I am accused by the Jews,

chokri: Kekrelamori no Jews miko zhorele se meve kebala risa, süla sügü sühe afü reni süte.
english: especially because you are expert in all customs and questions which are among the Jews. Therefore I beg you to hear me patiently.

chokri: Akehü azho rele akhresace ashuyelü I Jerusalem lü hü ketaceri, Jews miko metü seba.
english: "Indeed, all the Jews know my way of life from my youth up, which was from the beginning among my own nation and at Jerusalem;

chokri: Uko zhopo khasü ketosa I Jews mi zhorele kemetsütho lü hü, hako Jews mi nanilü Pharisee kehülü.
english: having known me from the first, if they are willing to testify, that after the strictest sect of our religion I lived a Pharisee.

chokri: Jihova hako potsa nekoce popü kemeta kemeri la I tsühi jünyo ketiti balü ba.
english: Now I stand here to be judged for the hope of the promise made by God to our fathers,

chokri: Reshu de popü hako shuye kerikena koce kesü vori keto kemeri la tüso tüzü metsü Jihova reshü tayo. Kemeri hiola Agrippa kedeo Jews miko a mejoba.
english: which our twelve tribes, earnestly serving night and day, hope to attain. Concerning this hope I am accused by the Jews, King Agrippa!

chokri: Kedipü bide Jihova kesami thi merhü kesü sizi yiveta keti lüpü neko vedi batho?
english: Why is it judged incredible with you, if God does raise the dead?

chokri: Sübi, I sheri akelüce mhakekro thipü Jisu Nazareth za metsuti mesüle thisü.
english: "I myself most assuredly thought that I ought to do many things contrary to the name of Jesus of Nazareth.

chokri: Süko I Jerusalem lü sheri thisü, kemesamiko tüpü thejüthi, thevo kezhoko celü keku khiyide, mu uko dokhri ketilü I a medo le I ri posü.
english: This I also did in Jerusalem. I both shut up many of the saints in prisons, having received authority from the chief priests, and when they were put to death I gave my vote against them.

chokri: I tsükenu uko methi synagogue kolü, ukopo Jihova zhoshü, I azho shüshu de uko metsu, I uko hupü thethi sekemo nadekolü sü.
english: Punishing them often in all the synagogues, I tried to make them blaspheme. Being exceedingly enraged against them, I persecuted them even to foreign cities.

chokri: Süla I thevo kezhoko celü keku ngo keyila ta Damuscus lü kizü.
english: "Whereupon as I traveled to Damascus with the authority and commission from the chief priests,

chokri: Kehe tüluce, Hihe, kedeo, tüyo kevepü I ngosü, nace ceri veku tipü, keve süo akhü metü mu azü revü ketako metü va vekrüta.
english: at noon, O King, I saw on the way a light from the sky, brighter than the sun, shining around me and those who traveled with me.

chokri: Hako metü retu cejü tsüta, I mhafüpü ace kepo se, “Saul, Saul, no dipüla amethiba? N phepü tsahu meshüti nla rü zoto mome?”
english: When we had all fallen to the earth, I heard a voice saying to me in the Hebrew language, 'Saul, Saul, why are you persecuting me? It is hard for you to kick against the goads.'

chokri: I püce poti, Niepoo, No sopü? Pü pokesü, I Jisu no pü methi ketao.
english: "I said, 'Who are you, Lord?' "He said, 'I am Jesus, whom you are persecuting.

chokri: Vele tasüte, side npheyo tha yite, kekrelamori mha hihila I nmengosü, npo mha hiko zhopo thi ketola, mu nmese mengo sü ketoko ri.
english: But arise, and stand on your feet, for I have appeared to you for this purpose: to appoint you a servant and a witness both of the things which you have seen, and of the things which I will reveal to you;

chokri: Npo thümako kelayi mu Gentiles miko kelayi ketola n ketsü süzüho.
english: delivering you from the people, and from the Gentiles, to whom I send you,

chokri: Uko mhü khakhrisü, mu kezülü redekhi kevelü vori ketola, mu Satan celü ta Jihova ce vori ketola, side uko kephoma vakeyi ngo yito, kemelü lü uko thi mesa keyiko kalü khapü tsüsü keto ayoba.
english: to open their eyes, that they may turn from darkness to light and from the power of Satan to God, that they may receive remission of sins and an inheritance among those who are sanctified by faith in me.'

chokri: O Agrippa kedeo, I tüyo zümho kengo füzü thimo tamozo.
english: "Therefore, King Agrippa, I was not disobedient to the heavenly vision,

chokri: Vele theralü süko khapü Damascus lü kebako mesesü, Jerusalem lü, Judea khüko metülü, mu süsa Gentiles koceri, ukopo thelü redeyi de ta Jihova ce vori ketola, side ukopo thelü rede ketabi mhatho thiyi ketola.
english: but declared first to them of Damascus, at Jerusalem, and throughout all the country of Judea, and also to the Gentiles, that they should repent and turn to God, doing works worthy of repentance.

chokri: Kekrelamori mhatho süola Jews miko kereshücelü atüyi de dokhri vato süte,
english: For this reason the Jews seized me in the temple, and tried to kill me.

chokri: Jihova akhrühi kesülü tha ketso kezhomi ceri mu keniko ceri zhopo thi tazo, mha kekre mo veli Moses mu saju kepoko vori tole popü kemeta kozo.
english: Having therefore obtained the help that is from God, I stand to this day testifying both to small and great, saying nothing but what the prophets and Moses said would happen,

chokri: Christa vorito de therao kemezhe süto side kesako dolü rhüla sü keratho thito, pü kevepü thümako mese mengo süto, side pü Gentiles kori mese süto.
english: how the Christ must suffer, and how, by the resurrection of the dead, he would be first to proclaim light both to these people and to the Gentiles."

chokri: Pü mhasüko po kesüce Festus jüpo meto shude poti, Paul no mhaseshu ketala no ntülü ba mode melo bate.
english: As he thus made his defense, Festus said with a loud voice, "Paul, you are crazy! Your great learning is driving you insane!"

chokri: Vele pü poti I melo bamo Festus mhakese kemenuo; vele I rhorhüpü jütho mu kemetse jüko po bazo.
english: But he said, "I am not crazy, most excellent Festus, but boldly declare words of truth and reasonableness.

chokri: Kekrelamori kedeo sheri mhahiko seba I akekha bamode pü mhüjülü jüpo kebala; akelüce mhahiko pü mhüjülü resü reva keti bamozo: kekrelamori I keresükü kolü thiba mo tila.
english: For the king knows of these things, to whom also I speak freely. For I am persuaded that none of these things is hidden from him, for this has not been done in a corner.

chokri: Kedeo, Agrippa no saju kepoko melü yome? I sekeba no melü tayo.
english: King Agrippa, do you believe the prophets? I know that you believe."

chokri: Süsa Agrippa Paul ce poti, no dopülü athipü Christanmi thita tomehe.
english: Agrippa said to Paul, "With a little persuasion are you trying to make me a Christian?"

chokri: Paul posala kesü, Rekri sheri mo sheri, nlebi mori tha afü reni kebako metü abi keto I Jihova ce choba, rophapü abe phapü kebahi zo kemosa.
english: Paul said, "I pray to God, that whether with little or with much, not only you, but also all that hear me this day, might become such as I am, except for these bonds."

chokri: Pü mha hiko po kesüsa kedeo sütha yita mu Angh, mu Bernice sheri sütha yita, püzü kebako metü.
english: The king rose up with the governor, and Bernice, and those who sat with them.

chokri: Uko takre pra ketasa, ukotho kerethisü, Thüma hio sa keshü, mori thejüthi püphapü meta zü mozo.
english: When they had withdrawn, they spoke one to another, saying, "This man does nothing worthy of death or of bonds."

chokri: Agrippa Festus cepoti, Thüma hio khasü varive, mhace pü Caesar ce chosü moto süze.
english: Agrippa said to Festus, "This man might have been set free if he had not appealed to Caesar."

chokri: Hako ru thopü Italy lü vo keto mepeyi ketace, Paul mu thejümi hu khapü Julius zitipü melüsü, pü Augustus kefümipü.
english: When it was determined that we should sail for Italy, they delivered Paul and certain other prisoners to a centurion named Julius, of the Augustan band.

chokri: Hako ta rulü lü Adramytium thethilü, side ta shudüsü, Asia khükolü, Aristarchus hako zü ketala pü Macedonia mipü Thessalonia lü.
english: Embarking in a ship of Adramyttium, which was about to sail to places on the coast of Asia, we put to sea; Aristarchus, a Macedonian of Thessalonica, being with us.

chokri: Süsanha tavo Sidon tsoyi, Julius püzho veshude Paul po tavo püzümiko züde pü nüyi no sü.
english: The next day, we touched at Sidon. Julius treated Paul kindly, and gave him permission to go to his friends and refresh himself.

chokri: Süsa hako ta sü Cyprus lü vota, kekrelamori tükhra hako vo ketoco vamo ketala.
english: Putting to sea from there, we sailed under the lee of Cyprus, because the winds were contrary.

chokri: Hako rulü ta Cilicia mu Pamphylia kolü vo de tasü Myra lü tsoyi Lycia lü nadepü.
english: When we had sailed across the sea which is off Cilicia and Pamphylia, we came to Myra, a city of Lycia.

chokri: Sülü hako füketao rupü ngoyi Alexandria lü ta Italy lü lüto kezü pü, sülü hako khasü.
english: There the centurion found a ship of Alexandria sailing for Italy, and he put us on board.

chokri: Rele shude hako ru thopü tüsozho kekrosa tavo Cnidus tsoyi, tükhra hako metsu ketala hako ru thopü Crete khrilü ta Salmone lü vo.
english: When we had sailed slowly many days, and had come with difficulty opposite Cnidus, the wind not allowing us further, we sailed under the lee of Crete, opposite Salmone.

chokri: Rüshude ruthopü tavo thethipü Fair Haven (kerelüthethi) tsoyi, süo Lasea nade zü kemeno ba.
english: With difficulty sailing along it we came to a certain place called Fair Havens, near the city of Lasea.

chokri: Rutho keta pütü kekro mediva, mu hicehi rutho keti kemeche bashuta, mevo keti (Yon Kippur) vota, Paul uko tarhosü.
english: When much time had passed and the voyage was now dangerous, because the Fast had now already gone by, Paul admonished them,

chokri: Uko cepoti, kezhoko, I sekeba ruketho lü kempe bate mu mhako ka keta zü kesüto, ruyo keta mhanyoko lebimo vele thüma sheri.
english: and said to them, "Sirs, I perceive that the voyage will be with injury and much loss, not only of the cargo and the ship, but also of our lives."

chokri: Vele hako kefüo Paul fü kereniceri ruketho kezhoo, mu ru niepoo fü reni kuta.
english: But the centurion gave more heed to the master and to the owner of the ship than to those things which were spoken by Paul.

chokri: Ru relüthi sülü tüsütü cela thethi pütükemopü, süla makro kono rulü tavo Phoenix tsoyi ri ve tila, sülü uko tüsüce ba ketola. Thethi süo rurelü thipü Crete, meso mu mekhro nakro kevaco.
english: Because the haven was not suitable to winter in, the majority advised going to sea from there, if by any means they could reach Phoenix, and winter there, which is a port of Crete, looking northeast and southeast.

chokri: Mekhroco tükhra mehiba, uko kelüce uzhotülü uko ruthoyi keve thita lizu, uko ru phrüsü de ruthopü Crete kemenoce jükhüce keba thethilüvo.
english: When the south wind blew softly, supposing that they had obtained their purpose, they weighed anchor and sailed along Crete, close to shore.

chokri: Vele pütü choba kemolü khradepü vorita, Euroclydon khrade süo uko ru hüta.
english: But before long, a tempestuous wind beat down from shore, which is called Euroclydon.

chokri: Ruo hüyi ketala uko tükhra süo metsu yihota de tükhrapo pühüpü tata.
english: When the ship was caught, and couldn't face the wind, we gave way to it, and were driven along.

chokri: Cejü rethitso kemo kenipü Clauda khrilü ruthopü vozü, kedopüce hako ruyoketa büfüruko yo kuyi.
english: Running under the lee of a small island called Clauda, we were able, with difficulty, to secure the boat.

chokri: Ru fühako ha keyice rulü rophako pha kemetüyi, kemosa ru ta Syrtis (tesünyo) lü vo keta mpe tila; side tüsümethakeyi thegiro (anchor) phrü kerechusü de tasü.
english: After they had hoisted it up, they used cables to help reinforce the ship. Fearing that they would run aground on the Syrtis sand bars, they lowered the sea anchor, and so were driven along.

chokri: Tükhrapo hako hüshupütata, süla süsanha mhakhapü kegüdüva depüpü thi kemejosü.
english: As we labored exceedingly with the storm, the next day they began to throw things overboard.

chokri: Zhosünha hakotho hako bepü ru kenhipü tata.
english: On the third day, they threw out the ship's tackle with their own hands.

chokri: Tüsozho kekro nace mori thenyü kori ngo mota, tükhra hako kehü ketsapü thimo, rhüyi keto kemeri sheri bamota.
english: When neither sun nor stars shone on us for many days, and no small tempest pressed on us, all hope that we would be saved was now taken away.

chokri: Vele pütü kecho ketico bamo ketala, Paul süthayi de poti, kezhoko, neko afü reniyi ti mesü, Crete lü ru phrü kesü mesümo, size mhapüri medi vaho mu kemezhe ri taho.
english: When they had been long without food, Paul stood up in the middle of them, and said, "Sirs, you should have listened to me, and not have set sail from Crete, and have gotten this injury and loss.

chokri: Tsüsahi, neko nüyite, neko mipüri kesakedi thi saho ruo zo kemosa.
english: Now I exhort you to cheer up, for there will be no loss of life among you, but only of the ship.

chokri: Tsüzi tüyo Jihova choyimipü azü ketao, mu I pü choyi thi ketao acepoti,
english: For there stood by me this night an angel, belonging to the God whose I am and whom I serve,

chokri: Mpe hite, Paul, no Caesar mhüjülü voto, Hihe, nzü revü ketako kelapü ntsü süto.
english: saying, 'Don't be afraid, Paul. You must stand before Caesar. Behold, God has granted you all those who sail with you.'

chokri: Süla kezhomiko, neko nüyite, kekrelamori I Jihova kemelümi, acepokesübi sübi thisü zo ketola.
english: Therefore, sirs, cheer up! For I believe God, that it will be just as it has been spoken to me.

chokri: Kehothi sheri uko kelhoupü cejü rethitsokemo pülü süvatoho.
english: But we must run aground on a certain island."

chokri: Tüsozho keridazi Adria jükezholü ruthokho thoki kebace, rukethoko lükeba tavo rünahu tsota zütho ziba.
english: But when the fourteenth night had come, as we were driven back and forth in the Adriatic Sea, about midnight the sailors surmised that they were drawing near to some land.

chokri: Jülüfü sekeyice botho (fathom) mecü keta lüba, süsa zace thefü ketsüsala sü, ziri keripüngu zota.
english: They took soundings, and found twenty fathoms. After a little while, they took soundings again, and found fifteen fathoms..

chokri: Tavo ketsüro thoyi keta mpe tila ru tüsü ketola tsahu (anchor) da kehüsü, side sünha sülü relüta ketola.
english: Fearing that we would run aground on rocky ground, they let go four anchors from the stern, and wished for daylight.

chokri: Ru kethomiko ukodo thide ru metha keyice büfüruko phrü kerenu süta, tsahuko kelhu kesüzhü.
english: As the sailors were trying to flee out of the ship, and had lowered the boat into the sea, pretending that they would lay out anchors from the bow,

chokri: Paul uko kefüo mu thipaheko cepoti, Thüma hiko rulü ba kemosa neko kelayi hote.
english: Paul said to the centurion and to the soldiers, "Unless these stay in the ship, you can't be saved."

chokri: Süsa thipaheko kero dathüsü de rupo tata.
english: Then the soldiers cut away the ropes of the boat, and let it fall off.

chokri: Tüso prari kezüce Paul uko cepoti, neko mhati yite, tha tüsozho kerida teneko mhati kemo, khüta keta.
english: While the day was coming on, Paul begged them all to take some food, saying, "This day is the fourteenth day that you wait and continue fasting, having taken nothing.

chokri: Süla I nekoce posüyo mhahu tiyi te, kekrlemori neko sürhola, kekrelamori neko mipü pithari retu tahote.
english: Therefore I beg you to take some food, for this is for your safety; for not a hair will perish from any of your heads."

chokri: Pü jü sübi po kesüsa tsükhri tiyi, uko mhüjülü Jihova kemeza posü, pü tsükhri cephro süde khi tiyi.
english: When he had said this, and had taken bread, he gave thanks to God in the presence of all, and he broke it, and began to eat.

chokri: Süsa uko unüyi de mhatiyi.
english: Then they all cheered up, and they also took food.

chokri: Hako rulü ketako thüma krakena mu hethüna shurü ta.
english: In all, we were two hundred seventy-six souls on the ship.

chokri: Uko mhati keyi sa rupü thikemejova, ata kori kegüdüpü jükezholü süva.
english: When they had eaten enough, they lightened the ship, throwing out the wheat into the sea.

chokri: Keshulü uko cejü dipülü vota sheri semo, vele uko jükhü cejü kewhipü ngoba süla ru thopü sülü vo keto lüsü, mhace thiyi ve lizo.
english: When it was day, they didn't recognize the land, but they noticed a certain bay with a beach, and they decided to try to drive the ship onto it.

chokri: Uko rulü kero khiyita süce side füha pümesüsü, ru thopü kerelü thethi lü kebalü phe ketola.
english: Casting off the anchors, they left them in the sea, at the same time untying the rudder ropes. Hoisting up the foresail to the wind, they made for the beach.

chokri: Jükezho kena vo kesü kebalü lüta side uko ru cejülü bata, uvade taki cejülü thoyita de melü yi hota, vele ru saco thophrova jü rüphüpo.
english: But coming to a place where two seas met, they ran the vessel aground. The bow struck and remained immovable, but the stern began to break up by the violence of the waves.

chokri: Thipaheko kelüce thejümiko dokhri va keto rhüta, motisa uko jüvü de ta keta mpe ketala.
english: The soldiers' counsel was to kill the prisoners, so that none of them would swim out and escape.

chokri: Vele kefü kezhoo Paul kelakesü lüba tila uko khameta, ukopo jüvü kevemi jüvüpü cejü kesolü prata no posü.
english: But the centurion, desiring to save Paul, stopped them from their purpose, and commanded that those who could swim should throw themselves overboard first to go toward the land;

chokri: Humipo büfü rukolü tavo, mu kekre miko ru kepro kolü ta sizi. Sübide uko metü cejü kesolü pra tsoyi.
english: and the rest should follow, some on planks, and some on other things from the ship. So it happened that they all escaped safely to the land.

chokri: Hako tapra tso keyisa sekeyi cejü rethi tsokemo süo Malta le ziti seyi.
english: When we had escaped, then they learned that the island was called Malta.

chokri: Sülü kehüko hakoyo merhe dapü thimo. Türü vorita mu meko phita vele uko me thipü jükhü metülü hako meso shusü.
english: The natives showed us uncommon kindness; for they kindled a fire, and received us all, because of the present rain, and because of the cold.

chokri: Paul methi ketola süpa kevoce tenhi nhibüpü melü vori mu süo pübe mereyita.
english: But when Paul had gathered a bundle of sticks and laid them on the fire, a viper came out because of the heat, and fastened on his hand.

chokri: Thüma sülü kehüko süo ngode uko dolü kepo keba thüma hio mikedokhri pütazo metse pütho jülü taprari tazo sheri, khüpo sazho sü pü merhü süho.
english: When the natives saw the creature hanging from his hand, they said one to another, "No doubt this man is a murderer, whom, though he has escaped from the sea, yet Justice has not allowed to live."

chokri: Pü khonu süo keshüpü melü süva mu mhapüri pü methi mota.
english: However he shook off the creature into the fire, and wasn't harmed.

chokri: Kehothi sheri reme vori metse siziba, mori saretu ta keto lüba; vele uko pütü kecho peba sheri püyo mhapüri thi mo ketala uko kelü redeta, side püpü therü mipü le sizita.
english: But they expected that he would have swollen or fallen down dead suddenly, but when they watched for a long time and saw nothing bad happen to him, they changed their minds, and said that he was a god.

chokri: Thethi süce kehü mipü thüma kemele mipü püza Publius hako mesoyi mu hako thekü shusü, tüso zho sü merhe shude.
english: Now in the neighborhood of that place were lands belonging to the chief man of the island, named Publius, who received us, and courteously entertained us for three days.

chokri: Publius po mhathi shu mu thüza bü de püzilü zü, Paul pücelü de kecho thisü, pübe chopü püyosü, side püthi kesürho sü.
english: It happened that the father of Publius lay sick of fever and dysentery. Paul entered in to him, prayed, and laying his hands on him, healed him.

chokri: Sü thi kesüla thüma sülü kehümiko mhathi kebako tavori de uko thi kesürhoyi.
english: Then when this was done, the rest also who had diseases in the island came, and were cured.

chokri: Uko hakoyo therho mhathoko thi shupü hako tsüsü, hako rulü tata ketoce hakola khimo kesuko metü khapü hako tsüsü.
english: They also honored us with many honors, and when we sailed, they put on board the things that we needed.

chokri: Hako tekhri sü sa tavo cejü rethi tsokemo pülü voyi Alexandria ru nube za Castor mu Pollux thopü meta.
english: After three months, we set sail in a ship of Alexandria which had wintered in the island, whose sign was "The Twin Brothers."

chokri: Tavo Syracus lü zho sü relüyi.
english: Touching at Syracuse, we stayed there three days.

chokri: Sülü mede tipü mhanyo (compass) püyi de tavo Rhegium tsoyi, zhopü sülü ba de sülü mekhroco tükhra vorita, süsanha tavo Puteoli tsoyi.
english: From there we circled around and arrived at Rhegium. After one day, a south wind sprang up, and on the second day we came to Puteoli,

chokri: Hako tavo sülü kemelümiko ba ngoyi, de zho thüna sülü banota, süsa ta Rome kevaco tavota.
english: where we found brothers, and were entreated to stay with them for seven days. So we came to Rome.

chokri: Kemelü miko hako tavo kezü sesü, de uko Appius ketso Resoce sülü ta vori hako züyi, Paul süko ngokesüce Jihova meza ti posü, mu thepuyi.
english: From there the brothers, when they heard of us, came to meet us as far as The Market of Appius and The Three Taverns. When Paul saw them, he thanked God, and took courage.

chokri: Hako tavo Rome tsokeyice kefüo thejüko khapü sülü kefüko tsüva, vele Paul khapü thipahe mipüpo uni bata.
english: When we entered into Rome, the centurion delivered the prisoners to the captain of the guard, but Paul was allowed to stay by himself with the soldier who guarded him.

chokri: Zhosü sa Paul Jews krüta sülü kebako cüsü, uko tavori ti salü Paul uko cepoti, thümako mu sazüjürako, I thümako nümo thimo mori upotsaneko zho sheri varhümo, sizo sheri Jerusalem lü atüyi de akhapü Roman miko belü süvele.
english: It happened that after three days Paul called together those who were the leaders of the Jews. When they had come together, he said to them, "I, brothers, though I had done nothing against the people, or the customs of our fathers, still was delivered prisoner from Jerusalem into the hands of the Romans,

chokri: Uko aju keti kevoce ayo kejo sa keshü ngoyi mo tila uko akhasüva nyüsü.
english: who, when they had examined me, desired to set me free, because there was no cause of death in me.

chokri: Vele Jews miko süo metsude jüpo ketala apo Caesar ce cho sala moze suta. I ashuye zü kenyo nyümo zo sheri.
english: But when the Jews spoke against it, I was constrained to appeal to Caesar, not that I had anything about which to accuse my nation.

chokri: Kekrelamori mha hiohila I neko cüsü, neko zü kesüsü mu neko ceposü kenyüla; kekrelamori Israel miko kemerila thegi ropü aphapü tatele.
english: For this cause therefore I asked to see you and to speak with you. For because of the hope of Israel I am bound with this chain."

chokri: Hako njula Judea lü lesipüri ngomo zolu, mori sazüjüra celü mipüri vori de mhapopü nmetsu ti bamo.
english: They said to him, "We neither received letters from Judea concerning you, nor did any of the brothers come here and report or speak any evil of you.

chokri: Vele hako nju senyüba no dipü lü ba sheri, hako kese thethi metülü kro hio metsu ti se süte.
english: But we desire to hear from you what you think. For, as concerning this sect, it is known to us that everywhere it is spoken against."

chokri: Tüso zhopü chepü pü tsüsü, thüma kekro ta pü keba thethilü vobayo; pü süko ce Jihova kede kethi zhopo thi bayo, Jisu ju pü uko ce po, Moses thezhoko sheri, mu saju kepoko sheri sa de keshulü vo theva ketso po bayo.
english: When they had appointed him a day, many people came to him at his lodging. He explained to them, testifying about the Kingdom of God, and persuading them concerning Jesus, both from the law of Moses and from the prophets, from morning until evening.

chokri: Humi po kesüko melüyi vele humi melü mota.
english: Some believed the things which were spoken, and some disbelieved.

chokri: Uko dolü umedo kemetha mo ketala kekhota, süsalü Paul jüpü poti, Kemesa Rüpü Isaiah saju kepoo talü po meve shu pü upotsa neko ce süzo.
english: When they didn't agree among themselves, they departed after Paul had spoken one word, "The Holy Spirit spoke rightly through Isaiah, the prophet, to our fathers,

chokri: Pokesü, Ta thüma hikoce vote, de posüte, kereni sü neko renito, vele seyiho; kepesü neko peto vele mhalü yiho.
english: saying, 'Go to this people, and say, In hearing, you will hear, But will in no way understand. In seeing, you will see, But will in no way perceive.

chokri: Thüma hiko melü revote, mu uko ni sheri kenibite; uko mhüma tate mhangomo ketola, mu unipü reni mo ketola. Uko melüpü mhalüyi mu redeyi lize I uko thi kesürho süto vele.
english: For this people's heart has grown callous. Their ears are dull of hearing. Their eyes they have closed. Lest they should see with their eyes, Hear with their ears, Understand with their heart, And would turn again, And I would heal them.'

chokri: Neko hihi seyite, Jihova kelakeyi kha kesü khipü Gentiles miko tsü vato, size uko melü yito.
english: "Be it known therefore to you, that the salvation of God is sent to the Gentiles. They will also listen."

chokri: Pü jü hiko po kevasa Jews miko tata, uko dolü mha kepo shuta.
english: When he had said these words, the Jews departed, having a great dispute among themselves.

chokri: Paul cepü nyepü hü kebalü tütse kena photü sülü ba, püce kevoriko metü zü kesü.
english: Paul stayed two whole years in his own rented house, and received all who were coming to him,

chokri: Jihova kede kethi zhopothi mu Niepo Jisu Khrista jupü mi metha, kemelü kene mu mipüri pü kha kemo thi.
english: preaching the Kingdom of God, and teaching the things concerning the Lord Jesus Christ with all boldness, without hinderance.

chokri: Süla nmezi kepo bamo, somino milümejoya; no milü mejo kebao ntho nmejo bate: mi mejo kebasü sübizo.
english: Therefore you are without excuse, O man, whoever you are who judge. For in that which you judge another, you condemn yourself. For you who judge practice the same things.

chokri: Vele usekeba Jihova sheco sü ketülü co süyo, somi mimejo yo sheri sükoyo siziyo.
english: We know that the judgment of God is according to truth against those who practice such things.

chokri: No sopüleno mimejo kebao no Jihova sheco thürayi tole lübame?
english: Do you think this, O man who judges those who practice such things, and do the same, that you will escape the judgment of God?

chokri: Mori no pü ketsükeve kene, sügüsühe keti, pütü kecho fükeyi, Jihova keveko uzüvo thelü rüde keyilü süyo siti semo zome?
english: Or do you despise the riches of his goodness, forbearance, and patience, not knowing that the goodness of God leads you to repentance?

chokri: Vele nlükerü, thelü rude kemo hiko kengupüvo Jihova kemezi sheco nha hala süketo lekhu nmetsu tato.
english: But according to your hardness and unrepentant heart you are treasuring up for yourself wrath in the day of wrath, revelation, and of the righteous judgment of God;

chokri: Kekrelamori pü utho umhatho cesü züde zhokhro khasüto.
english: who "will pay back to everyone according to their works:"

chokri: Sügüsühede mhatho keve thi kebako zapozale, thechu therho m sakemo kechothe kehü phu tayo.
english: to those who by patience in well-doing seek for glory and honor and incorruptibility, eternal life;

chokri: Vele somino mizüde kezhenyüsahühü, kethojü zümu, vele mezikemoko füzü, ulükekri (unhyüshethi) m thelü reda.
english: but to those who are self-seeking, and don't obey the truth, but obey unrighteousness, will be wrath and indignation,

chokri: Somi mhakesu thiyo sheli lüzhe, mhalüphayi keho hiko ukoyo voto; Judahmi ra süsa Israelmi kemoko yori voto.
english: oppression and anguish, on every soul of man who works evil, on the Jew first, and also on the Greek.

chokri: Vele mhakeve kethimiko zale, therho, ulüthsizi keti, Jewmiko ra m süsa Israelmi kemoko yo ri voto.
english: But glory and honor and peace to every man who works good, to the Jew first, and also to the Greek.

chokri: Kekrelamori Jihova thümapüri rhomekre yomo.
english: For there is no partiality with God.

chokri: Kekrelamori thezho kedira kephoma kethiko thezho kedira shütazoto; thezholü phoma ketako thezholü uko neketi zo ketola.
english: For as many as have sinned without law will also perish without the law. As many as have sinned under the law will be judged by the law.

chokri: Kekrelamori thezho reni mori thezho se ketila Jihova mhüjülü matho tamo, vele thezhoko thi m zü keyila mezi yito.
english: For it isn't the hearers of the law who are righteous before God, but the doers of the law will be justified

chokri: Kekrelamori Judahmi kemoko thezho tamo tila süculü zhoko züta, thezho bamozori süculü zhoko pü uko zho thiyo.
english: (for when Gentiles who don't have the law do by nature the things of the law, these, not having the law, are a law to themselves,

chokri: Thezho mathoko pü umelülü thopü tayo, thive thisuko uko zhopo ngoyiyo; uko melülü kemüjo mori kejo keva sheri kemeli ketako seba.
english: in that they show the work of the law written in their hearts, their conscience testifying with them, and their thoughts among themselves accusing or else excusing them)

chokri: Jisu Khrista yono Jihova pünha thüma kemekhrüko jünyo ketito I jüyikeve hio poyo
english: in the day when God will judge the secrets of men, according to my Gospel, by Jesus Christ.

chokri: Hihe, ne Judahmile ne thezholü relü m Jihova la kemeko tayo.
english: Indeed you bear the name of a Jew, and rest on the law, and glory in God,

chokri: Pü medoko se m thezholü neko metha kesüla mhakevethoko thi keto nemedole sizi tayo.
english: and know his will, and approve the things that are excellent, being instructed out of the law,

chokri: No mhücemi chotha keto ntho nlünve, kezülü ketako la kevepü thi keto sheri.
english: and are confident that you yourself are a guide of the blind, a light to those who are in darkness,

chokri: Mhasekemo (kenyoko) thi kemezi keto, natsimiko metha keto; mhase zhoko m ketho le zitiko.
english: a corrector of the foolish, a teacher of babies, having in the law the form of knowledge and of the truth.

chokri: No mi kemethao ntho nmethayi hola? Mharego hino po vele no mharego yola?
english: You therefore who teach another, don't you teach yourself? You who preach that a man shouldn't steal, do you steal?

chokri: No mipo kekhrükezü hinopo vele no peü thi yola? Marhe sü phikemeropüle si vele no marhe reshü yola?
english: You who say a man shouldn't commit adultery, do you commit adultery? You who abhor idols, do you rob temples?

chokri: No thezhokola kemeko vele thezho varhü Jihova rho kemothi yola?
english: You who glory in the law, through your disobedience of the law do you dishonor God?

chokri: Kekrelamori Judahkemoko mhüjülü Jihova zhoshü, side thometa.
english: For "the name of God is blasphemed among the Gentiles because of you," just as it is written.

chokri: Yikerü sü mevezo thezho kezümila, vele no mhace thezho varhü tasüze yikerü sü nla yirükemo mipü thite.
english: For circumcision indeed profits, if you are a doer of the law, but if you are a transgressor of the law, your circumcision has become uncircumcision.

chokri: Süla yirükemomi thezho züyo tisa yirükemopü yirüle sitaho, ile?
english: If therefore the uncircumcised keep the ordinances of the law, won't his uncircumcision be accounted as circumcision?

chokri: Vele süculü zhokezüko yirümode thezholü mezi ta yolizo uko neyikerüko vajo ketako mejo zoto.
english: Won't the uncircumcision which is by nature, if it fulfills the law, judge you, who with the letter and circumcision are a transgressor of the law?

chokri: Pü chozu kengo zono Israelmi mozori, mori chozuco uthi uphoyo yikerümi mo zori:
english: For he is not a Jew who is one outwardly, neither is that circumcision which is outward in the flesh;

chokri: Sizizori pü Judahmipü tazo thelücono, yikerümipüri tazo pümelülüno; süsü rüpülü, lesi ketho kerolümo: süko chu ketosü thüma yomo vele Jihova yolü to.
english: but he is a Jew who is one inwardly, and circumcision is that of the heart, in the spirit not in the letter; whose praise is not from men, but from God.

chokri: Size Judahmi la kemeve kedipü ba? Yikerü la kedipü kepra ba?
english: Then what advantage does the Jew have? Or what is the profit of circumcision?

chokri: Mha bashu metükolü, theralü Jihova talü kepopü Judahmi melüsü.
english: Much in every way! Because first of all, they were entrusted with the oracles of God.

chokri: Kekrelamori mhace humi melümo tali mhame? Uko melükemo sü Jihova kemelü mhathi yiho tatome?
english: For what if some were without faith? Will their lack of faith nullify the faithfulness of God?

chokri: Suto, (kenitho) “thüma metü kemedami, veli Jihova jütho side thometa,” süla njülü no meziti ngoyito, nne keti ketsülü no meziyito.
english: May it never be! Yes, let God be found true, but every man a liar. As it is written, "That you might be justified in your words, And might prevail when you come into judgment."

chokri: Vele uko mezikemo sheco pü Jihova kemezice kedipü poyi veta?
english: But if our unrighteousness commends the righteousness of God, what will we say? Is God unrighteous who inflicts wrath? I speak like men do.

chokri: (I thüma thi jüpoba). Suto, Kekrelamori Jihova tsüsa kedipü bide cejüo ne keti ta?
english: May it never be! For then how will God judge the world?

chokri: Mhace akemedalü Jihova kejütho pü zapozale thiyi veta ketosa, kedipüla akhipü kephomami leno amejo yi veta?
english: For if the truth of God through my lie abounded to his glory, why am I also still judged as a sinner?

chokri: (Humi umejode upoyole side poyo). Mhakesu thikhe, side mhakevepo vori ketola? Thüma sizi ketakosü mejova züzo.
english: Why not (as we are slanderously reported, and as some affirm that we say), "Let us do evil, that good may come?" Those who say so are justly condemned.

chokri: Sizitaze dipü? Uko ukoce veku teme? Sizi mozo, Judahmi sheri m Judah kemomi sheri kephoma khro tazo.
english: What then? Are we better than they? No, in no way. For we previously charged both Jews and Greeks, that they are all under sin.

chokri: Hizi thopü meta, “Kematho kemezi püri dide, mipüri ta mote.”
english: As it is written, "There is no one righteous. No, not one.

chokri: Mhase mhalu keto püri dite, mipüri Jihova kephu ta mote.
english: There is no one who understands. There is no one who seeks after God.

chokri: Uko metü chokhü prate, uko kesade mevekemolü vote; mhakeve kethi tamote: mote mipüri dite.
english: They have all turned aside. They have together become unprofitable. There is no one who does good, No, not, so much as one."

chokri: Uko mezhoroko mhakhre kecebi, uko meyikhi kecejüpo; uko mülü tenhi therü ta.
english: "Their throat is an open tomb. With their tongues they have used deceit."  "The poison of vipers is under their lips;"

chokri: Uko talü reshu chupü, m jükekhuko.
english: "Whose mouth is full of cursing and bitterness."

chokri: Uko phezi chorhürhü mi kedokhrilü,
english: "Their feet are swift to shed blood.

chokri: Uko chokolü mhakemeka m kemezhe keti zo.
english: Destruction and misery are in their ways.

chokri: Uko chokolü kezükeve semo;
english: The way of peace, they haven't known."

chokri: Uko mhüyo Jihova khri kemeprü bamo.
english: "There is no fear of God before their eyes."

chokri: Tsü usekeyi thezhoko dipüpo sü sheri thezho khro ketakola po, cejülü kehü metüpo kemekre bakeyi; Jihova mhüjülü keve kesu hazo ketola.
english: Now we know that whatever things the law says, it speaks to those who are under the law, that every mouth may be closed, and all the world may be brought under the judgment of God.

chokri: Thüma kehopüri thezhokhi pümhüjülü utho upo kemeziyiho, thezhola kephoma mhase vori.
english: Because by the works of the law, no flesh will be justified in his sight. For through the law comes the knowledge of sin.

chokri: Vele tsüno thezho sa mode Jihova kemezi mese mengo süte, thezhoko m saju keseko hijü zhopo thi meta.
english: But now apart from the law, a righteousness of God has been revealed, being testified by the law and the prophets;

chokri: Jihova kemezi Jisu Khrista kemelü yono khapü pükemelü metü tsüsüve. Thüma metü kela kekre ho.
english: even the righteousness of God through faith in Jesus Christ to all and on all those who believe. For there is no distinction,

chokri: Kekrelamori thüma metü phomate, Jihova zapo zale tso mote.
english: for all have sinned, and fall short of the glory of God;

chokri: Jisu Khrista urhakeyiyono pü kelakeyi ngokemezhepü methuzode upo kemeziyi.
english: being justified freely by his grace through the redemption that is in Christ Jesus;

chokri: Jihova pü khapü uphosü thide püza mekrüsü, kemelümi sü seyiyo.
english: whom God set forth to be an atoning sacrifice, through faith in his blood, for a demonstration of his righteousness through the passing over of prior sins, in God's forbearance;

chokri: Tsüdo I hihi poyo, pükemezi m pükejüthola somi Jisu kemelümi sheri sümi thi kematho vayo.
english: to demonstrate his righteousness at this present time; that he might himself be just, and the justifier of him who has faith in Jesus.

chokri: Kedipüla u kemekota? Sü saphri mote. Thezho dipülü? Mhatholame? Movele, thezholü melü keyila.
english: Where then is the boasting? It is excluded. By what manner of law? Of works? No, but by a law of faith.
