chokri: I shusü, Apollo jüpü ketisü, vele Niepoo tho thekünma kesü zo.
english: I planted. Apollos watered. But God gave the increase.

chokri: Süla shu kesüo zori mhapümo, mori jü khipü keti kesüo zori mhapüri zomo, vele Jihova thekü nmasü.
english: So then neither he who plants is anything, nor he who waters, but God who gives the increase.

chokri: Tsühi, shu kesüo mu jü chu kesüo uni mipüzo; thüma keho püsheri utho uzhokhro ngo yito, pü dipü bide mhatho thi sheri.
english: Now he who plants and he who waters are the same, but each will receive his own reward according to his own labor.

chokri: Kekrelamori ukometü kezü Jihova yitho mizo, neko Jihova yi kefü mizo, neko Jihova cetho mi phrüzo.
english: For we are God's fellow workers. You are God's farming, God's building.

chokri: Jihova phikemezhe khapü aketsü cekethi krüta keve mipü, I pübodü shudüsü, mu kekre miko süyo thi. Vele kehopü sheri mhalü menute, pü dipü bide mhathi yo sheri. (Bodü pü lebi)
english: According to the grace of God which was given to me, as a wise master builder I laid a foundation, and another builds on it. But let each man be careful how he builds on it.

chokri: Kekrelamori bodü süo zo kemo sa, kekremi shudü sala yo tila, Jisu lebizo.
english: For no one can lay any other foundation than that which has been laid, which is Jesus Christ.

chokri: Tsühi, humi bodü kethi suna, raka, ketsü pümakerü, sü, mu nicu;
english: But if anyone builds on the foundation with gold, silver, costly stones, wood, hay, or stubble;

chokri: Thüma kehopü sheri pü mhatho mengo sü zoto, kekrelamori tüsozhopü vori ketola; mu süo mepü thüsü peto, mu thüma kehopü mhatho sheri melü rü kesüce seyi mu ngo yito.
english: each man's work will be revealed. For the Day will declare it, because it is revealed in fire; and the fire itself will test what sort of work each man's work is.

chokri: Mhace mha kethi mipü ce kethi zholü thi lize, pü zhokhro ngo yito.
english: If any man's work remains which he built on it, he will receive a reward.

chokri: Mhace pümhatho tova lize, pü ka tato; vele pütho pü kela yito, vele melü rüpü peto.
english: If any man's work is burned, he will suffer loss, but he himself will be saved, but as through fire.

chokri: Neko Jihova kereshüce ziti semo zome? Jihova Rüpü neko lü hütayo ziti.
english: Don't you know that you are a temple of God, and that God's Spirit lives in you?

chokri: Mhace thüma humi Jihova kereshüce thi merho tayo lize, Jihova pü meka vato, Jihova reshüce mesa, neko reshüce süo zo.
english: If anyone destroys the temple of God, God will destroy him; for God's temple is holy, which you are.

chokri: Thüma kehopü pori pütho püshu menyo va hite. Mhace thüma humi cejülü mhase mu tsü lize, püpo kenyomi thi tate, sizi lize pü mhakesemi pü thite.
english: Let no one deceive himself. If anyone thinks that he is wise among you in this world, let him become a fool, that he may become wise.

chokri: Kekrelamori cejü hilü mhase Jihova la kenyo püzo. Kekrelamori hiziti thopü meta, Pü mhakeseko dokerü khi yite.
english: For the wisdom of this world is foolishness with God. For it is written, "He has taken the wise in their craftiness."

chokri: Mu süsa sheri Niepoo mhakeseko kelü sebate, mu süko kemethu thite.
english: And again, "The Lord knows the reasoning of the wise, that it is worthless."

chokri: Süla thüma kehopüri thüma la zapozale thiba hite. Kekrelamori mha metü püzhü tila.
english: Therefore let no one boast in men. For all things are yours,

chokri: Paul la sheri, mori Apollo la sheri, mori Cephas la sheri, mori cejüo la sheri, mori kerhümi la sheri, mori kesa la sheri, mori tsüdo mha kebako la sheri, mori hisa vori keto mhako la sheri, mhapüri mozo, vele püla zo.
english: whether Paul, or Apollos, or Cephas, or the world, or life, or death, or things present, or things to come. All are yours,

chokri: Neko Khrista zhü zo, mu Khrista sü Jihova zo.
english: and you are Christ's, and Christ is God's.

chokri: Mimetü kepo keba neko dolü kekhrükezü ti batele ziba, Gentile miko dolü zori theza sübiti bazoyo kemo, humi upone cemi züba keta.
english: It is actually reported that there is sexual immorality among you, and such sexual immorality as is not even named among the Gentiles, that one has his father's wife.

chokri: Neko kemeko ulü kerekrü bate, ulü su ulü kemezhe mori, mha sübi thita ketako neko dolü khivate.
english: You are puffed up, and didn't rather mourn, that he who had done this deed might be removed from among you.

chokri: Kekrelamori athi apho zono neko züba mota kethozo, vele rüpülü neko zü bazo, neko mezi mu mezi kemo mese süte, I neko züba tibi, somi mhatho hibiti thiba sheri.
english: For I most assuredly, as being absent in body but present in spirit, have already, as though I were present, judged him who has done this thing.

chokri: Uko Niepo Jisu Khristaa zalü neko tavo kengu keyice, a rüpü uko Niepoo Jisu Khristaa kekulü neko züba.
english: In the name of our Lord Jesus Christ, you being gathered together, and my spirit, with the power of our Lord Jesus Christ,

chokri: Thüma sümi khapü Satan belü sü vate, püthi püpho mekava ketola, side pü rüpü Niepo Jisu tüso zhonha kelayi ketola.
english: are to deliver such a one to Satan for the destruction of the flesh, that the spirit may be saved in the day of the Lord Jesus.

chokri: Ntho nthipü zapozale thizo keba vemo, Khre kedopüla photü po khrü keta seba mo zome?
english: Your boasting is not good. Don't you know that a little yeast leavens the whole lump?

chokri: Süla khre kevüo huphra vate, sizi lize neko khre kesa thita ketola; neko khre kevüpo neko merho va kemoko. Kekrelamori Khrista khipü ukola ta kepra nyelü rütho thi süte.
english: Purge out the old yeast, that you may be a new lump, even as you are unleavened. For indeed Christ, our Passover, has been sacrificed in our place.

chokri: Süla uko thenye nye tokhe, khre kevüo mori, mori hunehu meshu kemo, keretse kethi mo, vele khre khasü kemo tsükhri, kesürhü mu ketho.
english: Therefore let us keep the feast, not with old yeast, neither with the yeast of malice and wickedness, but with the unleavened bread of sincerity and truth.

chokri: I lesithopü neko tsü kesülü nekopo kerezhü miko zü kemekro hino süzo.
english: I wrote to you in my letter to have no company with sexual sinners;

chokri: Sizizotamo, metükolü, cejülü kerezhümiko, mha kemeho miko, mizhü süketimiko, marhe kereshü miko, kekrelamori sizi talize neko cejüo khava tata moze su tato,
english: yet not at all meaning with the sexual sinners of this world, or with the covetous and extortioners, or with idolaters; for then you would have to leave the world.

chokri: Vele tsühi I lesi tho kesü, süko zü kemekro hite, mhace cüpü sazüjüra thiyi keta humi regürezhüta, mhakemeho mi thita, mori marhe reshüta, mizü kezheta, mori hezo kra mezüta, mori mizhü kesü tita, sümi züde mhati mhakra hite.
english: But as it is, I wrote to you not to associate with anyone who is called a brother who is a sexual sinner, or covetous, or an idolater, or a slanderer, or a drunkard, or an extortioner. Don't even eat with such a person.

chokri: Kekrelamori I chozu keta süko zü dipü thita? Neko ukrolü kebako sheco thi motame?
english: For what have I to do with also judging those who are outside? Don't you judge those who are within?

chokri: Vele Jihova sekemoko zori sheco thi bayo, Süla neko dolü thüma kesuko khipü düvate.
english: But those who are outside, God judges. "Put away the wicked man from among yourselves."

chokri: Neko dolü humiyo kenyo ta lize, jünyoo khipü matho kemoko mhüjülü vo, kemesa miko mhüjülü mori.
english: Dare any of you, having a matter against his neighbor, go to law before the unrighteous, and not before the saints?

chokri: Kemesako cejüo jünyo keti keto neko semo zome? Mhace neko cejüo sheco thito lize neko dolü mhakenikosheco thiyi hozome?
english: Don't you know that the saints will judge the world? And if the world is judged by you, are you unworthy to judge the smallest matters?

chokri: Uko tüyo choyi miko sheco thi keto neko seba moteme? Sizilize cejü hilü kehükola u dipü thiti mesü ba?
english: Don't you know that we will judge angels? How much more, things that pertain to this life?

chokri: Mhace neko tsüdo kehüko la sheco thi to lize kehulü keniko la mhalü ti mesü mome.
english: If then, you have to judge things pertaining to this life, do you set them to judge who are of no account in the assembly?

chokri: A kepo neko la thenga. Neko dolü mhakese mipüri di teme? Mote, neko sazü jüra dolü sheco kethi bamozo teme?
english: I say this to move you to shame. Isn't there even one wise man among you who would be able to decide between his brothers?

chokri: Vele njürao püsazüozüde jünyo celü vo, süo kemelümi kemopü.
english: But brother goes to law with brother, and that before unbelievers!

chokri: Tsüdo neko dolü kekrishu bate, neko neko hunehu züde thezho kekhi jünyo celü kenyo bate. Dipüla neko humi süo khiyi mota? Neko dipü thide kekri ketako khisü mota?
english: Therefore it is already altogether a defect in you, that you have lawsuits one with another. Why not rather be wronged? Why not rather be defrauded?

chokri: Mote, neko kekri bate, vajo tate, süko nesazü jürako zo (uthi upho thi kemesa keyi, ukhrü kemesa keva).
english: No, but you yourselves do wrong, and defraud, and that against your brothers.

chokri: Mezi kemoko Jihova kede kalü ngoyi ho, siti neko semo zome? Mipo neko kedova hite, kerezhü miko, mori therü marhe kereshüko, mori thethenu miko, mori hüketheno ketako, mori mihino kethiko, mori thepumi züde mhakesu kethiko sheri.
english: Or don't you know that the unrighteous will not inherit the Kingdom of God? Don't be deceived. Neither the sexually immoral, nor idolaters, nor adulterers, nor male prostitutes, nor homosexuals,

chokri: Mori keregomi sheri, mhakemehoko, kemezü miko, kezhe kenyüko, mizhü süti ketako, Jihova kede kalü thiyi ho.
english: nor thieves, nor covetous, nor drunkards, nor slanderers, nor extortioners, will inherit the Kingdom of God.

chokri: Neko dolü humi süko bi tate, vele neko khrü kemesa kevakote, vele neko Niepoo Jisu zalü thi kematho kesüko, mu Kemesa Rüpü uko Rüo zalü.
english: Such were some of you, but you were washed. But you were sanctified. But you were justified in the name of the Lord Jesus, and in the Spirit of our God.

chokri: Mhametüko ala thüzho tsozo, vele thezho metü vetsomo, mha metülü ala thezho ba, vele akhipü thezho khro süho.
english: "All things are lawful for me," but not all things are expedient. "All things are lawful for me," but I will not be brought under the power of anything.

chokri: Ketico sü uvade lazo, mu uvade sü ketico la; vele Jihova süni kenani meka vato. Tsühi uthi upho kekhrü kezü zo keto lamote, vele Niepoo zhüte. Mu Niepoo sü uthi upho late.
english: "Foods for the belly, and the belly for foods," but God will bring to nothing both it and them. But the body is not for sexual immorality, but for the Lord; and the Lord for the body.

chokri: Mu Jihova Niepoo phu süte sübi uko sheri phu merhü sü tato, pü kekulü.
english: Now God raised up the Lord, and will also raise us up by his power.

chokri: Neko semome? Uthi upho sü Niepoo zapüzo. Sizisüze uthi upho khipü thethenu mipü zhü thiva tome? Shütozo. (Jihova khave).
english: Don't you know that your bodies are members of Christ? Shall I then take the members of Christ, and make them members of a prostitute? May it never be!

chokri: Dipüla neko semota? Mipü püthi püpho khipü thethenumi tsüva lize unithipü thi tato.
english: Or don't you know that he who is joined to a prostitute is one body? For, "The two," says he, "will become one flesh."

chokri: Vele mipü ukhapü Niepoo tsü kevasa uni rüpü zhürhü thite.
english: But he who is joined to the Lord is one spirit.

chokri: Keregü kerezhülü tatate, Kephoma kehopü sheri uthi upho chozu laco thi, vele keregü kerezhü thi kesüo kephoma thipü püthi püpho metsu süte.
english: Flee sexual immorality! "Every sin that a man does is outside the body," but he who commits sexual immorality sins against his own body.

chokri: Dipüta? Neko semo zome, nethi nepho sü Kemesa Rüpü nelü kebao reshüce? Neko lü Jihova ba mu netho nezhü moketa?
english: Or don't you know that your body is a temple of the Holy Spirit which is in you, which you have from God? You are not your own,

chokri: Kekrelamori mhapü neko khriyi ketala, süla nephopü Jihova la zapozale thi süte, mu ne rüpü sheri süo sü Jihova zhüzo tila.
english: for you were bought with a price. Therefore glorify God in your body and in your spirit, which are God's.

chokri: Hice neko lesi thopü ace kesü kola; thepu mipü thenomi yi kemo ve,
english: Now concerning the things about which you wrote to me: it is good for a man not to touch a woman.

chokri: Sisheri keregü kerezhü therüyi keto la thepumi mipü tho pücemi neta, mu thenomi sheri pü nopu nete.
english: But, because of sexual immoralities, let each man have his own wife, and let each woman have her own husband.

chokri: Mi nopu ne ucemi neyo thi kezüdü thipü tsüte, mu sübi micemi neri unopu neyo thi kezü dü thi tsü te.
english: Let the husband render to his wife the affection owed her, and likewise also the wife to her husband.

chokri: Mi cemi ne utho uthi upho yo pü, keku bamo; vele pü nopu zhüte; mu sübi mi nopu ne sheri püpho yo pü keku ba mote, vele pü cemizhü.
english: The wife doesn't have authority over her own body, but the husband. Likewise also the husband doesn't have authority over his own body, but the wife.

chokri: Hunehu kemenyü thisü mota hite, mhapüce umedo kemethade bata zo kemosa, kecho kemevo kethi pü lazo kemosa; süsalü ta sala voriyi, Satan ne kethü kerü kebalü vota ne kedo taze su tila.
english: Don't deprive one another, unless it is by consent for a season, that you may give yourselves to fasting and prayer, and may be together again, that Satan doesn't tempt you because of your lack of self-control.

chokri: Vele I hio kepo neko medo ketso lüzo, moze su sitilü mode;
english: But this I say by way of concession, not of commandment.

chokri: Kekrelamori thüpumi metüpo abita nyü, vele thüma püpü hi Jihova celü mhaphrü ba phrü zo, mipü püzho kre mu kekre mipü mha kekre püba.
english: Yet I wish that all men were like me. However each man has his own gift from God, one of this kind, and another of that kind.

chokri: I süla ceyo kemoko mu samemiko ce poti, uko po abi bata lize ve zoto.
english: But I say to the unmarried and to widows, it is good for them if they remain even as I am.

chokri: Vele uko siziyi hota lize, uko po ceyo tate, kekrelamori ceyo keta kemenyü rüpü kebace veku tila.
english: But if they don't have self-control, let them marry. For it's better to marry than to burn.

chokri: Ceyo ketako ce neko po thino kesü, I no mori Niepoo no, micemi ne po unopu necelü tata hite,
english: But to the married I command--not I, but the Lord--that the wife not leave her husband

chokri: Mhace tata lize püpo ceyo sala hite, mori püpo pü nopu zü kefüse sala yite, mu mi nopu neko sheri ucemi hu meta va hite.
english: (but if she departs, let her remain unmarried, or else be reconciled to her husband), and that the husband not leave his wife.

chokri: Vele mha kekreko I po süyo, Niepoo po moho, mhace usazüjürako ucemi melü kemomi ta lize, mu theno pü püzü tanyü ta lize pücemi süo hu meta va hite.
english: But to the rest I--not the Lord--say, if any brother has an unbelieving wife, and she is content to live with him, let him not leave her.

chokri: Thenomi unopu melü kemomi pü ta lize mu thepuo pücemi zü hüta nyü tisa unipo kezü tate, thenopü po pü nopu khava hite.
english: The woman who has an unbelieving husband, and he is content to live with her, let her not leave her husband.

chokri: Kekrelamori melü kemo nopu-o pü cemi la pü thi kemesa yiyo tila, mu melü kemo cemi pü nopu kemelü la püthi kemesa yi ziyo; kemosa ne nuneko mesamo tato; vele tsühi uko mesa bate.
english: For the unbelieving husband is sanctified in the wife, and the unbelieving wife is sanctified in the husband. Otherwise your children would be unclean, but now are they holy.

chokri: Vele melü kemoo tata nyüta lize püpo tata te. Sazüjüra mu ulipü neko kemelü ba ketami uko phapü mezümo tila, vele Jihova uko cü mekrepü kezü keve thelü kereyi lü sü.
english: Yet if the unbeliever departs, let there be separation. The brother or the sister is not under bondage in such cases, but God has called us in peace.

chokri: Kekrelamori micemi neko neko se bame neko nopu neko kelayi keto, mori minopu neko neko cemi neko kelayi keto se bame?
english: For how do you know, wife, whether you will save your husband? Or how do you know, husband, whether you will save your wife?

chokri: Vele Jihova mha kezapü mi püpü ti tsüsü, Niepoo uko cüpü kemeta uko sülü tayi khe, I kehu metüla a ketsüsü.
english: Only, as the Lord has distributed to each man, as God has called each, so let him walk. So I command in all the assemblies.

chokri: Thüma humi yi kerülü pü cüyi lize püpo yirü kemomi thita hite, humi yi rü kemolü pücüyi lize püpo yi kerümi thita hite.
english: Was anyone called having been circumcised? Let him not become uncircumcised. Has anyone been called in uncircumcision? Let him not be circumcised.

chokri: Yirü tisü mhapü zomo, mu yi rü kemo sü sheri mhapü zomo, vele Jihova jüpo khakesüko thimu zü keyizo.
english: Circumcision is nothing, and uncircumcision is nothing, but the keeping of the commandments of God.

chokri: Thüma kehopüri pücü keyilü bayi te;
english: Let each man stay in that calling in which he was called.

chokri: N cüpü pü thekümi thisü me? Süo no thejü thi kebace No süo lümo hite; vele ncüpü hekesümi thisü me süo khiyi te.
english: Were you called being a bondservant? Don't let that bother you, but if you get an opportunity to become free, use it.

chokri: Niepoo cüyi ketamiko pü thekü mithi te Niepoo he kevami, cüyi ketako hesü ve, mu Khrista thekümi thite.
english: For he who was called in the Lord being a bondservant is the Lord's free man. Likewise he who was called being free is Christ's bondservant.

chokri: Neko mhama khipü khriyi ketako, neko thüma ceta thihi tate.
english: You were bought with a price. Don't become bondservants of men.

chokri: Sazüjürako, thüma kehopüri pücüyi ketako Jihova yo thayi te.
english: Brothers, let each man, in whatever condition he was called, stay in that condition with God.

chokri: Reliphosako Niepoo celü jü khakesü I ngo zoyi mo, siri a tho alü kelü khasüto, mipü phikemezhe Niepoo celü ngoyi ta lize, kemelülü thayi te.
english: Now concerning virgins, I have no commandment from the Lord, but I give my judgment as one who has obtained mercy from the Lord to be trustworthy.

chokri: A kelüce mha hiko tsü kehülü kemezhe keba tülü la vezoto, A kepo thüma püla keve sülü bayi te.
english: I think that it is good therefore, because of the distress that is on us, that it is good for a man to be as he is.

chokri: No thenopü züta keto rophalü bateme sülü bayi te, rephrü keto lü hite. N cemi ropha kedimi theno keyi lühi.
english: Are you bound to a wife? Don't seek to be freed. Are you free from a wife? Don't seek a wife.

chokri: Vele mhace no theno yiyi teme, kephomo thi tamo, mu mhace reliphosa mipü ceyo (nhita) zori süo kephoma tamo; sizi zori nthi npho kemenyü lüzhe la, ntho mhayi te, (I pomo tato).
english: But if you marry, you have not sinned. If a virgin marries, she has not sinned. Yet such will have oppression in the flesh, and I want to spare you.

chokri: Vele akepola, azüneko, pütü kejüla utho uthilü tha yite, ucemi kene kori mu ucemi kediko sheri kenani la.
english: But I say this, brothers: the time is short, that from now on, both those who have wives may be as though they had none;

chokri: Kra ketako, krakemobi; rechü ketami rethu kemobi; mhakhri keyimi mhapüri kedi bi.
english: and those who weep, as though they didn't weep; and those who rejoice, as though they didn't rejoice; and those who buy, as though they didn't possess;

chokri: Cejükhi ketako uko süo mesu kemobi, kekrelamori cejü hio tata zoto.
english: and those who use the world, as not using it to the fullest. For the mode of this world passes away.

chokri: Vele I nekopo mha süko khi menu zoba ti amedomo, Pü ceyo ketami cejülü zhüko la mhalü tayo, vele ceyo kemoko Niepoo nü kethi lü tayo.
english: But I desire to have you to be free from cares. He who is unmarried is concerned for the things of the Lord, how he may please the Lord;

chokri: Vele ceyo ketami cejülü zhüko menu de lü tayo; dipü bide pü cemi nü thita ziti.
english: but he who is married is concerned about the things of the world, how he may please his wife.

chokri: Reliphosapü mu micemi ni dolü sheri kekreti ba; ceyo kemomi Niepoo la mhalü tayo, pü thi püpho sheri mu pü rüpü la sheri kenani la vele ceyo ketapü cejülü lüzheko lü tayo, dipü bide pü nopu nü thiyi ta.
english: There is also a difference between a wife and a virgin. The unmarried woman cares about the things of the Lord, that she may be holy both in body and in spirit. But she who is married cares about the things of the world--how she may please her husband.

chokri: Neko kemevela I hiohi posüho, keba (zoma) khi neko mava keto lamo; mha kezüve mu Niepoo la tso keti neko kemeka lamo.
english: This I say for your own profit; not that I may ensnare you, but for that which is appropriate, and that you may attend to the Lord without distraction.

chokri: Vele mipü mhace reli kezüve pü nyepü pü kebace pü krokrüyi hota lize mu süo khimo suta lize püpo pü kelü thi tate, pü kephoma thimo unipo keri tate.
english: But if any man thinks that he is behaving inappropriately toward his virgin, if she is past the flower of her age, and if need so requires, let him do what he desires. He doesn't sin. Let them marry.

chokri: Siri pü melü tha metüyi, khi moze sumo ti bamo, vele pü keku pü kethükerü ti pi ba mu pü merho vamo de phosa thiyi ve keto sa, süo vetüzo.
english: But he who stands steadfast in his heart, having no necessity, but has power over his own heart, to keep his own virgin, does well.

chokri: Süla ceyo ta zori vezo, vele ceyo homo zi to lize süo vekuzo.
english: So then both he who gives his own virgin in marriage does well, and he who doesn't give her in marriage does better.

chokri: Mi cemi ne thezho khro batayo, pü nopu rhü kebathe, vele mhace pü nopu sata lize, thenopü pü medo thi ta to, thenopü sopü ce nhito sheri pü medo te, Niepoo lebi zalü.
english: A wife is bound by law for as long as her husband lives; but if the husband is dead, she is free to be married to whoever she desires, only in the Lord.

chokri: Vele thenopü pünü kuto ceyo mode pülebi bata lize, akelüce, vele alü kelü ce Niepoo rüpü azüta.
english: But she is happier if she stays as she is, in my judgment, and I think that I also have God's Spirit.

chokri: Tsühi mha khapü therümarhe reshü kevako la uko metü se bate. Mhase uthi kerekrü sü, vele mikekhrü mhathi kezüve süyo.
english: Now concerning things sacrificed to idols: We know that we all have knowledge. Knowledge puffs up, but love builds up.

chokri: Mhace thüma humi mhahü se bale lüta lize, pü mhase kezü sheri se ba mo zolu.
english: But if anyone thinks that he knows anything, he doesn't yet know as he ought to know.

chokri: Vele humi mhace Jihova khrü ta lize thüma sümi utho use bate.
english: But if anyone loves God, the same is known by him.

chokri: Mha khapü therümarhe reshü keva keti la, uko sekeba marhe sü mhapüri zomo cejülü therü kekre bamo, mipü lebi zo, (Jihova zo)
english: Therefore concerning the eating of things sacrificed to idols, we know that no idol is anything in the world, and that there is no other God but one.

chokri: Kekrela mori mhakehopüri cüpü Jihova le zi keta, tülü sheri mu cejü lü sheri therü kekro ba mu niepo sheri kekroba.
english: For though there are things that are called "gods," whether in the heavens or on earth; as there are many "gods" and many "lords;"

chokri: Vele ukola Therü pü lebi uko Po püyo mha metü ba, mu uko ulü ba; mu Niepo Jisu Khrista pü lüba, püyo mha metü ba, mu uko püyoba.
english: yet to us there is one God, the Father, of whom are all things, and we for him; and one Lord, Jesus Christ, through whom are all things, and we live through him.

chokri: Siri thüma kehopüri se bamo, bavüdo hilü ketso humi therü marhe reshü keva zhü lü mode ti tazo, uko lü kelü thathi mu merho ve.
english: However, that knowledge isn't in all men. But some, with consciousness of the idol until now, eat as of a thing sacrificed to an idol, and their conscience, being weak, is defiled.

chokri: Vele themu ukechüpü Jihova ce sümo, mori uko themu keti la mice veku tamo, mori uko themu timo ketala thathi ku tamo.
english: But food will not commend us to God. For neither, if we don't eat, are we the worse; nor, if we eat, are we the better.

chokri: Vele menu neceriyi te, pü co keho püla zo sheri, n kekha kedila, n yo n lano, miphe kemesü thi ta to kemelü kethathi kola.
english: But be careful that by no means does this liberty of yours become a stumbling block to the weak.

chokri: Kekrelamori mhace thüma hu therü marhe celü n ngosü lize, ketico ti kebace, uko kelüthi kethepu sü uko po (kemelü kethathiko) therü marhe reshü keva ketico ti tato.
english: For if a man sees you who have knowledge sitting in an idol's temple, won't his conscience, if he is weak, be emboldened to eat things sacrificed to idols?

chokri: N mhase sülü n sazüo kemelü kethathio po kata to, püla Khrista sasü zori.
english: And through your knowledge, he who is weak perishes, the brother for whose sake Christ died.

chokri: Vele neko sübi kephoma thisü n sazüo metsu sü, mu kemelü kethathi kelü thi kesüla neko kephoma thipü Khrista metsu süte.
english: Thus, sinning against the brothers, and wounding their conscience when it is weak, you sin against Christ.

chokri: Süla mhace ketico püla nsazüo po jota lize, I themu timo tato, cejülü kebace, sizi kemosa asazüo po jota ze kesu la.
english: Therefore, if food causes my brother to stumble, I will eat no meat forevermore, that I don't cause my brother to stumble.

chokri: I apostle mome? I hekevami moteme? I uko Niepo Jisu Khrista ngo sü mome? Nekohi Niepoo zalü amhatho mome?
english: Am I not free? Am I not an apostle? Haven't I seen Jesus Christ, our Lord? Aren't you my work in the Lord?

chokri: Mhace I mi kekre mila I apostle mota sheri, nekola I apostle zo; kekrelamori I apostle zo tisü mohor a yo vü keyi Niepoo la nekoyo.
english: If to others I am not an apostle, yet at least I am to you; for you are the seal of my apostleship in the Lord.

chokri: I kelakesüfü ukofükho kesü, a pe adojü kebako la hiohi.
english: My defense to those who examine me is this.

chokri: Ha mhati mhakra keti keku di teme?
english: Have we no right to eat and to drink?

chokri: Hako ulipü neko ucemi chotha züta ti thiyi su teme, apostle kekreko bi, u sazü jürako neko Niepoo yo baketako bi mu Cephas (neko bi).
english: Have we no right to take along a wife who is a believer, even as the rest of the apostles, and the brothers of the Lord, and Cephas?

chokri: Mori I mu Barnaba hani lebi, mhatho thiyi su bateme? (Somi jüyikeve methayo sheri jüyikeve lü hü mozesu).
english: Or have only Barnabas and I no right to not work?

chokri: Somi therilü lavo sheri pütho pü medolü tavo yome? Mori humi khubü yi te pü meta de püse timo keba bame? Mori metho meri de pü nojü krakemo ba me?
english: What soldier ever serves at his own expense? Who plants a vineyard, and doesn't eat of its fruit? Or who feeds a flock, and doesn't drink from the flock's milk?

chokri: Mha hiko kepo I thüma zholü po bame? Mori thezholü süko pomo zome?
english: Do I speak these things according to the ways of men? Or doesn't the law also say the same thing?

chokri: Kekrelamori Moses lesida lü thopü kemeta, no thodo zü teha ni kebace püta phava hite, Jihova thodoko kekhrüla posüme?
english: For it is written in the law of Moses, "You shall not muzzle an ox while it treads out the grain." Is it for the oxen that God cares,

chokri: Mori pü ukola mhaposü mome? Kekrelamori uko lazo, sejo mozo; uko la thosüzo; süla tefü (yite) he ketiko ukemeri kebala thi ba, mu niyo tiko sheri ukemeri kebala teha ni bayo.
english: or does he say it assuredly for our sake? Yes, it was written for our sake, because he who plows ought to plow in hope, and he who threshes in hope should partake of his hope.

chokri: Mhace hako rüpü tsava thi nekoyo sü neko thi upho zhüko rü sala keyi süo mhakezhome?
english: If we sowed to you spiritual things, is it a great thing if we reap your fleshly things?

chokri: Mhace kekremi keku hilü nekoyo keku bata lize, hako süko ceri kukuti mesüzo. Sizo sheri hako nekoyo sizi yomo (keku süo khi yomo). Vele mha metülü kemezhe tazo, kemosa jüyi keveo Khrista chokha thiva su tila.
english: If others partake of this right over you, don't we yet more? Nevertheless we did not use this right, but we bear all things, that we may cause no hindrance to the Gospel of Christ.

chokri: Neko semo teme, kemesa choyi kethi mi kereshücelü mhakemesalü hü moze kesu? Mu reshü bayo kezü mhathi kebako sheri sübizo mome?
english: Don't you know that those who serve around sacred things eat from the things of the temple, and those who wait on the altar have their portion with the altar?

chokri: Niepoo mhatholü jüyikeve po ketola kenhü keyiko jüyi kevelü hüti mesü zo.
english: Even so the Lord ordained that those who proclaim the Gospel should live from the Gospel.

chokri: Vele I mha süko püri khitamo, mori mhahiko püri I tho sümo; nekopo thipü ayo süno ziti, kekrelamori ala zono kesa veku, thüma kehopü sheri azapozale khipü kemethu thiva keti ce sheri.
english: But I have used none of these things, and I don't write these things that it may be done so in my case; for I would rather die, than that anyone should make my boasting void.

chokri: Kekrelamori I jüyikeve zhopo thita sheri I mhapü lari zapozale thita mozo, moze kesu khapü ayo sü kevala, ile, I jüyikeve zhopo thimo ketosa, kesukerho ayo!
english: For if I preach the Gospel, I have nothing to boast about; for necessity is laid on me; but woe is to me, if I don't preach the Gospel.

chokri: Kekrelamori I mhace anü amedolü mha hio thita lize, azhokhro ngoyi to; vele mhace anü amedo metsu de thiyo lize jüyikeve keza kesülü kha amesülü sü kevala.
english: For if I do this of my own will, I have a reward. But if not of my own will, I have a stewardship entrusted to me.

chokri: Kedipü azhokhro ba? Kethozo, I jüyikeve kepoce mikethu kesü püma thipü atsü no ziyomo, side jüyikeve keku I kebü vamo ketola.
english: What then is my reward? That, when I preach the Gospel, I may present the Gospel of Christ without charge, so as not to abuse my authority in the Gospel.

chokri: Kekrelamori thüma metü celü akekha püri bamozo sheri I athipü metüko thekümi thi tazo, sizi lize pra meve sayi ketola.
english: For though I was free from all, I brought myself under bondage to all, that I might gain the more.

chokri: Jewsmiko ce I Jewsmi thi yo, side Jewmiko züpü vori ketola; uko thezhoo khrikhro ketako zü de thezhoo khrota, side thezho kezümi püzü vori ketola;
english: To the Jews I became as a Jew, that I might gain Jews; to those who are under the law, as under the law, that I might gain those who are under the law;

chokri: Thezho kediko zü ketace thezho kedimi bi, (Jihova zhoko kedimi) Khrista zhozü; side thezho kedimi zü vori ketola.
english: to those who are without law, as without law (not being without law toward God, but under law toward Christ), that I might win those who are without law.

chokri: Kethathimi zü ketace I sheri kethathiko bi, side uko zü vori ketola; athipü thüma kehopü sheri bisü, sizi keyilü humi kelayiketola.
english: To the weak I became as weak, that I might gain the weak. I have become all things to all men, that I may by all means save some.

chokri: I mhatho hibi thiyo züyi keve lano, sizi keyilü I sheri neko züde zhürhü thite.
english: Now I do this for the Gospel's sake, that I may be a joint partaker of it.

chokri: Neko semo zome, takemerha tilü metü tasü zori mipü lebi vekeyi zhokhro ngoyi yo? Süla neko tasüte, süo ngoyi ketola.
english: Don't you know that those who run in a race all run, but one receives the prize? Run like that, that you may win.

chokri: Thüma kehopüri thehe kethilü u kethükerü moze su, mha metülü tsüdo uko thi kebao kata keto dopülebi la kedepira süo phu tayo, vele uko suta keho kedepira la.
english: Every man who strives in the games exercises self-control in all things. Now they do it to receive a corruptible crown, but we an incorruptible.

chokri: I süla ta kemerha tazo, setü kemozhü lamo, I süo merha tazo, humi tükhra zü kevü tayo, vele I süko bi tamo.
english: I therefore run like that, as not uncertainly. I fight like that, as not beating the air,

chokri: Vele I athi apho kekhapü akhro süyi, sizi kemosa I mice jüyikeve posü süce mipo a hu kedü kevami thita mo ketola.
english: but I beat my body and bring it into submission, lest by any means, after I have preached to others, I myself should be rejected.

chokri: Sülebi zo mori, azüneko, I nekopo semo melo banyümo, dipü bide uponeko kemhü khrolü ta vo sheli, mu jükezho lü cuvo sheli.
english: Now I would not have you ignorant, brothers, that our fathers were all under the cloud, and all passed through the sea;

chokri: Mu uko metü Moses belü jülopü kemhülü mu jükezholü.
english: and were all baptized into Moses in the cloud and in the sea;

chokri: Mu uko metü rüpülü themuti.
english: and all ate the same spiritual food;

chokri: Mu uko metü rüpülü jükra, rüpülü tsüro uko sameji ketao yo jükra, süo Khrista tsüro.
english: and all drank the same spiritual drink. For they drank of a spiritual rock that followed them, and the rock was Christ.

chokri: Vele uko kekro Jihova po pünüyi mote, kekrelamori uko kha nhatsalü kelhu kevala.
english: However with most of them, God was not well pleased, for they were overthrown in the wilderness.

chokri: Tsüdo mhahiko khi uko la thaka pü thiba, mhakesuzhüko meho menyü mo ketola, uko kemenyü zhüko.
english: Now these things were our examples, to the intent we should not lust after evil things, as they also lusted.

chokri: Mori nekopo therümarheko reshü moketo, süko humibi, theju thopü kemeta, thümako mhati mhakra keyisa tosü rezhü ta.
english: Neither be idolaters, as some of them were. As it is written, "The people sat down to eat and drink, and rose up to play."

chokri: Mori uko keregükerezhü thimo lekhe, uko humi thi ketabi, mu uko tüsozho pü nha thüma nye mecüsü retuta (nye sü Ex. 32; 28).
english: Neither let us commit sexual immorality, as some of them committed, and in one day twenty-three thousand fell.

chokri: Mori humi Khrista medophi keta bi uko sizi mo lekhe, mu tenhi po uko medokhri kevabi.
english: Neither let us test the Lord, as some of them tested, and perished by the serpents.

chokri: Mori humi panyo paro ketala neko panyo paro ta, mu kekalü uko mekava.
english: Neither grumble, as some of them also grumbled, and perished by the destroyer.

chokri: Tsüdo mhahiko thaka thi tate, uko yo kethiko mu mha süko thopü ukola tarho kesüko la cejü pü thüla thi keta vori te.
english: Now all these things happened to them by way of example, and they were written for our admonition, on whom the ends of the ages have come.

chokri: Süla mhakelümiko thayi te mo lize retu tari semo tila.
english: Therefore let him who thinks he stands be careful that he doesn't fall.

chokri: Medo kephi neko yo khasümo, thüma metü zho zo kemosa, vele Jihova jüthozo, pü nepo hüyikeve kezüpü neko medophi süho, vele hüyikeveko lebi zoto, mu medokephi vori tice sheri chode phupü therü therayi keve thi süto, mu neko hüyikeve thi sü to.
english: No temptation has taken you except what is common to man. God is faithful, who will not allow you to be tempted above what you are able, but will with the temptation also make the way of escape, that you may be able to endure it.

chokri: Süla akekhrü miko, marhe kereshülü tazo tate.
english: Therefore, my beloved, flee from idolatry.

chokri: I mhakese m ketsümi ce kepobi po süho, neko I dipüposü sheri lü medo bate.
english: I speak as to wise men. Judge what I say.

chokri: Tehe khapü uko kechothi kesü (jokesü) ce Khrista za la kehu ba mome? Tsükhri yo jo kesüce uko cephro kesü Khrista thi püpho la kehu ba mome?
english: The cup of blessing which we bless, isn't it a communion of the blood of Christ? The bread which we break, isn't it a communion of the body of Christ?

chokri: Uko thüma kekro tsükhri pü yozo, mu phopü zo, kekrelamori uko tsükhri pü keza ti kebala.
english: Because we, who are many, are one bread, one body; for we all partake of the one bread.

chokri: Isreal uthiupho laco vo keta pesü te, uko themu ketiko rütho kethilü reshüba zü kerhü mome?
english: Consider Israel after the flesh. Don't those who eat the sacrifices have communion with the altar?

chokri: Süsa I dipü po bata? Therü marhe sü mhapü zome? Mori reshü thipü therü marhe ce kesü mhapü zome?
english: What am I saying then? That a thing sacrificed to idols is anything, or that an idol is anything?

chokri: Vele a kepo therüna (judakemoko) rütho thikesüko thipü therükesuko ce süyo; mu thipü Jihova ce sü yomo; mu I nekopo therü kesuo zü ketekhre nyü mo.
english: But I say that the things which the Gentiles sacrifice, they sacrifice to demons, and not to God, and I don't desire that you would have communion with demons.

chokri: Neko Niepoo he kra mu therükesuko he sheri kra yi su; neko Niepoo zü kemekro (lütilüle) thi pü miz püyo mu therükesuko miz sheri püzü kemekro sizi yiho.
english: You can't both drink the cup of the Lord and the cup of demons. You can't both partake of the table of the Lord, and of the table of demons.

chokri: Uko Niepoo lüda valapü püpo uro kene thisü tome? Neko püce ku meco ku teme?
english: Or do we provoke the Lord to jealousy? Are we stronger than he?

chokri: Mha metü ala thezho zo; vele metü ala khimo kesu zomo; mhametü ala thezho tsüzo, vele metü mhathi kezüve sü yomo.
english: "All things are lawful for me," but not all things are profitable. "All things are lawful for me," but not all things build up.

chokri: Thümapü pori pütho pü zhozhü lü hite, vele thüma kekrepü cene lü sü te.
english: Let no one seek his own, but each one his neighbor's good.

chokri: Mha kehopüri ketukeholü zhu kesüko, mhaketila, pülü pütü püzho me moshi ketso zo mori ve.
english: Whatever is sold in the butcher shop, eat, asking no question for the sake of conscience,

chokri: Kekrelamori cejüsü Niepoo zhüzo, mu sülü chupü keta metü sheri.
english: for "the earth is the Lord's, and its fullness."

chokri: Mhace uko humi melüyi kemomi nzü tavo npo tehü tehülü hino ncüta lize mu no tavota, lize mhakehopü sheri kha n mhüjülü kesüo tite, pütü püzho nlü ketsakhu ketsozo mori ve.
english: But if one of those who don't believe invites you to a meal, and you are inclined to go, eat whatever is set before you, asking no questions for the sake of conscience.

chokri: Vele humi nce poti, mha hiohi rütho thipü therü marhe reshü ti zhüle zita lize, süo tihite po mese kevala, nlükelü ketsode n medo mo ketala; kekrelamori cejü sü Niepoo zhü mu sülü chupü keta metü.
english: But if anyone says to you, "This was offered to idols," don't eat it for the sake of the one who told you, and for the sake of conscience. For "the earth is the Lord's, and all its fullness."

chokri: Kelü a kepo nla zomo, vele kekremila, atho amedo kethila kekremi lü kelü ketso bazota?
english: Conscience, I say, not your own, but the other's conscience. For why is my liberty judged by another conscience?

chokri: Kekrelamori I ngokemezhelü kezüpü thita lize, I dipü la mhakesu poba ta pü kemeza kha sü keva kola?
english: If I partake with thankfulness, why am I denounced for that for which I give thanks?

chokri: Neko mhati sheri, mori kra sheri, mori mhakehopü nekotho thizo sheri, metü Niepoo zapozale thi zote.
english: Whether therefore you eat, or drink, or whatever you do, do all to the glory of God.

chokri: Mipü sheri mejo nümo thiva hite; Jewsmiko yo sheri mori Gentilemi yo sheri; mori Jihova kehuce yo sheri.
english: Give no occasions for stumbling, either to Jews, or to Greeks, or to the assembly of God;

chokri: I sheri thüma metüko nüthi ta mhametülü, sübi I atho ala kemeve lü tamo, vele metüla kemeve, side uko po kelakeyi ngoyi ketola.
english: even as I also please all men in all things, not seeking my own profit, but the profit of the many, that they may be saved.

chokri: Neko asameji yite, I sheri Khrista meji keta bi.
english: Be imitators of me, even as I also am of Christ.

chokri: Tsühi I neko chu süyo, azüneko, neko mha metülü a rekra kebala, kesüzho I khapü neko ketsü bi.
english: Now I praise you, brothers, that you remember me in all things, and hold firm the traditions, even as I delivered them to you.

chokri: Vele thüma kehopü pi sheri thepio sü Khrista zo, mu thenomi pio sü thepumi, mu Khrista pio sü Jihova zo.
english: But I would have you know that the head of every man is Christ, and the head of the woman is the man, and the head of Christ is God.

chokri: Thüma kehopüri kecho kethice mu saju kepoce upi hupü, süo püpi rho mote.
english: Every man praying or prophesying, having his head covered, dishonors his head.

chokri: Vele thenomi kehopüri kechothi mu saju poti upi hupü mo (hepü) keba upi rhomo, kekrelamori süo püpi tha keva bi ketala.
english: But every woman praying or prophesying with her head unveiled dishonors her head. For it is one and the same thing as if she were shaved.

chokri: Mhace thenomi upi humo lize püpo upitha vate, vele mhace thenopü upitha menga lize püpo püpi huyi te.
english: For if a woman is not covered, let her also be shorn. But if it is shameful for a woman to be shorn or shaved, let her be covered.

chokri: Thepumi upi huzü mozo, pü Jihova zapozale hü tila, vele thenomi thepumi la zapozale.
english: For a man indeed ought not to have his head covered, because he is the image and glory of God, but the woman is the glory of the man.

chokri: Thepumi sü thenomi lamo, vele thenomi thepumi lazo.
english: For man is not from woman, but woman from man;

chokri: Mori thepumi thenomi la thi sümo, vele thenomi thepumi la thisü.
english: for neither was man created for the woman, but woman for the man.

chokri: Mha hio la thenomi keku upiyo ba, kekrelamori tüyo jüyimi la.
english: For this cause the woman ought to have authority on her head, because of the angels.

chokri: Kemosa thepumi thenomi samo, mori thenomi thepumi samo, Niepoo celü.
english: Nevertheless, neither is the woman independent of the man, nor the man independent of the woman, in the Lord.

chokri: Thenomi sü thepumi la mu thepumi sheri thenomila; vele metü Jihova zhü.
english: For as woman came from man, so a man also comes through a woman; but all things are from God.

chokri: Neko mhape süte, thenomi upi humo de kecho kethi pe züve me?
english: Judge for yourselves. Is it appropriate that a woman pray to God unveiled?

chokri: Süculü (hülü) zho sheri neko methasümome? Thepumipü upitha kecho püta lize püla thenga ta mome?
english: Doesn't even nature itself teach you that if a man has long hair, it is a dishonor to him?

chokri: Vele theno mipü pü pitha kecho püta lize püla thechutherho, kekrelamori püpitha pü pühuyi ketola.
english: But if a woman has long hair, it is a glory to her, for her hair is given to her for a covering.

chokri: Vele thüma hu mhace kemeli nyüta lize, hako thezho siziti bamo; mori Jihova kehuce kolü.
english: But if any man seems to be contentious, we have no such custom, neither do God's assemblies.

chokri: Tsü mha hiola I neko chumo keta, kehala kesü, neko mha veku tila tavo kenguyi kemo, vele suku keta,
english: But in giving you this command, I don't praise you, that you come together not for the better but for the worse.

chokri: Theralü neko tavo kehucelü kengu keyi ce, I sekeba neko dolü kelakre ba ketapoba mu I süo (melüte) ketüpü melü bate.
english: For first of all, when you come together in the assembly, I hear that divisions exist among you, and I partly believe it.

chokri: Neko dolü ketherati bata metsete, züyi ketako utho uthi kerekrü ba keta, neko dolü.
english: For there also must be factions among you, that those who are approved may be revealed among you.

chokri: Neko tavo thethi pülü kengu keyice, hio tülü mha keti tümo, Niepoo kerakra co la.
english: When therefore you assemble yourselves together, it is not possible to eat the Lord's supper.

chokri: Kekrelamori kehopüri pütho hi mhüjü mha tiyi ketola; mipü meri ta lize, mu kekremi pü mezüta.
english: For in your eating each one takes his own supper before others. One is hungry, and another is drunken.

chokri: Dipüla? Neko bamo netho nece lü mha keti mhakra ketola mono Jihova kehuce meshu (rhomo) süyo me? Mu thenga kha ba kemoko tsü sü züme? I dipü neko ce pota? I neko chusü tomo mhahiko la? I neko chu mo. (Niepo Miz püco mu pütü kethi)
english: What, don't you have houses to eat and to drink in? Or do you despise God's assembly, and put them to shame who don't have? What shall I tell you? Shall I praise you? In this I don't praise you.

chokri: Kekrelamori I Niepoo celü ngo keyi I khapü neko tsü süyo. Niepoo Jisu tüzü ta keta zi pü tsükhri püyi.
english: For I received from the Lord that which also I delivered to you, that the Lord Jesus on the night in which he was betrayed took bread.

chokri: Mu pü kemeza (kethezü) khasü kevasa pü süo cephro sü de poti, “khiyite, püti yite, hiohi aphozo, adophro pü neko la kha kesü, mha hiohi thite a rekra ta ketola”
english: When he had given thanks, he broke it, and said, "Take, eat. This is my body, which is broken for you. Do this in memory of me."

chokri: Süsübi pü teheo ri püyi süo püyi de poti, tehe hio jükho kesa, aza kha kesü, tsü kenu thithite neko kra keto hio, a rekrata ketola.
english: In the same way he also took the cup, after supper, saying, "This cup is the new covenant in my blood. Do this, as often as you drink, in memory of me."

chokri: Neko tsü hio ketimetsü mu tehe hio kekra metsü, pü sü mese bazo, pü la vori kemothe,
english: For as often as you eat this bread and drink this cup, you proclaim the Lord's death until he comes.

chokri: Kehopü sheri kehopü ceri, tsükhri hio ti mu tehe hio kra tico pütü kemo thi ta lize, Niepoo za mu püthi püphoyo jo tato.
english: Therefore whoever eats this bread or drinks the Lord's cup in a manner unworthy of the Lord will be guilty of the body and the blood of the Lord.

chokri: Vele thümapü putho pülü pü ketsote side tsükhrio ti mu teheo krate,
english: But let a man examine himself, and so let him eat of the bread, and drink of the cup.

chokri: Pütü kemolü tso mode ti mukra pütho pü mejo tato, Niepoo thi püpho lü mo tisa.
english: For he who eats and drinks in an unworthy manner eats and drinks judgment to himself, if he doesn't discern the Lord's body.

chokri: Neko mha hiko la neko dolü thathi mu sürhomo tate, mu humi sa ri te.
english: For this cause many among you are weak and sickly, and not a few sleep.

chokri: Kekrelamori uko utho usheco thizori, mipo uko sheco kethi mesümo.
english: For if we discerned ourselves, we wouldn't be judged.

chokri: Vele uko utho usheco thi zori, Niepoo uko metha (kethipü), side cejüo po uko mejo vamo ketola.
english: But when we are judged, we are punished by the Lord, that we may not be condemned with the world.

chokri: Süla azüneko neko tavo kengu yide ti ketoce, hunehu fü meri süte.
english: Therefore, my brothers, when you come together to eat, wait one for another.

chokri: Mhace humi meri ta lize püpono pücelü mhati merai tate; neko tavori tice kejo kemo thimo ketola, mhakekreko la I tavori tice atho mha pü lüpü püzhoko po süto.
english: But if anyone is hungry, let him eat at home, lest your coming together be for judgment. The rest I will set in order whenever I come.

chokri: Hice tsüdo Rüpü mhaphrüko la, a sazüjürako, I neko po melo de semo ba nyümo.
english: Now concerning spiritual things, brothers, I don't want you to be ignorant.

chokri: Neko Gentilemiko thajü therü marheko po neko tüzü püta, neko chotha zü vote.
english: You know that when you were heathen, you were led away to those mute idols, however you might be led.

chokri: I neko po semu lüyi keto, mipüri Jihova Rüpü kenemi Jisu po reshu kepüo thite sizi yomo, mori mipüri Jisu sü Niepoo zo ziti po yomo, vele Kemesa Rüpü thozo.
english: Therefore I make known to you that no man speaking by God's Spirit says, "Jesus is accursed." No one can say, "Jesus is Lord," but by the Holy Spirit.

chokri: Tsüdo mhaphrü kekrekre tiba, vele Rüpü püzo.
english: Now there are various kinds of gifts, but the same Spirit.

chokri: Mhaketso kekrekre ba, vele Niepo pü lebi zo.
english: There are various kinds of service, and the same Lord.

chokri: Uko mhathoko ri kekrekre, vele Therü püzo pütho mhametü lü mhathothi.
english: There are various kinds of workings, but the same God, who works all things in all.

chokri: Vele Rüpü mhatho mesekesü, sülü thüma kehopü pori meve rüyi ketola.
english: But to each one is given the manifestation of the Spirit for the profit of all.

chokri: Rüpü süo mhapü mipü tsü kesü ketsü kekremipü mhase mhalü, Rüpü süo zo.
english: For to one is given through the Spirit the word of wisdom, and to another the word of knowledge, according to the same Spirit;

chokri: Kekremipü kemelü, Rüpü süo zo, kekre mipü mhaphrü mithi kesürhorüpü süo zo.
english: to another faith, by the same Spirit; and to another gifts of healings, by the same Spirit;

chokri: Kekremipü ungo mhathoko kethi, kekremipü saju kepo; kekremipü kevekesu se kekre keyi; kekremipü jü suko kekro kepo; mu kekremipü jü süko co kepo.
english: and to another workings of miracles; and to another prophecy; and to another discerning of spirits; to another different kinds of languages; and to another the interpretation of languages.

chokri: Vele mhatho süko kethi metülü Rüpü pütho thi, pü tho pü medolü mha kezapü tsü, mipü püla.
english: But the one and the same Spirit works all of these, distributing to each one separately as he desires.

chokri: Kekrelamori uphosü püzo, uzatho kekroba; uzatho metü uphoyoba, zatho kekro vele upho pü lebi zo, süosü Khrista.
english: For as the body is one, and has many members, and all the members of the body, being many, are one body; so also is Christ.

chokri: Rüpü püyo uko metü jülopü, phopüyo; uko tho Jewmi sheri, mori Gentilemi sheri, mori thejümi sheri mori ukepha kedimi sheri, metü Rüpü pü jükra.
english: For in one Spirit we were all baptized into one body, whether Jews or Greeks, whether bond or free; and were all given to drink into one Spirit.

chokri: Upho püyo zatho pü lebimo, vele kekroba.
english: For the body is not one member, but many.

chokri: Mhace uphe poti, I ubemo süla I uthi upho hio mo, sizi taze pü umoyo bamo teme?
english: If the foot would say, "Because I'm not the hand, I'm not part of the body," it is not therefore not part of the body.

chokri: Mu mhace unipoti, I uni kemola I umoyo bamo, pü sizi ketala uphoyo bamo teme?
english: If the ear would say, "Because I'm not the eye, I'm not part of the body," it's not therefore not part of the body.

chokri: Mhace umo metü umhü thita lize, dipüce mhafü seta? Mhace umo metü mhafü kese thita lize, dipüce mharü seyi veta?
english: If the whole body were an eye, where would the hearing be? If the whole were hearing, where would the smelling be?

chokri: Vele tsühi Jihova zatho kekre tiko metü khapü umoyo sü meta, pünü pü medolü.
english: But now God has set the members, each one of them, in the body, just as he desired.

chokri: Mu mhace zathoko metü mhapü lebi ta lize, umo dipü bi tata?
english: If they were all one member, where would the body be?

chokri: Vele tsühi zatho kekro ba, siri umo püzo.
english: But now they are many members, but one body.

chokri: Umhü ube ce I nsü mori ve siyi keyi di, mori upi uphe ce poti I nsa mori ve sizi ti po keyi di.
english: The eye can't tell the hand, "I have no need for you," or again the head to the feet, "I have no need for you."

chokri: Mole, umoyo süpi kezü ba, mele phi kemo kethathiko sheri sü moze suku.
english: No, much rather, those members of the body which seem to be weaker are necessary.

chokri: Umoyo melephi kemo zathoko, ukelüce usüko khimo kesu kekroba phiyo, mu uyo krokrü pü keta zathoko süko mhakekro züveti kemele ba.
english: Those parts of the body which we think to be less honorable, on those we bestow more abundant honor; and our unpresentable parts have more abundant propriety;

chokri: Uyo krokrü pü kebako u sümori veta zi sheri, vele Jihova süko khi umoyo thü kesa sü, therho kha mekro pü kethathi zathoko tsü ku süte.
english: whereas our presentable parts have no such need. But God composed the body together, giving more abundant honor to the inferior part,

chokri: Umoyo kelakre ti bazü mozo, vele umoyo zatho kekrekre tiko mipü kekremipü tsakhu vaphi ti mesüzo.
english: that there should be no division in the body, but that the members should have the same care for one another.

chokri: Uko dolü humi thi bata lize zatho metü thi batayo, süo züde, mori zathopü therho ngo keyi la zatho metü unü rechü batayo.
english: When one member suffers, all the members suffer with it. Or when one member is honored, all the members rejoice with it.

chokri: Tsühi neko Khrista mo thite, mu zathoko mipü pü thi bate.
english: Now you are the body of Christ, and members individually.

chokri: Mü Jihova humi khapü kehucelü sü meta, therao apostleko, kenao sajukepoko, süo kemethamiko, ungo mhatho kethiko, mithi kesürho ketiko, kekhrühiko, mikekha tiko, jü kekre po keyiko;
english: God has set some in the assembly: first apostles, second prophets, third teachers, then miracle workers, then gifts of healings, helps, governments, and various kinds of languages.

chokri: Uko metü apostle miko me? Uko metü saju kepo mi teme? Metü kemetha thime? Metü ungo mhatho kethimi teme?
english: Are all apostles? Are all prophets? Are all teachers? Are all miracle workers?

chokri: Metüko mithi kesürho süme? Jü kekrekre mhaphrü metü pome? Metü jü süko co pome?
english: Do all have gifts of healings? Do all speak with various languages? Do all interpret?

chokri: Vele mhaphrü kevethoko menyü zo yi te, sizi ve rekri ti I tha neko ka süto.
english: But earnestly desire the best gifts. Moreover, I show a most excellent way to you.

chokri: I thüma mu tüyo jüyimiko jülüni poyi zo sheri, kekhrü (mivaphiti) bamo lize I bavü kevü fü bizo, mori bazho kekhrüti bizo.
english: If I speak with the languages of men and of angels, but don't have love, I have become sounding brass, or a clanging cymbal.

chokri: I saju kepo mhaphrü bazo sheri, mu kemekhrü zhüko se mu lüyi zo sheri mu mhase metü bazo sheri, mu I kemelü metü bazo sheri, side cejütuko thi retuyi tazo sheri, vele mi vaphi ti kekhrü bamo lize I sü mhapüri mo.
english: If I have the gift of prophecy, and know all mysteries and all knowledge; and if I have all faith, so as to remove mountains, but don't have love, I am nothing.

chokri: Amha azhü kengupü kedimi vathi sü, mu upho pü melü thüsü, vele mivaphiti kekhrü bamo lize süko ala kepra kemeve di.
english: If I dole out all my goods to feed the poor, and if I give my body to be burned, but don't have love, it profits me nothing.

chokri: Kekhrü (mivaphi tisü) ba mecho sügü sühe keyi, miyo tsülive, kekhrü süo mha meho ta yomo, kekhrü süo ko kemeha khobü ta yomo, utho uchuti, ulü kerekrü ri yomo.
english: Love is patient and is kind; love doesn't envy. Love doesn't brag, is not proud,

chokri: Seli ngokemo do thi tayomo, pütho püzhozhülü tamo, pü nümo mhamha ta yomo, mha kesu lü ta yomo.
english: doesn't behave itself inappropriately, doesn't seek its own way, is not provoked, takes no account of evil;

chokri: Kesu kerho kolü rechü tayo mo, vele kethokolü unü rethu tayo.
english: doesn't rejoice in unrighteousness, but rejoices with the truth;

chokri: Mha metükolü hepü tayo, mha metü melüyo, mha metü lüpü kemeri bayo, metülü sügü sühe yiyo.
english: bears all things, believes all things, hopes all things, endures all things.

chokri: Miva phi ti kekhrü thathi ta yomo, vele saju kepo bazo sheri uko thathi tato; jü lüni kepo bazo sheri süko latato, mhase bazo sheri di tato.
english: Love never fails. But where there are prophecies, they will be done away with. Where there are various languages, they will cease. Where there is knowledge, it will be done away with.

chokri: Kekrelamori uko zapü lebi kesezo mu saju sheri zapü lebi poyo,
english: For we know in part, and we prophesy in part;

chokri: Vele vetsütsoti kevori ce süce vetsütso kemoo tatato.
english: but when that which is complete has come, then that which is partial will be done away with.

chokri: I natsi kebace I natsimi bide jüpo I mhalü ti natsimi kelü bide mhalü, vele I pethi ta thüma thiyi ketasa, I natsimi bi ketako metü khave.
english: When I was a child, I spoke as a child, I felt as a child, I thought as a child. Now that I have become a man, I have put away childish things.

chokri: Kekrelamo u tsühi khriva pülü mha ngokemetsemode ngo kebazo, vele pünha mhotsu kezüngoto, tsühi I zapü lebi seba vele pünha I sekebako metü seyi tato.
english: For now we see in a mirror, dimly, but then face to face. Now I know in part, but then I will know fully, even as I was also fully known.

chokri: Tsühi kemelü, kemeri mu kekhrü hi süko kezüba, vele metüko dolü kezhotho (kemeletho) sü kekhrü.
english: But now faith, hope, and love remain--these three. The greatest of these is love.

chokri: Mhakha miketsü kekhrü hu zote, mu rüpü mhaphrü menyü sayi zote, vele saju kepo neko phuyi te.
english: Follow after love, and earnestly desire spiritual gifts, but especially that you may prophesy.

chokri: Sekemo jü po keba thüma ce po bamo vele, siri pü rüpülü ketekhri ti jüpo ba.
english: For he who speaks in another language speaks not to men, but to God; for no one understands; but in the Spirit he speaks mysteries.

chokri: Vele saju kepo thüma ce poba püthi meve sü ketola, tarho kesü mu thelü tho melu kesü.
english: But he who prophesies speaks to men for their edification, exhortation, and consolation.

chokri: Sekemo fü po keba pütho püthi meve ba, vele saju po kebao kehu thi meve keto la poba.
english: He who speaks in another language edifies himself, but he who prophesies edifies the assembly.

chokri: I neko metüpo sekemo jüpo nyüzo süo ceri saju poti veku; kekrelamori pü saju kepoo jü sekemo kepoo ce zhoku tila, püco po kemese sü kemosa, mu kehu thi mevesü.
english: Now I desire to have you all speak with other languages, but rather that you would prophesy. For he is greater who prophesies than he who speaks with other languages, unless he interprets, that the assembly may be built up.

chokri: Tsühi, azüneko, mhace I jüse kemo po de I neko ce vori, nekoce püco vacho sü kemo sa nekola dipü kemeve ba, mori mhase mori saju kepo mori zü keta thezhoko khi vori molize?
english: But now, brothers, if I come to you speaking with other languages, what would I profit you, unless I speak to you either by way of revelation, or of knowledge, or of prophesying, or of teaching?

chokri: Kerhü kediko zori ufüseyo, leli mori harp libüko, uko fü thi kemetse sü mo lize, dipü bide thefü seyi ta süo lelime mori harp libü sheri?
english: Even things without life, giving a voice, whether pipe or harp, if they didn't give a distinction in the sounds, how would it be known what is piped or harped?

chokri: Mhace thecü khu kemetse sümo lize sopü therila keredeyi veta?
english: For if the trumpet gave an uncertain sound, who would prepare himself for war?

chokri: Sübi neko sekemojü popü mipo seyi keve thisü molize, dipü poba sheri seyi tome? Kekrelamori no popü tükhralü sü bate.
english: So also you, unless you uttered by the tongue words easy to understand, how would it be known what is spoken? For you would be speaking into the air.

chokri: Jükekro, thefü kekro cejü ba, süko püri uco kedi kethi bamo.
english: There are, it may be, so many kinds of sounds in the world, and none of them is without meaning.

chokri: Süla I püco mhace semo lize, I po kebao mhüjülü mhase kemomizo, mu mhapo kebao sheri amhüjülü mhase kemomi zo.
english: If then I don't know the meaning of the sound, I would be to him who speaks a foreigner, and he who speaks would be a foreigner to me.

chokri: Neko sübi rüpü mhaphrükola neko lü keni thiba zosheri, kehuce thi meveyi keto ve rekriti phuzo yite.
english: So also you, since you are zealous for spiritual gifts, seek that you may abound to the building up of the assembly.

chokri: Se kemojü kepoo sheri püco vachopü mice süyi ketola kecho thiyi te.
english: Therefore let him who speaks in another language pray that he may interpret.

chokri: Mhace I se kemojü kepomi pü lize a rüpü kechothi ba, vele akese kelülü ala kemeve ngoyi mo.
english: For if I pray in another language, my spirit prays, but my understanding is unfruitful.

chokri: Süsa dipü bi tata? I rüpülü kecho thito mu akese kelü lü sheri, I rüpülü tsale thito, mu amhase lü sheri I tsale thito.
english: What is it then? I will pray with the spirit, and I will pray with the understanding also. I will sing with the spirit, and I will sing with the understanding also.

chokri: Kemosa no rüpülü kethezü khasü süce thethilü kebako nkepo semo lize dipü bide “Amen” zisü ta, no kemeza kha kesü semo talize?
english: Otherwise if you bless with the spirit, how will he who fills the place of the unlearned say the "Amen" at your giving of thanks, seeing he doesn't know what you say?

chokri: No kethezü khameve shusü zori, vele kekremiko thimevesü mote.
english: For you most assuredly give thanks well, but the other person is not built up.

chokri: I a Jihova meza, I neko metüko ceri sekemo jü po mekro ku ba yoti la.
english: I thank my God, I speak with other languages more than you all.

chokri: Siri kehucelü jüco püngu akeselü popü mi metha kesü no jü nyekeri sekemo jülü kepo ce veku.
english: However in the assembly I would rather speak five words with my understanding, that I might instruct others also, than ten thousand words in another language.

chokri: Asazüjürako ne mhakeselü natsimi thita hite; vele mithimesu tilü neko natsimi thite, vele mhaselü ne pethimi thite.
english: Brothers, don't be children in thoughts, yet in malice be babies, but in thoughts be mature.

chokri: Thezho lesilü thopü kemeta, thüma jükekre kepoko umü kekre ketiko I thüma hikoce jüpo süto, sizosheri uko afü renyiyi ho, Niepoo posüte.
english: In the law it is written, "By men of strange languages and by the lips of strangers I will speak to this people. Not even thus will they hear me, says the Lord."

chokri: Süla jü kekrekepo zasi püzo, kemelü milamo, vele melü kemokola, vele saju kepojü kemelü lü ta kemoko khrühi yomo, vele kemelü miko la.
english: Therefore other languages are for a sign, not to those who believe, but to the unbelieving; but prophesying is for a sign, not to the unbelieving, but to those who believe.

chokri: Mhace kehukro metü tavo thethi pülü kemekroyi, de metü semo meyilüjüpo ta, mu sülü melü kemomi, mhase kemoko vori, uko nyo bate siti pota mo tome?
english: If therefore the whole assembly is assembled together and all speak with other languages, and unlearned or unbelieving people come in, won't they say that you are crazy?

chokri: Vele mhace metüko shaju pota, mita ukodolü vori melü kemo mori mhasekemomi, pü kelüce metüko pü mejo bata lizu mhalü tato.
english: But if all prophesy, and someone unbelieving or unlearned comes in, he is reproved by all, and he is judged by all.

chokri: Mhahiko kemekhrü pü melülü thi kesü, mu püzü nekü tsü Jihova reshü tato, mu Jihova neko züta kethozo sizi tato.
english: And thus the secrets of his heart are revealed. So he will fall down on his face and worship God, declaring that God is among you indeed.

chokri: Dipü biti thiti mesü ta, azüneko? Neko tavo kengu keyice neko metü tsale ba, hülüzho koba, umeyilüjü ba, kehala kesüla, mu ucokepo ba; mha metü thi meve kesülü thite.
english: What is it then, brothers? When you come together, each one of you has a psalm, has a teaching, has a revelation, has another language, has an interpretation. Let all things be done to build each other up.

chokri: Mhace humi sekemo jü poyo lize, mikena mori kekrotholü misü züde kebadolü mipüpo ufü kela kesü (uco kepo) thite.
english: If any man speaks in another language, let it be two, or at the most three, and in turn; and let one interpret.

chokri: Vele uco kepo bamota lize püpo kehucelü kemekre thita te, pütho pü rethi mu pü Rüo zü kerethi batate.
english: But if there is no interpreter, let him keep silent in the assembly, and let him speak to himself, and to God.

chokri: Saju kepo mikena mori misü pote, mu kekremiko po pütüme moshi lü süte.
english: Let the prophets speak, two or three, and let the others discern.

chokri: Mhace mhahu kehala prari ta lize, pükhüce kebako kerao po kemekre thi batate.
english: But if a revelation is made to another sitting by, let the first keep silent.

chokri: Neko mi püpü de saju metüko poyi ve, side metüpo seyi ketola, mu metüpo thelü tho kemelu thisü ketola.
english: For you all can prophesy one by one, that all may learn, and all may be exhorted.

chokri: Saju kepo rüpüko saju kepomiko füzüyo.
english: The spirits of the prophets are subject to the prophets,

chokri: Kekrelamori Jihova kebükerüti kekuomo, vele kezü keve, kemesamiko kehuce metülü.
english: for God is not a God of confusion, but of peace. As in all the assemblies of the saints,

chokri: Thenomiko po kemekre thiba tate kehucelü, ukopo jüpo mori ve, vele uko jüzülü bayi te zisü, thezholü sheri popü metazo.
english: let your wives keep silent in the assemblies, for it has not been permitted for them to speak; but let them be in subjection, as the law also says.

chokri: Mhace uko mhahu seba lize, uko unopu neko ce mhaketso yite, celüno; kekrelamori thenomi kehucelü mhakepo kethenga püzo.
english: If they desire to learn anything, let them ask their own husbands at home, for it is shameful for a woman to chatter in the assembly.

chokri: Neko yo Jihova jü dipü voriba? Mori süo nlebi prari me?
english: What? Was it from you that the word of God went out? Or did it come to you alone?

chokri: Mhace thüma humi utho ulüpü saju kepomi lezi, mori rüpü thüma le zi, püpo semu lü yite, I thopü neko ce kesü Niepoo apo thosü no ace kha kesü jüzo.
english: If any man thinks himself to be a prophet, or spiritual, let him recognize the things which I write to you, that they are the commandment of the Lord.

chokri: Vele mhasekemomi po mhasemo zo bate.
english: But if anyone is ignorant, let him be ignorant.

chokri: Sübi, asazüjürako saju kepo menyü sai zote, mu sekemo jü kepo sheri khalidü va hite.
english: Therefore, brothers, desire earnestly to prophesy, and don't forbid speaking with other languages.

chokri: Mhametü kezüvelü mu pütüpüzholü thiyi te.
english: Let all things be done decently and in order.

chokri: Süpi sheri I jüyikeve poketa halapü neko ce süve, neko sheri süo ngopü tate, mu neko sülü thayi tazo.
english: Now I declare to you, brothers, the Gospel which I preached to you, which also you received, in which you also stand,

chokri: Neko sülü kelayite, mhace neko sase tayo lize I neko metha kesüko, sizi kermosa neko kemelü kemethu tazoto.
english: by which also you are saved, if you hold firmly the word which I preached to you--unless you believed in vain.

chokri: Kekrelamori I melü keyi therace uko la Khrista sa keva ukephomako lano, ketholesilü thopü kemeta bi.
english: For I delivered to you first of all that which I also received: that Christ died for our sins according to the Scriptures,

chokri: Pü khi khrüva, mu zhosünha pü rhüla süta, ketholesilü thopü kemetabi.
english: that he was buried, that he was raised on the third day according to the Scriptures,

chokri: Pütavo Cephas mengosü, mu methakeyi mi kerikenako sheri;
english: and that he appeared to Cephas, then to the twelve.

chokri: Süsalü ukromiko (sazüjürako) thüma kra püngu kezü sheri vapülü mengosü, kekrolaco tsüdo sheri rhü balu vele humi sate.
english: Then he appeared to over five hundred brothers at once, most of whom remain until now, but some have also fallen asleep.

chokri: Süsalü James mengosü, mu apostelmiko metü sheri mengosü.
english: Then he appeared to James, then to all the apostles,

chokri: Mu thelalü a sheri mengosü, pütü kemo lü (tümolüpü) menu keyio.
english: and last of all, as to the child born at the wrong time, he appeared to me also.

chokri: Kekrelamori apostlemiko dolü kenitho, I apostle mile ziti cüsümo, kekrelamori I Jihova kehu methi tayo tila.
english: For I am the least of the apostles, who is not worthy to be called an apostle, because I persecuted the assembly of God.

chokri: Vele Jihova kelakeyi kekhrü la I tha hibi tate; mu pü kekhrü süo kemechü thi tamo vele I kekremiko ceri mhatho thi mekro kutate, siri I no mori Jihova kekhrü ayo keta thozo.
english: But by the grace of God I am what I am. His grace which was bestowed on me was not futile, but I worked more than all of them; yet not I, but the grace of God which was with me.

chokri: Süla I sheri mu uko sheri hako zhopo thitayo mu neko melüyi.
english: Whether then it is I or they, so we preach, and so you believed.

chokri: Mhace tsüdo Khrista kesalü rhüla sü keta uzhopo thita lize, neko dolü dipü bide rhüsalakesü bamo siti pota yola?
english: Now if Christ is preached, that he has been raised from the dead, how do some among you say that there is no resurrection of the dead?

chokri: Vele mhace kesami rhüla kesü di lize, size Khrista rhüla sü mote,
english: But if there is no resurrection of the dead, neither has Christ been raised.

chokri: Mhace Khrista rhüla sü mota lize, süsa uko zhopo kemechü kemerü tazo, mu neko kemelü sheri kemethu tazo.
english: If Christ has not been raised, then our preaching is in vain, and your faith also is in vain.

chokri: Ile, uko Jihova zhopo kethi kemedami ta zoto Jihova Khrista merhü sala sü mozo sheri uko Khrista merhü sala süte ziti zhopo thita ketala, sübi kesamiko rhülayi mote.
english: Yes, we are found false witnesses of God, because we testified about God that he raised up Christ, whom he didn't raise up, if it is so that the dead are not raised.

chokri: Mhace kesami rhüla kesüdi ze süsa Khrista rhüla sü mote.
english: For if the dead aren't raised, neither has Christ been raised.

chokri: Mu mhace Khrista rhüla sümo ta lize, nkemelü kemethu kemerü, neko ne kephoma lü bazolu.
english: If Christ has not been raised, your faith is vain; you are still in your sins.

chokri: Süsa Khrista yolü tade zü ketakori kate.
english: Then they also who are fallen asleep in Christ have perished.

chokri: Mha ukerhülü lebi la ukemeri Khrista yo batisa, uko hesheherü phikemezhe kropü tazo.
english: If we have only hoped in Christ in this life, we are of all men most pitiable.

chokri: Vele tsüdo ce Khrista rhüla sütazo, kesalü no, mu zükezülü rhüla kesü püse kera thoo.
english: But now Christ has been raised from the dead. He became the first fruits of those who are asleep.

chokri: Kekrelamori thümala kesa vorita, mu kesalü rhüla keyi sheri vorita.
english: For since death came by man, the resurrection of the dead also came by man.

chokri: Adam la kesa vori, metüyo sübi Khrista lano kesalü rhüla keyi thümayo vorite.
english: For as in Adam all die, so also in Christ all will be made alive.

chokri: Vele kehopü sheri utho uyo thezho püta: Khrista rase zhürao, süsalü Khrista yolü ketako pü sala vori tice.
english: But each in his own order: Christ the first fruits, then those who are Christ's, at his coming.

chokri: Süsa thelao vorito, pü Jihova kede rüvü phu mesü kesüce, uko Poo, pü thezho mikekhapü ketako, keku mu kemeco metü nüda kevasa;
english: Then the end comes, when he will deliver up the Kingdom to God, even the Father; when he will have abolished all rule and all authority and power.

chokri: Kekrelamori püpo kede thita ketola, pü ngomumiko metü khapü püphekhro kedüva kemothe.
english: For he must reign until he has put all his enemies under his feet.

chokri: Ngomu thelathoo meka ketoo sü kesaozo.
english: The last enemy that will be abolished is death.

chokri: Pü mha metü pü pü phekhro süve; vele mha metü kha pü khrosü vale po kebasü, pü lebi pü mesemengo sü, mha metü pü pükhro sü kevao tho.
english: For, "He put all things in subjection under his feet." But when he says, "All things are put in subjection," it is evident that he is excepted who subjected all things to him.

chokri: Mhametü kha pü khro sü kevao sa pü Nuo ri kha pükhro süsü, side Jihova po mhametü lü püpo süta ketola.
english: When all things have been subjected to him, then the Son will also himself be subjected to him who subjected all things to him, that God may be all in all.

chokri: Sizi kemosa uko dipü bi tata, saketako zalü jülopü keyiko, mhace kesami rhüla kesü dile zitaze? Dipüla uko kesami sülü jülopü ba?
english: Or else what will they do who are baptized for the dead? If the dead aren't raised at all, why then are they baptized for the dead?

chokri: Uko dipüla bavüdo metülü kebükerü ba zota?
english: Why do we also stand in jeopardy every hour?

chokri: I neko rechü keba Khrista Jisu la I ngoyi keta hio kenyo ti I Niepoo la tüsonha sasü tayo.
english: I affirm, by the boasting in you which I have in Christ Jesus our Lord, I die daily.

chokri: Mhace thüma zholü I Ephesus lü khonukozü ketü kezhe keba, ala dipü kepra ba, mhace kesami rhüla kesü bamo zo lize? Uko mhati mu krazo bata khe, kekrelamori uko sodo sata teru.
english: If I fought with animals at Ephesus for human purposes, what does it profit me? If the dead are not raised, then "let us eat and drink, for tomorrow we die."

chokri: Mipo ukedova hikhe; mhapo kekri kesüla uzhokeveko jümerho vayo;
english: Don't be deceived! "Evil companionships corrupt good morals."

chokri: Cosü kemezilü voyi khe, kephomathi hi, kekrelamori humi Jihova la thenga pü.
english: Wake up righteously, and don't sin, for some have no knowledge of God. I say this to your shame.

chokri: Vele humi kelüce, dipü bide kesamiko rhüla süta? Mu umo dipü hü bide vorita?
english: But someone will say, "How are the dead raised?" and, "With what kind of body do they come?"

chokri: Nekemeloko, neko rase so kesüko sata mode süri yomo.
english: You foolish one, that which you yourself sow is not made alive unless it dies.

chokri: No so kesü uthi umo bi sosü, süo rase püzo, süo ata (teha) zori, mori ubete tsa kekre sheri.
english: That which you sow, you don't sow the body that will be, but a bare grain, maybe of wheat, or of some other kind.

chokri: Vele Jihova pütho pünü pümedolü uthi umo thisü, mu rase sheri utho umo ne.
english: But God gives it a body even as it pleased him, and to each seed a body of its own.

chokri: Uthiko metü kemhamo, vele thüma thi kre, khonuko uthi kekre, fü (khu) sheri pü thikre, merasheri uthi kre.
english: All flesh is not the same flesh, but there is one flesh of men, another flesh of animals, another of fish, and another of birds.

chokri: Tülü kehümi ri uthi upho kekrekre phrüzo, vele tülü kehü zapozale kethipü mu cejülü kehü zapozale kekrepü.
english: There are also celestial bodies, and terrestrial bodies; but the glory of the celestial differs from that of the terrestrial.

chokri: Nace zapozale kethi ba, mu khre sheri zapozale ba, thenyü zapozale kekre püba, thenyüko sheri zapozale kekrekre phrüzo.
english: There is one glory of the sun, another glory of the moon, and another glory of the stars; for one star differs from another star in glory.

chokri: Süla rhüla kesü kesamiko sheri sübi, cemu reta ketolü sosü, süo kece kere thi kemo lü rhü sü tato.
english: So also is the resurrection of the dead. It is sown in corruption; it is raised in incorruption.

chokri: Thechu therho kedilü sosü, süo merhü pü zapozale lü sü; kethathilü sasü, suo phu mesüpü kekulü sü.
english: It is sown in dishonor; it is raised in glory. It is sown in weakness; it is raised in power.

chokri: Uthi upho hülü zholü sosü, süo rüpü kehülü phu mesüyi; hülü zho uthi umo kehü ba, mu rüpü mo sheri ba.
english: It is sown a natural body; it is raised a spiritual body. There is a natural body and there is also a spiritual body.

chokri: Thopü kemeta, thüma kerao Adam, thipü ukerhüro kenepü thisü, mu Adam thelao thimerhü pü rüpü thisü.
english: So also it is written, "The first man, Adam, became a living soul." The last Adam became a life-giving spirit.

chokri: Sisheri rüpü kehü süo theraomo vele hülü zho kehüo ra, süsalü rüpü kehüo vori.
english: However that which is spiritual isn't first, but that which is natural, then that which is spiritual.

chokri: Thüma kerao cejülü mu cejü bi, mu thüma kenao tülü vori, Niepoo zo.
english: The first man is of the earth, made of dust. The second man is the Lord from heaven.

chokri: Cejüo zhorele bi, cejülü vori, mu tülü kehüko tüyo zhorele thisü.
english: As is the one made of dust, such are those who are also made of dust; and as is the heavenly, such are they also that are heavenly.

chokri: Mu uko cejülü kehü hübi, uko sheri tülü kehüko hü thipü to.
english: As we have borne the image of those made of dust, let's also bear the image of the heavenly.

chokri: Tsühi, asazüjürako, I po keba, uthi umo khipü tülü kalü thiyi ho, Jihova kede ka yi yi ho; mori kamu dita keto (cemu reta keto) püvo cemu retayi keho kalü thiyi ho.
english: Now I say this, brothers, that flesh and blood can't inherit the Kingdom of God; neither does corruption inherit incorruption.

chokri: Hihe, I mha ketekhri tipü neko mese süto, uko metü zü taho; vele uko metü rede tato.
english: Behold, I tell you a mystery. We will not all sleep, but we will all be changed,

chokri: Dopülü, umhü mhüphra kesü pülü, thecü kekhulü, thecü khusü ti tülü, kesamiko phu mesü pü ce mu retayi keholü sü vato, mu uko redetato.
english: in a moment, in the twinkling of an eye, at the last trumpet. For the trumpet will sound, and the dead will be raised incorruptible, and we will be changed.

chokri: Kekrelamori cemu reta keto hio thipü cemu retayi keholü süva moze kesula, mu sata keto zhühio thipü sata keho zhü thisü moze kesula.
english: For this corruptible must put on incorruption, and this mortal must put on immortality.

chokri: Süla cemu reta keto zhüo thipü cemu reta keho thisü, sata ketozhü thipü sata keho mo thisü, sizi keyice thopü kemeta jü, thitso yito, “süo poti”, kesao kukeyi po kesao mezo yita.
english: But when this corruptible will have put on incorruption, and this mortal will have put on immortality, then what is written will happen: "Death is swallowed up in victory."

chokri: O kesao ntha dipüce ba? O mhakhreo nokukeyi dipüce ba?
english: "Death, where is your sting? Hades, where is your victory?"

chokri: Kesa thasü kephoma mu kephoma keku sü thezho.
english: The sting of death is sin, and the power of sin is the law.

chokri: Vele Jihova meza pü uko Niepo Jisu yono keku khapü uko tsü sü kevala.
english: But thanks be to God, who gives us the victory through our Lord Jesus Christ.

chokri: Süla azüneko akekhrüko, tha melü yite, rede releta kemo, Niepoo mhatholü ukhashu kesü, neko seba metse Niepoo la ne mhatho thi kesüko kemethu thita mo zo tila.
english: Therefore, my beloved brothers, be steadfast, immovable, always abounding in the Lord's work, because you know that your labor is not in vain in the Lord.

chokri: Tsüdoce I kemesakola mhakhrüpü tsüsü keto I Galatia koce thino posü kehuce kolü, neko süo thisü te.
english: Now concerning the collection for the saints, as I commanded the assemblies of Galatia, you do likewise.
