chokri: Kechothe kerhü meri keta Jihova lano, medakemo, cejü shudü kemolü jü phrapü kemeta.
english: in hope of eternal life, which God, who can't lie, promised before eternal times;

chokri: Vele pütü züde jüo zhopo kethilü tha meka kesü, khapü amelükeva, Jihova uko kelakeyi ukopo thino ketsü kesü jülü.
english: but in his own time revealed his word in the message with which I was entrusted according to the commandment of God our Savior;

chokri: Titus cekesü, atho anu kemelü zholü; ngomezhe kesü kekhrü, phikemezhe, mu kezükeve, Jihova celü kevori, u Po, Niepo Jisu Khrista ukelakeyio.
english: to Titus, my true child according to a common faith: Grace, mercy, and peace from God the Father and the Lord Jesus Christ our Savior.

chokri: Mhaco hiola n Crete lü nkha meta, n po mha umedo thikenyüko ketso kemesapü pütü thisü ketola u pethi mehiko kedapü nade metükolü süyi keto süla I n kedasü.
english: I left you in Crete for this reason, that you would set in order the things that were lacking, and appoint elders in every city, as I directed you;

chokri: Kehopüri mejova kesumi, ucemi pü lebi timi, unune kekha kemelülü ketami, mha kebü kemo, mifü zü kemu thikemo.
english: if anyone is blameless, the husband of one wife, having children who believe, who are not accused of loose or unruly behavior.

chokri: Bishop sü mipo mejoyi kesumi, Jihova yitholü, utho umedo thita kemomi, thelü reda mhamha ta kemomi hezo kranyü ba kemomi, mhakebü kemomi, mhamesa kemo meho menyü kemomi.
english: For the overseer must be blameless, as God's steward; not self-pleasing, not easily angered, not given to wine, not violent, not greedy for dishonest gain;

chokri: Vele reso kekhrümi, thüma keveko kekhrümi, rhorhü, jütho, mesa, krokrü de mhakelü mi;
english: but given to hospitality, as a lover of good, sober-minded, fair, holy, self-controlled;

chokri: Pü metha kesübi kemelü jü khi metü keyimi, size lize jüpo kenyüko sheri pü thezho kemesa khipü süko tahitarho mu uko shuyi mu jürenu yi veto.
english: holding to the faithful word which is according to the teaching, that he may be able to exhort in the sound doctrine, and to convict those who contradict him.

chokri: Kekrelamori kekhayi keho, kemethu kemerü po kenyü mu mishu ketse ketako, yikerü poketako sheri sübiti kekro tate.
english: For there are also many unruly men, vain talkers and deceivers, especially those of the circumcision,

chokri: Süko ta kekhava moze sü, uko cekro metü kebü pü tate, methazükemoko khi metha tate, mesakemo kemehola.
english: whose mouths must be stopped; men who overthrow whole houses, teaching things which they ought not, for dishonest gain's sake.

chokri: Uko dolü saju kepo mipü poti, “Cretemiko, kecejümi kemedamiko zo, khonu kesuko, kemetsü mhati kenyümi,”
english: One of them, a prophet of their own, said, "Cretans are always liars, evil beasts, and idle gluttons."

chokri: Zhopo hio kengo kepo kethozo. Süla thüma hibiti kepoko kethi shusü ta mozesu, side uko po kemelü lü pütü thita ketola;
english: This testimony is true. For this cause, reprove them sharply, that they may be sound in the faith,

chokri: Jew miko ketheju tiko po ketako yo menu hite, reni hite, mu thüma tho po keta thino züno po ketako, uko ketholü regü ta ketako.
english: not paying attention to Jewish fables and commandments of men who turn away from the truth.

chokri: Kemesamila mha metü mesa, vele jü merho kevako melü mo ketako la mhapüri kemesa di; vele uko lüdo sheri ukelü sheri jü merho pü tate.
english: To the pure, all things are pure; but to those who are defiled and unbelieving, nothing is pure; but both their mind and their conscience are defiled.

chokri: Uko Jihova sele potayo, vele uko mhathoko lü süko mela tate, (Jihova sekemo thitate), uko keretse phikemero, mu mifü zü kemu, mu mhatho kevelü umelü revote.
english: They profess that they know God, but by their works they deny him, being abominable, disobedient, and unfit for any good work.

chokri: Vele no thezho keve zhüko pote.
english: But say the things which fit sound doctrine,

chokri: Pethimiko rhorhüte, meshishü, pemetü mete, thegigü krokrü kemelü keve, miva phise, sügü sühe,
english: that older men should be temperate, sensible, sober-minded, sound in faith, in love, and in patience:

chokri: Theno pethi ketako sübite, mesati zhokomi mejo nyükemo, hezo kranyü ba kemo, mhakeve kemetha kethimi.
english: and that older women likewise be reverent in behavior, not slanderers nor enslaved to much wine, teachers of that which is good;

chokri: Side theno natsiko (relimiko) methapü rhorhü, unopune khrü, uneko khrüti;
english: that they may train the young women to love their husbands, to love their children,

chokri: Metsümetse, uzho matho, ukrouce khimeve keyive mu unopu füzüthi, side Jihova jü thimerho va kemo;
english: to be sober-minded, chaste, workers at home, kind, being in subjection to their own husbands, that God's word may not be blasphemed.

chokri: Khresamiko sheri sübi rhorhü ti metha süte;
english: Likewise, exhort the younger men to be sober-minded;

chokri: M mhametükolü nekhapü mhakeve kethilü thaka thisüte, thezho keve thi merho va kemo, metüte, mu sürhü sürho tilü.
english: in all things showing yourself an example of good works; in your teaching showing integrity, seriousness, incorruptibility,

chokri: U jüpojüli kevepo, mipo thalice va kemo, size kemoko ketherü ketasa thenga thitayo, mhakesu mipo n yo pota kemo.
english: and soundness of speech that can't be condemned; that he who opposes you may be ashamed, having no evil thing to say about us.

chokri: Mi thekü kethiko tarhopü ukopo u niepomiko jüzü thi yite, mu mhametükolü ukonü kethi, thi meve kesü, mu umezi pota kemo.
english: Exhort servants to be in subjection to their own masters, and to be well-pleasing in all things; not contradicting;

chokri: U be pü mha rego kevatahiri, vele mhakeve metülü melüyi keve thisüte, side uko thezho kevekhi bemene keyila therüo uko kelakeyio zhoreleko thitsosü ketola.
english: not stealing, but showing all good fidelity; that they may adorn the doctrine of God, our Savior, in all things.

chokri: Kekrelamori kekhrü ngokemezhe uko kelakeyio khapü thüma metüko mengo süte.
english: For the grace of God has appeared, bringing salvation to all men,

chokri: U methakesü mesakemo kehü, cejülü menyüko, khava de uko metsümetse, kemezi, kemesa zho, hiko khiyi cejülü kehüce.
english: instructing us to the intent that, denying ungodliness and worldly lusts, we would live soberly, righteously, and godly in this present world;

chokri: Thejo kepü kemeri Therü kezhoo, u kelakeyi Jisu Khrista zapozazle thi lavori ketoce.
english: looking for the blessed hope and appearing of the glory of our great God and Savior, Jesus Christ;

chokri: Pü pükhapü uko tsüsü, pü uko mesakemo metülü uko rhayi, uthi kemesa pü thüma uhü kekre kropü, mhatho kevelü ulüni phite.
english: who gave himself for us, that he might redeem us from all iniquity, and purify for himself a people for his own possession, zealous for good works.

chokri: Mhahiko khipo, tarho, lekhu keku metü khide, mi püpori nkhapü mhapüri kemo thiva hite.
english: Say these things and exhort and reprove with all authority. Let no man despise you.

chokri: Süko khipü akelülü süyi thezho kekhiko mu kekuko khro, shecomiko jü kezü, mhatho keve kethilü keredepü keta.
english: Remind them to be in subjection to rulers and to authorities, to be obedient, to be ready for every good work,

chokri: Mipürei sheli (kesu) pohite, kezhe bühi thihi te, vele tsü menu tate, renu metsati thipü thüma metü kate.
english: to speak evil of no one, not to be contentious, to be gentle, showing all humility toward all men.

chokri: Kekrelamori uko thozori hucepü melo nyonyü tayo, mifüzümo, mikedo, umenyü kekrekre tiko nüthi, zü mezhokemo mu miresa meho, miphi metsü, hunehu ngounümo.
english: For we were also once foolish, disobedient, deceived, serving various lusts and pleasures, living in malice and envy, hateful, and hating one another.

chokri: Vele süsalü ketsükeve mu kekhrü therüo celü uko kelakeyi thüma yo thi kesü ngoyi,
english: But when the kindness of God our Savior and his love toward mankind appeared,

chokri: Uko mhatho kemezi kethi lazomo, vele pü uko kekhrü pü uko kelasü, uthi kesa salasü mu Kemesa Rüpü lü uthi kesa sü.
english: not by works of righteousness, which we did ourselves, but according to his mercy, he saved us, through the washing of regeneration and renewing by the Holy Spirit,

chokri: Pü ketsokele lapü uko yosü,
english: which he poured out on us richly, through Jesus Christ our Savior;

chokri: Jisu Khrista uko kelakeyiola.
english: that, being justified by his grace, we might be made heirs according to the hope of eternal life.

chokri: Pü ngokemezhe uthi kemathoyi, de uthipü kechothe kalü kerhü kene kemeri khasü.
english: This saying is faithful, and concerning these things I desire that you affirm confidently, so that those who have believed God may be careful to maintain good works. These things are good and profitable to men;

chokri: Mha hiohi kemelü kene jüpo, mu mhasüko neko thimu lüpü keta amedozo, uko therüo melü ketako menuyi mu mhakeve kethilü tayi, mha hiko vezo mu thümako la meve.
english: but shun foolish questionings, genealogies, strife, and disputes about the law; for they are unprofitable and vain.

chokri: Vele kemelolü mhaketso tiko kekhayite, nuhü kephriko, ketükemeli ti, mu thezholü kemeli keti, mha süko mevemo mu kemechü zo tila.
english: Avoid a factious man after a first and second warning;

chokri: Thüma mipü va kerao mu va kenao pü tahi tarho süzo sheri pü ketho kemo laco (kehucephro) vato lize pü khava te. Thüma sübi ketako kethoo cu vote, kephomalü tate mu pü thopü mejo votato.
english: knowing that such a one is perverted, and sins, being self-condemned.

chokri: I Artemas mori Tychicus ketsüpü nekoce kesüce neko mhalü menu de ta Nicopolis lü ace vorite, kekrelamori I tüsü tüce sülü bata keto lü bate.
english: When I send Artemas to you, or Tychicus, be diligent to come to me to Nicopolis, for I have determined to winter there.

chokri: Zenas thezho kekhio, mu Apollos ni neko revü cholüzüpü vori keto rekra menu süte, mu unipo mhapü sheri tsakhu ta kemo kesü süte.
english: Send Zenas, the lawyer, and Apollos on their journey speedily, that nothing may be lacking for them.

chokri: Mu ha thümako pori mhakhimo kesuko khipü ukesüyi keto mhakeve kethilü ukopo usezhü kemopü thita mo ketola.
english: Let our people also learn to maintain good works for necessary uses, that they may not be unfruitful.

chokri: Azü kebako metü neko kelü süzüho; kemelülü keta hako kekhrü miko kelü süte, ngokemezhe kekhrüpo neko metü züba keyi thite, Amen.
english: All who are with me greet you. Greet those who love us in faith. Grace be with you all. Amen.

chokri: Khrista Jisu jü küthio Paul m Timothy ni lesi thopü uzüo Philemon u khrüo m küzü yitho thi kübako,
english: Paul, a prisoner of Christ Jesus, and Timothy our brother, to Philemon, our beloved fellow worker,

chokri: Mo u lipü Apphia m Archippus Khrista thipaheo m n celü kühuyi yo küti kühuo ce küsü,
english: to the beloved Apphia, to Archippus, our fellow soldier, and to the assembly in your house:

chokri: Ngokümüzhie n yo mo kezü keve Therü uko Po yo celü küvori ma Niepo Jisu Khrista.
english: Grace to you and peace from God our Father and the Lord Jesus Christ.

chokri: I a küchoko lü n rüle pü pono a Jihova kümüzapo sü bayozo,
english: I thank my God always, making mention of you in my prayers,

chokri: Seküyi no Niepo Jisu ceco mo kümüsako metüko ceco ri n kükhrü mo n kümülü ba küti la,
english: hearing of your love, and of the faith which you have toward the Lord Jesus, and toward all the saints;

chokri: Mi zü n kümülü küzakütilü, uma küne thiyi küto no mipo uko lü küba Khrista Jisu küve metüko setü küyi lü zoto.
english: that the fellowship of your faith may become effective, in the knowledge of every good thing which is in us in Christ Jesus.

chokri: Size-e ha n kükhrü la hanü phaho ba, mo ha lüri luyi kükrelamori, a sazüo no kümüsako mülü thi merhüsala küyi la.
english: For we have much joy and comfort in your love, because the hearts of the saints have been refreshed through you, brother.

chokri: Süla I Khrista celaco thepushu-e no n po thiküzüko küpfü kha n tsüsüyi vezo,
english: Therefore, though I have all boldness in Christ to command you that which is appropriate,

chokri: Siri hunehu kükhrüo la, m I Paul, I tsate m süsa ri tsü Khrista Jisu la thejü thi kütala, n ce a müzhe mülo kütsü a nü kute.
english: yet for love's sake I rather beg, being such a one as Paul, the aged, but also a prisoner of Jesus Christ.

chokri: I thejü thikütace münu küyio a nu natsio Onesimus la n ce a müzhe mülo yo
english: I beg you for my child, whom I have become the father of in my chains, Onesimus,

chokri: Pü thajü tülü n po püla küka sü te, vele tsü n lari mo a lari kümüve bate
english: who once was useless to you, but now is useful to you and to me.

chokri: I pü kütsü sala sü ze: süla No pü züyite, süo a mülüo zo:
english: I am sending him back. Therefore receive him, that is, my own heart,

chokri: I pü khapüma zü banyü ba, thejücielü kübace püpo jüyiküve lü a khrühi küto la.
english: whom I desired to keep with me, that on your behalf he might serve me in my chains for the Gospel.

chokri: Vele n medo samo de I mha püri thi ho, m pon medo thi mode n kemu thi pü mhapü ri thiho.
english: But I was willing to do nothing without your consent, that your goodness would not be as of necessity, but of free will.

chokri: Size-e pü dopü ce n therü kütsü hio no küchothe püzü tata keto la;
english: For perhaps he was therefore separated from you for a while, that you would have him forever,

chokri: Tsüzono n cetao mori n cetao pi n sazü n khrüo bahori a ceco müpfü vele themo-o cecori mo Nyepoo cecori didü pü n zü kümüno pfü tato sü?
english: no longer as a slave, but more than a slave, a beloved brother, especially to me, but how much rather to you, both in the flesh and in the Lord.

chokri: Mhace no a pü n besüo le phri yo süze, n nü de a züyi yoti bi, a bizo no püzü yite.
english: If then you count me a partner, receive him as you would receive me.

chokri: Mhace pü n yo thizü kümo thiva mori n po püzü li mha süze, a zalü khasü müzüte;
english: But if he has wronged you at all, or owes you anything, put that to my account.

chokri: I Paul a tho a bepü hihi tho süho, a tho shuva to; no n pho zori a zhü bita zo, vele I süo poho.
english: I, Paul, write this with my own hand: I will repay it (not to mention to you that you owe to me even your own self besides).

chokri: Küthozo, a sazüo, Nyepoo celaco a po n la a nü tate; n Nyepoo la a mülü thi merhü sala süte.
english: Yes, brother, let me have joy from you in the Lord. Refresh my heart in the Lord.

chokri: No jüzü thi küta mülütü zono, I hihi tho n ce süyo, mo I n ce po kütsü piri no thiyi küto I seba.
english: Having confidence in your obedience, I write to you, knowing that you will do even beyond what I say.

chokri: Vele süzokümo, süsari a kübathi küsü pü mütate: kükrelamori, I mülütü küba, neko küchola, a khala pü neko cesü va mütse.
english: Also, prepare a guest room for me, for I hope that through your prayers I will be restored to you.

chokri: Epaphras, a zü Khrista Jisu la thejü thikütao n külü süho,
english: Epaphras, my fellow prisoner in Christ Jesus, greets you,

chokri: A zü yitho thikübako Mark, Aristarchus, Demas, Luke uko metü ri sa.
english: as do Mark, Aristarchus, Demas, and Luke, my fellow workers.

chokri: U Nyepo Khrista Jisu celü küprari ngokümüzheo po neko rüpü zü ba küyio thite. Amen.
english: The grace of our Lord Jesus Christ be with your spirit. Amen.

chokri: Thajü pütü mu mhaco kekrekre tilü sajukepoko celü upotsa neko ce mha povori.
english: God, having in the past spoken to the fathers through the prophets at many times and in various ways,

chokri: Pütü thüla hilü pü Nu mhametülü kalüo püpo cejü thi kesüo.
english: has at the end of these days spoken to us by his Son, whom he appointed heir of all things, through whom also he made the worlds.

chokri: Pü vekrü ti zapozale mu thümahü püyo thapü mesemengo kesü; mu püjü kekulü mhametü tüsüpü keba, pü ukephomako kerhupha kesüce side kekuthoo bo püza laco bata, kerekrü thilü.
english: His Son is the radiance of his glory, the very image of his substance, and upholding all things by the word of his power, when he had by himself made purification for our sins, sat down on the right hand of the Majesty on high;

chokri: Tüyochoyimiko ceri veku shuzo ti pü thisü, pü kalü ngo keyi theza verekri kuzo uko metü ceri.
english: having become so much better than the angels, as he has inherited a more excellent name than they have.

chokri: Pü dipüce tülüchoyiko ce, “No A Nu, tha I n menu süte, ‘siziti posü me?’ Mu süsari ‘I püpo thito, mu pü a Nu thito,’ size süme”?
english: For to which of the angels did he say at any time,  "You are my Son, Today have I become your father?" and again,  "I will be to him a Father, And he will be to me a Son?"

chokri: Süpi sheri pü menu pü cejülü kesüce, pü po kesü, “Jihova choyiko po pü reshüte.”
english: Again, when he brings in the firstborn into the world he says, "Let all the angels of God worship him."

chokri: Pü tüyochoyiko ce poti, pü tülüchoyiko thipü rüpüko thive, mu pü yithoko khipü meli keto thi süte.
english: Of the angels he says, "Who makes his angels winds, And his servants a flame of fire."

chokri: Vele Nuo la po kesü, “N kedeba, O Jihova, sü kechothe tato; mu n kede keku jü (mohor) kemezi sü n kede keku kene jü (mohor) zo.”
english: but of the Son he says, "Your throne, O God, is forever and ever; The scepter of uprightness is the scepter of your Kingdom.

chokri: No kemezi nnü, mu kerho phi metsü, süla Niepoo poti, “n Rüo kenü tirü khipü n kezümiko pi (mho) n kenhüsüho.”
english: You have loved righteousness, and hated iniquity; Therefore God, your God, has anointed you with the oil of gladness above your fellows."

chokri: Niepoo, no, shudülü cejü mejudüsü, mu tüko n be mhathozo.
english: And, "You, Lord, in the beginning, laid the foundation of the earth. The heavens are the works of your hands.

chokri: Uko rütato, vele no tasü zoto; mu uko metü fümene bi ra tato:
english: They will perish, but you continue. They all will grow old like a garment does.

chokri: No süko thode vato, füne thsü kezhi kevabi vele no kemhabi sü tazoto, mu n tütseko lata ho.
english: As a mantle you will roll them up, And they will be changed; But you are the same. Your years will not fail."

chokri: Vele pü dipü nha (dipütülü) tüyochoyiko ce, “no abo azalaco bate, I n ngomuko khipü n phezho thiva kemothe,” siti po?
english: But of which of the angels has he said at any time, "Sit at my right hand, Until I make your enemies the footstool of your feet?"

chokri: Uko choyi kethi rüpüko ketsüpü kelakeyi yitholü kalü kethiko thisü mome?
english: Aren't they all ministering spirits, sent out to do service for the sake of those who will inherit salvation?

chokri: Süla uko se keyi sükolü utütso thiku ti mesüzo, motisa mhasüko ucelü rezü di tato tila.
english: Therefore we ought to pay greater attention to the things that were heard, lest perhaps we drift away.

chokri: Kekrelamori tüyochoyimi jüpo mhace thi ketho tayo lize mu vajo ketako jüzü thimo ketala sazho püzhokhro ngoyi ta lize;
english: For if the word spoken through angels proved steadfast, and every transgression and disobedience received a just recompense;

chokri: Dipü bi de u therayi ta, mhace kelakeyi zho mhakezho u rekra mota lize; süo theralü Niepoo posü, mu süko thipü umesemengo sü ketala.
english: how will we escape if we neglect so great a salvation-- which at the first having been spoken through the Lord, was confirmed to us by those who heard;

chokri: Jihova süko zhopo zo, zasiko mu ungomhathoko, ketekhri kethi dolüni mu Kemesa Rüpü mhaphrüko pütho pü medolü.
english: God also bearing witness with them, both by signs and wonders, and by various works of power, and by gifts of the Holy Spirit, according to his own will?

chokri: Kekrelamori uko kepoketa cejü hisa laco vori keto khipü tüyochoyomiko khrikhro süva tole zi mo.
english: For he didn't subject the world to come, of which we speak, to angels.

chokri: Vele thethi pülü mipü zhopo kha zhopo süo, “thüma sü dipüle no pü rele lüzo ba? Mori thümanu no püpe zo taya?”
english: But one has somewhere testified, saying, "What is man, that you think of him? Or the son of man, that you care for him?

chokri: No pü thipü tüyochoyiko ce kedopü renuku sü, no zapozale mu thechu therho pü pü kedepira thisü, mu pü khapü nbe mhathoko pisü.
english: You made him a little lower than the angels; You crowned him with glory and honor.

chokri: “No mha metü khipü pü phekhro lü süte, süla mhametü pükhro tazo, mhapüri püphekhro sü mota mozo, vele tsühi uko pü khro ba keta süko metü ngo tsomo.”
english: You have put all things in subjection under his feet." For in that he subjected all things to him, he left nothing that is not subject to him. But now we don't see all things subjected to him, yet.

chokri: Vele uko Jisu ngo, pü thipü tüyochoyiko ce renuku zasü kekrelamori püpo kesalü kemezhe tila; süosü püpo Jihova kekhrü methu kha kesü thüma mipüpü la püpokesa metha.
english: But we see him who has been made a little lower than the angels, Jesus, because of the suffering of death crowned with glory and honor, that by the grace of God he should taste of death for everyone.

chokri: Kekrelamori süosü püzo, püyo mhametü ba, püyo mhametü prari, thüma nuko zapozale khasü, kelakeyi vetsü tsoti krüta (thipahe kezhoo) po kemezhe kesülü.
english: For it became him, for whom are all things, and through whom are all things, in bringing many children to glory, to make the author of their salvation perfect through sufferings.

chokri: Kekrelamori kenani pü thi kemesa kesüko mu mesakeyiko sheri metü püzo (pümpü) kekrelamori süo püpo uko cüpü sazüjüra le size keto pü menga tamo.
english: For both he who sanctifies and those who are sanctified are all from one, for which cause he is not ashamed to call them brothers,

chokri: Pü poti, I nza halapü a sazüjürako ce süto, I kehuce metsulü n kechu tsale thisü to.
english: saying, "I will declare your name to my brothers. In the midst of the congregation I will sing your praise."

chokri: Süsari I a kemelü pü n yo süto, hihe, I mu a nuneko Jihova kha atsü kesüko.
english: Again, "I will put my trust in him." Again, "Behold, here am I and the children whom God has given me."

chokri: Süsa natsimiko uthi uza thi kepüko, pü sheri süko bi uthi uza thipü; sa kesülü pü kesa meni sü ketola, kesa keku süo therükesu-o zo.
english: Since then the children have shared in flesh and blood, he also himself in like manner partook of the same, that through death he might bring to nothing him who had the power of death, that is, the devil,

chokri: Uko kerhüthe kesa mpe tipo uko pha liro pü ta sülü uko süphra pü pra sü.
english: and might deliver all of them who through fear of death were all their lifetime subject to bondage.

chokri: Kekrelamori pü tülüchoyiko kehüzho rele sümo, vele pü Abraham nuneko zho sü.
english: For most assuredly, not to angels does he give help, but he gives help to the seed of Abraham.

chokri: Sübide pü mhametülü püthipü pü sazüjüra biyi, miphikemezhe, kemelü kene, thevo kekrekrüo thi, Jihova ceco mhatho kolü, thüma kephomako lü vetho thila kesülü.
english: Therefore he was obligated in all things to be made like his brothers, that he might become a merciful and faithful high priest in things pertaining to God, to make atonement for the sins of the people.

chokri: Kekrelamori pütho zori dophilü kemezhe, pü dophi süko lü mikhrühi yi ve bate.
english: For in that he himself has suffered being tempted, he is able to help those who are tempted.

chokri: Süla kemesa asazüjürako, tülü phremi thi tole cüyi ketako, apostle mu thevo kerekrüo Jisu Khrista rele lüsü phite.
english: Therefore, holy brothers, partakers of a heavenly calling, consider the Apostle and High Priest of our confession, Jesus;

chokri: Pü jüthozo pü keda kesüo yo, Moses sheri pü cekro yo jütho zo kesü bi.
english: who was faithful to him who appointed him, as also was Moses in all his house.

chokri: Kekrelamori thüma hio Moses ceri zapozale thi züku zo ceo ce sheri cethi kesüo therho neku tibi.
english: For he has been counted worthy of more glory than Moses, inasmuch as he who built the house has more honor than the house.

chokri: Kekrelamori ce kehopüri thüma mipü po thiyo, vele mhametü thi kesüo sü Jihova zo.
english: For every house is built by someone; but he who built all things is God.

chokri: Mu Moses pü cekro yo jütho süzo, mithekümi, pübi, zhopo hiko hisalaco sheri kepota zoto.
english: Moses indeed was faithful in all his house as a servant, for a testimony of those things which were afterward to be spoken,

chokri: Vele Khrista cekrolü nupü bi, puce sü ukozo, mhace ukemelü püyo sü tüpü ta lize mu u kemeri sülü thela ketso rechü unüvo lize.
english: but Christ is faithful as a Son over his house; whose house we are, if we hold fast our confidence and the glorying of our hope firm to the end.

chokri: Süo Kemesa Rüpü po kesü, “tha neko mhace püfü reni to lize.”
english: Therefore, even as the Holy Spirit says,  "Today if you will hear his voice,

chokri: “Ne melü rüta hite, pü lüda thivamo, rayhothilü keta tüce medokephi lü lü keta.”
english: Don't harden your hearts, as in the provocation, Like as in the day of the trial in the wilderness,

chokri: “Ne potsa neko a medophi süte, umedephite, mu uko tetse heda mhatho ngo.”
english: Where your fathers tested me by proving me, And saw my works for forty years.

chokri: Süce nuhü süo alüzhe thisü, mu poti, “uko melü retse to thisü tayo; mu uko acho semo.”
english: Therefore I was displeased with that generation, And said, 'They always err in their heart, But they didn't know my ways;'

chokri: Süla a lekhulü reshu kesü, “uko po a kerelü thethilü lü lho.”
english: As I swore in my wrath, 'They will not enter into my rest.'"

chokri: A züneko, menuyi te, mo lize neko humi melülü melükemo mha kesu ba tari sendi mo, side rhü keta Jihova therü tata tomo.
english: Beware, brothers, lest perhaps there be in any one of you an evil heart of unbelief, in falling away from the living God;

chokri: Vele tüsonha hunehu tarho bazote, tha sizi kebahi; molize neko humi nelü thi merüpü kephoma kemeda (shuketse) tilü lü tari se homo.
english: but exhort one another day by day, so long as it is called "today;" lest any one of you be hardened by the deceitfulness of sin.

chokri: Kekrelamori uko thipü Khrista phremi thi süte, mhace shudülü ukemelü thametü zovo thela ketso bayi tisa.
english: For we have become partakers of Christ, if we hold fast the beginning of our confidence firm to the end:

chokri: Neko mhace tha püfü reniyi to lize thajü pü lüda mesü keva bita hite; ne melü thi merü kesülü.
english: while it is said, "Today if you will hear his voice, Don't harden your hearts, as in the rebellion."

chokri: Kekrelamori humi püfü sesü pü lüda mesü süyo, siri Egypt lü Moses po zü taprari tiko metümo.
english: For who, when they heard, rebelled? No, didn't all those who came out of Egypt by Moses?

chokri: Vele tetse heda pü lüzhe thi kesüko, uko kephoma thi kesüko mome? Süko kesamo rayholü retu ketako mome?
english: With whom was he displeased forty years? Wasn't it with those who sinned, whose bodies fell in the wilderness?

chokri: Thüma süko po pü kerelü thethilü lümo keta reshu kevako, vele uko melü mu ketako zo.
english: To whom did he swear that they wouldn't enter into his rest, but to those who were disobedient?

chokri: Süla uko se mu ngo keba uko melümo ketala talü mote.
english: We see that they were not able to enter in because of unbelief.

chokri: Süla uko mülüyi tokhe, kemosa jüpopü utsü kemeta pü kerelü thethilü lüyi mota ze su, kehopüri ne süo tsoyi mota ze suho.
english: Let us fear therefore, lest perhaps a promise being left of entering into his rest, anyone of you should seem to have come short of it.

chokri: Kekrelamori uko ce jüyikeve zhopo uko thisüve, uko ce po kesü bi; vele zhopo uko la meveyi mote, kemelü khipü reni keyi meveyi mo ketala kemelü khi renyi mote.
english: For indeed we have had good news preached to us, even as they also did, but the word they heard didn't profit them, because it wasn't mixed with faith by those who heard.

chokri: Uko pü melü ketako pü kerelü thethilü lute pü hibi posü, “I alüdalü reshu sü, mhace uko a kerelü lü lüri hote; mhathoko cejü meju lü thitso metava sheri.”
english: For we who have believed do enter into that rest, even as he has said, "As I swore in my wrath, they will not enter into my rest;" although the works were finished from the foundation of the world.

chokri: Kekrelamori pü thethi kekre puce pola kesü zho thüna nha pü mhathoko yo relüyi.
english: For he has said this somewhere about the seventh day, "God rested on the seventh day from all his works;"

chokri: Mu thethi hice posa kesü, “mhace uko a kerelü lü talüri ze.”
english: and in this place again, "They will not enter into my rest."

chokri: Ngokeyi, süla humi talü to, sülü, mu humi talü mote, melü kemola. Zhopo thisü zori.
english: Seeing therefore it remains that some should enter therein, and they to whom the good news was before preached failed to enter in because of disobedience,

chokri: Süsa sheri tüsozho che sala sü David yo po kesü, “tha pütü kecho salü, posakesü, ‘tha’, neko mhace melüyi to lize, püfü se kesüce ne melü khri mu rüta hite.”
english: he again defines a certain day, today, saying through David so long a time afterward (just as has been said), "Today if you will hear his voice, Don't harden your hearts."

chokri: Kekrelamori mhace Joshua kerelü pü uko tsüsü lize, süsa pü tüsozho kekrepü posü moto me.
english: For if Joshua had given them rest, he would not have spoken afterward of another day.

chokri: Sülü Jihova thümako la kerelü thethi püba.
english: There remains therefore a Sabbath rest for the people of God.

chokri: Kekrelamori pü pü kerelü thethilü lü tice, pü pü mhathoko lü relüto, Jihova sheri pü mhathoko lü relü ketabi.
english: For he who has entered into his rest has himself also rested from his works, as God did from his.

chokri: Süla uko kerelü thethilü lüyi ketola mhatho thisü khe, sizi kemosa thüma humi melü kemoko thajü thipü mipo se mu ngo ketako bi retu ta ketola.
english: Let us therefore give diligence to enter into that rest, lest anyone fall after the same example of disobedience.

chokri: Kekrelamori Niepo Jihova jü rhü mu rekri züte, keku kene, mu zecho yi keve ceri veku, u kerhüro mu u rüpü zükhro kekre süyo, u kerüko mu ukelü urolü kebako, ukelü medoko mu u thitole medo kebako metü zükhro kekre süde seyi tayo.
english: For the word of God is living, and active, and sharper than any two-edged sword, and piercing even to the dividing of soul and spirit, of both joints and marrow, and is able to discern the thoughts and intentions of the heart.

chokri: Mori kerhü khonu pü mhüjülü mese mengo sü kemo püri di, vele mhametüko pümhü ce methu mu keva küyi bamo, uko sheri pü mhüjülü baphrü zo.
english: There is no creature that is hidden from his sight, but all things are naked and laid open before the eyes of him with whom we have to do.

chokri: Ngo keba uko la thevo kerekrü püba, pü tükolü tayo, Jisu Jihova Nu, uko zho rele (kemelü) khi kemetüpü tazo khe.
english: Having then a great high priest, who has passed through the heavens, Jesus, the Son of God, let us hold tightly to our confession.

chokri: Kekrelamori u thathi kebace uphimezheyi keho Thevo kezhoo la mori, vele püco metülü uko bi zode dophi pü kedozo, siri pü kephoma thimo.
english: For we don't have a high priest who can't be touched with the feeling of our infirmities, but one who has been in all points tempted like we are, yet without sin.

chokri: Süla uko thepu shu zode ngokemezhe kedebalü ta vokhe, side uko phikemezhe ngoyi ketola, mu utsakhulü ngokemezhe pü kekhrühi süo ngoyi ketola.
english: Let us therefore draw near with boldness to the throne of grace, that we may receive mercy, and may find grace for help in time of need.

chokri: Kekrelamori thevo kerekrüo thüma dolü keda mekre pü Jihova mhatho kethila rütho mu kephoma reshü kha kesüko thümala thisü yo.
english: For every high priest, being taken from among men, is appointed for men in things pertaining to God, that he may offer both gifts and sacrifices for sins.

chokri: Pü moti matumi yo pü kekhrü kene mu cho vajo ketako la sheri, pütho pula sheri vetsü tso kemola, nkethathi püyo ba tibi.
english: The high priest can deal gently with those who are ignorant and going astray, because he himself is also surrounded with weakness.

chokri: Püco pula hibi tila, thümako la sheri mu pütho pula sheri kephomako reshü thisüyo.
english: Because of this, he must offer sacrifices for sins for the people, as well as for himself.

chokri: Mu thüma pü sheri therho hio khiyi tamo, vele süo khipü Jihova tsüyo, Aaron thi kesübi.
english: Nobody takes this honor on himself, but he is called by God, just like Aaron was.

chokri: Süla Khrista sheri pütho pü khipü zapozale kethilü sü vamo, pethi kezhoo (thevo kerekrüo) Kethilü; vele püpo kesü, “No a nu, tha I n menusü.”
english: So also Christ didn't glorify himself to be made a high priest, but it was he who said to him, "You are my Son. Today I have become your father."

chokri: Thethi kekre pülü sheri posa kesü, “No thevo mipü, Melchizedec biti, kechothe la.”
english: As he says also in another place, "You are a priest forever, After the order of Melchizedek."

chokri: Jisu püthi püpho thipü keta ce pü kechothi mu pü mezhemelo, pü mhüjü kri zode krasefü de kesalü pü kelasüno, pü mpe ketace pü füse mu reni süyo.
english: He, in the days of his flesh, having offered up prayers and petitions with strong crying and tears to him who was able to save him from death, and having been heard for his godly fear,

chokri: Pü Nuo zo sheri jüzü thisü mhatholü kemezhe.
english: though he was a Son, yet learned obedience by the things which he suffered.

chokri: Mu pü thipü vetsütso sü, pü kechothe la kelakeyi kekuo püfü zü keyi koyo, metüko.
english: Having been made perfect, he became to all of those who obey him the author of eternal salvation,

chokri: Jihova pü cüpü “thevo kerekrüo” Melchizedec zho rele biti.
english: named by God a high priest after the order of Melchizedek.

chokri: Püla mhakekro po keto ba, mu pula potsoyi kelho, neko kerenilü medu (melo) tila.
english: About him we have many words to say, and hard to interpret, seeing you have become dull of hearing.

chokri: Kekrelamori neko kemethami thi keta mesü neko mipo neko metha zoba keta Jihova jü kerace kepoko; jükemetü mori nojü jü kemenü, ketico (themu) mori natsimi co.
english: For when by reason of the time you ought to be teachers, you again need to have someone teach you the rudiments of the first principles of the oracles of God. You have come to need milk, and not solid food.

chokri: Kekrelamori nojü nokebako kemezilü natsimi bi mhathiyi semo bayo; pü payhomi zo tila.
english: For everyone who lives on milk is not experienced in the word of righteousness, for he is a baby.

chokri: Vele ukehüzho pethi ketakola ketico kemetü püco süo sü ketako kevekesu se kekre tayo, mu thi mu zü vo ta yo.
english: But solid food is for those who are full grown, who by reason of use have their senses exercised to discern good and evil.

chokri: Süla Khrista ju shudü jüko khapü meta de vetsütso keto lü metoyi khe, kesa mhatho shudüjü thelü redekeyi laco zota mode kemelü Jihova ceco va keyi lü metoyi khe.
english: Therefore leaving the doctrine of the first principles of Christ, let us press on to perfection--not laying again a foundation of repentance from dead works, of faith toward God,

chokri: Jülokepü zho, ubechopü mila kechokethi, kesalü rhüla kesü, mu kechothe la usheco thi keto.
english: of the teaching of baptisms, of laying on of hands, of resurrection of the dead, and of eternal judgment.

chokri: Mha hiko thizoto, mhace Jihova pü medo tisa.
english: This will we do, if God permits.

chokri: Kekrelamori vapü humi kevelü tava mu tülü zhüko metha ri va, mu Kemesa Rüpü phremi ri thi tato;
english: For concerning those who were once enlightened and tasted of the heavenly gift, and were made partakers of the Holy Spirit,

chokri: Jihova jü keve methava, mu hisa vori keto cejü kekuko ri methava,
english: and tasted the good word of God, and the powers of the age to come,

chokri: Mhace uko tsüsa sala keto, Jihova Nu gu sala ukoyo thi süte, side püpü thengalü sü sala bate.
english: and then fell away, it is impossible to renew them again to repentance; seeing they crucify the Son of God for themselves again, and put him to open shame.

chokri: Kekrelamori türüpü cejülü kesüce cejü jü süo krayi, side nhapo thü prari, khakesü tirü süo bi, uko Jihova thejo ngoyi.
english: For the land which has drunk the rain that comes often on it, and brings forth a crop suitable for them for whose sake it is also tilled, receives blessing from God;

chokri: Vele tsahu mu hushi hü kesüriko khavayo, süko reshu meno bate, pü thelalü melü thü keva zo.
english: but if it bears thorns and thistles, it is rejected and near being cursed, whose end is to be burned.

chokri: Vele ukekhrümiko, neko süce vekuti seba keta ha melü tüpü bazo, kelakeyi sade po keba hibiti po bazo sheri.
english: But, beloved, we are persuaded of better things for you, and things that accompany salvation, even though we speak like this.

chokri: Kekrelamori Jihova mezi motalhozo ne kekhrü mhatho thi kesüko rekramo taho, neko pü zalü thi kesüko, neko kemesakoyo thi kesü mu yithothi kesüko.
english: For God is not unrighteous, so as to forget your work and the labor of love which you showed toward his name, in that you served the saints, and still do serve them.

chokri: Mu ha kemenyü neko kehopü pü sheri sürhü sürho de ne kemeri tavo thelatso keyilü tüpü bazo tho.
english: We desire that each one of you may show the same diligence to the fullness of hope even to the end,

chokri: Neko metsüta hite, vele kemelülü sügüsühe pü reshupü kemeta jüo kalü la mhathi ketako süko sajimi thi yite.
english: that you won't be sluggish, but imitators of those who through faith and patience inherited the promises.

chokri: Kekrelamori Jihova reshujü thipü Abraham cesü, kekrelamori puce zhokuti ba samo, pütho reshu pü püyo sü.
english: For when God made a promise to Abraham, since he could swear by none greater, he swore by himself,

chokri: Pü pokesü, I thejo kha n yo sü ketho zoto, mu thimekro kesü n mehü mekro süto.
english: saying, "Most surely I will bless you, and I will surely multiply you."

chokri: Mu sübi, pü sügüsühe de gü keyila pü thejo süo ngoyi.
english: Thus, having patiently endured, he obtained the promise.

chokri: Kekrelamori thüma lize reshu thipü upi kezho mice süyo, mu reshu thi kesü ketükesoti basamo tade thela thiva yo.
english: For men indeed swear by a greater one, and in every dispute of theirs the oath is final for confirmation.

chokri: Süsü bi Jihova sheri pü medolü kalü khasütsü keto pü mharhü rederele kemo reshu pü thi kemetü süzo.
english: In this way God, being determined to show more abundantly to the heirs of the promise the immutability of his counsel, interposed with an oath;

chokri: Rede kemo mha kena sü Jihova meda yo kemo, upono süo melütüpü ulüluba keto, ukemeri püyo rezhüyi leo mu u mhüjülü khakesüo thezü kemetüyi ketola.
english: that by two immutable things, in which it is impossible for God to lie, we may have a strong encouragement, who have fled for refuge to take hold of the hope set before us.

chokri: Kemeri süo u kerhüro fükela ba, kethozo ba kemetü zoto siti kenani mu süo füha salü kemesatho thethilü lute.
english: This hope we have as an anchor of the soul, a hope both sure and steadfast and entering into that which is within the veil;

chokri: Umhüjü ukola talü ketao, süo Jisu zo, thevo kerekrüo kechothe Melchizedec zhorele biti.
english: where as a forerunner Jesus entered for us, having become a high priest forever after the order of Melchizedek.

chokri: Kekrelamori Melchizedec Salem lü kede hio Therü kerekrüthoo thevo (mvo) tila, Abraham kedeko dokhri vade sala vori kezüce püzü kesü mu reliphri putsü.
english: For this Melchizedek, king of Salem, priest of God Most High, who met Abraham returning from the slaughter of the kings and blessed him,

chokri: Abraham sheri pümha püzhü zakerizapü khipü putsü sü, püzaco thera po kesü, “kemezi kedeo” mu sü salü “Salem kedeo,” süosü kezükeve kedeo;
english: to whom also Abraham divided a tenth part of all (being first, by interpretation, king of righteousness, and then also king of Salem, which is king of peace;

chokri: Püpo di, püzu di, pü hükeye kevori shuye ri bamo, pü mehü kesü shudü bamo mu pü kehü thela ri di; vele pü thipü Therü kezhoo Nu thisü, thevo thesüta ketola.
english: without father, without mother, without genealogy, having neither beginning of days nor end of life, but made like the Son of God), remains a priest continually.

chokri: Tsühi thüma hio didü zhoshi lüsüphi, ukrü utsüpumi Abraham tho zori mhava keyiko zakeri zapü khi pütsü.
english: Now consider how great this man was, to whom even Abraham, the patriarch, gave a tenth out of the best spoils.

chokri: Levi nunuko sheri sizi ta kethozo, uko thevo mereko ngo mu thita, thezholü jüko khapü kemeta, zakeri zapü thümako celü khiyi, süosü usazüjürako celü, süko Abraham valü prari tiko.
english: They indeed of the sons of Levi who receive the priest's office have a commandment to take tithes of the people according to the law, that is, of their brothers, though these have come out of the loins of Abraham,

chokri: Vele püshuye phrimo ri Abraham celü zakeri zapü khiyi, reshupü kemeta zalü jopü tsüsü.
english: but he whose genealogy is not counted from them has taken tithes of Abraham, and has blessed him who has the promises.

chokri: Mhakehopü lüri kepide ta kemo jü, thejo pü mitsü kesüo thejo pükeyio ce zhoku, veku. (Act. 20: 35)
english: But without any dispute the less is blessed by the better.

chokri: Hice lize zakeri zapü uko celü pükeyio satamode rhütazo ziti zhopo ba.
english: Here people who die receive tithes, but there one receives tithes of whom it is testified that he lives.

chokri: I posasüyi velize, Levi zosheri keri zapü khiyi, Abraham zakeri zapü khakesü bi.
english: So to say, through Abraham even Levi, who receives tithes, has paid tithes,

chokri: Kekrelamori pü püpo valü balutice, Melchizedec püzü khasü.
english: for he was yet in the loins of his father when Melchizedek met him.

chokri: Mhace Levimiko thevo kethizhoko vetsütso mesazo lize, kekrelamori thümako thezho süo khrolü mhangoyi yo tila, dipüla thevo kekrepü Melchizedec zho biti vori moze suta, mu Aaron zhorele lü meji kemo, thimosuta süse?
english: Now if there was perfection through the Levitical priesthood (for under it the people have received the law), what further need was there for another priest to arise after the order of Melchizedek, and not be called after the order of Aaron?

chokri: Kekrelamori thevo merebü sheri kedetayo, thezho sheri thi kede moze kesu ne.
english: For the priesthood being changed, there is of necessity a change made also in the law.

chokri: Mhahiko popü kemeta shuye kekrepü pometa, shuye süo mipüri reshü bayo ku mozolu.
english: For he of whom these things are said belongs to another tribe, from which no one has officiated at the altar.

chokri: Kekrelamori süo setüküyi uko Niepoo Judah shuyelü hü vori, shuye süo la Moses thevo mhathola mhapüri posümo.
english: For it is evident that our Lord has sprung out of Judah, about which tribe Moses spoke nothing concerning priesthood.

chokri: Süpi sheri semeku keyi kekrelamori thevo kekrepü Melchizedec zhorele biti tapra ri keto popü meta.
english: This is yet more abundantly evident, if after the likeness of Melchizedek there arises another priest,

chokri: Pü thikesü, uthiupho zhorelelü kemo jükha kesüko, vele keku pü thela kedi kerhü.
english: who has been made, not after the law of a fleshly commandment, but after the power of an endless life:

chokri: Süo ju hibi popü meta; “no thevo mipü Melchizedec zhorele biti lü kechothe tato.”
english: for it is testified, "You are a priest forever, According to the order of Melchizedek."

chokri: Kekrelamori jü khakesü himhüjü ketao khisa mote süo thathi te, meve mo ketala.
english: For there is an annulling of a foregoing commandment because of its weakness and uselessness

chokri: Thezho mhapüri thi meve tsütso sü yomo, vele veku keto kemeri khipü vori yo; süo yolü u süsa püvo Jihova (Jihova) menosü.
english: (for the law made nothing perfect), and a bringing in thereupon of a better hope, through which we draw near to God.

chokri: Süsübi pü thipü thevo thikesü reshu jü samo de püthi sümo;
english: Inasmuch as he was not made priest without the taking of an oath

chokri: Kekrelamori thevo kekreko reshu sa mode thevo thi, vele thevo hio no reshulü thi, jü pula popü kemeta, “Niepoo reshu sü mu pülü rede taho,” No kechothe thevo Melchizedec zhorele biti lü.
english: (for they indeed have been made priests without an oath), but he with an oath by him that says of him, "The Lord swore and will not change his mind, 'You are a priest forever, According to the order of Melchizedek'".

chokri: Hibi tilü Jisu thipü (jamin) thepü püyite jutsüjükho veku tiola.
english: By so much has Jesus become the collateral of a better covenant.

chokri: Thevo kekro tazo ketho kekrelamori kesapo uko mediva va ketala.
english: Many, indeed, have been made priests, because they are hindered from continuing by death.

chokri: Vele thüma hio kechothe tasü tazo, thevo mhatho redereleyo kemola.
english: But he, because he lives forever, has his priesthood unchangeable.

chokri: Süyono pü mi kelayi veba keta kekhotholü ketako zori püza khi Jihova ce vori keyiko, pü kechothe rhü keta bi ukola pü mezhemelo sü ba ketala.
english: Therefore he is also able to save to the uttermost those who draw near to God through him, seeing he ever lives to make intercession for them.

chokri: Kekrelamori pü thevo kerükrüthoo uko thita pü mesa, mi methi menimo, püthimerho vamo, kephomako ceri kreku, mu tülü ceri rekrüku.
english: For such a high priest was fitting for us: holy, guiltless, undefiled, separated from sinners, and made higher than the heavens;

chokri: Thevo kekreko tüsonha rütho khasüyo, therao pütho pü kephoma kola, mu süsalü thümako kephomala; pü süko bi tamo tüsonha rütho khi mori vete, kekrelamori pü vapü thi süve mu pütho püpho khasü te.
english: who doesn't need, like those high priests, to daily offer up sacrifices, first for his own sins, and then for the sins of the people. For this he did once for all, when he offered up himself.

chokri: Kekrelamori thümako thipü thevo kerekrü thi kesüko thathi, vele jü khakesü reshujü süo thezholü Nuo thisü; pü thikemesa pü kechothelü süve.
english: For the law appoints men as high priests who have weakness, but the word of the oath which came after the law appoints a Son forever who has been perfected.

chokri: Süsa jükho kerao zosheri kemesa zhoko yitho kethila cejülü kereshüce bayo.
english: Now indeed even the first covenant had ordinances of divine service, and an earthly sanctuary.

chokri: Kereshüce therao thipü kemetalü mefibü kemeto mu miz, kepe tsükhrida, cüpü kereshüce ziyo.
english: For there was a tabernacle prepared. In the first part were the lampstand, the table, and the show bread; which is called the Holy Place.

chokri: Füha keyo kenao salü kereshüba süo cüpü kemesatho, siyo.
english: After the second veil was the tabernacle which is called the Holy of Holies,

chokri: Sülü suna lezho, mharükethenyü thüti khu (fü) mu suna pü jütsüjükho bü pükhü metü nyho, suna lekhulü manna (tsükhri) kha mezü, mu Aaron thü pü prari kebao riba, mu miz yo jükho thipü kemetao riba.
english: having a golden altar of incense, and the ark of the covenant overlaid on all sides with gold, in which was a golden pot holding the manna, Aaron's rod that budded, and the tables of the covenant;

chokri: Süo pilü zapozale Cherubim niba phimezhe kesü bahu püba (ukhri), süko tsühi uko püpü po mekre sü hote.
english: and above it cherubim of glory overshadowing the mercy seat, of which things we can't now speak in detail.

chokri: Tsühi mha hikola thezho thi meta, thevomiko pütü metülü ta kereshüce theralü vo süyo; Jihova zhoko zü mu thitsosü ketola.
english: Now these things having been thus prepared, the priests go in continually into the first tabernacle, accomplishing the services,

chokri: Vele cebü ha kenalü kelü thevo kerekrüo lebi tetse pülü vapü lebi kelü zo, thüza samo de lü yomo, süo khipü rütho thisü pütho pula mu süsa thümako kekri kelako metüla.
english: but into the second the high priest alone, once in the year, not without blood, which he offers for himself, and for the errors of the people.

chokri: Kemesa Rüpü zasi thaka kha kesü kemesatho chode thipü mesemengo sümo zolu, kereshüce kerao balu tithe;
english: The Holy Spirit is indicating this, that the way into the Holy Place wasn't yet revealed while the first tabernacle was still standing;

chokri: Pü hüpi hizi kebathe, süce mhakha kesüko, mu rütho thi kesüko kenani, yitho kethilü pü thipü vetsü vetso mesaketa thisü mozo, ulü kelü umedoko laco.
english: which is a symbol of the present age, where gifts and sacrifices are offered that are incapable, concerning the conscience, of making the worshipper perfect;

chokri: Themu ketilü, mu hezo kekralü, mu uphe ube metü kemesa, ufüneko meni kemesa ti dolüniko, uthi upho zhoreleko, mha süko thi kedeva kemothe mipo thino mi kemu thipü miyo kesü lebi zolu.
english: being only (with meats and drinks and various washings) fleshly ordinances, imposed until a time of reformation.

chokri: Vele Jisu Khrista thevo kerekrüo thide vori ketasa mhakeve vorite, zhoku kereshüce vetsütso ku, thüma be khi thimo, süo kepo ce kengoo mo.
english: But Christ having come as a high priest of the coming good things, through the greater and more perfect tabernacle, not made with hands, that is to say, not of this creation,

chokri: Mori tenyü teko za rimo, vele pütho püza khipü ta kemesa thethilü, urhapü kechothe lü kesü ngoyi.
english: nor yet through the blood of goats and calves, but through his own blood, entered in once for all into the Holy Place, having obtained eternal redemption.

chokri: Kekrelamori thodo, tenyüza mu tholine kethü nyopha khipü kegüsü mesakemoko yo, mu süko uthi upho sheri thi mesayo mhalize.
english: For if the blood of goats and bulls, and the ashes of a heifer sprinkling those who have been defiled, sanctify to the cleanness of the flesh:

chokri: Khrista za dithedü süpi tata, pü kechothe Rüpü pütho kha rütho thipü Jihova tsü sü ta lize, kerho kokemo reshü, neko kelü kemedo thopha thophro süte kesa mhathoko metülü, rhü keta Jihova (Jihova) yitho kethilü.
english: how much more will the blood of Christ, who through the eternal Spirit offered himself without blemish to God, cleanse your conscience from dead works to serve the living God?

chokri: Püco hiola jutsüjükho lü metsulü (thedolü) kethao thite jükho kerao khrolü vajopü sata ketakori rhayi ketola pü sa süve, cükeyiko kechothe kalü popü kemetao ngoyi ketola.
english: For this reason he is the mediator of a new covenant, since a death has occurred for the redemption of the transgressions that were under the first covenant, that those who have been called may receive the promise of the eternal inheritance.

chokri: Kekrelamori jükho dipüce khapü meta sheri jükho sülü po kesüo sa moze kesuri ba.
english: For where a last will and testament is, there must of necessity be the death of him who made it.

chokri: Kekrelamori jutsüjükho süo thümao sa kevasa thipü tsü süyo, sizi molize jükho phra kesüo rhü kebathe jü keku bamo tila.
english: For a will is in force where there has been death, for it is never in force while he who made it lives.

chokri: Jükho kerao thüza sa mode thimo.
english: Therefore even the first covenant has not been dedicated without blood.

chokri: Kekrelamori Moses thüma metüko la thezho thopü kemeta püpü lü thüza metho, tenyü za mu jü kegüpü merezü ti thimafü vasü, mu Hyssop (süjü) sükhujü kegüpü lesida vasü mu thüma metüko sheri vasü.
english: For when every commandment had been spoken by Moses to all the people according to the law, he took the blood of the calves and the goats, with water and scarlet wool and hyssop, and sprinkled both the book itself and all the people,

chokri: Pokesü, “thüza hiohi Jihova jükho pü khapü neko zü phakerhü püthi kesü zhü,” zisü.
english: saying, "This is the blood of the covenant which God has commanded you."

chokri: Süpi sheri thüzao kegüpü reshüce mu mhabü khi yitho thi kebako kenani va süyo.
english: Moreover he sprinkled the tabernacle and all the vessels of the ministry in like manner with the blood.

chokri: Mu mhametüko thezholü thopü kemeta bi thüza khipü thi kemesa süyo, mu thüza samode kejovakeyi bamo.
english: According to the law, nearly everything is cleansed with blood, and apart from shedding of blood there is no remission.

chokri: Süosü tülü mhahüpü zo sheri mhako thi kemesa moze su ta lize tülü zhü kethoko süko ceri rütho vekuti moze suku tazo.
english: It was necessary therefore that the copies of the things in the heavens should be cleansed with these; but the heavenly things themselves with better sacrifices than these.

chokri: Kekrelmori Khrista thethi kemesa ube khipü kethilü talü timo ri, süo ketho hüpüzo tila; vele tülü lü ketho kethilü, tsühi uko metü la Jihova mhüjülü bate.
english: For Christ hasn't entered into holy places made with hands, which are representations of the true, but into heaven itself, now to appear in the presence of God for us;

chokri: Thevo kerekrüo tetse metsü thüza khipü kemesa thethilü talüyo, vele Khrista tsü kenu pütho pü khipü rütho thisü zo baho.
english: nor yet that he should offer himself often, as the high priest enters into the holy place year by year with blood not his own,

chokri: Kekrelamori pü cejü thikeyi culü tsükenu pü kemezhe moze su tato, vele tsühi cejü valalü vapü cejü mhüngolü kephoma thi medi sü keto la pütho püpü reshü thi süte.
english: or else he must have suffered often since the foundation of the world. But now once at the end of the ages, he has been revealed to put away sin by the sacrifice of himself.

chokri: Thüma po vapü sa süto, vele süsalü sheco thito (püshe ketito).
english: Inasmuch as it is appointed for men to die once, and after this, judgment,

chokri: Süla Khrista kephoma kekro khipü de pü khipü rütho thisü, mu pekeyiko pü vakena sala vori tice kephoma samo de kelakeyi ngoyito.
english: so Christ also, having been once offered to bear the sins of many, will appear a second time, without sin, to those who are eagerly waiting for him for salvation.

chokri: Thezho hisa mhakeve vori keto khripü ba, pühü tüo zomo, tetse metsü rüthoko thisü kevorio hisa vori ketoo hü thatü sümo.
english: For the law, having a shadow of the good to come, not the very image of the things, can never with the same sacrifices year by year, which they offer continually, make perfect those who draw near.

chokri: Kekrelamori reshü khakesü hi lata su; kekrelamori reshümiko uko kephomako khrü kemesa tsova lize kephoma rekramo tato veli.
english: Or else wouldn't they have ceased to be offered, because the worshippers, having been once cleansed, would have had no more consciousness of sins?

chokri: Vele rütho kethi metsü uko kephomako tetse metsü rekra sü tazo.
english: But in those sacrifices there is yearly reminder of sins.

chokri: Kekrelamori thodo mu tenyü za kephoma thimedi vaho tila.
english: For it is impossible that the blood of bulls and goats should take away sins.

chokri: Süola pü cejülü kevorice poti, rütho mu mhakha kesü no nmedo mote, vele no athi apho khapü atsü.
english: Therefore when he comes into the world, he says, "Sacrifice and offering you didn't desire, But a body did you prepare for me;

chokri: Kethü reshü mu rüthoko kephomala thi kesü no nnüyi mote.
english: In whole burnt offerings and sacrifices for sin you had no pleasure.

chokri: Süce I pokesü, “hihe, ‘I tavorite’ (lesida kekrolü aju thopü meta) n nü n medo thisü ketola, O Jihova!”
english: Then I said, 'Behold, I have come (In the scroll of the book it is written of me) To do your will, God.'"

chokri: Süpi posa kesü, rütho, mhakha kesü, kethüreshü, mu kephomala mhakha kesüko no nmedo mote, nkenü sükolü dite; thezholü reshü kethiko.
english: Previously saying, "Sacrifices and offerings and whole burnt offerings and sacrifices for sin you didn't desire, neither had pleasure in them" (those which are offered according to the law),

chokri: Süce püpo kesü, “hihe, I n medo thi ketola tavori te, O Jihova” pü kerao khi düva, mu kenao thisü ketola.
english: then he has said, "Behold, I have come to do your will." He takes away the first, that he may establish the second,

chokri: Mhathi kemesa kesü mhatho hilü Jisu Khrista püthi püpho khipü rütho thisü vapü pütü hisa laco vori keto metü la sheri.
english: by which will we have been sanctified through the offering of the body of Jesus Christ once for all.

chokri: Thevoko kehopü ri tüsonha reshü thi keba mhatho kethisa thisala bayo, süo kephoma khiva yomo.
english: Every priest indeed stands day by day ministering and often offering the same sacrifices, which can never take away sins,

chokri: Vele thüma hio rütho pü thi kevasa kechothe la thi süte side pü Jihova valaco bate.
english: but he, when he had offered one sacrifice for sins for ever, sat down on the right hand of God;

chokri: Hibi ketala pü ngomuko khi pü phezho thiva keto meri bate.
english: from that time waiting until his enemies are made the footstool of his feet.

chokri: Rüthopü thi kesüla kechothe uko thi kemeve.
english: For by one offering he has perfected forever those who are being sanctified.

chokri: Sübi Kemesa Rüpü sheri uko ce zhopo thisü te, pü süsalü posa kesü;
english: The Holy Spirit also testifies to us, for after saying,

chokri: Tüsozho vo ketako salü I jütsüjükho thisü ketoo sü hiohi zo, Niepoo po süte, I athezhoko kha uko melülü süto mu uko kelülü I lesitho süto.
english: "This is the covenant that I will make with them: 'After those days,' says the Lord, 'I will put my laws on their heart, I will also write them on their mind;'" then he says,

chokri: Uko kephomako mu kejokemuko I rekra samo tato.
english: "I will remember their sins and their iniquities no more."

chokri: Tsühi kejo keva sü hiohi zo, kephoma la rütho kethi basa mote.
english: Now where remission of these is, there is no more offering for sin.

chokri: Azüneko, usüla Jisu za khipü thepu de kemesatho thethilü talü yo.
english: Having therefore, brothers, boldness to enter into the holy place by the blood of Jesus,

chokri: Pü uthi kemesa sü kevala kehü kesa cholü fü hapü yo kemeta süo püthi püpho.
english: by the way which he dedicated for us, a new and living way, through the veil, that is to say, his flesh;

chokri: Jihova celü u thevo kerekrü pü bate.
english: and having a great priest over the house of God,

chokri: Umelü kethothi ukemelü thi kemetüpü pü meno voyi khe; ukelü mesakemoko püza kegü vasü, mu uthi upho jülü khrükemesa va de;
english: let's draw near with a true heart in fullness of faith, having our hearts sprinkled from an evil conscience, and having our body washed with pure water,

chokri: Uko kemelü regü reve ta hide uphra kesüo lü thishu thitha zo tokhe, (püphra kesü jü melü shüzo tila).
english: let us hold fast the confession of our hope unyieldingly. For he who promised is faithful.

chokri: Mu süla kekhrülü hunehu hü mu hunehu rele medode mhatho keveko thiyi to khe.
english: Let us consider how to provoke one another to love and good works,

chokri: Uko tavo kengu kehu tiko lüpü mhatho kemo thiva hi, humi tho sübi tazo sheri, vele hunehu tahitarho, pütü umenoku ta kezüla u süko thiba zokhe.
english: not forsaking our own assembling together, as the custom of some is, but exhorting one another; and so much the more, as you see the Day approaching.

chokri: Kekrelamori mhace umhaseketho ngoyi ketasa utho kenyülü kephoma thita lize süsa kephoma rütho thi keto basa mote.
english: For if we sin willfully after we have received the knowledge of the truth, there remains no more a sacrifice for sins,

chokri: Vele kemere thizo küto sheco melü kemetsumiko thüva keto zote.
english: but a certain fearful expectation of judgment, and a fierceness of fire which will devour the adversaries.

chokri: Thüma Moses thezho khakesü kemetsüko phikemezhe bamo, mi kena mori misü zhopo kha kesülü dokhri vayo.
english: A man who disregards Moses' law dies without compassion on the word of two or three witnesses.

chokri: Sazho dithedü rükuti somi Jihova Nu kedüda kesüko yo süta, thüzalü jükho thi keyiko, sülü pü thi kemesayi kesüo pü mha mesakemo püthi ketasa, mu Kemesa Rüpü kelakeyi kekhrü khakesüo khipü mhatho kemopü thi kevasa?
english: How much worse punishment, do you think, will he be judged worthy of, who has trodden under foot the Son of God, and has counted the blood of the covenant with which he was sanctified an unholy thing, and has insulted the Spirit of grace?

chokri: Kekrelamori uko püse bate, pokesü, khüpo sü azhü, I thipü tsüsüto, Niepoo poyo; süsa sheri Niepoo po kesü pü thümako sheco thito.
english: For we know him who said, "Vengeance belongs to me," says the Lord, "I will repay." Again, "The Lord will judge his people."

chokri: Süosü mha kempe shupü zo rhüketa Jihova belü lü ketosü.
english: It is a fearful thing to fall into the hands of the living God.

chokri: Vele ne rekra sala süte, hijü tülü neko thi metovesü neko kemethi kekro zü ketü vori.
english: But remember the former days, in which, after you were enlightened, you endured a great struggle with sufferings;

chokri: Huce mipo neko pe keti mhapü thi, ne meshu, ne methimeni, humi mipo u methi kesüko sheri zü kemekro püthi tayo.
english: partly, being exposed to both reproaches and oppressions; and partly, becoming partakers with those who were treated so.

chokri: Kekrelamori neko angomezhe ayo kekhrü khasü keropü apha aropü ketalü, mu ne mhakha aketesü (mhavapü atsü) neko tülü süce vekuti seba, mu mha sülü kebako kechothe ta sü keto.
english: For you both had compassion on me in my chains, and joyfully accepted the plundering of your possessions, knowing that you have for yourselves a better possession and an enduring one in the heavens.

chokri: Süla ne kemeri süo kelhu va hite, mhakha neko zhokhro shula sü keto kezho ba tila.
english: Therefore don't throw away your boldness, which has a great reward.

chokri: Neko sügüsüheti ba moze su, ne Jihova medo thi kesü salü, neko kalü jü popü neko tsüpü kemeta ngoyi ketola.
english: For you need endurance so that, having done the will of God, you may receive the promise.

chokri: Kekrelamori pütü kedopü salü pü vori tole kebao vori zoto, mu menu taho.
english: "In a very little while, He who comes will come, and will not wait.

chokri: Tsühi kemelü la kemethomi rhüyi zoto, vele mhace thüma humi tsüsata lize, akerhüro thüma süo la anümo tato.
english: But the righteous will live by faith. If he shrinks back, my soul has no pleasure in him."

chokri: Vele uko thüma size timi mo, tsüsa tade medi kevapü thita mo, vele kemelü kenemi ukerhüro ri kelayi ketomi zo.
english: But we are not of those who shrink back to destruction, but of those who have faith to the saving of the soul.

chokri: Tsühi meri kebao sü kemelü (ukra, utso kenezhü) ngokemo vele size tüzo ziti zhü.
english: Now faith is assurance of things hoped for, proof of things not seen.

chokri: Kekrelamori utsüpumi sheri süo la mipo uko kevepo mu yikeshü ngoyi (Niepoo mhüjülü).
english: For by this, the elders obtained testimony.

chokri: Uko kemelü lü sekeyi cejüko Jihova jülü rhepü prari; süla kengo zhüko ngokemo zhülü thi pü pra mengosü.
english: By faith, we understand that the universe has been framed by the word of God, so that what is seen has not been made out of things which are visible.

chokri: Kemelü lü Abel rütho khakesü Cain reshü ce vekuti thipü Jihova tsü, süola pü zhopo thüma kemezi ngoyi, Jihova püzho posü te; süla pü sata zori püju urethi tazo.
english: By faith, Abel offered to God a more excellent sacrifice than Cain, through which he had testimony given to him that he was righteous, God bearing witness with respect to his gifts; and through it he, being dead, still speaks.

chokri: Kemelü la Enoch kesa ngomo rhü süte, (püthi püphothi rede va); pü phu medita, kekrelamori Jihova püthi rede kevala; kekrelamori pü shüla keta mhüjü zhopo hio ba ketala, pü Jihova nü thiyi.
english: By faith, Enoch was taken away, so that he wouldn't see death, and he was not found, because God translated him. For he has had testimony given to him that before his translation he had been well pleasing to God.

chokri: Vele kemelü samode pünü thikeyi di, kekrelamori Jihova ce kevoko melü moze kesuo sü, pü mhakha kethothi pü phuyo kezhoko ketsüo zoti melü moze su.
english: Without faith it is impossible to be well pleasing to him, for he who comes to God must believe that he exists, and that he is a rewarder of those who seek him.

chokri: Noah sheri kemelü lü Jihova thedü jü khasü ketala ngomo zori mpe de melü shudü ta ru thipü pü cekro kelayi ketola, pü sülü cejüo mejo va, mu kemezi kalü kemelü lü ngoyi.
english: By faith, Noah, being warned about things not yet seen, moved with godly fear, prepared an ark for the saving of his house, through which he condemned the world, and became heir of the righteousness which is according to faith.

chokri: Kemelülü Abraham cüpü thethi pü pra püpo kalü ngoyi ketola, jüzülü; mu pü tapra; pü dipüce voto sheri se bamo zo sheri.
english: By faith, Abraham, when he was called, obeyed to go out to the place which he was to receive for an inheritance. He went out, not knowing where he went.

chokri: Kemelülü pü revü thethipü jüphrapü kemeta lü tavo, sekemo ramolü, pü Isaac mu Jacob ni züde jüfü celü hü; püzü de jüo pophra kemeta kalüo püzü kesa ngoyi ketola.
english: By faith, he lived as an alien in the land of promise, as in a land not his own, dwelling in tents, with Isaac and Jacob, the heirs with him of the same promise.

chokri: Kekrelamori pü nadepü bodü kebapü phu vo, süo thi kesüo sü Jihova zo, zite.
english: For he looked for the city which has the foundations, whose builder and maker is God.

chokri: Kemelülü Sarah sheri nuhü püyi, mu natsimi pü (nuranu) pü menusü pü kehüzho züta zo sheri, kekrelamori puce jüphra kesüo jüthozo to size melü tila.
english: By faith, even Sarah herself received power to conceive, and she bore a child when she was past age, since she counted him faithful who had promised.

chokri: Süla pü kesamipü bi tazo sheri tüyo thenyü kobi phriphayi keho krode mu jü kezho poyo teshünyo phiphayi kehoko bi thüma püyo hü süri ta.
english: Therefore as many as the stars of the sky in multitude, and as innumerable as the sand which is by the sea shore, were fathered by one man, and him as good as dead.

chokri: Hiko metü kesami thite, jüphrapü kemetao ngoyi kemolü, vele kemecu lü süko ngo; mu süko hu tazo, mu melü tsapü tavo, mu uko zowe (zono) sekemo revü thi ketami mu resomi thi de cejülü revümi thita.
english: These all died in faith, not having received the promises, but having seen them and embraced them from afar, and having confessed that they were strangers and pilgrims on the earth.

chokri: Kekrelamori mha süko thi kelaceri uko rünapü phukezü po metsü metse süzo.
english: For those who say such things make it clear that they are seeking after a country of their own.

chokri: Kethozo, mhace uko rünalü taprari ketao rekra ketho thita lize uko sala sülü vo tari pütü keve ukola bazo veli.
english: If indeed they had been thinking of that country from which they went out, they would have had enough time to return.

chokri: Vele uko rüna veku tipü menyü tate, süosü trülü, sülano Jihova uko cüpü uko Rüo kethi menga tamo; pü nadepü kesüpü ukotsüsü.
english: But now they desire a better country, that is, a heavenly one. Therefore God is not ashamed of them, to be called their God, for he has prepared a city for them.

chokri: Abraham kemelü dojü kepelü Isaac pü reshü thi, mu pünu pupo khi reshü thi, mu pünu puleo khi reshü kethila Jüphra kesüo kalü ngoyi.
english: By faith, Abraham, being tested, offered up Isaac. Yes, he who had gladly received the promises was offering up his one and only son;

chokri: Sülü mhapo kesü, “Isaac yo ntsa mehüto.”
english: even he to whom it was said, "In Isaac will your seed be called;"

chokri: Jihova kesami zori phü merhüsü yive ziti kelü, pü sülü pühüpü ngoyi tazo.
english: accounting that God is able to raise up even from the dead. Figuratively speaking, he also did receive him back from the dead.

chokri: Kemelü lü Isaac reliphri pü Jacob tsü mu, Esau sheri hisa vori ketoko la.
english: By faith, Isaac blessed Jacob and Esau, even concerning things to come.

chokri: Kemelülü Jacob sheri sata kezüce reliphri pü Joseph nuni tsüsü, püthü nürhü püde reshü thi tazo.
english: By faith, Jacob, when he was dying, blessed each of the sons of Joseph, and worshiped, leaning on the top of his staff.

chokri: Kemelülü Joseph pü sülü Isreal nuneko taprata keto posü tazo, mu püroko la jü kha uko tsüsü.
english: By faith, Joseph, when his end was near, made mention of the departure of the children of Israel; and gave instructions concerning his bones.

chokri: Kemelüla Moses sheri menu keyice tekhri sü pü krüni püpü keva, kekrelamori nuo pütü shuzo tila, mu uni kedeo jü kha kesüo mpe mozo.
english: By faith, Moses, when he was born, was hidden for three months by his parents, because they saw that he was a beautiful child, and they were not afraid of the king's commandment.

chokri: Kemelüla Moses sheri pü tetseko kevori ce (pethiprari) pü cüpü “Pharaoh nupü nuo” site mela vazo.
english: By faith, Moses, when he had grown up, refused to be called the son of Pharaoh's daughter,

chokri: Pütü dopü kephomalü pünü thi kebaceri Jihova thümako zü kemezhe keto kadayi ta.
english: choosing rather to share ill treatment with God's people, than to enjoy the pleasures of sin for a time;

chokri: Egypt lü ceneko ceri Khrista la zhoshu veku ti lü ketala, kekrelamori pü zhokhro hisaco veshusü keto rho kuta.
english: accounting the reproach of Christ greater riches than the treasures of Egypt; for he looked to the reward.

chokri: Kemelüla pü Egypt khava tata, kedeo lüda sheri mpe mozode, kekrelamori pü sügüsüheyi, püngokemo pü ngo kebabi sü.
english: By faith, he left Egypt, not fearing the wrath of the king; for he endured, as seeing him who is invisible.

chokri: Pü kemelülü ta kepra lü thüza khi kegü vasü, size kemosa kemekapo nuranuko meka vaze kesula.
english: By faith, he kept the Passover, and the sprinkling of the blood, that the destroyer of the firstborn should not touch them.

chokri: Kemelülü uko jükezho kemereo lü tavota, cejü kesolü keta thide, Egypt thümako sülü uko mehü khriva.
english: By faith, they passed through the Red sea as on dry land. When the Egyptians tried to do so, they were swallowed up.

chokri: Kemelülü Jericho sazho retuta, zhothüna tametu kevo salü.
english: By faith, the walls of Jericho fell down, after they had been encircled for seven days.

chokri: Rahab thethüpü sheri kekreko züde kesa katamo, melükemoko zü, thenopü pünü saide pü rami ra kevoriko meso keyila.
english: By faith, Rahab the prostitute, didn't perish with those who were disobedient, having received the spies in peace.

chokri: I dipü posata? Gideon ju posaketo pütü bamote, Barak sheri, mu Samuel sajukepoo sheri; Samson, Jephthae, David hiko metü.
english: What more shall I say? For the time would fail me if I told of Gideon, Barak, Samson, Jephthah, David, Samuel, and the prophets;

chokri: Hiko kede shuyeko menisü, kemezi khivori, jüphra kemeta ngoyi, losha tako kekhayi;
english: who, through faith subdued kingdoms, worked out righteousness, obtained promises, stopped the mouths of lions,

chokri: Meketo lüda kerü sheri va mhüsü, zecho yikeve sheri therayi, kethathi kemeza sheri thi meku meco sü, therimave thi kemetüsü, thipaheko rüna kekrelü kebako zü kezhevo.
english: quenched the power of fire, escaped the edge of the sword, from weakness were made strong, grew mighty in war, and turned to flight armies of aliens.

chokri: Sa ketako pho merhüpü thenoko tsüsü, mu kekremiko mipo uko methimeni, uko khasüva keto mela; kekrelamori hisa laco rhüsa kesü veku tipü ngoyi keto menyü ketala.
english: Women received their dead by resurrection. Others were tortured, not accepting their deliverance, that they might obtain a better resurrection.

chokri: Mu kekremi kropü mipo uko krünyü methimeni uko vü shusü, ile, süpi sheri uko phaliro kero khipü side thejü thita.
english: Others were tried by mocking and scourging, yes, moreover by bonds and imprisonment.

chokri: Mipo ketsüpü uko va (ukozhe), yisü süzhe lü uko rüthüsü, dophi kerüpü uko kedo, zecholü uko dokhri; uko tekoyi, tenyüyi khipü ta luce hice ru rehu vo, mhadi tsakhu, miko uko meshu, methimeni.
english: They were stoned. They were sawn apart. They were tempted. They were slain with the sword. They went around in sheep skins and in goat skins; being destitute, afflicted, ill-treated

chokri: Thüma süko cejü hio ukola bayi shü kemo thitazo, uko cejü kesakolü rehu ru, cejütu koyo vota, khonu cekolü, cejü kececelü;
english: (of whom the world was not worthy), wandering in deserts, mountains, caves, and the holes of the earth.

chokri: Hiko metü kemelü keve la mipo uko ju keve kepo mu thechu pü zo sheri jüphrapü kemetao ngoyi molu.
english: These all, having had testimony given to them through their faith, didn't receive the promise,

chokri: Jihova ukola mha veku tipü khapü utsü meta, sükopo uko sa mode ngoyi tamo ketola.
english: God having provided some better thing concerning us, so that apart from us they should not be made perfect.

chokri: Süla zhopo kemhü kezho umetupü kebabi uko metüpü ba, uko kemeshi süko khapü meta vakhe, kephoma ukeyhu ketako sheri khava side sügü sühe pü umhüjü laco ta kehu kezülü tasü khe.
english: Therefore let us also, seeing we are surrounded by so great a cloud of witnesses, lay aside every weight and the sin which so easily entangles us, and let us run with patience the race that is set before us,

chokri: Ukemelü kha kesüo, thitso kesüo Jisu pe zoyi khe, pü kenü pü mhüjülü kebala Cross yo kethenga sheri hüyi tazo, mu Jihova bo püza laco kedebao yo bate.
english: looking to Jesus, the author and perfecter of faith, who for the joy that was set before him endured the cross, despising shame, and has sat down at the right hand of the throne of God.

chokri: Kephomako püzho rele zü kepile tiko rele lüsü phite, molize ne sheri ne kelükolü meyhumezhe ta mu phesa tari sehomo.
english: For consider him who has endured such contradiction of sinners against himself, that you don't grow weary, fainting in your souls.

chokri: Neko kephoma metsu tilü nekoza mekrü ti ketso mizü ketü mozolu.
english: You have not yet resisted to blood, striving against sin;

chokri: Neko khipü natsimiko tarho kesübi po kesüko neko rekramote, Anu, Niepoo u methade u methi kesü lü kemejo va hite; mo pü neko lekhu kesüko sheri.
english: and you have forgotten the exhortation which reasons with you as with children, "My son, don't take lightly the chastening of the Lord, Nor faint when you are reproved by him;

chokri: Pü kekhrümi pü sümi methimeni de metha süyo, pü nu thi ketako sümi vüsü de züyi yo.
english: For whom the Lord loves, he chastens, And scourges every son whom he receives."

chokri: Mhace pü neko methimeni süyo lize ne sügüsühe keyilü Jihova neko pü pü nuko thi bate; kekrelamori mi nune kethimiko uponeko sopü methide metha kemoba?
english: It is for discipline that you endure. God deals with you as with children, for what son is there whom his father doesn't discipline?

chokri: Vele mhace neko methi meniyo mo lize, sülü neko ti methakemola, süsa neko tekhrünuko tazo, nuthonuko mote.
english: But if you are without discipline, of which all have been made partakers, then are you illegitimate, and not children.

chokri: Süsasheri uthi upho la uponeba, pü u thikemezi süyo, mu uko süko rho mu ukofüzüyo, siziyolize uko rüpü pü khrikhro ta lüzüzomome, side rhüyi zümome.
english: Furthermore, we had the fathers of our flesh to chasten us, and we paid them respect. Shall we not much rather be in subjection to the Father of spirits, and live?

chokri: Kekrelamori pütüdopü la zosheri ukhapü uko nü medolü süyo lize, vele süceri meveku, praku ukola, upo pü kemesa zhürhüthi pü phremi kethi ngoyi ketola.
english: For they indeed, for a few days, punished us as seemed good to them; but he for our profit, that we may be partakers of his holiness.

chokri: Tsühi umethi u metha kesü unüphimo, vele ulüsu ulüthi rizo, sizosheri hisalaco kenü sezhü tato süko la kemezi dojü keyilü.
english: All chastening seems for the present to be not joyous but grievous; yet afterward it yields the peaceful fruit of righteousness to those who have been exercised thereby.

chokri: N bo lolo metümo, nkhüba yoyo lulu metümo ketako thi kemetü yite, cho kerekrü süte.
english: Therefore, lift up the hands that hang down and the feeble knees,

chokri: Nphe kevo chodeko thi kemezi yite, kemosa nphe reyho keta chode vajo vato, vele süko po sürho yite.
english: and make straight paths for your feet, so that which is lame may not be dislocated, but rather be healed.

chokri: Thüma metü zü kezüve yite, kemesa lac ova yite, sizi kemosa mipüri Niepoo ngoho.
english: Follow after peace with all men, and the sanctification without which no man will see the Lord,

chokri: Mhape menu sürhü shuyite molize thüma kehopüri Jihova kekhrü tsomota ri ve, sizi kemosa kekhu nlü prü prari nthikemezhe vato, side mikekro thi merho vayo.
english: looking carefully lest there be any man who falls short of the grace of God; lest any root of bitterness springing up trouble you, and thereby the many be defiled;

chokri: Kemosa keregü kerezhü, mipothalice de kepomesu keva Esau libi, püticopüla pu pethizho zhuti ta.
english: lest there be any sexually immoral person, or profane person, as Esau, who sold his birthright for one meal.

chokri: Kekrelamori pü süsalaco dipü bita sheri neko thori sebato, pü thejo kalü ngonyü sü zori pümelakeva, pü thelü redekeyi thethi (pütü) ngosamote, pü mhüjü mekri de phu zo sheri.
english: For you know that even when he afterward desired to inherit the blessing, he was rejected, for he found no place for a change of mind though he sought it diligently with tears.

chokri: Kekrelamori neko cejütu ni ketola süyo vomo, süoyo meto keba, mori kemhüketü sükebalü, zübüyi, mu khradelü.
english: For you have not come to a mountain that might be touched, and that burned with fire, and to blackness, darkness, tempest,

chokri: Thecü khu keba fü mori jüko füphra, füphra süko sekeba posamorive ukopo seba ketola.
english: the sound of a trumpet, and the voice of words; which those who heard it begged that not one more word should be spoken to them,

chokri: Ukoce jükhakesü uko güyihota, mhace khonu zo sheri cejütuo nisü lize süo ketsü khi püzhekhri vato, mori sü utakeve pü zorhü vato.
english: for they could not stand that which was commanded, "If even an animal touches the mountain, it shall be stoned;"

chokri: Süo kengo kemere thi ketola Moses sheri poti, “I mpe ri zote mu reshü renaritazo.”
english: and so fearful was the appearance, that Moses said, "I am terrified and trembling."
